# Rust Rewrite Porting Ledger

The C source inventory is tracked with:

```bash
git ls-files "src/**/*.c" "src/**/*.h"
```

Current tracked C/C header file count: 302.

## Rust Module Architecture

The canonical layout rules live in `AGENTS.md` (`Module Layout Rules` +
`Persistence Rules`). Summary of the July 2026 v2 re-architecture:

- One file per NPC: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/<area>/<name>.rs`
  (driver data + parser + QA table + world logic together); registry
  surface stays in `character_driver.rs` with path-stable re-exports.
- Server NPC/queued-event passes: `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/<area>.rs`,
  one fn per legacy driver pass, ordered by `tick_npc::run_all`; `main.rs`
  does not grow with new NPCs.
- `player.rs` split into `player/` (struct + blob framing in `mod.rs`,
  per-system accessors/codecs in submodules).
- `world_events.rs` split into `world_events/` (generic drains, NPC death
  hooks, anti-cheat report formatting, admin DB tasks).
- Persistence: typed serde `characters.player_state_json` (migration 0020)
  is authoritative; legacy PPD/subscriber blobs are a read fallback.
  Local dev DB via `compose.yaml`; live DB tests via
  `cargo test -p ugaris-db -- --ignored` (testcontainers).
- Mechanical split tooling: `tools/rust_split/splitter.py` (spec examples:
  `spec_item_driver.py`, `spec_world.py`, `spec_player.py` in the tool's
  history; write a new spec per split).

## Coverage Rules

- A file is `Ported` only when its externally observable behavior is represented in Rust and covered by tests or a documented integration check.
- Constants-only headers may be marked `Ported` when all public constants and structs are represented in Rust.
- Large behavior files move through `Mapped`, `Partial`, then `Ported`.
- The C server remains the behavioral oracle until every file is `Ported` and client playtests pass.

| `src/system/prof.c` `cmd_steal`/`add_pk_steal` (`prof.c:106-222,894-908`) | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/steal.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_player.rs` | Iteration 175: `World::attempt_steal` ports the full `cmd_steal` body (thief-profession/idle/cursor-item/out-of-map/no-target gates, `can_attack` + arena-or-clan-tile + player-only + `CDR_LOSTCON`-lagging + Live-Quests(area 20) + victim-busy-or-just-regenerated checks, the stealable-item scan over every worn/carried inventory slot except the spell-slot range 12-29 - so equipped items are stealable too, a real C quirk copied as-is - filtered by [`crate::item_ops::can_carry`] and `IF_QUEST`, the `40 + (stealth-percept)/2` chance formula capped by `thief_points*3`, and the three-way `diff = chance - RANDOM(100)` outcome split) as a pure `StealOutcome` enum, so `ugaris-server` owns all player-facing text. `PlayerRuntime::add_pk_steal` ports the sibling `add_pk_steal` (a genuine C quirk: does *not* increment `pk_kills`, only bumps `pk_last_kill`, reusing the kill-timestamp field for steal events too - no new PPD field needed). `apply_steal_command` (`commands_player.rs`) is the command-layer wiring: exact-word match per C's `cmdcmp(ptr, "steal", 5)`, turns each `StealOutcome` into the caller's message plus (for `Caught`/`StolenNoticed`) a `COL_LIGHT_RED` victim notification, and applies `add_pk_steal` only when the thief has both `CF_PLAYER` and `CF_PK`. 18 new `world/tests/steal.rs` tests plus 3 new `tests/commands_player.rs` integration tests. No remaining gaps for this specific command. |

`IID_CARLOS_DOOR`/`IID_STAFF_DRAGONKEY1-4`/`IID_STAFF_DRAGONSTAFF`/`IID_MAX_CHRONICLES`/`IID_MAX_RITUAL` + `DEV_ID_RHORUN`/`DEV_ID_MAX` added for `carlos_driver`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/area3.rs` (new: `thomas_player_facts`/`apply_thomas_events`/`sir_jones_player_facts`/`apply_sir_jones_events`/`astro2_player_facts`/`apply_astro2_events`/`seymour_player_facts`/`apply_seymour_events`/`kelly_player_facts`/`apply_kelly_events`/`carlos_player_facts`/`apply_carlos_events`/`kassim_player_facts`/`apply_kassim_events`/`supermax_player_facts`/`apply_supermax_events`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs` (`award_dragonsbane_achievement`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`create_money_item_sprite` widened to `pub(crate)`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area3.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs` (`apply_area3_immortal_death_from_hurt_event`, `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` extended to include `CDR_KELLY`/`CDR_CARLOS`/`CDR_KASSIM`/`CDR_SUPERMAX`; `apply_lampghost_death_from_hurt_event`, new, `CDR_LAMPGHOST`'s own dedicated hook - not folded into `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` since C's `lampghost_dead` releases the NPC's lamp claim instead of the shared `immortal_dead` no-op), `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs` (`respawn_npc_character`'s new `CDR_LAMPGHOST` branch - `lampghost_respawn`'s `map[m].light > 4` respawn-block gate), `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `IDR_ONOFFLIGHT` timer registration, player light toggling, sprite/light modifier mutation, switched-on/off palace lamp counters, all-lamps-on keep-open window, `IDR_PALACEGATE` zero-character timer dispatch, gate open/close tile/item flag mutation, blocked close refusal, dirty-sector marking, and startup scheduling for existing palace lamps/gates ported with focused core/world tests. Area 3's first character driver, `astro1_driver` (the simplest in the file - no dialogue interaction, no quest, no item logic, just a 15-state ambient moon-telescope monologue auto-advancing every 10 ticks via `quiet_say`, plus an unconditional per-tick message drain and return-to-post walk), is now ported verbatim in `world/npc/area3/astro1.rs`, following the established one-file-per-NPC/`CharacterDriverState` variant/3-exhaustive-match/`tick_npc`-registration/immortal-death-hook pattern already used for area 1/area 2 NPCs. `thomas_driver` (the crypt entrance guard, a single `crypt_state` `0`->`1` greeting gated on `ch[co].level > 18`) and `sir_jones_driver` (the sixteen-state `0`-`15` crypt quest chain itself, cross-referencing the already-ported `CDR_VAMPIRE`/`CDR_VAMPIRE2` death hooks that complete quests 18/19 and advance `crypt_state` to `10`/`15`) are now also ported, sharing a new `AREA3_QA` table (`world/npc/area3/mod.rs`, only the 13 canned-greeting/repeat/restart/aye/nay entries these two drivers need - `list`/`money`/`shortcut to caligar`/`explain`/`engrave:`/the ~86-entry raise-lower-skill block are deferred to `kassim_driver`/`supermax_driver`). 22 focused tests (`world/tests/astro1.rs` x5, `thomas.rs` x5, `sir_jones.rs` x12). `astro2_driver` (`area3.c:1493-1675`, the lost-astronomer's-notes quest giver, `QLOG` 16: a four-state `0`-`3` greeting chain opening the quest at state 0, then an `NT_GIVE` handler that completes the quest and awards `MONEY_AREA3_MOONIES` (2500) as a literal carried money item on first completion when handed `IID_AREA2_ASTRONOTE`, otherwise handing any other item straight back) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/astro2.rs`), sharing `AREA3_QA` and following the identical `Astro2PlayerFacts`/`Astro2OutcomeEvent` split `thomas.rs`/`sir_jones.rs` established; its `QuestDone` reward reuses the same `ZoneLoader`-needs-`instantiate_item_template` precedent as `SirJonesOutcomeEvent::GoldEarned`/`LogainOutcomeEvent::QuestDone`. 6 new focused tests (`world/tests/astro2.rs`). `seymour_driver` (`area3.c:660-958`, `QLOG` 10-12, the Aston-entry greeting/army-enrollment chain) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/seymour.rs`), including its 3-branch `NT_GIVE` handler and the `set_army_rank(co, 1)` enrollment deviation (see that module's own doc comment). `kelly_driver` (`area3.c:984-1379`, the biggest chain: quests 13-15/54/60 across a 27-state `0`-`26` dialogue ladder covering park shrines, swamp-beast-head bounties via its own `collect_heads` helper, and the Caligar-plaque hunt) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/kelly.rs`), sharing `AREA3_QA` plus a new `shortcut to caligar`(7) QA entry for `kelly_driver`'s own god-only fast-forward, and reusing the `give_money_message`/inline-gold-add precedent (`world::gwendylon` et al.) for both `collect_heads`'s bounty payout and the plaque hand-in reward instead of a `ZoneLoader`-backed item reward. 24 new focused tests (`world/tests/kelly.rs`). `carlos_driver` (`area3.c:2067-2321`, the Imperial Army investigator running two independent quest chains gated on `questlog_count(co, 61) < 1`: while quest 61 - the Imperial Vault ritual - has never been completed, every `NT_CHAR` visit drives the `carlos2_state` `0`-`5` ritual chain instead of the `carlos_state` `0`-`6` dragon-staff chain, quest 20, `QLF_REPEATABLE`) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/carlos.rs`), sharing `AREA3_QA`. Two new gaps this port fills: `carlos2_state` had no `PlayerRuntime` accessor yet (`staffer_carlos_state` existed from the questlog port, but not its sibling field) - added `staffer_carlos2_state`/`set_staffer_carlos2_state` at `STAFFER_PPD_CARLOS2_STATE_OFFSET = 23*4` (`struct staffer_ppd`'s last field, confirmed against `src/common/staffer_ppd.h`'s 25-field declaration order); and this is the first area-3 driver needing `mem_check_driver`/`mem_add_driver` (already ported in `character_driver.rs`, reused via `Character::driver_memory`, slot `0`) to avoid repeating the low-level "check back later" line every tick. `case 4`'s conditional `carlos_key` grant has a real C quirk this port reproduces exactly: the "thou wilt need this key" follow-up line is spoken only on a successful `give_char_item`, which can't be known until `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template` runs server-side - so `CarlosOutcomeEvent::GrantCarlosKey` carries `carlos_id` too, letting `apply_carlos_events` call `World::npc_quiet_say` directly after a successful grant (a new pattern; every prior area-3 outcome event's NPC dialogue was spoken unconditionally inside `World`, not conditionally in `ugaris-server`). The `NT_TEXT` "repeat"/"restart" handler's C condition (`ppd->carlos2_state >= 0 && ppd->carlos_state <= 4`) is a real bug - `carlos2_state` only ever increments from `0`, so `>= 0` is always true, making the first branch effectively gated on `carlos_state <= 4` alone - reproduced verbatim per this file's "port the bug" hard rule, not "fixed". The dragon-staff turn-in's `achievement_award(ACHIEVEMENT_DRAGONSBANE)` call is unconditional (not gated on first completion, matching quest 20's `QLF_REPEATABLE` flag), unlike every other quest-completion achievement in this codebase; `award_dragonsbane_achievement` (new `achievement.rs` wrapper around `award_bare_achievement`) is called every successful hand-in. `character_has_item_template` (originally private to `kelly.rs`) was widened to `pub(crate)` and reused rather than duplicated. 13 new focused tests (`world/tests/carlos.rs`) plus 1 new `player/tests/staffer.rs` round-trip test for `carlos2_state`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2629 core [+13] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `kassim_driver` (`area3.c:327-656`, Aston's jewelry engraver) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/kassim.rs`), sharing `AREA3_QA` plus a new `explain`(9) entry. `DRD_ENGRAVE_DATA` (C's transient per-player pending-inscription-text slot) is ported as a new `CharacterDriverState::Engrave` variant stored on the *speaking player*, not on Kassim - same "player, not NPC" precedent as `ClanFoundData`, since the engraving transaction state belongs to whichever player is mid-conversation with Kassim. The `NT_CHAR` state machine (`ENTRY`/`ENGRAVE_TEXT`/`ITEM_WAIT`/`ENGRAVE`), the `"engrave: <text>"` free-text command (parsed *before* falling through to `analyse_text_qa`, matching C's `strcasestr`-first order - confirmed the `engrave: `(8) qa-table row is genuinely dead code in C and skipped it), the gold charge + `IF_ENGRAVED` flag/`description` item write on successful `NT_GIVE`, and the 12-line idle-muttering table (new relative to every prior area-3 driver, ported via `legacy_random_below_from_seed`, same precedent as `macro_npc.rs`'s `macro_idle_mutter`) are all ported verbatim, including a real C oddity reproduced exactly per the porting rules: `NT_GIVE`'s `didsay` (and therefore Kassim turning to face/remembering `current_victim`) is only set truthy for 3 of the 6 sub-branches (already-engraved / not-wearable / engrave-data-missing-error) - *not* for the successful engrave, the insufficient-gold rejection, or the wrong-state fallback, even though all of those also `quiet_say` a reply. 11 new focused tests (`world/tests/kassim.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2639 core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `supermax_driver` (`area3.c:2432-2594`, the past-maxes raiser) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/supermax.rs`), sharing `AREA3_QA` plus 82 new entries (`list`(5)/`money`(6) and the 80-row `raise <skill>`/`lower <skill>` block keyed on `CharacterValue as i32 + 100/200`, matching C's `V_* + 100`/`V_* + 200` `answer_code` encoding exactly since `CharacterValue`'s numeric values already equal every C `V_*` index one for one). New `supermax_canraise`/`supermax_cost` helpers (`item_driver/scrolls.rs`, transcribed from `src/system/skill.c:103-156`) are crate-visible siblings of the pre-existing `skillmax`/`raise_cost`, reused rather than duplicating the divergent `legacy_supermax_canraise` already living in `ugaris-server/src/commands_admin/skills.rs` (a `/setskill`-only helper, out of scope to touch here). `ppd->supermax_state`/`supermax_gold` live on C's *global* `misc_ppd` struct (not the area-3-specific `area3_ppd` every other area-3 driver's PPD fields use), so the new `PlayerRuntime` accessors (`supermax_state`/`set_supermax_state`/`supermax_gold`/`add_supermax_gold`) sit in `player/settings.rs` alongside the other `misc_ppd`-backed accessors (`swapped_at`/`complaint_date`), not `player/area3.rs`. Two real C oddities preserved verbatim (both documented in the module doc comment): `supermax_raise` calls `update_char`/sets `CF_ITEMS` after bumping the skill, but `supermax_lower` does neither; and every driver-initiated line uses the *loud* `say()`, not `quiet_say` like most other area-3 drivers. `supermax_list`'s two `log_char` calls (the header/percentage-list lines, the "Oops, none maxed" fallback) and the `CF_NOEXP` guard messages in all three helpers are private, ported via `World::queue_system_text`. `lampghost_driver`/`_respawn`/`_dead` (`area3.c:2631-2752`, the palace-light puzzle janitor - the last and most architecturally distinct driver in the file) is now also ported (`world/npc/area3/lampghost.rs`), closing Area 3. Its self-defense/aggro cascade uses the same `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 1, 0)` (`aggressive=1`) shape as `CDR_SUPERIOR`, tracking a single victim from both `NT_CHAR` sightings and `NT_GOTHIT`. Its idle job (once no enemy is being fought) is C's `lamp[MAXLAMP]` target-selection loop: scan every registered, currently-lit `IDR_ONOFFLIGHT` lamp for the closest one not already claimed by a strictly-cheaper competitor, claim it, then walk over and use it to extinguish it. C's `lamp[]` array (an `in`/`cn`/`cost` struct per slot, filled by `add_lamp`) is replaced by two pieces of state instead of a parallel registry: "is this item a registered lamp" was already `Item::driver_data[6]` (the flag `world::light`'s `schedule_registered_area3_lamp_extinguish` already scans), and "who currently claims this lamp, at what cost" is the new `World::area3_lamp_claims: HashMap<ItemId, (CharacterId, i32)>`, keyed directly by `ItemId`. Cost ties (an unobservable C implementation detail - C keeps whichever candidate was visited first in `lamp[]` registration order) are broken by lowest `ItemId` instead, the same substitution `world::janitor`'s nearest-light/nearest-take-item scans already use. `lampghost_respawn`'s `map[m].light > 4` respawn-block gate (`ch_respawn_driver`'s only non-trivial `CDR_*` case in this file - every sibling driver just `return 1` unconditionally) is ported in `ugaris-server`'s `spawns::respawn_npc_character`, the one place that already has direct tile access for the generic NPC respawn path; unlike every sibling area-3 driver's shared `immortal_dead` no-op death hook, `lampghost_dead` gets its own dedicated `apply_lampghost_death_from_hurt_event` hook that releases the dying ghost's lamp claim so a competitor (or the same ghost on respawn) can pick it up. `secure_move_driver` substitutes C's plain `move_driver(cn, ch[cn].tmpx, ch[cn].tmpy, 0)` return-to-post call, the same substitution `world::astro1`/`world::robber`/`world::superior` already use (a strict superset, never observably different in practice). 10 new focused tests (`world/tests/lampghost.rs`) plus 2 respawn-gate tests (`ugaris-server/src/tests/spawns.rs`) and 1 death-hook claim-release test (`ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2663 core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1104 server [+3], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. This closes the P4 "Area 3" task. |
| `src/server.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/effect.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/legacy.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/map.rs` | Core flags, values, map/item/character/effect shapes including character sprite, item template ID, death counter, inventory ranges, version, tick constants ported. |
| `src/module/base.c` `stat_scroll_driver`, `src/system/skill.c` `raise_value_exp` scroll path, `src/system/player.c` `cl_raise` / `src/system/skill.c` `raise_value` (`CL_RAISE`) | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `IDR_STATSCROLL` dispatch, carried-only and `/noexp` blocking, C skill-cost/start-factor/max checks needed by stat scrolls, XP grant/spend, bare/effective value raise, consume-on-success behavior, and runtime executed-outcome classification ported with focused tests. `CL_RAISE` now spends already-unspent exp (`raise_value`, distinct from the exp-granting `raise_value_exp` scroll path) through `World::raise_skill`, sending a single-value `SV_SETVAL0/1` + exp/exp_used feedback packet on success and staying silent on failure like C. Both raise paths now call `update_character`: `World::raise_skill` since iteration 17 and `World::apply_item_driver_outcome`'s `StatScrollUsed` arm since iteration 18 (the item driver itself only has `&mut Character`, so the `World`-level outcome handler recomputes once after the scroll's raise loop completes, matching C's per-raise `update_char` since the recompute is idempotent on the final `value[1]` state). As of iteration 20, the `StatScrollUsed` outcome handler also calls `check_levelup` before `update_character`, matching C `raise_value_exp`'s `check_levelup(cn)` call (`raise_value`/`CL_RAISE` never calls `check_levelup` in C, so `World::raise_skill` correctly has no such call). Achievement checks (`achievement_check_skill`/`achievement_check_level`) remain unported for both raise paths. |
| `src/system/act.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/legacy.rs` | Action IDs ported. |
| `src/system/questlog.h`, `src/system/questlog.c` (metadata table, `questlog_scale`, `questlog_done`, `questlog_open`/`close`), `src/system/player.c` `sendquestlog` base packet | `crates/ugaris-core/src/quest.rs`, `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Quest IDs, flags, fixed-size quest log behavior, C bitfield quest-entry packing, base `SV_QUESTLOG` payload shape with zeroed random-shrine PPD, and `CL_GETQUESTLOG` response path ported with tests. The 85-entry `struct questlog questlog[]` metadata table (name/level-range/giver/area/nominal-exp/flags, incl. `QLF_XREPEAT`) is now ported verbatim as `QUEST_TABLE`/`quest_meta()`; `questlog_scale`'s repeat-completion decay curve (`scale_exp`) and `questlog_done`'s level taper (`taper_exp_by_level`) are ported as pure functions; `QuestLog::complete_legacy` ports the full `questlog_done` bookkeeping + exp computation (caller still applies `give_exp`/`dlog`/`sendquestlog`); `QuestLog::open`/`close` now match C's exact flag assignment/guard semantics instead of bitwise OR/AND-NOT approximations. `src/area/1/area1.h`'s `struct area1_ppd` and `src/common/nomad_ppd.h`'s `struct nomad_ppd` are now real fixed-layout codecs on `PlayerRuntime` (`area1_ppd`/`nomad_ppd` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `DRD_AREA1_PPD`/`DRD_NOMAD_PPD` wired into the full ppd-blob decode/encode dispatch), with `questlog_init_area1`/`questlog_init_nomad` ported as pure functions (`init_area1_quests`/`init_nomad_quests` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/quest.rs`) taking a `PlayerRuntime`-built state snapshot. As of iteration 61, `questlog_init_area3`/`questlog_init_staff`/`questlog_init_twocity` are ported the same way (`init_area3_quests`/`init_staff_quests`/`init_twocity_quests` in `quest.rs`, snapshot builders `area3_quest_state`/`staff_quest_state`/`twocity_quest_state` in `player.rs`), with new named accessors added to the pre-existing `area3_ppd`/`staffer_ppd`/`twocity_ppd` raw-byte blocks for every NPC state field those three functions read. This also fixed a real size bug: `LEGACY_AREA3_PPD_SIZE` was `17 * 4` (68) but C `struct area3_ppd` is 18 `int` fields = 72 bytes; corrected to `18 * 4`. Two legacy C quirks are preserved verbatim rather than "fixed": `questlog_init_area3`'s `william_state` ladder has no final `else` (quests 22/23 keep their prior flags when `william_state <= 0`, unlike every other ladder in the function), and `questlog_init_staff`'s `yoatin_state` ladder's "open" branch tests `aristocrat_state` instead of `yoatin_state` (a copy-paste bug in the original C) - both have dedicated regression tests. As of iteration 62, `questlog_init` itself is ported as `PlayerRuntime::init_questlog` (sentinel gate + calling all 5 sub-functions) and `DRD_QUESTLOG_PPD` has a real fixed-layout codec (`encode_legacy_questlog_ppd`/`decode_legacy_questlog_ppd`, 100 packed bitfield bytes) wired into the full ppd-blob decode/encode dispatch, so `quest_log` now persists across saves; `clear_turn_seyan_ppd` resets it to default matching C's `del_data(cn, DRD_QUESTLOG_PPD)`. As of iteration 63, `PlayerRuntime::init_questlog` is wired into the live login path (`crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`'s `SessionEvent::Login` handler, matching C `login_ok`'s unconditional `questlog_init(cn)`), and the full `questlog_reopen`/`questlog_reopen_qN` per-quest switch (`src/system/questlog.c:342-826`) is ported as `PlayerRuntime::reopen_quest_legacy`/`reopen_dispatch` plus small `reopen_*` helper methods for each area-PPD side effect and series-exclusivity check, replacing the old generic-only `try_reopen_legacy` call in the `CL_REOPENQUEST` handler; new `QuestReopenResult::SeriesConflict`/`NoEffect` variants distinguish "reached the switch but a sibling series quest is open" from "reached the switch but this case is a silent no-op" outcomes (both still resend `SV_QUESTLOG`, matching C). Fixed a genuine pre-existing bug in the repeatability precondition: C's `!questlog[qnr].flags & QLF_REPEATABLE` is actually "table flags are exactly zero" (operator-precedence bug), not "missing the REPEATABLE bit" - the Rust port previously had the "obviously correct" (but C-incorrect) version; now matches C exactly, including letting `QLF_XREPEAT`-only quests (25-28) reach the switch. NPC dialogue drivers that would advance the underlying area PPD state (all P4, unported) still mean the reopened/initialized quest state can't be observed end-to-end yet. As of iteration 64, `quest_exp.h`'s 34 per-encounter exp/money constants are ported verbatim as `crate::quest::quest_exp` (data-only, no consumer yet - only 2 of the 34 are even referenced in the C source). The P3 task itself is now closed (`[x]`): its trailing `destroy_item_byID`/`remove_item_from_body_bg`/`destroy_item_from_body` helpers (`questlog.c:1630-1703`) remain unported pending a Rust `DRD_DEPOT_PPD` model and (for the last one) cross-server IPC, deferred to whichever P4 area task needs them first - see `PORTING_TODO.md` P3 "Questlog initialization & quest state machine" for detail. |
| `src/system/io.c` / `src/system/io.h` | `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/frame.rs`, `crates/ugaris-net/src/*`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Legacy tick frame envelope, TCP session skeleton, per-session server command channels, runtime-to-session framed payload sending, listener readiness/error reporting, default info logging, IPv4 plus IPv6 localhost listening for `localhost`, multi-payload login bootstrap queueing, and chunked full-map bootstrap below legacy frame limits ported. Full gameplay send buffering, compression modes, and backpressure policy remain partial. |
| `src/system/map.h` / `src/system/map.c` primitives | `crates/ugaris-core/src/map.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_ops.rs` | Legacy map indexing, bounds checks, movement/sight blocker helpers, grid wrapper, item map placement/removal, character map placement/removal, `NOMAGIC` flag sync, simple 3x3 drop order, extended pathfinder-backed drop order, carried-item removal, and carried-item replacement ported with tests. C `set_item_map`'s `IF_TAKE` decay-arming (`set_expire(in, item_decay_time)`) is ported at the `World::complete_drop` call site (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/actions.rs`) rather than inside `map.rs` itself, since only `World` owns the timer queue - see "Ralph Loop - Ground Item Decay" below. Light/trap/notify callbacks remain. |
| `src/system/los.h` / `src/system/los.c` primitive LOS | `crates/ugaris-core/src/map.rs` | Conservative line-of-sight helper with blocker tests ported. Full per-character cached LOS table remains. |
| `src/system/light.h` / `src/system/light.c` primitives | `crates/ugaris-core/src/light.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/map.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs` | Legacy light distance, inverse-square light falloff, non-negative tile accumulation, character/item/effect light add/remove gates, takeable-item `MF_NOLIGHT` behavior, lava groundlight sprite table, foreground/sightblock shadow daylight calculation including injectable legacy-randomized flicker path, indoor daylight recomputation, mixed indoor/outdoor reset checks, live world light add/remove wiring for map item insertion, take/drop, timer-driven light item state changes, visible effect map slots, and world-level dirty-sector marking for groundlight/shadow/dlight recomputation ported with tests. Exact global RNG wiring for shadow flicker and bulk LOS-change remove/add around live world characters plus LOS-changing map edits remain. |
| `src/system/path.h` / `src/system/path.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/path.rs` | A* path shape, legacy heuristic, first-direction result, node cap, 2/3 movement costs, inner-bound successor checks, and `path_ignore_char`-style character-ignoring movement mode ported with tests. Other custom target callbacks and exact global-state compatibility remain. |
| `src/system/sector.h` / `src/system/sector.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/sector.rs` | Dirty sector tick tracking, `skipx_sector`, 8x8 character sector head/links, sound sector flood fill, shout sector flood fill, temporary sound door traversal, and hearing checks ported with tests. Runtime character/map integration remains. Iteration 243: added a real profiling baseline instead of leaving that gap unquantified - `profile_map_diff_payloads_cost_at_realistic_player_counts` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/map_sync.rs`, `#[ignore]`d) measures the exact per-tile recompute cost (LOS/`char_see_char`) that a real `skipx_sector` integration into `crates/ugaris-server/src/map_sync.rs`'s `map_diff_payloads` would let the tick loop skip: ~27µs/player/tick, ~2.7ms/tick total at 100 concurrent players and `view_distance=15` against a 24-tick/second (~41.6ms) budget - ~6.5% at a player count far above any real Ugaris concurrency, negligible at realistic counts. `DirtySectors`/`skip_x_sector` remain unwired into the map-sync runtime pending real load data showing otherwise (see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s P3 "Sector skip optimization" entry, now `[~]` with the same numbers). |
| `src/system/see.h` / `src/system/see.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/see.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs` | Character and item visibility rules ported with tests: invisible checks, light/daylight blending, infrared/infravision boosts, light/dark profession boosts, stealth/perception scoring, carried item hiding, takeable item light requirement, and front-wall item side checks. Uses current conservative Rust LOS helper. |
| `src/system/date.h` / `src/system/date.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/game_time.rs` | Game date units, moon phases, solstice/equinox flags, sunrise/sunset, area light overrides, and daylight calculation ported with tests. |
| `src/system/do.h` / `src/system/do.c` primitive actions, `src/system/act.c` primitive completions | `crates/ugaris-core/src/do_action.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/actions.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/regen.rs` | Error codes, duration constants, `speed`, `end_cost`, `do_idle`, deterministic `do_walk` movement checks/reservation, terrain/underwater movement costs, fast-mode endurance cost, timed `do_take`/`do_use`/`do_drop`/`do_attack` setup, `act_walk`, `act_take`, `act_drop`, `act_use` validation to typed item-driver request, `act_attack` deterministic-resolution bridge using ported attack formulas, action step readiness/reset, world-backed action completion helpers, minimal world action tick dispatcher including `AC_ATTACK1..3`, C `tile_special_check` slowdeath hazard damage, underwater drowning damage, oxygen bubble creation cadence, and pre-action tick-loop invocation, `turn`, and `act()`'s `regen_ticker` non-idle-action stamp ported with tests. `World::regenerate_characters` (called once per tick from `main.rs`) now ports C `regenerate()` (skill-gated endurance/lifeshield regen throttled per real second via the new `Character.last_regen` field) and the HP/endurance/mana/lifeshield-leak regen from `act_idle()`, gated by `regen_ticker + regen_time` and the `MF_NOREGEN`/area-33 special cases, with focused tests in `world/tests/regen.rs`. Since Rust's tick loop skips characters with `action == 0` entirely (no per-batch idle completion event like C), the idle regen applies continuously once per real tick using the per-tick-equivalent amount instead of C's `act1`-scaled batch amount; the steady-state rate matches, only the batching granularity differs (documented in `regen.rs`). Not yet ported: `reduce_rage`/`increase_rage` (no `rage` field on `Character` yet), the `NT_CHAR` notify-area call at the end of `act_idle` (tracked by the separate P0 "NPC sighting messages" task), and `check_endurance` (tracked by the "Speed mode" P0 task). `World::complete_attack_with_rolls_and_clash_roll` (`world/combat.rs`) now also emits `notify_area(.., NT_CHAR, ..)` from the attacker's position after `apply_legacy_hurt`, gated on `!CF_NONOTIFY` and firing on both hit and miss (matches C `act_attack`, act.c:763-793), with a defensive attacker-still-alive (`!CharacterFlags::DEAD`) guard mirroring C's `if (!ch[cn].flags) return 0`. Weather/effect movement modifiers, full central action dispatcher, exact legacy RNG parity, `sub_surround`/`V_SURROUND` and `increase_rage` (act_attack's remaining side effects - no `rage`/`V_SURROUND` fields on `Character` yet), death/notify/surround integration, actual use-item driver effects, exact tile-special sound fan-out/random underwater sound selection, and spells remain. |
| `src/system/drvlib.c` distance helpers | `crates/ugaris-core/src/drvlib.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs` | `map_dist`, `char_dist`, `tile_char_dist`, and `step_char_dist` including `tox`/`toy` target-position handling ported with tests. Broader driver library remains. |
| `src/system/error.h` / `src/system/error.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/error.rs` | Legacy `ERR_*` constants, exact error string table, and out-of-bounds fallback behavior ported with tests. |
| `src/system/act.c` / `src/system/do.c` pure attack formulas | `crates/ugaris-core/src/attack.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/do_action.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs` | Attack chance breakpoint table, strict roll comparison, attack/parry skill math, side/back/assassin bonuses, direct melee damage units, armor/lifeshield reduction helpers, `do_attack` setup/reachability checks, core `can_attack` guard subset, `act_attack` HP/lifeshield damage application, `PAC_KILL` adjacent/path-to-target setup, and timed `AC_ATTACK1..3` world completion ported with tests. Exact legacy RNG parity, full `sub_attack` side effects, rage/surround hits, death/hurt/notify integration, clan/hate/group policy, and player fightback state remain. |
| `src/system/do.c` / `src/system/act.c` pure spell formulas and primitive queued spell bridge, `src/system/tool.c` spell timer core, `src/system/poison.c` poison lifecycle core | `crates/ugaris-core/src/spell.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/do_action.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs` | Spell constants, effect IDs, spell item driver IDs, spellpower/duration math, variable mana spend formulas, speed modifier formulas, combat spell helper formulas, player queued spell setup for heal/magic shield/pulse/bless/freeze/flash/ball, timed magic-shield lifeshield completion, timed heal target HP completion, no-op pulse completion bridge, timed bless completion that installs/replaces carried `IDR_BLESS` spell items with legacy modifier/drdata layout, timed flash self-spell installation, timed freeze nearby-target `IDR_FREEZE` speed-spell installation using C `freeze_value` math, C `do_ball`/`act_ball` setup plus `EF_BALL` creation/movement/collision/nearby strike-damage cadence and short-lived `EF_STRIKE` target visuals, C `create_spell_timer` timed spell-driver classification, absolute expiry timer scheduling for bless/freeze/flash/poison spell installation and existing carried spell scan, C `remove_spell` serial/slot guarded carried-spell deletion plus `IDR_FREEZE` action duration/step rescaling after speed restoration, `poison_someone` carried `IDR_POISON0..3` item creation with legacy drdata layout/duration, periodic `poison_callback` tick weakening/rescheduling plus C `hurt(cn, POWERSCALE / 3, 0, 1, 0, 50)` armor/lifeshield/death/message side effects, and `remove_poison`/`remove_all_poison` helpers ported with tests. Exact `hurt` side effects/death handling for ball ticks, notifications/sounds, ice-demon curse side effects, and exact visibility/help/attack policy remain. |
| `src/module/base.c` `enchant_item`, regular `anti_enchant_item` paths | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `IDR_ENCHANTITEM`, `IDR_ANTIENCHANTITEM`, and the regular `IDR_SPECIALANTIENCHANTITEM` dispatch boundary are ported for carried-orb/cursor-equipment semantics, wearable/no-enhance/left-hand blocks, +20 max modifier cap, 3 counted-enhancement limit excluding weapon/armor/demon/light, orb consumption, target modifier add/reduce/remove, and server outcome accounting/empty-cursor feedback with tests. Extracting anti-orb item creation, exact success/block text variants, requirement recomputation, item look output, orb spawner PPD/cooldowns, and live-data smoke coverage remain. |
| `src/system/timer.h` / `src/system/timer.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/scheduler.rs` | Sorted tick timer queue and due-event drain behavior ported with tests. Function-pointer callbacks represented as named timer events. |
| `src/system/task_scheduler.h` / `src/system/task_scheduler.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/scheduler.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | 128-task periodic scheduler, seconds-to-ticks conversion, due-task detection, and server tick-loop integration ported. |
| `src/system/talk.h` / `src/system/talk.c` low-level logging | `crates/ugaris-core/src/log_text.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs`, `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs` | `LOG_*` constants, text sanitization, scrollback behavior, raw color-marker preservation, `say`/`shout`/`holler`/`emote`/`whisper`/`murmur`/`quiet_say` message formats, `SV_TEXT` little-endian length layout, and `sound_area` positional player `SV_SPECIAL` fan-out math with legacy talk-sector gating ported with tests. `World::npc_say`/`npc_quiet_say`/`npc_emote`/`npc_murmur` (`world/text.rs`) generalize C's `say`/`quiet_say`/`emote`/`murmur` for NPC drivers via the existing `pending_area_texts` queue, at their respective `say_dist`/`quietsay_dist`/`emote_dist`/`whisper_dist` (murmur reuses whisper's) distances. `holler`/`shout`/`whisper` NPC-side helpers not yet added (no NPC driver calls them; only player local-speech commands in `commands_chat.rs` do). |
| `src/system/player.c` `log_player` | `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/log_text.rs` | Text packet framing and scrollback rules ported with tests. |
| `src/system/area.c` randomized `area_sound` ambient effects | `crates/ugaris-core/src/area_sound.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Wet dungeon, dry dungeon, woods, park, and underwater section-to-sound roll tables ported, including legacy `player_special` option math and server `SV_SPECIAL` packet emission after successful player-driver action completions, with focused core/server tests. Exact call cadence/RNG parity and remaining sound call-site wiring remain. |
| `src/system/player.c` login block and initial client sync scaffold, `kick_player`, `src/module/lostcon.c`, `tick_login()`/`read_login()` reclaim halves of `src/system/database/database_character.c` / `src/system/player.c` | `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/login.rs`, `crates/ugaris-net/src/session.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lostcon.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/lostcon.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Login block size, endian layout, vendor protocol version, password obfuscation, runtime character-id assignment, temporary `new_warrior_m` player-template instantiation with starter equipment/items, optional PostgreSQL `begin_login`/snapshot load when `DATABASE_URL` is configured, DB snapshot PPD decode into player runtime, logout snapshot save with carried items and re-encoded legacy PPD blob, runtime login/bootstrap response (`SV_LOGINDONE`, `SV_TICKER`, `SV_MIRROR`, `SV_PROTOCOL`, `SV_ORIGIN`, full visible diamond `SV_MAP11`, visible character `SV_MAP10`, visible character identity `SV_NAME`, `SV_SETVAL*`, resources, exp, gold, cursor item, initial equipment/inventory `SV_SETITEM`, `SV_TEXT`), C-mapped `SV_SCROLL_*` plus origin, character clear/update, newly visible diamond fringe tile/character/name packets for one-tile walk completions, per-session visible-diamond cache initialized at login/refresh, same-origin non-walk map diff packets for changed tile/character cells, and cached visible-character `SV_NAME` identity packets for newly seen or renamed characters ported with tests. Player spawn/despawn is no longer instant on disconnect: `kick_player`'s `CDR_LOSTCON` linger is ported (`World::enter_lostcon`/`reclaim_lostcon`/`is_lostcon`/`expired_lostcon_characters` plus a `CharacterDriverState::Lostcon(LostconDriverData { deadline })` state slot, `ugaris-server`'s `enter_lostcon_on_disconnect`/`reclaim_lostcon_on_login`/`take_expired_lostcon_characters`, and `PlayerRuntime::reclaim_for_session`) - a disconnecting player's character stays on the map under `CDR_LOSTCON` for `runtime.lagout_time` ticks, a reconnect within the window reclaims the same in-memory character in place (skipping a stale DB re-read) with its stashed `PlayerRuntime` (PPD blob, keyring, etc.) restored, and the tick loop saves+despawns it if the window expires unclaimed. `lostcon_driver`'s self-defense AI cascade now partially fights back: `World::process_lostcon_messages` (`world/lostcon.rs`) ports its `NT_GOTHIT`-only message loop, and `World::process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random` (`world/npc_fight.rs`, wired from `main.rs`'s tick loop over `runtime.lostcon_players`) reuses the simple-baddy fight-driver engine's `fight_driver_attack_visible`/`fight_driver_follow_invisible` cascade with suppressions built from the stashed `PlayerRuntime`'s `no*` toggles (`PlayerRuntime::fight_driver_suppressions`) - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Player-side fight-driver auto-combat" task for the still-open low-hp-heal/low-mana-potion/low-shield-magicshield pre-cascade, `do_idle` tail, and post-cascade bless/heal/magicshield fallback. Server smoke-tested listening without DB. Password hash verification, robust login rejection/client error flow, character selection beyond direct name lookup, true inventory delta cache, character color/clan/PK identity fields, visibility/light cache parity, full player state machine, lostcon's restarea/arena instant-leave and karma early-exit branches, and duplicate-login kick of a still-connected old session still remain. |
| `src/module/book.c` / `src/module/book.h` `IDR_BOOK` text driver | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Book driver dispatch, zero-character no-op boundary, C `BOOK_*`/`SIGN_*` text cases, raw color marker preservation, character-specific demon ritual words via the legacy `id_rand`/`demonspeak` formula, Earth Demon sign readability gates using Ancient Knowledge, random Book Nook joke selection, earth-demon diary `player_special` effects, runtime `SV_TEXT`/`SV_SPECIAL` emission, and C-compatible item-driver return code behavior ported with focused tests. Exact global RNG parity for joke selection remains. |
| `src/system/player_driver.h` / `src/system/player_driver.c` action setters and primitive runtime bridge, `src/system/area.c` look-section/walk-section slices | `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/area_section.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `player_driver_stop`, `halt`, direct action setters, serial-preserving item/character actions, teleport, spell queue insertion/last-slot overwrite behavior, server-side use of driver setters for direct/spell client actions, and primitive tick-loop setup/completion for idle, walk-dir including diagonal wall-slide fallback, `PAC_MOVE`, adjacent/path-to-item take, adjacent/path-to-target drop, adjacent/path-to-item use including front-wall pathing, `PAC_TELEPORT` as facing item-use with legacy `spec = teleport + 1`, immediate `PAC_LOOK_MAP` turn/LOS/request handling plus server `SV_TEXT` feedback for hidden targets, C `show_section` section-name/level difficulty text for all non-empty legacy area-sector tables, coordinate fallback, and rest/clan/arena/peace flags, C `walk_section_msg` per-player section tracking with dark-gray `Now entering`/`Now leaving` feedback after successful walks, `PAC_GIVE` adjacent/path-to-recipient setup plus `AC_GIVE` cursor-item transfer, and `PAC_KILL` adjacent/path-to-target setup plus timed attack completion, and the `PAC_KILL` pre-switch stale-target-serial guard (C's `ch[player[nr]->act1].serial != player[nr]->act2` check) plus live-traffic serial capture for Kill/Give/character-targeted spells (see "Ralph Loop - Serial Validation Everywhere" below) ported with tests. Queued spell priority execution, actual item use effects beyond potion, full combat/death/fightback side effects, wall-use/door interaction during movement, music/special sounds for section changes, and action error side effects remain. |
| Zone template/map parser scaffolding from `src/system/create.c` / `src/system/map.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs` | Legacy token parsing, `.itm`/`.chr` template record parsing including item `ID`, `.map` directive parsing with origin offsets, live item template ID retention, and tiny sample application into `World` ported with tests. Production zone validation, startup integration, full character template fields, item-driver creation side effects, and respawn/random-loot behavior remain. Iteration 219: confirmed (full re-read of `create.c`'s `load_zone`/`load_zones`/`process_map`/`get_text`, plus a recursive grep of every `.map`/`.itm`/`.chr` file under `ugaris_data/zones/`) that the PORTING_TODO.md "`.pre` zone preprocessor parity" task rested on a false premise: C `load_zones` only ever runs with masks `.itm`/`.chr`/`.map`, never opens `.pre` files, and `process_map`'s directive grammar has no `PRESET`/`LINEWALL`/`CHANCE`/include token at all - the 38 `.pre` files under `zones/*/` are closed-source Windows map-editor paint-palette sources whose expanded output is already baked into the plain `.map`/`.itm` files both the C server and this `ZoneLoader` load; no area's data references one. `MapDirective`'s 9 variants already mirror `process_map`'s token set exactly, so no expansion step was ever missing. Task closed as not-applicable, no code changed. |
| `src/system/death.c` `kill_char`/`die_char`/`god_save_char`/`respawn_callback`/`kill_score_level`/`death_loss`/`drop_grave` core, `src/system/respawn.c` boundary | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/death.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/hurt.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/attack.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `apply_legacy_hurt` now ports the C `hurt()` fatal-blow decision point for player `saves`: a non-PK death with `saves > 0` calls `World::god_save_character` (decrement+cap saves, `got_saved++`, hp reset, poison/burn removal, Ishtar feedback text, same-area rest transfer) instead of the normal kill path, exactly like C calling `god_save_char` before `kill_char` ever runs. Lethal hurt otherwise runs the C `kill_char` follow-up: death-driver dispatch and NT_DEAD fan-out (already ported) plus respawn-timer registration keyed by template/spawn tile, killer kill-score experience with the exact C level-taper table, hardcore kill bonus, LAG caps, queued server-side `give_exp` routing through runtime EXP modifiers, and the timed `AC_DIE` action (duration 12, act1 = killer, act2 = ispk). `AC_DIE` completion ports `die_char`: map/effect removal, C body rules (`CF_NOBODY` given-item drops, `CF_ITEMDEATH` slot-30 drop, `dead_body` items with the legacy sprite formula/description/player color drdata), extended-drop grave placement, body decay expire timers via a generic `expire_item` timer, loot containers as `contained_in` items (inventory + cursor + gold money item with the C sprite ladder; worn equipment kept except two shuffle-selected pieces; spells destroyed), player exp loss with the C newbie/used-exp taper and hardcore quarter, PK no-loss branch, resource restore, rest-position return, and NPC destruction. `respawn_callback` re-instantiates the stored zone template server-side with resource init and ten-second blocked-tile retries. Characters now carry serde-defaulted `template_key`/`respawn_ticks`, zone characters stamp spawn tiles into `rest_x/rest_y` like C `tmpx/tmpy`, and dying players can no longer cancel `AC_DIE` with new actions. Focused core tests cover the kill metadata, kill-score table, body/loot/money drops, NOBODY/ITEMDEATH branches, respawn scheduling/retry, player exp/PK/rest behavior, body expiry, and money sprites. As of iteration 72, `kill_character_followup` also queues a `KillAchievementAward` (`achievement_add_enemy_killed`/`achievement_add_demons`, `death.c:417-422`) whenever the killer is a player, drained server-side by `award_enemy_killed_achievement` (see the Achievements row). As of iteration 82, it also queues a `FirstKillCheck` (C `give_first_kill`, `death.c:196-254`) whenever a player kills an NPC with `ch.class` set (the new `Character::class` field, populated from zone templates), drained server-side by `apply_first_kill_check` for the `kill_score * 5` bonus exp, the class-range congrats message, and the Slayer of Demon Lords achievement (see the Achievements row) - the `get_army_rank_int` message variant + military-points grant stays unported pending the separate "Military ranks" task. Remaining gaps: death-mode loot tables (`loot.c`, currently unreferenced by zone data), `CDR_LOSTCON` exp cap, cross-area rest transfers, the army-rank first-kill message/military-points variant, and exact global RNG parity for equipment loss. |
| `src/module/merchants/store.c`, `src/module/merchants/merchant.c` core, merchant view slices of `src/system/player.c` / `src/system/act.c` `check_merchant`, `src/system/database/database_merchant.c`, `src/system/auction/auction_db.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/merchant.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/merchants.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_chat.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, `crates/ugaris-db/src/merchant.rs`, `migrations/0005_merchant_stores.sql`, `crates/ugaris-db/src/auction.rs`, `migrations/0006_auction_house.sql` | `CDR_MERCHANT = 6` now has a typed driver-state (`MerchantDriverData`) parsed from C `merchant_driver_parse` args at zone load. Merchant NPCs lazily create stores from carried inventory 30+ (beyond `ignore`) as `always` stock with `pricemulti` defaulting to 400, greet visible players once per legacy 12-hour memory window with the C greeting/say format and Fred's extended range, react to `"<name> ... trade"` NT_TEXT speech by setting the speaker's `ch.merchant`, and destroy given items. Plain player `say` speech now fans out as NT_TEXT driver messages to nearby NPC drivers. C `salesprice`/`buyprice` formulas (barter + trader profession + 400 divisor, money exemption) are ported with tests, `sell`/`buy` port cursor-based buying/selling with always-stock preservation, sold-out/gold-low/cursor guards, ware stacking via `store_items_equal`, quest/nodepot/bond/lab/money stocking exclusions, and store gold accounting. The server sends C `con_type 2` store views (`SV_CONNAME`, `SV_CONCNT`, `SV_CONTAINER` sprites, `SV_PRICE`, `SV_ITEMPRICE`, `SV_CPRICE`), routes `CL_CONTAINER`/`CL_LOOK_CONTAINER` merchant-first like `cl_container` with `check_merchant` validation, formats the legacy bought/sold/too-expensive feedback, supports fast-buy inventory storing (`store_citem`), pushes view updates when the active merchant changes, and closes the view with a `con_type 0` packet. `CL_FASTSELL` (`cl_fastsell`, `src/system/player.c:877`) now quick-sells straight from an inventory slot: `apply_fast_sell` in `crates/ugaris-server/src/merchants.rs` reuses the existing simplified `swap` (`inventory_swap_slot`) to pick the slot item onto the cursor, re-validates with `check_merchant`, blocks quest items with the exact C hold-SHIFT message (leaving the item on the cursor, matching C's early return after the swap already ran), and otherwise reuses `merchant_store_sell`/`buyprice` for the trade. `database_merchant.c`'s `load_merchant_inventory`/`save_merchant_inventory` are now ported as `PgMerchantRepository::load_store`/`save_store` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/merchant.rs`), keyed like C by `(merchant_name, merchant_x, merchant_y)` but storing the whole ware list as one `jsonb` array per merchant instead of one row per ware; `main.rs` loads on first store creation (diffing `world.merchant_stores` keys before/after `process_merchant_actions()` each tick, since `ensure_merchant_store` only creates once) or saves an initial snapshot when nothing was persisted yet, and both the buy (`Container`) and fast-sell (`FastSell`) command paths re-save the full store after a successful trade. Focused core tests cover prices, arg parsing, store creation, trade activation, buy/sell mutations, quest-item exclusion, busy/distance clearing, and greeting memory; server tests cover the snapshot<->store conversion helpers; db tests cover JSON round-tripping and (behind `DATABASE_URL`) a live save/load round trip against Postgres. `add_special_store`/`create_special_item` (`src/module/merchants/store.c:229-323`, `src/system/tool.c:2620-2789`) are now ported as `World::create_special_item`/`World::add_special_store`/`World::refresh_special_stores` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/special_item.rs`): the full 76-entry weighted `special_item[]` enchant table, the 21-entry base-item roll, the potion branch, `lowhi_random`, and `set_item_requirements_sub` are all transcribed from C, and a `special`-flagged merchant now seeds five special wares on first store creation and one more every 12 real-time hours, matching `merchant_driver`'s C timing exactly; `main.rs` persists the merchant's store whenever `refresh_special_stores` reports a change. Remaining gaps: `create_special_item` is not yet wired to chest/loot generation (`create.c:1102`'s `special_prob`/`special_str`/`special_base` template fields), day/night shop movement/door handling (so persistence currently keys off `character.x/y` at store-creation time rather than C's `tmpx/tmpy`), C's incremental per-item `merchant_tasks.c` task queue (Rust always does a full-store upsert instead), the periodic `save_all_merchants`/admin `#saveall` full-DB sweep, and exact global RNG parity. `merchant.c::analyse_text_driver`'s `qa[]` small-talk table is now ported behind a reusable, driver-agnostic matcher: `character_driver::{TextQaEntry, TextAnalysisOutcome, tokenize_text_words, analyse_text_qa}` tokenizes spoken text into lowercase words (C's `' ' ',' ':' '?' '!' '"' '.'` delimiter set, own-name filtering, 20-word cap, 250-byte-per-word bailout) and scans a qa table for an exact-length ordered match, returning `Said(text)` for entries with a canned `%s`-templated answer or `Matched(code)` for entries that only report an `answer_code` (C's `who are you`/`what's your name` style). `world::merchant::merchant_qa_reply` wires the `MERCHANT_QA` transcription of `merchant.c`'s table through it with the C guard clauses (`CF_PLAYER`, `char_dist <= 12`, `char_see_char`) and emits the reply via a new `merchant_quiet_say` helper using `settings.quietsay_dist` (C `quiet_say`'s `log_area(..., quietsay_dist, ...)` - the existing greet-on-sight message still uses `SAY_DIST`, which is a pre-existing mismatch left alone here). Remaining: `gwendylon.c`/`bank.c`/`base.c`/`military.c`/`forest.c`/`area3.c`/`arkhata.c`/`orb_bank_npc.c` each have their own `qa[]` tables and drivers that still need porting onto `analyse_text_qa`. `merchant.c::aclerk_driver` (`CDR_ACLERK = 4`, the Cameron arena clerk) is now ported in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/aclerk.rs`: typed `AclerkDriverData`/`parse_aclerk_driver_args` parsed at zone load, store creation shared with `ensure_merchant_store`/`refresh_special_stores` (generalized to accept either `CharacterDriverState::Merchant` or `::Aclerk`, since C's `create_store`/`add_special_store` calls are identical between the two drivers), a once-per-visible-player "Welcome to the Cameron Arena!" greeting within 5 tiles (memory slot 7, 12h clear timer, matching only the *first* of C's three copy-pasted `NT_CHAR` `quiet_say` blocks - the other two are unreachable dead code behind an unconditional `continue`), the hardcoded `abuser()` persistent-ID list reacting to `"<name> ... trade"` speech with a murmur/emote/murmur `RANDOM(3)` (checked against the raw runtime `CharacterId`, the same simplification already used for `TraderDriverData::c1_id`/`c2_id`), given-item vanishing, and an 11-line idle-murmur table including two lines with an embedded period in the C format string that doubles up with `emote()`'s own trailing period (`"...forest.."`, `"...himself up.."`) - copied exactly. Unlike `merchant_driver`, the arena clerk's trade-request handler never sets `ch[co].merchant = cn`, so saying "<clerk>, trade" never actually opens its store in C, and this port matches that. Remaining gaps: day/night shop movement/door handling (same gap as `CDR_MERCHANT`), and the separate `src/system/auction/*.c` + `database_merchant.c` auction-house subsystem the arena clerk driver itself never calls (the community client has no auction UI at all per `render.c`, so that slice may end up N/A once someone audits it directly). 13 focused tests in `world/tests/aclerk.rs`. Continuation (iteration 48): `src/system/auction/auction_db.c`'s full DB layer (create/update/get/delete/search/player-auctions/count-active auctions, plus delivery create/pending-list/claim/summary and expired-auction cleanup) is now ported as `AuctionRepository`/`PgAuctionRepository` in `crates/ugaris-db/src/auction.rs` with `migrations/0006_auction_house.sql`, storing the auctioned item as `jsonb` instead of C's raw-BLOB-plus-`SUBSTRING`-offset approach. Nothing calls this repository yet - `auction_house.c`'s business logic and the `/ah` command in `auction_cmd.c` are still unported, so this is DB-storage plumbing only. 9 new db tests (5 unit + 4 live, the live ones verified against a real ephemeral Postgres 16 container, not just compiled). |
| Area terrain startup loading from `ugaris_data/zones/<area>/*.map` | `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs` | Server startup resolves `UGARIS_ZONE_ROOT` or default `ugaris_data/zones` / `../ugaris_data/zones`, loads generic and area `.itm`/`.chr` templates best-effort, loads the first area `.map`, accepts signed legacy sprite IDs, tolerates missing item/character templates while preserving terrain, sanitizes `from/to` range copies so live item/character IDs and temporary item blockers are not duplicated across terrain ranges, reports load counts, keeps the loader alive for runtime template instantiation, and chooses an open spawn tile. Area 1 `above1.map` smoke-tested: 65,533 ground tiles, 16,969 blocked tiles, 1,780 item templates, 188 character templates, 2,236 placed items, and 446 placed characters. Process-level legacy login smoke confirmed map bootstrap payloads after loading real area data. Full `.pre` expansion/generator parity, complete template metadata, respawn/random loot, and all object driver side effects remain. |
| `src/module/bank.c`, `src/module/bank.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/bank.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `CDR_BANK = 22` now has a typed driver state (`BankDriverData`) parsed from C `bank_driver_parse` args at zone load. `World::process_bank_actions` ports the full `bank_driver` body: `NT_CHAR` greeting is a periodic nearby-player scan (same simplification `world/merchant.rs` already established for its own greeting, rather than reacting to `notify_area`'s `NT_CHAR` broadcasts), small talk via the shared `analyse_text_qa` matcher against a transcribed `BANK_QA` table (including the verbatim "I'm just a merchant" copy-paste quirk from `merchant.c`), `deposit`/`withdraw`/`balance` text commands (raw substring search matching C's `strcasestr`, including the "explain deposit" double-reply quirk), `NT_GIVE` cursor-item destruction, the 16-line idle-murmur table with `RANDOM(25)`/`RANDOM(16)` throttling, the 12h greet-memory-clear timer, and the day/night shop-position/door movement block (`is_closed`/`is_room_empty`/`opening_time` ported fresh here - no prior Rust equivalent existed; `move_driver`/`use_item_at` map onto the existing `World::setup_walk_toward`/`toggle_door`). The persistent `ppd->imperial_gold` balance (`DRD_BANK_PPD`, C `struct bank_ppd`) is ported as `PlayerRuntime::bank_gold` with `encode_legacy_bank_ppd`/`decode_legacy_bank_ppd`; since `World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime`, deposit/withdraw/balance requests that need the persistent balance are queued as `BankEvent`s (`pending_bank_events`/`drain_pending_bank_events`, matching the existing `pending_kill_exp`-style convention) and applied by `world_events.rs::apply_bank_events` from `main.rs`'s tick loop. Documented deviations: the full keyed-door `use_driver` dispatch (`item_driver::door_driver`'s key-requirement gate) is not replicated for the bank's own door (toggles directly, since no zone data is expected to key a bank door); the "account"/"explain deposit/withdraw/balance" qa answers drop their `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` color styling (the shared `&str`-based qa pipeline cannot carry the raw non-UTF8 legacy color marker byte) while keeping the wording byte-for-byte; `NT_GIVE` unconditionally destroys the received item rather than first trying `give_driver`'s give-it-back attempt (same simplification `world/merchant.rs` already made). 17 focused tests in `world/tests/bank.rs` plus 2 PPD round-trip tests in `player.rs`. |

`award_reskin_well_paid_gatherer_achievement`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs` (`lydia_driver_64`, `robber_driver_65`, `sanoa_driver_66`, `reskin_driver_67`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/cross_area.rs` (`GwendylonCrossAreaTransfer` consumer), `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `CDR_CAMHERMIT = 14` (the forest hermit's two-quest bear-kill/tooth-necklace chain, `QLOG_HERMIT_QUEST1`/`QLOG_HERMIT_QUEST2`) is fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 13-state dialogue machine, `NT_TEXT` "repeat" handling via the new `GWENDYLON_QA` table (shared by every `gwendylon.c` NPC, not camhermit-specific), `NT_GIVE` give-back through the new general `World::give_char_item_smart` primitive, and the teeth-quest reward (including `collect_teeth`'s documented destroy-without-reward quirk on a short final pass). Server-side `apply_camhermit_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs`) is this codebase's first live NPC-driven `questlog_open`/`_done`/`_reopen` call site, resolving exp through `QuestLog::complete_legacy`+`World::give_exp` and resending the legacy questlog packet, plus the teeth-reward gold's wealth-achievement tracking via the existing `award_swap_money_converted_achievement`. See `world::camhermit`'s module doc comment for the remaining open gap (one reminder line's color-marker styling dropped); `camhermit_kills` now does advance on a live server via the newly-ported `monster_dead` split (`World::apply_area1_monster_death_driver` for the noon/stone-circle weapon-glow half, `apply_area1_monster_death_from_hurt_event` in `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs` for the `PlayerRuntime`-backed kill-counter half, wired into `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`'s existing per-hurt-event dispatch). `CDR_YOAKIN = 9` (the hunter's bear-hunt quest at the knight castle, `QLOG_YOAKIN`, plus the shrike-talisman exp reward and generic leftover-give branches it also handles) is fully ported the same way: the `NT_CHAR` 6-state (0-5) dialogue machine including the 120-second intro-chain reset and the `logain_state`-gated state 2 -> 3 transition, `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table (reusing `analyse_text_qa`), and `NT_GIVE`'s three branches (bear-tooth turn-in with a first-completion-only gold reward via a new `World::destroy_items_by_template_id` port of C `destroy_item_byID`, the shrike-talisman exp reward with the fails/no-fails branch, and the generic give-back fallback through `give_char_item_smart`). See `world::yoakin`'s module doc comment for its two open gaps (the state-4 reminder's color-marker styling dropped, same reason as camhermit; `destroy_items_by_template_id` does not sweep the account depot, which lives outside `World`). `CDR_TERION = 11` (the village's ambient lore/storyteller NPC) is also fully ported: the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DIDSAY` cross-NPC talk-throttle pre-pass unique to this driver, the 14-state ambient dialogue chain gated on `gwendy_state`/`reskin_state` (including two silent state jumps that advance state without triggering dialogue/facing), `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat"/"restart" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 4-bucket state-reset ranges (and, unlike yoakin, no `current_victim` gate before the qa match - a genuine C-source asymmetry between the two drivers, preserved as-is), and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back. Terion is pure ambient dialogue with no quest log, item reward, or gold, so `world::terion`'s module doc comment records no outstanding gaps. `CDR_GWENDYLON = 8` (the main quest-giver mage at the knight castle - the highest-value area-1 NPC, first quest chain new players see) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 20-state (0-19) four-skull quest chain (`QLOG_GWENDY_FIRST_SKULL`/`_SECOND_SKULL`/`_THIRD_SKULL`/`_FOUL_MAGICIAN`) including all three `questlog_isdone`-gated skip-ahead jumps (a genuine C-source asymmetry preserved verbatim: skipping skull 1 jumps straight to the skull-2 "done" checkpoint, but skipping skulls 2/3 jump to the *next* tier's "wait" checkpoint instead of its own "done" one) and the final `GWENDYLON_STATE_DONE_BLESS` periodic-bless branch; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 5-bucket state-reset ranges; and `NT_GIVE`'s five branches (the four skull turn-ins, sharing a new `gwendylon_turn_in_skull` helper - acceptance line, `questlog_done`, `destroy_items_by_template_id` sweep of every quest-chain item still in the player's own inventory, state advance, held-item destroy, first-completion-only `MONEY_AREA1_SKULL1..4` gold reward - plus the `IID_CALIGARLETTER` teleport-letter hand-off and the generic give-back fallback). The `GWENDYLON_STATE_DONE_BLESS` branch reproduces a genuine C `return`-before-`remove_message` quirk verbatim: when `may_add_spell`+`do_bless` (`World::setup_bless_spell`) succeeds, C returns immediately without removing the triggering `NT_CHAR` message, so it gets reprocessed next tick (by which point the just-applied bless makes `may_add_spell` fail, falling through normally) - ported by restoring the triggering message (plus any not-yet-processed ones after it) back onto the NPC's own `driver_messages` and skipping the turn/idle-move tail entirely for that tick. `IID_CALIGARLETTER`'s `change_area(co, 36, 240, 10)` needed a new `GwendylonCrossAreaTransfer` pending-queue (mirroring `world::jail`/`world::macro_npc`'s existing pattern exactly) since `World` has no DB/session access; `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs::apply_gwendylon_cross_area_transfers` resolves it through the existing shared `attempt_cross_area_transfer` helper, falling back to Gwendylon audibly saying the C "rift in the space-time continuum" line (not a private message - C's own fallback here is a `quiet_say`, unlike every other cross-area call site's private "target area server is down" text) on failure. Nine new item IDs (`IID_AREA1_SKELKEY1`/`2`/`3`, `IID_AREA1_SKELSKULL`/`WOODKEY`/`WOODSKULL`/`MAGESKULL`/`WARLOCKSKULL`/`WARLOCKKEY`, `IID_CALIGARLETTER`, all `item_driver/ids.rs`). See `world::gwendylon`'s module doc comment for the one open gap shared with `world::yoakin` (`destroy_items_by_template_id` does not sweep the account depot). `CDR_GREETER = 13` (the tutorial-town Governor "Cameron" at the stronghold - class-aware weapon-choice/civics tutorial dialogue plus the "learn"/James-whimper hint) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 15-state (0-14) chain covering the `CF_WARRIOR`/`CF_MAGE` class-gated entry greeting (with a silent Seyan'Du fast-path straight to the terminal state for characters with both flags), the level-7-gated weapon tutorial (small blade -> two-handed/staff -> fists -> outro), the "learn"-prompt/James-whimpering hint (conditional on `james_state`), the level-7-gated rest-area/recall-scrolls/movement/look-ground civics tutorial, and the final `QLOG_LYDIA`-gated understand-prompt/reminder pair (states 12/13, each reading `questlog_isdone(co, QLOG_LYDIA)` exactly like C); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat"/"learn" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, with "learn" only rewinding from the state-7 "empty" checkpoint or state >= 13 (matching C's `current_victim`/`last_talk` reset-then-gate shape, the same as `world::yoakin`'s `NT_TEXT` handler rather than `world::terion`'s ungated one); and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back. Greeter never writes `QLOG_LYDIA`, only reads its completion flag. See `world::greeter`'s module doc comment for its one open gap (the `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` marker drop, same reason/precedent as every other ambient NPC in this file). `CDR_JESSICA = 125` (the robber-operations two-quest NPC, `QLOG_JESSICA_ROBBER_NOTE`/`QLOG_JESSICA_KILL`) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 13-state dialogue machine (entry gated on `QLOG_NOOK` being done, the five-line quest-1 intro, both `_DO` states' 60-second reminder gates, and the `_FINISH` states' auto-advance with `questlog_open`/`questlog_done`); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, resetting either quest's own `_GIVE_1` checkpoint; and `NT_GIVE`'s `IID_AREA1_ROBBER2NOTE` turn-in finishing quest 1. Jessica's own unwanted-item give-back is a genuine, confirmed C behavioral difference from every sibling NPC in this file: it calls plain `give_char_item` (no ground-drop fallback on a full inventory), not `give_char_item_smart` - ported via a new shared `World::give_char_item`, promoted out of what had been a private `trader.rs`-only duplicate (`trader_driver`'s own NT_GIVE early-exits use the identical plain semantics) so both call sites now share one implementation instead of two. The `QUEST2_DO` -> `QUEST2_FINISH` transition, previously blocked on the unported `bredel_dead` monster-death hook (`gwendylon.c:2825-2842`), is now wired via `apply_bredel_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`): a `CDR_BREDEL` kill by a `CF_PLAYER` with `jessica_state == JESSICA_STATE_QUEST2_DO` advances it to `_FINISH` and queues the exact C reward line, so jessica's kill-quest chain can now fully complete on a live server. `CDR_JIU = 127` (the forest sanctuary pilgrim's riverbeast-kill quest, `QLOG_JIU`) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 5-state (`JIU_STATE_ENTRY`/`_STORY1`/`_WAIT_FOR_KILL`/`_BEAST_KILLED`/`_DONE`) dialogue machine including the level-39-gated entry greeting split and `questlog_open`/`questlog_done`; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table (resetting to `JIU_STATE_ENTRY` below `_BEAST_KILLED`, or repeating the "no more requests" line at/after it); and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back. C's own `jiu_driver` has a redundant, unreachable second `NT_CHAR` throttle check (both branches gate on the identical `TICKS*10` threshold, unlike yoakin's genuinely distinct `TICKS*5`/`TICKS*10` pair) - ported as the single effective check rather than reproducing dead code. `riverbeast_dead` (the death hook that advances `_WAIT_FOR_KILL` -> `_BEAST_KILLED` when a player kills the riverbeast, itself just `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` under the hood per C's own `ch_driver` dispatch) is now wired via `apply_riverbeast_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`): a `CDR_RIVERBEAST` kill by a `CF_PLAYER` with `jiu_state == JIU_STATE_WAIT_FOR_KILL` advances it to `_BEAST_KILLED` and queues the exact C reward line, so this quest chain can also now fully complete on a live server. All three of these death hooks share the same dispatch shape as the pre-existing `apply_swamp_monster_death_from_hurt_event`/`World::apply_swamp_monster_death_driver` pair and are wired into the same `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` per-`LegacyHurtEvent` loop, rather than a new generic `ch_died_driver`-equivalent dispatch table (still not built - each species handler remains bespoke and manually invoked, same as every other death hook in this codebase). `CDR_FOREST_RANGER = 155` (`gwendylon.c:2284-2473`, "Vert" the bear-attack warning sentry near the forest stone circle) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 5-state (`ENTRY`/`WARNING_1`/`WARNING_2`/`HINT_1`/`GREET`) dialogue machine including a genuine, confirmed C-source asymmetry preserved rather than "fixed" - the `ENTRY` branch's hint-vs-warning fork gates on the *ranger's own* `ch[cn].level`, not the greeted player's, unique among every ambient NPC in this file - and the `GREET` steady-state's 60-second repeat-greeting window; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, resetting to `ENTRY` only from the `GREET` checkpoint; and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back. Also confirmed and documented a second genuine C dead-code quirk unique to this driver: the `NT_CHAR` branch's two consecutive throttle `if`s both test the identical `ticker < dat->last_talk + TICKS*10` condition (the second additionally checks `current_victim`, but can never be reached once the first has already passed) - ported as the single effective check. Uses a wider `char_dist(cn, co) > 15` greet range than every sibling ambient NPC's shared `> 10`. One documented, deliberate gap (see `world::forest_ranger`'s module doc comment): the idle body's `WN_LHAND` torch-relight upkeep (`gwendylon.c:2438-2451`) is not ported - a cosmetic light-radius detail around a single stationary `CF_IMMORTAL` NPC that would require threading the full `execute_item_driver_request`/`apply_item_driver_outcome` pipeline (built for player-initiated `use` requests) through a new NPC-idle call site nothing else in this codebase touches yet. `CDR_BRITHILDIE = 126` (`gwendylon.c:2474-2823`, the Governor's mother's ambient lore NPC unlocking `QLOG_BRITHILDIE`) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 22-state (0-21) story chain (four "wait" checkpoints gated on the *greeted player's* level - `< 9`/`9..20`/`20..37`/`>= 37` fork the entry branch, then `>= 20`/`>= 39`/`>= 43`/`>= 45` gate each subsequent wait state, each with its own 60-second "come back another day" reminder) through to `questlog_open(QLOG_BRITHILDIE)` and the terminal "no more tales" repeat; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat"/"repeat all" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, with its own 4-checkpoint reset ranges; and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back - like jessica's own fallback, a confirmed plain `World::give_char_item` call (no ground-drop fallback), not `give_char_item_smart`. Confirmed and preserved (not "fixed") a genuine C-source dead-code quirk unique to this driver: `BRITHILDIE_STATE_WAIT_STORY_3`'s high-level branch jumps directly to `STORY_4_1`, skipping `STORY_3_1`/`_3_2`/`_3_3` entirely - those three `case` labels are unreachable from anywhere in the driver (nor any `NT_TEXT` reset branch), true dead code in C itself. Also ported its `bigbadspider_dead` death hook (`gwendylon.c:2850-2870`, `CDR_BIGBADSPIDER = 153`, dispatched through `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` for combat like `CDR_RIVERBEAST`/`CDR_BREDEL`/`CDR_CAMERON_FORESTMONSTER`) as `apply_bigbadspider_death_from_hurt_event`: a `CDR_BIGBADSPIDER` kill by a `CF_PLAYER` with `brithildie_state == BRITHILDIE_STATE_NOMORETALES_QOPEN` runs a full `questlog_done` (exp reward + resend, unlike its bredel/riverbeast siblings which only flip a plain state flag) and advances to `_QDONE`, closing the loop so this quest can now fully complete on a live server too. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry lists the remaining ~8 unported NPC drivers in this file (the `monster_dead`/`bredel_dead`/`riverbeast_dead`/`bigbadspider_dead` quartet is now done; `gwendylon_dead`/`asturin_dead` remain, gated on their own still-unported NPC drivers). 9 tests in `world/tests/camhermit.rs`, 14 tests in `world/tests/yoakin.rs`, 13 tests in `world/tests/terion.rs`, 9 tests in `world/tests/gwendylon.rs`, 20 tests in `world/tests/greeter.rs`, 13 tests in `world/tests/jessica.rs`, 10 tests in `world/tests/jiu.rs`, 13 tests in `world/tests/forest_ranger.rs`, 17 tests in `world/tests/brithildie.rs`, 2 tests in `world/tests/hurt.rs` (`apply_area1_monster_death_driver`), 6 tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` (riverbeast/bredel/forest-monster/bigbadspider death-hook wiring). `CDR_NOOK = 15` (`gwendylon.c:3175-3449`, the identity-crisis judge/knight/jester NPC at the knight castle, `QLOG_NOOK`) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 17-state (0-16) chain (three silent identity-guess lines, the Gwendylon/Lydia door-direction hint, two `gwendy_state`-gated silent-until-ready checkpoints - one of which, state 5, also fires `questlog_open(QLOG_NOOK)` - the tower-hiding-place hint leading into the stolen-cap request, a silent waiting state 11, a `GWENDYLON_STATE_DONE_BLESS`-gated silent jump straight to the terminal state 16 with no dialogue at all, and state 13's standalone torches-hint repeat); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 3-bucket state-reset ranges (`<=4` -> 0, `5..=11` -> 5, `12..=16` -> 12); and `NT_GIVE`'s `IID_AREA1_JESTERCAP` turn-in (gated on `nook_state` in `5..=11`, `questlog_done(QLOG_NOOK)`, a `destroy_items_by_template_id` sweep of both the cap and `IID_AREA1_ROBBERKEY1`) plus the generic give-back fallback via plain `World::give_char_item` (confirmed C behavior, no ground-drop fallback, same asymmetry as jessica/brithildie's own give-backs). A genuine structural difference from every other area-1 NPC ported so far: `nook_driver` has no `realtime`/`seen_timer` reminder gate anywhere in its body at all (confirmed: no `nook_seen_timer` field exists in `area1_ppd`), so `NookPlayerFacts`/`NookOutcomeEvent` carry no `seen_timer`/`UpdateSeenTimer` - every state either advances unconditionally on sight or is a permanent silent no-op. Also ported the C source's own "Nook idle mutterings" identity-crisis table (12 lines, `RANDOM(25)` 1-in-25 gate once per minute) via the existing `World::npc_murmur`/`legacy_random_below_from_seed` pattern already established by `world::bank`/`world::merchant`. Two new item IDs (`IID_AREA1_JESTERCAP`/`IID_AREA1_ROBBERKEY1`, `item_driver/ids.rs`). Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`nook_player_facts`/`apply_nook_events`, wired into `tick_npc/area1.rs`'s `nook_driver_63`/`tick_npc/mod.rs`'s `run_all`) reusing the same `questlog_open`/`_done`+`World::give_exp` pattern every prior area-1 NPC established, minus achievement wiring since Nook's own quest carries no gold reward (`nook_driver`'s own turn-in line says so explicitly, matching `QuestMeta`'s `flags: 0`). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~8 unported character drivers (down from ~9; `nook_driver` is done). 13 tests in `world/tests/nook.rs` (entry greeting, the second/third-skull-done gates on states 4/5 both silent and passing, the silent state-11 wait, the silent gwendy-done-bless jump from state 12 to 16, the jestercap turn-in with quest completion, the outside-range normal give-back, all three text-repeat reset buckets, and current-victim text filtering). `CDR_LYDIA = 16` (`gwendylon.c:3458-3703`, Gwendylon's hungover daughter's hangover-potion quest chain, `QLOG_LYDIA`) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 8-state (0-7) chain (greeting-plus-`questlog_open(QLOG_LYDIA)`, the three-line ambush backstory, state 4's 60-second reminder gate with `notify_area(..., NTID_TUTORIAL, 0, co)`, the post-turn-in "here, this might save thy life"/Gwendylon-referral pair, and the terminal silent state 7), plus a genuine new pattern not seen in any prior area-1 NPC: a pre-`switch` auto-reset (`realtime - lydia_seen_timer > 120 && state > 0 && state < 4` resets to 0, modeled as a fact-based pre-adjustment before the state match); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, with C's own two independent (not else-if-chained) `if`s ported as-is since their ranges (`<=4`->1, `6..=7`->6) never overlap in practice;
