and `NT_GIVE`'s `IID_AREA1_WOODPOTION` turn-in (state `<= 4`, `questlog_done(QLOG_LYDIA)` plus `ACHIEVEMENT_A_HELPING_HAND` via the new `award_lydia_helping_hand_achievement`, a `destroy_items_by_template_id` sweep of the potion and `IID_AREA1_WOODKEY2`, state advance to 5) plus the generic give-back fallback via plain `World::give_char_item`. The class-conditional `mana_potion1`/`healing_potion1` reward (pure-mage check: `MAGE` set AND `WARRIOR` not set) is a genuine new architectural case: unlike every prior NPC reward in this file, C's `create_item`+`give_char_item` needs a real item template instantiation `World` cannot perform (no `ZoneLoader` access) - ported as a new `LydiaOutcomeEvent::GrantPotion { player_id, template }` (the class check itself runs in `World`, template resolution deferred to `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs::apply_lydia_events`, which is the first `apply_*_events` function in this file to take a `&mut ZoneLoader` parameter, mirroring `world_events::npc_events::apply_gate_welcome_events`'s own precedent; on give failure the freshly-instantiated `Item` is simply never registered into `world.items`, the same "nothing to destroy since never added" pattern `grant_clan_jewel` already established). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~7 unported character drivers (down from ~8; `lydia_driver` is done). 15 tests in `world/tests/lydia.rs` (entry greeting+quest-open, the state-4 reminder gate both ways, the 120-second state-reset-to-0 rule, the terminal silent state 7, the woodpotion turn-in with quest completion for both healing/mana reward branches, the outside-range normal give-back, both text-repeat reset buckets plus the no-op middle state, and current-victim text filtering). `CDR_ROBBER = 17` (`gwendylon.c:3771-3953`, the midnight-meeting forest patrol - no dialogue, pure movement/self-defense/torch upkeep) is also fully ported: the clock-gated wait at the guard post (`hour == 23 && minute > 45`) through the fixed nine-waypoint walk down a ladder, across, and back through a hole to the meeting point and home again (`secure_move_driver`/`setup_walk_toward` verbatim per state, matching C's `move_driver(..., 1)` mindist), the ladder/hole `use_driver` waypoints (`World::robber_use_waypoint_item`, identical shape to `World::janitor_use_light`), and the every-tick `WN_LHAND` torch upkeep (equip-if-missing via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template("torch", ...)`, relight-if-unlit via `World::execute_item_driver_request(IDR_TORCH, ...)`, matching C's synchronous `use_item` dispatch rather than `use_driver`'s queued-action one). Self-defense (`fight_driver_update`/`_attack_visible`/`_follow_invisible`) is ported as a single-victim model set from the `NT_GOTHIT` message every hurt character already receives unconditionally (`World::apply_legacy_hurt`), the same simplification `CDR_GATE_FIGHT` (`world/npc/gate_fight.rs`) already established, reusing the existing generic `World::attack_driver_direct`/`secure_move_driver` rather than building the full 10-slot `DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` engine; ported the C `NT_GOTHIT` handler's `group`-equality gate verbatim, noting (see `world::npc::area1::robber`'s module doc comment) that it is almost always inert in practice since neither this NPC nor a default player ever sets a non-zero `group` - matching C's own effectively-dead self-defense path for ungrouped characters, not a bug in this port. `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_ROBBER` case dispatches to the empty no-op `balltrap_skelly_dead`, so no death reward/hook was needed. New `CDR_ROBBER`/`CharacterDriverState::Robber(RobberDriverData)` added to `character_driver.rs` (three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, same as every other new NPC variant), driver state defaults lazily on first tick (no `zone.rs` wiring needed, following `world::lydia`'s own precedent rather than `world::brithildie`'s explicit-init one). Server-side `robber_driver_65` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs`) is the simplest area-1 registration yet: a single `world.process_robber_actions(&mut zone_loader, config.area_id)` call, no `ServerRuntime`/DB/achievement plumbing at all (mirroring `janitor_driver_52`'s shape, plus `zone_loader` for the torch template). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~6 unported character drivers (down from ~7; `robber_driver` is done). 9 tests in `world/tests/robber.rs` (guard-post walk-toward, clock-gate wait/pass, ladder use plus the ladder-missing state reset, torch equip-when-missing and relight-when-unlit, victim tracking from `NT_GOTHIT` plus adjacent-attack, and the group-equality self-defense gate's dead-in-practice behavior). `CDR_SANOA = 18` (`gwendylon.c:3961-4094`, a purely ambient dialogue-free city walker - twelve-waypoint post/library/south-loop route gated by five fixed clock-window departure times) is also fully ported, structurally identical to `robber_driver`'s own single-victim self-defense/spell/regen cascade (both call `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 0, 0)`, which only ever does anything on `NT_GOTHIT`) but with no torch upkeep and two `is_closed`/`use_item_at` door-toggle waypoints (states 3/10, the same door coordinate `(21,26)` both times) instead of robber's ladder/hole items - ported via new `sanoa_door_is_closed`/`sanoa_use_item_at` helpers, same shape as `World::bank_door_is_closed`/`bank_use_item_at` (`world/npc/bank.rs`). `fight_driver_set_dist`/`fight_driver_set_home` intentionally not ported, same precedent as `robber_driver`. `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_SANOA` case also dispatches to the empty no-op `balltrap_skelly_dead`, so no death hook was needed. New `CDR_SANOA`/`CharacterDriverState::Sanoa(SanoaDriverData)` added to `character_driver.rs` (three exhaustive-match update sites, same as every other new NPC variant). Server-side `sanoa_driver_66` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs`) is the simplest area-1 registration yet: a single `world.process_sanoa_actions(config.area_id)` call, no `zone_loader`/`ServerRuntime`/DB/achievement plumbing at all. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~5 unported character drivers (down from ~6; `sanoa_driver` is done). 8 tests in `world/tests/sanoa.rs` (guard-post walk-toward, clock-gate wait/leave both ways, already-closed-door state-advance-without-toggle, open-door toggle-and-stay-in-state, victim tracking from `NT_GOTHIT` plus adjacent-attack, and the group-equality self-defense gate's dead-in-practice behavior). `CDR_RESKIN = 20` (`gwendylon.c:4098-4417`, the tavern-keeper/alchemy-turn-in NPC in Cameron's hidden back room, `QLOG_RESKIN` = 17) is also fully ported: the `NT_CHAR` 10-state (0-9) dialogue chain gated on both `gwendy_state`/`terion_state` at entry (the alchemy-quest intro through the beer-shortage/ingredient-request lines, state 3's dual gate - a 600-second reminder line OR a silent `logain_state > 8` advance, `questlog_open(QLOG_RESKIN)` at state 4, the new-master/hidden-entrance hint lines, and the class-conditional (`CF_WARRIOR`-only vs. mage/hybrid) alchemy-recipe text at state 8); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 3-bucket state-reset ranges (`<=4`->0, `4..=7`->4, `8..=9`->8); a new `NT_NPC`/`NTID_TERION` branch replying to `terion_driver`'s own `TERION_STATE_RESKIN_BEER` broadcast ("No Terion, no beer..."); and `NT_GIVE`'s alchemy-ingredient turn-in (`IID_ALCHEMY_INGREDIENT`, ten level-gated "cannot pay at the moment" declines by ingredient `drdata[0]` type before the generic hand-back, an already-turned-in re-give, and the payment branch - `value * 5` gold plus `ACHIEVEMENT_WELL_PAID_GATHERER` once all 24 ingredient-type bits are collected, via the new `award_reskin_well_paid_gatherer_achievement`) plus the generic give-back fallback via `give_char_item_smart`. Confirmed and preserved (not "fixed") a genuine C-source bug unique to this driver: `case 7`'s `check_first_kill(co, 16)` branch (gated on a per-player "killed the Guild Master" fact, ported as `ReskinPlayerFacts::killed_guild_master`/`PlayerRuntime::has_first_kill(16)`) calls `quiet_say`+`questlog_done` but never sets `didsay`, unlike every other dialogue-producing case in this same `switch` - so `last_talk`/`current_victim`/the `NTID_DIDSAY` broadcast never update for that turn even though a line was said. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~4 unported character drivers (down from ~5; `reskin_driver` is done). 23 tests in `world/tests/reskin.rs` (both entry silent-gate branches, the greet-and-advance entry, both state-3 branches (reminder vs. silent advance), state 4's quest-open, state 7's silent-wait and its `didsay`-less quest completion, both state-8 class-conditional recipe branches, state 9's permanent no-op, both text-repeat reset buckets, the `NTID_TERION` reply, alchemy-ingredient payment plus its all-bits achievement trigger, the level-gate decline, the already-turned-in re-give, the non-alchemy give-back, and the cross-NPC `NTID_DIDSAY` throttle). `CDR_ASTURIN = 19` (`gwendylon.c:4421-4542`, the private-quarters guard NPC near the city gate) is also fully ported: unlike every other area-1 NPC ported so far, it combines a positional `NT_CHAR` greeting/warning state machine (gated on the *greeted player's* `x` coordinate against two fixed world-coordinate boundaries - `< 115` shouts "GUARDS!" plus a `notify_area(NTID_ASTURIN)` broadcast, `< 118` warns then forgives at states 4/5, otherwise a 3-state greet/re-greet/welcome-back chain - each with its own 10s/30s `realtime`-based state-reset windows) with a full self-defense/regen/spell-self/return-to-post cascade every tick (`fight_driver_update`/`_attack_visible`/`_follow_invisible`/`spell_self_driver`/`regenerate_driver`/`secure_move_driver`), which every prior area-1 NPC either omits entirely (peaceful dialogue-only NPCs) or replaces with a walking route (`world::robber`/`world::sanoa`); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling reuses the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table (`case 2` resets `asturin_state` to 0). Self-defense is the same single-victim `NT_GOTHIT`-tracking simplification `CDR_ROBBER`/`CDR_SANOA` already established, reusing `World::attack_driver_direct`/`secure_move_driver`. Also ported `asturin_dead` (`gwendylon.c:4535-4542`) as `apply_asturin_death_from_hurt_event`: unlike every other area-1 death hook, C's `set_data(co, DRD_AREA1_PPD, ...)` succeeds for *any* live killer (the generic per-character memory-slot allocator has no player-only restriction), so the "I'll remember that" `quiet_say` line fires regardless of who the killer is, while only the persistent `asturin_state = 4` write is player-only in this port (matching the observable difference, since an NPC killer's C-side shadow `ppd` write is discarded/never read again anyway). `struct asturin_driver_data`'s own `state` field is dead in C (never read/written anywhere in the driver body) and is not ported, same precedent as `world::terion`/`world::reskin`'s own dead fields. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists the remaining ~3 unported character drivers (down from ~4; `asturin_driver` is done). 11 tests in `world/tests/asturin.rs` (state-0 greet, the warning-zone warn/forgive pair, the guards-shout-plus-notify-area boundary, both 10s/30s seen-timer reset windows, the welcome-back line, the text-repeat reset, `NT_GOTHIT` victim tracking plus adjacent-attack, the group-equality self-defense gate, and the return-to-post movement). |
| `src/system/player_driver.c` `tutorial_ppd`/`tutorial()` (`:374-711`, P4 "Area 1"'s last remaining gap) | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tutorial.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/mod.rs` (`TutorialPpd`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs` (`WorldPlayerSpecial`/`queue_player_special`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tutorial.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/mod.rs` (`send_pending_world_player_specials`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_sync.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | The newbie in-window hint system - all 17 throttled hint branches ported in `World::process_tutorial_hints`/`process_tutorial_hint`/`tutorial_torch_hint`: the login-window welcome, the Lydia/thief quest-direction hints (`offset2dx`-derived compass text), the three-way dark-tile torch sub-system (light-an-equipped-torch, equip-an-inventory-torch, or create-and-grant one into the left hand or, if the right hand is two-handed, onto the cursor - reusing the `World::instantiate_item_template`-then-insert pattern `world::npc::area1::robber`'s own torch upkeep established), the area-1-only battle-prep pair (warrior Warcry vs. mage Bless/Shield, plus a documented one-time-only mage Lightning-Flash hint - see below), the shop/chest/cursor-item-held-too-long/skill-raise-available hints, and the generic "todays hint" tail (potion F-key column scan, shift/ctrl/left-click/chat/channel-chat/raise-strategy) gated on 3 idle minutes. Needed one new piece of `World`-to-client plumbing: `WorldPlayerSpecial`/`queue_player_special`/`drain_pending_player_specials` (a single-character `SV_SPECIAL` send, the missing sibling of the existing area-broadcast `WorldSoundSpecial`) for the two torch hints' UI-highlight packets (C `player_special(cn, 0, 5\|17, 0)`). Since `World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime` (same split as `world::gatekeeper`/`world::lydia`), the new `TutorialPlayerFacts`/`TutorialOutcome` pair follows that exact established bridge shape - `crates/ugaris-server/src/tutorial.rs`'s `tutorial_player_facts`/`apply_tutorial_outcomes` snapshot/apply the new `PlayerRuntime::tutorial: TutorialPpd` field (17 `_cnt`/`_last` counter pairs plus the shared `timer`/`citem_start` fields, a plain `#[serde(default)]` struct field per `AGENTS.md`'s persistence rule, no migration needed) - wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the pre-existing autobless/autopulse block. Two documented C-oracle quirks preserved rather than "fixed": (1) the mage-only Lightning-Flash hint (`battle2`) compares C's raw tick counter (`ticker`) against a wall-clock-epoch-seconds-stamped field (`ppd->battle2_last`), a unit-mismatch bug that in practice fires the hint at most once ever per character - reproduced directly via `battle2_cnt == 0` rather than replaying a mismatch this codebase's tick-relative clock model has no equivalent epoch for; (2) the "no torch anywhere, create one" sub-branches reset `torch_last`/`timer` unconditionally but only bump `torch_cnt` if the usual one-hour gate had separately elapsed - `apply_tutorial_outcomes` re-derives that one exception from the pre-update stamp still on `PlayerRuntime`. 19 tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/tutorial.rs`, 4 tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/tutorial.rs` (facts snapshot, counter-bump-plus-timer-reset, the torch counter exception both ways, citem_start applying without a fired hint). This closes the P4 "Area 1" task's last documented gap (`PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`). |

## Partial

| C File | Rust Location | Remaining Work |
|---|---|---|
| `src/system/database/database.h` | `crates/ugaris-db/src/lib.rs` | PostgreSQL pool, module boundary, and database handle ported. |
| `src/system/database/database_area.h`/`.c` (`area_alive`, `update_arealist`, `check_area`, `get_mirror`, `get_area`), `src/system/player.c` `player_to_server`/`change_area`'s area-lookup half | `crates/ugaris-db/src/area.rs`, `migrations/0001_core_accounts_characters.sql`, `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Iteration 220 (see the "Cross-area transfer" P3 task and this ledger's "Continuation Handoff" design plan for the full context): `AreaRepository`/`PgAreaRepository` (`mark_alive`/`mark_down`/`get_area` against the pre-existing `area_servers` table, C's `area_alive`/`update_arealist`/`check_area`+`get_mirror` folded into one repository call instead of a separate poll-then-cache step, since Postgres is this codebase's single shared source of truth rather than each area server's own in-memory `area[][]` cache) is now wired into `ugaris-server`: a new `--public-addr` CLI arg (falls back to `--bind-addr`) is registered via `mark_alive` right after the TCP listener confirms it's bound, and `mark_down` runs at clean shutdown (both `ctrl_c` and scheduled-shutdown exit paths share the same post-loop tail). `PacketBuilder::server_redirect` (`ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs`) encodes the 7-byte `SV_SERVER` packet C's `player_to_server` (`src/system/player.c:244-250`) sends - tag + raw little-endian 4-byte address + raw little-endian 2-byte port, no network-byte-order swap on either field, matching the existing `LoginBlock::his_ip` raw-bytes convention exactly (new `legacy_ipv4_addr` helper in `main.rs` converts a real `Ipv4Addr` to the same bit pattern). Remaining: the actual `get_mirror` mirror-fallback-search semantics (try the requested mirror, else the next one up) are not reproduced - `get_area` only ever looks up the exact `(area_id, mirror_id)` pair passed in, since nothing in this codebase generates a second mirror row yet; a periodic `mark_alive` re-poll/heartbeat (currently startup-once); and the `check_area`/`update_arealist`-style separate liveness cache the C server refreshes independently of `get_area` (not needed here, since every lookup already hits Postgres directly). Iteration 221 ported the first slice of the *mid-game* teleport half of C `change_area` (`database_character.c:343-364` + `kick_char`, `player.c:115-149`) that this row's iteration-220 note left as the next step: a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/cross_area.rs::attempt_cross_area_transfer` async helper does the real lookup -> save -> despawn -> redirect sequence (`AreaRepository::get_area`, a new `snapshots.rs::character_area_transfer_save_request` builder that saves via the existing `CharacterSaveMode::Logout` machinery with the *destination* area/mirror/coords instead of this server's own, `World::remove_character`, then the already-built `PacketBuilder::server_redirect` + session disconnect - reusing `login.rs::area_redirect_payload` for the "is the target actually live" check exactly like the login-side redirect does), wired into exactly one call site so far: transport-point travel (`TransportTravelResult::CrossArea` in `main.rs`). Since this codebase's `Character` has no separate `tmpx`/`tmpy`/`tmpa` fields the way C does, the destination coordinates are written directly into the saved snapshot's `x`/`y` fields, which the receiving server's existing `apply_character_snapshot` already spawns from - no new "arrival" packet or field pair needed. New tests: `tests/snapshots.rs`'s two `area_transfer_save_request_*` tests (pure, DB-free) and `tests/cross_area.rs`'s no-repository fallback test. The other 6 `CrossArea`/`area_id != config.area_id` call sites (clan-spawn exit, mine gateway, `/office`+`/goto`, `/jail`+`/unjail`, dungeon-master rescue) still only send the "down" message - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-221 REMAINING note for the full per-site plan. Iteration 222 wired two of those six: clan-spawn exit (`ItemDriverOutcome::ClanSpawnExit`, C `clanspawn_exit`, `src/area/30/clanmaster.c:1478-1490`) and mine gateway (`ItemDriverOutcome::MineGateway`, C's gateway-touch tail, `src/area/12/mine.c:1275-1303`) in `main.rs` now both call `attempt_cross_area_transfer` instead of unconditionally sending the "down" message, using `u32::from(config.mirror_id)` as the target mirror since C's `change_area` internally resolves the mirror via `ch[cn].mirror` (the character's own current mirror, never a different one) and this codebase's per-process `config.mirror_id` already represents every connected character's current mirror. Remaining 4 call sites unchanged: `/office`+`/goto`, `/jail`+`/unjail`, dungeon-master rescue - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-222 REMAINING note for the updated per-site plan. Iteration 223 wired two more: `/office` (`command.c:9670-9676`) and `/goto`/`/jump`'s shared tail `finish_goto_jump` (`command.c:8537-8567`/`8608-8625`), both in `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`. Since that module has no DB handle or `ServerRuntime` of its own (same reason `/kick`'s save and `/setclanjewels`'s clan-log write are deferred to `main.rs`), a new `KeyringCommandResult::cross_area_transfer: Option<(u16, u16, u16)>` field (`(target_area, target_x, target_y)`) carries the request out to a new `main.rs` handling block (right after the existing `clear_merchant_store_requested` block), which computes `target_mirror = result.mirror_changed.unwrap_or(u32::from(config.mirror_id))` - matching C's `change_area` reading `ch[cn].mirror`, which `/goto`/`/jump` already set to `m` beforehand via the pre-existing `mirror_changed` field when a valid mirror was given, else falling back to the caller's own current mirror (`/office` never touches mirror, so it always takes this fallback) - then calls `attempt_cross_area_transfer(...).await`, pushing the "Nothing happens" feedback only on failure. Updated the 4 existing unit tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` that previously asserted the eager "down" message to instead assert the new `cross_area_transfer` tuple. Remaining 3 call sites unchanged: `/jail`+`/unjail`, dungeon-master rescue - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-223 REMAINING note for the updated per-site plan. Iteration 224 wired `/jail`/`/unjail` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/jail.rs`, C `tool.c:4392-4425`'s tail): since `World::apply_jail_action` runs inside the *async* `resolve_jail_lookup` path with no `ServerRuntime`/DB handle of its own (unlike `/office`/`/goto`'s plain synchronous `fn`), it now pushes a new `JailCrossAreaTransfer` onto a new `World::pending_jail_cross_area_transfers` queue instead of sending the "down" message eagerly; a new `world_events.rs::apply_jail_cross_area_transfers` (called from `main.rs` right after `apply_jail_events`) drains it once per tick and calls the shared helper with `target_mirror = u32::from(config.mirror_id)`, same reasoning as the `ClanSpawnExit`/`MineGateway` sites. Updated 1 existing test, added 2 new ones. Only the dungeon-master eviction rescue (`world/dungeon_master.rs`) remains - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-224 REMAINING note. Iteration 225 wired that last remaining call site: the dungeon-master eviction rescue (`World::build_remove_tile`, C `build_remove` in `src/area/13/dungeon.c:743-786`'s tail, `change_area(cn, ch[cn].resta, ch[cn].restx, ch[cn].resty)` falling back to `exit_char(cn)`). A new `DungeonEvictionTransfer` + `World::pending_dungeon_eviction_transfers` queue (drained via `drain_pending_dungeon_eviction_transfers`) mirrors `JailCrossAreaTransfer`'s shape; a differing-area rest point now queues a transfer instead of calling `remove_character` immediately, while the same-area case is unchanged (an immediate `teleport_character_same_area`, still falling back to `remove_character` if that single teleport itself fails). A new `world_events.rs::apply_dungeon_eviction_transfers` (called from `main.rs` right after `apply_jail_cross_area_transfers`) drains the queue once per tick and calls the shared helper with `target_mirror = u32::from(config.mirror_id)` - but unlike every other call site, this one has no direct command caller to message on failure (C's own fallback here is a silent `exit_char(cn)`), so a failed hand-off calls `World::remove_character` directly instead of queuing a "down" message. Updated 1 existing `ugaris-core` test to assert the queued transfer instead of immediate removal; added 2 new `ugaris-server` tests for `apply_dungeon_eviction_transfers` (empty-queue no-op; missing-repository-pair falls back to `remove_character`, exercised via a real `build_remove_tile` call). Every originally-listed `area_id != config.area_id` call site is now wired; only a periodic `mark_alive` heartbeat and the three blocked commands (`/exterminate`/`/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow`, needing their own `server_chat`-equivalent design) remain open on this task - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-225 REMAINING note. Iteration 226 closed the heartbeat gap: C's `area_alive(0)` (`database_area.c:31-75`) re-runs every 85 seconds from `maintenance_60s_task` (`server.c:600`), not just once at startup (`server.c:586`); the previously startup-only inline `mark_alive` call in `main.rs` is now a shared `mark_area_alive` helper, invoked once at startup exactly as before plus from a new `world.tick.0 % (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 85) == 0` gate in the tick loop (same style as the existing once-a-minute auction-cleanup/clan-registry-save gates). No new tests: like every other periodic in-loop DB-maintenance gate already in `main.rs`, the tick-loop body has no extracted/testable function, and `mark_area_alive` takes a concrete `&PgAreaRepository` rather than the `AreaRepository` trait `attempt_cross_area_transfer`'s tests mock, so it can only be exercised against a live Postgres pool - same untested gap as the rest of the save+redirect path. Only the three blocked commands (`/exterminate`/`/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow`) and the live-Postgres test gap remain open on this task. Iteration 229 ported `/exterminate` in full (see the `database_admin.c`/`database_notes.c` ledger row for `db_exterminate`'s DB-side detail) plus `stats_online_time` (C `src/system/statistics.c:47-58`, a new `PlayerRuntime::stats_ppd` PPD wired into the once-a-minute tick-loop gate) and confirmed `/values`' only remaining infrastructure blocker is `paid_until` (bank_gold/PK/Hardcore/mirror-area-section-name all already have a reachable Rust equivalent). Iteration 230 closed that `paid_until` blocker: new `ugaris-db::CharacterRepository::find_paid_until_info(character_id)` joins `characters`/`accounts` by `account_id` (same shape as `BEGIN_LOGIN_SQL`, no new migration needed - `accounts.paid_until timestamptz` already exists) and a new `ugaris-core::world::values::compute_paid_till`/`paid_player_line` pair ports C `load_char_pwd`'s paid-account-expiration rounding arithmetic (`database_character.c:619-703`) and `show_values_bg`'s "Paying player: ..." format line (`tool.c:2903-2911`) digit-for-digit, deliberately skipping the `#ifdef STAFF` "staff accounts always get 24 hours" branch since `STAFF` is never `#define`d in the C tree's build config. 11 new tests. `/values` itself (`queue_values_command`/`values_lines`/`apply_values_events`/the `commands_admin.rs` dispatch arm) is still unwired - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration-230 REMAINING note for the precise remaining plumbing (full `V_MAX` skill-dump loop, threading `runtime: &mut ServerRuntime` into a new `apply_values_events` for `bank_gold`/`stats_online_time` reachability, the mirror-area line, and the `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF` dispatch gate). Iteration 231 wired the rest and closed the entire `/values` slice: new `ugaris-core::world::values::{ValuesRequest, World::queue_values_command, full_skill_name, values_lines}` port `look_values`/`look_values_bg` in full (`command.c:501-519`/`tool.c:2882-2939`) - a new full-43-entry `full_skill_name` table (letter-for-letter from `skill[V_MAX]`, deliberately separate from both `skill_display_name`'s abridged subset and `CHARACTER_VALUE_NAMES`'s divergent wording at several indices, e.g. "Warcry" not "War Cry", "Ancient Knowledge" not "Ancient Power") backs a new `V_MAX`-loop dump (15 lines, three columns each, falling back to C's own `"none"`/`0`/`0` placeholder for the final incomplete group), assembled alongside the header/desc/paid-till/PK-hardcore/playtime/gold/mirror-area lines and the reused offence/defence summary line (now with the `AV = base/20` integer-division shape `/values`' own summary line uses, distinct from `/showvalues`' `%.2f`-formatted armor line). New `ugaris-server::world_events.rs::apply_values_events` (modeled on `apply_punish_events`'s `runtime: &mut ServerRuntime` shape, the precedent the iteration-230 note called out) resolves the queued request via `find_login_target` + `find_paid_until_info`, reads `PlayerRuntime::stats_online_time`/`bank_gold`/`current_mirror_id` via `runtime.player_for_character` (falling back to `0`/`0`/this process's own `mirror_id` when the target has no live session), and delivers every line back to the *caller* (not the target - `/values` has no role swap, unlike `/showvalues`), wired into the tick loop right after `apply_showvalues_events`. New `commands_admin.rs` dispatch arm: `lower == "values"` (full-word only, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, modeled on `/look`/`/klog`'s exact-match gate rather than `/showvalues`'s open abbreviation gate), placed to mirror C's own adjacent `values`-then-`showvalues` dispatch order. 15 new tests (7 `ugaris-core` covering `queue_values_command`'s validation/queuing shape and `values_lines`'s header/paid/PK/hardcore/playtime/gold/mirror lines, the full 43-value dump's line count and "none" fallback, and the integer-division summary line; 2 `ugaris-server` no-op/missing-repository tests for `apply_values_events`, matching `showvalues_tests`'s two-test shape). Every previously-open piece of the "Cross-area transfer" task's three blocked commands (`/exterminate`/`/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow`) is now closed except `/allow` itself (the cross-server corpse-loot-access grant) - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Cross-area transfer" entry for the updated remaining scope. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2143 ugaris-core [+7] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1071 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Iteration 232 closed the last blocked command, `/allow` (`allow_body`/`allow_body_db`, `death.c:1013-1067`), by first designing and wiring the grave-loot-ownership ACL the iteration-228 note flagged as missing infrastructure: rather than adding three generic `owner`/`killer`/`access` fields to the crate-wide `Item` struct (which would have touched ~24 unrelated `Item` literal construction sites across the crate), the ACL triad is stored inline in a body-container item's own `driver_data` blob at bytes 12-23 (right after `create_dead_body_item`'s existing player-color bytes 2-8) via new `item_driver/helpers.rs` functions `set_grave_acl`/`grave_owner_id`/`grant_grave_access`/`grave_access_denied` - matching this codebase's established convention for driver-private per-item state (`drdata_u32`/`set_drdata_u32`). `world/death.rs`'s `fill_body_container` (which now also takes the already-computed `killer_id`) calls `set_grave_acl` right at body-container creation, reproducing C's `con[ct].owner = charID(cn); con[ct].killer = charID(co); con[ct].access = 0;` (`death.c:684-691`) for *every* lootable body (NPC corpses included, not just player graves - this is a real anti-kill-stealing mechanic: only the killer, not any other passerby, may loot an NPC's corpse until the owner - the victim, for their own past graves - grants third-party access via `/allow`). The actual access gate is wired into `item_driver/dispatch.rs::use_item`'s existing `item.content_id != 0` container-open branch (C's `use_item`, `do.c:1504-1508`): a foreign character now gets a new `UseItemError::AccessDenied` (the enum variant already existed, unused, evidently anticipating exactly this) instead of silently opening someone else's grave; both `ugaris-server` call sites (`inventory.rs::inventory_use_slot` and `main.rs`'s `item_use_requests` completed-action loop) now translate that into C's exact "Permission denied." reply (`do.c:1508`) instead of a silent no-op. `/allow` itself is a new `world/allow.rs` (`AllowRequest`/`World::queue_allow_command`/`drain_pending_allow_requests`/`grant_grave_access_to`) plus `ugaris-server::world_events.rs::apply_allow_events`, following the exact `/showvalues` queue-then-drain shape (`find_login_target` name resolution, "No player by that name." on a miss); a resolved target gets `allow_body`'s "Order scheduled." followed by `allow_body_db`'s "Area %d: Allowed access to %d corpses." once `grant_grave_access_to` (C `allow_body_db`, `death.c:1045-1067`) grants the resolved target access to every grave *this process's `World`* currently holds with `owner == caller` (never the caller's own kills - those are a separate, non-revocable `killer` slot `/allow` never touches, matching C exactly) - the same documented single-process-only caveat as every other cross-area command in this codebase. New `commands_admin.rs` dispatch arm: `"allow".starts_with(&lower)` with `lower.len() >= 3` (`cmdcmp(ptr, "allow", 3)`'s `minlen` 3, no permission gate), placed ahead of the pre-existing `/allowbless` handler in the same sequential dispatch chain so a bare "allow" resolves to this command first, exactly like C's own `command()` if-chain ordering (`/allow` at `command.c:8371`, `/allowbless` at `command.c:9597`). Deliberately not ported: C's `owner_not_seyan` ACL field (`container.h:26`) - it only feeds a secondary "may bypass the quest-item hold-shift restriction" nuance (`do.c:1396-1399`) this codebase's container swap path (`CL_CONTAINER`) does not implement at all yet, not the core access-grant/deny gate this iteration closes. 12 new tests (2 `item_driver` `use_item` gating tests including the "granting a new character overwrites the previous grant" case, 1 `world/death.rs` end-to-end kill test asserting the resulting body's ACL, 4 `world/allow.rs` queuing/validation/grant tests including the killed-but-not-owned distinction, 2 `world_events.rs` no-op/missing-repository tests, plus updated coverage from the existing `use_item`/container test suite). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2151 ugaris-core [+8] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Every one of the "Cross-area transfer" task's originally-listed blockers is now closed; only the long-standing live-Postgres save+redirect round-trip test gap remains open on this task (see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s entry, now closed to `- [x]`). |
| `src/system/database/database_character.h` / `database_character.c` login and snapshot paths, `src/system/player.c` `read_login`/`player_client_exit` reject path, `src/system/badip.c` | `crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/login.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/constants.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, `migrations/0001_core_accounts_characters.sql`, `migrations/0002_sessions_questlog_anticheat.sql`, `migrations/0003_character_snapshots.sql`, `migrations/0004_bad_passwords.sql` | Login status semantics, character target lookup, legacy plaintext subscriber-password comparison against `accounts.password_hash`, current-area update, login session insert, release semantics, guarded backup save, logout save, Rust character JSON snapshot load/save, character item snapshot rows, server-side optional snapshot load/logout-save integration for PostgreSQL, client-facing login rejection (every non-`Ready` `LoginOutcome`/DB error now sends the exact C `player_client_exit` `SV_EXIT` reject text and disconnects instead of spawning a scaffold character), IP-based bad-password rate limiting (`is_badpass_ip`/`add_badpass_ip`, `>3`/60s, `>8`/1h, `>25`/24h windows, backed by a new `bad_passwords` table) constructing `LoginOutcome::TooManyBadPasswords`, and same-account duplicate-online-character detection (`load_char_dup`) constructing `LoginOutcome::Duplicate` are scaffolded/ported. `clean_badpass_ips` confirmed dead code in C (declared, never called) and intentionally not ported. `begin_login_tx`'s full row-decision branching (unknown name, wrong password + bad-password recording, locked character/account, ip-locked, unfixed, not-paid, `allowed_area <= 0`, duplicate-login reject, `account_id == 1` duplicate exemption, `NewArea` routing, success `Ready` + `login_sessions` insert) now has a `DATABASE_URL`-gated live-Postgres test suite (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs::tests::live_login`, 12 tests, `tokio` dev-dependency added to `ugaris-db`) - skips cleanly with no `DATABASE_URL` set, verified against a throwaway local `postgres:16-alpine` Docker container. Live DB migration verification (actually running `migrations/*.sql` against a fresh Postgres, done manually this iteration but not automated), a true end-to-end reject test over a real TCP socket, and full legacy binary blob decode/encode remain. As of iteration 220, cross-area `NewArea` redirect (`player_to_server`) is ported: `main.rs`'s `SessionEvent::Login` handler now matches `LoginOutcome::NewArea` specially, calls the newly-wired `AreaRepository::get_area(area_id, mirror)`, and (via the new pure `login.rs::area_redirect_payload` helper, unit-tested without a database) sends the `SV_SERVER` redirect payload + disconnects when a live registered target exists, falling back to the pre-existing `login_reject_message`'s "target area server is down" text only when the target row is missing or marked offline - matching C `read_login`'s `if (get_area(...)) { player_to_server(...); } else { rescue_char(...); player_client_exit(...); }` branch structure exactly. See the `crates/ugaris-db/src/area.rs` ledger row above for the `AreaRepository` wiring/`SV_SERVER` encoder detail, and this ledger's "Continuation Handoff" design plan for what's still missing (mid-game teleport-triggered transfers, periodic liveness heartbeat). |
| `src/system/database/database_anticheat.h` / `database_anticheat.c` session/event core | `crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`, `migrations/0002_sessions_questlog_anticheat.sql`, `migrations/0014_ac_player_stats.sql`, `migrations/0015_ac_player_stats_warnings.sql`, `migrations/0016_ac_known_signatures.sql`, `migrations/0017_ac_player_stats_rollup.sql` | Session/event schema, typed PostgreSQL repository boundary, session create/character/fingerprint/status/bot-score/counter/end operations, event logging, cleanup (now called from `#accleanup`, iteration 199), and legacy result/action/risk text mappings ported with tests. Iteration 209 added a first, deliberately scoped-down slice of the player-stat table (`ac_player_stats`, keyed by `subscriber_id` == this codebase's pre-existing `characters.account_id` - no new subscriber-id concept needed, just `anticheat_sessions.account_id`, already stored there since iteration 196, read back via the new `account_id_for_session`): only `is_flagged`/`is_trusted` (the two columns `#acunflag`/`#actrust`/`#acuntrust` mutate, `set_flagged`/`set_trusted` upserts), not the full session-rollup-history schema (`total_sessions`, `lifetime_bot_score`, `risk_level`, fingerprint-on-login columns, etc.) C's real table also carries - that fuller schema is still unbuilt and blocks `#achistory`. Iteration 210 extended `ac_player_stats` with `warnings_issued`/`last_warning_at` and ported `#acwarn <player> [reason]` (`db_ac_issue_warning`, `database_anticheat.c:606-621`) on top - the new `issue_warning` repository method upserts the same row, incrementing the counter rather than overwriting it. The `ac_admin_actions` audit-log table `db_ac_log_admin_action` writes to on every mutation (including `db_ac_issue_warning`'s own call) is intentionally not ported (skipped, matching the `/kick`-`dlog` untracked-audit-log convention - documented on the new repository methods). Iteration 211 ported `#acsessions <player>` by reading directly from the existing `anticheat_sessions` table (new `recent_sessions` repository method) instead of porting C's separate `ac_sessions` table, since both are structurally the same per-login-session history - no rollup or new schema needed for this one, unlike `#achistory`/`#acviolations`. Iteration 212 ported `#acviolations <player>` the same reuse-over-new-schema way: C's `ac_violations` table joins a separate `ac_violation_types` lookup only because C stores the violation type as a numeric foreign key, while this codebase's pre-existing `anticheat_events` table already stores `event_type` as human-readable text, so the new `recent_violations` repository method just joins `anticheat_events` against `anticheat_sessions` for the account-id scope - no new violations table or violation-types lookup table needed. Iteration 213 added the new `ac_known_signatures` table (never itself defined in this codebase before, only referenced by name) and ported `#acsiglist`/`#acsigadd`/`#acsigdel` (`list_signatures`/`add_signature`/`delete_signature`) on top - the last three gap-(a) commands with no cross-account-join or rollup-schema dependency. Iteration 214 ported the session-end lifetime-rollup half of `ac_player_disconnect` itself (`db_ac_update_player_stats`, `database_anticheat.c:480-517`, called from `anticheat.c:141-163`) - the previously-missing piece that blocked `#achistory`: extended `ac_player_stats` (`migrations/0017_ac_player_stats_rollup.sql`) with `total_sessions`/`flagged_sessions`/`suspicious_sessions`/the three violation-total counters/`total_anomalies`/`lifetime_bot_score`/`max_session_bot_score`/`avg_session_bot_score`/`risk_level`/`last_seen`, added `AntiCheatRepository::update_player_stats` (a single Postgres upsert reproducing C's atomic MySQL `@variable` update via `GREATEST`/`CASE` over `excluded.*`), and wired it into `main.rs`'s disconnect handler right after `end_session`, using the same pre-mutation `find_session` snapshot `#acstatus` already reads. Every counter is currently always `0` (no detection engine ported, matching the session-lifecycle-only state since iteration 196), so real accumulation only currently applies to `total_sessions`/`last_seen`/a permanently-`low` `risk_level` - but the schema and upsert logic are real and tested against every `risk_level` tier. `#achistory` itself (the display command reading these columns) is still unported - this iteration only wired the write side. Iteration 216 closed gap (a) entirely by porting its last four members - `#acsharedip`/`#acsharedhw`/`#achighrisk`/`#aclookup` - adding `AntiCheatRepository::shared_ips`/`shared_hardware`/`high_risk_players`/`lookup_subscriber` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`). `shared_ips`/`shared_hardware` do reuse `anticheat_sessions`' existing `ip_address`/`hardware_hash` columns as iteration 214's note predicted, self-joining the table live rather than porting C's separate `ac_ip_history`/`ac_hardware_history` aggregate tables (no `db_ac_track_ip`/`db_ac_track_hardware`-equivalent writer exists or is needed). None of these four commands' C format strings' `email` field has a home in this codebase's schema (`accounts` has no email column) - `accounts.username` substitutes for it throughout, the same identity-column mapping this whole family already relies on for `subscriber.ID` == `accounts.id`. Runtime anti-cheat packet integration beyond session lifecycle (the actual detection engine), `ac_admin_actions`, and exact legacy MySQL report fan-out remain; no live `ugaris-db` fixture test yet covers the four new joined queries (a gap the next iteration touching this file should close). |
| `src/system/player.c` / `src/system/player.h` | `crates/ugaris-net`, `crates/ugaris-core`, `crates/ugaris-server` | Player states, `PAC_*`, command recognition, command payload parsing, login parse, runtime registry, session send channels, scaffold character spawn, direct action setters, action queue, and primitive world action bridge exist; full action execution, map cache/client sync, inventory delta sync, text logging, transfer, anti-cheat integration remain. As of iteration 38, the `ClientAction::Nop`/`ClientInfo`/`Log`/`ModPacket` dispatch audit is closed: `cl_nop`/`cl_clientinfo` (true C no-ops) get explicit non-logging match arms in `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`'s per-tick dispatch and `player_actions.rs::apply_player_action`'s immediate dispatch instead of falling through a catch-all; `cl_log` is ported as a `debug!`-logged `charlog`-shaped message via new helper `player_actions::format_client_log_message`; `ModPacket` (`cl_mod1`/`cl_mod3`) is a `debug!`-logged no-op matching C's own "acknowledge for now" handshake stub. |
| `src/system/tell.h` / `src/system/tell.c`, `/tell` slice from `src/system/command.c` / `src/system/chat/chat.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/tell.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | C-compatible ten-slot sent-tell tracking, duplicate suppression, first-empty insertion, received-tell removal, strict one-second timeout expiry, `DRD_TELL_DATA` ID, legacy not-listening feedback text, runtime `/tell <name> <text>` parsing, online character lookup, sender acknowledgement/self-tell feedback, staff-name/staff-code formatting, no-tell and ignore blocking with delayed not-listening feedback, recipient received-tell clearing, and spy fan-out are ported with focused tests. Offline cross-server tell delivery, lookup-in-progress retry semantics, dlog/audit logging, and exact chat-service transport remain. |
| `src/system/libload.c`, `src/system/drvlib.h`, module driver switch mapping | `DRIVER_PORTING_PLAN.md` | Static Rust driver registry architecture and prioritized character/item driver port order documented. Implementation remains. |
| `src/module/base.c` `ch_driver` / `ch_died_driver` / `ch_respawn_driver` dispatch boundary, `src/system/libload.h` `CDT_*`, `src/system/drvlib.h` base `CDR_*` IDs, `src/module/base.c` `trader_driver` / `janitor_driver` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/trader.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/janitor.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/drvlib.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs` | Legacy dispatch type constants, base `CDR_MACRO`/`CDR_TRADER`/`CDR_JANITOR` IDs, typed character-driver call/outcome shapes, C-compatible handled/unsupported return-code behavior for tick/death/respawn dispatch scaffolded. `CDR_TRADER` (the player-to-player trade middleman NPC) is now fully ported: `World::process_trader_actions` handles the "trade with <name>"/"stop trade"/"accept trade"/"show trade" text-command state machine with exact C string matching, `NT_GIVE` item collection capped at 10 per side with cross-partner notification, the three-minute timeout with item return, deal-swap semantics, a periodic-scan greeting (like `world/bank.rs`/`world/merchant.rs`, but also turning to face the greeted player via the new `drvlib::offset2dx`/`turn` combo since C's `talkdir` mechanic is part of this driver's observable behavior), the 12-line idle-murmur table, and the 12h driver-memory clear timer; a new `TraderEvent`/`pending_trader_events` queue (mirroring `BankEvent`) defers the "show trade" item dump and `NT_GIVE` cross-notify to `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs::apply_trader_events`, which needs `legacy_item_look_text` (lives in `ugaris-server`, not `ugaris-core`). `CDR_JANITOR` (the lamp-lighting/item-tidying NPC) is now also fully ported: `World::process_janitor_actions` toggles the nearest `IDR_TOYLIGHT` whose on/off state doesn't match the current day/night target, takes the nearest visible `IF_TAKE` junk item on the janitor's town half (the C `NT_ITEM` handler's `y == 192` divide filter) not already on one of the nine fixed home-area tiles, stashes held junk in the `item[30..INVENTORYSIZE]` deep-inventory range (C's own comment on `struct char.item[]`), and drops bagged junk off one at a time at the nine home tiles in C's exact candidate order, plus the idle-murmur table (rolled only right after a successful light-toggle, including the dynamic "N lights I turned on" counter case) - all recomputed directly from `World::items` every tick (`JanitorDriverData` keeps only the `cnt` murmur counter as real persistent state) instead of porting C's `NT_ITEM`-message item-ID cache, the same class of simplification already established for the merchant/bank/trader greeting scans; built entirely on existing `setup_walk_toward`/`setup_walk_toward_use_item`/`do_take`/`do_drop`/`do_use` primitives, no new pathfinding machinery was needed. Character message queues for drivers other than trader/janitor, and runtime invocation for the rest of the registry remain. Iteration 239 ported `CDR_MACRO`'s pure decision logic - the anti-macro/anti-bot "Macro Daemon" NPC's challenge generation/asking/checking (math/word/reverse, plus the C-dead-but-preserved multiple-choice branch), activity/AFK gate, area/section victim-search exclusions, history recording, and the correct-answer/failure `MacroPpd` mutations (karma/suspicion/failures/`nextcheck`, operating on a real `&mut MacroPpd`) and reward-type-roll classification - as a new standalone top-level module, `crates/ugaris-core/src/macro_daemon.rs` (22 tests), rather than a `world/macro_daemon.rs` `impl World` block, since none of it touches `World` at all: `PlayerRuntime::macro_ppd` (where this driver's state actually lives) is unreachable from `World`, the same constraint `world/military.rs`'s doc comment documents for its own PPD-backed driver, so this slice is the `PlayerRuntime`-decoupled "brain" a future `ugaris-server`-side `apply_macro_events`-shaped bridge (mirroring `apply_military_master_events`) will call once it also builds the `CharacterDriverState::Macro`/`struct macro_data` NPC-side state machine and `World::process_macro_actions` message loop/search/teleport driver that don't yet exist. See `macro_daemon.rs`'s own module doc comment and `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Macro-detection engine" task for the full remaining breakdown (live driver wiring, the cross-server challenge-room hand-off, reward execution via `grant_template_item_smart`, the `macro_track_*` activity-tracking call sites, and the cosmetic xmas reskin). Iteration 240 wired the `macro_track_*` activity-tracking call sites (`apply_macro_activity_events`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/macro_daemon.rs`). Iteration 241 built the `CharacterDriverState::Macro`/`struct macro_data` NPC-side state machine (`MacroDriverData`/`MacroDriverState`, `character_driver.rs`; the non-PPD half in `world/macro_npc.rs`) and the live driver loop itself (`apply_macro_events`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/macro_daemon.rs`) - `CDR_MACRO` NPCs now actually search for, teleport to, greet, challenge, reward, and time out victims each tick, including the `/summonmacro` forced-pickup and `isxmas` reskin; the cross-server challenge-room hand-off and pentagram save/restore remain unported (see this file's iteration-241 Progress Log entry for the full gap list). Iteration 242 closed the remaining gap: the cross-server "challenge room" teleport-and-restore flow. New pure helpers in `macro_daemon.rs` (`macro_should_banish_to_challenge_room`/`macro_is_pents_area`/`macro_begin_challenge_room_banishment`/`macro_save_pentagram_progress`) plus new `World` methods in `world/macro_npc.rs` (`macro_banish_to_challenge_room`/`macro_restore_original_respawn`/a new `MacroCrossAreaTransfer` queue mirroring `world/jail.rs`'s `JailCrossAreaTransfer`) back a fully live `MACRO_STATE_FOUND` suspicion/failure-triggered banishment (local teleport-and-fall-through when this area server *is* the challenge room, or a queued cross-area hand-off + immediate `Idle` otherwise, matching C's own early `return` before its "brought to a challenge room" message), an area-3-only `MACRO_STATE_IDLE` cross-server pickup scan for victims `change_area` already delivered there, a correct-answer return trip (restore original respawn, teleport back locally or via another `MacroCrossAreaTransfer`), and the "you remain in the challenge room" failure message (only for banished victims, matching C's asymmetry that an ordinary victim who steps out of sight mid-challenge gets no penalty at all). Pentagram progress is saved into `MacroPpd::saved_pent_*` from the already-existing `PlayerRuntime::pentagram_debug` when banishing from one of the four Pentagram areas - matching C's own save-without-restore gap exactly (C never reads those fields back anywhere in `base.c` either). The actual cross-area hand-off happens via a new `world_events.rs::apply_macro_cross_area_transfers` (wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_jail_cross_area_transfers`), reusing `attempt_cross_area_transfer`; like C's own `change_area` call sites here, a failed hand-off is not specially handled (C never checks the return value either). This closes the "Macro-detection engine" task (`[x]`). |
| `src/area/2/area2.c` `shrine_driver`, `src/system/drvlib.c` `add_bonus_spell` shrine use sites | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Area 2 zombie shrine `IDR_SHRINE` dispatch, cursor skull offering gates for bone/silver/gold skulls, legacy craving feedback, offering consumption, random item reward table for skull/torch/potion gifts, direct cursor gift placement, XP reward branches, temporary armor/weapon/HP/mana bonus spell installation for shrine rolls with C spell-slot/timer/modifier semantics, and legacy gift/experience/bonus feedback are ported with focused core/server tests. Exact legacy dlog/audit integration remains. |
| `src/area/2/area2.c` `superior_driver`/`moonie_driver`/`vampire_driver`/`vampire2_driver`/`vampire_dead_driver`/`vampire2_dead_driver` (P4 "Area 2") | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area2/superior.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area2/moonie.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area2/vampire.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area2/vampire2.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs` (`CDR_SUPERIOR = 26`/`CDR_MOONIE = 31`/`CDR_VAMPIRE = 34`/`CDR_VAMPIRE2 = 38` + 4 new `CharacterDriverState` variants, 3 exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs` (`CDR_SUPERIOR`'s `nr` arg parse at spawn), `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/ids.rs` (`IID_AREA2_SUN123`/`DAGGERWRONG`/`DAGGERRIGHT`/`SMALLSPIDER` added, `IID_AREA2_SUN1`/`SUN2`/`SUN3`/`SUN12`/`SUN13`/`SUN23` widened to `pub`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/items.rs` (`destroy_items_by_template_id` widened to `pub` for the death-hook cross-crate call), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area2.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs` (`apply_vampire_death_from_hurt_event`/`apply_vampire2_death_from_hurt_event`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Item drivers for this file were already done (`item_driver/area2.rs`); this closes the remaining four character drivers plus their two death hooks, all pure `World`-only (no `PlayerRuntime` facts/events split needed in the main tick pass, unlike every area-1 NPC). `CDR_SUPERIOR` (four named crypt guardians selected by the zone-file `nr` arg, `1`=Nazimah/`2`=Argatoth/`3`=Lorganoth/`4`=Markanoth) ports the unique "true name" stun mechanic (any overheard `say`/`shout` case-insensitively containing the guardian's own secret name stuns it `SM_STEALTH` for 60s) plus a low-HP/lifeshield `M_RUN`/`M_FIGHT` mode toggle; `fight_driver_flee`'s full 9-direction light-weighted pathing algorithm is simplified to fleeing directly away from the single tracked victim's position (documented deviation - see the module's own doc comment). `CDR_MOONIE` (the `moony_elite` spider-eating companion) ports the item-sighting/want-it/eat/munch-timer state machine plus the friend-bless mechanic, reusing the existing `World::simple_baddy_can_bless_friend`/`setup_bless_spell` primitives already built for `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`'s own friend-bless feature instead of re-deriving C's five bless conditions by hand. `CDR_VAMPIRE` (the Vampire Lord boss) ports the sun-amulet-sighting vulnerability window (`CF_NODEATH` toggle) and its low-HP fake-death mist-teleport escape while still immortal, plus the 120-second return-home-vs-roam-to-fixed-tile split; its `killed` driver-data field (C's `NT_CREATE` `dat->killed = ticker`) is seeded on this NPC's first-ever tick instead, since `ZoneLoader` has no `World` tick access at spawn time. `CDR_VAMPIRE2` (the Vampire Lord 2 boss) ports the two-ceremonial-dagger `NT_GOTHIT` special case: C's `kill_char(cn, co)` (bypassing `hurt`'s armor/HP math entirely) has no direct Rust primitive, so this port instead calls `World::apply_legacy_hurt` with `armor_percent = 0` and damage far beyond any realistic HP pool, reaching the identical `kill_character_followup` death bookkeeping (respawn/kill-score/achievements/first-kill/military-mission-check) through the normal death path - documented as a deliberate, behaviorally-equivalent substitution, not a shortcut. Every one of the four NPCs' self-defense/aggro cascade is the same single-victim simplification already established by `world::asturin`/`world::balltrap`, extended to also track the victim from `NT_CHAR` sightings (not just `NT_GOTHIT`) since this file's own `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 1, ...)` calls pass `aggressive=1`, unlike the area-1 self-defense-only NPCs. The two death hooks (`vampire_dead_driver`/`vampire2_dead_driver`) complete quests 18 ("The Toughest Monster")/19 ("The Toughestest Monster") through the existing `QuestLog::complete_legacy`+`World::give_exp`+questlog-resend pattern, gated on the killer's `PlayerRuntime::area3_crypt_state()` sitting in the exact `8..=9`/`12..=14` windows C checks (the crypt puzzle's own state machine, `src/area/3/area3.c`, remains unported - P4 area3 - so these two hooks are the only crypt-state consumers wired today); `vampire_dead_driver` additionally sweeps every sun-amulet piece (`IID_AREA2_SUN1..SUN123`) from the killer's inventory via `destroy_items_by_template_id`, widened from `pub(crate)` to `pub` since this is its first cross-crate (`ugaris-server`) caller. `dlog` debug-audit calls throughout this file are not ported, same precedent as every other `dlog` call site in this codebase. 19 new tests across `world/tests/superior.rs`/`moonie.rs`/`vampire.rs`/`vampire2.rs`. `cargo fmt --all` clean, `cargo test --workspace` (2553 core [+19] + 1101 server, all green), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. |
| `src/area/22/lab4.c` `lab4_seyan_driver`/`lab4_gnalb_driver`/`lab4_gnalb_driver_init`/`lab4_item` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab4_seyan.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab4_gnalb.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area22_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/{ids,types,dispatch}.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/{mod,labs}.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_fight.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_idle.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_completion.rs` | New `CDR_LAB4SEYAN = 192` (the "Observer" Seyan'Du quest giver): fully ported end-to-end, following the `world::gatekeeper`/`lab2_herald` `PlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent` split. C's `struct lab4_player_data { seyan4state; seyan4got; }` (`DRD_LAB4_PLAYER`, a distinct `set_data` slot from `DRD_LAB_PPD`/`lab_ppd`, and notably lacking C's `PERSISTENT_PLAYER_DATA` bit - i.e. non-persistent in C) becomes two plain `#[serde(default)]` `PlayerRuntime::lab4_seyan_state`/`lab4_seyan_got` fields per `AGENTS.md` (a documented, low-stakes deviation: this port persists the quest progress across relogin where C would reset it). `set_seyan_state`'s bit-to-state recompute is a pure `player::lab4_seyan_state_from_got` helper (`player/labs.rs`), reused both by the `NT_GIVE` crown/szepter bit-set path and the `NT_TEXT` "REPEAT" recompute. C's `static struct lab4_seyan_data datbuf` (a function-local static shared across every call - i.e. NOT per-`set_data`-NPC state) becomes a normal per-character `Lab4SeyanDriverData`, safe since exactly one Seyan is ever spawned (`zones/22/lab4.chr`). The full 4-branch/13-state dialogue ladder (intro chain 0-5, crown-only 10-11, szepter-only 20-21, both-received 30-33 ending in `queue_lab_exit_spawn(player_id, 10)`) is ported case-for-case. New `CDR_LAB4GNALB = 193` (the patrol-guard/crazy-gnalb triad, `type=1`/`type=3` in the shipped zone data - `type=2` is C's own dead `xlog("CANTBETRUE"...)` branch and `type=4`/`5`, carried by the king/mage quest-item holders, actually spawn as plain `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` in the real zone data, confirmed by grep, so this driver's own `type=4`/`5` movement dispatch is ported for structural completeness but never exercised). `type=1`'s single-victim self-defense (same narrowing precedent as `lab1_gnome`/`lab3_passguard`) plus a genuinely new mechanism for this codebase: a 64-entry branching path-graph patrol (`GNALB_PATH`, transcribed digit-for-digit from C's `gnalb_path[]` table including its two dead `{0,0,{0,0,0,0}}` sentinel entries at index 0/46), walked via a new `lab4_gnalb_swap_move` (the same two-`setup_walk_toward` `ignore_characters` fallback pattern already established by `palace_guard_move`, substituting for `swap_move_driver`) and a `RANDOM(4)`-retry-until-valid-branch picker matching C's own loop condition exactly (never re-picks `lastpath` at a real fork, but may re-traverse a dead-end's sole neighbor). `type=3`'s idle chatter (5 whisper lines + a `do_use(DX_RIGHT)` fireplace poke, reusing the `balltrap_fire_left`-established "use whatever's on the adjacent tile" pattern) is also ported. `fight_driver_set_dist`/`fight_driver_set_home` are not ported, same precedent as every other single-victim NPC in this codebase (never observably different with the narrowed single-victim model). `IDR_LAB4_ITEM`'s `drdata[0]==1` fireplace-key branch (`lab4_item`, the file's only item driver) is also now ported: new `Lab4FireplaceKeyGive`/`Lab4FireplaceKeyBlocked` outcomes, the give path reusing `grant_template_item_to_cursor("lab4_mage_key")`; C's blocked path (`if (ch[cn].citem) return;`) is a genuinely silent no-op in C (no `log_char` call), reproduced with zero feedback text. New `IID_LAB4_MAGEKEY`/`_SZEPTER`/`_CROWN` (`DEV_ID_MR`, `0x8`/`0xB`/`0xC`). 26 new tests (20 `world::tests::lab4_seyan`/`lab4_gnalb`, 4 `item_driver::tests::area22_lab`, 2 `player::tests::labs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3325 core [+26] + 1162 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on both area 1 and area 22 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of clean ticks (`processed lab-4 gnalb actions` visible on 3 live patrol/crazy-gnalb NPCs) and no panic. This closes `lab4.c`; only lab5 remains for Area 22. |
| `src/area/22/lab5.c` `lab5_seyan_driver`/`lab5_daemon_driver`/`lab5_daemon_driver_parse` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab5_seyan.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab5_daemon.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/{mod,labs}.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/ids.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_fight.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_idle.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area22.rs` | New `CDR_LAB5SEYAN = 191` ("Laros", the three-demon-head quest giver): fully ported end-to-end, following the exact `CDR_LAB4SEYAN` `PlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent` split precedent. C's `struct lab5_player_data`'s `seyanstate`/`seyangot` fields (`DRD_LAB5_PLAYER`, non-persistent in C, same `PERSISTENT_PLAYER_DATA`-bit gap as `DRD_LAB4_PLAYER`) become `PlayerRuntime::lab5_seyan_state`/`lab5_seyan_got` (intentionally persistent per `AGENTS.md`). `set_seyan_state`'s bit-to-state recompute is a new pure `player::lab5_seyan_state_from_got` helper (3-bit "all three heads" gate, unlike lab4's 2-bit crown/szepter scheme). C's function-local `static struct lab5_talk_data datbuf` becomes a normal per-character `Lab5SeyanDriverData` (safe: exactly one Laros is ever spawned, `zones/22/lab5.chr`). The full dialogue ladder (intro chain 0-5 including the `has_potion` deposit-your-potions gate at state 3, partial-heads 10-11, all-heads-received 20-23 ending in `queue_lab_exit_spawn(player_id, 15)`) is ported case-for-case; `has_potion` (scanning the seen player's own backpack slots 30+ and cursor for any `IDR_POTION` item) is pure `World` logic needing no `PlayerRuntime`. The three trophy heads (`IID_LAB5_HEAD1`/`_2`/`_3`, new `DEV_ID_MR` `0xD`/`0xE`/`0xF`) are ordinary `item="lab5_headN"` equipment on the already-zone-spawned `lab5_one_master`/`lab5_two_master`/`lab5_three_master` templates - they drop via the existing generic on-death item-drop mechanic, no scripted C reward call exists to port. New `CDR_LAB5DAEMON = 189` (the shared servant/master/gunned demon fight driver, `type=0`/`1`/`2` selected per zone-file `arg=`): fully ported, narrowing C's generic fight-driver enemy list to a single tracked victim (same precedent as `lab1_gnome`/`golemkeyholder`). The master's (`type=1`) `CF_IMMORTAL` toggle - immortal unless a currently-visible player wields the new `IID_LAB5_WEAPON` (`0x10`) in `WN_RHAND` - is ported digit-for-digit including C's `imm=-1`-sentinel "no visible player this tick means no flag change" semantics. The gunned demon's (`type=2`) unconditional `fight_driver_add_enemy` for any visible player north of `namecoordy[0]+25` is also ported, using C's own static initializer default for `namecoordy[0]` (`33`) since neither `lab5_mage_driver` nor `IDR_LAB5_ITEM`'s nameplate branch (the only two things that ever write it) are ported yet - documented as a gap to revisit once those land. `lab5_daemon_driver_parse`'s `type=` arg parse runs at spawn time (`ZoneLoader` has no ticker); the ticker-dependent `attackstart` tail runs from the real `NT_CREATE` message on the character's first live tick instead (same "map/tick-dependent remainder deferred to first tick" precedent as `CDR_LABGNOMEDRIVER`). Together these two drivers close a fully playable "kill 3 master demons (each needing the sacred weapon to even damage), turn in the heads, get the lab exit" loop on their own, independent of the still-unported mage/ritual system. Still unported: `CDR_LAB5MAGE` (the ritual/lore dialogue NPC, `lab5_mage_driver`), `IDR_LAB5_ITEM` (13 `drdata[0]` object types: obelisk/fireface/chestbox/combopotion/nameplate/realnameplate/entrance/backdoor/gun/pike/no-potion-door/manapotion/lightface), and the dynamic `ritual_start`/`ritual_create_char` room-spawning system those two drive together (finds one of 4 fixed candidate rooms, clears it, spawns 3 extra demons, teleports the player in). 19 new tests (`world::tests::lab5_seyan`/`lab5_daemon`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3344 core [+19] + 1162 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on area 22 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with `processed lab-5 daemon actions lab5_daemon_acted=12` (all 12 zone-spawned demons: 4 per each of the 3 demon rooms) on every tick for 30+ ticks, no panic. New `CDR_LAB5MAGE = 190` ("Mathor", `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab5_mage.rs`) is now also ported end-to-end, following the same `PlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent` split: the full intro/force/demon/ritual-explanation dialogue ladder (states 0-4/10-12/20-22/30-37), the `REPEAT`/`FORCE`/`DEMON`/`RITUAL` `NT_TEXT` keyword jumps (C's own `else if (strcasestr(str, "DEMONS"))` branch is unreachable dead code - the preceding "DEMON" check already matches any string containing "DEMONS" - documented, not ported), the god-only `SET 1/2/3` ritual-state debug command, and the "inside the name square, wants to shout/SET-call" ritual-invocation check are all ported case-for-case. `ritual_hurt` (pulseback effect + `COL_LIGHT_RED` log + 5-`POWERSCALE` self-damage, reusing the already-ported `create_show_effect`/`apply_legacy_hurt`) is pure `World` logic. The dynamic `ritual_start`/`ritual_create_char` room-spawning system (`lab5.c:170-243`) is also now fully wired: `World::attempt_ritual_start` does C's free-room scan (deliberately correctness-fixed rather than reproducing an obscure off-by-one in C's own nested-loop/sentinel idiom - documented in the module doc comment), clears non-player occupants, and places the 4 statue tiles, all pure `World` work; the character-instantiation half (`ritual_create_char`, needing `ZoneLoader`) is new `crates/ugaris-server/src/lab5_ritual.rs`, dispatched via a new `Lab5MageOutcomeEvent::AttemptRitualStart` from `crates/ugaris-server/src/area22.rs`/`tick_npc/area22.rs`'s new `lab5mage_driver_117` pass; `World::finish_ritual_start` does C's own spawn-before-teleport-check tail (`teleport_char_driver` first, success/fail messaging second, preserving C's real quirk that a failed teleport still leaves the just-spawned demons behind). New `PlayerRuntime::lab5_mage_state`/`lab5_ritual_daemon`/`lab5_ritual_state` fields (`magegot` is genuinely dead C code - never read or written anywhere in `lab5.c` - not ported) and a new `World::lab5_namecoords: [Option<(i32,i32)>; 4]` dynamic-override array (`None` = C's static initializer default), written by the mage's own `NT_CREATE` (index 0) and read by both this file and `lab5_daemon.rs`'s gunned-demon aggro-line check (closing that file's own previously-documented gap; its `NT_CREATE` handler was also generalized from a flat `attackstart = ticker` to C's real `attackstart += ticker`, needed so `ritual_create_char`'s pre-set relative `attackstart` survives the conversion to an absolute deadline). 14 new tests (`world::tests::lab5_mage`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3358 core [+14] + 1162 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on area 22 and area 1 both confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of clean ticks, no panic. `IDR_LAB5_ITEM` is now also ported (`crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area22_lab.rs::lab5_item_driver` + `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/types.rs`'s 15 new `Lab5*` outcome variants + `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs`'s resolvers) for 11 of its 13 `drdata[0]` flavors: obelisk (full heal + sound), combopotion/manapotion (heal + `log_area` drink broadcast + self-destroy, reusing `potion_driver`'s own "mutate `&mut Character` directly" precedent), chestbox (open/give-reward/close-timer, `check_chestbox`'s per-player opened-bitset ported as a new `PlayerRuntime::lab5_chestbox_opened: Vec<u32>` keyed by `ItemId` instead of C's lazily-assigned sequential `drdata[2]` index - documented deviation, see that field's own doc comment - since this port has no boot-time "scan the map and number every chestbox" pass and `drdata[2]` is write-only in C anyway), nameplate/realnameplate/entrance (the ritual-progress state machine - `ritualstate`/`ritualdaemon` read/write moved to new `ItemDriverContext::lab5_ritual_daemon`/`_state` facts, same `lab3_guard_talkstep` precedent; C's `ritual_hurt` gained a new `pub World::apply_lab5_ritual_hurt_at(player_id, x, y, daemon)` sibling taking an explicit position instead of always resolving via `namecoordx/y[ritualdaemon]`, since the item driver's three failure call sites each use a different position source than the NT_TEXT call site's own `lab5_ritual_hurt` does), backdoor (the 5-way `teleport_char_driver` fallback chain against `namecoordx/y`, including C's own mismatched-index oddity at the first attempt, reproduced digit-for-digit), gun (fire reusing the existing `FireballMachineProjectile` outcome directly instead of a new variant, `GUNRELOAD = 2*TICKS/3` reload timer), pike (always-hurt + one-shot arm/reschedule), and the no-potion-door (a new `ItemDriverContext::has_potion` fact, computed server-side the same way `lab5_seyan_has_potion` already does). This closes the checkbox's own "ritual only reachable via the god-only `SET` command" gap - nameplate -> realnameplate -> entrance is now a real in-game path. `World::lab5_namecoord` and the `lab5_mage.rs` `DAEMONNAME`/`DAEMONREAL` tables were promoted from `pub(crate)` to `pub`/wrapped in new free functions (`lab5_daemon_name`/`lab5_daemon_real_name`) for `ugaris-server` reuse. 23 new tests (17 core `item_driver::tests::area22_lab`, 6 server `tests::lab`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3375 core [+17] + 1168 server [+6], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on area 22 confirmed hundreds of clean ticks, no panic. Iteration 220 (a later session) closed the last remaining gap: `fireface`/`lightface` (`drdata[0]==2`/`13`) are now also ported (`lab5_item_driver`'s new `character.id.0==0` match arms + a new `lab5_face_direction` sprite-to-offset helper, reusing the existing `FireballMachineProjectile`/`BallTrapProjectile` outcomes exactly like the pre-existing `drdata[0]==9` gun branch already does). Getting their very first `call_item` to fire needed a narrow, low-risk slice of the "nothing primes an always-on ambient item driver's first timer call" cross-cutting gap - rather than the large, risky, ~45-call-site generic `ZoneLoader`/`World::add_item` refactor that gap would need in general (deliberately out of scope, see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Continuation Handoff" notes), both flavors are *only* ever placed as static `.itm` zone data (confirmed via `ugaris_data/zones/22/lab5.itm`/`.map`, never runtime-`create_item`'d), so extending the existing `World::schedule_existing_light_timers` per-area-load batch-prime sweep (`world/light.rs`) with an `IDR_LAB5_ITEM if drdata[0] in {2,13}` arm was sufficient - the same mechanism 11 other always-on ambient drivers already rely on. `BallTrapProjectile` gained a `schedule_after_ticks: Option<u64>` field (mirroring `FireballMachineProjectile`'s existing shape) so lightface's 3-way variable reschedule ladder (arm-once / every-9-seconds-after-4-quick-cycles / every-1.75-seconds) can drive it; its one pre-existing caller (`balltrap_driver`, a player-*touch*-triggered one-shot trap) passes `None`, unaffected. Verified against real zone data at boot: `scheduled_light_timers` went from a reproducible `0` (confirmed via `git stash`) to `29` on `--area-id 22`, exactly matching `grep -c` of the 16+4 fireface + 2+2+2+3 lightface placements across `zones/22/lab5.map`. This closes Area 22 for real - `PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`. 6 new tests (5 `item_driver::tests::area22_lab` + 1 `world::tests::light`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3381 core [+6] + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on area 22 confirmed hundreds of clean ticks, no panic. |
| `src/area/22/lab3.c` `lab3_plant` carried yellow/brown berry use paths, `lab3_passguard_driver`, `lab3_prisoner_driver`, `lab3_special` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area22_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab3_passguard.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab3_prisoner.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/labs.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/misc.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/ids.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area_apply.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/item_apply.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area22.rs` | `IDR_LAB3_PLANT` dispatch is ported for carried yellow berries and brown berries: yellow berries decode legacy freshness/count oxygen durations, remove existing oxygen spell items before installing the refreshed `IDR_OXYGEN` timed spell, consume only on successful install, and brown berries install the 10-second `IDR_UWTALK` timed spell with duplicate-active blocking and legacy failure feedback. Whiteberry light emission, plant growth/picking/rot timers, area log fan-out, and exact dlog/audit integration remain. `CDR_LAB3PASSGUARD = 194` (the password-gate guard, `lab3_passguard_driver`) and `CDR_LAB3PRISONER = 195` (the mute note-giving prisoner, `lab3_prisoner_driver`) are now both fully ported. New `PlayerRuntime::legacy_lab3_password1`/`_password2`/`_full_password`/`_guard_talkstep`/`_prisoner_talkstep` accessors (offsets 80/88/96/97 of `lab_ppd`, verified against the real 32-bit C server's `struct lab_ppd` layout - the existing `LEGACY_LAB2_HERALD_TALKSTEP_OFFSET`-family constants already used the correct 32-bit offsets, confirming this project's established convention). The guard's process-wide `static int talk` quirk (the *first* guard ever created server-wide latches `dat->talk` forever; any later respawn's fresh driver data never gets it set, so a respawned guard is permanently mute) is modeled as a new in-memory-only `World::lab3_passguard_talk_latched` field, matching C's own process-lifetime static (resets on server restart). The guard's fight/pursuit cascade narrows C's generic `fight_driver_*` enemy-list machinery to a single tracked `co`/`serial`/`attacking`/`pursuing` pair (same precedent as `lab2_deamon`), including a direct port of `fight_driver_update`'s `stop_dist=12` give-up-if-too-far-from-home trim via a new local `dist_from_home` helper. The prisoner's `give_driver` walk-then-give loop is simplified to a direct pathfind-and-walk-one-step-then-`World::give_char_item` call once adjacent (no queued `AC_GIVE` action/duration timer - the prisoner has no competing action that timer could interrupt). New `Lab3PrisonerOutcomeEvent::CreateNoteOnCursor` needs `ZoneLoader` (instantiating `"lab3_note_generic"` onto the prisoner's own cursor, `drdata[1]=21`), applied via a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area_apply.rs::create_lab3_note_on_cursor` (mirrors the pre-existing `grant_salt_to_cursor` shape). New `IID_LAB3_PRISONKEY` (`DEV_ID_MR`, `0x000007`). `IDR_LAB3_SPECIAL` (`lab3_special`, `lab3.c:897-1068`) is now also fully ported, closing the gap the guard/prisoner rows above used to flag: `drdata[0]` selects the object flavor. `1` = the password-protected teleport door - the raw `lab3_special_driver` (`item_driver/area22_lab.rs`) reads a new `ItemDriverContext::lab3_guard_talkstep` field (populated in `item_apply.rs`'s `item_driver_context_for_request` from `PlayerRuntime::legacy_lab3_guard_talkstep`, same "read-only per-player fact" precedent as `IDR_LABENTRANCE`'s `lab_solved_bits`) to decide the blocked/`Lab3TeleportDoorLocked` path; the unlocked path resolves entirely inside `World::apply_lab3_teleport_door` (new `world/lab.rs` addition, since `teleport_char_driver`/the underwater tail/`queue_lab_exit_spawn` need no `ZoneLoader`/`PlayerRuntime`) - `teleport_char_driver`, then (destination underwater) unconditionally extinguishing every carried/worn/cursor `IDR_TORCH` via the existing `pub(crate) fn extinguish_torch` primitive, then (not `CF_OXYGEN`) 5 sequential-tick `EF_BUBBLE` map effects + 3 `sound_area` splash sounds + `npc_say(cn, "Hrgblub.")`, then (password-protected) `World::queue_lab_exit_spawn(cn, 25)` reusing the pre-existing `create_lab_exit` queue. `2` = the note-giving skeleton -
