`ugaris-server`'s new `create_lab3_note_for_character` (sibling to the prisoner's own `create_lab3_note_on_cursor`, placing the note on the *using player's* cursor instead) copies the special item's own `drdata[1]` onto the new note and applies the `drdata[1]==20` sprite override (`11076`). `3` = the readable note itself (a freshly-created `lab3_note_generic` is itself `driver=192` with `drdata[0]==3`) - `tick_item_use_lab.rs`'s new `lab3_note_text` reproduces the full switch: cases `1..=6` are canned lore text (case `1`'s message embeds two literal U+FFFD replacement-character bytes, copied digit-for-digit from the C source's own already-mangled string literal), cases `20`/`21` call a new `lab3_init_password` (the first real writer of `PlayerRuntime::legacy_lab3_password1`/`_password2` - a 70-pair `LAB3_PASSWORD_PAIRS` table ported verbatim from C's `password[][8]`, `RANDOM(70)`-picked only once per player and persisted thereafter, matching C's `if (*ppd->password1) return;` early-out), any other value matches C's `default: xlog(...)` (no player-visible text, still "handled"). 21 new focused tests (6 pure-driver in `item_driver/tests/area22_lab.rs`, 7 `World`-resolution in a new `world/tests/lab3_special.rs`, 8 full-pipeline in `tests/lab.rs` - the password-persistence test also cross-checks that reading the `drdata[1]==21` companion note reveals the *other* half of the very same persisted pair). This closes every gap in `lab3.c` covered by this row. |
| `src/area/22/lab2.c` `lab2_herald_driver`/`lab2_deamon_driver`/`lab2_deamon_create`/`lab2_deamon_is_elias` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab2_herald.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab2_deamon.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/labs.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/misc.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/mod.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/{helpers,ids}.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_fight.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_idle.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs` | New `CDR_LAB2HERALD = 196` (the graveyard chapel keeper "Herald", quest giver for the Arathas hunt): fully ported end-to-end, following the `world::gatekeeper`/`world::camhermit` `PlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent` split since the dialogue state (`ppd->herald_talkstep`) lives in the *player's* `struct lab_ppd` byte blob, not `World`. New `PlayerRuntime::legacy_lab2_herald_talkstep`/`set_legacy_lab2_herald_talkstep` (offset 42 of `lab_ppd`, one byte before the already-ported `graveversion` at 43) plus a new `IID_LAB2_ARATHASRING` (`DEV_ID_MR`, 0x05 - also added `DEV_ID_MR` itself to `item_driver::helpers`, `key_registry.rs` already had a private copy for keys 0x6-0x11). Ports the full `NT_CHAR` greeting/dialogue state machine (13 talkstep cases: intro, Arathas/Elias/family-vault/administrative-building/diary lore branches with `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` markers restored via `npc_say_bytes`, and the post-kill ring-thanks/gate-reward finale calling the already-ported `World::queue_lab_exit_spawn(player_id, 30)`), the `NT_TEXT` keyword-driven talkstep jump (ARATHAS/ELIAS/FAMILY+VAULT/ADMINISTRATIVE+BUILDING/DIARY/REPEAT, case-insensitive substring match) plus the generic `tabunga` god-mode echo (both fire for the same message, matching C's fall-through `if` chain), and the `NT_GIVE` ring check (unconditionally destroys whatever is handed over, only advances talkstep to 60 when it's a player giving `IID_LAB2_ARATHASRING`). `dat->last_talk`/`dat->next_talk` (the herald's own driver memory, shared across every player it talks to - C's `dat` is per-NPC, unlike the per-player talkstep) are `Lab2HeraldDriverData`. `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 0, 0)`'s call after every message is not reproduced (dead with `agressive=0, helper=0` for a non-fighting NPC, same precedent as every other ambient-dialogue NPC's own doc comment). 9 new tests (8 `world::tests::lab2_herald` + 1 `player::tests::labs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3254 core [+9] + 1154 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke on area 22 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of clean ticks (`processed lab-1 torch-gnome actions`/no herald-specific panics) and no panic. New `CDR_LAB2DEAMON = 197` (the family-vault masquerade-detection seek-and-destroy guardian "Deamon") is now also fully ported: `lab2_deamon_is_elias` (four `WN_HEAD`/`WN_CLOAK`/`WN_BELT`/`WN_FEET`-slot template-id checks against new `IID_LAB2_ELIASHAT`/`ELIASCAPE`/`ELIASBOOTS`/`ELIASBELT` constants) drives `NT_CREATE`'s three-way talkstep seed (partial-gear masquerade/full-gear elias-first-time/full-gear elias-already-checked, the latter two reading/writing a new `PlayerRuntime::lab2_deamon_checked` field - a distinct C `DRD_LAB2_PLAYER` slot from `lab_ppd`'s own `DRD_LAB_PPD`, so a plain new field per `AGENTS.md` persistence rules, not a `lab_ppd` byte offset); the four independent dialogue ladders (warn/elias/quick-elias/masquerade, 15 talkstep cases total, `player_driver_halt` calls surfaced as `Lab2DeamonOutcomeEvent::HaltPlayer`) are ported case-for-case; the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_LAB2_DEAMONCHECK` attack-engagement message (already wired end-to-end from the item-driver side, confirmed no gap there) sets `attacking`/`pursuing` and shouts the observing/warned variant; the seek-and-destroy fight AI narrows C's generic `fight_driver_add_enemy`/`_remove_enemy`/`_update`/`_attack_visible`/`_follow_invisible` (a 10-slot enemy list this NPC only ever populates with one entry) to direct `attacking`/`pursuing`/`victim_visible`/`victim_last_x`/`victim_last_y` fields on `Lab2DeamonDriverData`, same simplification precedent as `world::gate_fight`; the `MF_NOMAGIC`-gated "stop attacking when player tries to run away" check is ported literal-condition-for-condition (worth noting: it *keeps* the attack engaged only while the victim's tile *has* `MF_NOMAGIC` set, matching the vault-approach trigger tile); the pathfinder-blocked-twice teleport-to-victim mechanic (`World::lab2_deamon_teleport_if_stuck`, reusing `path::pathfinder`/`World::teleport_char_driver`) and the final self-destruct/go-home/turn-to-target/idle tail are all ported. `lab2_deamon_create`'s dedup-loop/fallback-drop-position/`co`-`serial`-assignment split into `World::lab2_deamon_already_tracking`/`World::init_lab2_deamon` (pure `World` logic) plus the `ugaris-server::tick_item_use_lab::dispatch_lab_outcome`'s `Lab2StepActionDaemonWarning` handler, which previously only had an incomplete placeholder spawn (see the `IDR_LAB2_STEPACTION` row below) - it now dedups, tries `(x,y)` then falls back to `(x,y+3)` on drop failure, and calls `init_lab2_deamon` to seed `co`/`serial`/`dir`/`rest_x`/`rest_y` and push the `NT_CREATE` message. New `CharacterDriverState::Lab2Deamon` variant (3 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan). 14 new tests (`world::tests::lab2_deamon`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3268 core [+14] + 1154 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with hundreds of clean ticks and no panic. Still unported: the undead/grave system's remaining PPD count/reward gaps already tracked on the `CDR_LAB2UNDEAD` rows above (unrelated to the herald/deamon). |
| `src/area/22/lab1.c` `labgnome_driver`/`labgnome_died_driver`/`deathfibrin` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area22/lab1_gnome.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area22_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area22.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs` | New `CDR_LABGNOMEDRIVER = 199`: the guard/fighter/immortal-master triad is ported end-to-end - args parsing (data-only half at `ZoneLoader::instantiate_character_template`, map-dependent idle-facing-direction + `scan_gnometorches` torch-list on the character's own first tick, matching C's own `NT_CREATE` timing), the fixed farthest-first torch list, torch-attacker `NT_NPC`/`NTID_LABGNOMETORCH` defense, `standard_message_driver`'s `NT_CHAR`/`NT_SEEHIT`/`NT_GOTHIT` cases (parameterized by this driver's own `aggressive`/`helper` args, single-victim simplified per the usual precedent), the single-victim fight/follow cascade (`World::attack_driver_direct`/`secure_move_driver` reuse), nearest-unlit-torch re-selection + walk-and-light (`use_and_attack_driver`, reusing `setup_walk_toward_use_item`/`do_use`, plus a plain-`pathfinder`-probe substitute for C's custom `attack_check_target` blocker detection), the idle-nonsense table, and `labgnome_died_driver`'s `dat->text` speech branch (wired as a new `apply_labgnome_death_from_hurt_event` death hook). `IDR_DEATHFIBRIN` is also fully ported: the `sprite==10428` shrine dispenser (`grant_template_item_to_cursor("deathfibrin")`), the staff's `deathfibrin_scan` (new `World::deathfibrin_scan`, an 8-tile box scan for a visible `CDR_LABGNOMEDRIVER` named exactly "Immortal Master") and the strike itself (pulse-back show effect, `CF_IMMORTAL` clear/hurt-10*POWERSCALE/restore around `apply_legacy_hurt` exactly like C, `deathfibrin_check`'s sprite/description/vanish-at-0% update). Two documented, deliberate gaps: `deathfibrin`'s zero-character passive ticker (light-based decay, 10-minute unattended auto-vanish) is not ported - nothing schedules its `call_item` timer in this port, so a fresh staff instead lazily initializes to 100% charge on its *first player strike* (byte 4 of `driver_data` doubles as the C `dat->init` substitute) rather than incorrectly starting at 0%; and `labgnome_died_driver`'s `dat->master` `create_lab_exit(co, 20)` reward branch IS now also ported (see the `src/system/lab.c` row below for the shared machinery this and every other lab area's own master-kill hook now calls): `World::queue_lab_exit_spawn` queues the request, `CharacterFlags::PLAYER`-gated exactly like C's `if (co && (ch[co].flags & CF_PLAYER))`. `scan_gnometorches`'s pathfinder-reachability pre-filter is also not reproduced (LOS-only registration instead) - never observably different against `lab1.chr`'s actual shipped torch placements, all in open floor tiles. 10 new `ugaris-core` world tests (`world/tests/lab1_gnome.rs`: create-message parsing/immortal-flag, NT_CREATE torch scan farthest-first, fighter-never-scans, adjacent-torch light-and-walk, tracked/untracked torch-attacker defense, death-text branch on/off, and a full deathfibrin-strike-hurts-master-and-restores-immortality integration test) plus 7 new `item_driver` tests (`item_driver/tests/area22_lab.rs`: shrine give/occupied, staff needs-carrying/no-master/first-strike-lazy-init/vanish-on-empty, timer-call no-op). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3243 core [+17] + 1148 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of clean ticks and no panic. |
| `src/area/18/bones.c` `bonebridge` / `boneladder` / `boneholder` / `bonewall` / `exec_rune` core paths | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area18_bones.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/bones.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/areas_misc.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_keyassembly.rs` | `IDR_BONEBRIDGE` dispatch now ports the C full-bone cursor gate, area-18 libload guard, target tile fit checks, orientation-specific temporary bridge placement, cursor removal, dirty-sector marking, 60-second cleanup scheduling, timer retry while the bridge tile is temporarily blocked, bridge ageing sprite/drdata increments, and final removal while restoring permanent movement blocking with focused core tests. `IDR_BONELADDER` now ports the area-18 libload guard and paired same-area teleports using the C `drdata[0]` offset table. `IDR_BONEHOLDER` now ports the area-18 dispatch boundary for stand rune insertion gates, rune ID decoding for `rune1`..`rune9`, owner/tick metadata storage in `drdata[8..15]`, owner-only removal guards, 120-second timer expiry clearing, activation-holder classification, and legacy blocked feedback texts. `IDR_BONEWALL` now ports the area-18 libload guard, active/timer dispatch guards, adjacent dormant wall pulse scheduling, opening sprite/drdata progression, temporary map item removal with movement/sight unblock and void/use flag toggles, blocked restore retry, and final wall restoration. Partial-bridge add/remove inventory paths (`bones.c:236-270`) are now also ported: `bonebridge_driver`'s carried-bridge branch (`drdata[0] != 0 && drdata[1] == 0`) resolves to one of five new outcomes - `BoneBridgeAddBone`/`BoneBridgeFinished` (cursor holds another `IID_AREA18_BONE` item, capped at 5 with C's exact "The bridge is finished." text) or `BoneBridgeWrongCursorItem`/`BoneBridgeRemoveBone`/`BoneBridgeNotEnoughBones` (empty cursor, needs >=2 bones to pull one back out, both rejection paths sharing C's literal "Hu?" text) - applied by new `World::add_bone_to_bridge`/`remove_bone_from_bridge` (`world/area_mech.rs`, sprite `13030 + drdata[0]` matching C, destroying the exhausted cursor stack item same as C's `destroy_item(in2)`); the remove path's new "bone" item is instantiated in `tick_item_use_completion.rs` via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template("bone", ...)` + `World::give_char_item` (same empty-cursor-first precedent as `BoneHolderRemoveRune`), matching C's direct `ch[cn].citem = in2` assignment since the driver already verified the cursor was empty. This closes Area 18. Everything else the earlier row flagged as remaining is now closed: `update_holder`'s sprite/foreground-sprite refresh is `World::update_bone_holder_sprite` (`world/bones.rs`), wired into the `BoneHolderInsertRune`/`RemoveRune`/`Expired` outcome application in `item_outcomes.rs::apply_item_driver_outcome` (which also now actually destroys the consumed cursor rune and starts the 120s timer for `InsertRune`, matching C's `call_item(it[in].driver, in, 0, ticker + TICKS*120+1)`). `BoneHolderRemoveRune`'s and the activation scan's `remove_rune_from_holder` item-creation halves (`create_rune_from_holder` + `give_char_item`/direct-cursor-assign) are now wired in `tick_item_use_keyassembly.rs` via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template(&format!("rune{rune}"), ...)` (C's "bug #11970" text preserved for the creation-failure edge case, matching the single-touch-removal branch's own distinct message from the give-back branch's silent-destroy-on-failure). The activation-holder's three-preceding-stand scan (C `boneholder`'s `drdata[1]==2\|\|3` branch, `bones.c:698-723`) is `World::scan_and_clear_bone_holder_runes` (owner-gated, builds the concatenated combination number in stand order, clears+resprites each matched stand), returned to the server crate via a new `ItemDriverOutcome::BoneHolderActivateResolved` (the `World`-level resolution `apply_item_driver_outcome` transforms `BoneHolderActivate` into, carrying `nr` and up to 3 `(holder_item_id, rune)` pairs - a fixed `[Option<(ItemId, u8)>; 3]` array rather than `Vec` since the enum derives `Copy`). `exec_rune` itself (`bones.c:327-641`, all ~44 combination cases: nine level-progression single/double/triple-repeated-digit teleport/bonus-exp/"laugh" triads, six literal three-digit skill-raise combos with fixed lowercase messages, and the 25-slot `special_exec`-table dispatch with dynamic `skill[].name`-cased messages via `rune_raise`, including the `last_holder`-gated bonus-area case 20 that uniquely never sets the "used" flag under either branch) is `World::exec_rune`/`exec_rune_special`/`rune_raise`/`grant_rune_bonus_exp`/`raise_rune_skill` in the new `world/bones.rs`. The `used[]` bitmask gate (C `rune_check`/`rune_set`) is new `PlayerRuntime::rune_check`/`rune_set` methods (`player/areas_misc.rs`, operating on the already-round-tripped-but-previously-unused `rune_used_words` field), called from the server-crate dispatch around `World::exec_rune` (mirroring the pre-existing `BoneHint`/`bone_hint` split, since `World` has no `PlayerRuntime` access). 12 new focused tests (11 `ugaris-core` `world::tests::bones`, 1 `ugaris-core` `player::tests::misc::rune_check_and_set_track_the_used_bitmask_like_c`) cover sprite refresh, the 3-stand scan's digit-order/owner-gating, and every `exec_rune` case family including the `flag`-set-even-on-raise-failure C quirk. |
| `src/area/13/dungeon.c` dungeon item-driver boundary | `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/dungeon_maze.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | `IDR_DUNGEONTELE`, `IDR_DUNGEONFAKE`, `IDR_DUNGEONKEY`, and `IDR_DUNGEONDOOR` now dispatch only under the legacy area-13 libload guard, decode C little-endian `drdata`, preserve player-only dungeon teleport behavior, consume fake/teleport source items through world item destruction, create `maze_key1`/`maze_key2` cursor keys with the decoded legacy key ID, mark first key take via `drdata[2]`, enforce dungeon-door exact key requirements through carried item IDs, enforce the legacy 20-defender catacomb gate, clear solved door key IDs, mark `drdata[12]`, and attempt the C fallback teleport destinations, with focused core tests plus workspace coverage. Clan jewel theft/state, dungeon master/fighter character drivers, server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb player/NPC notifications, and exact clan policy remain. Iteration 139 (see the "Clan system" row's own tail entry too) ported the file's *maze-generation* half as a standalone, unwired `ugaris-core` module: `build_maze` (the explicit-stack recursive-backtracker carver, `dungeon.c:1005-1101` - including a real quirk where the un-initialized origin-cell visited flag sometimes produces one redundant "braided" back-edge/cycle instead of a strict spanning tree, faithfully reproduced and covered by a 2000-seed connectivity sweep), `special_fill` (the shortest-path/reachability flood fill, `dungeon.c:968-1003`), and `create_maze`'s own scoring/placement orchestration minus its final `build_cell` map-instantiation loop and `show_maze` debug printer (`dungeon.c:1134-1329` - path-length `cbrt` difficulty scoring, fake-wall midpoint selection and connectivity invalidation, exit-door/key-requirement placement, and the shared 50-NPC/200-attempt-budget warrior/mage/seyan/teleport-trap placement loops). 12 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/dungeon_maze.rs`. Iteration 140 ported the next slice: `build_warrior`/`build_mage`/`build_seyan` (`dungeon.c:217-700`, new `crates/ugaris-server/src/dungeon.rs`) - the three NPC-generation functions that scale a "warrior"/"mage"/"seyan" character template to a per-level `base` stat from `dungeon_tab.c`'s three tables (`warrior_tab`/`mage_tab`/`seyan_tab`, copied verbatim as new consts) via each function's own per-skill `switch (n)` formula, then attach the four "spell of equipment" items (`equip1`/`equip2`/`armor_spell`/`weapon_spell`, or their `b`/`c` mage/seyan variants) in non-worn inventory slots 12-15 exactly like C's `ch[cn].item[12..15]`, with a new `level2maxitem` port (`tool.c:2516-2577`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/exp.rs`) feeding the shared equip-item modifier formula. Also ported: the level>33 arch-flag + forced `V_RAGE`/`V_DURATION` pre-loop quirk (warrior/mage only, matching C), the clan-profession assignment (`prof[P_CLAN]`/`prof[P_LIGHT or P_DARK]`), and the full `update_char` tail (`World::update_character` after inserting into `World`, then `hp`/`endurance`/`mana` from the recomputed `value[0]`, matching `drop_char`'s call order) - reusing the existing `spawns.rs` template-instantiation recipe rather than duplicating it. 8 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/dungeon.rs` plus 1 new `level2maxitem` test in `ugaris-core`. Still unwired (module carries `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a doc comment, matching the `depot.rs::legacy_account_depot_codec` precedent) since C's own `build_cell` dispatch - the next slice - is what calls these. Still remaining: `build_cell`'s dispatch of a generated maze's per-cell `special` code into calls to these three functions plus the wall/door/key/teleport map-tile builders (`dungeon.c:849-937`), and the `dungeonmaster`/`dungeonfighter` NPC drivers (`create_dungeon`/`enter_dungeon`/`list_dungeon`/`warn_dungeon`/`destroy_dungeon`/`dungeon_potion`/`fighter_dead`, `dungeon.c:1343-2161`) that tie it all together - plus everything the original row already listed as remaining (clan jewel theft/state beyond the item-driver slice, server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb notifications). Iteration 141 ported the map-tile/item builders and `build_cell`'s own dispatch (`dungeon.c:715-937`), still in `crates/ugaris-server/src/dungeon.rs`: `build_wall` (opaque-wall map tile, coordinate-driven 4-variant brick sprite, no RNG), `build_teleport`/`build_fake`/`build_door`/`build_key` (each instantiates the matching `dungeon.itm` template - `teleport_trap`/`fake_wall`/`dungeon_door`/`maze_key_spawn` - writes the little-endian `driver_data` layout the already-ported `item_driver::area13_dungeon` readers expect, and places it via `MapGrid::set_item_map`), and `build_cell` itself (builds a maze cell's top/left wall segments with their fake-wall-substitution middle tile, then dispatches the cell's own `special` code - keys `3`/`4`, the six warrior/mage/seyan tiers `5..=22` via the already-ported `build_warrior`/`build_mage`/`build_seyan`, teleport traps `23..=27`'s five in-cell positions, and exit-door variants `28..=30` - matching every C case verbatim). `xoff`/`yoff`/`maze_clan`/`maze_base`/`maze_level` are taken as explicit parameters rather than C's file-scope statics, so the whole chain is directly testable without the still-unported orchestrator. Confirmed and preserved a real C quirk verbatim: `build_key`'s spawn item stores its `keyid` parameter (always `maze_base` at every call site) raw in `driver_data[4..8]`, *not* `MAKE_ITEMID`-wrapped, unlike `build_door`'s own key-requirement fields which *are* wrapped (`MAKE_ITEMID(DEV_ID_MAZE1/2, keyid)`, `dungeon.c:820,825`) - the wrapping only happens in C's `dungeonkey` driver (`dungeon.c:1913-1930`) at pickup time, which sets the *real* key item's C `ID` field to the wrapped value. Auditing the already-ported Rust equivalent (`item_driver::area13_dungeon::dungeon_key_driver` + its `main.rs` `DungeonKey` outcome handler) found this wrapping step is currently missing - `key_id` is passed straight from `drdata_u32(item,4)` to `cursor_item.template_id` with no `MAKE_ITEMID` applied, so a picked-up key's `template_id` would never actually match a `dungeon_door`'s wrapped `key1`/`key2` fields today. This is a latent bug in already-merged code from an earlier iteration, outside this iteration's own `dungeon.c:715-937` scope (the pickup driver lives at `dungeon.c:1890-1940`) - flagged here rather than silently fixed, since fixing it would be a different, unrelated slice. 12 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/dungeon.rs` covering every new builder plus `build_cell`'s wall-segment and warrior/door/key/teleport dispatch branches. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1870 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 601 server [+17], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Still remaining (before iteration 142): the `dungeonmaster`/`dungeonfighter` NPC drivers that actually call `create_maze`+loop-over-`build_cell` to spin up/track/tear down a live catacomb (`create_dungeon`/`enter_dungeon`/`list_dungeon`/`warn_dungeon`/`destroy_dungeon`/`dungeon_potion`/`fighter_dead`, `dungeon.c:1343-2161`), the `dungeon_key_driver`/`DungeonKey`-outcome `MAKE_ITEMID` wrapping gap just discovered, plus everything the original row already listed (clan jewel theft/state beyond the item-driver slice, server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb notifications). Iteration 142 closed the `MAKE_ITEMID` wrapping gap: new `crates/ugaris-server/src/dungeon.rs::dungeon_key_item_id(template, key_id)` reuses the module's own already-ported `make_maze_item_id`/`DEV_ID_MAZE1`/`DEV_ID_MAZE2` constants to wrap a picked-up key spawn's raw stored `keyid` exactly like C's `dungeonkey` (`dungeon.c:1913-1937`) - `MAKE_ITEMID(DEV_ID_MAZE1, keyid)` for the `maze_key1` template, `DEV_ID_MAZE2` for `maze_key2` - and `main.rs`'s `ItemDriverOutcome::DungeonKey` handler now calls it instead of assigning the raw `key_id` straight to `cursor_item.template_id`, so a picked-up key can finally match a `dungeon_door`'s own wrapped `key1`/`key2` requirement. New test `dungeon_key_item_id_wraps_raw_keyid_by_slot` in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/dungeon.rs` (both slot wrappings, cross-checked against a `build_door`-created door's own stored `key1`/`key2`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (602 server [+1], all other crates unchanged/green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings. Still remaining now: only the `dungeonmaster`/`dungeonfighter` NPC driver orchestration, plus everything the original row already listed (clan jewel theft/state beyond the item-driver slice, server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb notifications). Iteration 143 ported the next self-contained slice: `create_dungeon`/`enter_dungeon`/`list_dungeon`/`warn_dungeon`'s *pure decision* logic (`dungeon.c:1377-1569`), new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_master.rs` - `DungeonmasterDriverData` (C `struct master_data`'s 9-slot `target`/`level`/`created`/`warning`/`owner`/`created_by_clan` arrays, passed by reference rather than stored on any `Character` yet), `World::plan_create_dungeon` (every `create_dungeon` validation in C's own order - clan range, level cap, `clan_can_attack_inside`-or-GOD, target/own jewel counts via the already-ported `ClanRegistry::jewel_count`/`relations().can_attack_inside`, the three already-exists/already-raiding early-return checks, then the busy-slot-selection `max(dungeon_time, ticker-created)` eviction rule - returning a `DungeonRaidPlan` with the chosen slot, fee, guard-roster counts via `ClanRegistry::get_clan_dungeon`, and computed `xoff`/`yoff` on success, a `DungeonRaidError` matching each C `say()` message otherwise), `World::plan_enter_dungeon` (target-bounds/level-cap/at-war/about-to-collapse checks plus the slot's teleport-destination math), `World::list_dungeon_lines` (formatted per-slot strings or "No catacombs."), and `World::characters_in_dungeon_slot` (`warn_dungeon`'s player-in-this-81x81-block selection, for the caller to format and deliver the collapse-warning message to). Fee-charging, maze-building (`create_maze`+`build_cell`, both already ported and ready), teleporting the raider, and the two `add_clanlog` calls are all left to the future orchestrator, matching this codebase's "pure logic first, wiring later" precedent - no `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER` constant or `CharacterDriverState` variant added yet since nothing stores this data on a live `Character` in this slice. 20 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/dungeon_master.rs` covering every error branch (including the God-bypasses-war-but-not-jewels case and the "all empty slots outrank any occupied slot until every slot is occupied" busy-selection edge case) plus both success paths. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1890 ugaris-core [+20], 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 602 server unchanged, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed no panics. Still remaining after iteration 143: `destroy_dungeon`'s `build_remove`/`build_empty` map-teardown sweep (needs player-eviction/`change_area`/`exit_char` logic), the actual `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` driver wiring (constants, `CharacterDriverState` variant, message-loop entry point, tick-loop call site), the do-while `create_maze`+`build_cell`-retry-until-score-350 orchestration that spends the fee and actually spins up the map, and `dungeonfighter`/`dungeon_potion`/`fighter_dead` (the separate combat-adjacent driver) - plus everything the original row already listed (server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb notifications, the `DungeonDoorSolved` item-driver outcome's still-unwired jewel-stealing math/broadcast). Iteration 144 closed the `destroy_dungeon`/`build_remove`/`build_empty` gap (`dungeon.c:725-786,1343-1364`): `World::destroy_dungeon`/`build_remove_tile`/`build_empty_tile` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_master.rs`, real `World` mutations added alongside the module's existing pure-decision methods, since every primitive they need - `teleport_character_same_area`/`remove_character`/`destroy_item`/`remove_effect_from_map`/`set_item_expire`/`queue_system_text` - already exists). `build_remove_tile` evicts a player via the same four-coordinate same-area teleport chain C tries (with the "The catacomb collapsed on you." warning) or removes an NPC outright, scatters a takeable item or (for a non-takeable player body) tries the same four-coordinate `drop_item` chain before arming `item_decay_time` decay or destroying it, and clears every effect anchored to the tile; `build_empty_tile` destroys any leftover item and resets the tile to bare indoors floor with the exact `59130 + x%3 + (y%3)*3` sprite formula; `destroy_dungeon` sweeps a slot's 81x81 block with both, in C's own two-pass order. One real, documented gap: C's `change_area(cn, ch[cn].resta, ...)` fallback (for a player none of the four teleports could place) targets a possibly-*different* area, but this codebase runs one area per server process with no cross-area transfer yet (`World::area_id`'s own doc comment; matches the existing "target area server is down" precedent in `crates/ugaris-server/src/transport.rs`'s `TransportTravelResult::CrossArea`) - so only the same-area case of `change_area` (the player's own `rest_area` happening to equal this running area) is reachable; the cross-area case falls through to `remove_character`, matching what C's own `exit_char(cn)` fallback would produce. 13 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/dungeon_master.rs` (non-player eviction, player teleport+warning, same-area rest-point fallback, cross-area-unreachable eviction, plain-item destruction, player-body scatter-and-decay and scatter-failure-destroys, effect cleanup, tile reset, and a full-sweep slot-isolation check). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1900 ugaris-core [+13] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 602 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings. Still remaining now: only the actual `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` driver wiring, the do-while `create_maze`+`build_cell`-retry orchestration, and `dungeonfighter`/`dungeon_potion`/`fighter_dead` - plus everything the original row already listed (server-chat raid protocol messages, solved-catacomb notifications, the `DungeonDoorSolved` item-driver outcome's still-unwired jewel-stealing math/broadcast). Iteration 145 ported and wired the `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER` driver itself, closing the biggest remaining gap: `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` driver constants, `DUNGEONMASTER_QA` (C's own small-talk table, `dungeon.c:91-99` - codes `2`/`3` "help"/"list" are real reachable outcomes here, unlike `CLANMASTER_QA`'s dead codes), and `CharacterDriverState::Dungeonmaster` all added to `character_driver.rs` (also relocated `DungeonmasterDriverData`/`DUNGEON_SLOT_COUNT` there from `world/dungeon_master.rs`, gaining `Serialize`/`Deserialize`, matching every other driver-data type's home). `World::process_dungeonmaster_actions` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_master.rs`) ports `dungeonmaster` itself (`dungeon.c:1571-1731`): the `NT_CHAR` greeting (distance/visibility/driver-memory-slot-7 gates, using this codebase's real queued `NT_CHAR` messages rather than a periodic-scan simplification, since movement already emits them), the `NT_TEXT` small-talk-then-independent-keyword dispatch (`attack <nr>`/`enter <nr>`/GM-only `destroy <nr>`, all unconditional regardless of the qa-table outcome, matching C's plain `if` chain), `NT_GIVE`, the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DUNGEON` slot-reset handler (unreachable until `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` exists to send it), and the per-slot expiry/warning tick plus 12h memory clear. `attack`'s success path reuses `plan_create_dungeon`, charges the fee via `gate_take_money` (reusing the same shared `take_money` primitive `world/gatekeeper.rs` already ported), updates the slot's tracking fields, and queues a new `DungeonRaidBuildRequest`/`pending_dungeon_raid_builds` event (same "pure decision in `World`, I/O-heavy application in `ugaris-server`" split as every other `*Event` queue here) since the maze build itself needs `ZoneLoader`/`ServerRuntime`. In `crates/ugaris-server/src/dungeon.rs`, added `build_dungeon_raid_maze` (the do-while `create_maze`+`build_cell`-retry-until-350 loop, `dungeon.c:1500-1503,1327-1339` - discovered `create_maze` itself unconditionally calls `build_cell` before returning its score, so a low-scoring attempt is simply overwritten by the next attempt's `destroy_dungeon`+build pass) and `apply_dungeonmaster_events` (drains the queue, builds the maze, says the "collapse in" confirmation, teleports the raider, writes both `add_clanlog` entries via the already-ported `clan_log::write_clan_log_entry`). Wired `CharacterDriverState::Dungeonmaster` into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation (confirmed `ugaris_data/zones/13/*.chr` already has a real `driver=51` NPC) and the tick-loop call site in `main.rs` right after `clanclerk`. Not ported: C's own `secure_move_driver` call (dead in practice, no zone-file waypoints for this NPC) and `immortal_dead` (a trivial one-line bug-log message for the `ch_died_driver` case, since this NPC is never meant to die). 41 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/dungeon_master.rs` (greeting distance/visibility/memory-dedup, help/list wording, attack success charging the fee and queuing the build request, attack failure paths charging nothing, insufficient-gold handling, enter success/out-of-bounds, GOD-gated destroy, expiry-tick slot reset). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1911 ugaris-core [+11] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 602 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke on both area 1 and area 13 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Still remaining before iteration 146: `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` itself (`dungeonfighter`/`dungeon_potion`/`fighter_dead`, `dungeon.c:1956-2161`), `immortal_dead`, and `dungeondoor`'s `first_solve` jewel-stealing/`NTID_DUNGEON`-notify side effects (a pre-existing gap, not introduced this iteration - `item_driver::area13_dungeon::dungeon_door_driver` already computes `first_solve`/`catacomb` but `world::item_outcomes`'s `DungeonDoorSolved` handler only teleports the winner today). Iteration 146 ported `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`'s message-loop/potion half (`dungeonfighter`/`dungeon_potion`, `dungeon.c:1956-2161`), new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_fighter.rs`: `DungeonfighterDriverData` (C `struct dungeonfighter_data`'s 4 never-reset damage/potion-budget counters) + `CharacterDriverState::Dungeonfighter` (updated the 4 exhaustive `CharacterDriverState` matches across `character_driver.rs`/`npc_messages.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`; wired at zone-template instantiation in `zone.rs`, mirroring `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`). `World::process_dungeonfighter_actions`/`process_dungeonfighter_messages` port the `NT_DIDHIT`/`NT_GOTHIT` accumulation and the three simple-potion (mana/hp/combo) drink checks in C's exact order/gating (the `good_damage` repeat-gate, the combo block's `&& !flag` "only if neither specific potion already fired" rule, the shared tier-selection shape factored into `pick_simple_pot`); `World::dungeon_potion` ports the alchemy stat-potion grant (highest `V_INT`-qualifying stocked tier, 10-minute `IDR_POTION_SP` item, Attack/Parry/Immunity for `CF_WARRIOR` else Flash/MagicShield/Immunity - its own dedicated function rather than reusing `install_beyond_potion_spell`, since that helper requires an existing carried source item to consume and this potion is synthesized outright with no `EF_POTION` show-effect, matching C). C's actual stock-consumption side effect for both potion kinds is a `server_chat(1028, ...)` message to a master server owning the authoritative `struct clan` array (`clan.c`'s `clan_dungeon_chat`, cases `'s'`/`'a'`) - since this codebase has no master/slave server split, added `ClanRegistry::consume_simple_pot`/`consume_alc_pot` (direct local decrement) matching the existing `add_alc_potion`/`bump_simple_pot` increment-side precedent, called directly instead of round-tripping an unported IPC channel. Confirmed via a fresh C-tree grep that `fighter_dead`'s own `server_chat(1028, ...)` message is dead code in the original C (decrements a `[0]` sub-array that is never incremented anywhere), so it was deliberately not ported. Wired `process_dungeonfighter_actions` into the tick loop in `main.rs` right after `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`. 16 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/dungeon_fighter.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1927 ugaris-core [+16] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 602 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Still remaining: giving these NPCs their actual SimpleBaddy idle-wander/auto-attack AI (a real architecture change - C's own `dungeonfighter` ends with `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)`, reusing that driver's full behavior on a character whose own `driver` field is `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`, but every Rust `process_simple_baddy_*` function requires both `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` and a `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy` payload, and `Character::driver_state` can only hold one variant at a time unlike C's `set_data`, which lets one character carry both drivers' data blobs simultaneously), `immortal_dead`, and `dungeondoor`'s jewel-steal/notify gap. Iteration 79 (PORTING_TODO's "Area 13" P4 checkbox, resolved by this point via the "Clan system"/P0.5/cross-area-transfer work above): re-audited every function in `dungeon.c`/`dungeon_tab.c` one by one against the Rust tree and found every listed gap above already closed by iterations 147-151 (`dungeondoor`'s `first_solve` jewel-steal via `ClanRegistry::dungeon_jewel_steal`+`resolve_dungeon_door_first_solve`, `immortal_dead`, and the `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`/SimpleBaddy architecture gap via the new `Character::dungeonfighter` field) plus the "Cross-area transfer" task's dungeon-eviction wiring (iteration 225) and P0.5's dungeon-outcome `main.rs` extraction. No remaining gap found; `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 13 checkbox is now marked `[x]`. |
| `src/system/create.c` `update_char`/`armor_skill_req`/`armor_skill_bonus` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/character_values.rs` | `World::update_character(cn)`/`recompute_character_values` ports the full `value[0]` recompute: worn/spell item modifier sum with the seyan (72.5%) vs. single-class (50%) cap and non-warrior bless-item cap, `IF_BEYONDMAXMOD` uncapped bypass, skill-table base-attribute averaging (`skill[]` from `skill.c:27` hardcoded as `skill_base_attributes`), the `value[1]==0` skip for unraised skills, Cold/Demon special cases, Speed Skill/Athlete/Thief/Demon-profession bonuses, Body Control armor/weapon bonuses (with the bare-handed player weapon bonus) vs. the spell-average Armor bonus when Body Control is unraised, `armor_skill_bonus`'s body/head/legs/arms weighted requirement-vs-raised comparison, day/night/clan attribute profession bonuses, and the HP/endurance/mana current-value clamp to the new max. Wired into worn-slot equip/unequip (`crates/ugaris-server/src/inventory.rs::inventory_swap_slot`, `pos < 12` only, matching C `do.c:1294`). 11 focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/character_values.rs`. As of iteration 28, `World::character_attached_effect_light` sums `.light` across the character's currently-attached effects (`Effect::target_character`) and `recompute_character_values` adds it into `mod[V_LIGHT]`, matching C's `mod[V_LIGHT] += ef[fn].light` loop (`create.c:1785-1797`); the only remaining documented gap is an intentional approximation of C's fixed four-slot `ch.ef[]` cap (Rust sums the four lowest-id attached effects rather than tracking real slot occupancy, which only differs from C with 5+ simultaneous character-attached effects) and the trivial `player_reset_map_cache` display-cache no-op on infravision toggle (Rust has no client-scroll-diff cache to invalidate). As of iteration 25, `World` now has a real `pub area_id: u16` field (set once from `ServerConfig::area_id` at startup in `main.rs`, since this process is one area server for its whole lifetime) and the `P_CLAN` bonus checks `self.area_id == 13 || tile.flags.contains(MapFlags::CLAN)`, matching C `create.c:1856` (`areaID == 13 || (mmf & MF_CLAN)`) exactly - the catacombs special case is no longer a gap. As of iteration 21, sprite reselection (demon suits, weapon-in-hand offsets) *is* ported as `recompute_character_sprite`, called by `World::update_character` right after the value recompute and marking the character's tile dirty (`mark_dirty_sector`) on an actual sprite change, matching C's `set_sector` call; `reset_name(cn)` (colored-name cache invalidation) remains an intentional no-op since Rust has no such cache. As of iteration 17/18 it is also wired into spell install/expiry (`world/spells.rs`), skill raising (`World::raise_skill`, stat-scroll `apply_item_driver_outcome`), player-death respawn (`World::die_character`), and login (`ugaris-server/src/snapshots.rs` + `main.rs`) - see the "Ralph Loop - `update_char` Stat Recomputation" sections below for the exact call-site history; this row's prose above predates that wiring and is kept for the original algorithm description. |
| `src/system/tool.c` `exp2level`/`level2exp`/`level_value`/`check_levelup` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/exp.rs` | `exp2level`/`level2exp`/`level_value` (the `pow(level,4)`/`sqrt(sqrt(exp))` formulas) are now the single canonical copy, replacing three independent duplicates that had accreted in `ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs`, `ugaris-server/src/area_apply.rs`, and `ugaris-core/src/item_driver/helpers.rs` (the latter now delegates to this module; the two server-crate copies were deleted and all call sites repointed). `World::check_levelup(character_id)` ports the level-increment loop over `max(exp, exp_used)`, the "Thou gained a level!" text, save grant/reset (hardcore resets to 0, others +1 capped at 10) with feedback text, the level-20 profession unlock (`value[1][V_PROFESSION] = 1`, guarded on it not already being set), and the `set_sector` dirty-map refresh. Wired into the killer-exp and `/god exp` grant paths via `ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs::give_exp_with_runtime_modifiers` (kept in the server crate since its `exp_modifier`/`hardcore_exp_bonus` multipliers are live-tunable `ServerRuntime` fields), gated on `!NOLEVEL` exactly like C. 13 focused tests (`world/tests/exp.rs` + 2 server-crate assertions in `tests/commands_admin.rs`). `World::give_exp(character_id, base_exp, area_id)` (C `give_exp` `tool.c:1371-1423`) is now the single canonical grant entry point in `ugaris-core`, applying the hardcore/global exp multipliers, `CF_NOEXP`/area-21 gate, `CF_NOLEVEL` exp-band clamp, decrease-prevention guard, and `check_levelup` tail call; as of iteration 24 every known exp-grant call site in the tree (killer exp, `/god exp`, `/milexp`, lollipop, demonshrine, the four random/zombie shrines, the warp-bonus reward-sphere/step-trickle grants, bookcase library-solved, staffer animation book, and the stat-scroll driver's `check_levelup`/`update_character` wiring) routes through `give_exp`/`check_levelup` instead of a raw `character.exp` mutation - `scrolls.rs::raise_value_exp` intentionally stays a raw `+=` since C's own `raise_value_exp` (`skill.c:353-354`) does too (it calls `check_levelup` directly, not `give_exp`). 13+ focused tests in `world/tests/exp.rs` plus per-call-site tests across `tests/commands_admin.rs`, `tests/area_apply.rs`, `item_driver/tests/*`. As of iteration 26, the level-10-multiple "Grats" broadcast (C `server_chat(6, ...)`, `tool.c:1347-1350`) is also ported: a new `World::queue_channel_broadcast`/`drain_pending_channel_broadcasts` (`world/text.rs`, `WorldChannelBroadcast { channel, message_bytes }`) queues the exact C byte sequence (`"0000000000"` + `COL_MAUVE` + text), and `ugaris-server`'s new `send_pending_world_channel_broadcasts` (`world_events.rs`, wired into the tick loop in `main.rs`) drains it each tick and fans it out to every session with that chat channel joined, reusing the same join-bit rule `apply_chat_command` uses for player channel messages. 3 new focused tests in `world/tests/exp.rs`. Remaining documented gaps (not silently dropped): `achievement_check_level` has no Rust equivalent (needs a general achievement engine), and `reset_name(cn)` is an intentional no-op (no server-side colored-name cache exists to invalidate). As of iteration 27, the P1 "Experience/level-up side effects" task is closed (`- [x]`): a full workspace re-audit found zero remaining raw `character.exp` grant mutations outside `give_exp`/`check_levelup` (the three raw-`exp` writers that remain - `raise_value_exp`'s bare `+=`, the potion/death exp-loss `saturating_sub`s, and `/setlevel`'s debug override - all correctly bypass `give_exp` because C does too). `achievement_check_level` is tracked separately under the P4 "Achievements" task; `reset_name` stays a documented no-op. |

| `src/module/achievements/achievement.c` / `achievement.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/achievement.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/snapshots.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Core data model and stat-driven award logic ported: the full 127-entry `AchievementType` enum + `achievement_defs` table (Steam ids/names/descriptions/categories/targets), `Achievement`/`AccountAchievements`/`AchievementStats` structs, `award`/`add_progress`/`get_stat_progress`/`area_to_pent_index`/`fix_all_stat_thresholds`, and every `achievement_add_*`/`achievement_check_*` stat-update function, as a standalone leaf module with no `World`/`PlayerRuntime`/networking access (same pattern as `crate::quest` before its own live wiring landed). As of iteration 66, persistence is wired: `PlayerRuntime::achievement_data`/`achievement_stats` fields hold the live account-side state; the `DRD_ACHIEVEMENT_DATA`(7176B)/`DRD_ACHIEVEMENT_STATS`(176B) legacy byte layouts (offsets verified against `achievement.h` via a C `sizeof`/`offsetof` probe) are encoded/decoded as subscriber-blob blocks in `ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, following the exact pattern `DRD_ACCOUNT_WIDE_DEPOT` established, and wired into `apply_character_snapshot`/`character_save_request`. Iteration 67 ported the `SV_ACH_*` mod-packet protocol (`crates/ugaris-protocol/src/mod_achievements.rs`) plus the login-time `DEFERRED_ACHIEVEMENTS` sync/award tick-loop sweep. Iteration 68 ported command dispatch: `apply_achievement_command` (`commands_player.rs`), wired into `main.rs`'s command if-let chain, handles all six C verbs (`command.c:9076-9227`) - `/achievements`/`/achstats` (player-accessible, self only, colored output incl. a UTC-approximated unlock date since this workspace has no `chrono` dependency - C uses `localtime()`) and the `CF_GOD`-gated `/achgive`/`/achfix`/`/achclear`/`/achsync` (the latter three take an optional target name defaulting to the caller, like `/reset`). `/achfix` uses the new `fix_all_stat_thresholds` to re-derive every stat-driven achievement from current totals without a fresh delta. A `send_raw_payloads_to_character` helper sends pre-built `SV_ACH_UNLOCK`/`SV_ACH_SYNC` packets directly via `runtime.send_to_session`, since `KeyringCommandResult`'s `target_message_bytes` field is always re-wrapped as `SV_TEXT` at drain time (correct for colored text, wrong for already-packetized binary payloads). Note: the C `DRD_ACHIEVEMENT_DATA`/`_STATS` ids are `PERSISTENT_SUBSCRIBER_DATA` (account-wide) but this port persists them per-character (same compromise the account-depot code already makes) pending a real multi-character-per-account model; `crate::player::AchievementState`'s small pre-existing chests/transport-only subset is still left untouched and unmerged with this model. Not yet done: `database_achievement.c`'s "first player globally" DB tracking + cross-server grats announcement, and most real gameplay call sites (chest opens, gathering, combat, mining, clans, professions, login streak/play time) that need to invoke the new `add_*`/`check_*` functions - see `ACHIEVEMENT_STATUS.txt` in the C repo for the full call-site list. As of iteration 69, one call site is wired: `ACHIEVEMENT_QUESTER` fires from `main.rs`'s `ClientAction::ReopenQuest` handler exactly when C's `questlog_reopen` leaves `ret` truthy (`QuestReopenResult::Reopened`). Iteration 70 wired both `achievement_add_chests` call sites from `src/module/base.c`: `chest_driver` (`base.c:1648-1654`, treasure chests, plus the treasure-#63/Mines-lvl-80-gold-room special case that awards `ACHIEVEMENT_GOLD_LOOTER` outright) and `randchest_driver` (`base.c:3168-3175`, random chests, fired for both the money and item reward branches - C calls it once per non-empty `in2` regardless of which branch produced it). Added a shared `award_chest_opened_achievement(world, runtime, character_id, gold_looter_treasure_index)` helper in `crates/ugaris-server/src/chests.rs` (gates on a live `PlayerRuntime` existing for the character, mirroring C's `ch[cn].flags & CF_PLAYER` check), called from both `ChestTreasureApplyResult::Granted` and `RandomChestApplyResult::{Money,Item}` in `main.rs`'s item-driver dispatch. 5 new tests in `tests/chests.rs` covering the sub-threshold stat bump, the Looter unlock at 10 chests with its `SV_ACH_UNLOCK` packet landing in the right session's `tick_out`, the Gold-Looter-only-on-#63 special case, and the no-`PlayerRuntime` no-op path. `RatChest` (`sewers.c`, a different C source file) does not call `achievement_add_chests` in C and was deliberately left unwired. Iteration 71 wired the "play time" call site (`player.c:3448-3462`, `player_update`) via `award_play_time_minute`, fired once per real-time minute for every connected character (see its own ledger section for the per-slot-stagger divergence note). Iteration 72 wired the "combat kill" call site (`death.c:417-422`, `kill_char`'s `achievement_add_enemy_killed`/`achievement_add_demons`) via a new `KillAchievementAward` queue and `award_enemy_killed_achievement`, fired for any kill scored by a player (broader than the sibling exp-grant condition - see its own ledger section). Iteration 73 wired two more `src/module/alchemy.c` call sites: `flower_driver` (`alchemy.c:1306-1315`, `IDR_FLOWER` - not the unrelated area-31 `IDR_PICKBERRY` driver, which never calls an achievement function in C), which awards `achievement_add_flowers`/`_mushrooms`/`_berries` based on the picked item's `drdata[0]` kind (1-7/8-16/17-20 respectively), via a new `award_gathering_achievement(world, runtime, character_id, kind)` helper called from the `PickAlchemyFlower` outcome's `Picked` arm in `main.rs`; and `flask_driver`'s `mixer()` success branch (`alchemy.c:1077-1082`), which awards `achievement_add_potions`, via a new `award_potion_brewed_achievement` helper called from the `FlaskMixed` outcome arm. Both helpers (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) follow the existing `award_chest_opened_achievement`/`award_play_time_minute` no-op-without-`PlayerRuntime` pattern. Added 8 focused tests in `tests/achievement.rs` (flower/mushroom/berry threshold unlocks by kind range, out-of-range kind no-op, potion-brewed threshold unlock, sub-threshold stat bump, and the no-`PlayerRuntime` no-op path for both helpers). Iteration 74 wired the weapon/magic/fighting skill-mastery call sites: C `raise_value`/`raise_value_exp` (`src/system/skill.c:204-266`,`:311-373`, the `CL_RAISE` and `IDR_STAT_SCROLL` paths respectively) both end with `achievement_check_skill(cn, v, ch[cn].value[1][v])` under a `CF_PLAYER` gate; `ugaris_core::achievement::check_skill` already existed and was tested but had no live call site. Added `award_skill_achievement(world, runtime, character_id, skill_type, skill_level)` following the same no-op-without-`PlayerRuntime` pattern, wired into `main.rs`'s `ClientAction::Raise` handler (using `RaiseSkillOutcome::Raised`'s `bare` field) and into a newly-split-out `ItemDriverOutcome::StatScrollUsed` match arm (previously grouped into a catch-all `executed += 1`-only arm) that reads the post-charge bare value from `world.characters`. Confirmed professions (`learn_prof`/`improve_prof`, C `professor.c`) are entirely unported in Rust, so `achievement_check_profession`'s call sites were correctly left out of this slice. Added 6 focused tests. Iteration 75 wired the "wealth beyond chests/trading" call site: C `achievement_add_gold_earned` is only ever called from 3 places - inside `give_money` (`tool.c:1459-1483`, the general NPC-reward/quest-completion gold-and-message helper used by 38 separate call sites across the C tree), inside `swap`'s `IF_MONEY` branch (`do.c:1285`), and inside `give_char_item_smart`'s silent branch (`tool.c:3422`) - and `give_money` itself had no Rust port at all. Added a byte-exact `give_money(world, runtime, character_id, amount, feedback_bytes)` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`): adds silver to `character.gold`, sets `CF_ITEMS`, builds the exact colored "You received <COL_YELLOW>amount<COL_RESET>. It has been placed in your gold pouch." message (`"%ds"`/`"%.2fG"` formatting matching `tool.c:1465-1469`) into `feedback_bytes`, and (gated on a live `PlayerRuntime`, mirroring C's `CF_PLAYER` check) calls `ugaris_core::achievement::add_gold_earned` with the silver-to-gold conversion done via integer division (`amount / 100`, matching C's `(unsigned int)(val / 100)` cast, a real precision-losing conversion in the original C, preserved as-is). Wired the one existing Rust call site that maps 1:1 to a real C `give_money` call: `warpbonus_driver`'s reward-kind-4 branch (`area/25/warped.c:434-436`) in `main.rs`'s `WarpBonus` outcome match arm, replacing its previous silent direct `character.gold +=` mutation with no message/achievement tracking. The ~11 other pre-existing scattered Rust gold-mutation sites (bank, auction, merchant trade, GM commands, death) were confirmed to be transfers/fees/resets that C's own code never routes through `give_money` either, so they were correctly left alone. Added 5 focused tests. Still unwired: (3) DB first-unlock/grats announcement; the ~37 other `give_money` call sites, all inside NPC/area dialogue drivers not yet ported (each its own P4 area task); mining reward RNG; professions; exploration beyond transport; clans; military; tunnels; arena PvP; pentagram solve reward. Iteration 76 wired the stone-pickup call site: C `act_take` (`src/system/act.c:305-327`)'s `if (it[in].ID == IID_ALCHEMY_INGREDIENT) { ... achievement_add_stones(cn, 0/1/2, 1); }` block, keyed on the picked item's `drdata[0]` (23/24 = Earth, 21 = Fire, 22 = Ice), which only runs when the sibling `keyring_try_auto_add` did *not* consume the item (that C branch `free_item`s and `return`s early, skipping the stone check entirely). Added `award_stone_pickup_achievement(world, runtime, character_id, stone_drdata)` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, same no-op-without-`PlayerRuntime` pattern as the sibling `award_*` helpers), wired into `main.rs`'s TAKE-completion loop (the same block that already runs `apply_keyring_auto_add_pickup`), gated on `keyring_result` not being `Added` and the taken item's `template_id == IID_ALCHEMY_INGREDIENT`. Also confirmed via a fresh C grep that `achievement_add_pvp_kill`/`achievement_add_military_mission` have zero call sites anywhere in the legacy C tree (dead code in C itself, not just unported) - the "arena PvP"/"military" gaps noted by earlier iterations are therefore not real porting gaps and can be dropped from the remaining-work list. Added 5 focused tests in `tests/achievement.rs` (Earth-stone unlock at 50 for both drdata 23 and 24, Fire-stone unlock at 100, Ice-stone unlock at 1000, an out-of-range-drdata no-op, and the no-`PlayerRuntime` no-op path). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (468 server [+5], all other crates unchanged, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, and a 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Still unwired: (3) DB first-unlock/grats announcement; ~37 other `give_money` call sites (P4 area tasks); mining reward RNG; professions; exploration beyond transport; clans; tunnels; pentagram solve reward. (PvP-kill and military-mission achievements are legacy dead code, not gaps.) Iteration 77 closed the DB half of gap (3): C `achievement_award`'s tail (`achievement.c:610-631`) calls `database_achievement.c`'s `db_achievement_record_unlock` (upserts `achievement_firsts`, inserts `achievement_history`, returns whether this was the first global unlock) and, if so, `achievement_announce_first` (a channel-6 `server_chat` "Grats: NAME is the FIRST to unlock ACH!" broadcast). Ported as a new `ugaris-db` repository - `migrations/0007_achievement_firsts.sql` + `crates/ugaris-db/src/achievement.rs`'s `AchievementRepository`/`PgAchievementRepository::record_unlock`, wired into `Database::achievements()` - keyed by `character_id` instead of C's `subscriber_id` (no live multi-character-per-account model yet, same compromise `DRD_ACHIEVEMENT_DATA`'s per-character persistence already makes); Postgres's `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` has no `mysql_affected_rows()`-equivalent, so `RETURNING (xmax = 0) AS is_first` is used instead. Confirmed via a fresh grep that `db_achievement_get_first`/`_get_unlock_count`/`_get_recent_firsts` are dead code in C (zero call sites outside their own file), so only `record_unlock` was ported. Added `record_achievement_firsts_and_announce` (async, `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) which awaits the DB call then reuses the existing `World::queue_channel_broadcast`/`send_pending_world_channel_broadcasts` pipeline (the same one `check_levelup`'s level-milestone grats message already uses) for the announcement - no new broadcast plumbing needed. Wired at 2 of the ~6 unlock call sites (both already inline in `main.rs`'s async tick loop, so no sync-to-async refactor was needed): the login achievement sweep (`StartedUgaris`/`check_level`/`check_exploration`/`check_login_streak`) and the questlog-reopen `Quester` award. `achievement_repository: Option<PgAchievementRepository>` added to `main()`'s startup tuple alongside the sibling repositories. Added 2 tests to `achievement.rs`'s `send_tests` (no-op without a configured repository; no-op for an empty unlock list) and 2 to the new `ugaris-db::achievement` module (a static-SQL guard for the `xmax` idiom, and a `DATABASE_URL`-gated live round trip - first call reports `is_first = true`, second reports `false`, `total_unlocks`/`achievement_history` both land correctly). Still unwired: the other ~7 unlock call sites (`award_play_time_minute`/`award_enemy_killed_achievement`/`award_gathering_achievement`/`award_potion_brewed_achievement`/`award_skill_achievement`/`award_stone_pickup_achievement`/`give_money`'s gold-earned award/`chests.rs`'s chest-opened award, plus `/achgive`) each build their own inline unlock-send loop and would need an `async fn` signature change (touching their existing sync unit tests) to get the same treatment - left for a future slice; ~37 other `give_money` call sites; mining reward RNG; professions; exploration beyond transport; clans; tunnels; pentagram solve reward. Iteration 78 finished wiring gap (3) (DB first-unlock/grats announcement) into every remaining unlock call site (the previous iteration only reached 2 of ~8): converted `award_play_time_minute`/`award_enemy_killed_achievement`/`award_gathering_achievement`/`award_potion_brewed_achievement`/`award_skill_achievement`/`give_money`/`award_stone_pickup_achievement` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) and `award_chest_opened_achievement` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/chests.rs`) to `async fn`, each now calling `record_achievement_firsts_and_announce` internally right after sending its own `SV_ACH_UNLOCK` packet(s), mirroring C `achievement_award` doing the client packet, DB record, and grats announce in one call. Updated all 9 call sites in `main.rs` and all 46 existing unit tests across `tests/achievement.rs`/`tests/chests.rs` to `#[tokio::test]`/`async fn` (passing `&None` for the repository, matching the existing no-repository-configured no-op convention) - no test assertions changed, only signatures/call sites; no sync-to-async refactor of any enclosing function was needed since every call site already lived inside `async fn main()`'s tick loop body. This closes gap (3) completely. Still unwired: the `/achgive` GM command's unlock loop (`commands_player.rs` is a synchronous command-dispatch function - would need its own async refactor, left for a future slice); ~37 other `give_money` call sites (P4 area tasks); mining reward RNG; professions; exploration beyond transport; clans; tunnels; pentagram solve reward. Iteration 79 closed that last known gap (3) spot: made `apply_achievement_command` (`commands_player.rs`) an `async fn` taking `world: &mut World` and a new `repository: &Option<PgAchievementRepository>` parameter, mirroring the sibling `award_*` helpers' shape; `/achgive`'s single award and `/achfix`'s multi-award batch both now call `record_achievement_firsts_and_announce` right after sending their `SV_ACH_UNLOCK` packet(s), matching C `achievement_award`'s unconditional DB-record/announce tail (`show_congrats` only gates the chat text, not the DB call). `achclear`/`achsync` correctly stay untouched (C's `achievement_clear_all`/`_sync_all` never touch the DB). The one `main.rs` call site (already inside `async fn main`'s tick loop) needed only `&mut world`/`&achievement_repository`/`.await` added. Updated all 12 existing achievement-command tests to `#[tokio::test]`/`async fn` with `&None` repository (no assertion changes, signatures only). Gap (3) is now closed across every known unlock call site in the codebase, gameplay and GM-command alike. Remaining unwired achievement work is entirely gated on unported source systems: mining reward RNG, professions, exploration beyond transport, clans, tunnels, pentagram solve reward - each needs its own area/system task ported first. Iteration 80 wired the "Trader deal" call site: C `trader_driver`'s "accept trade" success branch (`base.c:4416-4428`) fires `achievement_award(..., ACHIEVEMENT_TRUST_BUT_VERIFY, 1)` independently for both trading partners once both sides accept - the Trader NPC's trade state machine (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/trader.rs`) was already fully ported except this one call, which its own module doc comment explicitly flagged as deferred. Added `TraderEvent::DealCompleted { c1_id, c2_id }`, queued in the same branch that already swaps items and says "Deal.", following the module's existing `ShowTrade`/`ItemAddedToTrade` deferred-event pattern for concerns that need the `ugaris-server` crate. Added `award_trader_deal_achievement`/`award_single_trader_achievement` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) - since C calls the bare `achievement_award` primitive directly here (no stat-based helper exists for this achievement), the new code calls `AccountAchievements::award` directly per character (the same primitive `main.rs`'s pre-existing `Quester` call site uses), independently no-op-safe per side without a live `PlayerRuntime` (mirrors C's per-side `find_char_byID` null check). Converted `apply_trader_events` (`world_events.rs`) to an `async fn` taking `runtime`/`repository` (previously `world`-only), matching `apply_bank_events`'s existing shape, to consume the new event; the one `main.rs` call site needed only the extra arguments and `.await` added, no surrounding refactor. Added 2 core tests (`world/tests/trader.rs`) and 3 server tests (`tests/achievement.rs`). A research pass this iteration also confirmed the other remaining gaps are all still genuinely blocked on unported gameplay systems (not achievement-side plumbing): mining reward RNG (`mine.c`'s `handle_mining_result`/`handle_silver_find`/`handle_gold_find` cascade has zero Rust port - only the wall-digging mechanic itself is ported), pentagram solve/lucky-pent state (`pents.c`'s `pentagram_player_data`/`distribute_rewards_to_player`/`handle_lucky_pentagram`/`check_for_color_combo` have zero Rust port - only pentagram activation/door access is ported), and clan/club founding/membership (`clan.c`'s `add_member`, `area/30/clanmaster.c`'s clan-founding branch, and `clubmaster.c`'s club-founding/joining branches all have zero Rust port - only the GM `/joinclan`/`/joinclub` debug cheat mutates the relevant fields today) - each is tracked by its own "Area 12"/"Area 4"/"Clan system" P4 task below and must land before its achievement call sites can be wired. Iteration 81 wired the second of the three `achievement_add_gold_earned` call sites iteration 75 had identified but left open: C `swap`'s `IF_MONEY` branch (`src/system/do.c:1276-1287`) - a money item held on the cursor is never actually placed in the target slot on swap, it's destroyed and its value credited straight to `ch[cn].gold`, then the wealth-ladder achievement fires under the `CF_PLAYER` gate. This was a genuine behavior gap, not just an unwired achievement call: `inventory_swap_slot` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/inventory.rs`) had no `IF_MONEY` handling at all, so money items were previously placed into inventory slots like any other item, which C never allows. Added the money check/gold-credit/item-destroy inline in `inventory_swap_slot`, preserving C's exact order (the slot's original occupant still lands on the cursor via `ch[cn].citem = ch[cn].item[pos]`, which runs before the money check); added `InventoryCommandResult::MoneyConverted { price }` so the `main.rs` call site can both refresh the inventory and award the achievement; added `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, same no-op-without-`PlayerRuntime`/DB-first-unlock-announce shape as every sibling `award_*` helper), wired into the `ClientAction::Swap` match arm. `stats_update`/`dlog` remain unported (same documented omission as `give_money`). The third call site, `give_char_item_smart`'s silent branch (`tool.c:3422`), has no direct Rust equivalent function at all - the closest analog (`grant_template_item_smart`) only ever grants scroll/orb templates today, never money-item templates, so wiring an achievement call there now would be speculative and untested; left for whichever future task first grants a money-item template. Added 6 focused tests (3 in `tests/inventory.rs` covering the conversion with an empty/occupied target slot and the worn-slot `can_wear` rejection; 3 in `tests/achievement.rs` covering the wealth-ladder unlock, sub-100-silver no-op, and no-`PlayerRuntime` no-op, mirroring `give_money`'s existing test shapes). Iteration 82 wired the `ACHIEVEMENT_SLAYER_OF_DEMON_LORDS` call site: C `give_first_kill` (`src/system/death.c:196-254`), the `kill_char`-adjacent "first time this NPC class was ever killed" congrats/reward function, had zero Rust port at all (not just the achievement half) - discovered while investigating why this achievement call site was still unwired, since unlike the other remaining gaps (mining/professions/clans/military/tunnels/arena/pentagram) it isn't blocked on a whole unported gameplay system, just this one un-ported function. Porting it required adding the missing `ch.class` concept to the runtime `Character` struct first (`crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs`'s new `class: i32` field, populated from the zone-template `CharacterTemplate.class` field at NPC instantiation in `zone.rs` - that template field already existed and was parsed but silently dropped on the floor at spawn time; always `0` for players). Added `PlayerRuntime::first_kill_ppd` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`) as the real typed backing for `DRD_FIRSTKILL_PPD` (moved out of the "9 unmodeled ids, `del_data`-only" doc group into its own `encode_legacy_firstkill_ppd`/`decode_legacy_firstkill_ppd`/`mark_first_kill`/`count_demon_lord_kills` codec, following the exact `misc_ppd` raw-blob pattern) - a flat 128-byte/1024-bit little-endian bitmask (`class / 8` byte, `class % 8` bit) equivalent to C's `unsigned int kill[32]` word/bit-offset math. `World::kill_character_followup` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/death.rs`) now queues a `FirstKillCheck { killer_id, victim_class, victim_level, victim_has_name, victim_name }` whenever a player kills an NPC with `ch.class` in C's `1..=1023` guard range (the class-range and `CF_PLAYER` guards run there, since only the server crate owns `PlayerRuntime`); a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs::apply_first_kill_check` drains it, mirrors C's bit-test/set + `kill_score * 5` bonus exp (`world.give_exp`) + the four-way `log_char` congrats-message dispatch (`CF_HASNAME` / 14 named-monster class ranges / demon-lord ranges `258..=305`+`404..=411` / generic fallback, copied digit-for-digit) + `count_demon_lord_kills(ppd) >= 20` triggering `AchievementType::SlayerOfDemonLords` through a newly-generalized `award_bare_achievement` helper (renamed from the trader-specific `award_single_trader_achievement`, now shared by both the trader-deal and first-kill call sites since C calls the same bare `achievement_award` primitive at both spots). The `get_army_rank_int(cn)` branch inside the demon-lord case (which would otherwise pick a different message string and grant `give_military_pts_no_npc`) always takes the "no army rank" path, since army ranks are a wholly separate unported P3 system ("Military ranks") - documented as a deliberate simplification, not a bug, matching this task's existing convention of noting cross-system gaps rather than half-porting them. Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing `drain_pending_kill_achievements` loop. All 13 pre-existing test-only `Character { .. }` literals across the workspace needed a new `class: 0,` field added (mechanical, no behavior change). Added 9 new `ugaris-core` tests (`player.rs`: `mark_first_kill`/`count_demon_lord_kills`/encode-decode round trip/`clear_turn_seyan_ppd` now truly clears+omits the block; `world/tests/death.rs`: `FirstKillCheck` queuing for a set/unset class and a non-player killer) and 7 new `ugaris-server` tests (`tests/achievement.rs`: generic/named-monster/`CF_HASNAME` congrats message text, no-op on a repeat kill of the same class, the 20th-unique-demon-lord unlock with its `SV_ACH_UNLOCK` packet, and the no-`PlayerRuntime` no-op path); one existing `player.rs` test (`clear_turn_seyan_ppd_strips_unmapped_ids_but_keeps_other_raw_blocks`) was updated to exercise `DRD_RANK_PPD` instead of `DRD_FIRSTKILL_PPD` for its "still has no Rust representation, still raw-block-stripped" assertion, since the latter now has a real codec. `cargo fmt --all` clean, `cargo test --workspace` (1404 ugaris-core [+7 net across both edited/added tests] + 38 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 485 server [+7], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, and a 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this change touches the tick loop's kill-achievement-drain call site). Remaining unwired achievement work is now down to gameplay call sites gated on wholly unported source systems: mining reward RNG, professions, exploration beyond transport, clans, military (beyond this task's own documented army-rank-message simplification), tunnels, arena, pentagram solve reward - each needs its own area/system task ported first. Iteration 83 wired the last self-contained gap, `check_levelup`'s `achievement_check_level` call site (see its own ledger section below). Iteration 84 closed the task (`[x]`): a fresh exhaustive grep across every C file that calls into `achievement.c` confirmed every reachable call site is wired and every remaining one (`area/4/pents.c`'s pentagram reward mechanic, `area/33/tunnel.c`'s tunnel-level counter, `area/12/mine.c`'s mining reward cascade, `common/professor.c`'s profession checks) is blocked purely on its own host system having zero Rust port, not on any achievement-side gap; the earlier "clans/clubs" item was a documentation overstatement (`achievement.c` has no clan call site in the current C tree) and was dropped. Added a one-line cross-reference note to each of the four affected P4 tasks in `PORTING_TODO.md` (Area 4, Area 12, Area 33, Common NPCs) pointing back at this module's `award_*` helper pattern for whoever ports that system next. No functional code changed this iteration. |

| `src/system/clan.c` / `clan.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/do_action.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanmaster.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanclerk.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_log.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs` | Second self-contained slice adds clan identity + membership on top of the first slice's pure relation state machine. `ClanRelation` (`CS_*` levels 1-5), `ClanRelations` (per-pair `current_relation`/`want_relation`/`want_date` over `MAX_CLAN`=32 clan slots plus an `exists` flag standing in for `clan[n].name[0]`), `found_clan`/`delete_clan` (relation reset to Neutral in both directions), `set_relation` (unilateral relation request, only bumps the want-timestamp on an actual change), `may_enter`/`can_attack_outside`/`can_attack_inside`/`alliance` (read-only policy queries), and the full `update_relations` daily tick (immediate-agreement fast path plus the 1h one-sided war-escalation / 24h one-sided alliance-treaty-feud de-escalation timers, all 7 distinct C log-message transition shapes modeled as `ClanRelationChange` variants) are ported with 21 focused tests. `ClanRelations` implements `do_action::ClanAttackPolicy` (`are_allied`/`can_attack_inside_clan_area`/`can_attack_outside_clan_area`), closing those three stubs that previously only had `NoClanAttackPolicy` (always-false); one new end-to-end test (`can_attack_wired_against_real_clan_relations_registry` in `do_action.rs`) confirms it satisfies the pre-existing trait/call-site plumbing identically to the hand-written `TestClanPolicy`. Intentionally skipped: the `dungeon.doraid` relation clamp inside `update_relations` (documented in a code comment as dead in practice after a clan's first tick, and meaningless without the unported dungeon/raid system). New this iteration: `ClanIdentity` (`name`/`rank_names[5]`/`website`/`message`) and `ClanRegistry` (wraps `ClanRelations`, adds a per-slot `u32` serial plus `Option<ClanIdentity>` array), porting `found_clan` (first-free-slot allocation + `clan_standards` default rank names + relation reset, with `NameTooLong`/`ClanListFull` errors), `delete_clan` (generalizes the bankrupt-clan deletion path, bumping the slot serial), `get_char_clan`/`get_char_clan_name` (validates `Character.clan`/`.clan_serial` against the registry, clearing all three fields on mismatch exactly like C; treats `clan >= CLUB_OFFSET`=1024 as out of jurisdiction rather than invalid), `add_member`/`remove_member` (wired onto `Character.clan`/`.clan_rank`/`.clan_serial` - confirmed `add_member` never sets `clan_rank`, matching C), and the identity mutators `set_clan_rankname`/`set_clan_website`/`set_clan_message` (length-cap validation ported; the C trailing-character-strip quirk on website/message text is deferred to the future `/clan` command-parsing task since it depends on a raw command-line calling convention that doesn't exist in Rust yet, documented inline rather than guessed). 20 new focused unit tests (plus a new `test_character()` fixture mirroring `spell.rs`'s) cover slot reuse/serial invalidation after delete+refound, club-number rejection, stale-reference clearing, and identity mutator validation. Not yet ported: treasury/bonus/dungeon-guard economy (`update_treasure`/`update_training`/`struct clan_dungeon` - jewels, cost-per-week/debt, bonuses, dungeon guard counts, potions - `struct clan`'s remaining fields), any `crates/ugaris-db` repository/migration (no DB table exists), actually constructing/using a live `ClanRelations` instance anywhere in `World`/the server tick loop (it exists only as a type with tests so far), clan-log persistence (`add_clanlog`), `clan_trade_bonus` (blocked on the unported merchant system), clan chat channel gating, and clan-hall transport access beyond direct membership. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Clan system" P3 entry for the itemized remaining list. New this iteration (87): `World` gained a real `clan_registry: ClanRegistry` field, and all four live combat call sites that previously always used `NoClanAttackPolicy` now use it - `World::player_can_attack_target`/the `PAC_KILL` setup path (both via `world::combat::RuntimePlayerAttackPolicy` gaining a `clan_relations: &ClanRelations` field alongside its existing PK-hate field) and the two spell/effect-tick attack-policy closures in `ugaris-server/src/main.rs` (whose own `RuntimePlayerAttackPolicy` copy in `world_events.rs` got the same treatment, cloning `world.clan_registry.relations()` once before each tick call since the closures can't hold a live `&World` borrow across the `&mut World` tick methods). Behavior is a no-op today (every character's `clan` defaults to `0`), but is now real, tested plumbing: two new `world/tests/combat.rs` tests (`world_kill_setup_allows_clan_feud_attack_without_pk_flags`, `world_kill_setup_blocks_neutral_clan_attack_without_pk_flags`) prove a clan feud between two founded clans exempts non-`PK`-flagged `PLAYER` characters from the ordinary PK-flag requirement, while a neutral relation still blocks it. Iteration 88 added `ClanRegistry::set_name` (`clan_setname`, `clan.c:1419-1423` - unlike every other identity mutator this one never rejects an over-long name, just silently truncates to 78 bytes, and (unlike C's always-allocated static array) requires the clan to already exist, consistent with `set_rankname`/`set_website`/`set_message`) and wired the first two real GM commands onto the registry in `commands_admin.rs`: `/killclan <nr>` (`command.c:6468-6482`, `CF_GOD`-gated, no player feedback either way, matching C) now calls `ClanRegistry::delete_clan` directly instead of a no-op - since `update_treasure`'s weekly bankruptcy tick (the thing C's `kill_clan` actually sets up via a 9999-jewel debt spike) isn't ported, this produces the eventual real-world outcome (clan deleted) without the week-long delay, documented inline as a deliberate simplification; and `/renclan <nr> <name>` (`cmd_renclan`, `command.c:4497-4531`, `CF_STAFF|CF_GOD`-gated, Aston-area-3-only per C) now calls `ClanRegistry::set_name`.
