Also fixed the pre-existing `/joinclan` cheat to read `clan_serial` from `world.clan_registry.serial(nr)` instead of a hardcoded `0` (a no-op today since no command populates a nonzero serial yet, but now reads through the registry like C's `clan_serial(nr)` instead of bypassing it). 5 new tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (killclan deletes/require-god/out-of-range no-op, renclan renames/rejects-outside-Aston/rejects-unknown-clan/requires-staff-or-god) plus 3 new `clan.rs` unit tests for `set_name`. `add_member`/`found_clan`/`remove_member` still have no live command wiring (clan founding is an NPC-dialogue flow, `area/30/clanmaster.c`, not a `/clan` command - a separate, larger future task), and the full treasury/dungeon-guard economy, DB persistence, clan-log, achievement-on-join, `clan_trade_bonus`, chat channel gating, and clan-hall-beyond-membership gaps are all unchanged - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Clan system" entry. Iteration 89 closed the "no DB repository/migration exists" gap: added `crates/ugaris-db/src/clan.rs` (`ClanRegistryRepository`/`PgClanRegistryRepository`) + `migrations/0008_clan_registry.sql`, a single-row `clan_registry(id smallint primary key, registry_json jsonb, updated_at)` table storing the whole `ClanRegistry` (already fully `Serialize`/`Deserialize`) as one JSON blob, mirroring how C's own `struct clan clan[MAXCLAN]` only ever survives a restart as part of the single memory-image world save file - not as individual relational rows (the only real per-row clan table C has, `clanoverview`, is a write-only external-website mirror of a handful of display fields, a different concern, not ported). Wired into `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`: `Database::clans()` added alongside the sibling repository constructors; `world.clan_registry` is loaded from the DB once at startup (before "entering Rust game loop", replacing the freshly-`Default` registry only if a row exists) and saved back on the same once-a-minute maintenance cadence already used for auction cleanup/play-time credit (`world.tick.0 % (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 60) == 0`) - REMAINING: add a `clan_changed`-style dirty flag to `ClanRegistry` (mirroring C's own flag of the same name) so the periodic save can skip unchanged ticks instead of always rewriting the whole (currently tiny) registry. 5 new tests in `crates/ugaris-db/src/clan.rs` (a static-SQL guard for the singleton-row upsert/select shape, a `serde_json` round-trip of a founded clan, and 3 `DATABASE_URL`-gated live tests: save-then-load round trip with before/after state restoration so repeated runs don't clobber shared test-DB state, and the empty-database `None` case). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1449 ugaris-core + 42 db [+5] + 3 net + 37 protocol + 494 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration adds an `.await` in the startup path and the tick loop's once-a-minute maintenance block). Runtime wiring for `add_member`/`found_clan` from a real command, clan-log, achievement-on-join, `clan_trade_bonus`, chat channel gating, clan-hall-beyond-membership, and the treasury/dungeon economy all still need their own future slices - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Clan system" entry. Iteration 90 closed the "no dirty flag" gap iteration 89 left open: ported C's `static int clan_changed` (`clan.c:61`) as a `#[serde(skip)] dirty: bool` field on `ClanRegistry`, set on every successful mutation (`found_clan`/`delete_clan`/`set_rankname`/`set_website`/`set_message`/`set_name`, plus conservatively on every `relations_mut()` call since it hands out an unguarded `&mut ClanRelations`) with `dirty()`/`clear_dirty()` accessors mirroring C's own read (`clan.c:416`) and post-save clear (`clan.c:430`); `#[serde(skip)]` means a registry just loaded from the DB always starts clean rather than forcing an immediate redundant save. The once-a-minute periodic save in `main.rs` now checks `dirty()` before calling `save_registry` and only calls `clear_dirty()` after a successful save (a failed save leaves it dirty so the next tick retries). 11 new unit tests in `clan.rs` (dirty-on-mutation for each mutator, no-dirty-on-each-mutator's-failure-path, `clear_dirty`, `relations_mut` marking dirty, and a `serde_json` round-trip proving the flag doesn't survive (de)serialization). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1458 ugaris-core [+11] + 42 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 494 server, all green), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 91 closed the read/admin-clear half of the "clan-log persistence/message formatting" REMAINING item: ported C's `clanlog` SQL table (`add_clanlog`/`lookup_clanlog`/`db_read_clanlog`, `database_notes.c:74-153,283-345`) as `crates/ugaris-db/src/clan_log.rs` (`ClanLogRepository`/`PgClanLogRepository`, `migrations/0009_clan_log.sql` - a real per-row table, unlike the single-blob `clan_registry` migration, since C's own `clanlog` table is already fully relational) and `src/system/clanlog.c`'s `/clanlog` command (full `-p`/`-c`/`-x`/`-s`/`-e`/`-i`/`-h` flag parser in the new `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_log.rs`, the "priority > 20 forces your own clan" gate, the 51-row "not all entries displayed" cutoff hint, and the "Former clan N" fallback when a row's stored serial no longer matches the live registry) plus `command.c`'s `/clearclanlog` GM command - including a real legacy bug preserved verbatim (`command.c:7550-7556` checks `execute_query`'s success/failure return backwards, so a successful delete reports "Failed to clear clan log" and a failed one reports "... cleared"). `-p <player>` only resolves currently-online characters (no persistent name index exists in Rust yet), matching C's "unresolved name -> command not recognized" fallback exactly. The *write* side (`add_clanlog` itself, now `ClanLogRepository::add_entry`) has zero live call sites still: every C call site sits on a clan mutation this codebase either has no command wiring for yet (`found_clan`/`add_member`) or doesn't tick at all yet (the daily relation-transition tick has no game-loop caller - a separate gap, noted for whoever wires it next). 22 new `ugaris-server` tests (`tests/clan_log.rs`) + 5 new `ugaris-db` tests (2 static-SQL guards, 3 `DATABASE_URL`-gated live tests). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1458 ugaris-core + 47 db [+5] + 3 net + 37 protocol + 516 server [+22], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (adds a command-dispatch call site to the tick loop). Iteration 92 closed the "clan chat channel gating" gap: `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_chat.rs::apply_chat_command`'s channel-12 (`ClanA`, alliance chat) delivery filter and its `[SPY/ALLIANCE]` forward's would-see-normally check were both exact-same-clan-only, missing C's `clan_alliance` fallback (`chat.c:284`,`:184-193`); both now call `world.clan_registry.relations().alliance(sender_clan, target.clan)`. Channels 5/7 needed no change (5 has no clan-specific gating beyond the already-ported join-first rule; 7 is genuinely exact-clan-only in C). 2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_chat.rs`. `cargo test --workspace` (1458 ugaris-core + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 518 server [+2], all green), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 93 closed the "clan-hall transport access beyond direct membership" gap: `ClanRelations::may_enter` (`clan.c:881-905`) already existed and was fully tested, but `crates/ugaris-server/src/transport.rs`'s own `may_enter_clan` helper (backing both the `SV_TELEPORT` per-clan access-mask bits and the actual clan-hall travel gate, `transport.c:185-223`) never called it - it only checked `character.clan == clan` (exact membership), so an allied non-member could never see or use an allied clan's hall, unlike C. `may_enter_clan` now takes `&World` and delegates to `world.clan_registry.relations().may_enter(character.clan, clan)`; both call sites (`transport_clan_access`'s mask loop and `resolve_transport_travel_with_random`'s travel gate) updated to pass `world` through. 2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/transport.rs` (`transport_clan_travel_allows_allied_clan_hall_not_just_direct_member` proves an allied non-member now reaches the hall; `transport_clan_travel_blocks_merely_neutral_clan_hall` proves a merely-neutral relation still blocks travel with C's exact `"You may not enter (N)."` text). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1458 ugaris-core + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 520 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 94 closed the "`add_member`/`found_clan`/`remove_member` command wiring", "clan-log write persistence", and "achievement-on-join wiring" gaps together by porting `src/area/30/clanmaster.c`'s `clanmaster_driver` (the clan foundations NPC, `CDR_CLANMASTER`/27) as a self-contained slice in the new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanmaster.rs`: the `name:`/`accept:`/`join:`/`leave!` free-text keyword handshake, the Clan Jewel `NT_GIVE` handoff that completes founding (`found_clan`), the accept/join handshake (`add_member`), `leave!` (`remove_member`), the generic small-talk qa table (`CLANMASTER_QA`), the periodic greeting, the idle-murmur table, and the 12h driver-memory clear timer - plus `character_driver.rs`'s new `CDR_CLANMASTER`/`ClanmasterDriverData`/`ClanFoundData` (the latter stored on the *player* being talked to via the existing generic `driver_state` slot - a new case for this codebase, documented inline as safe since no other feature uses a player's `driver_state`) and `zone.rs`'s spawn wiring. This is the first live caller of `ClanRegistry::found_clan`/`add_member`/`remove_member` (previously only reachable via the `/joinclan`/`/killclan`/`/renclan` GM cheats). Since the pure `ClanRegistry` methods deliberately leave clan-log persistence and achievement awards to the caller, this iteration also added `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_log.rs::write_clan_log_entry` (the first caller of `ClanLogRepository::add_entry`, previously implemented and tested but never invoked) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs::award_clanmaster_member_achievement`/`award_clanmaster_master_achievement`, both wired through a new `ClanmasterEvent` queue/`apply_clanmaster_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, mirroring the existing `TraderEvent`/`apply_trader_events` split since `World` has no `ServerRuntime`/DB access), called from `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `process_trader_actions`. Deliberately out of scope for this slice (documented inline and in `PORTING_TODO.md`'s REMAINING notes): `rank:`/`fire:` (leader rank management plus the offline-player `task_set_clan_rank`/`task_fire_from_clan` async DB-task fallback), `CDR_CLANCLERK` (`clanclerk_driver`, the members-only economy driver - needs 8+ new `clan.rs` functions for money/jewel-vault-counts/bonuses/relation-by-command/raid-flags that don't exist yet), and club founding/joining (`get_char_club` approximated as a bare `clan >= CLUB_OFFSET` range check since `club.c` isn't ported - this driver never actually joins anyone to a club in C either). Two deviations matching established precedent, documented inline: the `NT_GIVE` "try to give the item back first" `give_driver`/`dat->give_try` fallback is simplified to an unconditional `destroy_item` (same as `world/bank.rs`/`world/merchant.rs`); `secure_move_driver` is ported via the same `setup_walk_toward`/`turn` fallback `world/bank.rs` already established. 21 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/clanmaster.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1479 ugaris-core [+21] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 520 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 95 ported the treasury/bonus economy half of `struct clan` (`clan.h:22-39,66-87`): new `ClanTreasure` (`jewels`/`cost_per_week`/`debt`/`payed_till`) and `ClanEconomy` (bonus levels over `MAX_BONUS`=14, depot money, treasure, plus `training_score`/`last_training_update` pulled out of `struct clan_dungeon`) structs embedded as a new `economy` field on `ClanIdentity`, initialized by `clan_standards`'s treasury reset inside `ClanIdentity::standard`. New `ClanRegistry` methods: `clan_money`/`clan_money_change` (`get_clan_money`/`clan_money_change`, `clan.c:222-244`, returning a `ClanMoneyChange` log-event enum rather than logging itself, matching this module's established pattern), `jewel_count`/`add_jewel`/`swap_jewels` (`cnt_jewels`/`add_jewel`/`swap_jewels`, `clan.c:494-513` - confirmed and tested that `swap_jewels` only ever charges debt to the source, never decrementing its jewel count directly), `bonus_level`/`set_bonus_level` (`get_clan_bonus`/`set_clan_bonus`, `clan.c:518-520,536-544` - the `doraid` gate on bonus slot 3 intentionally skipped, same reasoning as the existing `update_relations` `doraid` skip), free function `bonus_name` (`get_bonus_name`, `clan.c:522-527`), and the two periodic ticks `update_treasure`/`update_training` (`clan.c:1105-1182`): the C `do`/`while` bonus-affordability-shrinking loop translated literally (including its "check against the pre-reduction cost" subtlety), weekly cost recomputation (flat `CLANHALLRENT` rent + bonus upkeep), 5-minutes-late debt accrual, debt auto-payoff with jewels, and bankrupt-clan deletion (`debt >= 2000`) - returning `ClanTreasuryEvent::PaidDebtWithJewels`/`WentBroke` for the caller to log (only `WentBroke` is a real player-facing `add_clanlog` entry in C; `update_training`'s `xlog` is server-debug-only). Like `update_relations`, neither tick has a live game-loop caller yet - this iteration is data-model-and-logic only, no runtime wiring. 19 new unit tests covering the bonus-name table, economy defaults on founding, money-change threshold-gated logging, jewel add/swap semantics, bonus get/set validation, and every `update_treasure`/`update_training` branch. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1498 ugaris-core [+19] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 520 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (run as a sanity check even though not strictly required). REMAINING as of iteration 95: the dungeon-guard economy proper (guard counts/potions/raid flags), `CDR_CLANCLERK` driver wiring, `rank:`/`fire:`, club founding/joining, and `clan_trade_bonus` (blocked on the unported merchant system). Iteration 96 ported `CDR_CLANCLERK` (`clanclerk_driver`, `area/30/clanmaster.c:662-1213`) in a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanclerk.rs`: the rank-gated `help` text, `deposit` (any nearby player)/`withdraw` (treasurer+), the C-dead-code `buy` ("disabled" reply), leader-rank+ `set bonus`/`rank name`/`website`/`message`/`relation`/`raiding on`/`raiding off`/`raiding god on`/`raiding god off`, the Clan Jewel `NT_GIVE` handoff, and the "jewels" qa small-talk reply - plus the driver plumbing (`CDR_CLANCLERK`/`ClanclerkDriverData`/`parse_clanclerk_driver_args` in `character_driver.rs`, zone-template wiring in `zone.rs`). Closed two real gaps this needed: `ClanEconomy` gained `raid`/`raid_on_start` (C's `struct clan_dungeon`'s `doraid`/`raidonstart`, pulled out on their own like iteration 95's `training_score`) plus `ClanRegistry::get_clan_raid`/`set_clan_raid`/`set_clan_raid_god` (`clan.c:547-580,1541-1543` - `set_clan_raid` only ever sets the *pending* timer, never `raid` itself, matching C exactly since nothing but the still-unported `update_relations` first-tick auto-enable or the GM-only `set_clan_raid_god` ever promotes it); and the `set_clan_website`/`set_clan_message` trailing-character-strip quirk was ported at the driver layer, since `clanclerk_driver` is the *only* real call site of either function in the whole C tree (a stale note calling this a "future `/clan` command" concern was corrected). Clan-log persistence for every new write path is queued as `ClanclerkEvent`/`apply_clanclerk_events` (`world_events.rs`, mirroring `apply_clanmaster_events`), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop. Out of scope, left for a future slice: `add potions`/`NT_GIVE`'s `IDR_FLASK` branch and `use` (both need the unported alchemy-potion/dungeon-guard economies - `buy` itself needed no further work, C's own implementation is unconditionally disabled dead code). 30 new tests (23 in `world/tests/clanclerk.rs`, 6 `clan.rs` raid-method unit tests + a `ClanMoneyChange::log_message` format test, 2 `character_driver.rs` constant/arg-parser tests). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1528 ugaris-core [+30] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 520 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (adds a new tick-loop call site). REMAINING (see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Clan system" entry for the up-to-date list): the clanmaster NPC's `rank:`/`fire:` text commands, club founding/joining, `clan_trade_bonus`, and the dungeon-guard economy proper (guard counts/potions - `use`'s real logic). Iteration 97 ported the clanmaster NPC's `rank:`/`fire:` leader rank-management text commands (`clanmaster.c:446-547`) in `world/clanmaster.rs`, online-target branch only: both gate on `char_clan_if_leader(speaker_id, 4)`; `rank:` replicates C's `ptr += 6` quirk (one byte past the keyword's own 5, unlike `name:`/`join:`'s `+= 5` in the same function) via a new `take_name_token` helper feeding the remainder into `clanclerk.rs`'s `parse_int_atoi` (made `pub(super)` for this, first cross-module reuse) for `rank = atoi(ptr)`, validates the `0..=4` range, the not-paying-above-rank-1 gate, and the same-clan gate (in C's own order) before setting `Character::clan_rank` directly (C's own handler never calls a `clan.rs` setter here either); `fire:` reuses the existing `ClanRegistry::remove_member` after the same same-clan check. New `find_online_player_by_name` helper (C's `find_char_byname`/`getfirst_char`+`getnext_char` loop, first `CF_PLAYER` case-insensitive name match, no distance/visibility restriction - matching C) mirrors `world/trader.rs`'s sibling helper. The offline-player fallback (C's `lookup_name`+`task_set_clan_rank`/`task_fire_from_clan`, an async DB-task queue applying the mutation on that player's next login) has no equivalent subsystem in this codebase (no persistent name index, no task queue) - left unported and documented inline, not silently dropped. Two new `ClanmasterEvent` variants (`RankSet`/`MemberFired`) carry the clan-log entries C's `rank:` handler (`clanmaster.c:493-494`, prio 30, `"%s rank was set to %d by %s"`) and `remove_member` (`clan.c:1210-1213`, prio 15, `"%s was fired from clan by %s"`, master = the firing leader, unlike `leave!`'s self-master `MemberLeft`) write, applied by two new `apply_clanmaster_events` match arms. 10 new tests in `world/tests/clanmaster.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1538 ugaris-core [+10] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 520 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING (unchanged list, minus `rank:`/`fire:`'s online branch): the offline-player DB-task fallback for `rank:`/`fire:`, club founding/joining, `clan_trade_bonus`, and the dungeon-guard economy proper (guard counts/potions - `use`'s real logic). Iteration 98 ported the player-facing `/clan` and `/relation` read-only display text commands (`showclan`/`show_clan_relation`, `clan.c:128-233,311-357`, dispatched from `command.c:5978-6011`) in a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_command.rs`: the full clan-list header (per-clan jewels/raiding-state/training level), the "Your Clan" section (rank, and - for rank > 0 - the Treasury line, the Training line, website/message, Active Bonuses, and the ENABLED/PENDING-with-hours-remaining/DISABLED raiding status), and `/relation`'s per-clan-pair current/want-relation-with-timestamps table. Two small `ugaris-core` additions this needed: `clan::score_to_level` (`clan.c:72-74`, `score/100`) and `ClanRelations::want_relation`/`want_date` read accessors (the mutator `set_relation` already existed; nothing previously exposed the "want" side for display). Intentionally NOT ported: `/clanpots` (`show_clan_pots`, `clan.c:1426-1453` - reads the still-unported dungeon-guard potion stockpile, no Rust data exists) and `showclan`'s "--- Dungeon Guards ---"/"Dungeon points: X / 400" lines (same reason, guard counts aren't part of `ClanEconomy`) - both documented inline and left for whichever future iteration ports the dungeon-guard economy. 14 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/clan_command.rs` plus 3 new `clan.rs` unit tests (`score_to_level`, `want_relation`/`want_date` read/invalid-clan cases). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1541 ugaris-core [+3] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 532 server [+14], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (adds a new tick-loop command-dispatch call site). REMAINING (unchanged): the offline-player DB-task fallback for `rank:`/`fire:`, club founding/joining, `clan_trade_bonus`, `/clanpots`, and the dungeon-guard economy proper. Iteration 99 ported `/clanpots` (`show_clan_pots`, `clan.c:1426-1455`): added `ClanEconomy::alc_pot`/`simple_pot` storage (`struct clan_dungeon`'s `alc_pot[2][6]`/`simple_pot[3][3]`, `clan.h:74-75`), `#[serde(default)]` for snapshot backward compatibility and all-zero in `ClanEconomy::standard` (nothing feeds these yet - the alchemy-potion economy's `add_alc_potion`/`add_simple_potion` `NT_GIVE` call site is still unported, so every clan reads zero, matching a freshly-founded C clan), and wired the command itself in `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_command.rs` (`show_clan_pots_lines` + dispatch, `Only for clan members.`/`Not of sufficient rank.` guards, the 21-line potion-tier report including the literal `\016` (0x0E) tab marker byte-for-byte, and `/clanpots`'s C `cmdcmp` minlen 5 checked ahead of `/clan`'s minlen 0). 4 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/clan_command.rs`. REMAINING (updated, `/clanpots` dropped): the offline-player DB-task fallback for `rank:`/`fire:`, club founding/joining, `clan_trade_bonus`, and the dungeon-guard economy proper (guard counts/potions, `use`'s real logic, and the alchemy-potion economy that would actually populate `alc_pot`/`simple_pot`). Iteration 100 closed the offline-player DB-task fallback: audited `CharacterRepository` and confirmed every primitive C's `task_set_clan_rank`/`task_fire_from_clan`/`set_clan_rank`/`fire_from_clan` (`system/task.c:87-133,213-295,333-356`) need already exists (name->ID lookup via `find_login_target`, online-elsewhere check via `current_area`, full snapshot load via `load_character_snapshot`, guarded compare-and-swap save via `CharacterSaveMode::Backup`'s `expected_current_area`/`expected_current_mirror` WHERE clause) - no new DB method, SQL, or migration needed. Added `ClanmasterEvent::OfflineRankLookup`/`OfflineFire` to `world/clanmaster.rs`, queued by `clanmaster_handle_rank_command`/`clanmaster_handle_fire_command`'s previously-no-op "no online match" branch, and `apply_offline_clan_rank`/`apply_offline_clan_fire` in `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs::apply_clanmaster_events` (now takes a `character_repository` parameter, wired at its `main.rs` call site): resolves the DB row directly (a synchronous stand-in for C's cached `lookup_name` + async task-worker, so unlike C this always resolves definitively rather than leaving C's ambiguous "still resolving" `uID == 0` case), sends the "Update scheduled"/"Sorry, no player by the name %s found." feedback exactly like C, silently no-ops on the "online somewhere else" guard (matching C's own silent `xlog`-only branch), replicates `set_clan_rank`/`fire_from_clan`'s clan-membership (reusing `ClanRegistry::get_char_clan`'s existing stale-reference self-heal against the loaded offline snapshot) and paid-status validation with their exact `tell_chat` wording, mutates `clan_rank` or clears `clan`/`clan_rank`/`clan_serial`, writes the same prio-30/prio-15 clan-log entries `RankSet`/`MemberFired` already use, and reuses `World::npc_quiet_say` (nearby-only) in place of C's inter-mirror `tell_chat` chat-channel relay (documented as a deliberate simplification, consistent with every other message this driver sends). Updated the two existing "ignores unmatched offline name" tests to assert the new event payload instead of a no-op. No direct test coverage of `apply_offline_clan_rank`/`_fire` themselves (no fake `CharacterRepository` exists in this codebase - same pre-existing gap as `apply_trader_events`/`apply_bank_events`); covered at the `World` layer (event payload) plus a full boot-smoke. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1541 ugaris-core [2 tests renamed, no net count change] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 536 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING (updated): only club-variant achievement wiring, `clan_trade_bonus`, and the dungeon-guard economy proper - all three genuinely blocked on other unported systems (club.c, the merchant system, and the dungeon/raid + alchemy-potion systems respectively), not self-contained slices of this task anymore. Iteration 101 discovered and closed a real regression in that "REMAINING" bookkeeping: despite iterations 85/95 describing `ClanRelations::update` (`update_relations`, the daily relation escalation/de-escalation tick) and `ClanRegistry::update_treasure`/`update_training` (the weekly treasury tick and hourly training decay) as "closed", none of the three had ever actually been invoked by the running server - grepping `main.rs` for any call to them turned up nothing, so in a live game clans could never escalate to war, go broke, or decay training scores. Added `ClanRelationChange::log_message` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs`, formats the seven `add_clanlog` message shapes given the other clan's name/number, letter-for-letter matched against C including the `rel_name[]`-driven "Peace-Treaty" vs the hardcoded "Peace Treaty" text discrepancy C itself has) and a `serial: u32` field on `ClanTreasuryEvent::WentBroke` (capturing the pre-deletion serial inside `update_treasure` itself, since C's own `add_clanlog` call happens before `status.serial++` and `ClanRegistry::delete_clan` already bumps it - a post-hoc `registry.serial(nr)` call after `update_treasure` returns would read the wrong, already-bumped value). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs::apply_clan_economy_tick` (same shape as the neighboring `apply_clanclerk_events`) runs all three sub-ticks every server tick (matching C's own `tick_clan` cadence - each function self-gates on its own hour/day/week timers), writes both sides of each relation-change pair's clan-log entry (actor `CharacterId(0)` "system", prio 10) and the bankrupt-deletion entry (prio 1), and correctly skips logging `PaidDebtWithJewels` (C's own `xlog` there is server-debug-only). Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_clanclerk_events`. Also corrected several stale doc comments this false-closure claim had left behind, including two unrelated ones caught in the same audit that had actually been fixed by other iterations without updating the module doc comment (the DB clan repository existing since iteration 93/94, and `ClanAttackPolicy` being wired since iteration 85). 3 new tests: `relation_change_log_messages_match_c_add_clanlog_text_exactly` (`ugaris-core`) plus three `apply_clan_economy_tick` wiring tests in `ugaris-server`'s `tests/world_events.rs` (the escalation state machine and treasury arithmetic themselves stay covered by `ugaris-core`'s existing exhaustive unit tests). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1542 ugaris-core + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 539 server, all green), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 103 ported `clan_trade_bonus` (`clan.c:1545-1552`), the last of the three REMAINING items from iteration 100 that was actually unblocked (the merchant store system had landed since; that REMAINING note was simply stale). `World::clan_trade_bonus` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/merchant.rs`) resolves the caller's clan via `ClanRegistry::get_char_clan` and returns `get_clan_bonus(cnr, 2) * 7.5` truncated to `i32` (`0` for non-members), folded into the barter term at all three C call sites (`salesprice`/`buyprice`'s `barter + 100 + trader*5 + clan_trade_bonus`): `World::merchant_barter_and_trader` (feeding `merchant_store_buy`/`merchant_store_sell`) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/merchants.rs::merchant_store_payload`'s per-slot/per-inventory-slot/cursor price display (signature changed from `&World` to `&mut World`, both call sites in `main.rs` already held a mutable binding). 3 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/merchant.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1552 ugaris-core [+3] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 539 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING (before iteration 134): club-variant achievement wiring and the dungeon-guard economy proper (guard counts/potions, `use`'s real logic, alchemy-potion economy) - both still genuinely blocked on other unported systems. Iteration 134 found that "dungeon-guard economy proper" was overstated: the guard-count *configuration* accessors - `get_clan_dungeon_cost`/`set_clan_dungeon_use`/`get_clan_dungeon` (`clan.c:617-799`) - are pure state on `struct clan_dungeon` with a real, reachable caller today (the clanclerk NPC's `use` command), independent of the still-unported `area/13/dungeon.c` raid-spawn consumer or the alchemy-potion economy. Ported all three: `ClanEconomy::dungeon_guard_use: [i32; 21]` (flattens C's `warrior[1]`/`mage[1]`/`seyan[1]`/`teleport[1]`/`fake[1]`/`key[1]` "use per dungeon" pairs by `type - 1`; the mirrored `[0]` "total owned" half stays unmodeled since it's only ever written by C's dead `buy` command), free function `get_clan_dungeon_cost` (the per-type multiplier table), `ClanRegistry::get_clan_dungeon` (read accessor), and `ClanRegistry::set_clan_dungeon_use` (validates `cnr`/type/per-type number cap, recomputes the 21-slot training-point total with the candidate value substituted in, rejects over-400 raises without mutating - `ClanDungeonUseError` enum for C's two `-1`/`cost` failure shapes). Wired the `use` command in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanclerk.rs`: `parse_dungeon_use_args` (matches C's exact `atoi`/whitespace/digit/whitespace two-number extraction, including its "negative first number breaks the second atoi" quirk, dead in practice since a negative type is always rejected first), `World::clanclerk_handle_dungeon_use` (byte-exact C feedback strings for the outer range checks plus all three `set_clan_dungeon_use` outcomes), a new `ClanclerkEvent::DungeonUseSet` applied in `world_events.rs::apply_clanclerk_events` (prio-35 clan-log entry, same shape as its five sibling arms). `use` sits in the treasurer-tier gate (same as `withdraw`/`buy`), confirmed against the exact C branch ordering. 11 new tests (5 `clan.rs`, 6 `world/tests/clanclerk.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1788 ugaris-core [+11] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING now genuinely only: club-variant achievement wiring (blocked on unported `club.c`), the potion half of the dungeon-guard economy plus its `add potions`/`NT_GIVE` `IDR_FLASK` call site (blocked on the unported alchemy-potion economy), and the raid-spawn consumer of `get_clan_dungeon` itself (blocked on unported `area/13/dungeon.c`). Iteration 135 found the "potion half ... blocked on the unported alchemy-potion economy" claim was itself overstated: the `IDR_FLASK`/`IDR_POTION` item drivers (`crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/alchemy.rs`) are already fully ported, so `add_alc_potion`/`add_simple_potion` (`clan.c:1457-1533`) only needed to read their already-modeled `modifier_index`/`modifier_value`/`driver_data` fields. Ported `ClanRegistry::add_alc_potion` (takes the flask's own modifier arrays directly rather than a whole `Item`; matches C's two three-modifier recipes via `CharacterValue` discriminant casts, computes the `min(5, (mod_value[0]/4)-1)` tier with a documented `.clamp(0, 5)` replacing C's theoretical-only negative-index UB) and `ClanRegistry::bump_simple_pot` (the plain stockpile-increment half of `add_simple_potion`, given an already-classified `(kind, size)` slot) in `crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs`. Wired both real call sites in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanclerk.rs`: the `NT_GIVE` handler's new `IDR_FLASK` branch (success destroys the flask and says "Added one potion to our storage.", failure says "Failed to add potion to storage, please try again." and falls through to the existing "let it vanish" fallback, matching C's own fallthrough-on-failure) and a new `add potions` text command (works for any nearby player like `deposit`, ahead of the members-only gate) backed by `World::add_simple_potions_from_inventory` (scans the speaker's own inventory slots `INVENTORY_START_INVENTORY..INVENTORY_SIZE`, pattern-matches each `IDR_POTION` item's `driver_data[1..4]` against C's nine healing/mana/combo Small/Medium/Big triples, destroys every match via the existing `World::destroy_item`, which already clears the owning character's inventory slot and `CharacterFlags::ITEMS`). A full `add_clanlog`/`dlog` grep of `clanmaster.c` confirmed neither function's C call site logs anything, so no new `ClanclerkEvent` variant was needed. 13 new tests (6 in `clan.rs`, 7 in `world/tests/clanclerk.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1801 ugaris-core [+13] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING before iteration 136 genuinely only: club-variant achievement wiring (blocked on unported `club.c`) and the raid-spawn consumer of `get_clan_dungeon` itself (blocked on unported `area/13/dungeon.c`) - both gated on a whole separate unported system, not a self-contained slice of this task. Iteration 136 made a first dent in the "blocked on unported `club.c`" gap: ported the club identity/serial registry itself (new `crates/ugaris-core/src/club.rs`, see its own ledger row) and wired its `get_char_club` into `crate::world::clanmaster::is_club_member`'s membership gate, replacing the bare `clan >= CLUB_OFFSET` range-check approximation with real registry validation - the first genuine (if still partial) unblocking of this task's own club dependency. Club founding/joining (`CDR_CLUBMASTER`) and the club-variant achievement award still have no live path (nothing ever creates a real club yet outside tests/GM cheats), so the "club-variant achievement wiring" REMAINING item is unchanged; only its blocking foundation now exists. See `crates/ugaris-core/src/club.rs`'s own ledger row for full detail. Iteration 147 ported `area/13/dungeon.c`'s `dungeondoor`'s `first_solve` block (`dungeon.c:1855-1891`, the jewel-steal/broadcast/notify side effects of solving a clan-raid catacomb door for the first time, via `clan.c:1343-1372`'s `'J'` chat-channel handler for the actual economy mutation - applied directly here rather than through legacy master/slave IPC, since this codebase is one process per area): `ClanRegistry::dungeon_jewel_steal` (the bespoke `training_score += 150`/`debt += cnt*1000+1000`/`jewels += cnt` formula, deliberately not `swap_jewels`) and `World::resolve_dungeon_door_first_solve` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_master.rs` - the winner's-own-clan/12-jewel-threshold guards, the stolen-count computation and "You won..." feedback, the catacomb-collapse broadcast via the already-ported `characters_in_dungeon_slot`, and the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DUNGEON` `push_driver_message` to every live `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER` NPC - finally making reachable that driver's own `process_dungeonmaster_messages` consumer, ported in an earlier iteration but never previously invoked). `world::item_outcomes`'s `DungeonDoorSolved` handler now calls this on `first_solve` before its pre-existing teleport chain. A new `DungeonJewelStealEvent`/`apply_dungeon_jewel_steal_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/dungeon.rs`, same pure-decision/async-I/O split as `DungeonRaidBuildRequest`) writes the two prio-5 clan-log entries, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_dungeonmaster_events`. 4 new `world/tests/dungeon_master.rs` tests plus 2 new `world/tests/item_outcomes.rs` wiring tests plus 3 new `clan.rs` unit tests. REMAINING for the "Clan system" task, updated: only club-variant achievement wiring (blocked on unported `club.c`'s founding/joining) and the `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` idle-wander/auto-attack AI architecture gap (documented in `world/dungeon_fighter.rs`) remain. Iteration 148 ported `immortal_dead`'s one-line `charlog` bug message for `CDR_DUNGEONMASTER` (`dungeon.c:1735-1737,2197-2200`) as `apply_dungeonmaster_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`), an exact mirror of the existing `apply_gate_welcome_death_from_hurt_event` precedent, wired into `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`'s dispatch list. 2 new tests. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1936 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 604 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING unchanged from iteration 147's note (this was a trivial documented sub-item, not one of the two real remaining gaps): club-variant achievement wiring and the `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` AI architecture gap. Iteration 149 closed the `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` AI architecture gap: added `Character::dungeonfighter: Option<DungeonfighterDriverData>` (mirroring the `merchant`/`template_key` precedent of data living outside `driver_state`), freeing `driver_state` to hold a real `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy` payload for these NPCs (parsed from the same `arg="aggressive=1;..."` string the "warrior"/"mage"/"seyan" `zones/13/dungeon.chr` templates already carry) so C's `dungeonfighter` tail `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` reuse now works via the existing `process_simple_baddy_*` dispatch functions, whose `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` gates were widened to also accept `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`. 5 new tests. `cargo test --workspace` (1940 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 604 server, all green). REMAINING now genuinely only: club-variant achievement wiring (blocked on unported `club.c`). Iteration 150 ported `show_clan_info` (`clan.c:294-309`) into `World::look_character_text` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs`) as part of closing the "Look at character" task's own long-standing clan-info gap - see that file's ledger section and `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Progress Log for detail.Iteration 176 ported `/setclanjewels` (`command.c:7563-7596`, `CF_GOD`-gated): added `ClanRegistry::set_jewels` (direct `clan[clan_nr].treasure.jewels = jewels` assignment, returning the old count for the "changed from %d to %d" message; a no-op returning `None` for an out-of-range or never-created clan number, matching the same "cannot occur here" reasoning as `clan_money_change`'s doc comment for C's harmless nameless-slot-write quirk) and wired it into `commands_admin.rs` right after `/killclub`, deferring the optional clan-log write (`do_log`, default 1) through a new `KeyringCommandResult::clan_log_entry` field handled at the `main.rs` call site via the existing `clan_log::write_clan_log_entry`. 3 new tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs`. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task for the up-to-date list of what else in `command.c` is still unwired. |
| `src/system/club.c` / `club.h` (+ `src/system/database/database_clubs.c` for reference) | `crates/ugaris-core/src/club.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanmaster.rs` | Iteration 136: first self-contained slice of the club system (a parallel, much larger-scale variant of the clan system reusing `Character.clan`/`.clan_rank`/`.clan_serial`, distinguished by `clan >= CLUBOFFSET`=1024). New `ClubRegistry`: `serials: Vec<u32>` (persists across deletion, matching C's always-allocated static array leaving `club[n].serial` untouched when only `name[0]` is cleared) + sparse `identities: HashMap<u16, ClubIdentity>` (`MAXCLUB`=16384 is too large for `crate::clan::ClanRegistry`'s own fixed-array-of-`Option` approach at its much smaller `MAX_CLAN`=32). Ported: `create_club` (`club.c:140-183` - `isalpha`/space name validation, `n > 75` length cap, duplicate-name rejection including the empty-name-always-collides-with-an-unused-slot quirk, first-free-slot allocation, serial always incremented on create - unlike `crate::clan::ClanRegistry::found_clan`, which preserves the old serial across re-creation), `rename_club` (`club.c:185-212` - same name validation, but (like C) never checks whether `nr` already has an identity, so it can "create" a club at an explicit slot with a never-bumped serial, an admin-tool quirk kept for fidelity, same precedent as `ClanRegistry::set_name`'s doc comment), `kill_club` (`club.c:132-138` - forces bankruptcy, a no-op on a nonexistent slot matching C's own no-op-on-nameless-slot outcome), `get_char_club` (`club.c:29-61` - validates `Character.clan`/`.clan_serial` against the registry with the same self-healing clear-on-mismatch idiom as `ClanRegistry::get_char_clan`; always fully validates, unlike C's `!club_update_done` boot-loading skip, same documented deviation `get_char_clan` already uses), and `tick_billing` (`tick_club`'s `areaID == 3` branch, `club.c:82-111` - processes at most one due club per call, weekly 10000-gold rent or bankruptcy deletion, `paid`/`money`/serial left untouched on deletion exactly like C). Not ported: any DB persistence (no `crates/ugaris-db` repository/migration exists yet, same "not yet persisted" state `ClanRegistry` was in before its own DB wiring), `tick_club`'s `areaID != 3` branch (`schedule_clubs`/`db_read_clubs`, a periodic full DB re-sync for other area processes - nothing to resync from with no DB wiring), `show_club_info`/`showclub` (the `look_char`/`/club` text formatters - deferred alongside the still-unported `show_clan_info` wiring, see the "Look at character" task's REMAINING notes), the `/joinclub`/`/killclub`/`/renclub` GM commands (not wired to any command dispatcher yet), and the `CDR_CLUBMASTER` founding/joining NPC driver (`clubmaster.c`, driver 113, 628 lines - a separate, larger future slice; no club is ever actually founded or joined by a real player yet). Real wiring landed this iteration despite that: `World` gained a `club_registry: ClubRegistry` field, and `crate::world::clanmaster::is_club_member` (previously a bare `character.clan >= CLUB_OFFSET` range-check approximation, since removed) now calls `self.club_registry.get_char_club(character)` for real serial/existence validation at its one live call site (the clanmaster NPC's `char_is_clan_or_club_member` gate, `name:`'s "already a member of a clan or club" rejection). 21 new unit tests in `club.rs` (create/rename/kill validation and quirks, slot-reuse serial bump, `get_char_club` success/stale-serial/deleted-club/clan-number/no-membership cases, `tick_billing` pay/delete/skip/one-per-call/area-gating), plus the existing `world/tests/clanmaster.rs::name_command_rejects_existing_club_member` updated to create a real `ClubRegistry` entry instead of a bare `clan` field assignment. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1822 ugaris-core [+21] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration adds a field to `World` and a new call site inside the clanmaster driver's per-tick logic). REMAINING: everything not listed as ported above - most importantly the `CDR_CLUBMASTER` founding NPC (nothing populates `club_registry` in a live game yet) and DB persistence. Iteration 137 added `ClubRegistry::club_money`/`club_money_change` (`club[n].money` direct read/mutate, backing the new `clubmaster_driver` port's own `deposit:`/`withdraw:` commands - see `src/system/clubmaster.c`'s own ledger row) with 3 new unit tests; `get_char_club`/`create_club` now have their first real, reachable call site (`clubmaster_driver`'s `found:`/`join:`), so a real club can actually be founded/joined by a player in a live game. DB persistence and the `/joinclub`/`/killclub`/`/renclub` GM commands remain unchanged/unported. `show_club_info` (`club.c:65-80`) was ported in iteration 150 into `World::look_character_text` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs`), reusing the global army-rank name table for its "rank" display exactly like C's own quirky `rankname[]` index - see that file's ledger section. Iteration 162 ported `showclub` itself (`club.c:114-131`, the `/club` command) into `crates/ugaris-server/src/clan_command.rs` (extending the existing `/clan`/`/clanpots`/`/relation` module rather than a new file): `showclub_lines` (membership + treasury lines) and a fourth `apply_clan_command` dispatch branch reusing `ClubRegistry::get_char_club` unchanged. Matches C's quirk that a non-member gets zero output (the whole function body is gated on `get_char_club` succeeding - no "not a member" message at all, unlike `/clan`/`/clanpots`). 5 new tests in `tests/clan_command.rs`. Iteration 173 wired the last two GM commands, `/killclub` (`command.c:6484-6497`, `CF_GOD`) and `/renclub` (`cmd_renclub`, `command.c:4548-4585`, dispatched at `command.c:9650` gated `CF_STAFF|CF_GOD`), into `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs` right alongside the existing `/killclan`/`/renclan` - both `ClubRegistry` methods (`kill_club`/`rename_club`) already existed unused since iteration 136 and needed no changes. Preserved a genuine C bug verbatim: `/killclub`'s bounds check compares `nr` against `MAXCLAN` (32), not `MAXCLUB` (16384) - a copy-paste leftover from the adjacent `killclan` block in C - so club numbers `>= 32` can never be killed via this command despite being otherwise legal club numbers. 6 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` (bankrupt-not-delete + follow-on `tick_billing` deletion, the buggy-cap no-op, successful rename in Aston, outside-Aston/illegal-name rejection, staff-or-god gating). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1987 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 710 server [+5], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Remaining unchanged: DB persistence and the `CDR_CLUBMASTER` founding-NPC gaps noted earlier in this entry. |
| `src/system/clubmaster.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clubmaster.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Iteration 137: first self-contained slice of `clubmaster_driver` (`CDR_CLUBMASTER`/113), the club system's single combined founding+administration NPC (unlike the clan system, which splits this across two NPCs, `CDR_CLANMASTER`/`CDR_CLANCLERK`). Ported: `found:` (a single-step 10,000-gold founding payment straight into `ClubRegistry::create_club` - no Clan-Jewel two-step handoff like `clanmaster_driver`'s `name:`, since `clubmaster.c` has no `struct clan_found_data` equivalent at all), `accept:`/`join:` (leader-invites-member handshake, gated at club rank `>= 1` unlike clan's `>= 2`, using a direct-field-set rather than a dedicated `add_member`, matching `clubmaster.c`'s own commented-out `// add_member(...)` line), `leave!` (reuses `ClanRegistry::remove_member` verbatim - it is already clan/club-agnostic field-clearing with no clan-specific validation, so no new function was needed), `deposit:`/`withdraw:` (club treasury, `withdraw:` gated at founder rank `2`, backed by two new `ClubRegistry::club_money`/`club_money_change` methods - see `club.c`'s own ledger row), the generic small-talk qa table (new `CLUBMASTER_QA` constant in `character_driver.rs` - a different, shorter table than `CLANMASTER_QA`: it stops after a single `"club"` entry with no `"jewels"`/`"repeat"`/`"raid"` special cases), the periodic greeting, the 8-entry idle-murmur table (`RANDOM(8)`, C's `clubmaster.c:552-579` happens to share its first 8 entries byte-for-byte with `clanmaster.c`'s own 13-entry table, duplicated rather than shared per C's own per-file duplication), and the 12h driver-memory clear timer. New `character_driver.rs` additions: `CDR_CLUBMASTER`=113 (verified against `drvlib.h:161`), `ClubmasterDriverData` (C's `struct clubmaster_driver_data`, `clubmaster.c:198-213` - its `new_name`/`new_co`/`new_ID`/`new_timeout` fields are genuinely dead in C itself, never read *or* written anywhere in the file, so - unlike `ClanmasterDriverData::accept_cn`, which is at least written once - they were dropped rather than kept for fidelity), `parse_clubmaster_driver_args` (same `dir=N;` shape as `parse_clanmaster_driver_args`), and a new `CharacterDriverState::Clubmaster` variant (requiring new match arms in `npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`/`npc_messages.rs`/`character_driver.rs`'s existing exhaustive-`CharacterDriverState` switches). Preserved one genuine C bug verbatim, documented inline rather than "fixed": the `NT_CHAR` greeting's membership check (`clubmaster.c:269`, `if (!get_char_club(cn) && !get_char_clan(cn))`) tests the *clubmaster NPC's own* `cn` membership, not the visiting player `co`'s, unlike `clanmaster_driver`'s equivalent check (`clanmaster.c:352`), which correctly uses `co` - since NPCs never have a clan/club this condition is always true in practice, so the clubmaster greets every single visitor and never stops for existing members (contrast the clanmaster, which does stop). Achievement awards (`ACHIEVEMENT_CLUB_MEMBER`/`ACHIEVEMENT_CLUB_MASTER`) are queued as a new `ClubmasterEvent` enum (mirroring `ClanmasterEvent`'s shape) and applied by a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs::apply_clubmaster_events` plus two new `achievement.rs` helpers (`award_clubmaster_member_achievement`/`award_clubmaster_master_achievement`), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_clanclerk_events`; `zone.rs` gained the `CDR_CLUBMASTER` spawn-wiring branch. Club founding/deposit/withdraw's bare C `dlog(...)` calls (`clubmaster.c:304,496,515`) are server-debug-only - no `club_log` table exists, matching the exact precedent `world/clanclerk.rs`'s own module doc comment already documents for clan's bare `dlog` calls - so no persisted-log wiring was added for any of them. This is the first live, reachable call site for `ClubRegistry::create_club`/`get_char_club` outside tests: a real club can now actually be founded and joined by a player in a live game. Deliberately out of scope for this slice (documented inline, not silently dropped, same phased multi-iteration approach `world/clanmaster.rs` itself took for its own `rank:`/`fire:` across iterations 94/97/100): `rank:`/`fire:` (`clubmaster.c:379-483`) and their `lookup_name`/`task_set_clan_rank`/`task_fire_from_clan` offline-player DB-task fallback. 18 new tests in `world/tests/clubmaster.rs` plus 2 `character_driver.rs` constant/arg-parser tests. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1845 ugaris-core [+23] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration adds a `World` field, a new tick-loop call site, and a new zone spawn-wiring branch). REMAINING: `rank:`/`fire:` (online + offline-DB-fallback). Iteration 138 closed that REMAINING item: `clubmaster_handle_rank_command`/`clubmaster_handle_fire_command` (founder rank `>= 2`/leader rank `>= 1` gates, target range 0-1, unpaid-above-rank-0 rejection, founder-can't-be-retargeted/fired guards - all validated against `set_clan_rank`/`fire_from_clan`'s club (`else`) branch in `task.c`, not just `clubmaster_driver`'s own online-target code path), reusing `world::clanmaster`'s `take_name_token`/`find_online_player_by_name` helpers (promoted to `pub(super)`) verbatim since C's own parsing/search-loop code is byte-for-byte identical between the two drivers. New `ClubmasterEvent::OfflineRankLookup`/`OfflineFire` variants resolved by new `apply_offline_club_rank`/`apply_offline_club_fire` helpers in `world_events.rs` (mirroring `apply_offline_clan_rank`/`apply_offline_clan_fire` but with *no* clan-log entry at all - C's own club branch of `set_clan_rank`/`fire_from_clan` never calls `add_clanlog`, so `setter_name` is genuinely dead state in the club path, same as C's own unread `set->master_name` there); `apply_clubmaster_events` gained a `character_repository` parameter for the DB lookup. 14 new tests in `world/tests/clubmaster.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1857 ugaris-core [+12] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING: none - this driver is now fully ported. |
| `src/module/military.c` / `military.h`, `src/system/tool.c` (`get_army_rank_int`/`set_army_rank`/`get_army_rank_string`/`give_military_pts`/`give_military_pts_no_npc`) | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/military/{mod,missions,military_master,military_advisor}.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/death.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | First self-contained slice: the Imperial Army rank table and the shared point-award/promotion helper only, not the mission system itself. `ARMY_RANK_NAMES` (`tool.c:1868-1907`'s `rankname[]`, all 41 entries letter for letter) and `army_rank_for_points` (`get_army_rank_int`'s `cbrt(military_pts)` formula clamped to `MAX_ARMY_RANK`=40) are ported as pure functions; rank is deliberately derived on the fly from `Character.military_points` instead of adding a second persisted `army_rank` field, since the only two C `set_army_rank` call sites (`give_military_pts`/`give_military_pts_no_npc`) always write exactly this formula's output - documented inline, including the one narrow C off-by-one promotion-guard quirk this simplification doesn't reproduce (unreachable short of a deliberately huge single admin grant). `World::give_military_pts` ports `give_military_pts_no_npc` (`tool.c:3279-3306`): awards exp via `give_exp`, records raw exp onto `military_normal_exp`, applies the hardcore military-specific bonus to points, and queues the "You've been promoted..." system text plus the above-Sergeant-Major server-wide "Grats:" channel-6 broadcast on promotion. Wired both existing `military_points`-mutating call sites onto it (previously neither did any promotion/feedback at all): the `/milexp` admin command and the Area 25 `warpbonus_driver` reward-kind-3 case. Also fixed a pre-existing inconsistency found while wiring `/milexp`: `hardcore_military_exp_bonus` lived only on `ServerRuntime` (unreachable from `ugaris-core`, unlike its siblings `exp_modifier`/`hardcore_exp_bonus` which already live on `world.settings`) - moved it onto `world.settings` and removed the now-redundant `ServerRuntime` field. 8 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs`. Iteration 105 wired the one remaining self-contained gap the above slice couldn't yet close: C `give_first_kill`'s (`death.c:196-254`) demon-lord-class `if (get_army_rank_int(cn))` branch, previously always taking the "no rank" path in `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`'s `apply_first_kill_check`/`first_kill_congrats_message` (ported in iteration 82, before army ranks existed) - now reads `army_rank_for_points(character.military_points)` for the killer before the kill's own bonus applies, selects the "...! The Governor will be proud of you." message when ranked, and calls `World::give_military_pts(killer_id, min(victim_level/3, 10), kill_score_level(victim_level, killer_level) * 15, area_id)` matching `give_military_pts_no_npc`'s call in C exactly. Also corrected a stale `#rank`-command note: no such player command exists anywhere in the current C tree (only admin-only `/milinfo`/`/milpoints`/`/milstats`), so that was dropped as a documentation fix, not a real gap. 2 new tests in `tests/achievement.rs`. Iteration 106 ported the next self-contained slice - every *pure* mission-generation function `military.c` uses to build a mission offer, with zero character/NPC/storage state: `SingleMission` (`struct single_mission`), `specific_mission_price` (`military.c:392-467`, the paid-advisor price formula), the five level/rank scaling helpers behind `calculate_mission_exp` (`military.c:580-785`: `get_level_experience_cap`/`get_minimum_expected_rank`/`get_maximum_reasonable_rank`/`get_expected_level_for_rank`/`get_enhanced_level_scaling_factor`, reusing the existing `world::exp::level2exp`), and the three per-difficulty mission-instance generators (`generate_single_demon_mission`/`generate_single_ratling_mission`/`generate_single_silver_mission`, `military.c:795-1007`), seeded off the existing `legacy_random_below_from_seed` LCG instead of a bare `rand()` call so results are deterministic in tests. Also confirmed, while reading the DB layer, that `military_ppd`'s own `military_pts`/`normal_exp` fields are *not* part of the remaining gap: their only two C writers are already ported straight onto `Character.military_points`/`.military_normal_exp`, and the whole `Character` struct (including those two fields) already round-trips through the `character_json` `jsonb` DB column on every save/load - documented inline so a future iteration doesn't have to re-derive this. 14 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs` (every price/cap/rank boundary, hand-computed `calculate_mission_exp` values, every generator's difficulty table, level-gating rejection for ratling/silver missions, rank-scaling for silver's `opt1`). REMAINING: the `military_ppd` per-character save data itself (mission slots, advisor state, take/solve tracking), the ppd-populating wrappers (`generate_demon_mission`/`generate_sewer_mission`/`generate_mine_mission`/`generate_mission_with_preference`) and ppd-mutating state transitions (`accept_mission`/`complete_mission`/`handle_specific_mission_request`) built on top of this slice, `check_military_solve` (`death.c:290-379` - has a real call site queued right next to `give_first_kill` in `world/death.rs::kill_character_followup`, but needs the ppd's `took_mission`/`mis[5]` fields to decrement), the Military Master/Advisor NPC drivers + `qa[]` dialogue + storage state machines, the `SV_QUEST_EXT` mod-packet, and the admin commands (`cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_forcesolve`) - most of `military.c`'s 2,881 lines. Iteration 107 closed the `check_military_solve` gap: `PlayerRuntime::military_ppd` (`LEGACY_MILITARY_PPD_SIZE` = 256 bytes, `military.h:28-60`'s 64-`int` struct) is now a typed PPD following the `arena_ppd` pattern, with accessors for the mission-progress fields this needed (`military_mission`/`set_military_mission` for `mis[5]`, `military_took_mission`/`military_solved_mission` and their setters), wired into `decode_legacy_ppd_blob`/`encode_legacy_ppd_blob` and graduated out of `clear_turn_seyan_ppd`'s stripped-raw-bytes list into a real `.clear()`. `world/military.rs` gained the `check_military_solve` class-range guards (`is_pent_demon_mission_class`/`is_sewer_ratling_mission_class`, `ELITE_DEMON_CLASS_BASE`/`LESSER_DEMON_CLASS_BASE`), `get_demon_mission_value` (`death.c:281-288`, elite demons count as 10), and the progress-message log-spam gate (`military_mission_progress_message_should_display`). `PlayerRuntime::check_military_solve(victim_class, victim_level)` ports the full `death.c:290-383` state machine as a pure mutation + `MilitaryMissionProgress` outcome (`NoMatch`/`Progress`/`Solved`). Wired the real call site: `World::kill_character_followup` queues a `MilitaryMissionKillCheck` for every player kill (same `killer_is_player` guard as `FirstKillCheck`, no victim-class restriction, matching C), and the new `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs::apply_military_mission_kill_check` drains it and sends the exact `log_char` text (`COL_DARK_GRAY "Mission kill, %d to go."` / `"Elite demon slain! ..."` / uncolored `"You solved your mission. ..."`) - which needed a small new plumbing addition since `COL_DARK_GRAY`'s raw `\xb0c1` marker isn't valid UTF-8 and can't fit the existing `String`-only `World::queue_system_text`: `WorldSystemTextBytes`/`queue_system_text_bytes`/`drain_pending_system_text_bytes` (`world/text.rs`) plus `send_pending_world_system_text_bytes` (`world_events.rs`), wired into the tick loop alongside the existing string variant. 20 new tests (8 in `world/tests/military.rs`, 12 in `player.rs` covering every `check_military_solve` branch). Still REMAINING (before iteration 108): `military_ppd`'s advisor-state/yday/preference fields (no accessors yet), the mission-offer/accept/complete wrappers, the Military Master/Advisor NPC drivers, the `SV_QUEST_EXT` quest-log packet (so `check_military_solve`'s own `sendquestlog` calls are also not reproduced - cosmetic only), and the admin commands - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Military ranks" P3 entry for the itemized remaining list. Iteration 108 ported the ppd-populating mission-offer wrappers as pure functions plus their ppd-mutating wrapper: `player.rs` gained `mission_type_preference`/`mission_difficulty_preference`/`mission_yday` typed accessors (`military.h:50,51,41`) alongside the existing `mis[5]`/`took_mission`/`solved_mission` ones; `world/military.rs` gained `generate_demon_mission`/`generate_sewer_mission`/`generate_mine_mission`/`generate_mission_with_preference`/`generate_mission` (`military.c:847-1139`) as pure functions over an already rank-cubed-floored `military_pts` and raw level (internally `max(7)`-floored like C); `PlayerRuntime::apply_mission_offer(level, military_pts, preferred_type, yday, rng_seed)` is the new ppd-mutating wrapper mirroring `generate_mission_with_preference`'s C half that actually touches `ppd` (writes `mis[]`, stamps `mission_type_preference`/`mission_yday`). No real call site yet - still needs the Military Master/Advisor NPC driver to resolve `military_pts`/level/yday from `Character`/`World` and invoke it. 13 new tests (10 in `world/tests/military.rs`, 3 in `player.rs`). Iteration 109 ported `accept_mission`/`complete_mission` (`military.c:1300-1436`), the remaining ppd-mutating state transitions: `player.rs` gained `military_current_pts`/`military_took_yday`/`military_solved_yday` typed accessors (`military.h:29,46,48`) plus `PlayerRuntime::accept_mission(difficulty, yday)` (the full gate chain - already-has-a-mission/already-completed-today/not-offered-today/insufficient-points-unless-advisor-paid/empty-slot-unavailable - returning the new `crate::world::AcceptMissionOutcome` enum and, on success, stamping `took_mission`/`took_yday`, deducting `current_pts` where C does, and clearing the mission-preference pair); `world/military.rs` gained `World::complete_mission(character_id, player, area_id)` (awards exp via the existing `give_exp`, the mercenary bonus gold/points formula via `legacy::profession::MERCENARY`, the raw `military_points` add deliberately *not* routed through `World::give_military_pts` since C's own `complete_mission` never applies the hardcore bonus to `pts` and the exp was already awarded separately, and the identical rank-promotion message/broadcast pattern `give_military_pts` already has), returning `CompleteMissionResult`/`CompletedMission`. Both queue their "Well done"/gold-received/promotion feedback via the already-generically-drained `World::queue_system_text`/`queue_system_text_bytes` - no new wiring needed. REMAINING unchanged in shape, just smaller: `handle_specific_mission_request`, the Military Master/Advisor NPC drivers + storage state machines + their `dat->storage_data` per-difficulty counters, the wealth-achievement ladder on the mercenary gold bonus, `SV_QUEST_EXT`, and the admin commands. 15 new tests (8 for `accept_mission`, 4 for `complete_mission`, plus the promotion/broadcast-gate cases) in `world/tests/military.rs`. `cargo test --workspace`: 1612 ugaris-core + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 541 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean; 10s boot-smoke confirmed no panics. Iteration 112 ported `CDR_MILITARY_MASTER`'s own driver (`military_master_driver`, `military.c:2108-2206`), the first real call site for every ppd-mutating function the previous 4 iterations left dangling. `character_driver.rs` gained `CDR_MILITARY_MASTER = 42`, `MilitaryMasterDriverData`/`parse_military_master_driver_args` (the `storage=N;` zone-file arg), a new `CharacterDriverState::MilitaryMaster` variant, and `MILITARY_QA` (the 44-row `qa[]` table, `military.c:89-164`, transcribed verbatim - shared with the still-unported Advisor driver, matching C's own single global table); `zone.rs` wires zone-load parsing next to the `CDR_BANK` block. `world/military.rs` gained the mission-rendering text (`describe_mission_text`/`display_mission_text`/`offer_missions_text`/`mission_difficulty_name`, C `describe_mission`/`display_mission`/`offer_missions`/`diff_name[]`), `World::handle_mission_request` (the "mission" keyword handler, `military.c:1842-1896`, generating a fresh offer table via the existing `apply_mission_offer` when none exists today with the same rank-cubed floor-up `mission_reroll` already applies at its own call site, short-circuiting to an advisor-recommendation reply when applicable), a new `MilitaryMasterEvent` enum (`NearbyPlayer`/`Repeat`/`MissionRequest`/`AcceptMission`/`Failed`/`Hear`/`Reroll`), and the driver body itself (`process_military_master_actions`/`_messages`/`greet_nearby_military_master_players`/`_tick_action`) - `NT_CHAR` ported as the same periodic nearby-player-scan simplification `world/bank.rs`/`world/merchant.rs` already established, safe here because `greet_player`'s `master_state` gate and `complete_mission`'s `solved_mission` gate already make repeated per-tick delivery a no-op once handled. Like `world/bank.rs`, `World` can't reach `PlayerRuntime` (where `military_ppd` lives), so nearly the entire message body is deferred as a `MilitaryMasterEvent` - wider than bank's narrower `BankEvent` since almost every branch here touches `military_ppd`. `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs` gained `apply_military_master_events` (mirroring `apply_bank_events`'s shape): drains the queue, reaches `PlayerRuntime` via `runtime.player_for_character_mut`, calls `greet_player`/`accept_mission`/`handle_mission_request`/`mission_reroll`, and renders each outcome into the exact C `say()` text, including two verbatim C quirks preserved on purpose - the "failed"/"hear" no-active-mission branches substitute the army rank *title* (`get_army_rank_string`), not the player's name, unlike their success branches. Wired into the tick loop in `main.rs` right after `clanclerk`'s call site. Deliberately out of scope this slice (see the task's own REMAINING note above): `process_clan_recommendation`/`process_advisor_recommendation` (need the unported NPC-scoped `military_master_data.storage_data.clan_pts[]`), the admin-only qa codes 18-21, the entire Military Advisor NPC, and both drivers' storage-blob statistics (same "no generic storage-blob persistence in `ugaris-db`" gap the Arena rankings task flags). 23 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs` (driver arg parsing, mission-text rendering incl. edge cases, every `handle_mission_request` branch incl. the advisor short-circuit and today's-table reuse, `NT_CHAR` greet-scan distance/visibility gating, every qa-code-to-event mapping incl. all 5 difficulty keywords/3 reroll aliases, Master-ignored advisor/admin/combo codes staying silent, out-of-range text ignored, and the `NT_GIVE` destroy-plus-reply path). `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1653 ugaris-core [+23 this slice] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 10s+ boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 113 ported `CDR_MILITARY_ADVISOR`'s own driver (`military_advisor_driver`, `military.c:2607-2699`), the paid mission-recommendation NPC the previous iteration's REMAINING note listed as entirely unported. `character_driver.rs` gained `CDR_MILITARY_ADVISOR = 43`, `MilitaryAdvisorDriverData`/`parse_military_advisor_driver_args` (`military_advisor_parse`, the `storage=N;` zone-file arg, same shape as the Master's), and a new `CharacterDriverState::MilitaryAdvisor` variant (plus the 5 now-non-exhaustive match sites needing a new arm: `character_driver.rs` itself, `world/npc_messages.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, and `zone.rs`'s new parse-wiring block next to `CDR_MILITARY_MASTER`). `world/military.rs` gained the ppd-mutating halves of `handle_specific_mission_request`/`offer_favor`/`process_favor_payment` (`military.c:481-566,2339-2474`) as `World::handle_specific_mission_request`/`offer_favor`/`process_favor_payment` (reusing the already-ported pure cost math - `calculate_advisor_index`/`advisor_price`/`offer_favor_cost`/`specific_mission_price` - plus new `SpecificMissionRequestOutcome`/`OfferFavorOutcome`/`ProcessFavorPaymentOutcome` result enums), `adv_introduction_text`/`adv_favor_desc_lines` (the dialogue-rendering halves of `adv_introduction`/`adv_favor_desc`, `military.c:2262-2308`), `favor_size_name`/`mission_type_name` name tables, and `MilitaryAdvisorEvent`/`World::process_military_advisor_actions` (mirroring `MilitaryMasterEvent`/`process_military_master_actions`'s exact shape: same periodic `NT_CHAR` nearby-player-scan simplification, same shared `MILITARY_QA` table via `analyse_text_qa`, same `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split). Confirmed and preserved a genuine C difference: the Advisor rests facing `DX_RIGHT` vs. the Master's `DX_DOWN`. `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs` gained `apply_military_advisor_events` (mirroring `apply_military_master_events`'s shape), rendering every outcome into the exact C text (dropping `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` color markers per this codebase's established `quiet_say`-text convention), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the Master's own call site. Deliberately out of scope (documented inline): the admin-only qa code 18 (`info`) and `update_advisor_storage`/`process_advisor_storage`'s sales-economy `struct cost_data` counters - both need the same unported NPC-scoped storage-blob concept the Master driver's own gap and the Arena rankings task both flag. 46 new tests (28 in `world/tests/military.rs`: driver-arg parsing, `advisor_storage_id`'s driver-state read, name-table/introduction-text/favor-desc text, every `offer_favor`/`handle_specific_mission_request`/`process_favor_payment` gate and success path including simultaneous already-completed/active-mission warnings, and the driver-level greet-scan/qa-code-to-event mapping covering all 5 favor sizes and all 15 specific-mission keyword combinations plus Master-only/admin-only codes staying silent and the `NT_GIVE` destroy-plus-reply path). `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1681 ugaris-core [+28] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 114 ported `process_advisor_recommendation` (`military.c:1685-1755`) as `World::process_advisor_recommendation`, the last entirely-unported gap in the Master driver besides the storage-blob economies - wired into `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`'s `apply_military_master_nearby_player` right before `greet_player`, matching C's own call order exactly (`greet_player`'s pre-existing `AdvisorRecommendationAlreadyShown` short-circuit was written for exactly this call order back when it was first ported). New `AdvisorRecommendationOutcome` enum (`AlreadyProcessed`/`SpecificMission { greeting, description, followup }`/`StandardRecommendations(Vec<String>)`) and a dedicated `advisor_recommendation_difficulty_text` ternary helper (deliberately distinct from `mission_difficulty_name`'s out-of-range clamp to `"easy"` - C's own ternary here falls through to `"insane"` for anything other than `0..=3`). Reuses `handle_mission_request`'s exact rank-cubed-floor/level-7-floor/`apply_mission_offer` sequence for the `mission_yday != yday + 1` regeneration branch, verified against C's `generate_mission_with_preference(co, ppd, ppd->mission_type_preference)` call (`military.c:1712-1714`, confirmed to be the *full* ppd-mutating C function of that name, not the pure Rust table-builder sharing it). Also researched (not implemented, to keep this slice self-contained) the shared "storage-blob" architectural gap this task and the Arena rankings task both flag: confirmed via `src/system/database/database_storage.c` that C's generic `storage` table (`create_storage`/`read_storage`/`update_storage`, id/version/blob with optimistic-concurrency) is used by only 4 C files/6 blobs total (military master's `clan_pts[32]`+quest counters, military advisor's `cost_data[5]`, arena's global toplist + per-NPC fighter record, weather's cross-mirror sync buffer, clan's whole-array save) - recommendation for whoever closes that gap: a small typed-struct-per-consumer table/repository in `ugaris-db` (following the existing `clan_registry` singleton-row-jsonb pattern, but keyed per storage id since these aren't singletons), not a generic byte-blob framework. 10 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs` (already-processed-today no-op, empty/populated `StandardRecommendations` with a stale non-today entry correctly excluded, regenerate-vs-reuse-todays-table paths, both blocking follow-up messages beating the accept prompt, and the difficulty-text ternary's fall-through to `"insane"`). `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1689 ugaris-core [+8] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 115 closed the `process_clan_recommendation`/`update_clan_points` gap (`military.c:1654-1674,1815-1832`) using the exact scoped design iteration 114's research note recommended: a small typed-struct-per-consumer model, not a generic byte-blob framework. New in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`: `MilitaryMasterStorage` (`struct military_master_storage`'s `clan_pts[MAXCLAN]` plus the 4 quest counters, which still have no other reader/writer) and `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry` (a `BTreeMap<i32, MilitaryMasterStorage>` keyed by each NPC's zone-file `storage_id`, `Serialize`/`Deserialize` end to end like `ClanRegistry` but *not yet wired to any DB repository* - in-memory only, resets on restart, documented as a deliberately smaller regression than it sounds since the underlying `clan_pts` economy only grows in small increments and is spent in large chunks - see that type's own doc comment for the scoped `military_master_storage(storage_id integer primary key, storage_json jsonb, updated_at)` table design left for whoever adds persistence next), wired onto a new `World::military_master_storage` field. `World::process_clan_recommendation`/`World::update_clan_points` port the two functions as real `World` methods; `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`'s `MilitaryMasterDriverData` gained the two `dat`-scoped runtime fields both need (`last_clan_update`/`last_recom`), with a `last_clan_update == 0` lazily treated as "just created" (stamped to `now` without granting a bonus) since Rust has no zone-parse-time equivalent of C's `NT_CREATE`-time `realtime` stamp. Added `crate::clan::CLAN_BONUS_MILITARY_ADVISOR = 1` alongside the existing `CLAN_BONUS_MERCHANT` constant. Wired into real call sites: `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`'s `apply_military_master_nearby_player` now calls `process_clan_recommendation` immediately before `process_advisor_recommendation` (matching C's own `NT_CHAR` call order exactly, `military.c:2150-2153`), and `World::process_military_master_actions` gained a `now: i64` parameter, calling `update_clan_points` once per NPC per tick (its one `main.rs` call site and all 10 pre-existing test call sites updated accordingly). 10 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs` (lazy-init-without-bonus, the 60-second throttle including a still-within-window no-op, zero-bonus clans getting nothing, two clans updating independently in the same tick, two NPCs' storage staying independent, above/at/below-threshold recommendation gating, non-clan-member no-op, same-player dedup via `last_recom`, and a different player still getting recommended after another already was). `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1699 ugaris-core [+10] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for the whole "Military ranks" task: DB persistence for `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry` (in-memory only today), the admin-only qa codes (Master 18-21, Advisor 18 - `info` needs the quest counters, which are modeled but have no reader/writer call site), and the Advisor driver's own `struct cost_data` storage-blob economy (no Rust model at all yet) - the DB-persistence and Advisor-economy gaps still share the generally-missing generic storage-blob persistence concept in `ugaris-db` that the Arena rankings task also needs, though the *in-memory* half of that gap is now closed for the Master's `clan_pts`.
