Iteration 116 wired the Master's own `quests_given`/`quests_solved`/`exp_given`/`pts_given[difficulty]` counters (`military.c:1348,1382,1407,1411`), which `MilitaryMasterStorage` had modeled with read-only accessors since iteration 115 but nothing incremented: added private mutators on `MilitaryMasterStorage` plus `storage_id`-keyed wrappers on `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry` (`add_quests_given`, `add_completed_mission_stats` bumping solved/exp/pts together, mirroring `add_clan_pts`'s existing lazy-`or_default()` shape), a new `World::record_mission_offered(master_id, difficulty)` for the accept-side counter (wired into `ugaris-server`'s `apply_military_master_accept_mission`, since `PlayerRuntime::accept_mission` itself has no `World`/`master_id` access), and a new `master_id: CharacterId` parameter on `World::complete_mission` so the solved/exp/pts bump happens inline exactly like C's own `complete_mission` does (using the mission's raw `pts`/`exp` cost fields, not the larger mercenary-formula-adjusted `military_pts_awarded`). Also researched and explicitly declined to port the literal `process_master_storage`/`process_advisor_storage` async-DB-blob state machines (`military.c:1468-1531,1560-1615`) - confirmed they're C's own generic `create_storage`/`read_storage`/`update_storage` round-trip mechanism, already superseded by the simpler direct in-memory registry approach from iteration 115; documented as a closed non-gap rather than a silent skip. 6 new tests in `world/tests/military.rs`. `cargo test --workspace`: 1704 ugaris-core [+6] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Iteration 117 closed the Master driver's own admin-only qa codes 18-21 (`info`/`reset`/`raise`/`promote`, `military.c:2037-2089`): 4 new `MilitaryMasterEvent` variants gated on the speaker's `CharacterFlags::GOD` in `process_military_master_messages` (a non-admin speaker gets the same silent no-op C's guard produces), and 4 new `apply_military_master_*` functions in `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs` - `info` renders the speaker's own `military_pts`/`normal_exp` plus the master's nonzero `clan_pts[1..32]` and per-difficulty quest stats (solve rate, average exp) via consecutive `npc_quiet_say` lines, using the counters iteration 116 already wired reader/writer call sites for; `reset` zeroes the speaker's own `solved_yday`/`mission_yday`; `raise` adds 1000 to the speaker's own ppd `military_pts` field; `promote` reuses `World::give_military_pts(player_id, 100, 1, area_id)` for the point/rank math exactly as that function's own doc comment anticipated (its promotion text still goes through `queue_system_text` rather than this NPC's `npc_quiet_say` - the same pre-existing simplification `complete_mission`'s reward text already carries). 2 new/renamed tests in `world/tests/military.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1705 ugaris-core [+1] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings; 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for the whole "Military ranks" task, unchanged otherwise: DB persistence for `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry`, the Advisor driver's own admin-only qa code 18, and the Advisor driver's own `struct cost_data` storage-blob economy. Iteration 118 closed the DB-persistence item: `migrations/0010_military_master_storage.sql` (`military_master_storage(storage_id integer primary key, storage_json jsonb, updated_at)`, one row per Military Master NPC's `storage=N;` id, not a singleton like `clan_registry`) plus `crates/ugaris-db/src/military.rs` (`MilitaryMasterStorageRepository` trait + `PgMilitaryMasterStorageRepository`, `save_registry` upserting one row per entry, `load_registry` rebuilding the whole registry). `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`) gained `iter()`/`from_rows()` to support this without exposing its private mutators. Wired boot-time load and a once-a-minute `dirty`-gated save into `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, directly mirroring the existing `clan_registry` load/save blocks; `Database::military_master_storage()` added in `crates/ugaris-db/src/lib.rs`. 4 new tests in `ugaris-db`'s `military.rs` (SQL-shape assertion, a `serde_json` round-trip test, 2 `live` DB tests following the `clan.rs` convention). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1705 ugaris-core + 51 db [+4] + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for "Military ranks" now: the Advisor driver's own admin-only qa code 18 (`info`) and its `struct cost_data` storage-blob economy (no Rust model at all yet), the wealth-achievement ladder wiring, the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT` packet, and `complete_mission`/`promote`'s reward/promotion text still going through `queue_system_text` rather than `npc_quiet_say`. Iteration 119 closed the Advisor driver's own `struct cost_data` sales-economy gap and its admin-only qa code 18: `CostData`/`MilitaryAdvisorStorage`/`MilitaryAdvisorStorageRegistry` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`), mirroring `MilitaryMasterStorage`/`MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry`'s in-memory-only shape exactly - only `earned`/`sold` per favor-size slot are ported, since C's own `amount[20]`/`date[20]` rolling sale-history window and `created` timestamp exist solely to feed `calc_cost`'s market-pricing formula (`tool.c:3187-3215`) and `calc_cost` is never called anywhere in the C tree (confirmed via `grep -rn calc_cost src/`), so those fields would be dead weight with no reader (documented on `CostData`'s own doc comment). `add_cost(ppd->advisor_cost, dat->storage_data + ppd->advisor_storage_nr)` (`military.c:2421`) is now wired into `World::process_favor_payment` right after the gold deduction, matching C's own call order (`update_advisor_storage`'s own async-DB-blob state-machine kickoff is not reproduced, same rationale as `process_master_storage` never being ported). Added `MilitaryAdvisorEvent::Info` (qa code 18, `military.c:2525-2538`), gated on the speaker's `CharacterFlags::GOD` exactly like the Master driver's own codes 18-21, and `apply_military_advisor_info` in `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs` (mirrors `apply_military_master_info`'s shape, but needs no `PlayerRuntime` at all since every value lives on the NPC's own storage registry). Also added `ADVISOR_INFO_FAVOR_NAMES` (`["small", "normal", "big", "huge", "vast"]`) as its own table distinct from `favor_size_name`'s `["small", "medium", "big", "huge", "vast"]` - C's own `handle_advisor_message` info branch genuinely uses a different static array than `offer_favor`'s switch (index 1 is "normal" vs "medium"), reproduced verbatim as a real (if seemingly accidental) C inconsistency rather than "fixed". 2 new tests plus new assertions in an existing test, all in `world/tests/military.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 1707 ugaris-core [+2] + 51 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green; `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings; boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for "Military ranks" now (before iteration 120): DB persistence for `MilitaryAdvisorStorageRegistry` (in-memory only, following `PgMilitaryMasterStorageRepository`'s exact pattern), the wealth-achievement ladder wiring, the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT` packet, and `complete_mission`/`promote`'s reward/promotion text still going through `queue_system_text` rather than `npc_quiet_say`. Iteration 120 closed the DB-persistence gap for `MilitaryAdvisorStorageRegistry`, directly mirroring iteration 118's Master storage repository: `migrations/0011_military_advisor_storage.sql` (`military_advisor_storage(storage_id integer primary key, storage_json jsonb, updated_at)`, one row per Military Advisor NPC's `storage=N;` id, same shape as the Master's own table) plus `crates/ugaris-db/src/military.rs` (`MilitaryAdvisorStorageRepository` trait + `PgMilitaryAdvisorStorageRepository`, `save_registry` upserting one row per entry via the registry's already-public `iter()`, `load_registry` rebuilding the whole registry via `from_rows()` - both methods already existed on `MilitaryAdvisorStorageRegistry` since iteration 119, so no `ugaris-core` changes were needed this iteration). Added `Database::military_advisor_storage()` in `crates/ugaris-db/src/lib.rs` alongside the Master's own constructor. Wired boot-time load and a once-a-minute `dirty`-gated save into `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, directly mirroring the existing `military_master_storage_repository` load/save blocks (the startup repository tuple grew one more `Option<PgMilitaryAdvisorStorageRepository>` slot). 4 new tests in `ugaris-db`'s `military.rs` (SQL-shape assertion, a `serde_json` round-trip test building a synthetic `MilitaryAdvisorStorage` via `serde_json::from_value` against its field names rather than any crate-private mutator, 2 `live` DB tests following the Master repository's own "skip without failing when `DATABASE_URL` is unset" convention). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1707 ugaris-core + 55 db [+4] + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration's load/save blocks live in the runtime tick loop and startup path). REMAINING for "Military ranks" now (before iteration 121): the wealth-achievement ladder wiring on `complete_mission`'s gold bonus, the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT` packet, and `complete_mission`/`promote`'s reward/promotion text still going through `queue_system_text` rather than `npc_quiet_say`. Iteration 121 closed the wealth-achievement ladder gap: C's `complete_mission` pays its mercenary bonus gold through `give_money` (`military.c:1391`), which (`tool.c:1475-1481`) also tracks `achievement_add_gold_earned` (whole-gold units, `val / 100`) whenever the payout is positive and the character is a player - `World::complete_mission` only ported `give_money`'s inlined gold-add/message half, not this achievement half (by design, since it needs the DB-backed first-unlock announce living in the server crate). Wired at `apply_military_master_nearby_player`'s one real call site (`crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`): a `Completed` outcome with `gold_awarded > 0` now calls the already-existing, already-tested `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` helper (same "silver amount, `CF_PLAYER`-gated, `/100` integer division" shape `swap`'s `IF_MONEY` branch uses) with `gold_awarded` as the silver price. Both `apply_military_master_nearby_player` and its dispatcher `apply_military_master_events` became `async fn` to support the awaited achievement-announce tail (mirroring `apply_clanmaster_events`'s own shape); `main.rs`'s one call site now passes `&achievement_repository` and `.await`s it. 2 new end-to-end tests in new file `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/military.rs` (registered in `tests/mod.rs`), each driving a real `process_military_master_actions` nearby-player scan (not a hand-built event) into `apply_military_master_events`: one mercenary-profession completion asserting `achievement_stats.gold_earned`/`Character.gold` land correctly, one non-mercenary completion asserting the wealth ladder stays untouched when `gold_awarded` is 0. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1707 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 555 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for "Military ranks" now: the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT` quest-log packet, and `complete_mission`/`promote`'s reward/promotion text still going through `queue_system_text` rather than `npc_quiet_say`. Iteration 123 closed the promotion/reward-text half of that REMAINING note: split `World::give_military_pts` into a private `give_military_pts_core` (shared point/rank math + the above-Sergeant-Major server broadcast) plus two public wrappers matching C's two distinct functions exactly - `give_military_pts` (C `give_military_pts_no_npc`, `tool.c:3281-3306`: private "You've been promoted to X!" system text, no name, used by `/milexp` and the Area 25 `warpbonus_driver`) and the new `give_military_pts_from_npc` (C `give_military_pts`, `tool.c:3250-3277`: the Military Master NPC's own `say(cn, ...)` - "You've been promoted to X. Congratulations, NAME!" - ported as `npc_quiet_say(master_id, ...)`, matching every other line in this NPC's driver). Found and fixed a latent text bug while doing this: the previous single `give_military_pts` had the NPC variant's "Congratulations, NAME!" text but the no-npc variant's private delivery mechanism, matching neither C function exactly. Rewired qa code 21 ("promote", `military.c:2083-2089`, which C itself calls via the NPC-announcing `give_military_pts(cn, co, 100, 1)`) from `give_military_pts` onto `give_military_pts_from_npc`. Also fixed `World::complete_mission` itself (`military.c:1394,1418`'s `say(cn, ...)` calls): its "Well done"/promotion lines now go through `self.npc_quiet_say(master_id, ...)` instead of `self.queue_system_text(character_id, ...)` - the mercenary gold-received line stays a private system message since that one genuinely is `give_money`'s own `log_char` (`tool.c:1470-1471`). Updated 3 existing tests in `world/tests/military.rs` whose `master_id` was a nonexistent `CharacterId(999)`/`(2)` (now spawns a real NPC so `npc_quiet_say` actually fires) and whose assertions moved from `drain_pending_system_texts` to `drain_pending_area_texts` for the NPC-speech lines; 2 new tests for `give_military_pts_from_npc` (announces via NPC speech with the "Congratulations" text; unknown-character no-op). REMAINING for "Military ranks" now: only the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT`/`mod_send_info_sync` mod-packet halves of `sendquestlog` (confirmed out of scope - the "Client command audit completion" P1 task already established mod-protocol packets as logged no-ops, not a tracked gap). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1709 ugaris-core [+2] + 55 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 557 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. |
| `src/system/arena.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/arena.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/arena.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/teleport.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | The per-character `arena_ppd` data model, the pure `score_fight` win/loss/score math (`PlayerRuntime::arena_fight_worth`/`record_arena_fight_result`, now split into `apply_arena_win`/`apply_arena_loss` halves - see the "Continuation Handoff" bullet below), `master_driver` (`CDR_ARENAMASTER = 48`), `fighter_driver` (`CDR_ARENAFIGHTER = 49`, the autonomous practice-bot), and `manager_driver` (`CDR_ARENAMANAGER = 50`, the arena-rental NPC - despite this file's own "paid" naming, C's `manager_driver` never touches gold) are all ported and wired into the live tick loop: contender registration/pairing (`add_contender`/`find_contender`), the box-entry gate and fight-timeout/win/loss/draw detection (`check_inside`/`check_fight`), the post-fight kick-out (`empty_arena`), `enter`/`leave` text commands, `NT_GIVE` handling, the server-wide `update_toplist` ranking table (`World::arena_update_toplist`/`arena_toplist_entries`, in-memory only - no DB/file persistence yet, same gap as `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry`), the fighter bot's own `FS_LEISURE`/`FS_START`/`FS_REGISTER`/`FS_WAIT`/`FS_ENTER`/`FS_WAIT2`/`FS_FIGHT` state machine (narrowed to a single tracked enemy instead of the generic `fight_driver_data`, same precedent as `world/gate_fight.rs`; its own win/loss ledger lives directly on `ArenaFighterDriverData` since it has no `PlayerRuntime`, resetting on respawn/restart - the same class of gap as the ranking table's own missing persistence), and the rental manager's `rent`/`invite:`/`enter`/`leave` state (`ArenaManagerDriverData`, entirely self-contained within `World`, no `PlayerRuntime` interaction at all). `apply_arena_master_events` falls back to the fighter's local ledger whenever a `FightScored` participant isn't a real player, instead of silently dropping the event. `main.rs`'s `ArenaToplist` item-driver outcome renders real `arena_toplist_lines` output instead of emitting nothing. Porting `manager_driver`'s `leave` (which teleports onto the manager NPC's own occupied tile) surfaced and fixed a real latent bug shared by every arena driver: `World::arena_teleport_char_driver` was calling the exact-tile-only `teleport_character_exact` instead of `teleport_character(..., extended=false)` (C's real `drop_char`: exact tile then its 8 neighbors) - a prior iteration had misread `drop_char`'s 4th `nosteptrap` parameter as a "search radius" flag. REMAINING: DB/file persistence for the ranking table and the fighter bot's own local ledger (shared architectural gap - no generic storage-blob primitive exists anywhere in this codebase yet). See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Arena rankings" P3 entry (now `[x]`) for the full history. |
| `src/module/weather/weather.c`, `src/module/weather/weather_client.c` | `crates/ugaris-server/src/weather.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/weather.rs`, `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/mod_weather.rs` | Was admin-command-only scaffolding (a `WeatherState` mutated only by `/setweather`/`/clearweather`/`/setareaweather`/`/weather`, with a `calculate_weather_effects` that didn't actually match C's table). Now has the full autonomous weather cycle: a digit-for-digit port of `weather.c:148-192`'s `weather_effects[type][intensity]` table (`WeatherEffectData`/`WEATHER_EFFECTS`, fixing a real bug where the old simplified version never set `WEATHER_EFFECT_SLOW` for Fog), the desert-area (19/20) clear-only fix to `is_weather_allowed_in_area` plus a new `area_has_weather`, `get_current_season`/`update_weather`'s season-change and periodic-change branches (`current_season`/`pick_seasonal_weather`/`pick_intensity`/`start_weather_transition`/`update_weather_transition_tick`/`update_weather_tick`, RNG-injectable via the `impl FnMut(i32) -> i32` pattern from `rng.rs`/`spawns.rs`), the `SV_MOD2`/`SV_VIS_WEATHER` client packet (`mod_weather.rs`, byte-exact against the sibling `Ugaris_Protocol` repo's header) with `day_night_position`/`transition_progress_byte`/`weather_packet_bytes`/`broadcast_weather_packet`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop (autonomous-change broadcast) and the 3 admin commands (immediate broadcast on mutation, matching C's `cmd_set*weather` each calling `broadcast_weather_packet()` synchronously), and real per-tick outdoor damage (`World::apply_weather_damage` porting `handle_weather_damage`'s `CF_GOD`/`CF_IMMORTAL`/indoors/`CF_PLAYER` guards, wired per-player with an independent `RANDOM(TICKS*12)` roll each tick, not one shared roll for the whole area). 19 new `ugaris-server` tests + 6 new `ugaris-core`/`ugaris-protocol` tests. REMAINING: movement-speed/visibility-range/skill-value modifiers (`modify_movement_speed`/`modify_visibility_range`/`modify_skill_value`) aren't wired into `do_action::speed_ticks`/`see::char_see_char` (many call sites, needs its own slice - the effect table already has `move_mod`/`vis_mod` ready); lightning strikes/elemental debuffs are unported (no Rust representation of the C-internal `WEATHER_EFFECT_LIGHTNING`/`_COMBAT`/`_ELEMENTAL` flags); slip is intentionally not ported (C's own `apply_weather_effects` has it permanently disabled, dead code in the C tree too); the multi-server mirror storage sync/DB persistence are N/A (single-area-per-process architecture, no generic storage-blob concept in `ugaris-db` - same gap the Arena rankings task's toplist flags). Iteration 125 closed the initial per-login/area-change weather send: `init_player_weather_packet` (`weather.rs`) inlines C `init_player_weather`'s three mutually-exclusive-and-exhaustive branches (no-weather area forced indoor Clear; indoor tile in a weather-capable area gets real weather+`INDOOR` flag with hardcoded transition/day-night bytes; outdoors gets the real computed transition/day-night bytes, weather coerced to Clear if disallowed) into one pure packet-building function, using the new `area_weather_type` helper (C `get_area_weather`) for the `affected_areas` pinning gate. Wired into `login_bootstrap_payloads` (`login.rs`, gained a `weather: &WeatherState` parameter) so every login (fresh spawn/DB-loaded/reclaimed lostcon) now appends a weather packet computed from the login character's own tile - this Rust server has no in-process area-change path yet, so only the login call site exists to wire. 4 new tests. Iteration 126 wired the first of the three remaining modifiers, `modify_movement_speed` (`weather.c:477-493`), for player walking: `do_action::speed_ticks_with_weather_movement` (new, `speed_ticks` now delegates to it with `100`=no-op) folds the `speedy = speedy * weather_movement_percent / 100` step into the shared duration formula exactly where C's `speed()` calls `modify_movement_speed`; `do_walk` gained a `weather_movement_percent: i32` parameter and resolves the indoor-tile override itself from its own already-fetched current tile (matching C's `map[m].flags & MF_INDOORS` check using the *character's* position, not an area-global). `GameSettings::weather_movement_percent` (default `100`) is the new `ugaris-core`-visible resolved value, following the `exp_modifier` precedent; all 4 `do_walk` call sites in `world/actions.rs` now read `self.settings.weather_movement_percent`. Server side, `current_movement_percent` (`weather.rs`) mirrors the existing `weather_damage_amount` pattern to resolve the `WEATHER_EFFECT_SLOW` flag + `move_mod` table lookup into one percentage without `ugaris-core` ever seeing the weather table; `main.rs`'s tick loop refreshes `world.settings.weather_movement_percent` every tick, gated on `area_has_weather` like the existing damage roll. 5 new tests. REMAINING (updated): visibility (`modify_visibility_range`) and skill-value (`modify_skill_value`) modifiers are still fully unported (~28 `char_see_char` call sites with no weather-aware parameter yet, and `modify_skill_value` additionally needs the collapsed `has_skill_mod: bool` restored to a real `skill_mods: [i32; V_MAX]` table first); `speed_ticks`'s ~25 non-walking call sites (attack/spell/heal/use durations) still use the weather-unaware wrapper since most of those `do_*` functions don't take a `MapGrid` parameter for the indoor check - a documented gap vs. C's uniform `speed()` application, left for a future slice; lightning strikes/elemental debuffs and the toplist-style storage-blob gap are unchanged. Iteration 127 ported `handle_lightning_strike` (`weather.c:534-575`) end-to-end and fixed a genuine pre-existing gap in `handle_weather_damage`'s tick (the per-weather-type `log_char` message on a damage hit was never queued). Added a real `lightning_chance` field to `WeatherEffectData`/`WEATHER_EFFECTS` (only `MOD_WEATHER_STORM` is nonzero: 5/15/30% at Light/Moderate/Heavy) and the new server-internal `WEATHER_EFFECT_LIGHTNING` (`0x100`) bit. `World::character_weather_eligible` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/weather.rs`) factors the shared player-only/no-gods-immortals/no-indoors guard out of `apply_weather_damage` and a new sibling `World::apply_lightning_strike_damage` (`armor_divisor=0` instead of `1` - lightning bypasses armor). New `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/mod_sfx.rs` module (`SV_MOD2`/`SV_VIS_SFX` packet family, byte-exact `sv_sfx_packet` against the sibling `Ugaris_Protocol` repo's `mod_sfx.h`) backs a full port of `broadcast_weather_thunder_effect` (`weather_client.c:313-337`) in `weather.rs`, sending every in-radius player a positional lightning-bolt SFX plus a distance-fading screen-flash SFX - including a genuine C integer-truncation quirk preserved digit-for-digit (`thunder_screen_flash_intensity`'s `(uint8_t)(200-dist*10)` cast-before-floor wraparound past `dist=20`). Also confirmed via full-tree grep that C's own nearby-players text broadcast for the lightning strike is unreachable dead code in C itself (`log_char`'s `LOG_INFO` gate always fails when `dat1=0`, which this call site hardcodes) - intentionally not ported, documented inline. Separately confirmed `modify_visibility_range`/`modify_skill_value`/`modify_attack_speed`/`modify_spell_power`/`modify_fire_resistance`/`modify_cold_resistance`/`send_weather_screen_flash` are all **permanently dead C code** (zero non-`weather.c` callers anywhere in the tree), deprioritizing that whole REMAINING item versus merely "unwired". Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop with the same per-player independent-roll shape as the damage tick, checking eligibility *before* spending an RNG call to match C's guard-before-roll order exactly. 19 new tests (8 `ugaris-core`, 2 `ugaris-protocol`, 9 `ugaris-server`). See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Weather driver" P3 entry for the itemized remaining list (now: `apply_elemental_debuffs`'s flavor-text-only half, and `speed_ticks`'s non-walking call sites). Iteration 128 closed the melee-attack slice of that non-walking-call-sites gap: C's `do_attack`/`act_attack` (`system/do.c:424`) calls the same unconditional `speed(cn, ...)` as `do_walk`, so `do_action::do_attack` gained a `weather_movement_percent: i32` parameter, switched from `speed_ticks` to `speed_ticks_with_weather_movement`, and resolves its own attacker-tile indoor override (mirroring `do_walk`'s pattern, using the `map: &MapGrid` parameter it already had). All 3 real call sites - the player `Kill` action setup in `world/actions.rs`, plus both NPC melee call sites in `world/npc_fight.rs` (`simple_baddy` and `attack_driver_direct`) - now pass `self.settings.weather_movement_percent`, so melee combat (both player- and NPC-initiated) is now slowed by weather exactly like walking is, with no gameplay-visible change when weather is clear (percent stays 100). 2 new tests mirroring the existing `do_walk` weather-percent pair. REMAINING for this sub-item: `speed_ticks`'s other ~24 non-walking, non-attack call sites (spell cast/heal/use durations across `do_action.rs`/`spells.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`) still use the weather-unaware wrapper. Iteration 129 closed that remaining item entirely: confirmed via `do.c` grep that `take_item`/`use_item`/`drop_item` (`do.c:232,290,367`) and all 13 spell-cast/heal/bless functions (`do.c:630`-`1205`) call the same unconditional `speed(cn, ...)` as `do_walk`/`do_attack`. Added a shared `do_action::resolve_weather_movement_percent` helper (the indoor-tile-override check factored out, without retroactively touching `do_walk`/`do_attack`'s own already-passing inline copies) and threaded `map`/`weather_movement_percent` through `do_take`/`do_use`/`do_drop` (via `set_timed_item_action`), `do_magicshield`/`do_pulse`/`do_warcry`/`do_freeze`/`do_flash`/`do_firering`/`do_fireball`/`do_ball`/`do_earthrain`/`do_earthmud`/`do_heal`/`do_bless` - all 15 now call `speed_ticks_with_weather_movement`. Updated every real call site (`world/actions.rs`'s immediate and queued player-action dispatch, `world/spells.rs`'s fireball/ball/bless/heal setup helpers, `world/janitor.rs`'s NPC use/take/drop, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s/`world/npc_idle.rs`'s simple-baddy spell attacks and self-buffs, including widening the shared `setup_simple_baddy_spell_action` closure signature) to read the same `self.settings.weather_movement_percent` field `do_walk`/`do_attack` already use. 4 new tests plus updated every existing direct test call site for the new parameters. This closes the weather-driver task's entire `speed_ticks` gap - only the confirmed-dead-in-C visibility/skill-value/combat modifiers and the unported `apply_elemental_debuffs` flavor-text function remain. Iteration 131 closed that last item and the weather-driver task as a whole: ported `apply_elemental_debuffs` (`weather.c:614-655`) as a periodic (once-per-10-real-seconds-per-character) flavor-text notification only ("You are getting soaked by the rain."/"The cold is seeping into your bones."/"The scorching heat is draining your energy."), since the function's own persistent `elemental_debuff[cn]`/`elemental_debuff_expire[cn]` state and its internal "wearing off" branch are provably dead code too (the only 4 consumers of `get_elemental_debuff` are the already-confirmed-dead `modify_attack_speed`/`modify_spell_power`/`modify_fire_resistance`/`modify_cold_resistance`, and the call-site gate on `WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` guarantees the function is never invoked with a `DEBUFF_NONE` current cell, so its own internal clear-on-expire branch can never run either). Added `WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` (`0x400`), the `DEBUFF_*` constants, and a restored `elemental_debuff_type` column on `WeatherEffectData`/`WEATHER_EFFECTS` (wet for rain/storm, cold for snow, scorched for sandstorm, none for clear/fog); `WeatherState` gained `elemental_debuff_last_notify: HashMap<CharacterId, u64>` (C's `static int last_notify[TOTAL_MAXCHARS]`), cleared on disconnect. Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop alongside the damage/lightning rolls, gated on `WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` and the same `character_weather_eligible` guard. 6 new tests plus fixture updates for the new struct field/effects bit. With this, every item in the weather-driver task is now either ported or confirmed permanently dead/N/A in C itself - the task is fully closed (`PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`). |

| `src/system/loot/loot.c`/`.h` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/loot.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/death.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/loot.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs` | Iteration 155: the full JSON loot-table data model + parser + roll engine ported to `world/loot.rs` (`LootTable`/`LootGroup`/`LootEntry`/`LootCondition`/`LootPity`, `LootRegistry` with `load_str`/`find`/`pity_get`/`pity_set`/`clear_tables`), transcribed digit-for-digit from `parse_entry`/`parse_condition`/`parse_group`/`parse_table`/`parse_document`/`roll_group`/`roll_table`/`pick_weighted`/`compose_modifier`/`eval_condition` - including the `LCOND_QUEST_OPEN` "permissive `!questlog_isdone` proxy, not real `is_open`" quirk the C source's own comment calls out. Reuses `GameSettings::loot_modifiers` (added in iteration 152) for the modifier registry instead of duplicating it; pity counters are new state on `LootRegistry`. Condition evaluation takes a `LootQuestContext` trait (implemented for `crate::quest::QuestLog`) plus a `LootKiller` bundle (character id, level, quest ref), so the core roll engine needs no dependency on the server-owned `PlayerRuntime` - `World::characters` supplies the killer's level directly, the caller supplies the quest log. `World::die_character` queues a `PendingDeathLootRoll` (container id, killer id, template key) at the exact point C calls `apply_death_loot_for_template(ct, co, tmp)` (`death.c:741`, only for non-player deaths that got a body/container). Server-side `loot.rs` recursively scans `ugaris_data/loot/**/*.json` at startup (`resolve_loot_root`/`load_loot_tables`, mirroring `resolve_zone_root`'s `../` fallback and `load_zone_template_dir`'s warn-and-skip pattern) and `apply_pending_death_loot_rolls` drains the queue every tick right after the military-mission-check drain in `main.rs`, building the killer context from `World::characters` (level + `CF_PLAYER` gate) and `ServerRuntime::player_for_character` (quest log) - `None` whenever either is unavailable, the closest reachable equivalent of C's `valid_killer` gate given the architecture split. `serde_json` promoted from a `ugaris-core` dev-dependency to a real one. 29 new tests (21 in `world/tests/loot.rs`, 2 in `world/tests/death.rs`, both using the real `ugaris_data/loot/pents/{demons,equipment}.json` content transcribed as fixtures) plus a boot-smoke log line (`loaded loot tables ... tables_added=9`) confirming the real fixture data parses cleanly at startup. Iteration 157: wired spawn-mode (`loot_table=`/`loot_apply_to_npc`, `create.c:1121-1125`). `World::loot_apply_to_npc` added alongside `loot_apply_to_container`, both now sharing a single `roll_loot_table`/`roll_loot_group`/`place_loot_item` implementation parameterized by a new private `LootSink` enum (`Container(ItemId)` | `Npc(CharacterId)`, mirroring C's `struct DropSink`'s "at most one of cn/container is set") instead of duplicating the roll/group/placement logic per mode - `place_loot_item`'s `Npc` arm places into the first free slot in `character.inventory[30..INVENTORY_SIZE]` (C `place_in_npc`, `loot.c:665-675`), a no-op when every carried slot is full, exactly like C's `place_item`+`free_item` fallback. Wired at the two call sites verified to matter: `ZoneLoader::apply_map_directives`'s `MapDirective::Character` branch (the zone-population path every regular area `.chr` NPC goes through, C `pop_create_char`) and `ugaris-server`'s `respawn_npc_character` (C `respawn_callback`'s recreate-from-template path, which also runs through `create_char_nr` in C and must roll the table again on every respawn). REMAINING: the ~8 other exotic one-off NPC-creation call sites in `crates/ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs` (edemon/fdemon gate, chestspawn, warp-trial-fighter, swampspawn, teufel-ratnest, lq-npc, gatekeeper `enter_test` opponents) are not wired - none of their templates carry `loot_table=` today, so this is forward-compatible groundwork, not a known-missing behavior; wire the same 3-line `loader.character_templates.get(key).loot_table` + `world.loot_apply_to_npc(...)` snippet at each if a future template needs it. `loot_reload` (the admin hot-reload half, `command.c:8193`) still has no admin-command call site (`LootRegistry::clear_tables` exists, unused). See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Death-mode loot tables" P3 entry. |
| `src/module/events/events.c`/`.h`, `src/module/events/recurring/*.c`, `src/module/events/seasonal/easter_event.c` | `crates/ugaris-server/src/events.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/game_settings.rs` | Iteration 152: generic calendar-matching primitives (`is_date_in_range`/`is_time_in_range`/`is_day_matching`/`is_week_matching`, plus a from-scratch `days_from_civil`/`weekday_from_days`/`week_number` implementing glibc `strftime("%W")`, validated against real `date +%W` output) and all five recurring boosted-rate events (Double Experience Thursday, Double Drop Rate Tuesday, Double Experience & Drop Rate Weekend, Mining Monday, Mining Wednesday) ported as `RecurringEventKind`/`RecurringEventsState`, wired into `main.rs`'s existing once-a-minute tick gate. Added `GameSettings::loot_modifiers` (`get_loot_modifier`/`set_loot_modifier`, default 1.0) matching C `loot_get_modifier`/`loot_set_modifier` for the `event_drop_rate` hook. Preserved a real C quirk: Mining Monday/Wednesday's `_end` hooks hardcode multipliers back to `1.0` rather than restoring their (captured-but-unread) start-time snapshot, while Double Experience Thursday/Weekend's `_end` hooks do restore their snapshot. Iteration 153 closed the "Easter is a complete gap" item: `calculate_easter_date` (Meeus/Jones/Butcher, transcribed digit-for-digit), `easter_date_range` (the ±1-week window `update_easter_dates` computes via `mktime` normalization, reproduced here by reusing the already-ported `days_from_civil`/`civil_from_unix_seconds` day-count round-trip so month/year rollover matches exactly), and `check_easter_event` (the `RECUR_YEARLY` branch of `should_event_be_active` applied to this one event, recomputing the current year's window every call rather than porting C's Jan-1-only `yearly_easter_update` cache - equivalent since the window never crosses a year boundary) plus `easter_event_start`/`_end`'s `lucky_pentagram_chance` halve-and-restore, wired as a new `EasterEventState` field on `ServerRuntime` checked in the same once-a-minute tick gate as `check_recurring_events`. `CalendarNow` gained `year`/`month`/`day` fields and `is_date_in_range` is no longer test-only. Iteration 154 ported the generic `EventDecoration` spawn/remove primitives (`spawn_event_decoration`/`remove_event_decoration`, `events.c:186-224`): `EventDecoration` struct plus the two functions, using `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template` + `MapGrid::drop_item` (C `drop_item`'s neighbor-search fallback) + `World::add_item` for spawn, and a `World::items` existence check (C's `it[decoration->in].flags` liveness guard) + `World::destroy_item` for remove (which already internally performs C's separate `remove_item_map` call), both gated on the `spawn_area != 0 && spawn_area != areaID` early-return. `#[allow(dead_code)]`'d since no currently-ported event defines any decorations (Christmas's tree is static zone data, not a runtime spawn/remove; Easter's C array is empty) - ready for a future event that needs them. Iteration 155 gave `event_drop_rate` its real consumer: `world/loot.rs`'s `compose_loot_modifier` reads `GameSettings::get_loot_modifier` for every loot-table/group whose JSON `"modifiers"` array names it. Iteration 156 closed this task (`PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`): verified via a fresh grep of the whole legacy C tree that `RECUR_DAILY`/`RECUR_MONTHLY` and the generic `schedule_one_time_event`/`cancel_event`/`get_event_bonus_multiplier` API have no call site anywhere outside `events.c` itself - genuinely dead C code, not a porting gap. REMAINING (all explicitly out of scope for this task, not gaps in it): Christmas keeps its own independent date logic in `xmas.rs` (not migrated onto this framework, same as before, still not a problem since it works); the four `mining_*_multiplier` settings have no consumer at all (belongs to the unstarted P4 "Area 12 - `mine.c`" task's `handle_mining_result` reward cascade). 17 new tests from iteration 152 (16 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/events.rs`, 1 in `game_settings.rs`), 6 new tests from iteration 153, and 6 new tests from iteration 154 (all in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/events.rs`). |
| `src/system/consistency.c` | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/consistency.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs` | Iteration 194: full Rust port of the four `consistency_check_items`/`_map`/`_chars`/`_containers` self-healing scans (453 C lines) behind the `CF_GOD`-gated `/checksanity` command (`command.c:7443-7457`), not just a command handler. `World::consistency_check` runs all four in C's own order sharing one "reference count per item id" `HashMap<ItemId, u32>` across the map/character/container passes (C's file-static `item_used` array, reset every call), returning a new `ConsistencyReport{item_errors, map_errors, char_errors, container_errors}`. Since this `World` keeps the same *duplicated*-reference data model as C (`Item::carried_by`/`x`+`y`/`contained_in` vs. `Character::inventory`/`cursor_item`, `MapTile::item`), the exact same class of desync bug remains possible and this is a meaningful port, not cosmetic. Because this Rust port has no separate `con[]` array (a "container" is just an `Item` with `content_id != 0`, contents derived on the fly from every item whose `contained_in` points back), `consistency_check_containers` walks the reverse direction from C's forward array scan - functionally equivalent since there's no forward array to desync against. No `MAXITEM`/`MAXCHARS`/`MAXMAP` bound violations are possible in a `HashMap`-keyed world, so only the "does the referenced id exist, and does it agree" class of C's checks has a Rust analogue. Wired inline into `apply_admin_character_command` right after `/clearmerchantstores`; the `/help`-text line already existed in `commands_player.rs` with no real handler behind it. 20 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/consistency.rs` (every fix branch of all four checks, plus the multi-pass case where a legitimately-carried item's stale `contained_in` is caught by the characters pass before the containers pass ever sees it) and 3 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` (clean-world report, a repaired dangling item, gate/minlen rejection). REMAINING: C's per-anomaly `elog` console line and the player-facing "You encountered a bug (consist1)..." message to the *owning* player (`consistency_check_chars`) are both intentionally not reproduced - `ugaris-core` has no logging convention and only the aggregate counts reach the admin who ran the command, the same untracked-console-side-effect convention already used for `dlog`/`write_scrollback` elsewhere. C's unconditional periodic invocation every 20 seconds (`validate_data_20s_task`, `server.c:227-234`, independent of the `/checksanity` command) is not wired into the tick loop - only the on-demand command exists; wiring the periodic sweep is a small follow-up (call `World::consistency_check` from `main.rs`'s existing 20-second monitor gate and decide whether/how to surface non-zero counts, e.g. a `tracing::warn!` line, since there's no admin listening synchronously). |

| `src/area/32/missions.c` (P4 "Area 32") | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/governor.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/governor.rs` (`MissionPpd`/`SingleMission`, `PlayerRuntime::governor`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs` (`CDR_MISSIONGIVE = 111`, `CharacterDriverState::MissionGiver`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs` (spawn-time driver-state init), `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area32.rs` (`mission_giver_driver_147`, registered in `tick_npc::run_all`) | `CDR_MISSIONGIVE` ("Mister Jones", the governor's job-board NPC) is fully ported. `start_mission`/`build_fighter` (`missions.c:678-1130`, the 41x41-tile instance-dungeon spawn "accept job Alpha/Beta/Gamma" triggers) are now also ported: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/mission_start.rs` (`World::plan_start_mission` - busy-slice search over the 6x6 area/n grid, existing-occupant/junk-item cleanup, `IID_MISSIONFIGHTER`/`_CHEST`/`_DOOR1`/`_DOOR2`/`_ENTRY` map scan and key wiring, `build_fighter_stat_values`/`special_item_tier_for_level` pure math, `mission_status_lines` HUD text - all plain `World` map/item mutation, no `ZoneLoader` needed) plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::spawn_mission_fighter` (the `ZoneLoader`/`ServerRuntime::allocate_character_id`-needing half: template instantiation, stat rescale, `mis_key`/big-boss-special-item/`armor_spell`/`weapon_spell` equip, exp/level finalize, map placement), wired through a new `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::SpawnMissionFighters` event from `governor.rs`'s case 7-9 text handler (which also now calls `teleport_char_driver` directly, no event needed for that half since `World` already owns it). Spawned fighters get a new `driver = CDR_MISSIONFIGHT = 112` (`character_driver.rs`) rather than plain `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`: C's own `mission_fighter_driver` is an unconditional `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` tail call (same reuse precedent as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`/`CDR_WARPFIGHTER`), but a distinguishable id is needed so the death hook below can tell a mission fighter apart from every other SimpleBaddy-driven NPC; the SimpleBaddy AI gates in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` (`process_simple_baddy_attack_action*`/`process_simple_baddy_noncombat_action*`, both the single-character and the all-characters-in-`self.characters` variants) are widened to also accept `CDR_MISSIONFIGHT`, same shape as every other driver already on that list. `mission_fighter_dead(cn, co)` (`missions.c:1852-1881`, `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_MISSIONFIGHT` case) is now also ported: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/mission_start.rs`'s pure `record_mission_fighter_kill`/`try_solve_mission` (the `nr = ch[cn].deaths` kill-counter `switch` and `mission_done`'s five-objective completion check, `Character::deaths` doubling as C's `fID` fighter-tier tag exactly like `build_fighter` already writes it) plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs::apply_mission_fighter_death_from_hurt_event` (needs `PlayerRuntime::governor`, which `World` cannot reach - looks up the killer's ppd, bumps the matching counter, re-prints `mission_status_lines`, and on completion promotes `active` to `solved` and announces "You've finished the job..."), registered in `world_events::mod.rs`'s hurt-event fan-out. This closes the loop for the kill-type objectives (`kill_easy`/`kill_normal`/`kill_hard`/`kill_boss`); `missionchest_driver`/`mission_done` (`IDR_MISSIONCHEST`, `missions.c:1790-1847`) is now also ported (`crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area32_missions.rs`'s pure `character.id != 0` gate returning a new `ItemDriverOutcome::MissionChestOpen`, plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::apply_mission_chest_open` for the `md->itemtemp`/key-search/cursor/`find_item`/`mission_status`/`mission_done` logic that needs `PlayerRuntime::governor` and a `ZoneLoader`), closing the `find_item` objective too - a real mission can now fully auto-solve end-to-end through live gameplay. Confirmed against the real `ugaris_data/zones/32/{monsters,chests,dungeon_doors,mission}.itm`/`newdungeon.map` data that every `IID_MISSIONFIGHTER`/`_CHEST`/`_DOOR*`/`_ENTRY` marker item and the `mis_warrior`/`mis_seyan`/`mis_beast`/`mis_mage` fighter templates this code depends on already exist and load correctly (boot-smoke on area 32 loaded 2217 placed items with no errors). *Everything downstream of `start_mission` remains unported* - see below. Ported: the full `NT_CHAR` greet/dialogue state machine (states 0/1/2, the 30s-absence `lastseenmissiongiver` reset, the `solved`-reward payout text incl. `remove_mission_items`), `offer_mission`/`offer_mission_sub` (the 3-slot Alpha/Beta/Gamma job roll including the real C quirk where the dedup check compares against still-zero-valued unrolled slots on the very first roll), the 32-entry `qa[]` small-talk table (`MISSIONGIVE_QA`) plus the separate `"show <code>"`/`"ibuy <code>"` free-text fallback matcher (`tokenize_text_words`, C's own two-stage `analyse_text_driver` structure), "fail"/"increase"/"decrease"/`CF_GOD`-only "gimme*" and "reset me", and the full `mis_rew[]` reward shop (`mission_reward_list`/`mission_show_reward`/`mission_give_reward`) - `GOLD`/`MEXP` rewards apply directly in `ugaris-core` (`Character::gold`, `World::give_military_pts_from_npc`/`army_rank_for_points` reused from the already-ported `military.c` mission system), while the 18 generic item-template rewards (12 rings, `CBPOT`, `FGPOT`, `SCPOT`) go through a `GiveItemReward`/`ShowItemReward` event pair applied server-side (`apply_mission_giver_events`, needs `ZoneLoader`/`legacy_item_look_text`) - 22 of 24 reward-table entries are fully functional; `CTPOT`/`RNORB` gracefully degrade to "out of stock" (see below). Event ordering matters here and is documented inline: `UpdatePpd` must apply before `GiveItemReward` in the same batch, since the latter's point deduction mutates `PlayerRuntime` directly and only after `ZoneLoader` confirms the item was actually created and handed over - the `ppd` snapshot taken before that is known can't include it yet. The rotating procedural "special offer" purchase (`dat->next_spec`/`ch[cn].item[30]`, qa codes 18/19) is now also ported: `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::regenerate_mission_giver_special_offers` runs the 12h `World::create_special_item` reroll once per driver call *before* `process_mission_giver_actions` (matching C's single-function ordering), `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::ShowSpecialOffer` previews it server-side, and buying is fully in-`World` (no `ZoneLoader` needed to give an item that already exists) - 23 of 24 reward-table entries are now functional. `CTPOT`'s multi-turn skill-naming flow is now also ported: the "deeper, cross-cutting gap" this row used to describe (`Item::mod_index`/`mod_value` not existing anywhere in this port) turned out to already be closed by unrelated earlier work - `Item::modifier_index`/`modifier_value` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs`) already existed and are already exercised by `world::special_item`/`world::alchemy`/several area-procedural-item systems. `mission_giver_give_reward`'s `"CTPOT"` arm (`missions.c:1202-1211`) now sets `ppd.statowed`/resets `statcnt`/`stat` and prompts for a skill count; qa codes `20`/`21`/`22` (`missions.c:1652-1681`) now set `statcnt` and prompt for skill name(s); a new `parse_stat_potion_skill_text` (`governor.rs`, C `find_skill_text`, `missions.c:293-443` - preserves two real C quirks digit-for-digit: the `"lightning"` prefix check only compares 8 of 9 characters, and `"speed"` maps to `V_SPEED`/`CharacterValue::Speed`, not `V_SPEEDSKILL`) recognizes free-text skill names in the `NT_TEXT` `NoMatch` arm once nothing else matched, accumulating into `ppd.stat[]` until `statcnt` names have been given (C's own "only `stat[0]`/`stat[1]` are checked, so a third skill always lands in `stat[2]` by elimination" quirk is also preserved). The finalize step (`missions.c:1702-1734`: `create_item("mis_potionbase")` + `mod_index`/`mod_value` stamping + `give_char_item`, success resets `statowed` to `0`, failure resets `stat[]` to `[0,0,0]` and leaves `statowed` untouched so the player can retry) is a new `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::GiveCustomStatPotion` applied server-side in `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs` (needs `ZoneLoader`, same split precedent as `GiveItemReward`). Once drunk, `mis_potionbase`'s own `driver=32`/`IDR_FLASK` template (its `arg=` bytes pre-set `shaken=1`) needed zero new code: the already-ported generic `flask_driver`/`install_beyond_potion_spell`/`recompute_character_values` pipeline already reads any item's `modifier_index`/`modifier_value` fields directly. This raises the reward-shop count to 23/24 functional entries (only `RNORB`, needing a `create_orb()`-equivalent that still does not exist anywhere in this port, remains). 20 new focused tests (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/governor.rs`: the buy/skill-count-select/single-and-multi-skill-naming/unrecognized-text/no-" skill"-substring/`"speed"`-quirk/`"lightning"`-quirk/third-slot-elimination cases; `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs`: the `GiveCustomStatPotion` event's success/per-`statcnt`-modifier-value/no-room/missing-template cases). Test note: free-text skill-naming messages needed the real upstream NT_TEXT payload shape (`"<Name> says: <text>"`, same precedent as `world::strategy_worker_trim_command_prefix`'s own tests) since `find_skill_text`'s leading skip chain consumes two whole words - plain unprefixed text like `"attack skill"` gets both words eaten by the skip itself and never matches. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3944 core [+11] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1203 server [+21], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (area-32 boot with real zone data, 2217 placed items including `mis_potionbase`). `RNORB` ("Random Orb", the last unported reward-shop entry) is now also ported: rather than adding a 5th duplicate `"Orb of {skill}"`-naming implementation, `mission_giver_give_reward`'s generic-branch fallthrough (`_ => return Some(idx)`) is left as-is (it already produces the same `GiveItemReward` event every other generic reward uses) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::apply_mission_giver_events`'s `GiveItemReward` handler special-cases `reward.itmtmp == "RNORB"` to roll one of the 32 `V_*` skills via `world.roll_legacy_random(32)`/the pre-existing `area_apply::legacy_orb_value_from_seed` (already an exact port of C's `create_orb()`'s 32-case switch, `tool.c:3678-3778`, just previously unwired to anything RNORB-related) and build the item via the pre-existing `area_apply::instantiate_orb_with_modifier` (`"empty_orb"` + `"Orb of {name}"` + `driver_data[0]=skill,[1]=1`) instead of `loader.instantiate_item_template("RNORB", ...)`, which would always fail (no such item template exists). This closes the reward-shop count to 24/24 fully functional entries; all previously-documented gaps in this row (`dlog` calls, tab-stop control bytes, `mis_rew[]` sort-as-const-table, `NT_GIVE`'s single-retry simplification) are cosmetic/dev-only and remain the only deviations. New test: `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs::apply_give_item_reward_rnorb_creates_a_random_orb_and_deducts_points` (asserts the granted item name starts with `"Orb of "`, `driver_data[1] == 1`, points deducted by 400, and the "Here you go..." trailer text). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3944 core + 1204 server [+1], all green, zero failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. |

| `src/area/33/tunnel.c` (P4 "Area 33") | `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area33/gorwin.rs`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs` (`CDR_TUNNELER_GORWIN = 158`, `CharacterDriverState::Gorwin`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/zone.rs` (spawn-time driver-state init), `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/ids.rs` (`IID_TUNNELDOOR1/2`, `IID_TUNNELENEMY1/2/3/ALL`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/area33.rs`, `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area33.rs` (`gorwin_driver_158`, registered in `tick_npc::run_all`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs` (`apply_gorwin_death_from_hurt_event`) | `gorwin_driver`/`CDR_TUNNELER_GORWIN` (the Long Tunnels entrance-lobby "Tunnel Changer" NPC) is fully ported: the 9-state (0-8) introduction sequence, the shared `analyse_text_driver`/`qa[]` table (`GORWIN_QA`, 24 rows), `handle_tunnel_info`, `initialize_gorwin_ppd` (fired on every `NT_CHAR` sighting, mirroring C's own placement ahead of the player/lostcon guard clauses), and `change_tunnel_level`'s full state machine including every RNG-driven easter egg (same-level petty-fee quip, maxed-level auto-promote quip, tiny-diff flavor line, free-default-level "forgot it was free" gag, and the six-way fee-fudge ladder with its `counts_out_loud` line) - all reusing the already-ported `PlayerRuntime::tunnel_used`/`tunnel_clevel`/`gorwin_tunnel_level` accessors (`crate::player::tunnel`, ported in an earlier `/tunnel` command iteration) via a `GorwinPlayerFacts`/`GorwinOutcomeEvent` split (`World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime`); `GorwinPlayerFacts::tunnel_used` snapshots the full `0..=MAX_TUNNEL_LEVEL` completion-count array per player per tick since `find_next_available_level`/`change_tunnel_level` both need arbitrary-index reads, unlike most `*PlayerFacts` structs which only carry scalars. The "on break"/idle-mutter/fourth-wall-break easter eggs and the `TICKS*30`-idle "return to post" (`secure_move_driver` toward `rest_x`/`rest_y`) are also ported. The `ch_died_driver`/`CDR_TUNNELER_GORWIN` -> `generic_immortal_dead` charlog-only bug line is ported (`apply_gorwin_death_from_hurt_event`, same precedent as `CDR_GATE_WELCOME`/`CDR_DUNGEONMASTER`). Standard-message-driver self-defense (`NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SEEHIT`, called with `agressive=0,helper=0` so already dead code in C) is deliberately not ported, matching the `world::npc::area29::spiritbran` precedent for immortal/no-attack stationary NPCs. **Not yet ported**: the `IDR_TUNNELDOOR`/`IDR_TUNNELDOOR2` item drivers (`tunneldoor`/`mean_door`, `tunnel.c:603-772`) and the procedural creeper-dungeon generator they trigger (`build_fighter`'s dynamic `tunnel_creeper` fighter spawn scaled by `creeper_tab[]`, `handle_block_marker`/`handle_creeper_marker`'s per-generation random marker resolution, `find_unused_sector`'s 6x2-sector busy scan, `update_exit_door`) - this is the actual "long tunnel" playable content. `give_reward` (the exp/military-rank payout on exiting via `DOOR_EXIT_EXP`/`DOOR_EXIT_MILITARY`, including the `achievement_add_tunnel_level` call the P4 task's own checkbox text calls out) lives inside `tunneldoor` and is deferred to that same follow-up slice, not Gorwin's. `IID_TUNNELDOOR1/2`/`IID_TUNNELENEMY1/2/3/ALL` item-id constants (matching `src/common/item_id.h:221-226` exactly, `DEV_ID_DB` namespace) are already added in `item_driver/ids.rs` ahead of that slice needing them. 12 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/gorwin.rs` (first-contact greeting text, `tunnel` QA -> `handle_tunnel_info`, `repeat` QA state reset, out-of-range/free-default-reset/same-level-no-fee/maxed-auto-promote/paid-fee-with-controlled-RNG-seed/insufficient-gold `level X` text-command branches, and the `is_player_in_tunnel` lobby-bounds block). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3954 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics on both area 1 (default) and area 33 (real zone data, 1 character placed - Gorwin). **Update**: `IDR_TUNNELDOOR`'s `DOOR_EXIT_EXP`/`DOOR_EXIT_MILITARY` branches plus `give_reward` in full (reward math, auto-promote ladder, `achievement_add_tunnel_level` wiring) are now also ported - `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area33_tunnel.rs` (`tunneldoor_driver`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tunnel.rs` (`TunnelRewardFacts`/`TunnelRewardOutcome`/`World::apply_tunnel_reward`), `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/tunnel.rs` (new shared `find_next_available_tunnel_level`, also now used by `gorwin.rs`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_tunnel.rs` (`dispatch_tunnel_outcome`), `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs` (`award_tunnel_level_achievement`). **Update**: `IDR_TUNNELDOOR2` (`mean_door`, `tunnel.c:736-772`) is now also ported - `item_driver::area33_tunnel::mean_door_driver`, `World::tunnel_mean_door_area_clear` (C's `check_area_clear`), and `apply_item_driver_outcome`'s `TunnelDoorAreaCheck`/`TunnelDoorFlavor` outcome arms (door-open map mutation + unconditional self-reschedule; the player-interaction branch is C's own "not implemented" flavor line), dispatched through `tick_item_use_completion.rs`'s outer match (both variants are no-ops there - the world mutation already happened inside `apply_item_driver_outcome`). **Update**: `tunneldoor`'s `DOOR_ENTRY`/`DOOR_CONTINUE` branches (`tunnel.c:638-734`) - the procedural creeper-dungeon instance scan - are now also ported, closing out this task. `World::plan_tunnel_entry` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tunnel.rs`) ports `find_unused_sector` (the 7x2-sector busy scan), the seeded-RNG marker resolution (`handle_block_marker`/`handle_creeper_marker`, C `srand(ch[cn].ID * ppd->clevel)` reproduced via the same `legacy_random_below_from_seed`-shaped local-seed precedent `crate::dungeon_maze` established - not a bit-exact libc PRNG port), `update_exit_door`, the mid-scan `IDR_TUNNELDOOR2` reset-to-closed arm, the stray-non-player-character cleanup, and the post-scan HP/teleport update; `tunnel_build_fighter_stat_values` (deliberately separate from `world::npc::area32::mission_start::build_fighter_stat_values` - the two C `build_fighter`s group skills differently, e.g. `V_HP` alone at `diff-15` vs missions.c's `V_HP`/`V_ENDURANCE`/`V_MANA` together) is the pure per-skill stat formula; the actual `tunnel_creeper` character/`equip1`/`equip2`/`armor_spell`/`weapon_spell` item instantiation (needs `ZoneLoader`) lives in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_tunnel.rs`'s new `dispatch_tunnel_enter_outcome`/`spawn_tunnel_creeper`, mirroring `area32.rs`'s `spawn_mission_fighter` plan/spawn split. The `PlayerRuntime`-dependent entry guard (block entry until Gorwin has been spoken to) and level-selection logic (`DOOR_ENTRY`'s `min(gorwin_level, char_level)` plus maxed-out warning, `DOOR_CONTINUE`'s increment-and-clamp, including C's own quirk of persisting the level change even when the following busy-sector check then refuses entry) also live in `dispatch_tunnel_enter_outcome`. New `ItemDriverOutcome::TunnelDoorEnter` variant (`item_driver::area33_tunnel::tunneldoor_driver` now dispatches all four `TunnelDoorType` values, not just the exit pair). The map-marker block/wall toggle deliberately uses `MapFlags::TMOVEBLOCK`/`TSIGHTBLOCK` rather than the literal C `MF_MOVEBLOCK`/`MF_SIGHTBLOCK`, matching this same door family's already-ported `TunnelDoorAreaCheck` open-door arm for consistency within one feature (documented deviation, not a fidelity gap - both bit-pairs are load-bearing for `path::pathfinder`). 16 new tests in `world/tests/tunnel.rs` (stat-formula grouping/250-cap, sector-scan first-slot/skip-occupied-exclude-self/all-busy, seeded block-marker open-vs-wall resolution, seeded creeper-marker single-spawn plus `CREEPER_MARKER_ALL`-always-spawns, exit-door relabeling, mean-door mid-scan reset, entry-vs-continue HP formulas, and the landing-tile teleport) plus 1 updated `item_driver/tests/area33_tunnel.rs` test (the old "entry/continue fall through to `Unsupported`" assertion now asserts `TunnelDoorEnter`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3985 core [+31 net] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics on both area 1 (default) and area 33 (real zone data, 4232 placed items). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 33 marked `[x]`. |

## Continuation Handoff

Use this section as the starting point for the next session.

### Design Plan: Cross-Area Transfer (P3 task, written iteration 220)

**Decision: N OS processes, one area+mirror per process, DB-mediated
handoff.** No in-process multi-area mode. This was already the implied
target architecture before this plan was written - `Args`/`ServerConfig`
take a single `--area-id`/`--mirror-id`, `World::area_id` is a scalar set
once at startup and never mutated, and one `ZoneLoader`/map exists per
process - reworking that to host multiple areas in one process would be
a much larger, higher-risk change (dozens of call sites assume "this
process's one area") for no real benefit versus just running more
processes, which is also how the legacy C server itself was deployed
(one binary per area, per `server.c`'s own `areaID`/`areaM` globals).

**Mechanism, reverse-engineered from the C source** (`src/system/
player.c` `player_to_server`/`kick_char`/`change_area`, `src/system/
database/database_character.c` `change_area`/`get_area`/`update_arealist`,
`src/system/database/database_area.c` `area_alive`): cross-area transfer
in C is **entirely client-driven reconnect**, not a server-to-server live
character handoff:

1. The sending area server decides a character should move (a teleport
   destination resolves to a different `area_id`, or login discovers
   `allowed_area != this_area_id`).
2. It looks up the target's server/port via `get_area` (backed by an
   `area` DB table each area server keeps fresh with a periodic
   `area_alive(0)` heartbeat UPDATE, `alive_time`/`idle`/`players`/etc.,
   with a `time_now - alive_time < 300` staleness check standing in for
   "is this area server actually still running").
3. If found: for a mid-game teleport, `change_area` sets `tmpx/tmpy/tmpa`
   + `CF_AREACHANGE`, calls `kick_char` (which removes the character from
   the live map and **saves it to DB with the *target* area/coords
   already written**, i.e. `save_char(cn, target_area)`), then
   `player_to_server` sends the 7-byte `SV_SERVER` packet (raw IP +
   port, no byte-order conversion) and marks the session `ST_EXIT`. For a
   fresh login that resolves to the wrong area server, `read_login` does
   the `get_area`+`player_to_server` call directly with no save needed
   (nothing was loaded into memory on this server yet).
4. The **client**, seeing `SV_SERVER`, disconnects and reconnects to the
   new address/port with the same login credentials.
5. The **receiving** area server's own `read_login` sees the DB row's
   `current_area`/`allowed_area` now already matches its own `area_id`
   (written by step 3's `save_char`), so login proceeds normally as a
   `Ready` outcome - no special "arrival" packet or in-memory handoff
   blob is ever sent between the two server processes. Postgres is the
   only channel; the two server processes never talk to each other
   directly at all.

This is good news for the Rust port: it means "cross-area transfer" does
**not** require any new inter-process protocol or shared memory - every
piece of state involved (target area/coords, area-server liveness) is
already expressed as ordinary Postgres rows this codebase already has
migrations and repository types for (`characters.current_area`/
`allowed_area`, the `area_servers` table). The work is almost entirely
"plumb the already-scaffolded pieces together", not new distributed-
systems design.

**What iteration 220 ported** (see the `crates/ugaris-db/src/area.rs`
ledger row and the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry for
detail): the `SV_SERVER` encoder, `AreaRepository` wired into
`ugaris-server` (startup `mark_alive`/shutdown `mark_down`, `--public-
addr` CLI arg), and the **login-side** half of the redirect (`read_login`'s
area-routing branch) - a login that resolves to the wrong area server
now actually redirects to a live registered target instead of always
rejecting with "down".

**What iteration 221 ported:** the first slice of item 1 below - a
reusable `crates/ugaris-server/src/cross_area.rs::attempt_cross_area_
transfer` async helper (lookup via `AreaRepository::get_area` ->
save via a new `snapshots.rs::character_area_transfer_save_request`
builder, which is exactly `character_save_request`'s existing
`CharacterSaveMode::Logout` machinery but with `allowed_area`/`mirror`
set to the *destination* instead of this server's own `area_id`/
`mirror_id`, and the snapshot's `x`/`y` overwritten with the
destination coordinates since this codebase has no separate `tmpx`/
`tmpy`/`tmpa` fields to carry them the way C does -> `World::
remove_character` despawn -> the already-built `PacketBuilder::
server_redirect` + session disconnect, reusing `login.rs::
area_redirect_payload` for the liveness check exactly like the
login-side redirect), wired into exactly one of the seven call sites
below: transport-point travel (`TransportTravelResult::CrossArea` in
`main.rs`'s `TransportTravel` outcome handler). The other six call
sites listed in item 1 are unchanged and still only send the "down"
message - each should become a one-line switch to calling
`attempt_cross_area_transfer` (or, for the two `world/`-side callers
which have no direct path to an async DB call, a thin `ugaris-server`
event-drain wrapper around it, matching the `world_events.rs`
offline-lookup convention `world/jail.rs` already uses for its other
DB-touching half).

**What's still missing, in priority order for whoever picks this up
next:**

1. ~~The remaining mid-game teleport call sites.~~ **Done (iterations
   221-225).** `attempt_cross_area_transfer` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/
   cross_area.rs`) is now wired at every `area_id != config.area_id` call
   site: transport-point travel, `/office`+`/goto`/`/jump`
   (`commands_admin.rs`), mine gateway, clan-spawn exit (`main.rs`),
   `/jail`/`/unjail` (via `World::pending_jail_cross_area_transfers` +
   `world_events.rs::apply_jail_cross_area_transfers`), and the
   dungeon-master eviction rescue (via `World::
   pending_dungeon_eviction_transfers` + `apply_dungeon_eviction_
   transfers`) - see the "Cross-area transfer" `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's
   iteration 222-225 Progress Log notes for each site's own detail. (This
   bullet was stale until iteration 227 corrected it - the strikethrough
   above was missing even though the todo file already recorded every
   site as wired.)
2. ~~Periodic `mark_alive` heartbeat.~~ **Done (iteration 226).** C's own
   `area_alive(0)` is re-run from `maintenance_60s_task` every 85 seconds
   (`server.c:600`); `main.rs` now has a matching `world.tick.0 %
   (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 85) == 0` gate in the tick loop calling a new
   shared `mark_area_alive` helper (same startup call, factored out),
   right alongside the other once-a-minute-style in-loop DB maintenance
   gates (auction cleanup, clan-registry save). `AreaServerRecord` still
   doesn't expose `last_seen` staleness to callers, and `get_area` still
   only trusts the `online` flag rather than a `time_now - alive_time <
   300`-style staleness check - since Postgres is this codebase's single
   shared source of truth (no separate poll-then-cache step like C's
   `update_arealist`), a stale-but-`online=true` row only matters if a
   process crashes without running its shutdown `mark_down` path, which
   is a smaller/rarer risk than in C and left as a future hardening step
   if it ever proves necessary in practice.
3. ~~The three blocked text commands.~~ **`/exterminate` done (iteration
   227); `/showvalues` done (iteration 228); `/values`/`/allow` still
   open.** Re-reading
   `cmd_exterminate`'s full C call chain
   (`database_admin.c:29-95,503-507`) found it is a **direct DB
   mutation** (lock the target's account, ban its IP history), not a
   `server_chat` relay at all - `server_chat(31, ...)` in
   `cmd_exterminate` is only a same-message staff broadcast, unrelated to
   the command's actual effect. Ported as `CharacterRepository::
   exterminate_account` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`) + new
   `ip_bans` table (`migrations/0019_ip_bans.sql`, this codebase's
   `iplog`/`ipban` stand-in, populated from the existing `login_
   sessions.ip_address` history) + `world/exterminate.rs` (`ugaris-core`)
   + `world_events.rs::apply_exterminate_events`. Also closed a related
   pre-existing gap: `LoginOutcome::IpLocked` was only driven by a static
   per-account `accounts.ip_locked` flag nothing ever set; `begin_login_
   tx` now also checks the new `ip_bans` table for the *connecting* IP
   (`is_ip_banned`, matching C's real `isbanned_iplog` semantics -
   independent of which account is logging in), `||`-ed with the old
   flag. `/values`/`/showvalues` (`look_values`/`show_values`,
   `command.c:499-533`) and `/allow` (`allow_body`/`allow_body_db`,
   `death.c:1013-1063`) are genuinely different from `/exterminate` -
   they really do need to reach a character's live in-memory state
   (values, corpse containers) or, wherever it currently is, which
   could be a different area-server process. `/showvalues` is now done
   (iteration 228, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/values.rs` +
   `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs::apply_showvalues_events`), using
   the recommended pragmatic single-process-only slice: resolve the name
   via `find_login_target`, always send the caller the "Sent."
   confirmation once it resolves (matching C's unconditional log
   regardless of where the target actually is), but only deliver the
   caller's own class-specific stat block if the target happens to be
   loaded in *this* process's `World` - cross-process reach is an
   explicit documented gap, same as every other cross-area chat-routing
   gap already in this file. `/values` (the staff-only, fuller
   `look_values_bg` dump) remains open - it is a strictly bigger port
   than `/showvalues` since it also needs paying-player/PK/hardcore/
   playtime/bank-gold/mirror-area lines this codebase has no
   `Character`/`World` equivalent of yet (bank gold is session-scoped on
   `PlayerRuntime`, not persisted `Character`; `paid_until` is a DB-only
   `accounts` column never threaded through to `World`). `/allow`
   remains open and turns out to need real new infrastructure first: C's
   `struct container` (`container.h:22-33`) has a per-body
   `owner`/`killer`/`access` loot-ACL this codebase has **no** equivalent
   of at all - the Rust body-container is just a plain `Item` with
   `contained_in` (`world/death.rs`) and `owner_id` is never even set on
    body creation - so `/allow` needs that whole grave-loot-ownership
    model designed and wired before the command itself can mean anything;
    flag it as its own future task once picked up rather than treating it
    as a same-sized slice of this one.
    Iteration 229 closed one of `/values`' three missing-infrastructure
    gaps: **`stats_online_time`** (C `src/system/statistics.c:47-58`,
    the "Playing for %d hours." line, `tool.c:2917`). Ported the whole
    `struct stats_ppd` (`statistics.h`: a `MAXSTAT`(365)-day rolling ring
    buffer of daily exp/gold/online samples) as a new raw-blob
    `PlayerRuntime::stats_ppd` PPD (`DRD_STATS_PPD`, wired into
    `encode_legacy_ppd_blob`/`decode_legacy_ppd_blob`'s existing
    had-flag/rewrite-or-append machinery exactly like every sibling PPD),
    plus `PlayerRuntime::stats_update`/`stats_online_time`
    (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`) reproducing C's day-bucket
    zero-on-gap-and-wrap `while` loop exactly (verified with a dedicated
    test for the "wraps past bucket 0 without ever revisiting it as
    `idx`" edge case). Wired the once-a-minute `stats_update(cn, 1, 0)`
    call (`player.c:3460`) into the same tick-loop gate that already
    calls `award_play_time_minute` (`main.rs`); the two sibling
    `stats_update(cn, 0, price)` call sites (`store.c:381`/`do.c:1282`)
    feed the `.gold` field, which nothing in this codebase reads anywhere
    (confirmed by grepping the whole C tree), so they're deliberately
    left unwired. 6 new `ugaris-core` tests. `/values` itself is still
    unported: still missing `paid_until` (a DB-only `accounts` column
    never threaded through to `World`/`Character` - `/values`' resolved-
    target async round trip would need a new repository query for it)
    and live `bank_gold` access (session-scoped on `PlayerRuntime`,
    reachable via the already-threaded `ServerRuntime::
    player_for_character_mut`, so this piece is actually straightforward
    once the command itself is assembled) - `PK`/`Hardcore` flags and the
    mirror/area/section-name line (`section_name_by_id`/`section_at`
    already exist in `area_section.rs`) have no remaining gap. Next slice
    should thread `paid_until` through (likely a new `CharacterRepository`
    method returning it alongside `find_login_target`'s existing lookup,
    or a dedicated query) and then assemble the full `/values` command
    (`world/values.rs`'s sibling `queue_values_command`/`values_lines`,
    following `/showvalues`'s established queue-then-drain shape but
    *without* the role swap - C's `look_values_bg` sends the resolved
    target's info back to the caller, not the other way around, and has
    no "Sent." confirmation of its own).

### Current Runnable State

- The Rust workspace lives at the repository root (`Ugaris_Server_Rust`); the legacy C oracle lives in the sibling `Ugaris_Server` repository.
- The three former monolithic files (`world.rs`, `item_driver.rs`, `main.rs`) are split into module directories; see `Rust Module Architecture` at the top of this ledger before adding code.
- The Rust server loads real area 1 data from `ugaris_data/zones/1/above1.map` plus generic/area templates via the `ugaris_data` symlink.
- Latest real area 1 smoke counts: 65,533 ground tiles, 16,969 blocked tiles, 1,780 item templates, 188 character templates, 2,236 placed items, and 446 placed characters.
- Default login scaffold uses `generic/player.chr` template `new_warrior_m`, then renames the character to the login name and sends starter equipment/inventory to the client.
- NPC deaths now run the full kill loop: death animation, lootable body containers, decay timers, kill experience, and template respawns.
- Death-mode JSON loot tables (`ugaris_data/loot/**/*.json`) are scanned at startup and rolled into NPC corpse containers via `apply_death_loot_for_template`'s Rust port; spawn-mode (`loot_table=`) rolls into the NPC's own carried inventory (slots 30+) at zone-population and respawn time (a handful of exotic one-off spawn sites still don't call it - see the loot ledger row).
- `CDR_MERCHANT` NPCs create stores from carried stock, greet players, and trade through the legacy `con_type 2` store view after `"<name>, trade"`.

### How To Run

Build and run the Rust area server from the repository root:

```bash
cargo build -p ugaris-server
target/debug/ugaris-server --bind-addr 0.0.0.0:5556
```

Run the legacy client from the client repo:

```bash
cd /home/eddow/Development/UgarisProjects/astonia_community_client
bin/moac -u Godmode -p test123 -d localhost -t 5556 -o 3141 -c 8000 -k 60 -m 8 -n 0
```

Expected server startup log markers:

```text
legacy TCP listener ready
loaded area zone map ... placed_items=2236 placed_characters=446 ...
initialized scaffold player character id allocator next_character_id=447
entering Rust game loop
legacy login block parsed
login accepted by compatibility scaffold ... payload_count=5
```

### Verification Commands

Run from the repository root:

```bash
cargo fmt --all
cargo test --workspace
cargo build -p ugaris-server
```

Last verified after the playtest fix session (spell queue, one-frame-per-tick
pacing, LoS blacking, door pathing):

- `cargo test --workspace`: passed (1,367 tests).
- `cargo build -p ugaris-server`: passed with zero warnings.
- Process-level area 1 boot smoke: passed with all legacy startup markers.

Continue from `PORTING_TODO.md` (prioritized task list); this ledger stays
the record of completed work.

### Recent Client-Facing Fixes

- NPC duplication was mostly caused by `.map` `from/to` range copies duplicating live `item`/`character` tile IDs. `ZoneLoader` now sanitizes range copies so terrain is copied without live object IDs or temporary object blockers.
- Movement stutter was caused by sending a full visible diamond after every completed walk. One-tile walks now send `SV_SCROLL_*`, `SV_ORIGIN`, old-position character clear, center character update, and newly visible fringe tile/character packets.
- Full visible-diamond refresh is still used for non-walk actions until a proper cached map-diff system is ported.
- `PAC_LOOK_MAP` no longer drops pending output in the server loop. It now sends legacy `SV_TEXT` feedback for hidden targets (`Too far away or hidden.`), area 1 `show_section`-style section names and level-difficulty text, coordinate fallback for areas without ported section tables, and the exact rest/clan/arena/peace zone flag messages.
- The runtime text command loop now handles legacy `/sort`, sorting only inventory slots 30+ with the C ordering (empty slots last, value descending, sprite descending, then first-35-byte name ascending) and refreshing inventory after the command.

### Chest Driver State

Current implemented slices:

- `IDR_CHEST = 5` dispatches in `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver.rs`.
- The core driver decodes `drdata[0]` as `treasure_index` and returns `ItemDriverOutcome::ChestTreasure`.
- The server applies `ChestTreasure` by using the retained `ZoneLoader` to instantiate `treasure_<index>` and place it on the character cursor.
- The existing post-action inventory snapshot sends the new cursor item to the client.
- Cursor-occupied chests are blocked.
- Keyed chests check exact legacy item `ID` and skeleton key `ID` against cursor and inventory slots 30+.
- Runtime keyring state stores up to 100 legacy key IDs/names with duplicate/full checks and keyed chests consult it for exact key matches.
- Successful keyed chests send the legacy key-use feedback before the loot feedback.
- Character runtime/snapshot shape now includes a serde-defaulted `deaths` counter.
- Normal chests with `drdata[7]` require at least that many character deaths and otherwise report as empty.
- `PlayerRuntime` now tracks chest-open achievement progress and markers for looter thresholds plus gold-looter chest `63` on successful loot only.
