## Ralph Loop - Iteration 85: Clan System (P3) - Relation State Machine Slice

- Started the "Clan system" P3 task (first `[ ]` in the highest-priority
  section with remaining work; P0/P1/P2 are all `[x]`). Researched the
  full C `src/system/clan.c`/`clan.h` (1581/150 lines), every other C
  file touching clan data (character persistence, `can_attack`, chat
  channels, transport, club.c's parallel system, admin commands,
  `clanlog`/`clanoverview` SQL tables), and the existing Rust state
  (`Character.clan`/`clan_rank`/`clan_serial` fields already exist and
  round-trip via JSON snapshot; `do_action::ClanAttackPolicy` trait
  already has the exact seam shape needed, but only `NoClanAttackPolicy`
  - always `false` - existed; no `crates/ugaris-db/src/clan.rs`, no DB
  migration, no clan repository at all).
- Given the full system is five semi-independent concerns (repository/
  founding/membership, relation state machine + attack policy, chat
  gating, trade bonus, treasury/dungeon economy) - too large for one
  sitting - ported a self-contained, fully-tested slice: the pure clan
  relation escalation/de-escalation state machine, with zero I/O/DB
  dependency, as `crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs`.
- Ported: `ClanRelation` (`CS_ALLIANCE`/`PEACETREATY`/`NEUTRAL`/`WAR`/
  `FEUD`, `clan.h:41-56`, with `display_name()` matching C `rel_name[]`
  verbatim including the "Peace-Treaty" hyphen); `ClanRelations` (the
  per-pair `current_relation`/`want_relation`/`want_date` state over
  `MAX_CLAN`=32 slots, plus an `exists` flag standing in for C's
  `clan[n].name[0]` truthiness check); `found_clan`/`delete_clan`
  (`found_clan`+`clan_standards`+`zero_relation`, `clan.c:76-95,
  450-492` - resets relations to Neutral in *both* directions, i.e. the
  new clan's own row and every other clan's column toward it);
  `set_relation` (`set_clan_relation`, `clan.c:839-860` - unilateral
  relation request, only bumps the want-timestamp when the requested
  value actually changes, matching C's `if (...want_relation[onr] !=
  rel)` guard); `may_enter`/`can_attack_outside`/`can_attack_inside`/
  `alliance` (`may_enter_clan`/`clan_can_attack_outside`/
  `clan_can_attack_inside`/`clan_alliance`, `clan.c:881-934`); and the
  full `update_relations` tick (`clan.c:936-1089`) - the immediate-
  agreement fast path (`want1 == want2` bypasses the per-level switch
  entirely and jumps straight to the agreed value, regardless of how
  many levels away, which two of my own first-draft tests initially got
  wrong before re-reading the C order of checks), the 1h one-sided war-
  escalation timer from Neutral, and the 24h one-sided de-escalation
  timers from Alliance/Peace-Treaty/Feud - all 7 distinct C
  `add_clanlog` message shapes are modeled as `ClanRelationChange`
  variants (`Agreed`/`AllianceEnded`/`PeaceTreatyEnded`/`WarStarted`/
  `PeaceTreatyStarted`/`WarEnded`/`FeudEnded`) returned from `update` as
  `ClanRelationEvent`s for a future caller to format/log once clan-name
  lookup and log persistence exist.
- Implemented `do_action::ClanAttackPolicy` for `ClanRelations`
  (`are_allied`→`alliance`, `can_attack_inside_clan_area`→
  `can_attack_inside`, `can_attack_outside_clan_area`→
  `can_attack_outside`; `has_pk_hate` left at the trait's `false`
  default, unrelated PK-hate-list data), closing three stubs that
  previously had no real implementation anywhere in the tree - the
  `do_action.rs` trait/call-site plumbing (`can_attack_internal`,
  `can_attack_in_area_with_clan_policy`) needed zero changes, confirming
  the pre-existing seam was already shaped correctly for this. Added one
  new end-to-end test, `can_attack_wired_against_real_clan_relations_
  registry`, proving `ClanRelations` produces the same war/feud gating
  as the hand-written `TestClanPolicy` fixture already used by the
  existing `can_attack_clan_*` tests.
- Intentionally skipped porting C's `dungeon.doraid` relation clamp
  inside `update_relations` (`clan.c:945-968`): traced through the C
  logic and confirmed it's dead in practice after a clan's first tick
  (every clan's `doraid` is unconditionally forced to `1` the moment the
  outer loop visits it, before the inner loop's clamp check can ever see
  a `0`), and it's meaningless without the wholly unported dungeon/raid
  economy anyway - documented with a code comment explaining the
  reasoning rather than silently dropped.
- 21 new focused unit tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs` covering
  founding/relation-reset, out-of-range validation, want-timestamp
  bump-only-on-change, immediate agreement across multiple levels vs.
  the one-step switch-branch de-escalation/escalation paths, all four
  timer-gated transitions (1h war, 24h alliance/treaty/feud), `may_enter`
  (own clan/non-member/deleted-clan/alliance-only), attack-inside/
  outside gating, alliance query, clan deletion, and out-of-range-query
  safe defaults; plus the one `do_action.rs` wiring test above.
- Registered the new `pub mod clan;` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/lib.rs`.
- Marked the task `[~]` in `PORTING_TODO.md` with a precise REMAINING
  list (clan identity/treasury/dungeon economy, membership wiring onto
  `Character.clan`/`clan_rank`/`clan_serial`, `crates/ugaris-db/src/
  clan.rs` repository + migration, actually constructing a live
  `ClanRelations` in `World`/the tick loop, clan-log persistence,
  `clan_trade_bonus`, chat channel gating, clan-hall transport beyond
  direct membership) and added a new `Partial` table row plus this
  progress bullet in `PORTING_LEDGER.md`.
- Verification: `cargo fmt --all` clean. `cargo test --workspace`: 1429
  ugaris-core [+21 clan.rs, +1 do_action.rs] + 38 db + 3 net + 37
  protocol + 489 server, all green, zero failures. `cargo build -p
  ugaris-server` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke: `timeout 12 target/
  debug/ugaris-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:5556` confirmed "entering
  Rust game loop" with ticks advancing and no panics for 10+ seconds
  (  ran this since the change touches `do_action.rs`, part of the shared
  combat/attack-policy path, even though the live server still uses
  `NoClanAttackPolicy` unchanged - `ClanRelations` is not yet
  constructed anywhere outside tests).

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 86: Clan System (P3) - Identity + Membership Slice

- Health check first: `cargo test --workspace` was green (1429
  ugaris-core + 38 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 489 server) before any
  changes, so this iteration proceeded to task work.
- Resumed the `[~]` Clan system task (iteration 85 left it partially
  done with a precise REMAINING list). Read the full `clan.c`/`clan.h`
  again plus `entity.rs`'s `Character.clan`/`.clan_rank`/`.clan_serial`
  fields (already present, `PORTING_LEDGER.md` iteration 85 confirmed
  only the GM `/joinclan` cheat mutates them) and confirmed via a fresh
  grep that `ClanRelations` is still not constructed anywhere outside
  tests, and that `add_member`/`remove_member`/`get_char_clan`/
  `found_clan` had zero Rust equivalents anywhere in the workspace.
- Given the remaining work is still five semi-independent concerns
  (identity/membership, treasury/dungeon economy, DB persistence, live
  runtime wiring, chat/transport gating beyond direct membership),
  picked the next self-contained, fully-testable slice: clan identity
  (name/rank names/website/message) plus the membership functions that
  read/write `Character.clan`/`.clan_rank`/`.clan_serial`.
- Added to `crates/ugaris-core/src/clan.rs` (extends, does not replace,
  the existing `ClanRelations`):
  - `CLUB_OFFSET: u16 = 1024` (C `#define CLUBOFFSET`, `club.h:5`).
  - `ClanIdentity` (`name`/`rank_names: [String; 5]`/`website`/
    `message` - C `struct clan`'s identity fields, `clan.h:88-101`,
    minus the treasury/bonus/dungeon-guard fields left for a future
    slice), with a `standard(name)` constructor matching C
    `clan_standards`'s rank-name defaults (`clan.c:79-83`:
    `["Member","Member","Recruiter","Treasurer","Leader"]`).
  - `ClanRegistry`: wraps a `ClanRelations` plus a per-slot `[u32;
    MAX_CLAN]` serial array and `[Option<ClanIdentity>; MAX_CLAN]`.
    The serial is tracked independently of identity presence because
    C's `struct clan[MAXCLAN]` is a permanently-allocated array where
    `status.serial` survives a slot's identity being cleared and only
    increments on deletion (`clan.c:1157-1158`), then gets restored
    verbatim by a subsequent `found_clan` reusing that slot
    (`clan.c:480-484`) - modeling this as two parallel arrays (rather
    than folding serial into `Option<ClanIdentity>`) was necessary to
    reproduce that persistence-through-deletion behavior.
  - `found_clan(name, now)` (`clan.c:460-492` + `clan_standards` +
    `zero_relation`): scans for the first slot with no identity
    (never-used or previously-deleted), same as C's linear scan;
    returns `ClanFoundError::NameTooLong` (`strlen(name) > 78`) or
    `ClanFoundError::ClanListFull` (scan reaches `MAXCLAN`). Delegates
    the relation-reset half to the existing (unmodified)
    `ClanRelations::found_clan`.
  - `delete_clan(nr)`: generalizes C's bankrupt-clan deletion path
    (`update_treasure`, `clan.c:1154-1160`) to any deletion trigger -
    clears identity, bumps the slot's serial (invalidating stale
    `Character.clan_serial` references from former members), and
    delegates to `ClanRelations::delete_clan`.
  - `get_char_clan(&self, character: &mut Character)` (`clan.c:242-
    272`): validates `character.clan`/`.clan_serial` against the
    registry and clears all three fields on any mismatch, matching C's
    `ch[cn].clan = ch[cn].clan_rank = ch[cn].clan_serial = 0` exactly.
    `clan >= CLUB_OFFSET` is treated as out-of-jurisdiction (returns
    `None` but leaves the fields untouched), mirroring C's own `cnr >=
    CLUBOFFSET` early return (`clan.c:249-251`) rather than as an
    invalid value to clear - clubs are a separate, still-unported
    system (`club.c`) reusing the same character fields. Deliberately
    always fully validates, unlike C's `!update_done` skip-validation-
    during-boot-storage-load window (`clan.c:259-261`), which has no
    equivalent in this always-ready in-memory registry.
  - `char_clan_name` (`get_char_clan_name`, `clan.c:274-284`), `name`
    (`get_clan_name`, `clan.c:286-292`).
  - `add_member(character, cnr)` (`clan.c:1186-1206`): sets `clan`/
    `clan_serial` only - confirmed via careful re-reading that C's
    `add_member` never touches `clan_rank`, so a new member keeps
    whatever rank they already had (normally `0`/Member). Returns
    `ClanMembershipError::IsClub`/`NotFound` for out-of-range inputs.
    Achievement awarding (`ACHIEVEMENT_CLAN_MEMBER`/`_CLUB_MEMBER`),
    the clan-log entry, and the "everyone forgets this character's
    name" reset (`set_player_knows_name`, `clan.c:1194-1196`) are
    runtime-wide side effects explicitly left to the future caller,
    documented inline (same pattern the Achievements task's `award_*`
    helpers already establish for similar split-responsibility cases).
  - `remove_member(character)` (`clan.c:1208-1221`): unconditionally
    clears all three fields, matching C's lack of validation.
  - `set_rankname`/`set_website`/`set_message` (`clan.c:584-604,862-
    879`): length caps ported (37 chars for rank names, 79 for website/
    message). Deliberately did *not* port C's trailing-character-strip
    quirk on website/message (`website[strlen(website)-1] = 0`) since
    it depends on a raw command-line calling convention (an assumed
    trailing delimiter byte) that doesn't exist in the not-yet-wired
    Rust command layer - guessing at it now risked baking in wrong
    behavior once a real `/clan` command parser lands; documented
    inline as deferred rather than silently dropped.
- Added a `test_character()` fixture to `clan.rs` (full `Character`
  struct literal, following the exact pattern already used by
  `spell.rs`'s own `test_character()`), since this is the module's
  first slice with real `Character`-mutating side effects.
- 20 new focused unit tests: slot allocation/name-length/list-full
  errors, slot reuse after delete with serial bump verified, add/remove
  member round trip (confirming `clan_rank` is untouched by
  `add_member`), rejecting unknown/club clan numbers, stale-serial
  clearing after a clan is deleted and a *different* clan re-founded in
  the same slot (the scenario the two-array serial design exists for),
  club-number pass-through untouched, zero-clan no-op, rank-name/
  website/message validation and truncation.
- Updated the module doc comment and `PORTING_TODO.md`'s REMAINING list
  to reflect what's now done vs. still open (treasury/dungeon economy,
  DB repository/migration, live runtime wiring of `ClanRegistry` as the
  `ClanAttackPolicy` and into an actual `/clan` command instead of only
  the `/joinclan` cheat, achievement award wiring, clan-log persistence,
  chat/transport gating beyond direct membership, the deferred website/
  message trailing-strip quirk).
- Verification: `cargo fmt --all` clean. `cargo test --workspace`: 1444
  ugaris-core [+20 clan.rs tests over iteration 85's 1429 baseline]
  + 38 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 489 server, all green, zero failures.
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, zero warnings. No runtime-loop/
  login/map-sync/protocol code touched this iteration (pure
  `ugaris-core` data-model addition with no call sites wired into
  `ugaris-server` yet), so boot-smoke was not required per the recipe
  and was not re-run.

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 102: Military Ranks (P3) - Rank Table + Promotion Slice

Picked "Military ranks" (`src/module/military.c`, topmost `[ ]` P3 task -
the "Clan system" `[~]` task above it was re-audited first and confirmed
genuinely blocked: its only remaining REMAINING items - the dungeon-guard
economy, `clan_trade_bonus`, and the club-variant achievement wiring - all
need other wholly-unported systems, per iterations 99-101's own notes, so
there was no self-contained slice left to resume this iteration).

Ported the rank-threshold table and the shared point-award/promotion
helper as a first self-contained slice, deliberately not the mission
system (`military_ppd`, the advisor NPC driver, `check_military_solve`)
which is the bulk of `military.c`'s 2,881 lines and needs its own future
task slice(s). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`:
`ARMY_RANK_NAMES` (C `tool.c:1868-1907`'s `rankname[]`, all 41 entries),
`army_rank_for_points` (`get_army_rank_int`'s `cbrt(military_pts)` formula
clamped to `MAX_ARMY_RANK`=40 - derived on the fly from
`Character.military_points` rather than a second persisted field, since
C's only two `set_army_rank` call sites always write exactly this
formula's output), `army_rank_name`, and `World::give_military_pts` (ports
`give_military_pts_no_npc`, `tool.c:3279-3306`: exp via `give_exp`, raw
exp recorded onto `military_normal_exp`, hardcore military-bonus applied
to points, and the promotion feedback text + above-Sergeant-Major
server-wide "Grats:" channel-6 broadcast on rank-up).

Wired both existing `military_points`-mutating call sites onto it (neither
previously did any promotion or feedback at all): `/milexp`
(`commands_admin.rs`) and the Area 25 `warpbonus_driver` reward-kind-3
case (`main.rs`). While wiring `/milexp`, found and fixed a pre-existing
bug this task's new call site would otherwise have inherited:
`hardcore_military_exp_bonus` lived only on `ServerRuntime` (unreachable
from `ugaris-core`, unlike its siblings `exp_modifier`/`hardcore_exp_bonus`
which already live on `world.settings`) - moved it onto `world.settings`,
removed the now-dead `ServerRuntime` field, and updated
`/sethardcoremilexpbonus` plus its existing tests accordingly. (Noted but
deliberately left alone as out of scope: `hardcore_kill_exp_bonus` has the
exact same pre-existing bug - `world/death.rs` reads it from
`world.settings` but `/sethardcorekillexpbonus` still writes only to
`ServerRuntime` - a separate, unrelated task's fix.)

8 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/military.rs` (rank
formula thresholds including the >41 clamp, rank-name table lookups,
no-promotion-below-threshold, promotion feedback text, above-rank-9
broadcast, hardcore bonus applied only to points not recorded exp,
unknown-character no-op) plus 2 existing `ugaris-server` tests updated for
the `world.settings` move. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`
(1549 ugaris-core [+8, net of the untouched pre-existing military tests]
+ 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 539 server, all green, zero failures),
`cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke
confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration touches
the tick loop's Area 25 warp-bonus reward branch).

REMAINING for "Military ranks": the mission PPD/advisor NPC driver
(`military_ppd`, `mission_ppd.h`, the mission-accept/solve/reroll flow and
its admin commands like `cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_forcesolve`) and the governor
mission flow (`check_military_solve`) are unported; no player-facing
`#rank`-style status command was added (nothing existing reads military
state to extend).

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 105: Military Ranks (P3) - `give_first_kill` Army-Rank Branch

Resumed the `[~]` "Military ranks" task (the only genuinely resumable
`[~]` this iteration - "Clan system" and "Arena rankings" were re-checked
first and both confirmed still blocked purely on other wholly-unported
systems: dungeon-guard economy/club.c for Clan, and the tournament NPC
state machine plus a not-yet-designed generic storage-blob concept for
Arena, per their own iteration 96-104 notes).

Closed a concrete, self-contained gap that iteration 102's own note had
left open pending this exact task landing: C `give_first_kill`
(`src/system/death.c:196-254`)'s demon-lord-class branch has an
`if (get_army_rank_int(cn))` check that picks a different congrats
message and grants a `give_military_pts_no_npc` bonus for killers who
already hold an army rank - `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`'s
port of this function (`first_kill_congrats_message`/
`apply_first_kill_check`, added in iteration 82) always took the "no
rank" branch, with a code comment explicitly deferring it to army ranks
landing. Since iteration 102 already added
`army_rank_for_points`/`World::give_military_pts`, this rank check is now
real: `apply_first_kill_check` reads `army_rank_for_points(character.
military_points)` for the killer *before* this kill's own bonus is
applied (matching C's evaluation order - C checks the rank, then calls
`give_military_pts_no_npc` which may itself cause a promotion), and
`first_kill_congrats_message` gained a `has_army_rank: bool` parameter
that selects between "...! The Governor will be proud of you." (ranked)
and the previous plain "...!" (unranked) text, copied digit-for-digit
from C. When ranked and the victim class is in the demon-lord ranges
(`258..=305`/`404..=411`), `World::give_military_pts(killer_id,
min(victim_level / 3, 10), kill_score_level(victim_level, killer_level) *
15, area_id)` is now called, matching `give_military_pts_no_npc(cn,
min(ch[co].level / 3, 10), kill_score(co, cn) * 15)` exactly (including
reusing the already-ported hardcore-bonus/promotion-feedback/above-
Sergeant-Major broadcast behavior inside `give_military_pts` itself, for
free).

Also confirmed and corrected a stale claim in the task's own
`PORTING_TODO.md` REMAINING note: a fresh grep of the current C
`command.c` tree found no player-facing `#rank`-style command anywhere -
only the admin-only `/milinfo`/`/milpoints`/`/milstats` (all gated behind
`CF_GOD`, none of them player commands) - so the earlier note asking for
a player `#rank` command was based on a misreading, not a real gap; the
task note is corrected to reflect that here.

2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/achievement.rs`
(`apply_first_kill_check_uses_plain_demon_lord_congrats_message_without_army_rank`,
`apply_first_kill_check_grants_army_rank_bonus_message_and_military_points_when_ranked`
- the latter asserts the exact `min(level/3,10)` point delta on top of a
pre-set nonzero `military_points`, plus the Governor message text).
`cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1558 ugaris-core + 47 db + 3
net + 37 protocol + 541 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo
build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed
"entering Rust game loop" with no panics (this iteration touches the
`kill_char`/first-kill death-path feedback).

REMAINING for "Military ranks" (unchanged from iteration 102): the
mission PPD/advisor NPC driver (`military_ppd`, `mission_ppd.h`, the
mission-accept/solve/reroll flow and its admin commands like
`cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_forcesolve`) and the governor mission flow
(`check_military_solve`, referenced from the death path) remain unported
- most of `military.c`'s 2,881 lines - and need their own future task
slice(s), likely following the same "typed PPD data model first, NPC
driver wiring later" pattern this task and "Arena rankings" have both
already used.

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 106: Military Ranks (P3) - Pure Mission Generation Math Slice

Health check first: `cargo test --workspace` was green (541 server + 1558
ugaris-core + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol, all passing) before starting,
so no repair work was needed this iteration.

Resumed the topmost in-progress task among the three `[~]` P3 entries
(Clan system, Military ranks, Arena rankings - all in document order).
Re-read the Clan system task's own REMAINING note first: both of its
open items (the dungeon-guard economy, and the achievement wiring for
clubs) are still genuinely blocked on wholly-unported systems
(`club.c`, the dungeon/raid system), confirming no self-contained slice
is available there this iteration either - so moved to "Military ranks",
whose own REMAINING note names concrete C functions this slice could
attack.

Read `src/module/military.c` in full (2,881 lines). Confirmed the
mission-generation math (pricing, exp-reward scaling, and the three
per-type single-mission generators, `military.c:342-1027`) is entirely
pure - it operates on plain `int`/`float` values and a `struct
single_mission` return value, with no reads/writes of the `military_ppd`
per-character save data at all - making it a clean, self-contained,
fully-testable slice independent of the still-unported ppd/NPC-driver
work, following the exact "typed data model / pure math first, stateful
wiring later" pattern this task's own first slice (iteration 102's rank
table) and "Arena rankings" (iteration 104-ish) both already used.

Ported to `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`:
- `SingleMission` (`military.h:21-26`'s `struct single_mission`) plus
  `MISSION_TYPE_DEMON`/`_RATLING`/`_SILVER` constants and an `is_empty()`
  helper for C's `type == 0` "unavailable mission" convention.
- `specific_mission_price` (`military.c:392-467`): the Advisor NPC's
  paid-recommendation price formula - quadratic level-based base price,
  a 5-tier difficulty multiplier, a 3-tier mission-type multiplier, the
  `min(1.0, 100/level)` level-discount scaling clamped to `[0.5, 1.0]`,
  and a per-difficulty minimum-price floor (200/400/800/1500/3000).
- The five scaling helpers behind `calculate_mission_exp`
  (`military.c:580-785`): `get_level_experience_cap` (15% of the exp to
  the next level, clamped `[1000, 1_000_000]`, reusing the existing
  `world::exp::level2exp` instead of re-deriving the `level^4` formula),
  `get_minimum_expected_rank`/`get_maximum_reasonable_rank` (the
  level-banded reasonable-rank tables), `get_expected_level_for_rank`
  (the inverse piecewise rank-to-level table), and
  `get_enhanced_level_scaling_factor` (the perfect-progression bonus
  ladder: 1.5x/1.25x/1.1x/1.0x by level-diff, falling back to 1.0x when
  the rank is outside the level's reasonable band).
- `calculate_mission_exp` itself, preserving a real C quirk verified by
  re-reading the function twice: its internal `military_rank` is
  `cbrt(military_pts)` truncated to `int` *without* the
  `MAX_ARMY_RANK`-clamp that `army_rank_for_points` (this task's earlier
  slice) applies - a genuinely different value from the persisted army
  rank, not a bug to "fix", so it's computed inline rather than reusing
  `army_rank_for_points`.
- `generate_single_demon_mission`/`generate_single_ratling_mission`/
  `generate_single_silver_mission` (`military.c:795-1007`): the
  per-difficulty mission-instance generators for all 3 mission types,
  including the ratling/silver level-gating (`adjusted_level` shifts for
  difficulty >= 3, then a re-check that can make an otherwise-valid
  difficulty tier unavailable) and silver's rank-scaled quantity formula
  (same unclamped-cbrt `rank` quirk as above). C's `RANDOM(a)` macro
  (`rand() % a`) is ported as a `seed: &mut u32` parameter threaded
  through `mission_random`, reusing the existing
  `legacy_random_below_from_seed` LCG (already used elsewhere in
  `world/death.rs`/`world/merchant.rs`/etc.) instead of a bare `rand()`
  call, so every generator is deterministic and directly testable.

While reading `military.c`'s `set_data(cn, DRD_MILITARY_PPD, ...)` call
sites and cross-referencing `system/game/character.c`'s
`DRD_MILITARY_PPD` JSON-debug-dump case, confirmed a fact worth recording
so no future iteration re-derives it: `military_ppd`'s own
`military_pts`/`normal_exp` fields are *not* part of this task's
remaining gap. Their only two C writers, `give_military_pts`/
`give_military_pts_no_npc`, are already ported (iteration 102) writing
straight onto `Character.military_points`/`.military_normal_exp`, and
`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`'s `save_character_snapshot_tx`/
`load_character_snapshot` already serialize/deserialize the *entire*
`Character` struct as one `character_json` `jsonb` column
(`.bind(Json(character.clone()))` / `Json<Character>` on load) - so both
fields already fully round-trip through the DB today, with zero
additional work needed. The real remaining gap is only the *other*
`military_ppd` fields (mission slots, advisor state, take/solve
day-tracking) that have no Rust field at all yet.

Deliberately did **not** port this iteration (documented as REMAINING in
`PORTING_TODO.md`): the `military_ppd` struct itself (needs a storage
decision - typed fields vs. the raw-block-plus-offset-accessor pattern
`arena_ppd`/`first_kill_ppd` use); `generate_demon_mission`/
`generate_sewer_mission`/`generate_mine_mission`/
`generate_mission_with_preference` (the ppd-populating wrappers around
this slice's generators); `accept_mission`/`complete_mission`/
`handle_specific_mission_request` (ppd-mutating state transitions);
`check_military_solve` (`death.c:290-379` - already has a clean call site
identified, right next to the `give_first_kill` queue push in
`world/death.rs::kill_character_followup`, but has nothing to decrement
without the ppd's `took_mission`/`mis[5]` fields existing first); the
Military Master/Advisor NPC drivers, their `qa[]` dialogue table
(`analyse_text_driver`), and storage state machines
(`process_master_storage`/`process_advisor_storage`); the `SV_QUEST_EXT`
mod-packet (`common/mod_packet.c:351-397`) that shows the active mission
in the client's quest log; and the admin commands (`cmd_milinfo`/
`cmd_forcesolve`).

14 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/military.rs`:
every `specific_mission_price` difficulty/type multiplier and every
difficulty's price floor; `get_level_experience_cap`'s clamps at both
ends and a hand-computed mid-range boundary; every
`get_minimum_expected_rank`/`get_maximum_reasonable_rank` threshold;
every `get_expected_level_for_rank` piecewise segment; the
perfect-progression scaling tiers and the out-of-band fallback;
`calculate_mission_exp` against two independently hand-computed values
(one neutral-scaling, one 1.5x-bonus case) plus a level-cap-clamping
sanity check; every demon-mission difficulty's point value and opt2
level-cap/shift behavior; ratling-mission level-gating (below/above/even
level rejection) and the difficulty-shift re-validation; and
silver-mission's level-12 gate plus its rank-scaled `opt1` range.

`cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1572 ugaris-core [+14] + 47
db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 541 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo
build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings. This iteration touched
only `ugaris-core` pure math with zero new call sites into the runtime
loop/login/map-sync/protocol, so a boot-smoke wasn't strictly required
per the recipe, but one was run anyway as a sanity check: 10s of
`target/debug/ugaris-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:5556` produced normal
per-tick log lines with no panics.

REMAINING for "Military ranks": see the itemized list above and
`PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Military ranks" P3 entry - the `military_ppd` data
model, its ppd-populating/mutating wrappers, `check_military_solve`, the
NPC drivers, and the `SV_QUEST_EXT` packet all still need their own
future slice(s).

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 107: Military Ranks (P3) - `military_ppd` Data Model + `check_military_solve`

Health check first: `cargo test --workspace` was green (541 passed for
the server crate, all other crates likewise all-green) before starting,
so no repair work was needed.

Resumed "Military ranks" (the topmost `[~]` task in document order whose
own REMAINING note names a concrete, self-contained next slice: iteration
106 explicitly identified `check_military_solve` as having "a real call
site queued right next to `give_first_kill`... but has nothing to
decrement without the ppd's `took_mission`/`mis[5]` fields existing
first"). Checked the other two `[~]` P3 entries (Clan system, Arena
rankings) again first - both remain genuinely blocked on wholly-unported
systems (the dungeon/raid economy; the arena tournament NPC state machine
and a still-undecided ranking-table storage architecture) - confirming
Military ranks was the right pick again.

Read `src/module/military.h`'s full `struct military_ppd` (64 flat
`int` fields, 256 bytes: 6 header fields, `advisor_last[20]`,
`military_pts`/`normal_exp`/`mission_yday`, `mis[5]` (5 `struct
single_mission` each), then `took_mission`/`took_yday`/`solved_mission`/
`solved_yday`/`recommend`/mission type+difficulty preference/temp
mission selection/`reroll_yday`) and `src/system/death.c:281-383`
(`get_demon_mission_value`/`check_military_solve`) end to end before
writing any Rust, per the task recipe.

Ported to `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs` (following the exact
`arena_ppd` raw-block-with-offset-accessor pattern):
- `LEGACY_MILITARY_PPD_SIZE` (256 bytes) plus offset constants for the
  `mis[5]` table base, `took_mission`, and `solved_mission` - the other
  ~50 header/advisor/yday/preference fields are deliberately left
  without named accessors for now (not needed until the mission-offer/
  accept/reroll wrappers land; they still round-trip byte-for-byte
  through the opaque `Vec<u8>` either way).
- `PlayerRuntime::military_ppd: Vec<u8>`, `encode_legacy_military_ppd`/
  `decode_legacy_military_ppd`, wired into `decode_legacy_ppd_blob`/
  `encode_legacy_ppd_blob`'s per-id match arms exactly like every other
  typed PPD (including the "only append if nonzero" omit-on-save rule).
- `military_mission`/`set_military_mission` (reading/writing one of the
  5 `mis[]` slots as `world::SingleMission`, reusing the struct iteration
  106 already ported), `military_took_mission`/`set_military_took_mission`,
  `military_solved_mission`/`set_military_solved_mission`.
- `DRD_MILITARY_PPD` graduated out of `clear_turn_seyan_ppd`'s raw-block
  `strip_ppd_blocks` list to a real `self.military_ppd.clear()`, matching
  how `arena_ppd`/`first_kill_ppd` made the same transition earlier.
- `PlayerRuntime::check_military_solve(victim_class, victim_level)`: the
  full `death.c:290-383` state machine ported as a pure mutation plus a
  `MilitaryMissionProgress` outcome enum (`NoMatch`/`Progress{remaining,
  elite_count}`/`Solved`) instead of C's inline `log_char` side effects,
  so the message text/color decision could be tested independently of
  the state mutation. Correctly reproduces the `if (opt1 < 0) opt1 = 0`
  clamp and the "already solved -> no-op" early return.

Ported to `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`:
- `ELITE_DEMON_CLASS_BASE`/`LESSER_DEMON_CLASS_BASE` (700/600,
  `death.h`/`pents.h`), `is_pent_demon_mission_class` (the 5-way class-
  range union check.c uses: 3 normal-pent ranges plus the elite/lesser
  palette-swap ranges), `is_sewer_ratling_mission_class` (`85..=100`).
- `get_demon_mission_value` (`death.c:281-288`, identically duplicated at
  `area/4/pents.c:255-262`): elite demons are worth 10 mission-kill
  credits, everything else (including lesser demons) worth 1.
- `military_mission_progress_message_should_display` (`death.c:339-341`/
  `:369-370`'s "only echo every 5th/10th kill past 10 remaining" log-spam
  gate), extracted as its own pure predicate so the server-side text
  wiring could reuse the exact same condition without duplicating it.

Wired the real call site: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/death.rs` gained
`MilitaryMissionKillCheck { killer_id, victim_class, victim_level }`,
queued in `kill_character_followup` right alongside `FirstKillCheck`
(same `killer_is_player` guard C's own `CF_PLAYER`-on-the-killer check
maps to - but, matching C exactly, with no victim-class-range
restriction, unlike `FirstKillCheck`'s `1..=1023` gate) plus a
`drain_pending_military_mission_checks` for the server to consume.

New `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`: `apply_military_mission_kill_check`
drains the queue, calls `PlayerRuntime::check_military_solve`, and on a
`Progress`/`Solved` outcome sends the exact `log_char` text - `COL_DARK_GRAY
"Mission kill, %d to go."` (or `"Elite demon slain! Counts as %d. %d to
go."` when `elite_count > 1`), or the uncolored `"You solved your mission.
Talk to the governor to claim your reward."` on solve. This needed a
small new plumbing addition, discovered while trying to reuse the existing
`World::queue_system_text`: `COL_DARK_GRAY`'s raw `\xb0c1` color-marker
byte is not valid UTF-8 (`0xb0` alone is a bare continuation byte), so it
cannot be embedded in a Rust `String` without lossy corruption - unlike
`give_first_kill`'s congrats text (this codebase's only prior `log_char`-
via-`queue_system_text` precedent), which happens to carry no color
prefix in C. Added a byte-payload sibling of the existing string-only
queue: `WorldSystemTextBytes`/`World::queue_system_text_bytes`/
`drain_pending_system_text_bytes` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs`)
plus `send_pending_world_system_text_bytes`
(`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`, using the existing
`ugaris_protocol::packet::system_text_bytes` the plain-string path
couldn't reach), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right next to the
existing `send_pending_world_system_texts` call. This is now the
established pattern for any future colored single-character `log_char`
port that needs exact color-byte fidelity.

Deliberately did **not** port this iteration (documented as REMAINING in
`PORTING_TODO.md`): `military_ppd`'s advisor-state (`current_advisor`/
`advisor_state`/`advisor_cost`/`advisor_storage_nr`/`advisor_last[20]`)/
`mission_yday`/`took_yday`/`solved_yday`/`recommend`/mission type-
difficulty preference/temp-mission-selection/`reroll_yday` fields (no
accessors yet - not needed until the wrappers below land);
`generate_demon_mission`/`generate_sewer_mission`/`generate_mine_mission`/
`generate_mission_with_preference` (the ppd-populating wrappers around
iteration 106's pure per-instance generators); `accept_mission`/
`complete_mission`/`handle_specific_mission_request` (the remaining
ppd-mutating state transitions); the Military Master/Advisor NPC drivers,
their `qa[]` dialogue table, and storage state machines; the
`SV_QUEST_EXT` mod-packet (so `check_military_solve`'s own two
`sendquestlog` calls are also not reproduced yet - a cosmetic-only gap,
since the mission-progress state itself is already fully correct); and
the admin commands (`cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_forcesolve`).

20 new tests: 8 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/military.rs`
(every disjoint class-range boundary for both guard helpers, elite-vs-
everything-else mission value, and every message-display threshold
including the sub-10/percent-5/percent-10/`>=100` cases); 12 in
`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs` (mission-slot/progress accessor round
trip including untouched-slot zeroing, full PPD blob encode/decode round
trip, `clear_turn_seyan_ppd` clearing, and every `check_military_solve`
branch: no active mission, already-solved no-op, wrong class, wrong
level, every accepted adjacent-level variant, elite-demon 10x count with
the exact resulting `opt1`, a full ratling progress-then-solve two-kill
sequence including the post-solve no-op, and the opt1-can't-go-negative
clamp on an overshooting elite kill).

`cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1587 ugaris-core [+20 new
this iteration] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 541 server, all green,
zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings.
This iteration adds a new queued-check drain to the tick loop (the
`MilitaryMissionKillCheck` loop, mirroring the existing first-kill-check
loop) and a new per-tick send function, so a 10s boot-smoke was run per
the recipe: `target/debug/ugaris-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:5556`
logged "entering Rust game loop" with normal per-tick output and no
panics.

REMAINING for "Military ranks": see the itemized list above and
`PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Military ranks" P3 entry - `military_ppd`'s
remaining opaque fields, the mission-offer/accept/complete wrappers, the
NPC drivers, and the `SV_QUEST_EXT` packet all still need their own
future slice(s).

- 2026-07-04 (iteration 110): ported the 7 `/mil*` GM admin commands
  (`cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_milpref`/`cmd_milreset`/`cmd_milpoints`/`cmd_milrec`/
  `cmd_milstats`/`cmd_milsolve`, `src/system/command.c:5071-5613`,
  dispatch at `command.c:10085-10138`, all `CF_GOD`-gated, full-word-only
  - no abbreviation accepted for any of the 7), closing the "admin
  commands (`cmd_milinfo`/`cmd_forcesolve`)" REMAINING item (the real C
  name is `cmd_milsolve`, not `cmd_forcesolve` - a stale note, corrected).
  New in `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`: `military_advisor_last`/
  `set_military_advisor_last` (`military_ppd::advisor_last[MAXADVISOR]`,
  `military.h:37`) and `military_reroll_yday`/`set_military_reroll_yday`
  (`military_ppd::reroll_yday`, `military.h:59`) - the last two opaque
  `military_ppd` fields this task's REMAINING note had flagged, now typed
  accessors following the same offset-computed raw-block pattern as every
  other `military_ppd` field. All 7 commands added to
  `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs` right after the existing
  `/milexp` block, following its exact `CF_GOD`-gate/`KeyringCommandResult`
  pattern: `/milinfo [name]`/`/milreset [name]`/`/milsolve [name]
  [announce]` self-fallback when no name given (matching C); `/milpref
  <name> <type> <difficulty>`/`/milpoints <name> <points>`/`/milrec <name>
  <points>` require an explicit name (C prints a `Usage:` line instead).
  Confirmed by reading each C function directly that `cmd_milpoints`/
  `cmd_milsolve` deliberately do **not** call `give_military_pts_no_npc`
  (unlike `/milexp`): no hardcore bonus, no `give_exp`/`normal_exp` touch
  from `cmd_milpoints`, a hardcoded `newrank < 25` promotion cap (not
  `MAX_ARMY_RANK`=40), and their own distinct message text - reused
  `army_rank_for_points`/`army_rank_name` for the rank math/display (this
  codebase's established on-the-fly-derivation simplification, documented
  since the first military-ranks slice) but inlined the C-specific
  promotion gate/text/broadcast at each of the two call sites rather than
  reusing `World::give_military_pts`. `/milstats` (`command.c:5456-5489`)
  always returns C's own "Could not find Military Master NPC." message:
  no `CDR_MILITARY_MASTER` driver/NPC exists in Rust at all yet (confirmed
  by grep), so C's own early-exit branch for an unspawned Military Master
  is the only reachable behavior today - not a shortcut, the exact correct
  port of the current unported-NPC state. Reproduced one real, verified C
  quirk in `/milpref` rather than "fixing" it: `diff`'s C-side default
  value of `-1` is *itself* inside the `diff>=-1 && diff<5` acceptance
  range, so omitting the difficulty argument silently resets the stored
  preference to "None" (verified by reading `cmd_milpref` directly, not
  guessed). 16 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/
  commands_admin.rs` (one god-gated behavior test plus one full-scenario
  test per command, covering self-fallback, named-target-not-found, the
  `/milpref` missing-diff quirk, `/milpoints`/`/milsolve`'s promotion-vs-
  no-promotion branches and the `newrank<25`/`newrank>9` broadcast gates,
  and `/milreset`'s all-20-advisor-slot clear) plus 1 new `player.rs` unit
  test for the two new accessors. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test
  --workspace` (1613 ugaris-core [+1] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553
  server [+16], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`
clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game
loop" with no panics (this iteration adds new command-dispatch branches
to the existing per-tick command-processing chain). REMAINING for
"Military ranks" unchanged otherwise: `military_ppd`'s
`recommend`/`temp_mission_type`/`temp_mission_difficulty` fields are
still opaque (not needed by any ported behavior yet); the mission-offer/
accept/complete wrappers still have no live NPC call site; the Military
Master/Advisor NPC drivers, their `qa[]` table, and storage state
machines remain entirely unported; the `SV_QUEST_EXT` mod-packet remains
unported (cosmetic-only gap).

- 2026-07-04 (iteration 111): closed the "`military_ppd`'s `recommend`/
  `temp_mission_type`/`temp_mission_difficulty` fields are still opaque"
  REMAINING item and the 5 remaining header fields alongside it - every
  one of `military_ppd`'s 64 `int` fields now has a typed accessor in
  `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`: `master_state`/`current_advisor`/
  `advisor_state`/`advisor_cost`/`advisor_storage_nr` (the 5 header ints
  that weren't already covered), `military_pts`/`military_normal_exp_ppd`
  (right after `advisor_last[20]`), `military_recommend`, and
  `temp_mission_type`/`temp_mission_difficulty`. Also ported the next
  self-contained slice of pure driver logic on top of them, still with no
  live NPC call site (`military_master_driver`/`military_advisor_driver`
  themselves need their own future slice, and their storage-blob
  persistence shares the same "no generic storage-blob concept in
  `ugaris-db` yet" architectural gap the Arena rankings task's own
  REMAINING note flags): `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/military.rs`
  gained `calculate_advisor_index(storage_id)` (`military.c:2239-2249`,
  the two-disjoint-linear-band mapping onto `advisor_last[]`'s 20 slots,
  out-of-range falls back to slot 0), `advisor_price(level)`
  (`military.c:2288-2299`, the 5 flat level-banded base prices), and
  `offer_favor_cost(level, favor_size)` (`military.c:2318-2372`'s cost
  half, the 5 favor-size multipliers, `None` for C's own invalid-size
  `return 0`). `player.rs` gained `PlayerRuntime::greet_player(has_army_
  rank, yday)` (C `greet_player`, `military.c:1764-1798`, the Military
  Master driver's `NT_CHAR` dialogue-state machine as a
  `GreetPlayerOutcome`-returning pure mutation), reproducing two exact C
  quirks: the stale `master_state == 10` confirmation state resets to 0
  *then* falls through to the rest of the function fresh (C's `!= 0`
  guard runs after the reset, not as an `else`), and an advisor's
  already-shown specific-mission recommendation takes priority over every
  other greeting branch (matches C's `if`/`else if` chain order). `world/
  military.rs` gained `World::mission_reroll(character_id, player, yday,
  rng_seed)` (C `handle_mission_reroll`, `military.c:1889-1936`, the paid
  two-step reroll-confirmation flow: already-rerolled-today/has-active-
  mission/insufficient-200-gold gates, a first call that only stamps
  `master_state = 10` and asks for confirmation, and a confirmed second
  call that deducts the gold, stamps `reroll_yday`/resets `mission_yday`,
  and calls the existing `PlayerRuntime::apply_mission_offer` to
  regenerate the offer table) - also reproduces `generate_mission_with_
  preference`'s own rank-cubed `military_pts` floor-up ("Adjust military
  exp for rank if the player gained a rank elsewhere") at this call site,
  since that clamp lives in the C caller, not the already-ported pure
  generator functions. 17 new tests (16 in `world/tests/military.rs`
  covering the advisor index/price/favor-cost math, every `greet_player`
  branch including both quirks above, and every `mission_reroll` gate
  plus the two-step confirm flow and the rank-cubed floor-up; 1 in
  `player.rs` for the 8 new accessors round-tripping without disturbing
  neighboring fields). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1630
  ugaris-core [+17] + 47 db + 3 net + 37 protocol + 553 server, all
  green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero
  warnings. No runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol changes this
  iteration (pure `ugaris-core` additions with no call site yet), so
  boot-smoke was not required per the recipe and was not re-run.
  REMAINING for "Military ranks": the Military Master/Advisor NPC
  drivers themselves (their `NT_CHAR`/`NT_TEXT`/`NT_GIVE` message loops
  and dialogue-rendering halves), their `qa[]` dialogue table (not yet
  added to `character_driver.rs`'s `TextQaEntry` tables), the storage
  state machines + `dat->storage_data` per-difficulty counters (needs the
  storage-blob architectural decision shared with Arena rankings),
   `handle_specific_mission_request` and `process_clan_recommendation`/
   `process_advisor_recommendation`, the wealth-achievement ladder wiring,
   and `SV_QUEST_EXT`.

- Ralph Loop iteration 122 - `src/system/death.c` `check_military_solve`
  continued (P3 "Military ranks" task, resuming its `[~]` entry): closed
  the `sendquestlog` gap the iteration-121 REMAINING note flagged. C's
  `check_military_solve` (`death.c:290-383`) calls `sendquestlog(cn,
  ch[cn].player)` in both its demon (`death.c:333`) and sewer-ratling
  (`death.c:362`) branches the instant a kill matches the active
  mission's type/class/level target - i.e. on both the `Progress` and
  `Solved` outcomes, never on `NoMatch` - so the client's quest log
  immediately reflects the new remaining count or the just-flipped
  `solved_mission` flag. `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`'s
  `apply_military_mission_kill_check` (the existing wiring for this
  check) now also builds a legacy `SV_QUESTLOG` payload via the
  already-existing `legacy_questlog_payload` helper (`login.rs`, the same
  one `CL_GETQUESTLOG`/`ReopenQuest` already reuse) whenever the outcome
  isn't `NoMatch`, and sends it directly to every session for the killer
  character via `sessions_for_character`/`send_to_session` - before the
  progress-text message is queued, matching C's own call order
  (`sendquestlog` then `log_char`). Only the legacy `SV_QUESTLOG` half of
  `sendquestlog` is reproduced, matching every other `sendquestlog` call
  site already ported in this crate; the Ugaris-specific `SV_QUEST_EXT`
  mod-packet and the unrelated `mod_send_info_sync` call `sendquestlog`
  also makes remain unported - checked the whole C tree: this
  `sendquestlog` function is the only call site of either, so this is now
  the single remaining gap for both, not two separate ones. 2 new focused
  tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/military.rs`: one drives
  `apply_military_mission_kill_check` with a `Progress`-matching kill and
  asserts exactly one `SV_QUESTLOG` packet lands in `tick_out` alongside
  the separately-queued progress text; one drives a non-matching kill and
  asserts neither a packet nor queued text appear. `cargo fmt --all`,
  `cargo test --workspace` (1707 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 37
  protocol + 557 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p
  ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed
  "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. REMAINING for "Military
  ranks" overall: only the cosmetic `SV_QUEST_EXT`/`mod_send_info_sync`
  mod-packet halves of `sendquestlog`, and `complete_mission`/`promote`'s
  reward/promotion text still going through `queue_system_text` rather
  than `npc_quiet_say`.

- Ralph Loop iteration 130 - `src/system/arena.c` `master_driver` (P3
  "Arena rankings" task, resuming its `[~]` entry): ported the arena
  tournament master NPC end-to-end, closing the task's biggest REMAINING
  item ("the entire tournament NPC state machine ... no Rust equivalent
  exists"). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/arena.rs`: `add_contender`
  (`register`), `find_contender` (`MS_PAIR` pairing search - minimum
  `abs(score diff)*100 - wait time` across all registered pairs),
  `check_inside` (`MS_IN` - both-fighters-in-the-box gate, always
  advancing to `MS_FIGHT` once the 30s timeout expires regardless of
  who's inside), `check_fight` (`MS_FIGHT` - box-position/timeout win/
  loss/draw detection), `empty_arena` (post-fight kick-out - faithfully
  reproduces a real C quirk where `teleport_char_driver`'s target is the
  master NPC's own occupied tile, so this legitimately no-ops today with
  a single master NPC), `enter`/`leave` text commands, and the `NT_GIVE`
  give-back-item handler (unconditional destroy, same simplification as
  `world/bank.rs`/`world/clanmaster.rs`), plus the server-wide
  `update_toplist` ranking table (`World::arena_update_toplist`/
  `arena_toplist_entries`, in-memory only - no DB/file persistence yet,
  same architectural gap as `MilitaryMasterStorageRegistry`). New
  `character_driver.rs` types: `CDR_ARENAMASTER`=48 (plus the still-
  unported `CDR_ARENAFIGHTER`=49/`CDR_ARENAMANAGER`=50 constants for
  future slices), `ArenaMasterDriverData`/`ArenaContender` (merging C's
  `ID`/`cn` contender fields into one `CharacterId`, same simplification
  as `TraderDriverData::c1_id`), `ARENA_QA` (the driver's shared `qa[]`
  table). Scoring a completed fight needs `PlayerRuntime` (owned by
  `ugaris-server`), so `check_fight` queues `ArenaMasterEvent::
  FightScored` instead of mutating PPD directly - refactored
  `PlayerRuntime::record_arena_fight_result` into two new pub halves,
  `apply_arena_win`/`apply_arena_loss` (each only needs the *opponent's*
  pre-fight score, avoiding two simultaneous `&mut PlayerRuntime`
  borrows from the same `ServerRuntime::players` map), applied by a new
  `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs::apply_arena_master_events`
  that also folds the resulting scores into `arena_update_toplist` -
  wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the clan economy tick,
  with a new `arena_score_of` closure parameter on
  `process_arena_master_actions` (mirroring the RNG-closure-injection
  pattern already used for weather) so `add_contender` can read a
  registrant's live arena score without `World` needing `PlayerRuntime`
  access itself. Also wired `main.rs`'s `ArenaToplist` item-driver
  outcome (previously emitted nothing) to real `arena_toplist_lines`
  output. Not ported this slice: `fighter_driver`/`manager_driver` (two
  separate NPC state machines - the autonomous practice-bot and the paid
  arena-rental system, `arena.c:790-1039`) and DB/file persistence for
  the ranking table. 38 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/
  tests/arena.rs` plus 1 in `player.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test
  --workspace` (1748 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 580
  server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/
  workspace clean with zero warnings, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering
  Rust game loop" with no panics.

- Ralph Loop iteration 131 - `src/module/weather/weather.c`
  `apply_elemental_debuffs` (P3 "Weather driver" task, resuming its `[~]`
  entry, this closed the task entirely): ported the function
  (`weather.c:614-655`) as a periodic (once per 10 real seconds per
  character) flavor-text notification only. Analysis confirmed the
  function's own persistent `elemental_debuff[cn]`/
  `elemental_debuff_expire[cn]` state, and its internal "debuff expired,
  clear it" branch, are both genuinely dead code, not just unwired: the
  call site (`apply_weather_effects`) only invokes the function when
  `world_weather.weather_effects & WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` is set, which
  is derived from the exact same table lookup the function itself
  re-reads, so the function is *never* called with a `DEBUFF_NONE` current
  cell - its own `debuff_type == DEBUFF_NONE` branch can't execute; and the
  persistent state is only ever read by `get_elemental_debuff`, whose only
  4 callers (`modify_attack_speed`/`modify_spell_power`/
  `modify_fire_resistance`/`modify_cold_resistance`) were already confirmed
  dead code in iteration 127. Added `WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` (`0x400`)
  and `DEBUFF_NONE`/`DEBUFF_WET`/`DEBUFF_COLD`/`DEBUFF_SCORCHED` to
  `crates/ugaris-server/src/weather.rs`, restored the `elemental_debuff_
  type` column to `WeatherEffectData`/`WEATHER_EFFECTS` (digit-for-digit
  from `weather.c:148-192`: wet for rain/storm at every intensity, cold for
  snow, scorched for sandstorm, none for clear/fog), wired it into
  `calculate_weather_effects`, and added `elemental_debuff_type`/
  `elemental_debuff_message`/`should_notify_elemental_debuff` pure helpers.
  `WeatherState` gained `elemental_debuff_last_notify: HashMap<CharacterId,
  u64>` (C's `static int last_notify[TOTAL_MAXCHARS]`), cleared on
  disconnect in `ServerRuntime::disconnect` alongside `account_depots`.
  Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop in the same `area_has_weather` block as
  the damage/lightning rolls, gated on `WEATHER_EFFECT_ELEMENTAL` and the
  same `character_weather_eligible` guard used by both. 6 new unit tests
  (`elemental_debuff_type` table boundaries, `elemental_debuff_message`
  switch, `should_notify_elemental_debuff` cooldown gate at zero and
  nonzero baselines) plus updated `calculate_weather_effects`'s existing
  boundary test for the new bit and one `commands_admin.rs` fixture for the
  new struct field. With this, every item in the weather-driver task is
  now either ported or confirmed permanently dead/N/A in C itself -
  `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Weather driver" P3 entry is now marked `[x]`.
  `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1748 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3
  net + 40 protocol + 583 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build
  -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed
  "entering Rust game loop" with no panics.

- Ralph Loop iteration 132 - `src/system/arena.c` `fighter_driver` (P3
  "Arena rankings" task, resuming its `[~]` entry): ported the autonomous
  arena tournament practice-bot NPC (`CDR_ARENAFIGHTER = 49`) end-to-end,
  closing the second of the task's two remaining NPC state machines
  (`master_driver` was closed in iteration 130; `manager_driver` is the
  only one left). New `character_driver.rs` types: `ArenaFighterDriverData`
  (C `struct fighter_data`, minus the generic storage-blob state machine -
  same simplification as `ArenaMasterDriverData` - plus its `struct
  fighter_storage.ppd` payload tracked as plain `score`/`fights`/`wins`/
  `losses` fields directly on the driver state, since this NPC has no
  `PlayerRuntime` to own a real `arena_ppd`), the `FS_LEISURE`/`FS_START`/
  `FS_REGISTER`/`FS_WAIT`/`FS_ENTER`/`FS_WAIT2`/`FS_FIGHT` state constants,
  and `ARENA_FIGHTER_MASTER_POS`/`ARENA_FIGHTER_REST_POS` (`MASTER_POSX`/
  `Y`, and the `NT_CREATE` handler's hardcoded `restx`/`resty=247,148`).
  Zone-spawn wiring in `zone.rs` hardcodes the rest position and seeds
  `last_act` deeply in the past (`-TICKS*60*6`), matching C's own
  immediate-advance trick exactly. The full per-tick body lives in
  `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/arena.rs`: `process_arena_fighter_messages`
  (the `NT_GIVE`/`NT_NPC`+`NTID_ARENA` dat2 0-5 state transitions
  `master_driver` drives it through), `arena_fighter_update_enemy_visibility`/
  `arena_fighter_state_action` (the `FS_*` switch, narrowed to a single
  tracked enemy instead of porting the generic 10-slot `fight_driver_data`
  - same precedent as `world/gate_fight.rs`, since `fighter_driver` itself
  only ever calls `fight_driver_add_enemy` once per fight), reusing
  `World::attack_driver_direct`/`secure_move_driver`/
  `spell_self_simple_baddy`/`idle_simple_baddy` for combat/movement/idle
  exactly like `gate_fight.rs` does. Since this port calls the master's
  `arena_add_contender`/`arena_handle_enter` directly (no generic "NPC
  speech also reaches other NPCs' `NT_TEXT` queues" plumbing exists - only
  player speech does, in `ugaris-server::commands_chat`), added
  `World::arena_fighter_find_master` (nearest visible `CDR_ARENAMASTER`)
  instead of faking a say, while still emitting the real "register"/
  "enter" `npc_say` text for player-visible flavor parity with C's own
  `say(cn, ...)`. Extended `apply_arena_master_events`
  (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`) to fall back to two new
  `World::arena_fighter_score`/`apply_arena_fighter_win`/
  `apply_arena_fighter_loss` methods (the bot's own local ledger, reusing
  `PlayerRuntime::arena_fight_worth`'s pure ladder function) whenever a
  `FightScored` participant has no `PlayerRuntime` at all, instead of
  silently dropping the event - so a fight against (or between) fighter
  bots now really does get scored and folded into the toplist, not just
  player-vs-player fights. Wired `World::process_arena_fighter_actions`
  into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `process_arena_master_actions`.
  25 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/arena.rs` (every
  `FS_*` state transition and message-driven advance, register/enter
  wiring with a real nearby master, attack-visible/fight-over,
  give-message, local ledger win/loss math) plus 1 in
  `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` proving
  `apply_arena_master_events` scores a fighter-bot loser via its own
  local ledger while the real player winner still goes through
  `PlayerRuntime` as before. Not ported this slice: `manager_driver`, and
  DB/file persistence for both the ranking table and the fighter bot's
  own local win/loss ledger - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Arena rankings"
  entry for the itemized remaining list. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test
  --workspace` (1763 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584
  server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`
  clean with zero warnings, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game
  loop" with no panics.

- Ralph Loop iteration 133 - `src/system/arena.c` `manager_driver` (P3
  "Arena rankings" task, resuming its `[~]` entry - now closed `[x]`):
  ported the arena-rental NPC (`CDR_ARENAMANAGER = 50`), the last unported
  driver for this task. New `character_driver.rs` types:
  `ArenaManagerDriverData` (C `struct manager_data`, minus its own dead
  `timeout` field - C writes it twice but never reads it anywhere in
  `manager_driver`, verified by grep; every other `dat->timeout` in
  `arena.c` belongs to the unrelated `struct master_data`) and
  `parse_arena_manager_driver_args` (the `arenax`/`arenay`/`arenafx`/
  `arenafy`/`arenatx`/`arenaty` zone-file args, verified against the real
  `arg=` string in `ugaris_data/zones/3/above3_generic.chr`); zone-spawn
  wiring in `zone.rs`. The full per-tick body lives in
  `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/arena.rs`: `arena_manager_evict_renter_
  if_left` (the per-message "has the renter wandered off" check, using
  C's own hardcoded `232..=238` x-bounds rather than the dynamic
  listening box - a real, deliberately-preserved C quirk), `arena_manager_
  handle_rent`/`_leave`/`_enter` (the `rent`/`leave`/`enter`
  `analyse_text_qa` switch, reusing the same shared `ARENA_QA` table
  `master_driver` already established, including its long-dead `rent`=6
  entry), `arena_manager_handle_invite_command` (the independent
  `strcasestr(..., "invite:")` check, which runs alongside - not instead
  of - the switch above, exactly like C), `arena_manager_is_anybody_in`
  (the occupancy scan gating `rent`), and message-loop/return-to-post/
  give-message plumbing mirroring `master_driver`'s own shape. Wired
  `World::process_arena_manager_actions` into `main.rs`'s tick loop right
  after `process_arena_fighter_actions` - entirely self-contained within
  `World`; unlike `master_driver`/`fighter_driver`, this driver never
  reads or writes any `PlayerRuntime` state (C's own `manager_driver`
  never touches gold despite this task's original "paid arena-rental"
  description). Porting `leave` (which C teleports the caller onto the
  *manager NPC's own occupied tile*, `ch[cn].x, ch[cn].y`) surfaced a real
  latent bug shared by every arena driver: `arena_teleport_char_driver`
  was calling `World::teleport_character_exact` (exact-tile-only
  placement, which always fails onto an occupied tile) instead of
  `World::teleport_character` (`extended=false`, C's real `drop_char`:
  exact tile then its 8 neighbors) - re-read `drop_char`'s C source to
  confirm its 4th `nosteptrap` parameter is a boolean flag, not a "search
  radius" as a prior iteration's doc comment had misread it. Fixed
  `arena_teleport_char_driver` accordingly and corrected the one existing
  master test that had encoded the wrong assumption
  (`check_fight_scores_the_survivor_when_the_loser_leaves_the_box`, which
  asserted the winner stayed exactly on the master's own tile instead of
  landing on a neighbor tile). 26 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/
  world/tests/arena.rs` (rent reserves/rejects-when-occupied, leave from
  renter/non-renter, enter accepts/rejects, invite sets/rejects-non-
  renter, listening-box filtering, the narrow-x-band renter eviction,
  give-message) plus 4 in `character_driver.rs` (arg parsing against the
  real zone-file string, unknown-arg tolerance, `CDR_ARENA*` constants).
  `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Arena rankings" P3 entry is now marked `[x]`: every
  driver it named is ported end-to-end; the remaining DB/file
  storage-blob persistence gap (ranking table + fighter bot ledger) is a
  shared architectural limitation, not a self-contained slice of this
  task. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1777 ugaris-core + 55
  db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 584 server, all green, zero failures),
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke
  confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics.

  the two prio-5 `add_clanlog` entries, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop

  two early-return guards run *before* `dungeondoor` clears the door's
  `drdata`/sets the solved flag, so a failed attempt leaves the door

- Ralph Loop iteration 151 - closed the P3 "Clan system" task's last open

  (`warrior`/`mage`/`seyan`/`teleport`/`fake`/`key`): audited every

  through) against the whole C tree. Confirmed `'T'`/`'F'`/`'K'`/`'W'`/

  slot - are permanently dead code: a full-tree grep shows nothing ever
  increments those `[0]` slots (the only would-be writer, the clanmaster

  `return`-before-doing-anything code, `clanmaster.c:803-806`), so their
  `if (...[0] > 0)` guard can never pass - same precedent as
  `fighter_dead`, not ported, documented in `PORTING_TODO.md`. Ported

  jewel spawner item's (`IDR_CLANSPAWN`, `clanspawn_driver`,
  `area/30/clanmaster.c:1373-1397`) "won a Jewel" announcement and

  `ItemDriverOutcome::ClanSpawnAward` handler (`grant_clan_jewel`,

  itself). Added `World::resolve_clan_spawn_jewel_award`
  (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/clanmaster.rs`): resolves the winner's
  clan via the same self-healing `ClanRegistry::get_char_clan` every
  other clan-membership check uses, broadcasts the clanned/unclanned

  `ClanmasterEvent::JewelWonFromSpawner` (needs a DB handle `World`

  `ugaris-server`'s `apply_clanmaster_events`
  (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`) to write the
  `add_clanlog` entry. Wired at the one production call site:
  `main.rs`'s `ItemDriverOutcome::ClanSpawnAward` handler now calls this
  unconditionally *before* `grant_clan_jewel`, matching C's own ordering
  and the fact that C never gates the announcement on
  `award_clan_jewel`'s return value (it still fires even on a failed
  item delivery, e.g. a full inventory). C's own
  `clan[cnr].treasure.jewels += 1` at this same call site is dead,
  commented-out code even in the legacy source ("I like this better. -
  Dar"), so the treasury genuinely isn't touched by this path in C
  either - preserved as-is (a spawner-won jewel only feeds the treasury
  once handed to the clanclerk NPC, already wired via
  `ClanRegistry::add_jewel`). 2 new tests in `world::tests::clanmaster`
  (clanned winner: broadcast wording + queued clan-log event; unclanned
  winner: wording + no event queued). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test
  --workspace` (1949 ugaris-core [+2] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol +
  604 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`
  clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game
  loop" with no panics. With this closed and no other open REMAINING
  note left anywhere in the task's text (verified by grepping the whole
  task block for "not ported"/"unwired"/"still needs"/etc. - every hit
  is historical narration of a gap already closed in an earlier
  iteration), marked the P3 "Clan system" task `[x]` in
  `PORTING_TODO.md`.

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 152: Events (P3) - Recurring Boosted-Rate Events + Scheduler Primitives

With P0/P1/P2 fully closed, picked the topmost open P3 task, "Events"
(`src/module/events/**`). Ported the generic calendar-matching primitives
from `src/module/events/events.c` - `is_date_in_range`/`is_time_in_range`/
`is_day_matching`/`is_week_matching` - transcribed with their exact (and, for
`is_date_in_range`, slightly asymmetric) logic, plus a from-scratch
`days_from_civil`/`weekday_from_days`/`week_number` trio implementing
glibc `strftime("%W")` semantics (Monday-first week numbering, every day
before the year's first Monday is week 0), validated line-for-line against
real `date -u -d <date> +%W` output for 13 reference dates spanning
year/month/week boundaries. All new to `crates/ugaris-server/src/events.rs`.

Ported all five `src/module/events/recurring/*.c` boosted-rate events as a
`RecurringEventKind` enum + `RecurringEventsState` (per-event `is_active`/
snapshot state, mirroring each C file's `static Event <name>;` +
`static <Type>Data event_data;`): Double Experience Thursday (`exp_modifier
*= 1.5`), Double Drop Rate Tuesday (`event_drop_rate` loot modifier `=2.0`),
Double Experience & Drop Rate Weekend (both, biweekly - `week_number=1`,
`interval=2`), Mining Monday (`mining_silver_gold_multiplier`/
`mining_cavein_multiplier`/`mining_golem_event_multiplier` all `=2.0`), and
Mining Wednesday (`mining_artifact_multiplier=5.0`). `check_recurring_events`
is wired into `main.rs`'s existing once-a-minute tick gate (the same
`world.tick.0 % (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 60) == 0` pattern already used for
auction/clan/military maintenance), matching C's
`add_scheduled_task(check_events, 60, "event_check", true)`.

Added `GameSettings::loot_modifiers: HashMap<String, f64>` +
`get_loot_modifier`/`set_loot_modifier` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/
game_settings.rs`), a direct port of C `src/system/loot/loot.c`'s
`loot_get_modifier`/`loot_set_modifier` (default `1.0` for an unregistered
or empty name) - this is the hook the drop-rate events call, and the future
JSON loot-table roll engine ("Death-mode loot tables" task) is the intended
eventual consumer.

Preserved two genuine C quirks rather than "fixing" them (confirmed by
re-reading both files fully): `boosted_mining_event.c`/
`boosted_artifacts_mining_event.c` both capture an `original_*` snapshot in
their `_start` hook that their own `_end` hook never reads - `_end`
hardcodes the multiplier(s) back to `1.0` regardless of what they were
before the event started - so Mining Monday/Wednesday need no snapshot
storage in Rust at all. In contrast, `boosted_experience_event.c`/
`boosted_weekend_event.c` genuinely do restore their captured
`original_exp_modifier` snapshot on end (not a hardcoded `1.0`), so Double
Experience Thursday/Weekend snapshot and restore the pre-event value
exactly - covered by a dedicated regression test (`settings.exp_modifier =
1.2` before the event, asserting the post-event value is `1.2` again, not
`1.0`).

Scope explicitly not covered by this slice (left as `[~]` REMAINING notes
in `PORTING_TODO.md`): the generic seasonal `EventDecoration`/date-range
(`RECUR_NONE`/`RECUR_YEARLY`) scheduling `christmas_event.c`/
`easter_event.c` use - Christmas keeps its own already-ported, independent
date logic in `xmas.rs` (not migrated onto the new framework to avoid
regressing its existing tests), and Easter remains a complete gap (no
`calculate_easter_date` port, no Rust code at all); the `RecurrenceType::
Daily`/`Monthly` branches of `should_event_be_active` (no ported event
needs them yet); an actual loot-table consumer for `event_drop_rate`
(blocked on the separate, still-unported "Death-mode loot tables" task);
and any consumer for the four `mining_*_multiplier` settings themselves
(blocked on porting the mining "dig" mechanic, out of scope for this task).

17 new tests: 16 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/events.rs` (calendar
primitives, all five events' recurrence-matching gates, state-transition/
snapshot-restore behavior including the two hardcoded-reset quirks above,
and a `CalendarNow::from_unix_seconds` cross-check against a real
`date -u -d @<epoch>` reference) plus 1 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/
game_settings.rs` (`loot_modifier_defaults_to_one_and_round_trips_like_c`).
`cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1950 ugaris-core [+1] + 55 db
+ 3 net + 40 protocol + 620 server [+16], all green, zero failures),
`cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 12s boot-smoke
confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Marked the P3
"Events" task `[~]` in `PORTING_TODO.md` with the REMAINING note above.

## Ralph Loop - Iteration 153: Events (P3) - Easter Seasonal Event

Resumed the `[~]` "Events" task, picking the Easter gap iteration 152's
REMAINING note flagged as a complete gap: `src/module/events/seasonal/
easter_event.c`.

Ported `calculate_easter_date` (`events.c:164-183`, the Meeus/Jones/Butcher
algorithm for Gregorian Easter Sunday) transcribed with C's exact
variable-for-variable integer-division truncation, validated against 6
reference years (2000, 2018, 2019, 2024, 2025, 2026) with published Easter
dates. Ported `update_easter_dates`' (`easter_event.c:24-56`) ±1-week
active-window computation as `easter_date_range(year)`: C computes this via
`mktime`-normalized `struct tm` arithmetic (`easter.day - 7`/`+ 7` on a
`tm_mday` that can go negative or past month-end, then renormalized by
`mktime`) - rather than reimplementing `mktime`, this reuses the
already-ported `days_from_civil`/`xmas::civil_from_unix_seconds` pair
(day-count out, day-count back) to do the exact same round-trip
normalization, verified against two real month-rollover cases (2024's
March 31 Easter producing a March 24 - April 7 window, 2018's April 1
Easter producing a March 25 - April 8 window) plus one no-rollover case
(2025's April 20 Easter).

Ported `easter_event_start`/`easter_event_end` (`easter_event.c:71-83`):
halves `lucky_pentagram_chance` while active (a lower `RANDOM(chance)`
denominator means a *higher* hit rate despite the C comment's "Double the
lucky pentagram chance" describing probability, not the stored value
going up), matching C's `lucky_pentagram_chance *= 0.5` on an `int`
truncating the float product toward zero (Rust's `as i32` on the same
`f64` product does the same truncation). Restores the exact pre-event
snapshot on end - unlike Mining Monday/Wednesday's hardcoded-`1.0` C
quirk, this one really does read back `original_pentagram_lucky_chance`,
confirmed by re-reading the C file.

Ported `check_easter_event` as the `RECUR_YEARLY` branch of C's
`should_event_be_active`/`check_events` (`events.c:141-143,274-292`)
applied to this one event: just `is_date_in_range` against a date range
recomputed fresh from `easter_date_range(now.year)` every call, rather
than porting C's `yearly_easter_update` scheduled task (which only
recomputes the cached date range once a year, on January 1st, as a minor
optimization) - equivalent in effect since Easter's window (always
March/April) never crosses a year boundary, so there's no correctness gap
from recomputing every minute instead of caching. Made the pre-existing
`is_date_in_range` primitive a live (non-test-only) function since this is
its first production call site. Extended `CalendarNow` with `year`/
`month`/`day` fields (previously computed internally by `from_unix_
seconds` and discarded) to support this.

Wired `EasterEventState` (new, mirroring `RecurringEventsState`'s per-event
snapshot-state shape) as a new `ServerRuntime::easter_event` field, checked
in `main.rs`'s existing once-a-minute tick gate right alongside
`check_recurring_events`, logging the same "recurring event started/ended"
message with `event = "Easter"`.

Scope explicitly left for a future slice (unchanged from iteration 152's
REMAINING note, now narrower): the generic `EventDecoration` spawn/remove
plumbing (`spawn_event_decoration`/`remove_event_decoration`) is still
unported - not needed by Easter, which has zero decorations in C
(`static EventDecoration easter_decorations[] = {}`); `RecurrenceType::
Daily`/`Monthly` remain unported (no currently-ported event needs them);
`event_drop_rate`'s loot-table consumer and the `mining_*_multiplier`
settings' dig-mechanic consumer are both still blocked on their own
separate unported tasks.

6 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/events.rs`:
`calculate_easter_date_matches_known_reference_years` (6 reference years),
`easter_date_range_spans_one_week_before_and_after_with_month_rollover` (2
rollover cases + 1 non-rollover case), and
`check_easter_event_starts_and_ends_halving_and_restoring_lucky_pentagram_
chance` (start transition, no-op while still active, end transition
restoring the exact pre-event value). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test
--workspace` (1950 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 623 server
[+6 vs. iteration 152's 620], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p
ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed
"entering Rust game loop" with no panics. Kept the P3 "Events" task `[~]`
in `PORTING_TODO.md` (still missing `EventDecoration` plumbing,
`RecurrenceType::Daily`/`Monthly`, and the two blocked-elsewhere
consumers) with an updated REMAINING note and a new Progress Log entry.

Iteration 157 closed the "Death-mode loot tables" task's last open
REMAINING item: spawn-mode loot (`loot_table=` in `.chr` templates,
C `loot_apply_to_npc`, `create.c:1121-1125`). Added `World::loot_apply_to_npc`
in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/loot.rs`, refactoring the existing
death-mode-only `roll_loot_table_into_container`/`roll_loot_group_into_
container`/`place_loot_item_in_container` into mode-agnostic
`roll_loot_table`/`roll_loot_group`/`place_loot_item` parameterized by a
new private `LootSink` enum (`Container(ItemId)` | `Npc(CharacterId)`) so
both entry points share one roll engine, matching C's own `struct
DropSink` abstraction instead of duplicating the recursion/pity/modifier
logic per mode. The `Npc` sink places into the NPC's own
`character.inventory[30..INVENTORY_SIZE]` (first free slot, C
`place_in_npc`), no-op when full. Wired at the two call sites that cover
every currently-loadable NPC: `ZoneLoader::apply_map_directives`'s
`MapDirective::Character` branch (regular zone-population NPC spawns, C
`pop_create_char`) and `ugaris-server::spawns::respawn_npc_character` (C
`respawn_callback`'s recreate-from-template path - also funnels through
`create_char_nr` in C, so it must re-roll the table on every respawn
too). Left unwired (documented, not a regression): the ~8 other one-off
NPC-creation call sites in `spawns.rs` (edemon/fdemon gate, chestspawn,
warp-trial-fighter, swampspawn, teufel-ratnest, lq-npc, gatekeeper
`enter_test` opponents) - none of their templates carry `loot_table=`
today. Marked the task `[x]` in `PORTING_TODO.md` (the only remaining
gap, `loot_reload`'s admin hot-reload command, was already out of scope
for this task's title/description). 7 new tests: 4 in
`world/tests/loot.rs` (unknown-table/death-mode-mismatch -1 returns,
placement starting at slot 30 with slots 0-29 pre-occupied as a canary,
no-op when every carried slot is full), 1 in `zone.rs`'s own test module
(`zone_population_rolls_spawn_mode_loot_table_into_new_npcs_own_inventory`,
exercising the real `MapDirective::Character` path end-to-end through
`apply_map_str`), and 1 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/spawns.rs`
(`respawn_npc_character_rolls_its_templates_spawn_mode_loot_table`).
`cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (1973 ugaris-core [+5] + 55
db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 630 server [+1], all green, zero failures),
`cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`cargo build --workspace` clean with zero
warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no
panics and the pre-existing `loaded loot tables ... tables_added=9`
startup log line unchanged.

- Player/staff text commands now cover the admin-teleport family from
  `src/system/command.c`: `/goto` (`is_lqmaster`-gated: `CF_GOD`,
  `CF_EVENTMASTER`, or `CF_LQMASTER` in area 20), `/jump`
  (`CF_STAFF|CF_GOD`-gated), `/gotolist`, and `/gotosearch` (both
  `CF_GOD`-only), added to `apply_admin_character_command`
  (`commands_admin.rs`). The `gl[]` shortcut table (79 entries) is
  transcribed digit for digit; `resolve_goto_jump_args` reproduces C's
  exact pointer-stepping quirks, including that a name lookup compares
  the *entire* remaining argument string (so a trailing mirror argument
  after a name silently fails to match), and that supplying a mirror
  always forces the resolved area non-zero (even when it equals the
  caller's current area), routing through the same unported cross-area
  `change_area` no-op ("Nothing happens - target area server is down.")
  as every other cross-area teleport in this codebase, while still
  mutating the mirror (matching C's unconditional `ch[cn].mirror = m`
  before the handoff attempt). Non-`CF_GOD` `is_lqmaster` callers get the
  resolved area forced to 0 (C `if (!(ch[cn].flags & CF_GOD)) a = 0;`),
  so they always land locally on the resolved x/y even for a
  different-area shortcut name - copied as-is, a real quirk not a bug.
  `/jump` has no such `CF_GOD` restriction on its own cross-area branch
  (a genuine asymmetry vs `/goto`, also copied as-is). Added
  `World::teleport_char_driver` (`world/teleport.rs`, C
  `teleport_char_driver`, `drvlib.c:2651-2673`) as the canonical port and
  refactored `arena.rs`'s previously-private, near-identical
  `arena_teleport_char_driver` to delegate to it. Added
  `KeyringCommandResult::mirror_changed`, wired at the `main.rs` call
  site to send the same `mirror` packet + `PlayerRuntime::
  set_current_mirror` update the same-area transport-travel path already
  performs. 15 new focused tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs`. Remaining
  admin-teleport gaps: `/summon`, `/summonall`, and `/office` (still
  unported); real cross-area `change_area` handoff (tracked separately as
  the `Cross-area transfer` P3 task).

- Player/staff text commands now cover `/summon <name>` and `/summonall`
