Considered the sibling `/pentinfo`/`/setpentcount`/`/setpentstatus`/`/setpentbonus`/`/resetpent`/`/penthelp` debug family first (`command.c:1136-1362`) but a fresh read of `src/area/4/pents.c` confirmed their backing `DRD_PENT_NPPD` layout (re-declared identically three times as `pent_debug_data` in `command.c`, `macro_pent_data` in `module/base.c`, and the real `pentagram_player_data` in `area/4/pents.c`) is the actual live per-player pentagram-minigame run-state, not throwaway debug scaffolding - porting the debug commands first would still require inventing that whole run-state model ahead of the real "Area 4 - `pents.c`" gameplay task (a separate P4 task), exactly the same conclusion iteration 172's note already reached; left them unported rather than re-litigate that finding a third time. Added `PlayerRuntime::tunnel_clevel`/`set_tunnel_clevel` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, next to the pre-existing `tunnel_used`/`set_tunnel_used` pair, same `tunnel_ppd` raw-bytes-plus-accessors convention as `gorwin_tunnel_level`) for the struct's first field (`tunnel_ppd::clevel`, byte offset 0). `changetunnel`/`settunnel`/`cleartunnel` all require an explicit target name via the pre-existing `take_legacy_alpha_name` helper - no bare-number-means-self shortcut like `/setrd`'s `parse_exp_command_target`, verified by reading C's own `isalpha` name-parse loop, which has no self-fallback branch at all - reject an unmatched/empty name with "Sorry, no one by the name ... around.", validate the level against `MIN_TUNNEL_LEVEL..=MAX_TUNNEL_LEVEL` (newly imported into `main.rs`'s `ugaris_core::player::{...}` re-export list alongside the pre-existing `MAX_TUNNEL_USES`), then mutate the target's `tunnel_ppd` and notify both the caller and (when different) the target player. The target notification needed a new `KeyringCommandResult::other_messages: Vec<(CharacterId, String)>` field - a plain-text message-to-non-caller shape matching the pre-existing `auction::AuctionCommandResult::other_messages` convention rather than the raw-bytes `target_message_bytes` field, which turned out to have never been read at the `apply_admin_character_command` call site in `main.rs` before this iteration (now wired there too, alongside `messages`/`message_bytes`). `/solvetunnel` parses/validates its `exptype` argument and reports the reward-kind message but mutates no state at all, matching C exactly: its own `give_reward(cn, ppd, door_type)` call is commented out in the oracle itself (`command.c:2218`), so the real reward mechanic has never existed there to port. 8 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: named-target `clevel` set plus target notification plus caller-self-untouched, out-of-range level and unknown-name rejection, the `CF_GOD` gate, `settunnel`/`cleartunnel` completed-amount mutation plus notification, a self-targeted `changetunnel` sending no `other_messages`, `solvetunnel`'s three message variants (exp/military-exp/invalid), and its own `CF_GOD` gate. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 791 server [+8], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: unchanged from prior notes - still ~90 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries, mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, anticheat/macro-detection systems, the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`, and the pentagram debug/admin commands needing the live pentagram-run-state model the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task must build first); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 186 ported `/labsolved [name] [number]` (`cmd_labsolved`, `src/system/command.c:3081-3130`, dispatched at `command.c:6043-6046` right after `/milexp`, `cmdcmp(ptr, "labsolved", 8)` - `CF_GOD`-gated) into `apply_admin_character_command` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), placed right after the existing `/exp`/`/milexp` block to mirror C's own source order. Reused the pre-existing `parse_exp_command_target` helper unchanged for the target/value parsing (verified byte-for-byte identical to `cmd_exp`'s own `isdigit(*ptr) || !*ptr` self-vs-named-target branch, since `cmd_labsolved` copies that exact parsing block) rather than re-deriving it, and reused the already-fully-ported `PlayerRuntime::lab_solved_bits` field (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, backing the `DRD_LAB_PPD` gate-welcome/lab-progression system since an earlier iteration) instead of adding any new storage - this command turned out to be pure dispatch wiring over two already-existing pieces, no new backing model needed, unlike the sibling pentagram debug commands right next to it in C which do need one (see iteration 185's note, left unchanged). A nonzero value in `1..=63` XORs (not ORs) that lab number's solved bit - calling it twice on the same number un-solves it again, an easy-to-miss C quirk preserved as-is - an out-of-range nonzero value reports "Lab number is out of bounds." without mutating anything, and either way (including a zero/absent value) every currently-solved lab number 0..63 is listed lowest-to-highest as its own message, exactly matching C's unconditional trailing `for` loop that runs regardless of whether a toggle happened. 5 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: the `CF_GOD` gate plus the C `cmdcmp(..., 8)` 8-char-prefix (`/labsolve`) accepted vs 7-char (`/labsolv`) rejected boundary, self-target toggle-on/toggle-off/second-lab-listed/out-of-bounds sequence (asserting the XOR and the lowest-to-highest listing order), named-target success plus the shared "Sorry, no one by the name ... around." not-found message, and the "Could not get lab data for ..." fallback when the target has no live `PlayerRuntime` session (an online `Character` with no matching `runtime.players` entry, the same defensive shape `/milinfo`/`/milrec` already use for their own PPD-backed fields). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 795 server [+4], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: unchanged from prior notes - still ~90 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries (now minus `/labsolved`), mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, anticheat/macro-detection systems, the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`, and the pentagram debug/admin commands needing the live pentagram-run-state model the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task must build first); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 187 ported `/setseyan <name>` (`cmd_setseyan`, `src/system/command.c:3055-3078`, dispatched at `command.c:9989-9996`, `CF_GOD`-gated, `cmdcmp(ptr, "setseyan", 8)` - `minlen` equals the full word's length, so no abbreviation is accepted). Turned out to be pure dispatch wiring: the underlying `turn_seyan` reroll (`src/system/tool.c:4278-4389`) was already fully ported as `World::apply_turn_seyan` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/turn_seyan.rs`) plus `PlayerRuntime::clear_turn_seyan_ppd`, since the gate-fight class-8 reward path (`World::apply_gate_fight_reward`, `world/gate_fight.rs`) already drives the exact same reroll for a different trigger. Added `apply_setseyan_command` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), looking up the target by name via the pre-existing `find_online_character_by_name`/`take_legacy_alpha_name` helpers (no self-fallback, matching C's plain `isalpha` name-parse loop with no bare-number shortcut) and the `"seyan_m"` template's base values via `ZoneLoader::character_templates` (mirroring C's own `create_char("seyan_m", 0)` + copy + `destroy_char`, exactly like the gate-fight caller in `world_events.rs` already does). Kept this as its own function taking `&ZoneLoader` directly - called at the `main.rs` dispatch chain right alongside `apply_create_command`/`apply_create_orb_command` (both of which already take a loader reference there) - rather than widening `apply_admin_character_command`'s signature, which would have required touching all ~320 of its existing test call sites for a single command. Preserved a real C quirk verbatim: the confirmation message goes to the *target* character (`log_char(co, ...)` in `cmd_setseyan`), not the caller - only the "no one by that name" error reaches the caller - represented via the pre-existing `KeyringCommandResult::other_messages` field (the same target-notification shape iteration 185's tunnel-editor family introduced). 3 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: a full reroll asserting level/exp reset, `CF_MAGE|CF_WARRIOR` flags set, the target-only message (caller gets none), and `clear_turn_seyan_ppd`'s demonshrine-PPD side effect; the `CF_GOD` gate plus the "Sorry, no one by the name ... around." missing-target message; and the exact-8-char `cmdcmp` boundary rejecting the `/setsey` abbreviation. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 798 server [+3], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: unchanged from prior notes - still ~89 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries (now minus `/setseyan`), mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, anticheat/macro-detection systems, the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`, and the pentagram debug/admin commands needing the live pentagram-run-state model the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task must build first); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 188 ported the pentagram-debug GOD command family `/pentinfo`/`/setpentcount`/`/setpentstatus`/`/setpentbonus`/`/resetpent` (`command.c:1136-1360`, dispatched at `command.c:10416-10465`, all `CF_GOD`-gated, `cmdcmp` `minlen` equal to each word's full length so no abbreviations are accepted) - the same family iterations 172/185 had previously deferred pending a real backing model. Rather than inventing a throwaway debug-only model, added `PlayerRuntime::pentagram_debug: PentagramDebugData` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`) mirroring C's `struct pent_debug_data`/`struct pentagram_player_data` (`command.c:1136-1143`, `area/4/pents.c:130-139`) field-for-field - both are the *same* `DRD_PENT_NPPD` struct in C, and `pents.c`'s `pentagram_player_data` is the real live per-player pentagram-minigame run-state the debug commands inspect/mutate, not separate scaffolding. `DRD_PENT_NPPD` is the only `_PPD`-family id in `drdata.h` with no `PERSISTENT_PLAYER_DATA` bit, so C treats it as session-only scratch memory; kept as a plain `#[serde(default)]` field like every other `PlayerRuntime` field regardless, since this port has no separate volatile-vs-persistent storage tier and the distinction has no observable effect on a debug-only feature - a future port of the real Area 4 pentagram-solving gameplay (still unported, tracked by the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task) should reuse `pentagram_debug`'s fields directly rather than duplicating them. `pent_find_player` (`command.c:1150-1160`) has no self-fallback unlike the `/milinfo` family, so all five commands require an explicit online player name via `take_legacy_alpha_name`/`find_online_character_by_name` and use their own distinct message text ("Player '%s' not found online."/"Could not access pent data for %s.") rather than the `/milinfo`-family's "Sorry, no one by the name ... around.". Added a new `parse_pent_name_and_int` helper (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`) reproducing `sscanf(args, "%79s %d", name, &value) != 2` exactly - the integer token must start with an optional sign then at least one digit or the whole match fails, unlike `legacy_atoi_prefix`'s silent-zero-on-no-digit fallback the self-fallback command families (e.g. `/exp`/`/labsolved`) rely on - for the three `setpent*` commands. Skipped two C side effects that have no reachable Rust counterpart yet: the `macro_ppd` "saved pent data" display (`/pentinfo`) and clear (`/resetpent`) branches, since the whole macro-detection system is unported; and both commands' `dlog` moderation-log calls, matching the established untracked-side-effect skip `/kick` (iteration 174) already uses for the same function. All five commands added directly into the existing `apply_admin_character_command` if-chain (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/toggleflag` at the end of the function, following the same `lower == "..."` exact-match style as `/milinfo`/`/labsolved`. 8 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: the `CF_GOD` gate across all five commands, `/pentinfo`'s usage/missing-target/empty-data/populated-data output (including the out-of-range `pent_color` -> `"?"` fallback, matching C's `colors[]` bounds check), the three `setpent*` commands' successful named-target mutation plus `/setpentcount`'s usage/missing-integer/missing-target error paths, `/resetpent`'s usage/missing-target/full-zero-reset (asserted via `PentagramDebugData::default()` equality) sequence, and the shared "online character but no live `PlayerRuntime`" edge case both commands report as "Could not access pent data for ...". `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 805 server [+8], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: still ~85 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries (now minus the whole pentagram-debug family), mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, the anticheat/macro-detection systems - including this iteration's new `pentagram_debug` field, which the macro system would need to read/write for its save/restore-across-area-transition behavior once ported - and the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 189 ported `/noarch` (`cmd_noarch`, `src/system/command.c:3163-3192`, dispatched at `command.c:9049-9057`) and `/noprof` (`command.c:9226-9235`), both `CF_GOD`-gated exact-word `cmdcmp` matches (`minlen` equal to each word's full length, so no abbreviation is accepted) - two more entries from the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task's own note found genuinely unported (both had a `/help`-text line in `commands_player.rs` but no real dispatch handler, the same false-"done" trap iteration 183 found for `/tunnel`/`/tunnels`). Added both directly into the existing `apply_admin_character_command` if-chain (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/resetpent` at the end of the function. `/noarch` looks up an online character by (case-insensitive) name via the pre-existing `take_legacy_alpha_name`/`find_online_character_by_name` helpers - no self-fallback; a bare `/noarch` with no name resolves an empty-string lookup that never matches any real character, reproducing C's literal double-space "Sorry, no one by the name  around." (its own `log_char` format string has one literal space before `%s`, and `name` is empty) - then caps every one of the target's `values[1][0..=37]` entries (`CharacterValue::Hp` through `CharacterValue::Immunity` inclusive, matching `V_IMMUNITY = 37` digit-for-digit, confirmed against `server.h:348`) at `50` without ever raising a value already at or below the cap, and clears `CharacterFlags::ARCH`. `/noprof` is a genuine behavioral trap distinct from its own `/help` text's `<name>` usage hint: C's `cmdcmp` match never advances `ptr` past the matched word at all, so the command takes no argument whatsoever and always zeroes the *caller's own* `professions` array (`PROFESSION_COUNT` = 20, matching C's `P_MAX`) - a named bystander's professions are left completely untouched, verified against the C source line-by-line since the help text (apparently written aspirationally by an earlier iteration rather than from `command.c` itself) actively suggests the opposite. Neither command sends any confirmation message to anyone on success in C (`/noarch`'s only output is the caller-facing not-found error; `/noprof` has no `log_char` call at all), so both return `KeyringCommandResult::default()` on their success paths - both required adding `CHARACTER_VALUE_NAMES` in place of a separate `CHARACTER_VALUE_COUNT` import to avoid a `#[cfg(test)]`-only unused-import warning under a non-test `cargo build --workspace`. 6 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: the `CF_GOD` gate for both commands, `/noarch`'s not-found and bare-no-argument double-space error text, `/noarch`'s value-capping (asserting values past `V_IMMUNITY` are left untouched and values already at/below 50 are not raised) plus flag-clearing with an empty message list, `/noprof`'s self-only zeroing (an online bystander's professions survive untouched) plus its ignoring of any trailing argument text. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 811 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: still ~87 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries (now minus `/noarch`/`/noprof`), mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, the anticheat/macro-detection systems, the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`, and the pentagram-run-state model the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task must build first); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 190 ported `/fixit <name>` (`cmd_reset_questlog`, `src/system/command.c:3194-3218`, dispatched at `command.c:9058-9066`) and `/questfix <name>` (`cmd_reset_last_quest`, `command.c:3221-3251`, dispatched at `command.c:9067-9075`), both `CF_GOD`-gated exact-word `cmdcmp` matches (`minlen` equal to each word's full length) - two more entries from the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task's own note, closing the `/help`-text-only gap for `/fixit` (`commands_player.rs` already listed "/fixit <name> - Fix a player's questlog" with no real dispatch handler, the same trap iterations 183/189 found for `/tunnel`/`/noarch`). Added both directly into the existing `apply_admin_character_command` if-chain (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/noprof` at the end of the function. Both share an online-name lookup (`take_legacy_alpha_name`/`find_online_character_by_name`, reporting "Sorry, no one by the name %s around." on miss) and the pre-existing `legacy_questlog_payload`/`ServerRuntime::sessions_for_character`/`send_to_session` non-login `sendquestlog` resend pattern (`login.rs`/`military.rs`/iteration 181's `/setrd` trio). `/fixit` behaves sanely and operates on the named target throughout: wipes its entire quest log PPD (`QuestLog::default()`, matching C's `del_data`), fully re-derives it (`PlayerRuntime::init_questlog`, matching C's `questlog_init` - which now actually runs a full re-derivation since the wipe cleared the `quest[MAXQUEST-1].done == 55` sentinel `init_questlog` already checks), and resends the fresh log to the target's own session(s). `/questfix` instead reproduces a genuine, verified-by-reading-the-C-source-twice bug verbatim: `cmd_reset_last_quest`'s `set_data(cn, DRD_QUESTLOG_PPD, ...)` call uses the ACTING character's own slot `cn`, not the looked-up target `co`, so the named-target argument is really just an online-existence check with no other effect - the command's real, and only, effect is clearing the CALLER's own quest-log init-complete sentinel (new `QuestLog::clear_init_complete` method in `crates/ugaris-core/src/quest.rs`, the direct inverse of the pre-existing `mark_init_complete`) so the caller's own log gets fully re-derived from scratch on their *next* login, then resending the caller's own now-desynced-but-unchanged current log (`sendquestlog(cn, ...)`, not `co`); `questlog_init(co)` is also called on the actual target but is virtually always a no-op since an already-online character's sentinel is already set. 6 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: the `CF_GOD` gate for both commands, both commands' shared not-found error text, `/fixit`'s full wipe-and-reinit round trip (a pre-seeded bogus entry is gone and the sentinel is freshly set after the call), and `/questfix`'s exact bug reproduction (the caller's own sentinel is cleared while the named target's quest log entries are left completely untouched, confirming the swapped-character-number bug end to end). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 816 server [+5], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: still ~85 uncross-referenced `cmdcmp` entries (now minus `/fixit`/`/questfix`), mostly blocked on unported infra (the DB task queue for `punish`/`shutup`/`rename`-family commands, the anticheat/macro-detection systems, the per-character depot storage system behind `/depotsort`, and the pentagram-run-state model the "Area 4 - `pents.c`" P4 task must build first); a fresh cross-reference pass is still recommended before the next slice.
- Iteration 191 did a fresh cross-reference pass (every unique `cmdcmp(ptr, "...")` name in `src/system/command.c` grepped against `crates/ugaris-server/src/`), which found one genuine unported gap - `/clearppd <ppdname> [player]` (`cmd_clearppd`, `command.c:4214-4288`, dispatched at `command.c:10144-10146`, `CF_GOD | CF_STAFF`-gated) - and confirmed every other apparent gap was already correctly categorized (the `ac*`/`macro*` anticheat/macro-detection families, `punish`/`rename`/`lockname`/`unlockname`/`unpunish`, `showppd`, `depotsort`), plus surfaced a previously-unlisted handful of GOD-only debug/stats commands not yet examined at all: `checksanity`, `memstats`, `poolstats`, `profinfo`, `querystats`, `clearmerchantstores`, `respawn`, `rmdeath`, `exterminate`, `setnpcbodytimearea32`, `look`, `klog`, `col`, `values`/`showvalues`, `summonmacro`. Ported `/clearppd` into the existing `apply_admin_character_command` if-chain (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/questfix`. It is a raw, PPD-name-agnostic wipe over C's generic `del_data(co, ppd_id)` linked-list removal, supporting exactly three PPD names - `keyring` (`PlayerRuntime::keyring: Vec<KeyringEntry>`), `questlog` (`PlayerRuntime::quest_log: QuestLog`), `alias` (`PlayerRuntime::aliases: Vec<CommandAlias>`) - all three already fully ported as plain always-present fields, so no new storage was needed. Verified by reading the whole C function body that, unlike every other PPD-editor command in this file (`/setrd`, `/changetunnel`, `/fixit`, etc.), it performs NO resend of the cleared data to either party - just two `log_char` messages, faithfully reproduced with no resend call added. Two genuine quirks reproduced verbatim: the not-found message is "Player '%s' not found." (deliberately distinct from every other command's "Sorry, no one by the name %s around." text), and the optional player-name argument is matched via `strcasecmp` against the ENTIRE remaining text rather than a leading name token (so trailing garbage after a valid name breaks the match), searched only among `CF_PLAYER`-flagged characters (unlike most name-lookup commands in this file, which reuse the NPC-inclusive `find_online_character_by_name` helper and so needed a bespoke filtered search here instead). Since Rust keeps these three PPDs as always-present plain fields rather than C's lazily-allocated `del_data` blocks, "the PPD existed" (`del_data`'s nonzero return, which gates the "Cleared ..." vs "No ... PPD found" message text) is modeled as "the field is currently non-default" - exactly the set of players for whom C would actually have called `set_data` at least once. Added `QuestLog::is_empty` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/quest.rs`) for this purpose; `keyring`/`aliases` just check `Vec::is_empty` directly. 10 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: `CF_GOD`-or-`CF_STAFF` gate (both a GOD-gate rejection and a STAFF-only acceptance case, since this command's gate is the union of both flags unlike `/fixit`/`/questfix`'s GOD-only gate), no-argument usage text, unknown-PPD-name error, the distinct "Player '...' not found." text, the empty-vs-populated keyring found/not-found message split plus the actual field clear, a named-target alias clear with the correct messages sent to both the caller and the target, a self-target case confirming no second message is sent, a questlog clear, and a non-`CF_PLAYER` character sharing the target name being correctly skipped by the search. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2022 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 826 server [+10], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: same ~84 uncross-referenced entries as before (now minus `/clearppd`); the `ac*`/`macro*` families remain the largest unexamined chunk - there IS a `PgAntiCheatRepository`/`AntiCheatRepository` trait already in `crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`, fully async and entirely unwired into `ugaris-server` (no command, no call site anywhere), so porting even one `ac*` command requires first designing the sync-command/async-repository bridge pattern this codebase doesn't have yet - that bridge design should be its own slice before picking a first `ac*` command; the newly-listed GOD-only debug/stats commands (`checksanity` et al.) have not been individually read yet.
- Iteration 192 ported `/rmdeath <name>` (`command.c:8884-8903` dispatch -> `cmd_removedeath`, `command.c:2006-2019`), `CF_GOD`-gated, full-word only (`cmdcmp`'s `minlen` is 7) - one of the GOD-only debug/stats commands iteration 191 surfaced but hadn't read yet. C's dispatcher gates on `lookup_name` succeeding first (invalid shape -> immediate "No character by the name %s."; valid shape -> DB round trip), then `cmd_removedeath` mutates `ch[co].deaths--` with only a bounds check (`co < MAXCHARS`, effectively always true in C's architecture where every character that has ever existed keeps a permanent, always-resident `ch[]` slot whether online or not). `World` has no such permanent offline record, so - reusing the exact `/jail`/`/unjail` pattern from iteration 182/158 rather than inventing a new one - added `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/rmdeath.rs` (`RmdeathLookup`, `World::queue_rmdeath_lookup`/`drain_pending_rmdeath_lookups`/`resolve_rmdeath_lookup`, new `pending_rmdeath_lookups` field) plus `ugaris-server`'s `apply_rmdeath_events` (`world_events.rs`, wired into the game loop in `main.rs` right after `apply_jail_events`): an invalidly-shaped name resolves immediately; a validly-shaped one is queued, DB-confirmed via the existing `find_login_target` (read-only - `cmd_removedeath`'s only mutation is the in-memory `deaths` field, never a direct DB call, so no DB task-queue is needed unlike `punish`/`rename`), then resolved against an online-only scan (`World::find_online_player_by_name`) - a DB-confirmed-but-offline account reports "No player by that name." instead of silently mutating an absent in-memory record, the same deliberate deviation `/jail`/`/unjail` already established for this identical architecture gap. `deaths` decrements via `saturating_sub(1)` (`Character::deaths` is `u32`, unlike C's plain `int`). Wired dispatch in `apply_admin_character_command` (`commands_admin.rs`) right after `/jail`/`/unjail`. 5 new `ugaris-core` tests (`world/tests/rmdeath.rs`), 2 new `world_events.rs` tests (no-op/missing-repository), 4 new `ugaris-server` tests (`tests/commands_admin.rs`: GOD-gate, valid-name queuing, invalid-name immediate rejection, abbreviation-not-recognized). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2027 ugaris-core [+5] + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 832 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: same ~83 uncross-referenced entries as before (now minus `/rmdeath`); the `ac*`/`macro*` families remain the largest unexamined chunk; the other GOD-only debug/stats commands (`checksanity`, `memstats`, `poolstats`, `profinfo`, `querystats`, `clearmerchantstores`, `respawn`, `exterminate`, `setnpcbodytimearea32`, `look`, `klog`, `col`, `values`/`showvalues`, `summonmacro`) still have not been individually read - `exterminate` likely blocked on the same DB task-queue + `server_chat` infra as `punish`/`rename` (verified by reading `cmd_exterminate`/`database_admin.c`'s `exterminate()`); `respawn`'s `respawn_check()` is a C-internal respawn-tracker consistency logger with no clear Rust equivalent given this codebase's event-driven NPC-respawn architecture, likely low value.
- Iteration 193 ported `/clearmerchantstores <id>` (`command.c:7510-7538`, `CF_GOD`-gated, `cmdcmp` `minlen` 10), one of the GOD-only debug/stats commands iteration 191 surfaced but hadn't been individually read yet. Unlike C, which resets `ch[merchant_cn].gold` directly and destroys each carried store item one at a time (`remove_item_char`/`destroy_item` over the global `it[]` table), the Rust `MerchantStore` abstraction (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/merchant.rs`) already keeps a merchant's store gold and every ware as owned data in `world.merchant_stores`, so the port is just: look up the target character by numeric id (`CharacterId(legacy_atoi_prefix(rest))`, following the `ItemId(legacy_atoi_prefix(...))` precedent already used by `/listitem`), verify `driver == CDR_MERCHANT` (newly imported into `main.rs`'s `character_driver::{...}` re-export list), call the pre-existing `World::ensure_merchant_store` to guarantee an entry exists (matching C's unconditional field writes regardless of whether the merchant has ever actually traded), then set `store.gold = 10_000` and every `wares` slot to `None`. Persistence needed a new `KeyringCommandResult::clear_merchant_store_requested: Option<CharacterId>` field (the command layer has no DB handle of its own, same reason `/saveall`'s merchant sweep and `/setclanjewels`'s clan-log write are both deferred to the `main.rs` call site instead of the command layer) - wired at the `apply_admin_character_command` call site in `main.rs` to call the pre-existing `save_merchant_store_if_configured`, matching C's own `save_merchant_inventory(merchant_cn)` call immediately after the mutation. Added directly into the existing `apply_admin_character_command` if-chain (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/listchars`. The `/help`-text line ("/clearmerchantstores <id> - Reset a merchant's inventory") already existed in `commands_player.rs` from an earlier iteration with no real dispatch handler behind it - the same trap iterations 183/189/190 found for `/tunnel`/`/noarch`/`/fixit`. 3 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs`: a full success round trip (pre-seeded nonzero gold and an `always` ware both get reset/cleared, exact message text, `clear_merchant_store_requested` set), the shared "Invalid merchant ID or not a merchant character" error text for both a non-merchant online character and a nonexistent id, and the `CF_GOD` gate plus the 10-char minlen prefix rejection (confirming a merchant store is never created as a side effect of the rejected call). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2027 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 40 protocol + 835 server [+3], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: same ~83 uncross-referenced entries as before (now minus `/clearmerchantstores`); the `ac*`/`macro*` families remain the largest unexamined chunk; the other GOD-only debug/stats commands (`checksanity`, `memstats`, `poolstats`, `profinfo`, `querystats`, `respawn`, `exterminate`, `setnpcbodytimearea32`, `look`, `klog`, `col`, `values`/`showvalues`, `summonmacro`) still have not been individually read - `checksanity` needs a Rust port of `consistency_check_items`/`_map`/`_chars`/`_containers` (`src/system/consistency.c`, 453 lines total, invariant checks over C's fixed-size global arrays) adapted to the `HashMap`-based `World` model, a nontrivial chunk of its own; `memstats`/`poolstats`/`querystats`/`profinfo` all read C-only instrumentation counters (`mem_usage`/`used_chars`/`query_cnt`/connection-pool stats/a profiler) that have no Rust equivalent tracked anywhere yet - porting any of them means designing and wiring a whole new stats-counter subsystem first, not just adding a command handler.
- Iteration 195 closed out every previously-unread entry from iteration 194's list (`respawn`, `exterminate`, `setnpcbodytimearea32`, `look`, `klog`, `col`, `values`/`showvalues`, `summonmacro`), then re-ran the "every unique `cmdcmp` name" cross-reference from scratch (iteration 191's recommended pass, not repeated since then) and found one genuinely new gap. Verdicts on the unread list: `setnpcbodytimearea32`/`col` were both already done (miscategorized as unread) - `setnpcbodytimearea32` is the last entry in the `TickTuningSpec` table (`commands_admin.rs`) right next to `setnpcbodytime`, and "col" alone isn't a real `cmdcmp` entry (only `col1`/`col2`/`col3`/`color`, all done, `commands_player.rs:441-458`). `values`/`showvalues` (`look_values`/`show_values`, `command.c:501-534`) are blocked on `lookup_name` (unported offline-name-lookup cache) plus `server_chat` (cross-area relay), the same infra `/allow` is already blocked on. `look`/`klog` (`command.c:8990-9024`) are blocked on a third, previously-unnamed dependency: the notes subsystem (`karmalog`/`read_notes` -> `database_notes.c`/`database_lookup.c`), worth naming explicitly since `punish`'s note only ever mentioned the DB task-queue half. `summonmacro` is just another `macro_cmd_*` entry in the already-identified `macro*` family, not a new gap. `respawn`'s `respawn_check()` (`src/system/respawn.c:104-125`) confirmed low-value: a C-internal template-NPC-respawn-tracker consistency logger with no analogue in this codebase's different NPC-respawn architecture, producing only a console `xlog` line, no player-facing or persisted effect. `exterminate` confirmed blocked on the DB task queue + `server_chat`, same as `punish`/`rename`. The one new gap: `#ls <name> <dir>` / `#cat <name> <file>` (`command.c:9237-9253` dispatch, `CF_GOD`-gated, exact-word only) plus `cmd_ls`/`cmd_cat` (`command.c:2794-2845`) and `plr_ls`/`plr_cat` (`src/system/player.c:3750-3789`) - a debug feature asking the TARGET character's own game client to list a directory or dump a file from the client's own local disk, by forwarding a raw `SV_LS`/`SV_CAT` (opcodes 41/42) request packet to the target's connection. Unlike every other remaining gap, this one has no cross-area or DB dependency at all: it only needed the already-ported `find_online_character_by_name` (confirmed to have no `CF_PLAYER` filter, matching C's `getfirst_char`/`getnext_char` loop exactly - NPCs are valid targets) plus two new protocol-layer packet builders. Added `PacketBuilder::ls_request`/`cat_request` (`crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs`, backed by a shared private `remote_fs_request(opcode, arg)` free function): `[opcode, len_u8, arg_bytes...]` with NO trailing null on the wire (verified byte-for-byte against C - `strcpy` writes a null into its local `buf`, but `psend(nr, buf, len + 2)` only ever transmits `len + 2` bytes, so the null never reaches the client), returning `None`/`false` (no packet at all, not a truncated one) when the argument exceeds 200 bytes, matching C's `if (len > 200) return;` early exit - a deliberately different failure mode from the pre-existing `exit()` builder, which clamps and always sends. Wired dispatch into `apply_admin_character_command` (`commands_admin.rs`), right after `/questfix`, sending straight to `runtime.sessions_for_character(target_id)` from inside the handler (the same `target != caller` inline-send pattern `/fixit`/`/questfix` already established, no new `KeyringCommandResult` field needed). Reproduced C's unconditional confirmation message (`"ls {dir} scheduled on {target}."` / `"cat {file} scheduled on {target}."`) sent to the caller even when the packet send silently no-ops (target has no live session, or the argument is oversized) - only the target-not-found path skips it. Added a new `#ls`/`#cat` help-text pair to `commands_player.rs` - unlike most other gaps found in past iterations, this command had no pre-existing stray help line at all, a genuinely new entry rather than a trap. 4 new tests in `crates/ugaris-protocol/src/packet.rs` (opcode/length/no-null-byte layout for both builders, the 200-byte boundary still sending, 201 bytes producing an empty payload) and 6 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` (GOD-only gate, not-found message, real end-to-end packet-bytes-reach-the-target-session round trips for both `#ls` and `#cat` using a new `connected_god_and_target` helper modeled on `achievement.rs`'s real-session `connected_god`/`add_connected_target` pattern rather than the lighter `setup_god_and_online_target` - asserting `runtime.tick_out` bytes requires an actual registered session, `send_to_session` silently no-ops otherwise - plus the confirmation-still-sent-with-no-live-session case and the 201-byte-argument no-packet-but-still-confirmed case). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2047 ugaris-core + 55 db + 3 net + 44 protocol [+4] + 844 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: every entry from this fresh full cross-reference pass is now accounted for and falls into one of six buckets: (a) the `ac*`/`macro*` families (anticheat sync-command/async-repository bridge design needed first, iteration 191), (b) `punish`/`rename`/`lockname`/`unlockname`/`unpunish`/`exterminate` (DB task-queue), (c) `look`/`klog`/`values`/`showvalues` (the notes subsystem and/or cross-area `server_chat`), (d) `memstats`/`poolstats`/`querystats`/`profinfo` (need a new stats-counter subsystem), (e) `showppd` (needs many more named PPD field accessors), (f) `depotsort` (unported depot storage system), or (g) `respawn` (low-value, no clean architectural analogue). No remaining item is a quick win without tackling one of those six infra/architecture gaps first - the next iteration picking this task back up should pick ONE of (a)-(g) as its own slice (most promising: the anticheat bridge design, since `PgAntiCheatRepository` already exists fully unused and would unblock the largest single family) rather than re-scanning `command.c` for more `cmdcmp` names.
- Iteration 196 picked gap (a) from iteration 195's list - the anticheat sync-command/async-repository bridge - and ported the session-*lifecycle* half only (C `ac_player_login`/`ac_player_disconnect`, `src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:73-220`), deliberately not any of the 22 `ac_cmd_*` admin display commands (those would only ever show empty/zero data without real session rows existing first - `PgAntiCheatRepository`/`anticheat_sessions` existed fully implemented and migrated but completely unwired, no login/logout code ever created a row). Wired `AntiCheatRepository::create_session` at the `SessionEvent::Login` success site (`crates/ugaris-server/src/main.rs`, inside the `Ok(LoginOutcome::Ready { .. })` arm, right after character identity is known) and `end_session` at `SessionEvent::Disconnected` (unconditionally, independent of whether the character lingers under `CDR_LOSTCON` - matches C's `ac_player_disconnect` keying off the physical connection `nr`, not character presence; a lostcon-reclaim reconnect gets a *new* anti-cheat session at the login site instead of reusing the old one, matching a fresh physical connection). Added `PlayerRuntime::anticheat_session_id: Option<i64>` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`), cleared on `reclaim_for_session` for the same reason. Threaded the account id and a real `login_sessions.id` (previously discarded entirely - the insert never even used `RETURNING id`) through a new `LoginOutcome::Ready { login_session_id, account_id, .. }` pair (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`) so the anti-cheat session can link back to both without an extra query, atomic with the existing `begin_login_tx` transaction. Explicitly NOT ported this iteration: the detection engine (heartbeat/state/challenge/anomaly/fingerprint subsystems, `anticheat_state.c`/`anticheat_heartbeat.c`/`anticheat_challenge.c`/`anticheat_anomaly.c`/`anticheat_fingerprint.c`, ~1450 lines combined) that would populate `bot_score`/violation counters - every session is created and closed with every counter at its SQL default (0) - and all 22 `ac_cmd_*` admin commands, which now have real (if currently always-empty-of-violations) session rows to query but still need their own command-dispatch plus `World`-side async-lookup-queue wiring (the `world/lastseen.rs` pattern is the template) as a future slice. `set_character`/`set_fingerprint`/`update_bot_score`/`increment_counters`/`set_status`/`log_event`/`cleanup_old_records` are all exercised by a new live-DB test but still have no caller anywhere in `ugaris-server` outside that test - they await the detection engine. 3 new tests: `ugaris-db/src/character.rs`'s existing `live_login::accepts_matching_area_and_records_login_session` extended to assert the returned `login_session_id` is the real `login_sessions.id` primary key, not a placeholder; 2 new live tests in `crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs` (`create_update_and_end_session_round_trip` - a full create/set_character/set_fingerprint/set_status/update_bot_score/increment_counters/log_event/end_session chain verified against real rows, using a real fixture account+character for the `character_id` foreign key since that column, unlike the other two optional linking columns, is a genuine FK; `updates_on_an_unknown_session_id_report_not_found`, confirming every mutator returns `Ok(false)` rather than erroring or silently succeeding for a nonexistent id) - both follow `merchant.rs`'s manual-cleanup `live` convention since `AntiCheatRepository` methods commit directly via `&self.pool` with no transaction parameter to hook a rollback onto (unlike `character.rs`'s `live_login` module). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2047 ugaris-core + 57 db [+2] + 3 net + 44 protocol + 844 server, all green, zero failures) - verified by spinning up a throwaway `postgres:16-alpine` container, applying all 11 `migrations/*.sql` files by hand, running `DATABASE_URL=... cargo test -p ugaris-db` (57/57 passed including both new live tests, repeated against a fresh container after the first run showed cross-run test-data-collision flakiness unrelated to this change - confirmed pre-existing by reproducing the exact same collisions on `main` reruns), then destroying the container; without `DATABASE_URL` set (this repo's default state) the same tests compile and skip cleanly. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic and the expected "DATABASE_URL not set; starting without persistence" warning beforehand. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gap (a) is now partially closed (session lifecycle only); gaps (b)-(g) are all still open exactly as iteration 195 described. Next slice for gap (a): either the detection engine itself (large, likely warrants its own dedicated top-level task rather than staying folded into this one) or a first `ac_cmd_*` admin command or two (e.g. `acstatus <name>`, `aclist`) using the `World::queue_*_lookup` + `apply_*_events` async-bridge pattern `/lastseen` established.
- Iteration 197 picked iteration 196's suggested next slice for gap (a) - ported the 4 read-only display members of the `ac_cmd_*` family that need no detection-engine data to be meaningful: `#achelp`/`#acstatus <name>`/`#acstats`/`#aclist` (`command.c:10148-10192` dispatch -> `ac_cmd_help`/`ac_cmd_status`/`ac_cmd_stats`/`ac_cmd_list`, `src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:473-543,604-628,688-753`), all `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, exact-word only. `#achelp` is pure static text (C's own help text reproduced letter for letter, minus `COL_*` wrapping - matching `/global`'s established plain-text-dump simplification - even though most listed subcommands are still unported, since it's C's own aspirational help text, not a coverage reflection). The other three needed a genuine architecture bridge: C reads `player[nr]->ac` synchronously (kept live by the unported detection engine); this codebase's only source of that data is the `anticheat_sessions` Postgres row wired up in iteration 196, referenced by `PlayerRuntime::anticheat_session_id` - so, like `/lastseen`, this becomes an async DB round trip, except the online-name-scan (C's `ac_find_player`, `CF_PLAYER`-filtered) plus session-id lookup has to happen synchronously in `commands_admin.rs` (which has both `world` and `runtime`) *before* queuing to `World`, since `World` has no visibility into `PlayerRuntime` - a deviation from `/jail`'s "queue the raw name, resolve online-ness later" pattern forced by this being the first async-DB command whose input is a session id, not a name. Added `ugaris-db`'s `AntiCheatRepository::find_session`/`find_sessions` (single/batched `anticheat_sessions` row reads by id, backed by the new `AntiCheatSessionInfo` struct) and `ugaris-core`'s new `world/anticheat.rs` (`AcStatusLookup`/`AcListLookup`/`AcStatsLookup` queues + `ac_status_string`, C's status-name table). `ugaris-server`'s `commands_admin.rs` dispatches all four (gathering online `CF_PLAYER` targets sorted by ascending character id for determinism, matching `world/clanmaster.rs`'s sibling-helper convention, and silently omitting any online player with no known anticheat session - "DB not configured" or "session row never got created" - from `acstats`/`aclist`'s tallies rather than padding with defaults); `world_events.rs` gained `apply_ac_status_events`/`apply_ac_stats_events`/`apply_ac_list_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `/lastseen`'s. Message shapes reproduce C's own text exactly (status names, violation counts, bot score, fingerprint/mod-version/OS/screen block, the `Total players:`/`Total: N players` summaries) with `COL_*` wrapping dropped throughout, the same simplification `/global` already established for text-heavy admin dumps. 4 new tests in `ugaris-core`'s `world/tests/anticheat.rs` (status-string table + queue/drain round trips for all three lookup types), 2 new assertions folded into `ugaris-db`'s existing live-DB round-trip test plus 2 new assertions in its not-found test (both extended, not new test functions, so the db crate's test count is unchanged at 57), 12 new tests in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`ac_status_tests`/`ac_stats_tests`/`ac_list_tests`: pure `ac_status_lines` formatting for with/without-fingerprint and unknown-OS-type, plus the standard no-lookups/missing-repository no-op pair per command) and 13 in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (GOD-or-STAFF gate for all four commands, `#achelp`'s exact text including a staff-without-god case, `#acstatus`'s usage/not-found-online/no-connection-data/successful-queue paths, and `#acstats`/`#aclist`'s online-target-gathering with a session-less caller and a session-bearing target). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2051 ugaris-core [+4] + 57 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 863 server [+19], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: only 4 of the ~20-member `ac_cmd_*` family are ported; `acreset`/`acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`/`acwatch` (session-status mutations - `set_status` already exists, but `acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust` also call C's `db_ac_flag_player`/`db_ac_trust_player`/`db_ac_log_admin_action`, which key off a `subscriber_id` and a `subscribers`-adjacent table this codebase's schema doesn't have), `achistory`/`acsessions`/`acviolations` (need new aggregate queries plus the same `subscriber_id` gap), `acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup` (multi-account detection, a new query surface), `acsiglist`/`acsigadd`/`acsigdel` (a whole unported bad-signature table), `accleanup` (thin wrapper over the already-ported `cleanup_old_records`, likely the next quick win), `acwarn` (needs the notes/karmalog subsystem, gap (c)), and `acsuspicious` (same shape as `aclist` filtered to suspicious/flagged - should be a quick follow-up now the plumbing exists) all remain unported. Gaps (b)-(g) from iteration 195's list are untouched by this slice.
- Iteration 198 picked one of iteration 197's two named quick wins for gap (a) - ported `#acsuspicious` (`ac_cmd_suspicious`, `src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:754-780`, `command.c:10191-10192`), `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, exact-word only, completing the trio of "same shape as `#aclist`" read-only display commands (`#achelp` and the name-lookup-driven `#acstatus` were already done in earlier iterations). Gathers every online `CF_PLAYER` character with a known anticheat session exactly like `#aclist`/`#acstats` already do (the same synchronous-gather-then-queue-to-`World` split forced by `World` having no visibility into `PlayerRuntime`), but unlike `#aclist` - which shows every gathered target - `#acsuspicious` can only apply its `status >= AC_STATUS_SUSPICIOUS` filter after the async DB round trip resolves each session's actual status, so the filtering lives in the new `apply_ac_suspicious_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`), not in the command handler. Added `ugaris-core`'s `world/anticheat.rs::AcSuspiciousLookup` (+ `queue_ac_suspicious_lookup`/`drain_pending_ac_suspicious_lookups`, `world/mod.rs`'s matching `pending_ac_suspicious_lookups` field) and the `AC_STATUS_SUSPICIOUS = 2` constant (C `anticheat.h:84`) the new filter checks against. Reproduced C's exact zero-count text ("No suspicious or flagged players online.") as genuinely different from `#aclist`'s unconditional "Total: N players" tail, confirmed by reading the whole C function body which only emits a count line when `count == 0` is false. `commands_admin.rs` wires `#acsuspicious` into the existing shared `acstatus`/`acstats`/`aclist` dispatch arm (extending its condition rather than adding a new one, since the online-`CF_PLAYER`-with-a-session gather is identical); `main.rs`'s tick loop calls the new `apply_ac_suspicious_events` right after `apply_ac_list_events`. `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#acsuspicious` (added preemptively by an earlier iteration), so no help-text change was needed. 2 new tests in `ugaris-core`'s `world/tests/anticheat.rs` (queue/drain round trip, the `AC_STATUS_SUSPICIOUS` constant value), 2 new tests in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`ac_suspicious_tests`: no-lookups no-op, missing-repository no-op - matching the same shape as `ac_list_tests`/`ac_stats_tests`, neither of which has a live-DB status-filter test either, so none was added here for consistency), and 2 assertions extended into the existing `acstats_and_aclist_are_god_or_staff_only`/`acstats_and_aclist_only_gather_online_players_with_a_known_anticheat_session` tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (not new test functions, matching the sibling pair's own established shape). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2053 ugaris-core [+2] + 57 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 865 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: `accleanup` (thin wrapper over the already-ported `cleanup_old_records`) is now the clear next quick win in gap (a); `acreset`/`acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`/`acwatch`/`achistory`/`acsessions`/`acviolations`/`acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup`/`acsiglist`/`acsigadd`/`acsigdel`/`acwarn` remain unported, each still needing one of the architecture gaps iteration 195/197 already named. Gaps (b)-(g) from iteration 195's list are untouched by this slice.
- Iteration 199 picked gap (a)'s named quick win from iteration 198's REMAINING note - ported `#accleanup <days>` (`ac_cmd_cleanup`, `src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:1267-1285`, `command.c:10314-10319` dispatch), `CF_GOD`-only (unlike its `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF` siblings above), exact-word only. Unlike every other member of this family it needs no name/session resolution at all: `days` is parsed with the existing `legacy_atoi_prefix` and validated entirely synchronously in `commands_admin.rs` (empty argument -> two-line usage text; `days < 7` -> "Minimum retention is 7 days.", matching C's own `atoi` semantics where a non-numeric argument silently becomes `0` and hits the same rejection); only the delete itself needs the async DB round trip, finally calling `AntiCheatRepository::cleanup_old_records` (ported in iteration 196 but uncalled from anywhere in `ugaris-server` until now). Added `ugaris-core`'s `world/anticheat.rs::AcCleanupLookup` (+ `queue_ac_cleanup_lookup`/`drain_pending_ac_cleanup_lookups`, `world/mod.rs`'s matching `pending_ac_cleanup_lookups` field) and `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs::apply_ac_cleanup_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_ac_suspicious_events`. C also deletes from a separate `ac_heartbeat_log` table (`db_ac_cleanup_heartbeat_logs`) this codebase has no equivalent of - heartbeat counters live directly on the `anticheat_sessions` row instead of a standalone log table - so the reported heartbeat-log-deleted count is always `0`, matching C's own always-present "%d heartbeat logs deleted" clause rather than dropping that half of the message. Reproduced C's two-part message flow: the immediate "Cleaning up records older than %d days..." line returns synchronously from the command handler (standing in for C's own same-thread progress line before its blocking DB call), and the final "Cleanup complete: %d sessions, %d heartbeat logs deleted." line is queued separately once the async delete finishes; a failed delete is silently skipped, matching every other offline-DB-lookup event in `world_events.rs`. 1 new test in `ugaris-core`'s `world/tests/anticheat.rs` (queue/drain round trip), 2 new tests in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`ac_cleanup_tests`: no-lookups no-op, missing-repository no-op, same shape as every sibling), 4 new tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (`CF_GOD`-only gate rejecting both a plain online target and a `CF_STAFF`-only character - the one real behavioral difference from its siblings; missing-argument usage text; the `0`/`6`/`-5`/`notanumber` minimum-retention rejection sweep; the `>= 7` success path asserting both the confirmation message and the queued `AcCleanupLookup`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2054 ugaris-core [+1] + 57 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 871 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING: no more quick wins remain in gap (a) without first choosing a scoped-down design for the `subscriber_id`/`subscribers` schema gap (`acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`) or the new aggregate-query surface (`achistory`/`acsessions`/`acviolations`/multi-account detection/signature management) - the next iteration picking this up should make one of those an explicit design decision (scoped port vs. skip, matching `/kick`'s precedent) rather than looking for another already-solved building block.
- Iteration 200 re-read gap (a)'s two most recently flagged commands (`acreset`/`acwatch`) and found both are actually independent of the `subscriber_id`/`subscribers` schema gap blocking their siblings: `ac_cmd_reset` (`src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:527-561`, `CF_GOD`-only) and `ac_cmd_watch` (`anticheat.c:894-921`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`) never call `get_subscriberId_from_character`/`db_ac_*` at all, unlike `acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`. Ported both. `#acreset <player>` reuses `#acstatus`'s exact single-name-target resolution (online `CF_PLAYER` scan, ascending-id tiebreak, `PlayerRuntime::anticheat_session_id` lookup) but its DB half is a mutation: added `AntiCheatRepository::reset_session` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs` - zeroes `bot_score`/`heartbeat_violations`/`state_violations`/`challenge_failures`/`timeout_count`, restores `status` to a new `AC_STATUS_VERIFIED = 1` constant, deliberately leaving `max_bot_score`/`anomaly_count` untouched since C's own reset doesn't touch its equivalents either), `world/anticheat.rs`'s `AcResetLookup` queue (same shape as `AcStatusLookup`), and `world_events.rs`'s `apply_ac_reset_events` (queues "Reset anti-cheat data for {name}." only once the async update reports a row was actually touched, unlike C's unconditional same-thread reply), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_ac_cleanup_events`. `#acwatch <player>` needed no DB or queue at all: added `PlayerRuntime::ac_watch_enabled` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, a new bool next to `anticheat_session_id`, reset on `reclaim_for_session` like a fresh physical connection, mirroring C's per-connection `player[nr]->ac.watch_mode`) and toggled it directly via `runtime.player_for_character_mut(target_id)` in `commands_admin.rs`, replying synchronously with C's exact enabled/disabled text; the flag currently has no other behavioral effect since the detection engine that would read it for verbose logging is unported, matching every other pre-wired-but-inert toggle in `PlayerRuntime` (`no_ball` and siblings). 6 new tests in `ugaris-core` (`world/tests/anticheat.rs`: `AC_STATUS_VERIFIED` constant, `AcResetLookup` queue/drain round trip), 2 in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`ac_reset_tests`: no-lookups/missing-repository no-op pair, same shape as every sibling), and 12 in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (`acreset`'s God-only gate rejecting both a plain target and a staff-only character, usage/not-found/no-session/successful-queue paths; `acwatch`'s God-or-staff gate, usage/not-found paths, the genuinely-no-`PlayerRuntime`-at-all case - distinct from `acreset`/`acstatus`'s "online but no anticheat session" case - and the on/off toggle round trip with its exact confirmation text). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2056 ugaris-core [+2] + 57 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 883 server [+12], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING: every command in gap (a) that doesn't need the `subscriber_id`/`subscribers` schema or a new aggregate-query surface is now ported; the next iteration picking this up should make an explicit design decision (scoped port vs. skip, matching `/kick`'s precedent) for one of: the `subscriber_id` gap (`acflag`/`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`), the new aggregate queries (`achistory`/`acsessions`/`acviolations`), multi-account detection (`acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup`), or signature management (`acsiglist`/`acsigadd`/`acsigdel`) - or pick a different gap entirely from (b)-(g).
- Iteration 201 picked gap (d) from iteration 195's list - `memstats`/`poolstats`/`querystats`/`profinfo` (`command.c:7476-7513`/`6588-6612`), all `CF_GOD`-gated, `minlen=5`. Read all four C handlers fully and found a load-bearing distinction the task note hadn't captured: `/profinfo` and `/poolstats` send exactly one header line to the calling player in C (`"Profiling Information:"` / `"Connection Pool Statistics:"`) and then call `show_prof()` (`server.c:934-986`) / `log_connection_pool_state()` (`database_connection_pool.c:23-37`), both entirely `xlog()` console-only output - the player never receives the real profiler/pool data in the C oracle either. A byte-faithful port of those two is therefore just the header line, no new instrumentation needed; ported both into `apply_admin_character_command` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after the existing `/prof` block. `/memstats` is different: C sends every stat line to the player via `log_char`, so a faithful port needs real numbers. Ported it using `World`'s actual live collections as the closest honest equivalent: `world.characters.len()`/`world.items.len()`/`world.effects.len()` for `used_chars`/`used_items`/`used_effects`, and an `Item.content_id != 0` filter (the same "container" derivation `world/consistency.rs` already uses, since Rust has no separate container store) for `used_containers`. Two C fields have no Rust analogue at all and are reported as a fixed `0`, matching the established "no real equivalent -> always report the harmless constant" convention (`#accleanup`'s always-`0` heartbeat-log count is the precedent): `mem_usage` (C's heap-allocation byte counter) and `used_msgs` (C's pending-notify-message queue depth - Rust drains notifications to packets every tick with no persistent countable queue). Also dropped the C `used_X/MAXX` denominators entirely (just "N used", no "/N"): `MAXCHARS`/`MAXITEM`/`MAXEFFECT` are themselves runtime-configurable C globals, not fixed constants, and `World`'s character/item/effect stores are unbounded `HashMap`s with no analogous "capacity" concept to divide by. Left `/querystats` (`command.c:6588-6618`) unported: unlike the other three, its 12+ counters (`query_cnt`, `query_stat[20]`, `save_char_cnt`, etc.) are incremented at 6+ different call sites spread across `system/database/database*.c` with no existing single choke point, and `ugaris-db` has zero instrumentation of any kind today (confirmed by grepping every repository file) - porting it means designing a new cross-cutting counters mechanism threaded through every repository method first, not a same-slice quick win like the other three turned out to be. 3 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` (`god_profinfo_command_reports_header_only_like_c`, `god_poolstats_command_reports_header_only_like_c` - both covering the minlen-5 abbreviation boundary, the God-only gate, and the pre-existing `/prof` command's independence from the new `/profinfo` one; `god_memstats_command_reports_live_world_counts` - asserts the exact message set against a world seeded with 2 characters, 1 loose item, 1 container item, and 1 effect). The pre-existing stray `/memstats`/`/profinfo`/`/poolstats`/`/querystats` help-text lines in `commands_player.rs` (noted as a trap in earlier iterations) were already accurate for the first three now that dispatch exists; `/querystats`'s help line remains a stray pointing at nothing. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2056 ugaris-core + 57 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 886 server [+3], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING: `querystats` (this iteration's gap (d) remainder) plus every item in gaps (a), (b), (c), (e), (f), (g) from iteration 195's list remain unported. Next iteration should either design `querystats`'s counters mechanism as its own scoped slice (e.g. just `save_char_cnt`/`load_char_cnt`/`exit_char_cnt` since `CharacterRepository` is a single existing choke point for those three, rather than all 12+ at once) or pick a different lettered gap.
- Iteration 202 closed iteration 201's own suggested scoped slice: ported `/querystats`'s `save_char_cnt`/`exit_char_cnt`/`load_char_cnt` counters (`command.c:6596,6603-6604`), the three that share a single existing choke point (`ugaris_db::PgCharacterRepository`), leaving `query_cnt`/`query_long`/`query_long_max`/`query_time`/`save_area_cnt`/`save_storage_cnt`/`save_subscriber_cnt`/`save_char_mirror_cnt`/`query_stat[20]` unported (no instrumentation anywhere for any of them, and each is incremented at call sites this codebase has no matching single choke point for). Added a new private `CharacterQueryCounters` (three `AtomicU64`s, shared via `Arc` across `PgCharacterRepository` clones - the same cheap-clone-shares-pool convention every other `Pg*Repository` already follows) plus a public `CharacterQueryStats` snapshot and `PgCharacterRepository::query_stats()` reader (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`). Incremented `save_char_cnt`/`exit_char_cnt` unconditionally inside `save_character_snapshot` before the transaction begins (matching C's `save_char_cnt++`/`exit_char_cnt++` placement right before `execute_query`, regardless of success), keyed off the pre-existing `CharacterSaveMode::Backup`/`Logout` enum that already maps 1:1 onto C's `area_number <= 0`/`> 0` branches. `load_char_cnt` is trickier: C increments it right before `load_char`'s "mark character as online" `UPDATE chars ...` query, which only ever runs once every rejection branch (wrong password, locked, not paid, etc.) has already returned - the Rust equivalent (`begin_login_tx`'s own `update characters set current_area = ...` query) has the identical one-query-per-successful-login shape, so `begin_login` increments the counter whenever `begin_login_tx` returns `LoginOutcome::Ready` (the only outcome that query ever ran for), without threading the counter through the free `begin_login_tx` function itself. Command-side: since `commands_admin.rs`'s dispatcher only has `&mut World`/`&mut ServerRuntime` (no DB handle), reused the `/lastseen`/`#ac*` queue-then-drain async-bridge pattern verbatim - new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/querystats.rs` (`QueryStatsLookup` + `queue_querystats_lookup`/`drain_pending_querystats_lookups`, `world/mod.rs`'s matching `pending_querystats_lookups` field) and `world_events.rs::apply_querystats_events` (a plain, non-`async fn` for once, since `query_stats()` is a synchronous atomic read, not a real query - still routed through the tick-loop drain purely for architectural consistency with every other DB-backed command in that file), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_ac_reset_events`. Reply reproduces C's `"Database Query Statistics:"` header and `"Character operations:"`/`"Save chars: %u, Exit chars: %u, Load chars: %u"` lines verbatim; every other C line (`Total queries`/`Average query time`/`Other operations`/`Query type statistics`) is omitted entirely (not faked with zeroes) since nothing increments those counters - a deliberate scope boundary, not an oversight. 2 new tests in `ugaris-core`'s new `world/tests/querystats.rs` (queue/drain round trip, independent multi-caller queuing), 1 new test in `ugaris-db/src/character.rs` (`query_stats_start_at_zero_and_are_shared_across_clones` - constructed via `PgPoolOptions::connect_lazy` so it needs no live Postgres connection at all, since `connect_lazy` only parses the URL and `query_stats`/a direct `fetch_add` on the counters never issue a real query; confirms the zero start state and that increments through one clone are visible through another, i.e. the `Arc` sharing actually works), 4 new tests in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`querystats_tests`: the standard no-lookups/missing-repository no-op pair plus two pure `querystats_lines` formatting tests - nonzero and all-zero counters - split out exactly like `ac_status_lines`'s established testable-pure-function precedent), and 1 new test in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (`god_querystats_command_queues_a_lookup` - minlen-5 abbreviation boundary, God-only gate, no immediate reply since the real data needs the async round trip). Did not add a live-DB test for `save_character_snapshot`/`begin_login`'s actual counter increments: unlike `begin_login_tx`, which already has a rollback-friendly manual-transaction test fixture (`locked_tx`, never committed by the caller), the full trait-level `PgCharacterRepository::begin_login`/`save_character_snapshot` methods open and commit their own pool-level transactions internally, which would break that fixture's carefully-maintained "never actually persists" isolation convention - and `CharacterSaveRequest`/`save_character_snapshot_tx` have no existing test coverage at all to extend safely in this scope; noted here rather than silently skipped. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2058 ugaris-core [+2] + 58 db [+1] + 3 net + 44 protocol + 891 server [+5], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: `querystats` now reports its three scoped counters; the other 9 C counters remain unported (no single choke point, or genuinely no Rust instrumentation surface yet) and would need their own scoped design decision if ever prioritized. Gaps (a) (the `subscriber_id`/aggregate-query/multi-account/signature-management sub-gaps), (b), (c), (e), (f), (g) from iteration 195's list are all still open and untouched by this slice.
- Iteration 203 picked another `subscriber_id`-independent member of gap (a): `#acflag <player>` (`ac_cmd_flag`, `src/module/anticheat/anticheat.c:568-593`, `command.c:10167-10174` dispatch), `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, exact-word only. Read `ac_cmd_flag`/`ac_cmd_unflag`/`ac_cmd_trust`/`ac_cmd_untrust` fully first to confirm which of the four `#acflag` actually belongs with: unlike its three siblings (all gated on `get_subscriberId_from_character` succeeding before touching `db_ac_trust_player`/`db_ac_flag_player`/`db_ac_log_admin_action`, the still-missing `subscriber_id`/`subscribers`-adjacent schema iteration 197 flagged), C's `ac_cmd_flag` makes no DB call of any kind - it only assigns `player[nr]->ac.status = AC_STATUS_FLAGGED` in memory, so it needed nothing beyond the already-existing `AntiCheatRepository::set_status` mutation and the exact `#acreset`/`#acstatus` single-name-target resolution pattern (online `CF_PLAYER` scan, ascending-id tiebreak, `PlayerRuntime::anticheat_session_id` lookup). `#acunflag`/`#actrust`/`#acuntrust` remain correctly blocked on the schema gap. Added `ugaris-core`'s `world/anticheat.rs::AcFlagLookup` (+ `queue_ac_flag_lookup`/`drain_pending_ac_flag_lookups`, `world/mod.rs`'s matching `pending_ac_flag_lookups` field) and a new `AC_STATUS_FLAGGED = 3` constant (C `anticheat.h:85`); `ugaris-server`'s `commands_admin.rs` dispatches `#acflag` right after `#acreset` (same resolution, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF` gate matching `#acwatch` rather than `#acreset`'s `CF_GOD`-only), and `world_events.rs::apply_ac_flag_events` calls `set_status(session_id, AC_STATUS_FLAGGED)`, queuing "Manually flagged {name} for review." only once the async update reports a row was actually touched (C's own reply is unconditional and same-thread), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_ac_reset_events`. `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#acflag`, so no help-text change was needed. 2 new tests in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events.rs` (`ac_flag_tests`: no-lookups/missing-repository no-op pair, same shape as every sibling) and 5 in `tests/commands_admin.rs` (God-or-staff gate confirmed to accept staff alone, unlike `#acreset`'s God-only gate; usage/not-found/no-session/successful-queue paths). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2058 ugaris-core + 58 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 898 server [+7], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gap (a)'s `subscriber_id`-independent commands are now exhausted (`acstatus`/`acstats`/`aclist`/`acsuspicious`/`accleanup`/`acreset`/`acwatch`/`acflag` all ported); every remaining `ac*` command (`acunflag`/`actrust`/`acuntrust`/`achistory`/`acsessions`/`acviolations`/`acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup`/`acsiglist`/`acsigadd`/`acsigdel`/`acwarn`) needs either the `subscriber_id`/`subscribers` schema, a new aggregate-query surface, or the notes/karmalog subsystem (gap (c)) first - the next iteration picking this up should make an explicit scoped-design decision for one of those rather than looking for another already-solved building block, or pick a different lettered gap ((b)/(c)/(e)/(f)/(g)) entirely.
- Iteration 204 picked gap (e) - `/showppd <name> <ppd>` (`cmd_showppd`, `src/system/command.c:275-346`, dispatched at `command.c:8790-8837`, `CF_GOD`-gated, `cmdcmp(ptr, "showppd", 7)` exact-word only). Read the whole function first and found it's much smaller in scope than the task note's "needs many more named PPD field accessors" implied: only two PPD names are recognized at all (`area1`, `area3`), and `area3`'s branch reads exactly one field (`kassim_state`) out of that struct's 18. So the real gap was "every remaining `area1_ppd` field" (28 of 39 fields had no accessor: `yoakin_seen_timer`, `gwendy_seen_timer`, `terion_state`, `flags`, `darkin_state`, `gerewin_state`/`_seen_timer`, `lydia_seen_timer`, `asturin_state`/`_seen_timer`, `guiwynn_seen_timer`, `logain_seen_timer`, `reskin_seen_timer`/`_got_bits`, `shrike_state`/`_fails`, `brithildie_seen_timer`, `jiu_state`/`_seen_timer`, `greeter_state`/`_seen_timer`, `aclerk_state`/`_seen_timer`, `camhermit_seen_timer`/`_kills`, `jessica_seen_timer`) plus one `area3_ppd` field (`kassim_state`). Added get/set accessor pairs for all 29 (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`), matching the existing offset-constant/`read_area1_i32`/`write_area1_i32` pattern exactly (each new constant is `field_index * 4` per `src/area/1/area1.h:24-83`); `forest_ranger_state`/`_seen_timer` (the struct's last two fields) were left without accessors since `cmd_showppd` never reads them either. Wired the command itself into `apply_admin_character_command` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`), right after `/resetpent`: name/ppd-name parsing mirrors C's `isalpha`/`isalpha-or-isdigit` scan loops exactly via the pre-existing `take_legacy_alpha_name` plus a new sibling `take_legacy_alnum_name` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_player.rs`); message-check order matches C precisely (online-name lookup first - "Sorry, no player by the name %s online (offline chars not possible)." - then the remaining-argument-empty "Which ppd?" check, then the ppd-name match), and the ten `Area1 ppd of %s`/field-group `log_char` lines plus the one-line `area3` reply and the "Sorry, no ppd by the name %s."/"Reading PPD %s failed." fallbacks are reproduced with the exact C wording and grouping. 2 new tests in `ugaris-core`'s `player.rs` test module (`area1_ppd_exposes_remaining_fields_for_showppd` - all 26 new get/set pairs round-tripped through `encode_legacy_area1_ppd`/`decode_legacy_area1_ppd`; `area3_ppd_exposes_kassim_state_for_showppd`) and 6 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` (`showppd_is_god_only`, offline/unknown-name message, missing-ppd-name "Which ppd?", unknown-ppd-name rejection, a full `area1` dump asserting every one of the 11 output lines verbatim against 37 distinct seeded field values, and an `area3` dump confirming only `kassim_state` is reported even when a sibling field like `seymour_state` is also set). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2060 ugaris-core [+2] + 58 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 904 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed the tick loop advancing (tick 232+) with no panic. This closes gap (e) entirely (the task's own enumeration of `area1`/`area3` is now fully covered). REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gaps (a) (the `subscriber_id`/aggregate-query/multi-account/signature-management `ac*` sub-gaps), (b) (`punish`/`rename`/`lockname`/`unlockname`/`unpunish`/`exterminate`, DB task-queue), (c) (`look`/`klog`/`values`/`showvalues`, notes subsystem/cross-area relay), (f) (`depotsort`, unported per-character legacy depot storage), and (g) (`respawn`, confirmed low-value with no clean analogue, likely permanently skip) remain open - the next iteration picking this up should pick one of (a)/(b)/(c)/(f) as its own scoped slice.
- Iteration 205 re-read gap (b) in full and found it was actually two unrelated groups conflated together: `punish`/`shutup`/`unpunish` genuinely need `lookup_name` (an offline name->ID cache, `src/system/lookup.c`) plus C's DB task-queue (`task_punish_player`, `src/system/task.c`); `exterminate` needs the same `subscriber_id`/`subscribers`-adjacent schema gap already blocking half of gap (a) (`db_exterminate`, `src/system/database/database_admin.c:29-95`, queries a `subscriber`/`iplog`/`ipban` table trio this codebase's schema has none of). But `rename`/`lockname`/`unlockname` (`cmd_rename`/`cmd_lockname`/`cmd_unlockname`, `command.c:2657-2701`, all `CF_GOD`-gated, full-word only) call neither `lookup_name` nor any task-queue function - `do_rename`/`do_lockname`/`do_unlockname` (`database_admin.c:291-467`) each run a single direct, synchronous-shaped `UPDATE`/`INSERT`/`DELETE` query keyed by name, with no online/offline distinction at all. Ported all three as their own genuinely independent sub-slice, reusing the `world/complain.rs`/`world/admin_flag.rs` queue-then-drain async-bridge pattern verbatim (`World` has no DB handle) since `ugaris-server` already threads an `Option<PgCharacterRepository>` through the tick loop for exactly this offline-DB-round-trip shape.
