Added 3 new `CharacterRepository` trait methods (`rename_character`/`lock_name`/`unlock_name`, `crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`) - deliberately living on this trait rather than a new one, purely to reuse the already-threaded `PgPool`/`Option<PgCharacterRepository>` plumbing, matching this file's own `query_stats()` precedent of non-per-character methods sharing the same repository - backed by a new `locked_names` Postgres table (`migrations/0012_locked_names.sql`, this codebase's equivalent of C's schema-less `badname` table, which no C code outside `database_admin.c` itself ever queries either - it exists purely as an admin-facing audit/blocklist record in both codebases). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/rename.rs`/`world/lockname.rs` reproduce two genuinely different validation orders found by reading both C functions fully: `do_rename`'s `to`-name check is alpha-first (bailing "Illegal name." on the first non-alphabetic character while capitalizing/lowercasing in the same pass) *then* length-bounded (`3..=35`, "Name too long or too short.") only once the whole string passes; `do_lockname`/`do_unlockname` check length *first*, then alpha-validate - the exact reverse order, kept as two distinct code paths rather than a shared "validate name" helper that would blur the difference. `world_events.rs`'s three new `apply_rename_events`/`apply_lockname_events`/`apply_unlockname_events` reproduce each C function's exact three-way reply (query error / no-op / success wording), always addressed back to the caller (`tell_chat(0, masterID, ...)` in C is always the caller's own ID for these three, never the target - unlike `/jail`'s two-party messaging), wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_admin_flag_events`. Dispatch (`crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs`, right after `/rmdeath`) parses one or two `isalpha`-only name tokens via the pre-existing `take_legacy_alpha_name` helper, truncated to C's 79-byte buffer cap, matching `/showppd`'s (iteration 204) established precedent for that truncation. 10 new `ugaris-core` tests (`world/tests/rename.rs`, `world/tests/lockname.rs`: valid-name queuing, both validation-order edge cases per module, from-name passthrough) and 16 new `ugaris-server` tests (10 in `tests/commands_admin.rs`: God-only gates, valid-queue paths, invalid-name-rejected paths, abbreviation-not-recognized paths; 6 in `world_events.rs`'s `rename_tests`/`lockname_tests`: no-lookups/missing-repository no-op pairs for all three events, matching every sibling offline-DB-mutation event's established test shape - no live-Postgres round-trip test exists for any of them, consistent with `admin_flag_tests`/`ac_reset_tests` and the rest of this file's own precedent). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2070 ugaris-core [+10] + 58 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 920 server [+16], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. This closes the `rename`/`lockname`/`unlockname` slice of gap (b) entirely. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gap (b)'s `punish`/`shutup`/`unpunish` (lookup_name + DB task-queue) and `exterminate` (subscriber_id schema) are still open, alongside gap (a) (`ac*` subscriber_id/aggregate-query/multi-account/signature-management sub-gaps), (c) (`look`/`klog`/`values`/`showvalues`, notes subsystem/cross-area relay), (f) (`depotsort`, unported per-character legacy depot storage), and (g) (`respawn`, low-value, likely permanent skip) - the next iteration picking this up should either pick one of (a)/(b: punish family)/(c)/(f), or make the shared `lookup_name` + DB-task-queue infra a real, scoped design decision of its own, since both gap (b)'s remainder and all of gap (c) ultimately depend on it.
- Iteration 206 closed gap (f): ported `/depotsort` (`cmdcmp(ptr, "depotsort", 6)` -> `depot_sort`, `command.c:9350-9357`, no permission gate) and, since a sort command is meaningless without the storage it sorts, the entire previously-unported personal legacy storage depot: `src/system/depot.c`'s `swap_depot`/`player_depot`/`depot_cmp`/`depot_sort` plus its `struct depot_ppd { struct item itm[MAXDEPOT]; }` (`depot.h:19-23`, `MAXDEPOT` 80, `DRD_DEPOT_PPD`) - a distinct, older, per-character system from `ugaris-server::depot`'s account-wide `AccountDepotState` (opened via `IF_DEPOT` on the access item rather than `IDR_ACCOUNT_DEPOT`). Added `PlayerRuntime::depot: Vec<Option<Item>>` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`, default 80 `None`s, `MAXDEPOT` now a public core constant) as a genuinely typed PPD field (matching the `keyring`/`quest_log` precedent for structurally-rich PPDs), with its own `encode_legacy_depot_item`/`decode_legacy_depot_item` (an independent copy of the C `struct item` byte layout `ugaris-server::depot`'s `legacy_account_depot_codec` already encodes for `AccountDepotState` - duplicated across the crate boundary since `ugaris-server` depends on `ugaris-core`, not the reverse) and `encode_legacy_depot_ppd`/`decode_legacy_depot_ppd`, always persisting all 80 fixed-size slots (unlike `AccountDepotState`'s own subscriber-blob codec, which compacts empty slots out of a variable-length block) so a slot's index survives save/load exactly. Wired `DRD_DEPOT_PPD` into `decode_legacy_ppd_blob`/`encode_legacy_ppd_blob` following the established `had_x`-flag rewrite-or-append pattern. Also closed a second, smaller documented gap while in the area: `turn_seyan` (`tool.c:4379-4388`) sweeps `IF_QUEST`-flagged items out of the 80 depot slots on every gate-fight class-8 loss - previously a no-op since the depot didn't exist - now ported at `PlayerRuntime::clear_turn_seyan_ppd` (a real slot wipe, `ppd->itm[n].flags = 0`, not just stripping one flag off a kept item). Server-side gameplay logic (`crates/ugaris-server/src/depot.rs`, extending the existing account-depot-only file): `apply_personal_depot_command` (`CL_CONTAINER`/`CL_CONTAINER_FAST`/`CL_LOOK_CONTAINER` dispatch, gated on the open container item's `ItemFlags::DEPOT` rather than a driver id), `personal_depot_swap_slot` (`swap_depot` - only blocks `IF_NODEPOT`, deliberately NOT `IF_QUEST` like the account depot's own swap does, which is exactly why `turn_seyan`'s sweep above has to exist; a blocked store is a silent no-op with no message, unlike account depot's "You cannot store this item in the depot." reply), `store_cursor_item_into_inventory` (C `store_citem`, the same first-free-slot scan `apply_item_container_swap` already uses for generic containers), `personal_depot_payload` ("Your Depot", `MAXDEPOT` slots, `player.c:3282-3313`), and `personal_depot_sort`/`personal_depot_sort_command` (`depot_cmp`, sprite-desc/value-desc/name-asc/empties-last, identical ordering to `account_depot_sort`). C's `player_depot`'s `flag`/`fast` branching needed a genuinely separate read from both sibling systems: a fast *store* click searches for the first empty depot slot server-side, ignoring the clicked slot entirely (like `account_depot_store_cursor`), but a fast *withdraw* click still swaps the exact clicked slot and then unconditionally also runs `store_citem` afterward (even if the swap itself was blocked) - a shape neither generic containers nor the account depot share. Wired dispatch in `main.rs`'s account-depot-vs-generic-container branch (now a 3-way branch keyed on `IDR_ACCOUNT_DEPOT` / `ItemFlags::DEPOT` / neither), `containers.rs::current_container_payload` (added the matching `ItemFlags::DEPOT` branch, now taking an extra `personal_depot: Option<&[Option<Item>]>` parameter), and `/depotsort` itself as a new dispatch arm right after the pre-existing `/accountdepotsort` (the `/depotsort` help line in `commands_player.rs` already existed unwired, like several other gaps this task's own note has found before). While updating both container-refresh call sites for the new payload signature, fixed a small pre-existing bug for free: the item-*use* (double-click open) path's own separate `container_refresh` list only ever pushed on `OpenAccountDepot`, never on `OpenContainer`/`OpenDepot`, so opening a plain container or this new personal depot by double-click never sent an initial view - now pushes for all three `Open*` outcomes and its flush loop calls the same unified `current_container_payload` instead of a hardcoded `account_depot_payload` call. 3 new `ugaris-core` tests (depot-blob populated/empty-slot round trip, the outer `ppd_blob` had/append wiring, `turn_seyan`'s quest-purge keeping non-quest items) and 12 new `ugaris-server` tests (`tests/depot.rs`: store/withdraw swap, the silent `IF_NODEPOT` block vs. the loud account-depot one, quest items allowed in this depot, fast-store-finds-first-empty-slot-ignoring-clicked-slot, fast-store no-op when full, fast-withdraw's extra `store_citem` landing in inventory, look for populated/empty slots, the legacy container payload header/slot bytes, `/depotsort`'s always-sorts-regardless-of-open-state behavior, the sort ordering, and a `PlayerRuntime`-level codec round trip). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2073 ugaris-core [+3] + 58 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 932 server [+12], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. This closes gap (f) entirely. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gap (a) (`ac*` subscriber_id/aggregate-query/multi-account/signature-management sub-gaps), gap (b) (`punish`/`shutup`/`unpunish`/`exterminate`), and gap (c) (`look`/`klog`/`values`/`showvalues`) are still open; next iteration should pick one of those, or make the shared `lookup_name`+DB-task-queue infra decision that unblocks most of (b) and (c) at once. `destroy_item_byID`/`buggy_items` (`questlog.c:1663`/`tool.c:3887`, admin sanity/quest-purge sweeps that also touch `DRD_DEPOT_PPD` in C) are NOT ported - neither function has any Rust port at all yet, not even their non-depot inventory halves - so a future port of either should extend `PlayerRuntime::depot` rather than reinventing it.
- Iteration 207 closed most of gap (b) - ported `/punish <name> <level> <reason>` (`command.c:6500-6507` dispatch -> `cmd_punish`, `command.c:2354-2406`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated) and `/unpunish <name> <note id>` (`command.c:6541-6547` dispatch -> `cmd_unpunish`, `command.c:2706-2731`, `CF_GOD`-only) in full, including the shared `src/system/task.c`/`src/system/punish.c` machinery (`task_punish_player`/`punish_player`/`punish` and `task_unpunish_player`/`unpunish_player`/`unpunish`), making the shared DB-task-queue infra decision iteration 206's note flagged. New `crates/ugaris-db/src/notes.rs` (`NotesRepository`/`PgNotesRepository`, `migrations/0013_notes.sql`) mirrors C's schema-less `notes` table (`add_note`/`db_unpunish` - the latter's select-then-delete-by-bare-id with no `uID` scoping preserved verbatim); new `CharacterRepository::set_character_locked` writes the `characters.locked` column C's `set_task` touches as a third, unrelated column alongside its `chr`/karma/clan/exp update. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/punish.rs`: `PunishmentNote`/`encode_punishment_note`/`decode_punishment_note` (C `struct punishment`'s exact 92-byte layout), `apply_punishment`/`apply_unpunishment` (the pure karma/exp mutation halves of `punish()`/`unpunish()`, reused identically for a live `World::characters` entry or a freshly loaded DB snapshot), and `World::queue_punish_command`/`queue_unpunish_command`. Reused the exact "online (any loaded character) first, else read/mutate/write the persisted row, else silently no-op if logged in elsewhere" shape `world/admin_flag.rs`'s `cmd_flag` offline fallback already established (new `ugaris-server::world_events::apply_punish_events`/`apply_unpunish_events`) rather than building a literal generic task-queue abstraction. The lock/kick disconnect (C `kick_player`, triggered by `karma <= -12` or unpaid-and-`karma <= -5`) turned out simpler than expected: `kick_player` does not run `exit_char` (unlike `/kick`'s own handler) - it only detaches the socket and marks the character `CDR_LOSTCON`, so `apply_punish_events` just sends the exit packet and requests a session disconnect, funneling through this codebase's *already-existing* `SessionEvent::Disconnected` -> `enter_lostcon_on_disconnect` machinery exactly like a real network drop - no new disconnect/lostcon logic was needed. Deliberately simplified vs. C (documented in `world/punish.rs`'s module doc comment): `add_note`'s write is best-effort (a failure doesn't roll back the mutation, unlike C's own `punish()` gating all messaging on `add_note`'s return value, since there is no cross-table transaction spanning `characters` and `notes`); `write_scrollback`/`server_chat(31, ...)` are skipped, matching the established skip-untracked-C-side-effect convention. 17 new `ugaris-core` tests (`world/tests/punish.rs`), 4 new `ugaris-db` tests (2 SQL-shape + 2 `DATABASE_URL`-gated live round-trip), 13 new `ugaris-server` tests (9 `tests/commands_admin.rs` + 4 `world_events.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2090 ugaris-core [+17] + 62 db [+4] + 3 net + 44 protocol + 945 server [+13], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: `shutup`/`exterminate` (both blocked on the `subscriber_id`/`subscribers`-adjacent schema gap or `server_chat`, not unblocked by this iteration) close out gap (b); gap (a) (`ac*` sub-gaps) and gap (c) (`look`/`klog`/`values`/`showvalues`, notes-*read* subsystem - `db_read_notes`/`list_punishment` could now reuse this iteration's `notes` table/`NotesRepository` for the read side, though `list_punishment`'s own `lookup_ID` ID->name cache is a separate unported gap) remain open.
- Iteration 208 closed gap (c) - ported `/look <name>` (`command.c:8990-9019`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated inline handler, `read_notes`/`db_read_notes`/`list_punishment`) and `/klog` (`command.c:9022-9024` -> `karmalog`/`db_karmalog`/`karmalog_s`, also `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, no argument) in full, including the previously-unported reverse ID->name lookup (C `lookup_ID`/`db_lookup_id`, `src/system/lookup.c:98-135` + `src/system/database/database_lookup.c:20-48`) both commands' note-creator/note-target name display needs. Added `CharacterRepository::find_name_by_id` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`, a plain `select name from characters where id = $1` - no in-memory LRU cache like C's, since every lookup here is already a per-tick deferred DB round trip) and two new `NotesRepository` methods on iteration 207's `notes` table (`crates/ugaris-db/src/notes.rs`): `list_notes_for_character` (`db_read_notes`'s query, ordered by `id`, for `/look`) and `list_recent_notes` (`db_karmalog`'s 24-hour-window query, newest first, for `/klog`), both returning a shared `NoteRow` struct. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/look.rs`: `World::queue_look_command`/`drain_pending_look_requests` (name-shape validation reusing `world/lastseen.rs`'s `is_valid_lookup_name`) and `World::queue_klog_command`/`drain_pending_klog_requests` (no validation - `/klog` takes no argument). New `ugaris-server::world_events::apply_look_events`/`apply_klog_events` resolve the deferred round trips and reply via `World::queue_system_text`, reusing `world/punish.rs`'s `PunishmentNote`/`decode_punishment_note`/`PUNISHMENT_NOTE_KIND` to decode each `kind = 1` row, plus two pure formatting helpers (`format_look_note_line`/`format_klog_line`) reproducing `list_punishment`'s full-date line and `karmalog_s`'s time-only line byte for byte, reusing `xmas.rs`'s pre-existing `civil_from_unix_seconds` UTC-approximation helper (`clan_log.rs`'s established `chrono`-free convention). C's `ID == 0` ("lookup in progress") intermediate state has no analogue here since this codebase's name resolution is a single deferred-to-next-tick round trip, never a multi-tick cache-fill. Wired both commands into `apply_admin_character_command` (`commands_admin.rs`) right after `/unpunish`, and both event functions into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after `apply_unpunish_events`. 4 new `ugaris-core` tests (`world/tests/look.rs`), 7 new `ugaris-db` tests (1 SQL-shape in `character.rs` + 2 SQL-shape + 2 live round-trip in `notes.rs`), 13 new `ugaris-server` tests (8 `tests/commands_admin.rs` + 5 `world_events.rs`'s `look_tests`/`klog_tests`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2094 ugaris-core [+4] + 67 db [+5] + 3 net + 44 protocol + 958 server [+13], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. This closes gap (c) entirely. REMAINING for the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task: gap (a) (`ac*` subscriber_id sub-gaps) and `values`/`showvalues` (a different pair of commands from `/look`/`/klog`, confirmed by re-reading iteration 195's note - both need `server_chat` cross-area relay, same as `/allow`) and `shutup`/`exterminate` (gap (b)'s remainder) remain open.
- Iteration 209 picked gap (a) and found the `subscriber_id` schema gap iterations 197/199/200 kept deferring was smaller than assumed: `get_subscriberId_from_character` just reads `chars.sID`, exactly this codebase's pre-existing `characters.account_id` column - no new "subscriber id" concept was needed, only the small `ac_player_stats` table (`migrations/0014_ac_player_stats.sql`, `is_flagged`/`is_trusted` only). Ported `#acunflag <player>` (`ac_cmd_unflag`, `anticheat.c:790-823`, `CF_GOD`-only), `#actrust <player>` (`ac_cmd_trust`, `anticheat.c:827-849`, `CF_GOD`-only) and `#acuntrust <player>` (`ac_cmd_untrust`, `anticheat.c:860-882`, `CF_GOD`-only). New `ugaris-db` `AntiCheatRepository` methods `account_id_for_session` (reads the session's already-stored `account_id`) and `set_flagged`/`set_trusted` (single upsert queries). New `ugaris-core` `world/anticheat.rs` types `AcUnflagLookup`/`AcTrustLookup`/`AcUntrustLookup`. `#acunflag` uniquely gates on the target's *current* flagged status (a synchronous read in C, moved into the async `apply_ac_unflag_events` here since this codebase has no in-memory status to read synchronously) - a genuine deviation from every sibling event's silent-skip-on-missing-row convention. `db_ac_log_admin_action`'s audit-trail row is skipped entirely (no `ac_admin_actions` table), matching the `/kick`-`dlog` untracked-audit-log convention. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 209's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
- Iteration 210 picked iteration 209's own suggested quick win: extended `ac_player_stats` (`migrations/0015_ac_player_stats_warnings.sql`) with `warnings_issued`/`last_warning_at` and ported `#acwarn <player> [reason]` (`ac_cmd_warn`, `anticheat.c:1291-1314`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, `command.c:10323-10329` dispatch) in full. New `AntiCheatRepository::issue_warning` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) upserts the row, incrementing the counter rather than overwriting it (`db_ac_issue_warning`, `database_anticheat.c:606-621`). New `ugaris-core` `world/anticheat.rs::AcWarnLookup` - unlike every sibling in this family it also carries `target_id`, since C's handler messages the *target* character directly (two of its four `log_char` calls), not just the caller - the first command in this slice needing that. New `ugaris-server::world_events::apply_ac_warn_events` resolves the subscriber id via `account_id_for_session` (a `None` result reproduces C's "Could not find subscriber for '{name}'." branch) then calls `issue_warning` *ignoring its `Result`* and unconditionally sends all four reply lines, matching C's own disregard for `db_ac_issue_warning`'s return value at the call site - a deliberate, documented deviation from this file's usual "reply only once the mutation succeeds" convention. Dispatch (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `#acwatch`) reproduces C's `sscanf(args, "%39s %255[^\n]", target, reason)` name/reason split with the C-side pre-seeded default reason ("Anti-cheat warning") applied when the second token is absent. `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#acwarn` unwired, so no help-text change was needed. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 210's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
- Iteration 211 ported `#acsessions <player>` (`ac_cmd_sessions`, `anticheat.c:975-1017`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, `command.c:10241-10249` dispatch). Realized C's own backing `ac_sessions` table is structurally the same per-login-session history this codebase already maintains as `anticheat_sessions`, so no new table or rollup engine was needed (unlike `achistory`, which does need one - see the REMAINING note below). New `AntiCheatRepository::recent_sessions` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) queries the newest `max_count` rows for an `account_id`, formatting timestamps/duration server-side via Postgres `to_char`/`extract(epoch ...)` (matching `find_last_seen`'s no-chrono-in-Rust convention) rather than C's `DATE_FORMAT`/`TIMESTAMPDIFF`. New `ugaris-core` `world/anticheat.rs::AcSessionsLookup` (same shape as `AcTrustLookup`); `apply_ac_sessions_events` (`world_events.rs`) resolves the subscriber id the same way `#actrust`/`#acwarn` do, queries up to 10 rows (matching C's `sessions[10]` stack array), and formats them through a new pure `ac_sessions_lines` helper with color wrapping dropped (matching `ac_status_lines`'s/`/global`'s plain-text-dump convention). Dispatch (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `#acwarn`) reuses the identical online-name-scan-then-session-id-lookup shape every sibling has; `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#acsessions` unwired, so no help-text change was needed. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 211's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
- Iteration 212 ported `#acviolations <player>` (`ac_cmd_violations`, `anticheat.c:1019-1053`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, `command.c:10250-10255` dispatch), following iteration 211's own suggestion to try reusing `anticheat_events` before assuming a new schema was needed - it was: C's own `ac_violations` table joins a separate `ac_violation_types` lookup table only because C stores the violation type as a numeric foreign key, while this codebase's pre-existing `anticheat_events` table already stores `event_type` as human-readable text directly, so no join or new violation-types table was needed. New `AntiCheatRepository::recent_violations` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) joins `anticheat_events` against `anticheat_sessions` for the account-id scope and queries the newest `max_count` rows, formatting timestamps server-side via Postgres `to_char` (same no-chrono-in-Rust convention as `recent_sessions`). New `AntiCheatViolationRow` return type and `ugaris-core` `world/anticheat.rs::AcViolationsLookup` (identical shape to `AcSessionsLookup`); `apply_ac_violations_events` (`world_events.rs`) resolves the subscriber id via `account_id_for_session`, queries up to 15 rows (matching C's `violations[15]` stack array), and formats them through a new pure `ac_violations_lines` helper with color wrapping dropped but the numeric severity kept inline as text (`sev={n}`) rather than discarded, since no other column carries that information. Dispatch (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `#acsessions`) reuses the identical online-name-scan-then-session-id-lookup shape every sibling has; `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#acviolations` unwired, so no help-text change was needed. This closes the `acviolations` gap entirely, leaving `achistory` as gap (a)'s one remaining item that genuinely needs a new rollup schema. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 212's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
- Iteration 213 closed gap (a)'s `#acsiglist`/`#acsigadd`/`#acsigdel` trio (`ac_cmd_siglist`/`ac_cmd_sigadd`/`ac_cmd_sigdel`, `anticheat.c:1192-1266`, `command.c:10291-10311` dispatch, all `CF_GOD`-only exact-word) in one slice, since all three share one new backing table and need no player-name resolution at all - the simplest remaining `ac*` family member. C's own `ac_known_signatures` table was referenced by `db_ac_get_signatures`/`db_ac_add_signature`/`db_ac_delete_signature` but never itself defined anywhere in this codebase before this iteration; added it fresh (`migrations/0016_ac_known_signatures.sql`, columns matching `struct db_ac_signature_result` plus the two write-only columns `signature_value`/`created_by` no read query ever selects back - reproduced as-is, matching C's own query which never selects `signature_value` either). New `AntiCheatRepository::list_signatures`/`add_signature`/`delete_signature` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) and new `ugaris-core` `world/anticheat.rs` types `AcSiglistLookup` (no target, same shape as `AcCleanupLookup`), `AcSigaddLookup`/`AcSigdelLookup` (pure parsed-argument carriers - no online-name resolution needed since these operate on the global signature table, not a player). Dispatch (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `#acviolations`) reproduces C's `sscanf(args, "%31s %255s %63[^\n]", type, value, name)` three-token parse letter for letter via new helper `parse_ac_sigadd_args` plus the fixed five-member case-sensitive type allow-list check; `#acsigdel <id>` parses with `legacy_atoi_prefix` and rejects `0` immediately, approximating C's `atoi` + unsigned-cast wraparound quirk for negative input by simply letting the DB "not found" branch handle negatives instead (functionally equivalent, since neither can match a `bigserial` primary key). New `apply_ac_siglist_events`/`apply_ac_sigadd_events`/`apply_ac_sigdel_events` (`world_events.rs`) drain the three queues each tick; `#acsigdel`'s "not found" reply is genuine user-facing text in C (unlike most siblings' silent-skip-on-DB-error convention), reproduced by checking the mutator's `bool` result. New pure `ac_siglist_lines` formatter preserves C's literal double-space quirk before `Det:` when a signature has neither `auto_flag` nor `auto_ban` set. This closes the `acsiglist`/`acsigadd`/`acsigdel` gaps entirely, leaving only `achistory` (rollup schema) and `acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup` (cross-account joins) open in gap (a). See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 213's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
 - Iteration 214 ported the session-end lifetime-rollup half of `ac_player_disconnect` (`db_ac_update_player_stats`, `database_anticheat.c:480-517`, called from `anticheat.c:141-163` right after `db_ac_session_end`/`db_ac_session_set_status`) - the missing piece iterations 210-212's notes kept naming as `achistory`'s blocker. Extended `ac_player_stats` (new migration `0017_ac_player_stats_rollup.sql`) with the lifetime counters C's function actually reads/writes: `total_sessions`, `flagged_sessions`, `suspicious_sessions`, `total_heartbeat_violations`, `total_state_violations`, `total_challenge_failures`, `total_anomalies`, `lifetime_bot_score`, `max_session_bot_score`, `avg_session_bot_score`, `risk_level`, `last_seen` - deliberately NOT `first_seen`/fingerprint columns, which belong to the separate sibling `db_ac_update_player_fingerprint` mutation, still unported. New `AntiCheatRepository::update_player_stats` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) folds C's `db_ac_ensure_player_stats`-then-atomic-`UPDATE` two-step into one Postgres upsert (same shape as `set_flagged`/`set_trusted`/`issue_warning`), reproducing the exact `was_flagged`/`was_suspicious` (`session_status == 3`/`== 2`) -> `risk_level` thresholds (`max_session_bot_score >= 1.0 OR flagged_sessions >= 3` -> critical, `>= 0.8 OR >= 1` -> high, `>= 0.5 OR suspicious_sessions >= 3` -> medium, else low) via `GREATEST`/`CASE` over `excluded.*` instead of MySQL's `@variable` trick. Wired directly into `main.rs`'s `SessionEvent::Disconnected` handler right after `end_session`, using a `find_session` snapshot taken *before* `end_session` runs (the same row `#acstatus` reads) so the rollup call sees the session's state at disconnect time, then resolves the subscriber id via the pre-existing `account_id_for_session`, matching C's own read-in-memory-struct-before-either-DB-call-mutates-it ordering. Since no detection engine exists yet, every counter/bot-score argument at this call site is always `0`/status `0`, so in practice only `total_sessions`/`last_seen` accumulate real data and `risk_level` stays permanently `low` - exactly the same "real but always-zero" state iteration 196 established for session creation, extended one step further down the lifecycle. 1 new `ugaris-db` live test (`update_player_stats_accumulates_and_classifies_risk_tiers`: four sequential calls for one fixture subscriber walking all three risk tiers - low, then high via bot score alone, then critical, then a fourth low-score/verified-status call proving `max_session_bot_score` never drops back down and non-flagged calls don't touch `flagged_sessions` - verified against a real throwaway `postgres:16-alpine` container with all 17 migrations applied by hand before being torn down). This closes the write-side half of the `achistory` blocker; `#achistory` itself (the display command) remains unported, and `acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup` remain the other open gap-(a) items - `acsharedip`/`acsharedhw` in particular could plausibly reuse `anticheat_sessions`' existing `ip_address`/`hardware_hash` columns instead of porting C's separate `ac_ip_history`/`ac_hardware_history` tables, worth trying before assuming new schema is needed, following this file's established reuse-over-new-schema pattern. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 214's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
 - Iteration 215 closed gap (a)'s `#achistory <player>` item (`ac_cmd_history`, `anticheat.c:924-972`, `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, `command.c:10232-10239` dispatch) - the last piece iterations 210-214's notes had been tracking as blocked-then-unblocked. Same single-name-target resolution shape as `#acsessions`/`#acviolations` (new `AcHistoryLookup` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/anticheat.rs` plus its `World` queue/drain pair), but reads a single lifetime rollup row instead of a per-event list: added `AntiCheatRepository::find_player_stats` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/anticheat.rs`) - the first *read* of the `ac_player_stats` table `update_player_stats`/`set_flagged`/`set_trusted`/`issue_warning` already write into - backed by a new `AntiCheatPlayerStatsRow` struct. Added migration `0018_ac_player_stats_first_seen.sql`, closing the one remaining schema gap iteration 214's own migration doc comment had explicitly deferred: a `first_seen timestamptz not null default now()` column, populated once on a subscriber's first `update_player_stats` insert and never touched by that method's `on conflict ... do update` clause (verified by a new live test asserting it survives a second rollup call unchanged). New `apply_ac_history_events`/`ac_history_lines` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`) reproduce C's exact 7-line body (header + sessions/violations/bot-score/risk/flagged-trusted-warnings/first-seen/last-seen lines) digit for digit, `COL_*` color wrapping dropped matching this file's established plain-text convention for admin-only displays; "No AC history found for {name}." when no `ac_player_stats` row exists yet, matching C's null-`db_ac_get_player_stats` branch. Dispatch (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `#acviolations`) reuses the identical online-name-scan-then-session-id-lookup shape every sibling has; `#achelp`'s help text already listed `#achistory` unwired, so no help-text change was needed. This closes gap (a)'s `achistory` item entirely, leaving `acsharedip`/`acsharedhw`/`achighrisk`/`aclookup` as the family's only remaining unported members. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 215's Progress Log entry) for full test/verification detail.
 - Iteration 216 closed gap (a) entirely, porting its last four members: `#acsharedip <player>` (`ac_cmd_sharedip`, `anticheat.c:1058-1088`), `#acsharedhw <player>` (`ac_cmd_sharedhw`, `anticheat.c:1096-1126`), `#achighrisk` (`ac_cmd_highrisk`, `anticheat.c:1134-1157`) and `#aclookup <subscriber_id>` (`ac_cmd_lookup`, `anticheat.c:1158-1191`), all `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated (`command.c:10259-10289` dispatch). `#acsharedip`/`#acsharedhw` reuse the `#acsessions`/`#acviolations`/`#achistory` single-name-target resolution shape; `#achighrisk` needs no target (same shape as `#acsiglist`); `#aclookup` is the family's first raw-numeric-id target (parsed with the pre-existing `legacy_atoi_prefix`, no online-name-scan at all). Rather than porting C's separate `ac_ip_history`/`ac_hardware_history` aggregate tables (populated by an unported `db_ac_track_ip`/`db_ac_track_hardware` writer), the new `AntiCheatRepository::shared_ips`/`shared_hardware` derive the same shape live by self-joining the existing `anticheat_sessions` table on `ip_address`/`hardware_hash` - the reuse-over-new-schema approach iteration 214's note already flagged as worth trying. `AntiCheatHighRiskRow`/`AntiCheatSubscriberLookup` read the existing `ac_player_stats` table. None of these four commands' C format strings' `email` field has a home in this codebase's schema (`accounts` has no email column); `accounts.username` substitutes for it throughout (`#aclookup`'s missing `"Email: %s"` line is folded into its header instead of dropped). 33 new tests across `ugaris-core`/`ugaris-server` (queue/drain round trips plus `_lines` formatting coverage for every new command); no live `ugaris-db` fixture test yet covers the four new joined queries. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 1036 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 216's Progress Log entry) for full detail.
- Iteration 217 did a fresh full `cmdcmp(ptr, "...")` extraction pass (308 entries in `command.c`) against every `commands_*.rs`/`weather.rs`/`clan_command.rs` string literal, since the task note had drifted before. Found the previously-uncounted "~90 CF_GOD ac*/macro* commands" claim was wrong - the real `ac*`/`ach*` surface (31 names) is already fully accounted for (all ported, in this closed gap (a) or the achievement-system `ach*` family), and the only genuinely unported names left were `exterminate`/`values`/`showvalues` (blocked on the DB task-queue/`server_chat` infra, unchanged), `respawn` (a low-value internal-log-only NPC-respawn-checker diagnostic, left unported), and the 9-member "macro daemon" admin/debug command family (`macrostats`, `macrohistory`, `summonmacro`, `macroimmune`, `macrolist`, `macrosuspicion`, `macrokarma`, `macrofailures`, `macroreset` - `macrohelp`, the tenth member, was already ported). Ported all 9 following the `pentagram_debug`/`/pentinfo` precedent exactly: added `PlayerRuntime::macro_ppd` (`MacroPpd`/`MacroHistoryEntry`, `crates/ugaris-core/src/player.rs`), a field-for-field mirror of C's persistent `struct macro_ppd` (`DRD_MACRO_PPD`, `command.c:585-626`) so a future port of the real, unported ~800-line `macro_driver` detection engine (`src/module/base.c:802-1243` - activity tracking, math/type-word/reverse/multiple-choice challenges, reward/failure handling, cross-server "challenge room" teleport; deserves its own dedicated `PORTING_TODO.md` task) can reuse this same storage. All 9 dispatch arms added to `apply_admin_character_command` (`commands_admin.rs`, right after `/resetpent`), reusing `parse_pent_name_and_int` plus a new `find_online_macro_player` (C `macro_find_player`, `CF_PLAYER`-only online scan) and `macro_activity_ago`/`macro_challenge_type_name` helpers; `macrostats`/`macrohistory`/`macrolist` are `CF_GOD|CF_STAFF`-gated, the six mutating commands `CF_GOD`-only. Skipped `/macrostats`'s C sibling "Anticheat Bot Score" line (`ac_anomaly_get_bot_score`) - porting it would require an async `#acsessions`-style DB round trip for one optional line, flagged as a follow-up that should reuse `AntiCheatRepository`'s existing bot-score plumbing. 14 new tests in `tests/commands_admin.rs`: permission gating (including STAFF-alone acceptance for the three read-only commands), usage/not-found paths, `/macrostats`'s full conditional-line matrix, `/macrohistory`'s circular-buffer reverse-chronological ordering, the 0-100 clamps on suspicion/karma, the floor-at-0-no-ceiling on failures, `/macroreset`'s exact untouched-field scope, and `/macrolist`'s column formatting plus status-priority ordering across four sorted players. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 1050 server [+14], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` / `cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (iteration 217's Progress Log entry) for full detail.
- Iteration 218: closed the "Remaining `/` and `#` text commands" task (`PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`) instead of leaving it open on an ever-drifting REMAINING note. Re-verified iteration 217's leftover list is now fully accounted for: `exterminate`/`values`/`showvalues`/`/allow` are all blocked on the same unported cross-server IPC (`server_chat`'s real multi-node relay, plus - for `exterminate` specifically - the DB task-queue pattern) that the already-open "Cross-area transfer" P3 task exists to design/port, so a cross-reference note was added there rather than inventing a second tracking spot for the same blocker; `respawn` stays deliberately unported (confirmed again via `command.c:9665-9668` + `src/system/respawn.c` - internal `xlog`-only diagnostic, no player-visible or persisted effect). The two items iteration 217 explicitly flagged as "deserves its own task" were spun out into new P3 `PORTING_TODO.md` entries rather than folded back into this closed task: (1) "Player-side fight-driver auto-combat" - confirmed via `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_fight.rs` that `fight_driver_attack_enemy`/`fight_driver_attack_visible`/`fight_driver_follow_invisible` (`src/system/drvlib.c:1682-2320`) already have a substantial Rust port (`order_fight_driver_tasks`, the `simple_baddy_*_value` scoring helpers, built up across many iterations - see this file's `src/module/simple_baddy.c` rows above), but it is wired *only* to the `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` NPC driver tick; the player-side callers (the already-ported but inert `PlayerRuntime` no*/auto* toggle family from iteration 167, the `player_driver.c:1067-1070` auto-rebless/auto-pulse hooks, and the `CDR_LOSTCON` self-defense driver referenced by the closed "Logout/exit flow" task's REMAINING note) are entirely unwired, so none of those 16 toggles have any gameplay effect yet and a lingering lostcon character never fights back. (2) "Macro-detection engine" - the real `macro_driver` (`src/module/base.c:802-1243`) that would populate iteration 217's already-ported-but-forever-empty `PlayerRuntime::macro_ppd` storage. No code changes this iteration (paperwork/planning only, to stop the same drift the last several iterations' notes kept calling out); full workspace still green: `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 44 protocol + 1050 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings.
- Iteration 222 wired two more of the six remaining `attempt_cross_area_transfer` call sites the iteration-221 note in the `database_area.h`/`.c` row (above, "Cross-area transfer" section) had flagged as missing: clan-spawn exit and mine gateway (both `main.rs`, full detail in that row's updated note and `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Cross-area transfer" entry). No new tests added - the shared helper and the outcome-producing driver functions already have full coverage, and the `main.rs` tick-loop dispatch match has no extracted/testable function of its own (same gap as the pre-existing `TransportTravel` arm it mirrors). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1056 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic.
 - Iteration 223 wired two more of the remaining `attempt_cross_area_transfer` call sites the iteration-222 note in the `database_area.h`/`.c` row (above, "Cross-area transfer" section) had flagged as missing: `/office` and `/goto`/`/jump` (`finish_goto_jump`), both `crates/ugaris-server/src/commands_admin.rs` (full detail in that row's updated note and `PORTING_TODO.md`'s "Cross-area transfer" entry). Unlike the previous two sites, these live in a plain synchronous `fn` with no `ServerRuntime`/DB handle, so a new deferred `KeyringCommandResult::cross_area_transfer` field carries the request out to a new `main.rs` handling block, following the same established pattern as `/kick`'s save and `/setclanjewels`'s clan-log write. Updated 4 existing unit tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin.rs` that previously asserted the eager "down" message to instead assert the new field. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1056 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic.
 - Iteration 224 wired the next remaining `attempt_cross_area_transfer` call site the iteration-223 note flagged as missing: `/jail`/`/unjail` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/jail.rs`, C `jail_player`/`unjail_player`, `src/system/tool.c:4392-4425`'s tail). Unlike `/office`/`/goto` (synchronous `fn`, deferred via a `KeyringCommandResult` field), `World::apply_jail_action` runs inside the *async* `resolve_jail_lookup` DB-lookup path with no `ServerRuntime`/DB handle either, so it now pushes a new `JailCrossAreaTransfer { caller_id, target_id, target_area, target_x, target_y }` onto a new `World::pending_jail_cross_area_transfers` queue instead of sending the "down" message eagerly. A new `world_events.rs::apply_jail_cross_area_transfers` (called from `main.rs` right after the pre-existing `apply_jail_events`) drains that queue once per tick and calls the shared `attempt_cross_area_transfer` helper with `target_mirror = u32::from(config.mirror_id)` (matching C's `change_area` reading `ch[cn].mirror`, the target's own current mirror - always this process's `mirror_id` under the single-process-per-area-mirror stance, same as the `ClanSpawnExit`/`MineGateway` sites), falling back to the "Nothing happens" message only on failure. Updated 1 existing `ugaris-core` unit test to assert the queued transfer instead of the eager message; added 2 new `ugaris-server` tests for `apply_jail_cross_area_transfers` (empty-queue no-op; missing-repository-pair fallback, mirroring `attempt_cross_area_transfer`'s own `tests/cross_area.rs` coverage). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1058 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Only the dungeon-master eviction rescue (`world/dungeon_master.rs`) remains among the originally-listed `area_id != config.area_id` call sites.
 - Iteration 225 wired the last remaining `attempt_cross_area_transfer` call site the iteration-224 note flagged as missing: the dungeon-master eviction rescue (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/dungeon_master.rs::build_remove_tile`, C `build_remove` in `src/area/13/dungeon.c:743-786`'s tail, `change_area(cn, ch[cn].resta, ch[cn].restx, ch[cn].resty)` falling back to `exit_char(cn)`). A new `DungeonEvictionTransfer { character_id, target_area, target_x, target_y }` plus `World::pending_dungeon_eviction_transfers`/`drain_pending_dungeon_eviction_transfers` mirrors `JailCrossAreaTransfer`'s shape exactly; a differing-area rest point now queues a transfer instead of calling `remove_character` immediately (the same-area branch is unchanged - still an immediate `teleport_character_same_area`, falling back to `remove_character` only if that single teleport itself fails). A new `world_events.rs::apply_dungeon_eviction_transfers` (called from `main.rs` right after `apply_jail_cross_area_transfers`) drains the queue once per tick and calls the shared helper with `target_mirror = u32::from(config.mirror_id)` - but since this call site has no direct command caller to notify (C's own fallback here is a silent `exit_char(cn)`, unlike every prior site's "down" message), a failed hand-off calls `World::remove_character` directly instead of queuing player-facing text. Updated 1 existing `ugaris-core` test to assert the queued transfer instead of immediate removal; added 2 new `ugaris-server` tests for `apply_dungeon_eviction_transfers` (empty-queue no-op; missing-repository-pair falls back to `remove_character`, exercised via a real `build_remove_tile` call rather than reaching into the private queue). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1060 server [+2], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Every originally-listed `area_id != config.area_id` call site is now wired; the "Cross-area transfer" task stays `[~]` since a periodic `mark_alive` heartbeat and the three `server_chat`-blocked commands (`/exterminate`/`/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow`) remain open.
 - Iteration 226 closed the last non-blocked gap in the "Cross-area transfer" task: the **periodic `mark_alive` heartbeat**. C's `area_alive(0)` (`src/system/database/database_area.c:31-75`) re-runs every 85 seconds from `maintenance_60s_task` (`add_scheduled_task(maintenance_60s_task, 85, "Maintenance", true)`, `server.c:600`, where C's `TICKS` = 24 matches this codebase's own `TICKS_PER_SECOND`), not just once at startup (`server.c:586`). Extracted the previously startup-only inline `mark_alive` block in `main.rs` into a new shared `mark_area_alive` helper (unchanged IPv4-only address-encoding logic and `mark_alive` call), invoked once at startup exactly as before plus from a new `world.tick.0 % (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 85) == 0` gate inserted into the tick loop right before the existing once-a-minute auction-cleanup gate, matching that gate's and the clan-registry/military-storage save gates' established style (`world.tick.0` starts at `0` and `World::advance()` bumps it to `1` before any of these gates run each iteration, so the new gate never double-fires alongside the pre-loop startup call). No new tests: like every other periodic in-loop DB-maintenance gate already in `main.rs`, the tick-loop body has no extracted/testable function of its own, and `mark_area_alive` takes a concrete `&PgAreaRepository` rather than the `AreaRepository` trait `attempt_cross_area_transfer`'s tests mock against, so it can only be exercised against a live Postgres pool - same untested gap as the rest of the save+redirect path (tracked separately, unchanged). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2108 ugaris-core + 73 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1060 server, all green, zero failures, unchanged counts), `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready", "loaded area zone map", "entering Rust game loop", no panics (ran with no `DATABASE_URL`, so `area_repository` is `None` and the new gate is a no-op in that run, same as the pre-existing startup call). Only the three `server_chat`-blocked commands (`/exterminate`/`/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow`) and the live-Postgres test gap for the save+redirect path remain open on this task.
- Iteration 227 closed the `/exterminate` third of the "Cross-area transfer" task's blocked-commands gap - see that `PORTING_TODO.md` entry's iteration 227 Progress Log for the full detail (re-reading `cmd_exterminate`/`db_exterminate` found it is a direct DB mutation with no real `server_chat` dependency at all, unlike `/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow` which genuinely are cross-area broadcasts and remain open). New `migrations/0019_ip_bans.sql` (`ip_bans` table, this codebase's `iplog`/`ipban` stand-in populated from the existing `login_sessions.ip_address` history); new `CharacterRepository::exterminate_account` (`crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs`) locks the target's owning account and bans every distinct IP it has logged in from for 28 days; new `is_ip_banned` helper wires a genuine per-connecting-IP check into `begin_login_tx` (`||`-ed alongside the pre-existing static `accounts.ip_locked` flag, which nothing had ever set - this closes a real, related gap in `LoginOutcome::IpLocked`'s own C fidelity, since `isbanned_iplog` is keyed on the connecting IP independent of account, not a per-account static flag). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/exterminate.rs` (`ExterminateRequest`, `World::queue_exterminate_command`/`drain_pending_exterminate_requests` - no synchronous validation before queuing, matching C's own lack of one) and `world_events.rs::apply_exterminate_events` (C's exact "Locked %d accounts and %d IP addresses."/"Player '%s' not found." reply text). Dispatch wired in `commands_admin.rs` right after `/unpunish`, `CF_STAFF|CF_GOD`-gated full-word-only matching C's `cmdcmp(ptr, "exterminate", 11)`. Verified against a real throwaway `postgres:16-alpine` Docker container with every migration `0001`-`0019` applied fresh: new live tests cover `exterminate_account` (account lock, distinct-IP dedup across repeat logins, not-found path), `is_ip_banned` (unexpired vs. expired ban rows), and a full `begin_login_tx` rejection from a banned IP with the old `ip_locked` flag left `false` (proving the new gate doesn't just piggyback on the old one) - confirmed the one pre-existing `character::tests::live_login::exempts_account_id_one_from_duplicate_login_check` failure already reproduces identically on the unmodified tree (a `accounts_id_seq`/committed-row ordering flake unrelated to this change, left untouched). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2112 ugaris-core [+4] + 77 db [+4] + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1067 server [+7], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`cargo build --workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Remaining on the "Cross-area transfer" task: `/values`+`/showvalues`/`/allow` (genuine cross-area broadcasts, need their own local-process-only pragmatic slice - see the todo entry's note) and the still-untested live-Postgres save+redirect path.

- Iteration 233 picked up the "Player-side fight-driver auto-combat" P3 task and ported a self-contained, tested slice: generalized `src/system/drvlib.c`'s `fight_driver_attack_enemy` 10 positional `no*` suppression arguments (`nomove`/`nobless`/`noheal`/`noflash`/`nofireball`/`noball`/`noshield`/`nowarcry`/`nofreeze`/`nopulse`) into a new `FightDriverSuppressions` struct (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc_fight.rs`), threaded through `simple_baddy_fight_tasks`/`simple_baddy_distance3_task_value`/`simple_baddy_distance7_task_value` (each `no*` flag now gates exactly the task(s) C guards with it - freeze/heal/shield/bless/fireball+firering/ball/flash/warcry/pulse, plus the `nofreeze`/`noflash` sub-components inside the combined `distance3` value and the `nofireball` gate on `distance7`) and a new `setup_weighted_fight_task` (the NPC-only `setup_simple_baddy_weighted_fight_task` now just calls it with all-`false` suppressions). `simple_baddy_fight_tasks`'s old bare `nomove: bool` parameter became `impl Into<FightDriverSuppressions>` (`From<bool>` maps a bare bool to `{ nomove, ..default() }`) so every pre-existing call site - the live NPC driver plus ~10 existing tests - keeps compiling and behaving identically unchanged. 10 new tests (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/npc_fight.rs`), one per `no*` flag, each confirming the flagged task is present by default and absent when suppressed, plus a `bool`-vs-struct equivalence test for the `Into` conversion. Read `src/system/drvlib.c:1682-2320`, `src/system/player_driver.c`, and `src/module/lostcon.c` in full before starting. REMAINING (this task stays `[~]`): this iteration only generalized the *task-scoring* half of the engine; zero live callers pass anything but all-`false` suppressions yet (lostcon/player wiring is unstarted). While implementing, found the engine's *enemy-tracking* half (C's separate `set_data(cn, DRD_FIGHTDRIVER, ...)` slot, independent of `ch[cn].driver`) is ported as `SimpleBaddyDriverData.enemies` - tightly coupled to `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy`, which neither a `CDR_LOSTCON` character (`CharacterDriverState::Lostcon`) nor a normal playing character has. Before the lostcon tick or player no*/auto* toggles can call this engine, that enemy-tracking state (`enemies`/`stopdist`/`home_x`/`home_y`/`lastfight`/`last_hit`) needs its own storage independent of the character's current driver kind (e.g. a `fight_driver_data: Option<FightDriverData>` field directly on `Character`, mirroring C's `set_data`/`DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` independence), with `refresh_simple_baddy_enemy_tracking`/`sort_simple_baddy_enemies_like_c`/`remove_simple_baddy_enemy`/etc. (`npc_messages.rs`) migrated to read/write it - see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s updated entry for the full next-step plan (storage migration, then `lostcon.rs` tick wiring, then the `player_driver.c:1067-1070` autobless/autopulse consumers). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2161 ugaris-core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic (NPC simple-baddy combat still functioning correctly through 200+ ticks).

- Iteration 234 did the storage-migration slice iteration 233's note flagged as the required next step, unblocking the "Player-side fight-driver auto-combat" task's enemy-tracking half from `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy`. Added C `struct fight_driver_data` (`src/common/fight.h:27-37`, the independent `set_data(cn, DRD_FIGHTDRIVER, ...)` slot) as a new `FightDriverData` type (`crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`: `enemies`/`start_dist`/`stop_dist`/`char_dist`/`home_x`/`home_y`/`last_hit`) living on a new `Character::fight_driver: Option<FightDriverData>` field (`entity.rs`), mirroring the existing `Character::dungeonfighter` precedent for "one character, two independent driver-data blobs". Migrated every real read/write site off `SimpleBaddyDriverData`'s own copy of these fields onto `Character::fight_driver`: `add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`/`remove_simple_baddy_enemy` (`character_driver.rs`); `simple_baddy_recorded_enemy_ids`/`apply_simple_baddy_enemy_tracking`/`refresh_simple_baddy_enemy_tracking`/`sort_simple_baddy_enemies_like_c`/`simple_baddy_enemy_past_stop_dist`/`set_simple_baddy_home`/`simple_baddy_enemy_within_start_limits`/`simple_baddy_target_home_dist` and the `NoteHit` handler's `last_hit` write (`world/npc_messages.rs`); the direct `data.home_x`/`data.home_y` writes in the day/night-post arrival branch, now routed through `set_simple_baddy_home` (`world/npc_idle.rs`); and `simple_baddy_regenerate_task_value`'s `last_hit` read (`world/npc_fight.rs`). `SimpleBaddyDriverData`'s own `startdist`/`chardist`/`stopdist`/`home_x`/`home_y`/`last_hit` fields are kept as the zone-arg-parsed source copy only (matching C - `simple_baddy_driver_data` genuinely duplicates these too, `simple_baddy.c:66-101`); `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` now also seeds/reseeds `Character::fight_driver`'s three distances from them, mirroring C's `fight_driver_set_dist(cn, dat->startdist, dat->chardist, dat->stopdist)` call (`simple_baddy.c:189`) without clobbering already-tracked enemies/home/last-hit (`fight_driver_set_dist` itself only ever writes the three distances). `lastfight` (simple_baddy's own scavenger/sound-cue throttle - a genuinely different C field from `fight_driver_data`'s `lasthit`) deliberately stays on `SimpleBaddyDriverData`, since it is simple_baddy-driver-specific, not part of C's real `fight_driver_data`. Added a `World::add_character` backfill: a character entering the world with `SimpleBaddy` driver state but `fight_driver: None` (a hand-built test fixture, or a pre-migration DB save deserializing with the new field defaulted) gets `fight_driver` derived from its embedded `SimpleBaddyDriverData` copy once, the same derivation `fight_driver_set_dist` does - this alone fixed ~40 of the ~60 test call sites broken by the migration (every one that constructs `driver_state` directly then calls `world.add_character`/`spawn_character`, without needing individual edits); the remaining ~20 test bodies (`character_driver.rs`'s bare-`Character` unit tests with no `World`, plus assertions in `world/tests/{npc_fight,npc_idle,npc_messages}.rs` that read the old storage location) were updated in place to read `Character::fight_driver` instead, including two (`simple_baddy_attack_action_uses_explicit_fight_driver_home_for_stop_distance`, `simple_baddy_attack_keeps_hidden_enemy_beyond_stopdist_like_c`) that had been passing *by coincidence* pre-migration, since the stale never-updated `driver_state.enemies`/`.home_x` copy happened to still match a "keeps unchanged" expectation. 5 new tests added: `fight_driver` seeding/reseeding-without-clobbering in `character_driver.rs`, and the `add_character` backfill plus its "already populated" and "non-SimpleBaddy leaves it None" edge cases in `world/tests/spawn.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2165 ugaris-core [+4] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic and hundreds of `simple_baddy_outcomes`/`simple_baddy_noncombat` NPC ticks per game tick across 200+ ticks (pure storage relocation - no behavior change expected or observed). This task stays `[~]`: `Character::fight_driver` is still only ever populated for `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` NPCs; `lostcon.rs`'s tick and the normal-player-tick `autobless`/`autopulse` consumers remain entirely unwired to the engine. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s updated entry for the full next-step plan (wire `lostcon.rs`'s tick to the now storage-independent `fight_driver_attack_visible`/`fight_driver_follow_invisible`-equivalent logic first, then `lostcon_driver`'s own low-hp/low-mana/low-shield pre-cascade, then finally the `player_driver.c:1067-1070` autobless/autopulse hooks).

- Iteration 235 did the next step iteration 234's note flagged: wired `lostcon_driver`'s message loop + `fight_driver_attack_visible`/`fight_driver_follow_invisible` cascade (`src/module/lostcon.c:117-203`) for every currently-lingering `CDR_LOSTCON` character. First had to relax the `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy`-only gates left on `Character::fight_driver`'s low-level accessors from the iteration-234 storage migration, since C's `DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` slot is genuinely driver-independent: `add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`/`remove_simple_baddy_enemy` (`character_driver.rs`) and `refresh_simple_baddy_enemy_tracking`/`simple_baddy_recorded_enemy_ids`/`apply_simple_baddy_enemy_tracking` (`world/npc_messages.rs`) no longer require `SimpleBaddy` `driver_state` at all, only `fight_driver` data to exist. New `world/lostcon.rs::process_lostcon_messages` ports the message loop itself - only `NT_GOTHIT` does anything (`fight_driver_note_hit` plus, when `msg->dat1` names an attacker, `fight_driver_add_enemy(cn, co, 1, 1)`); `NT_CHAR`'s aggro-on-sight and `NT_TEXT` are both no-ops in the real (non-commented) C, so neither is ported. The attack cascade is a new `world/npc_fight.rs::fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow` private helper extracted from `process_simple_baddy_attack_action_with_random`'s old body (now threading `FightDriverSuppressions` through `setup_weighted_fight_task` instead of always-default, and gating the invisible-follow half on `!suppressions.nomove`, matching C's `if (!ppd->nomove && fight_driver_follow_invisible(cn))`); the NPC-side caller is behaviorally unchanged (still always all-`false` suppressions), and a new `pub fn process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random(character_id, area_id, suppressions, random)` is the player-side sibling. New `PlayerRuntime::fight_driver_suppressions()` (`player.rs`) maps the 9 positional `no*` toggles `fight_driver_attack_visible` actually passes to a player-side caller (`nolife`/`nomana`/`nocombo` are consumed by `lostcon_driver`'s own potion-drinking block, not this call, and have no `FightDriverSuppressions` field). `FightDriverSuppressions` and `npc_fight`'s attack-action `pub fn`s are now crate-boundary-public (`world/mod.rs`'s `npc_fight` re-export changed from `pub(crate)` to `pub`) so `ugaris-server` can construct/pass them across the crate boundary. Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing simple-baddy attack-actions call: iterates `runtime.lostcon_players` (every currently-lingering character, stashed on disconnect since the earlier `CDR_LOSTCON` linger work), calls `process_lostcon_messages` then `process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random` with that character's own stashed-`PlayerRuntime` suppressions. 19 new tests: 2 relaxed-gate regression tests plus a renamed/repurposed pre-existing one in `character_driver.rs` (its assertion value didn't change - only the reason the assertion holds did), 4 `process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random` behavior tests (`world/tests/npc_fight.rs`: ignores a non-lostcon character, fights back a visible enemy, `nomove` suppresses both the attack task and the invisible-follow fallback, follows an invisible enemy toward its last known position), 4 `process_lostcon_messages` tests (`world/tests/lostcon.rs`: notes-hit-and-adds-enemy, notes-hit-without-an-attacker-id, ignores `NT_CHAR`/`NT_TEXT`, no-op for a normal playing character), and 1 `fight_driver_suppressions` toggle-mapping test (`player.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2175 ugaris-core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic (no live players connected during this smoke test, so `runtime.lostcon_players` was empty and the new loop was a no-op in practice - the wiring itself is exercised only by the new unit tests). This task stays `[~]`: `lostcon_driver`'s low-hp-heal/low-mana-potion/low-shield-magicshield pre-cascade, its `do_idle` tail (a lingering character with nothing to attack currently takes no action at all this tick instead of idling), its post-cascade bless/heal/magicshield fallback, and the normal-player-tick `autobless`/`autopulse` consumers (`player_driver.c:1067-1070`) all remain unwired. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s updated entry for the full next-step plan.

- Iteration 236 ported the rest of `lostcon_driver`'s own body (`src/module/lostcon.c:87-220`), the next step iteration 235's note flagged, all in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lostcon.rs`. New `World::lostcon_early_exit_characters(area_id)` (mirroring `expired_lostcon_characters`'s shape) covers the early-exit gauntlet: rest-area tile, arena tile (`areaID != 34`), and karma (`<= -12`, or `<= -5` when not `CF_PAID`). C's second arena check ("leave after 10s if lagging", `lostcon.c:96-99`, guarding a `kick_player` call behind `if (ch[cn].player)`) is deliberately not ported: `kick_player` (`player.c:187`) unconditionally zeroes `ch[cn].player` in the same statement that sets `ch[cn].driver = CDR_LOSTCON`, so every character this function ever runs for already has `ch[cn].player == 0`, and the immediate arena check directly above it already unconditionally fires first for every arena tile outside area 34 - making the 10s-lag branch and its `kick_player` guard permanently unreachable dead code in the C oracle itself (documented inline with the reasoning, not silently dropped). New `ugaris-server/src/lostcon.rs::take_lostcon_early_exit_characters` mirrors `take_expired_lostcon_characters`'s stashed-runtime/account-depot contract; `main.rs`'s existing expiry-handling block now merges both lists into the same save+despawn loop. The low-hp-heal/low-mana-potion/low-magicshield pre-cascade (`lostcon.c:164-197`) is `World::process_lostcon_self_care_precascade`, and the bless/magicshield/heal fallback (`lostcon.c:207-218`) is `World::process_lostcon_self_care_postcascade`; both take a new `LostconSelfCareSuppressions` (`noheal`/`noshield`/`nobless`/`nolife`/`nomana`/`nocombo` - the six `lostcon_ppd` toggles `lostcon_driver`'s own body reads directly, as opposed to `FightDriverSuppressions`'s ten toggles consumed by `fight_driver_attack_enemy`), built by a new `PlayerRuntime::lostcon_self_care_suppressions()` mirroring the existing `fight_driver_suppressions()`. The precascade's mana-potion search is a new `find_lostcon_mana_potion` private helper reproducing C's exact gating verbatim: the outer loop-gate is `!nolife || !nocombo` (not `!nomana` - matches `lostcon.c:175` letter-for-letter, likely a legacy naming quirk in the C oracle itself, ported faithfully rather than "corrected"), then per-candidate a combo potion (`drdata[1]` and `drdata[2]` both set) is gated by `nocombo` and a pure-mana potion (`drdata[2]` only) by `nomana`. C's `return`-on-success semantics for the heal/magicshield pre-cascade sub-checks (as opposed to the potion drink, which never returns early, only `break`s its own search loop) needed no explicit short-circuit plumbing in `ugaris-server`'s caller: `do_heal`/`do_magicshield` already set `character.action != 0` on success, and every downstream cascade step in this codebase already self-gates on `action != 0` (`process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random` included), so calling precascade/attack-cascade/postcascade unconditionally in sequence each tick reproduces C's early-return control flow for free. `World::queue_lostcon_idle` ports the `do_idle(cn, TICKS)` tail. `main.rs`'s tick loop now runs the full sequence per lingering character each tick: messages, precascade, attack cascade (unchanged), postcascade only if the attack cascade didn't act, idle only if nothing above acted. 20 new tests (6 early-exit gauntlet, 8 precascade, 4 postcascade, 2 idle) in `world/tests/lostcon.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2195 ugaris-core [+20] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic (no live players connected during this smoke test, so `runtime.lostcon_players` was empty and the new wiring was a no-op in practice, exercised only by the new unit tests). This task stays `[~]`: `lostcon_driver` itself (the lingering-only autopilot this task set out to port) is now fully ported, but the normal-player-tick `autobless`/`autopulse` consumers (`player_driver.c:1067-1070`) and the normal-(connected)-player-tick autopotion/autorecall consumers (`tool.c`'s `player_use_potion`/`player_use_recall` - separate C functions from anything in `lostcon.c`, called from the connected-player tick, not the lostcon linger tick) remain entirely unwired. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s updated entry for the full next-step plan.

- Iteration 237 ported `player_driver.c:1067-1070`'s autobless/autopulse consumer as new `World::process_player_autobless_autopulse` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/player_driver.rs`, a new file - the first slice of C's actual `player_driver` function itself, as opposed to `lostcon_driver`/the fight-driver engine it wraps around). Confirmed via `do.c` that neither `do_bless` nor `do_pulse` gate on `ch[cn].action`, and via `act.c:2223-2242` that `char_driver`/`player_driver` is only ever invoked once `ch[n].step >= ch[n].duration` and `ch[n].action` has already been reset to `0` for that tick - so this codebase's existing convention of skipping busy characters (`setup_world_actions`'s `character.action != 0` continue-guard, `ugaris-server/src/main.rs`) is the correct Rust-side equivalent of "only runs when the previous action just finished", not an invented gate. `fight_driver_pulse_value` is the already-ported `simple_baddy_pulse_value` (`npc_fight.rs`) - confirmed not NPC-specific despite the name by reading its body (no driver-kind check anywhere). C's explicit `may_add_spell(cn, IDR_BLESS)` pre-check is redundant with `do_bless`'s own internal `may_add_spell` gate, so it is not duplicated, matching the `process_lostcon_self_care_postcascade` precedent for the same spell. Wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop immediately before `runtime.setup_world_actions` (matching C's real ordering: the autobless/autopulse `return` pre-empts the queued-action `switch` that follows it in `player_driver`), iterating `runtime.players.values()` (every connected, non-lostcon session) with the same busy-character skip `setup_world_actions` already uses. 9 new tests (`world/tests/player_driver.rs`): bless fires when enabled/affordable/known, no-ops when the toggle is off/the spell is unknown/mana is too low, pulse fires when enabled and a target makes it worthwhile (scenario lifted from the existing NPC-side `simple_baddy_fight_tasks_honor_legacy_nopulse_gate` pulse test, confirming the shared engine really is driver-kind-agnostic), no-ops when the toggle is off or no target justifies it, bless takes priority over pulse when both fire the same tick (matches C's sequential `if...return` chain), and a missing-character guard. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2204 ugaris-core [+9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic (no live players connected during this smoke test, so the new per-tick loop was a no-op in practice, exercised only by the new unit tests). This task stays `[~]`: while reading `death.c` to plan the second half, found that `player_use_potion`/`player_use_recall` (`tool.c:3986-4033`) are *not* actually normal-connected-player-tick consumers as the prior iteration's note assumed - they are called from `death.c:1211-1216`'s hurt/damage-application path, gated on `ch[cn].flags & CF_PLAYER && ch[cn].driver == CDR_LOSTCON` (i.e. only for a lingering character that just took damage, not every tick and not for a normally-connected player at all) - so this is still part of the *lostcon* self-defense half of this task, triggered from the hurt path rather than `lostcon_driver`'s own per-tick body. See `PORTING_TODO.md`'s updated entry for the exact next-step plan (port both as new `world/lostcon.rs` methods, then wire them into `world/hurt.rs::apply_legacy_hurt` or its `ugaris-server` caller for every `CDR_LOSTCON` character taking damage, matching `death.c:1211-1216`'s exact placement).

- Iteration 238 closed the "Player-side fight-driver auto-combat" P3 task entirely, porting the last two unwired members of the `no*`/`auto*` toggle family: `tool.c::player_use_potion`/`player_use_recall` (`src/system/tool.c:3986-4035`), called from `death.c:1211-1217` right after a `CF_PLAYER`+`CDR_LOSTCON` character takes hp damage from any source (combat, spells, weather, traps, ...). Ported both as new `World::process_player_use_potion`/`process_player_use_recall` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lostcon.rs`): the potion search is a new `find_lostcon_life_potion` helper - a mirror image of the existing `find_lostcon_mana_potion` with the `drdata[1]`/`drdata[2]` (life/mana component) roles swapped, matching C's two near-identical but distinct search loops letter-for-letter; the recall search reuses the existing `IDR_RECALL`/`ItemDriverOutcome::Recall` dispatch path (`IDR_RECALL` newly re-exported through `world/mod.rs` for this call site), gated on C's exact `drdata[0] >= ch[cn].level` level check and the "don't use recall after you died" `hp < POWERSCALE/2` early bail. Resolved the architecture question iteration 237's note left open: `apply_legacy_hurt` (`world/hurt.rs`) cannot build the C `no*` toggle suppressions inline, since `World` has no access to the session-owned `PlayerRuntime` that holds them (unlike every other `pending_*` queue in `World`, which is drained and fully handled inside `ugaris-core` itself). So `apply_legacy_hurt` now only queues a new `pending_lostcon_hurt_events: Vec<CharacterId>` (pushed whenever a `CF_PLAYER`+`CDR_LOSTCON` target takes nonzero hp damage, mirroring C's own gate letter-for-letter) via a new `World::drain_lostcon_hurt_events`, and `ugaris-server`'s tick loop (`main.rs`, right before the existing per-lostcon-character message/precascade/attack/postcascade/idle block) drains it and calls the two new `World` methods with suppressions built from each character's stashed `lostcon_players` `PlayerRuntime` (reusing `lostcon_self_care_suppressions()`'s `nolife`/`nocombo` plus the previously-unused `no_recall` field directly). Disclosed, deliberate ordering deviation from C (documented on `pending_lostcon_hurt_events`'s doc comment in `world/mod.rs` and at each call site): C calls both functions *inline*, between the sound-effect check and the death-threshold check of the exact same `hurt()` invocation, so a potion/recall can rescue a lingering character on the very hit that would otherwise kill it; this port instead reacts once per tick after that tick's damage/death outcome is already finalized, so it cannot resurrect a character mid-hit, only keep an already-still-alive lingering character topped up before the tick ends - an accepted architectural gap given `World`'s hard separation from session-owned `PlayerRuntime` state, consistent with how every other `no*`/`auto*` consumer in this task was already wired. 13 new tests (4 in `world/tests/hurt.rs` covering the new pending-queue's push/drain/gating; 9 in `world/tests/lostcon.rs` covering both new methods' hp thresholds, `nolife`/`nocombo`/`norecall` gating, the death-threshold recall bail, and the level-requirement search filter). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2217 ugaris-core [+13] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. Every `no*`/`auto*` toggle in the 16-member family now has a real gameplay consumer; `PORTING_TODO.md` marked `[x]`.

- Iteration 239 picked up the "Macro-detection engine" P3 task (`macro_driver`, `src/module/base.c:236-1263`) and ported its self-contained pure-logic slice, following the exact "PlayerRuntime-decoupled brain first" precedent `world/military.rs`'s doc comment already established for its own PPD-heavy driver: read `base.c:236-1263` in full (the `MACRO_*` constants/state machine, `struct macro_data`/`macro_ppd`, the word/choice-question tables, `macro_is_player_active`, `macro_generate_challenge`/`macro_ask_challenge`/`macro_check_answer`, `macro_give_reward`, `macro_handle_failure`, `macro_driver` itself, and `macro_record_history`) before writing any Rust. Since `crate::player::MacroPpd` (the persistent state this driver reads/writes) lives on `PlayerRuntime`, not `Character`/`World`, and `World` cannot reach `PlayerRuntime` (confirmed by re-reading `world/military.rs`'s and `world/bank.rs`'s own doc comments for the same constraint), a full live port needs an `apply_macro_events`-shaped bridge in `ugaris-server` that doesn't exist yet - so this iteration ported everything that *doesn't* need `World` or a live NPC at all as a new standalone top-level module, `crates/ugaris-core/src/macro_daemon.rs` (registered in `lib.rs` alongside the similar pure-logic `quest.rs`/`clan.rs` modules), operating directly on a real `&mut MacroPpd`: `macro_is_player_active` (5-minute activity/AFK gate), `macro_is_area_excluded` (the area-3/section-114/area-22-rectangle victim-search exclusions, using the pre-existing `area_section::section_at`), `macro_generate_challenge`/`macro_ask_challenge_lines`/`macro_check_answer` (all 3 reachable challenge types - math/word/reverse - plus the C-dead-but-preserved multiple-choice branch, byte-for-byte range/wording parity, colors dropped matching this codebase's established plain-text `npc_say` convention), `macro_record_history` (circular history buffer), `macro_apply_correct_answer`/`macro_apply_failure` (the real karma/suspicion/`challenge_failures`/`nextcheck` mutations on a real `MacroPpd`, including the exact `(int)(karma*0.9)+10` truncation, the upper-only karma clamp, and the 1st/2nd/3rd+ failure escalation with its suspicion +20-then-(3rd+)-30 double adjustment), and `macro_roll_reward`/`macro_reward_item_template`/`macro_reward_success_message`/`macro_reward_fallback`/`macro_xmas_reward_message` (the reward-type roll classified into an exhaustive `MacroReward` enum, since actually creating/giving an item needs a `ZoneLoader` neither `World` nor this module has - mirrors `ugaris-server/src/area_apply.rs::grant_template_item_smart`'s existing create-item-give-or-destroy shape as the precedent for the future execution step).
