Made `world::legacy_random_below_from_seed` `pub(crate)` (previously `world`-module-private) so this new top-level module can reproduce the exact same `RANDOM(n)` LCG sequence instead of duplicating it, documented with a doc comment explaining why the visibility widened. 22 new tests (`macro_daemon.rs`) covering the activity gate's exact 300-second boundary and its "default `MacroPpd` never looks active" safety property, all three area exclusions, all three difficulty tiers' exact random ranges (plus the failure-bumps-difficulty-by-one interaction), math/word/reverse/choice answer-checking (including quote/whitespace stripping and case-insensitivity), the say-text wording per challenge type, circular history-buffer wraparound and lifetime pass/fail tallying, karma-clamp-with-no-lower-bound and suspicion-floor-at-0 on a correct answer, all three failure tiers' exact reschedule windows and the suspicion +20/-30 double-clamp interaction, every reward-roll threshold/level-gate/karma-gate combination, the karma>50 50%-exp-bonus, and every reward's exact template/message/fallback text. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2239 ugaris-core [+22] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic (this slice adds no NPC-visible behavior yet - `CDR_MACRO` still resolves to its pre-existing `HandledStub`, so no live gameplay changed). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with a detailed REMAINING note listing the six items a future iteration needs before this NPC does anything live: the `CharacterDriverState::Macro`/`struct macro_data` state machine and `World::process_macro_actions` driver loop (follow `world/dungeon_master.rs`'s shape); the `ugaris-server`-side `apply_macro_events` bridge to a real `PlayerRuntime::macro_ppd`; the cross-server challenge-room `attempt_cross_area_transfer` hand-off; resolving `MacroReward` into a real `grant_template_item_smart` call; wiring `macro_track_exp_gain`/`_combat`/`_gold_change` at their real (`PlayerRuntime`-requiring) call sites; and the pentagram-save/isxmas-reskin cosmetic gaps.

- Iteration 240 closed sub-item (5) of iteration 239's remaining list for the "Macro-detection engine" P3 task: wired `macro_track_exp_gain`/`macro_track_combat`/`macro_track_gold_change` (`src/system/tool.c:385-426`, `death.c:1112-1117`) at their real call sites, so `macro_is_player_active` (from iteration 239) finally sees live, non-default values for any online player. Confirmed via a fresh `Task`-agent research pass that no `pending_exp_gain_events`/`pending_combat_events`/`pending_gold_change_events` bridge existed anywhere and that all three C tracking calls were undocumented or explicitly-disclosed gaps at their Rust call sites (`World::give_exp` in `world/exp.rs`, `World::apply_legacy_hurt` in `world/hurt.rs`, `World::gate_give_money_silent` in `world/gatekeeper.rs`, and `ugaris-server`'s `achievement::give_money`). Added three new `World` queues (`world/mod.rs`, mirroring `pending_lostcon_hurt_events`'s exact "`World` can't reach `PlayerRuntime`" architectural gap): `pending_exp_gain_events` (pushed by `give_exp` when C's `addedExp > 0`), `pending_combat_events` (pushed by `apply_legacy_hurt` for both defender and, if present, attacker when C's `dam > 0`, checked against the raw pre-armor damage exactly like `death.c:1112`), and `pending_gold_change_events` (pushed by `gate_give_money_silent` when C's `val != 0`), each with a matching `drain_*_events` method. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/macro_daemon.rs` (mirroring `military.rs`'s `apply_military_master_events` shape) exposes `apply_macro_activity_events(runtime, world, now)` - deliberately not named `apply_macro_events`, keeping that name free for the future, larger `CDR_MACRO` NPC-driver bridge sub-item (2) below still needs - draining all three queues and stamping `MacroPpd::last_exp_gain`/`last_combat`/`last_gold_change` on the matching online player's `PlayerRuntime`; wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing per-tick lostcon-hurt-reaction block (`mod macro_daemon;` + `pub(crate) use macro_daemon::*;` added alongside the other per-tick modules). `achievement::give_money` (C's other gold-granting entry point, `src/system/tool.c:1459-1483`) already runs server-side with direct `PlayerRuntime` access, so it stamps `last_gold_change` inline instead of going through the new queue - no bridge needed there. 9 new tests (2 in `world/tests/exp.rs`, 3 in `world/tests/hurt.rs`, 2 in `world/tests/gatekeeper.rs`) covering the positive/zero-or-negative gating for all three queues plus the unknown-character no-op case. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2246 ugaris-core [+29 net across this and prior iterations' reshuffling; this slice added 9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1073 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic and hundreds of NPC ticks per game tick. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: sub-items (1)-(4) and (6) from iteration 239's note (the NPC-side state machine/message loop, the real `apply_macro_events` bridge for the *NPC driver itself* as opposed to this activity-tracking slice's `apply_macro_activity_events`, the cross-server challenge-room hand-off, reward item grants, and the pentagram/xmas cosmetic gaps) remain.
- Iteration 241 closed sub-item (1) of iteration 239's remaining list for the "Macro-detection engine" P3 task (the `CharacterDriverState::Macro`/`struct macro_data` NPC-side state machine and the live driver loop) and folded in most of sub-items (3)/(4)/(6) as well: added `MacroDriverState`/`MacroDriverData` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`, plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Macro` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) mirroring C's `struct macro_data` field-for-field except `victim`/`v_ID` (collapsed to a single `CharacterId`, this codebase's stable non-recycled identity) and the six loose challenge fields (folded into the already-ported `crate::macro_daemon::MacroChallenge`, now also `Default`/`Serialize`/`Deserialize` for persistence). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/macro_npc.rs` (registered in `world/mod.rs`, tests in `world/tests/macro_npc.rs`, 6 tests) ports every *non*-`PlayerRuntime` piece of `macro_driver`: `macro_update_appearance` (the `isxmas`/"Macro Daemon" name/description/sprite reskin, `base.c:912-921`), `macro_daemon_ids` (live `CDR_MACRO` NPC scan), `macro_search_candidates` (the `MACRO_STATE_IDLE` non-PPD candidate filter - level/invisible/staff/god/area-exclusion, `base.c:963-993`), `macro_handle_give_message` (`NT_GIVE`), `macro_idle_mutter` (the 1/200 idle-murmur roll, `base.c:1216-1231`), and a family of `macro_*_seeded`/`macro_roll_*` wrappers that thread `World::legacy_random_seed` through the already-ported pure "brain" (`macro_generate_challenge`/`macro_apply_correct_answer`/`macro_apply_failure`) and the two RNG rolls `ugaris-server` itself needs (the `RANDOM(100)` reward-type roll, the gold-amount roll) - `ugaris-server` never calls `legacy_random_below_from_seed` directly (it stays `pub(crate)` to `ugaris-core`), matching every other RNG-consuming driver in this codebase. Widened `World::apply_tabunga_text_notification` from `pub(crate)` to `pub` (the only visibility change needed) so the new cross-crate driver can reuse the existing GM `tabunga` stat-dump hook instead of re-porting it. Since nearly every `MACRO_STATE_*` transition needs a player's `MacroPpd` (immunity/next-check/activity gating the search itself, karma/suspicion shaping the greeting/challenge difficulty and reward eligibility, and the correct-answer/failure mutations), the live driver is wired directly in `ugaris-server` (`apply_macro_events`, extending `crates/ugaris-server/src/macro_daemon.rs` alongside iteration 240's `apply_macro_activity_events`) rather than the usual `World`-decides/`ugaris-server`-applies queued-event split - `tick_macro_daemon` ticks every live `CDR_MACRO` NPC once per game tick: appearance reskin, the message loop (`NT_TEXT` answer-checking via `macro_process_messages`/`macro_resolve_correct_answer`, `NT_GIVE`), then `macro_run_state_machine`'s full `IDLE`->`FOUND`->`TELEPORTED`->`CHALLENGING`->`TIMEOUT` cascade using sequential `if`s (not `else if`s) matching C's exact same-tick-cascade control flow (e.g. a just-answered victim immediately re-enters the search and can find/challenge a *new* victim before the tick ends). The `/summonmacro` admin command's `force_summon` flag is wired end-to-end for the first time (`base.c:925-938`), closing that command's loop. Reward granting resolves every `MacroReward` variant: `HealingPotion`/`ComboPotion`/`Lollipop` via `grant_template_item_smart` (with the item-grant-fails exp-fallback message), `Gold` via a direct `character.gold` mutation plus `CharacterFlags::ITEMS` (matching C's own bypass of the take/give-money gate), and `Experience`/`Consolation` via `give_exp`. Disclosed, deliberate gaps still open (documented in the new code's own doc comments): the cross-server "challenge room" pickup/banishment/return-teleport (`in_challenge_room`/`needs_challenge`/`teleported_to_jail` never become meaningful yet, so suspicious players get a normal challenge instead of being banished - the substantial remaining piece of sub-item (3)); the pentagram-progress save/restore (sub-item (6), no-op until `pents.c` itself is ported); C's `realtime - ch[co].login_time < 60*5` recent-login grace period (this codebase tracks no player login timestamp anywhere yet); and `macro_teleport_char_driver`'s `MF_SOUNDBLOCK`/`MF_SHOUTBLOCK` destination-tile pre-check (reuses the plain, pre-existing `World::teleport_char_driver` instead - a narrow edge case). 6 new integration tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/macro_daemon.rs` exercising the whole live loop end-to-end: force-summon pickup (teleport + flag-clear + state), same-tick eligible-candidate search-to-challenge, AFK-candidate skip pushing back `nextcheck`, correct-answer reward-plus-karma resolution via a real `NT_TEXT` message, a three-failure timeout kicking the player, and the xmas reskin. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2252 ugaris-core [+6] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1079 server [+6], all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the cross-server challenge-room hand-off (reuse `attempt_cross_area_transfer`, same shape as `world/jail.rs`) is the last substantial gap before this task can close.

- Iteration 244 re-verified the "Sector skip optimization" P3 deferral independently (a fresh grep of every `DirtySectors`/`mark_dirty_sector`/`skip_x_sector` call site confirmed the same "large, cross-cutting, dozens of scattered tile-mutation sites, `map_sync.rs` never consults it" finding iteration 243 already recorded - nothing new to add, so no code change there), then picked up the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task (the topmost unchecked item once P0-P3 were exhausted) and ported its first self-contained NPC slice: `camhermit_driver` (`CDR_CAMHERMIT = 14`, `gwendylon.c:707-996`, the forest hermit's two-quest "Bear Control Hunt"/"Bear Tooth Necklace" chain, `QLOG_HERMIT_QUEST1`/`QLOG_HERMIT_QUEST2`). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/camhermit.rs` (`CamhermitDriverData`/`CDR_CAMHERMIT` added to `character_driver.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 13-state dialogue machine (entry greeting through the two quest chains to `CAMHERMIT_STATE_DONE`, all 10 additional `CAMHERMIT_STATE_*` constants promoted to `pub(crate)` in `quest.rs` alongside the 4 that already existed for `questlog_init_area1`/`reopen_quest_legacy`), `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via a new `GWENDYLON_QA` table (`character_driver.rs` - the small-talk table shared by *every* `gwendylon.c` NPC driver, not just this one, so future area-1 NPC slices can reuse it directly), and `NT_GIVE`'s item give-back. The teeth-quest reward path ports `collect_teeth` (`gwendylon.c:681-699`) verbatim including a real, documented C quirk: a single pass that finds fewer than 10 teeth still destroys every one it found before reporting failure, silently eating any remainder under a multiple of 10. Also added a new general-purpose `World::give_char_item_smart` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/items.rs`, ports `tool.c::give_char_item_smart:3408-3494` - the money/inventory/hand/ground-drop/destroy cascade every future NPC give-back can reuse) and `IID_AREA1_SMALL_BEAR_TEETH`/`DEV_ID_RH` (`item_driver/ids.rs`/`helpers.rs`). Since `area1_ppd`'s `camhermit_state`/`camhermit_seen_timer`/`camhermit_kills` accessors and `Area1QuestState` already existed from the "Questlog initialization" task, only the driver itself and its `quest_log.count(QLOG_HERMIT_QUEST2)` read were new PlayerRuntime-facing surface. Server-side wiring (new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs`, `camhermit_player_facts`/`apply_camhermit_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right before the pre-existing `gate_welcome` block) is the first live NPC-driven `questlog_open`/`_done`/`_reopen` call site in this codebase - `QuestDone` resolves through the existing `QuestLog::complete_legacy`+`World::give_exp` pattern and resends the legacy questlog packet exactly like `apply_military_mission_kill_check` already does for mission progress, and the teeth-reward gold's wealth-achievement tracking reuses `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` (the same split `World::complete_mission`'s mercenary bonus already established). Two documented, deliberate gaps (see `world::camhermit`'s module doc comment): the `CAMHERMIT_STATE_QUEST1DO`/`QUEST2DO` reminder line's `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` styling around the word "repeat" is dropped (the shared `npc_quiet_say`/`WorldAreaText` pipeline only carries a plain UTF-8 `String`, same simplification already documented on `BANK_QA`'s color-styled answers); `ppd->camhermit_kills` never actually advances yet since `monster_dead` (`gwendylon.c:5200-5219`, the generic area-1 monster-death hook that increments it) is a separate, still-unported piece of this same C file - so the `CAMHERMIT_STATE_QUEST1DO` branch can only take its "not enough kills yet" reminder path on a live server today, not the completion path (fully covered by unit tests with an injected fact instead). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 task stays `[~]` with the full remaining-NPC breakdown (18 more character drivers plus `monster_dead`). 9 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/camhermit.rs` (entry greeting, level-gated advance, kill-count completion, 60-second reminder throttle, teeth-reward gold+quest-completion end-to-end including inventory/item-table cleanup, insufficient-teeth reminder-with-state-transition, text-repeat state reset, and give-back). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2270 ugaris-core [+9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panic.

- Iteration 245 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2270 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its second self-contained NPC slice: `yoakin_driver` (`CDR_YOAKIN = 9`, `gwendylon.c:996-1217`, the hunter's bear-hunt quest at the knight castle, `QLOG_YOAKIN` - index 5, which has no `#define` name in C's `questlog.h`, so a new named constant was added here). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/yoakin.rs` (`YoakinDriverData`/`CDR_YOAKIN` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Yoakin` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 6-state (0-5) dialogue machine (entry greeting through the quest-given/quest-do states to done, including the 120-second "reset to state 1 if not seen recently" rule and the `logain_state >= 6` gate on the state 2 -> 3 "mad knights done" transition), `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling reusing the same `GWENDYLON_QA` table/`analyse_text_qa` matcher `world::camhermit` already established (plus yoakin's own extra "stale current-victim" reset the C source has that camhermit's copy doesn't), and `NT_GIVE`'s three branches: the bear-tooth turn-in (quest completion via `QuestLog::complete_legacy`, a first-completion-only 500-silver gold reward gated on a pre-tick `quest_done_count` snapshot standing in for C's `tmp = questlog_done(...)  == 1` post-increment check, and a new general `World::destroy_items_by_template_id` port of C `destroy_item_byID` that deliberately preserves the C quirk of sweeping *every* matching item across equipment/inventory/cursor, not just the one handed over), the shrike-talisman exp reward (fails/no-fails branch, `min(level_value(level)/5, N)` formula), and the generic give-back fallback reusing `give_char_item_smart`. Two new item IDs (`IID_AREA1_BIGBEAR_TOOTH`/`IID_SHRIKE_TALISMAN`, `item_driver/ids.rs`) and `MONEY_AREA1_BEARTOOTH`'s first live consumer (already present in `quest.rs`'s `quest_exp` module, previously unused). Server-side wiring extends the existing `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`yoakin_player_facts`/`apply_yoakin_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing camhermit block) reusing the same `questlog_open`/`_done` + `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` pattern camhermit's iteration established. Two documented, deliberate gaps (see `world::yoakin`'s module doc comment): the state-4 reminder line's `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` styling around "repeat" is dropped (same reason as camhermit's own gap); `destroy_items_by_template_id` does not sweep the account depot (`DRD_DEPOT_PPD`), since that storage lives in `ugaris-server`'s `PlayerRuntime`/DB layer outside `World` - a stray bear tooth parked in the depot at turn-in time won't be swept, unlike on the C server (rare in practice). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 task stays `[~]` with the updated remaining-NPC breakdown (now ~17 more character drivers plus `monster_dead`). 13 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/yoakin.rs` (entry greeting+quest-open, logain-state gate held/passed, the 120-second intro reset, state-4 reminder throttle both ways, bear-tooth turn-in with first-time gold reward, second-completion gold-skip, the stray-tooth inventory-sweep quirk, shrike-talisman exp reward, generic give-back, text-repeat state reset, and current-victim text filtering). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2283 ugaris-core [+13] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 246 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2283 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its third self-contained NPC slice: `terion_driver` (`CDR_TERION = 11`, `gwendylon.c:1228-1472`, the village's ambient lore/storyteller NPC - no quest log, no item reward, pure dialogue). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/terion.rs` (`TerionDriverData`/`CDR_TERION` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Terion` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop, including a genuine driver-specific quirk not shared by camhermit/yoakin: a leading pre-pass over `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DIDSAY` messages (`gwendylon.c:1240-1244`) that bumps `last_talk` to the current tick whenever *another* nearby NPC just finished a line of dialogue (self-broadcast via the same `notify_area(..., NTID_DIDSAY, cn, 0)` every one of these ambient NPCs sends after talking), without consuming/removing the message - so multiple nearby ambient NPCs don't all launch into their own dialogue back-to-back. The `NT_CHAR` branch is a 14-state ambient lore chain (states 0-13) gated on `gwendy_state`/`reskin_state` thresholds (reusing the existing `GWENDYLON_STATE_FIRST_SKULL_DONE`/`_SECOND_SKULL_DONE` `pub(crate)` constants from `quest.rs`, plus two new module-local ones for the two C threshold values that had no existing Rust name yet, `GWENDYLON_STATE_SECOND_SKULL_WAIT`/`_THIRD_SKULL_WAIT`), including two silent state jumps (state 0's "player already solved skull 2" fast-forward to state 4, gated the same way as state 6's `reskin_state`/`gwendy_state` gates) that advance `terion_state` without setting `didsay` - no dialogue, no facing update, no `last_talk` bump, matching C's `break` mid-`switch` exactly. `NT_TEXT` reuses the same `GWENDYLON_QA`/`analyse_text_qa` matcher `world::camhermit`/`world::yoakin` already established, with terion's own 4-bucket "repeat"/"restart" state-reset ranges (`<=4` -> 0, `5-6` -> 4, `7-9` -> 6, `10-14` -> 9); unlike yoakin's own `NT_TEXT` branch, C's `terion_driver` has no `current_victim`/`last_talk` gate before calling `analyse_text_driver` at all (`gwendylon.c:1421-1424` vs. yoakin's `gwendylon.c:1118-1125`) - a genuine asymmetry between the two NPCs confirmed by direct comparison of both C functions, preserved here rather than "fixed" to match yoakin's shape. `NT_GIVE` is the same generic give-back fallback through the existing `give_char_item_smart`. Server-side wiring extends the existing `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`terion_player_facts`/`apply_terion_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing yoakin block) - the simplest of the three area-1 NPC event-application functions so far, since terion has exactly one outcome variant (`UpdateState`) and needs no `ServerRuntime`/DB/achievement plumbing at all. 13 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/terion.rs` (silent-before-threshold entry, greet-and-advance entry, the silent state-0-to-4 jump, the `NTID_TERION` area notification on state 2, the gwendy-state-13 gate on state 6 held/passed, the reskin-state-9 gate on state 9 held/passed, state 13's notification plus permanent no-op past state 14, the cross-NPC `NTID_DIDSAY` throttle both triggering and correctly ignoring a self-originated broadcast, both text-repeat state-reset buckets, and generic give-back) - one test uncovered a real test-fixture gap along the way: `World::spawn_character` (unlike `zone.rs`'s real zone-file loading path) never seeds `rest_x`/`rest_y` from the spawn position, so a bare default-constructed test NPC with no explicit rest position would silently walk itself away from its spawn tile via the driver's own "return to post after 30s idle" tail on any call that doesn't `didsay` - worked around by setting `rest_x`/`rest_y` explicitly in the test's own NPC-fixture helper (a test-only fix, not a production one; `camhermit`'s/`yoakin`'s own single-call-per-test suites never happened to exercise this path). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2296 ugaris-core [+13] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 247 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2296 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its fourth and biggest self-contained NPC slice: `gwendylon_driver` itself (`CDR_GWENDYLON = 8`, `gwendylon.c:234-673`, the main quest-giver mage - new `CDR_GWENDYLON` constant, since it didn't exist yet unlike the three NPCs already sharing this file). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/gwendylon.rs` (`GwendylonDriverData`/`CDR_GWENDYLON` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Gwendylon` variant and its two exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 20-state four-skull quest chain (all four `QLOG_GWENDY_*` quests, reusing the existing `pub(crate)` `GWENDYLON_STATE_*_DONE` constants from `quest.rs` - `GWENDYLON_STATE_FOUL_MAGICIAN_DONE` needed widening from private to `pub(crate)` to be reachable from the new module - plus 15 new module-local state constants for every intermediate dialogue step C only spells out as bare `#define`s), including all three `questlog_isdone`-gated skip-ahead jumps ported as literal jump targets exactly as C writes them (a real, confirmed asymmetry: skipping skull 1 already done jumps to state 10, the *next* tier's own "done" checkpoint, but skipping skulls 2/3 jump to states 13/17, the tier *after that one's* "wait" checkpoint - not a typo to "fix", verified against the C source three times before committing to it) and the `GWENDYLON_STATE_DONE_BLESS` periodic-bless finale. `NT_TEXT` reuses `GWENDYLON_QA`/`analyse_text_qa` with its own 5-bucket "repeat" state-reset ranges. `NT_GIVE` has five branches sharing a new `gwendylon_turn_in_skull` helper for the four skull turn-ins (acceptance line, `QuestDone` event, `destroy_items_by_template_id` sweep of every matching quest item still in the player's own gear, `UpdateState`, held-item destroy, first-completion-only `MONEY_AREA1_SKULL1..4` gold reward gated on a pre-tick `quest_done_count` snapshot - the same pattern yoakin's bear-tooth reward already established) plus `IID_CALIGARLETTER`'s teleport-letter hand-off and the generic give-back fallback. The `GWENDYLON_STATE_DONE_BLESS` branch required modeling a genuine C `return`-before-`remove_message` quirk that none of the three prior area-1 NPCs have: `if (may_add_spell(co, IDR_BLESS) && do_bless(cn, co)) { ppd->gwendy_seen_timer = realtime; return; }` exits the whole driver call *before* the triggering `NT_CHAR` message is ever removed from the queue, so C reprocesses the identical message next tick (by which point the just-started bless cast makes `may_add_spell` fail, falling through to the normal `didsay` path that finally consumes it) - ported by switching the per-NPC message loop from a plain `for` to an index-based `while` so the triggering message (plus any others still unprocessed after it) can be spliced back onto the NPC's own `driver_messages` and the entire turn/idle-move tail skipped for that tick, exactly like C's `return`; `World::setup_bless_spell` (the existing player-facing bless-cast entry point, already wrapping both the `may_add_spell` and `do_bless` checks C calls separately) turned out to be exactly the right single call for this, needing no new spell-system code. `IID_CALIGARLETTER`'s `change_area(co, 36, 240, 10)` needed a brand-new `GwendylonCrossAreaTransfer` pending-queue on `World` (`pending_gwendylon_cross_area_transfers`/`drain_pending_gwendylon_cross_area_transfers`, mirroring `world::jail`/`world::macro_npc`'s existing `pending_*_cross_area_transfers` pattern field-for-field) since `World` has no DB/session access of its own; `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs::apply_gwendylon_cross_area_transfers` resolves it through the already-shared `attempt_cross_area_transfer` helper (`crates/ugaris-server/src/cross_area.rs`, unchanged), falling back to Gwendylon audibly saying the C "Uh-Oh...rift in the space-time continuum" line via `World::npc_quiet_say` on failure rather than a private system message - a genuine difference from every other existing cross-area call site's "target area server is down" text, since C's own fallback here really is a `quiet_say`, not a `log_char`. Nine new item IDs (`IID_AREA1_SKELKEY1`/`2`/`3`, `IID_AREA1_SKELSKULL`/`WOODKEY`/`WOODSKULL`/`MAGESKULL`/`WARLOCKSKULL`/`WARLOCKKEY`, `IID_CALIGARLETTER`, all `item_driver/ids.rs`, following the existing `make_item_id(DEV_ID_DB, ...)` pattern). Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`gwendylon_player_facts`/`apply_gwendylon_events`/`apply_gwendylon_cross_area_transfers`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the existing terion block, reusing the same `QuestLog::complete_legacy`+`World::give_exp`+`award_swap_money_converted_achievement` pattern camhermit/yoakin already established for `QuestDone`/`GoldEarned`). One documented, deliberate gap shared with `world::yoakin` (see `world::gwendylon`'s module doc comment): `destroy_items_by_template_id` does not sweep the account depot, since that storage lives outside `World`. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists ~13 remaining unported NPC drivers (down from ~16; `gwendylon_driver`, the file's single biggest and highest-value NPC, is done). 9 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/gwendylon.rs` (entry greeting+quest-open, the `NTID_TUTORIAL` reminder notification, the quest2-already-done silent skip-ahead, text-repeat state reset, skull turn-in with first-time gold reward end-to-end including the leftover-inventory sweep, second-completion gold-skip, the Caligar-letter give-back-plus-cross-area-transfer-queue, the generic give-back fallback, and the bless-success early-return message-retention quirk). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2305 ugaris-core [+9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 8s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 248 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2305 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its fifth self-contained NPC slice: `greeter_driver` (`CDR_GREETER = 13`, `gwendylon.c:1485-1798`, the tutorial-town Governor "Cameron" at the stronghold - new `CDR_GREETER` constant, since it didn't exist yet). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/greeter.rs` (`GreeterDriverData`/`CDR_GREETER` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Greeter` variant and its two exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 15-state (0-14) chain - a `CF_WARRIOR`/`CF_MAGE`-gated entry greeting with a silent Seyan'Du (both flags set) fast-path straight to the terminal state, a level-7-gated three-step weapon-choice tutorial (small blade -> two-handed sword/staff -> fists -> outro), the "learn"-prompt/James-whimpering hint (conditional on the pre-existing `james_state` accessor), a second level-7-gated civics tutorial (rest-area/recall-scrolls/movement/look-ground), and the final `QLOG_LYDIA`-gated understand-prompt/reminder pair (states 12/13). `NT_TEXT` reuses the same `GWENDYLON_QA`/`analyse_text_qa` matcher every other area-1 NPC in this file already shares, with "repeat" resetting to state 0 and "learn" rewinding to state 8 only from the state-7 "empty" checkpoint or state >= 13 - copying `world::yoakin`'s `current_victim`/`last_talk` reset-then-gate shape (not `world::terion`'s ungated one, a genuine, confirmed C-source asymmetry between the two). `NT_GIVE` is the same generic give-back fallback through `give_char_item_smart`. Caught and fixed a first-draft transcription mistake mid-iteration: states 12/13 both call `questlog_isdone(co, QLOG_LYDIA)` in C (`gwendylon.c:1684,1700`) to decide whether to jump to the terminal state 14 or keep nagging about James - an initial draft dropped this read entirely, assuming greeter never touches the quest log; re-reading the C source line-by-line caught it before committing, and `GreeterPlayerFacts::lydia_quest_done` (read via `player.quest_log.is_done(QLOG_LYDIA)`, never written) was added to fix it. All `area1_ppd` accessors this driver needs (`greeter_state`/`greeter_seen_timer`/`james_state`) already existed from iteration 204's `/showppd` work - no new PlayerRuntime surface beyond the `QLOG_LYDIA` read. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`greeter_player_facts`/`apply_greeter_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the gwendylon cross-area-transfer block) - the simplest event-application function of the five area-1 NPCs so far (two outcome variants, `UpdateState`/`UpdateSeenTimer`, no quest log writes, no `ServerRuntime`/DB/achievement plumbing). One documented gap shared with every other ambient NPC in this file (see `world::greeter`'s module doc comment): the `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` marker styling around "learn"/"repeat" is dropped (cosmetic, `World::npc_quiet_say` only carries a plain UTF-8 `String`). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists ~12 remaining unported NPC drivers (down from ~13). 20 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/greeter.rs` (all three class-flag entry-greeting branches including the Seyan'Du fast-path and the silent no-class-yet no-op, both level-7 tutorial-skip gates, the James-reminder conditional both ways, both state-12/13 `QLOG_LYDIA` branches including the 60-second reminder throttle both ways, the terminal state's silence, NPC/lostcon filtering, both text-repeat/text-learn rewind branches including the "does nothing mid-dialogue" negative case, the canned-answer QA reply, and generic give-back). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2325 ugaris-core [+20] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 249 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2325 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its sixth self-contained NPC slice: `jessica_driver` (`CDR_JESSICA = 125`, `gwendylon.c:1809-2065`, the robber-operations two-quest NPC - new `CDR_JESSICA` constant, since it didn't exist yet). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/jessica.rs` (`JessicaDriverData`/`CDR_JESSICA` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Jessica` variant and its two exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 13-state chain (entry gated on `QLOG_NOOK` being done - accessors/`Area1QuestState` already existed from earlier `showppd`/`questlog_init_area1` work, so only the driver itself was new PlayerRuntime-facing surface beyond that read - the five-line quest-1 intro through `QUEST1_DO`'s 60-second reminder, `QUEST1_FINISH`'s auto-advance into the quest-2 intro, `QUEST2_DO`'s own 60-second reminder, and `QUEST2_FINISH`'s auto-advance to done); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, with its own 2-bucket state-reset ranges (quest 1's `_GIVE_1..DO` range resets to `_GIVE_1`, quest 2's `_GIVE_1..DO` range resets to its own `_GIVE_1`); and `NT_GIVE`'s `IID_AREA1_ROBBER2NOTE` turn-in finishing quest 1 (new `IID_AREA1_ROBBER2NOTE`/`DEV_ID_KW` constants in `item_driver`, `DEV_ID_KW = 55` "ID of Kenneth Wright"). Two new `pub(crate)` state constants were needed in `quest.rs` that `questlog_init_area1` itself never reads (`JESSICA_STATE_QUEST1_DO = 6`/`QUEST2_DO = 10`), alongside widening `JESSICA_STATE_QUEST1_FINISH`/`QUEST2_FINISH` from private to `pub(crate)` to reach the new module - same pattern as `CAMHERMIT_STATE_*`'s widening in iteration 244. Caught and preserved (rather than "fixed") a genuine, confirmed C behavioral difference: jessica's own unwanted-item give-back branch calls plain `give_char_item` (`tool.c:3371-3394`, no ground-drop fallback on a full inventory), not `give_char_item_smart` like every sibling NPC in this same file (camhermit/yoakin/gwendylon's own NT_GIVE fallbacks are all confirmed `give_char_item_smart` call sites in C) - since no plain-give port existed in `World` yet (only a private near-identical duplicate inside `trader.rs`, `trader_give_char_item`, backing `trader_driver`'s own identical-semantics NT_GIVE early-exits), a new shared `World::give_char_item` was added to `world/items.rs` and `trader.rs` was updated to call it instead of its own now-deleted private copy, per `AGENTS.md`'s "extend existing code, never duplicate it" rule - `trader.rs`'s 21 existing tests all still pass unchanged. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`jessica_player_facts`/`apply_jessica_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the greeter block) reusing the same `questlog_open`/`_done` pattern camhermit/yoakin/gwendylon already established, minus the achievement-tracking half since jessica's own two quests carry no gold reward at all (both `QLOG_JESSICA_ROBBER_NOTE`/`_KILL` are pure exp quests per `quest.rs`'s own `QuestMeta` table). One documented, deliberate gap (see `world::jessica`'s module doc comment): the `QUEST2_DO` -> `QUEST2_FINISH` transition is driven by a separate `bredel_dead` monster-death hook (`gwendylon.c:2825-2842`, "the local robber leader" boss) that is not ported anywhere yet - same class of gap as camhermit's `monster_dead` bear-kill counter - so the kill-quest chain cannot fully complete on a live server until that hook (or the broader `monster_dead` death-dispatch table) lands; this driver's own `NT_CHAR` handling already closes out the quest correctly once `jessica_state` reaches `QUEST2_FINISH` by any means, verified directly via the fact-snapshot tests rather than left silently untested. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists ~11 remaining unported NPC drivers (down from ~12; also updated the shared `monster_dead`/death-hook tail note to explicitly call out `bredel_dead`). 13 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/jessica.rs` (entry silence before `QLOG_NOOK`/advance once done, quest-1 intro-line advance with `questlog_open`, both `_DO` states' 60-second reminder throttle both ways, `QUEST1_FINISH`'s auto-advance with `questlog_done`, `QUEST2_GIVE_1`'s `questlog_open`, `QUEST2_FINISH`'s auto-advance with `questlog_done` to the terminal state, the robber-note turn-in, the generic give-back-or-destroy-on-full-inventory fallback via the new plain `give_char_item`, both quest-1/quest-2 text-repeat state resets, and current-victim text filtering). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2338 ugaris-core [+13] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 250 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2338 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its seventh self-contained NPC slice: `jiu_driver` (`CDR_JIU = 127`, `gwendylon.c:2074-2247`, the forest sanctuary pilgrim's riverbeast-kill quest - new `CDR_JIU` constant, since it didn't exist yet). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/jiu.rs` (`JiuDriverData`/`CDR_JIU` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Jiu` variant and its two exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 5-state (`JIU_STATE_ENTRY`/`_STORY1`/`_WAIT_FOR_KILL`/`_BEAST_KILLED`/`_DONE`, all pre-existing `#define`s in `src/common/npc_states.h`) dialogue machine - the level-39-gated entry greeting split (both branches unconditionally `didsay`, only the high-level one advances state and says the "difficult impediment" line), `STORY1`'s unconditional advance-plus-`questlog_open(QLOG_JIU)`, `WAIT_FOR_KILL`'s silent no-op ("next state gets triggered from monster death" per C's own comment), and `BEAST_KILLED`'s unconditional thanks-plus-`questlog_done`; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" handling via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table (resetting to `JIU_STATE_ENTRY` when `< _BEAST_KILLED`, or repeating the "no more requests, may Ishtar's blessing" line at `_BEAST_KILLED`/`_DONE` - both branches count as `didsay` regardless, since C's `didsay` was already set truthy by the outer `switch` assignment before the inner `if`/`else if` runs, matching `world::terion`/`world::yoakin`'s identical `Matched(2)` shape); and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back through `give_char_item_smart`. `QLOG_JIU`/`area1_jiu_state`/`area1_jiu_seen_timer` all already existed as unused surface from earlier `showppd`/questlog work, so this slice needed no new `PlayerRuntime` accessors at all - `JiuPlayerFacts` is the simplest fact snapshot of the seven area-1 NPCs so far (`state` only; `jiu_seen_timer` is write-only from this driver's own body, never read back by it, matching C exactly). Caught and preserved (rather than "fixed") a genuine, confirmed C dead-code quirk while transcribing: `jiu_driver`'s `NT_CHAR` branch has two consecutive throttle `if`s that both gate on the exact same `ticker < dat->last_talk + TICKS*10` condition (unlike yoakin's genuinely distinct `TICKS*5`/`TICKS*10` pair) - the second `if`'s body can never execute, since the first already `continue`s on the identical condition; ported as the single effective check (documented in `world::jiu`'s module doc comment) rather than reproducing the unreachable branch verbatim. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`jiu_player_facts`/`apply_jiu_events`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the jessica block) reusing the same `questlog_open`/`_done` pattern every prior area-1 NPC established, minus achievement-tracking since Jiu's own quest carries no gold reward (`QLOG_JIU` is a pure exp quest per `quest.rs`'s `QuestMeta` table, same as jessica's two quests). One documented, deliberate gap (see `world::jiu`'s module doc comment): `riverbeast_dead` (`gwendylon.c:2255-2272`, the death hook that advances `_WAIT_FOR_KILL` -> `_BEAST_KILLED` when a player kills the riverbeast - itself just `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` under the hood per C's own `ch_driver` dispatch, `gwendylon.c:6127-6128`) is not ported anywhere yet - same class of still-missing generic per-species death-dispatch table (C's `ch_died_driver`) that gates jessica's `bredel_dead` gap and camhermit's `monster_dead` bear-kill counter - so a player who kills the riverbeast will not see `jiu_state` advance past `_WAIT_FOR_KILL` on a live server yet; this driver's own dialogue chain up to and including the beast-killed/done turn-in is fully ported and independently tested via direct state injection in the fact snapshot. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists ~10 remaining unported NPC drivers (down from ~11; also updated the shared death-hook tail note to explicitly call out `riverbeast_dead`). 10 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/jiu.rs` (both entry-greeting level branches, `STORY1`'s quest-open-and-advance, `WAIT_FOR_KILL`'s silence, `BEAST_KILLED`'s thanks-and-quest-done, `DONE`'s permanent silence, both text-repeat branches - reset-below-beast-killed and no-more-requests-at/after it, current-victim text filtering, and generic give-back). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2348 ugaris-core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1084 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- Iteration 251 confirmed the workspace was still green (`cargo test --workspace`: 2348 core + 1084 server, no failures), then continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task by closing the `monster_dead`/`bredel_dead`/`riverbeast_dead` death-hook gap that camhermit's/jessica's/jiu's own module doc comments (iterations 244/249/250) had all independently flagged as their shared remaining blocker. Added three new driver-ID constants to `crates/ugaris-core/src/character_driver.rs`: `CDR_RIVERBEAST = 128`, `CDR_CAMERON_FORESTMONSTER = 129`, `CDR_BREDEL = 154` (values straight from `src/system/drvlib.h:177,178,205`). Ported area 1's `monster_dead` (`gwendylon.c:5201-5231`) split across the two crates exactly like the pre-existing area-15 `apply_swamp_monster_death_driver`/`apply_swamp_monster_death_from_hurt_event` pair: `World::apply_area1_monster_death_driver` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/hurt.rs`) is the noon/stone-circle weapon-glow half (field-for-field identical to the swamp version but with area 1's own `x:10..=20, y:51..=60` bounding box, `hour == 12` trigger instead of midnight, and a `+= 5` charge amount instead of area 15's `+= 12`); `apply_area1_monster_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events.rs`) is the `camhermit_kills` counter half, since that needs `PlayerRuntime` which `World` has no access to - increments the counter when `camhermit_state == CAMHERMIT_STATE_QUEST1DO` and queues the exact "killed 10 big bears" `log_char` line once it reaches 10. Ported `bredel_dead` (`gwendylon.c:2825-2842`) and `riverbeast_dead` (`:2255-2272`) directly as `apply_bredel_death_from_hurt_event`/`apply_riverbeast_death_from_hurt_event` (same file): each checks the killer's `CF_PLAYER` flag and driver ID, reads the relevant `PlayerRuntime` quest state (`jessica_state`/`jiu_state`), and only on an exact state match (`JESSICA_STATE_QUEST2_DO`/`JIU_STATE_WAIT_FOR_KILL`) advances it (`_FINISH`/`_BEAST_KILLED`) and queues the verbatim C reward text. All three new hooks are wired into the existing `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` per-`LegacyHurtEvent` dispatch loop alongside the pre-existing swamp-monster/teufel-rat/caligar-skelly/lab2-undead/gate-fight/gate-welcome/dungeonmaster hooks it already runs every tick - no new dispatch infrastructure needed, following the same "bespoke, manually-invoked per-species handler" precedent every other death hook in this codebase already uses rather than building a generic `ch_died_driver`-equivalent table (confirmed via fresh exploration that no such table exists anywhere yet - `execute_character_died_driver`/`apply_character_death_driver` only special-case `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`). Added 2 `World`-level tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/hurt.rs` (`area1_monster_death_driver_upgrades_noon_stone_circle_weapon`/`_rejects_repeated_or_non_noon_kills`, mirroring the existing swamp-monster test pair) and 4 `ServerRuntime`-level tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` (`lethal_riverbeast_hurt_advances_jiu_state_to_beast_killed`, `lethal_riverbeast_hurt_ignores_players_not_awaiting_the_kill`, `lethal_bredel_hurt_advances_jessica_state_to_quest2_finish`, `lethal_forest_monster_hurt_counts_camhermit_kills_and_reports_at_ten`), each driving the full `apply_legacy_hurt`+`apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` pipeline exactly like the existing caligar-skelly/teufel-rat tests in that same file. Net effect: all three previously-documented, independently-flagged quest-completion gaps in camhermit/jessica/jiu are now closed - a player can finally finish all three area-1 kill-quest chains end-to-end on a live server, not just up to the "waiting for a kill" checkpoint. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 task stays `[~]` (still ~13 unported character drivers plus the `bigbadspider_dead`/`gwendylon_dead`/`asturin_dead` remainder, all gated on their own still-unported NPC drivers) but its REMAINING note and the ledger row above were both updated to reflect the closed death-hook gap. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2350 ugaris-core [+2] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1088 server [+4], all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.

### Architecture v2 Session (post-iteration-252)

- One-file-per-NPC restructure: every NPC's driver data, parser, QA table, and world logic now live in a single `world/npc/<area>/<name>.rs` file; `character_driver.rs` shrank 5.6K -> 3.3K lines keeping only the registry surface plus re-exports.
- `player.rs` (11.3K) split into `player/` with per-system submodules; `world_events.rs` (6.3K) split into `world_events/`; `commands_admin.rs` and its 10.4K test file split by command family; 62 per-NPC tick passes (1.5K lines) extracted from `main()` into `tick_npc/<area>.rs` behind order-preserving `tick_npc::run_all`.
- Persistence v2: `characters.player_state_json` (migration 0020) stores the full serde `PlayerRuntime` + account depot as the authoritative state; legacy blobs remain a read fallback for pre-0020 rows and are still written during the transition. Offline admin saves preserve the document via SQL `coalesce`. `Database::run_migrations` now applies `migrations/` automatically at startup.
- DB testing: workspace `compose.yaml` for a persistent dev PostgreSQL and a testcontainers live round-trip suite (`cargo test -p ugaris-db -- --ignored`), verified green against Docker.
- Loop guidance refreshed: `PORTING_TODO.md` compressed from 12.7K to ~400 lines (verbose done-task essays archived to `PROGRESS_ARCHIVE.md`), new `P0.5 Structural Maintenance` tasks added, `.ralph/PROMPT.md` rewritten with the v2 structure/persistence rules.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with an eighth self-contained NPC slice: `brithildie_driver` (`CDR_BRITHILDIE = 126`, `gwendylon.c:2474-2823`, the Governor's-mother ambient lore NPC unlocking `QLOG_BRITHILDIE`). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/brithildie.rs` (`BrithildieDriverData`/`CDR_BRITHILDIE` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Brithildie` variant and its two exhaustive-match update sites in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 22-state (0-21) story chain gated on the greeted player's own level at four checkpoints, each with its own 60-second reminder; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat"/"repeat all" via the existing shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table; and the generic `NT_GIVE` give-back via plain `World::give_char_item` (confirmed C behavior, no ground-drop fallback, same asymmetry already documented on `world::jessica`'s own give-back). Confirmed and preserved a genuine C dead-code quirk: `BRITHILDIE_STATE_WAIT_STORY_3`'s high-level branch jumps straight to `STORY_4_1`, permanently skipping the `STORY_3_1`/`_3_2`/`_3_3` case labels - unreachable from anywhere in the C source, not "fixed" here either. Also added `CDR_BIGBADSPIDER = 153` and its `bigbadspider_dead` death hook (`gwendylon.c:2850-2870`) as `apply_bigbadspider_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs`, wired into `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`): a `CDR_BIGBADSPIDER` kill by a player with `brithildie_state == BRITHILDIE_STATE_NOMORETALES_QOPEN` runs a full `questlog_done` (exp reward + resend) and advances to `_QDONE`, closing this quest chain end-to-end on a live server, same as the `bredel_dead`/`riverbeast_dead` precedent from the prior iteration. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`brithildie_player_facts`/`apply_brithildie_events`) and `tick_npc/area1.rs` (`brithildie_driver_62`, registered at the end of `tick_npc::run_all`). 17 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/brithildie.rs`, 2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` (bigbadspider death-hook wiring, success and no-op cases). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated (8 NPC drivers now unported, down from 9; the `bigbadspider_dead` death hook gap is closed). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2380 ugaris-core [+17] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- 2026-07-07: started the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task: extracted the tick loop's "world stepping" phase - everything the `tick.tick()` arm did *before* draining/processing queued client actions (game clock/regen advance, the autonomous weather cycle's damage/lightning/elemental rolls and movement-percent refresh, lostcon expiry + early-exit save/despawn, `tick_effects_with_attack_policy`, `process_due_timers` plus its four item-driver spawn-outcome branches (edemon/fdemon gate, chest, swamp), LQ NPC spawns, NPC respawns, and the five kill-hook drains (kill exp, kill/level/first-kill/military-mission achievements, death-mode loot rolls) plus timer feedback/due-task logging) - into a new `pub(crate) async fn tick_world::world_step` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_world.rs`, 525 lines, new `mod tick_world;` in `main.rs`, called by its fully-qualified path exactly like `tick_npc::run_all` rather than a glob re-export). This was a pure verbatim cut-and-paste (dedented, no logic changes): confirmed beforehand that none of this span's locals (`timer_outcomes`, `clan_relations`, `respawn_requests`, `lq_spawn_requests`, `death_loot_rolls`, `due_tasks`, the four spawn counters) leak past its own last statement into the client-command-loop code that follows (`clan_relations` is re-declared/shadowed later for a different, unrelated attack-policy closure) - so a plain 7-parameter signature (`world`/`runtime`/`zone_loader`/`config`/`args`/`achievement_repository`/`character_repository`, the only two repositories this span actually touches) using the same `mut world: &mut World` deref-coercion parameter style `tick_npc`'s per-NPC passes already established was sufficient; no new `World`/`ServerRuntime` fields, no behavior change. `main.rs` shrank from 7,150 to 6,689 lines (461 lines cut, the call site itself is 9 lines). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2380 ugaris-core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, identical counts to before this change since it moved code rather than adding it), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with 200+ ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: still inline in `main.rs` are the huge queued-client-action match (the "client command loop", ~5.1K lines) and the post-`tick_npc::run_all` "sync" phase (map/effects/resource broadcast) - both noted as REMAINING for a future iteration to extract the same way.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task: extracted the tick loop's post-`tick_npc::run_all` "sync" phase - PK hate updates from hurt events (`apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`), the ~20s-cadence shutdown scheduler check, the four pending world/area/system-text/channel-broadcast drains, `queue_resource_sync_frames`, `queue_periodic_player_frames`, and the final `runtime.flush_tick_frames(true)` - into a new `pub(crate) fn tick_sync::sync_phase` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_sync.rs`, 111 lines, new `mod tick_sync;` in `main.rs`). Verbatim cut-and-paste (dedented, no logic changes): the span's only cross-branch value was the `shutdown_due` bool gating the `break`, so `sync_phase` returns it directly and the call site becomes `if tick_sync::sync_phase(&mut world, &mut runtime, &zone_loader) { break; }`. Confirmed no other locals from this span (`realtime_seconds`, `pk_hate_updates`, the five `*_sessions`/`*_frames` counters) leak past their own logging statement. `main.rs` shrank from 6,689 to 6,632 lines (57 lines net after `cargo fmt --all`, the call site itself is 6 lines). `cargo test --workspace`: 1091 server tests unchanged (pure code motion), all green. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with 200+ ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: only the huge queued-client-action match (the "client command loop", ~5.1K lines between `tick_world::world_step`'s call site and `tick_npc::run_all`'s) remains inline in `main.rs`; unlike the two phases already extracted, moving it verbatim into one file would blow the ~2,000 line hard cap, so it needs an actual split by command-action family across several files (not just relocation) - left as the REMAINING note for a future iteration to plan.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task: extracted the tick loop's queued-client-action drain/dispatch/feedback-flush phase - `runtime.drain_actions_for_tick()`, the expired-tell/shutup feedback drains, the full per-session `match action { ClientAction::... }` (Text/Container/LookContainer/FastSell/Swap/TakeGold/DropGold/JunkItem/Speed/FightMode/Raise/LookCharacter/GetQuestLog/ReopenQuest/Ping/Nop/ClientInfo/Log/ModPacket), and the trailing feedback/inventory/container/name-refresh packet flush - into a new `pub(crate) async fn tick_client_actions::process_queued_client_actions` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_client_actions.rs`, 1,388 lines, new `mod tick_client_actions;` in `main.rs`). Verbatim cut-and-paste (dedented via a Python script to strip the extra 12 spaces of `tokio::select!`/loop/match nesting, no logic changes): confirmed the span's only external inputs were `world`/`runtime`/`zone_loader`/`config` plus the 6 repositories it actually references inside the `ClientAction::Text` branch's command dispatch (`achievement_repository`, `character_repository`, `area_repository`, `clan_log_repository`, `merchant_repository`, `auction_repository` - the other 5 repositories `tick_npc::run_all` takes, e.g. `clan_repository`/`military_*_repository`/`notes_repository`/`anticheat_repository`, are never touched by this span) and that none of its locals leak past the phase (the next inline code, autobless/autopulse, starts a fresh scope). Matched the established `tick_world`/`tick_npc` parameter-reborrow convention: `mut world: &mut World, mut runtime: &mut ServerRuntime, mut zone_loader: &mut ZoneLoader` (the `mut` prefix on each reference-typed parameter, not just the pointee, is required for the body's many bare `&mut runtime`/`&mut zone_loader` reborrow expressions to typecheck without individually rewriting them to `&mut *runtime`). `main.rs` shrank from 6,632 to 5,262 lines (1,370 lines net after `cargo fmt --all`, the call site itself is 12 lines). Also cleaned up an unrelated pre-existing `cargo test` warning found during the health check (`unused import: super::*` - a duplicate `use super::*;` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/tests/mod.rs`, harmless leftover from a prior edit). `cargo test --workspace`: 1091 server tests unchanged (pure code motion), all green, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" with 200+ ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the remaining inline block in `main.rs` is now the completed-action-outcome handling (`if !completed_actions.is_empty() { ... }` after `tick_basic_actions_with_attack_policy`, ~3.7K lines between this phase's call site and `tick_npc::run_all`'s) - also too large for one file verbatim, needs splitting by completed-action-kind family (attack/take/move/etc. outcome post-processing) across several files - left as the REMAINING note for a future iteration to plan.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task with its first completed-action-outcome family slice, following the "needs splitting by completed-action-kind family across several files" note left by the prior iteration. Chose the Warp-area (`src/area/25/warped.c`) family - 17 `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`WarpTeleportMissingSphere/Bug/Busy/Spheres`, `WarpBonusFinished/AlreadyUsed/NeedsSphere/WarpBonus`, `WarpKeySpawnCursorOccupied/WarpKeySpawn`, `WarpKeyDoorMissingKey/Bug/WarpKeyDoor`, `WarpTrialDoorWrongSide/Busy/Bug/WarpTrialDoor`) - because they formed one contiguous 357-line span inside the giant `match outcome { ... }` (main.rs original lines 2876-3232) with no interleaving from other areas, making the boundary unambiguous. Extracted verbatim into a new `pub(crate) async fn tick_item_use_warp::dispatch_warp_outcome` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_warp.rs`, 389 lines, new `mod tick_item_use_warp;` in `main.rs`, `use super::*;` for full main.rs-namespace access same as `tick_client_actions`). Confirmed the span's only external inputs were `world`/`zone_loader`/`runtime`/`achievement_repository`/`args.area_id` plus the shared `feedback`/`feedback_bytes` vecs and `executed`/`blocked`/`failed` counters (not `opened`/`unsupported`/`deferred_templates`/`special_feedback`/`area_feedback`/`container_refresh`, which this family never touches) - passed the three repo/world types by their existing `&mut` reference kind (reborrowed, not double-referenced, since the function now owns the reference itself) and the three counters as `&mut i32` (dereferenced with `*` at each `+= 1` site inside the new file). The call site in `main.rs` binds the moved `outcome` value via a single `outcome @ (Variant1 { .. } | Variant2 { .. } | ...) => { tick_item_use_warp::dispatch_warp_outcome(&mut world, &mut zone_loader, &mut runtime, &achievement_repository, &args, outcome, &mut feedback, &mut feedback_bytes, &mut executed, &mut blocked, &mut failed).await; }` or-pattern arm, replacing the 357 inline lines with 32. `main.rs` shrank from 5,263 (post mod-line-insertion) to 4,938 lines pre-fmt (`cargo fmt --all` reformats both files without changing line count materially). `cargo test --workspace`: 2393 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings on the first attempt (no borrow-checker fixups needed once the `&mut X` -> `X` reborrow-vs-double-reference distinction was applied correctly at each call site inside the extracted file). 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" plus 200+ ticks with `completed world actions`/NPC-driver logging and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: every other outcome family (chests, ice, dungeon, teufel, skel-raise, transport, clan-spawn, lq, arena, shrines, xmas, swamp, edemon/fdemon, burndown, palace doors, key-assembly, the large no-op catch-all) remains inline in `main.rs`'s match - continue one family per iteration following this same pattern; the REMAINING note in `PORTING_TODO.md` now spells out the exact recipe for future slices.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task with its second completed-action-outcome family slice: the chest family - 16 `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`ChestTreasure`, `RandomChest`, `RatChest`, `InfiniteChest`/`InfiniteChestCursorOccupied`/`InfiniteChestKeyRequired`/`InfiniteChestUnknown`, `ForestChest`/`ForestChestCursorOccupied`/`ForestChestLocked`, `PickChest`/`PickChestCursorOccupied`/`PickChestLocked`/`PickChestBug`, `ChestSpawn`/`ChestSpawnCheck`). Unlike Warp's single contiguous span, these 16 variants were scattered across 5 separate non-contiguous spots in the giant match (interleaved with ice/dungeon/junkpile/pick-door/swamp-spawn arms that stay inline); each spot's inline code was removed and replaced with either a single combined or-pattern call arm (at the first spot, covering all 16 variants) or deleted outright (the other 4 spots), since Rust match-arm order is irrelevant to correctness here. Extracted verbatim into a new `pub(crate) async fn tick_item_use_chests::dispatch_chest_outcome` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_chests.rs`, 353 lines, new `mod tick_item_use_chests;` in `main.rs`, `use super::*;` same convention). Confirmed the span's only external inputs were `world`/`zone_loader`/`runtime`/`achievement_repository`/`config.area_id`/`realtime_seconds` plus the shared `feedback` vec and `executed`/`blocked`/`failed` counters (this family never touches `feedback_bytes`/`opened`/`unsupported`/`deferred_templates`/`special_feedback`/`area_feedback`/`container_refresh`) - took `config: &ServerConfig` (matching the call site's existing `config.area_id` local, not `args.area_id` like Warp used) since `RandomChest`/`RatChest` need it for `apply_random_chest`/`apply_rat_chest`'s area-scoped RNG seed and cooldown key. `main.rs` shrank from 4,938 to 4,737 lines pre-fmt (201 lines net; the single combined call-site arm is 30 lines replacing what was ~230 inline lines across the 5 spots, offset by the 4 now-empty spots collapsing to nothing). `cargo test --workspace`: 2393 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" plus 200+ ticks with `completed world actions`/NPC-driver logging and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: remaining families (ice, dungeon, teufel, skel-raise, transport, clan-spawn, lq, arena, shrines, xmas, swamp, edemon/fdemon, burndown, palace doors, key-assembly, the large no-op catch-all) remain inline in `main.rs`'s match; this slice additionally demonstrates the non-contiguous-family pattern (grep all variant names for a family, replace the first occurrence with the combined dispatch call, delete the rest) for future iterations whose variants aren't conveniently contiguous like Warp's were.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task with its third completed-action-outcome family slice: the dungeon family (`src/area/13/dungeon.c`) - 7 contiguous `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`DungeonTeleport`, `DungeonFake`, `DungeonKey`, `DungeonKeyCursorOccupied`, `DungeonDoorMissingKeys`, `DungeonDoorTooManyDefenders`, `DungeonDoorSolved`). Like Warp, this span was a single contiguous block in the match, so it was replaced with one combined or-pattern call arm binding the moved `outcome` value, following the exact same convention. - 2026-07-07: continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with an eleventh self-contained NPC slice: `robber_driver` (`CDR_ROBBER = 17`, `gwendylon.c:3771-3953`, the midnight-meeting forest patrol - the first purely movement/self-defense NPC in this file, no dialogue at all). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/robber.rs` (`RobberDriverData`/`CDR_ROBBER` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Robber` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) ports the clock-gated guard-post wait, the fixed nine-waypoint ladder-to-hole patrol walk (`secure_move_driver`/`setup_walk_toward` per state, matching C's `move_driver(..., 1)` mindist verbatim), the two ladder/hole `use_driver` waypoints (a new `World::robber_use_waypoint_item`, identical shape to the pre-existing `World::janitor_use_light`), and the every-tick `WN_LHAND` torch upkeep (equip-if-missing via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template("torch", ...)`, relight-if-unlit via the existing `World::execute_item_driver_request(IDR_TORCH, ...)` synchronous-dispatch entry point, matching C's `use_item` rather than the queued-action `use_driver`). Self-defense is a documented single-victim simplification of C's generic 10-slot `DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` (same precedent as `CDR_GATE_FIGHT`'s own `world/npc/gate_fight.rs`), tracking the single most recent `NT_GOTHIT` attacker (a message every hurt character already receives unconditionally via `World::apply_legacy_hurt`) and reusing the existing generic `World::attack_driver_direct`/`secure_move_driver` rather than the full engine; ported C's `NT_GOTHIT` handler's `group`-equality gate verbatim, confirming (module doc comment) it is almost always inert in practice since neither this NPC's template nor a default player ever sets a non-zero `group` - the same dead-in-practice self-defense path C itself has, not a bug in this port. `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_ROBBER` case dispatches to the already-ported no-op `balltrap_skelly_dead`, so no death hook was needed. Driver state defaults lazily on first tick (no `zone.rs` wiring needed, following `world::lydia`'s precedent rather than the explicit-init one most other area-1 NPCs use). Server-side `robber_driver_65` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs`/`tick_npc/mod.rs::run_all`) is the simplest area-1 registration yet - a single `world.process_robber_actions(&mut zone_loader, config.area_id)` call, mirroring `janitor_driver_52`'s shape plus the `zone_loader` parameter the torch-equip path needs. See the ledger table row above for full detail. 9 new tests in `world/tests/robber.rs`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2415 core [+9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus 280+ ticks with no panic (area 1's real `wood_robber_meeting` template, `driver=17`, loads and ticks cleanly). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 entry now lists ~6 remaining unported character drivers (down from ~7).
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Finish main() phase decomposition" task with its seventh completed-action-outcome family slice: the Edemon (`src/area/6/edemon.c`) + Fdemon (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) boss-machinery family - 19 contiguous `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`EdemonSwitchStuck`, `EdemonDoorLocked`, `EdemonDoorLifeless`, `EdemonBlockBlocked`, `EdemonBlockMove`, `EdemonTubePulse`, `FdemonLoaderBlocked`, `FdemonCannonLifeless`, `EdemonLoaderBlocked`, `FdemonFarmHarvest`, `FdemonFarmCursorOccupied`, `FdemonFarmNotReady`, `FdemonFarmBug`, `FdemonBloodBlocked`, `FdemonBloodDestroyedFlask`, `FdemonBloodFilled`, `FdemonLavaBlocked`, `FdemonLavaActivated`, `EdemonDoorToggle`). Extracted verbatim into a new `pub(crate) fn tick_item_use_edemon_fdemon::dispatch_edemon_fdemon_outcome` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_edemon_fdemon.rs`, 225 lines, new `mod tick_item_use_edemon_fdemon;` in `main.rs`, `use super::*;` same convention). `EdemonDoorToggle` needed special handling unlike every prior slice's variants: it originally appeared *twice* in the giant match with different field patterns - once with a `key_name: Some(key_name)` guard (producing "You use X to lock/unlock the door.") right after the contiguous Edemon/Fdemon span (separated only by the unrelated `PotionDrunk` arm), and once bare (`EdemonDoorToggle { .. }`) far downstream inside the large no-op catch-all list (`FoodEaten | DoorToggle | EdemonDoorToggle | ...` => `executed += 1`) for the `key_name: None` case. Since Rust match-arm order only matters for overlapping patterns of the *same* variant (not across different enum variants), both `EdemonDoorToggle` arms were combined into the new dispatcher in the same relative order (guarded arm first, bare `{ .. }` fallback second matching the catch-all's `executed += 1`), the call site's or-pattern includes `EdemonDoorToggle { .. }` bare (catching both field shapes at once, since or-patterns in the call site can't carry the field guard), and both original main.rs occurrences (the specific arm and the `EdemonDoorToggle { .. }` entry inside the no-op catch-all list) were deleted. Confirmed the span's only external inputs were `world`/`zone_loader`/`runtime` (for `FdemonBloodFilled`/`FdemonLavaActivated`'s `player.advance_farmy_blood_stage`/`advance_farmy_lava_stage`, and `FdemonFarmHarvest`'s `grant_template_item_to_cursor`) plus the shared `feedback` vec and `executed`/`blocked` counters (this family never touches `achievement_repository`/`config`/`feedback_bytes`/`failed`). `main.rs` shrank from 4,556 to 4,359 lines pre-fmt (197 lines net). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2415 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with `completed world actions`/NPC-driver logging and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: remaining families (transport, clan-spawn, lq, arena, shrines, xmas, swamp, burndown, key-assembly, the large no-op catch-all) remain inline in `main.rs`'s match.
