This was the surrounding scaffolding around the giant outcome match that could not itself be sliced by outcome family (it calls the family dispatchers, it isn't one), so it had to wait until every family was already extracted; with all 19 prior family slices done, this final scaffolding-only slice was mechanical: dedented the ~1,275-line block (Python script, strip 12 of its original 16 leading spaces to go from `tokio::select!`/loop/match nesting to plain function-body nesting), wrapped it in an `async fn` taking `world`/`runtime`/`zone_loader` (as `mut world: &mut World` etc., the same reborrow-enabling parameter style `tick_client_actions`/`tick_world` established, needed because the body itself does many bare `&mut world`/`&mut zone_loader` reborrows), `config`/`args` (both used - `config.area_id` for item-use dispatch and map/sound payloads, kept for signature parity with `tick_npc::run_all`), `completed_actions: &mut Vec<WorldActionCompletion>`, and the three repositories the span's `award_stone_pickup_achievement`/family-dispatcher calls need (`achievement_repository`/`character_repository`/`area_repository` - confirmed via grep that no other repository, `zone_loader` aside, is touched anywhere in the span). Two small non-mechanical fixups were needed for the reference-parameter (rather than owned-local) `completed_actions`: its two `for completion in &completed_actions` loops became `for completion in completed_actions.iter()` (`&&mut Vec<T>` isn't `IntoIterator`; `.iter()` auto-derefs through the `&mut Vec<T>` binding correctly), and the function's `world`/`runtime`/`zone_loader` parameters needed the `mut` binding-modifier prefix (not just `&mut` in the type) since the body reborrows each of them repeatedly via bare `&mut world`/`&mut runtime`/`&mut zone_loader` expressions passed into the family dispatchers. `main.rs` shrank from 2,849 (post mod-line insertion) to 1,586 lines - under the 2,000-line hard cap for the first time since this task began (it was ~7,150 lines when the task started). This closes the P0.5 main() decomposition task: every tick-loop phase (world stepping, NPC passes, sync, queued-client-actions, completed-action outcomes) now lives in its own module, and `main.rs` only wires `RuntimeState`/CLI setup/the top-level tick loop calling each phase in order. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2415 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with `completed world actions`/NPC-driver logging (now attributed to `ugaris_server::tick_item_use_completion` in the tracing output) and no panic.
- 2026-07-07: P0.5 "Split `tests/commands_admin/character.rs` (~8K)": split the 7,933-line flat test file into `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/commands_admin/character/` (13 command-family files - `session.rs`/`clan_club.rs`/`debug_reset.rs`/`items_world.rs`/`military.rs`/`misc_commands.rs`/`teleport.rs`/`flags.rs`/`shrine_tunnel.rs`/`pent_macro.rs`/`questlog_fix.rs`/`anticheat.rs`/`moderation.rs`, largest 1,038 lines) using `tools/rust_split/splitter.py` with a name-keyed `ASSIGN` spec (each of the 275 top-level `fn`s mapped to its target file by an exact-name rule derived from the original file's line ranges, generated with a small one-off Python script rather than hand-written, since the 275 entries needed to be error-free). Two helpers used across multiple groups (`goto_test_world`, `setup_god_and_online_target`) were routed to stay in `character/mod.rs` (empty-string `ASSIGN` target) so every submodule's `use super::*;` can still reach them; three group-local helpers (`setup_god_and_target_with_military_ppd`, `setup_god_and_target_with_shrine_ppd`, `insert_runtime_for`, `connected_god_and_target`) stayed inside their own single-consumer file. `commands_admin/mod.rs`'s existing `mod character;`/`use character::*;` needed no changes (Rust resolves `character/mod.rs` automatically); fixed 26 `unused_imports`/`unreachable_pub` warnings the splitter's default `pub use` re-export style produced by switching `character/mod.rs`'s 13 submodule imports to `#[allow(unused_imports)] use x::*;`, matching the parent `commands_admin/mod.rs`'s own established style. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2415 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its next self-contained NPC slice: `sanoa_driver` (`CDR_SANOA = 18`, `gwendylon.c:3961-4094`), an ambient dialogue-free NPC whose entire body is a twelve-waypoint city walk gated by five fixed clock-window departure times plus the same single-victim self-defense/spell/regen cascade already established for `CDR_ROBBER`. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/sanoa.rs` (`SanoaDriverData`/`CDR_SANOA` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Sanoa` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) ports `process_sanoa_tick` verbatim: the `NT_GOTHIT`-only message loop (matching `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 0, 0)`'s effective behavior with `agressive=0`/`helper=0`), the twelve-state walk (`secure_move_driver`/`setup_walk_toward` per state, exact coordinates/mindist from C), and two `is_closed`/`use_item_at` door-toggle waypoints (states 3/10, both at tile `(21,26)`) via new `sanoa_door_is_closed`/`sanoa_use_item_at` helpers copied from `World::bank_door_is_closed`/`bank_use_item_at`'s existing shape. `fight_driver_set_dist`/`fight_driver_set_home` intentionally not ported, same precedent as `robber_driver`'s own module doc comment. `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_SANOA` case dispatches to the empty no-op `balltrap_skelly_dead`, so no death hook was needed. Server-side `sanoa_driver_66` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs`, registered in `tick_npc::run_all`) is the simplest area-1 registration yet: a single `world.process_sanoa_actions(config.area_id)` call, no `zone_loader`/DB/achievement plumbing at all (this NPC needs no torch upkeep, unlike robber). 8 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/sanoa.rs` (guard-post walk-toward, clock-gate wait/leave both ways, already-closed-door state-advance-without-toggle, open-door toggle-and-stay-in-state, `NT_GOTHIT` victim tracking plus adjacent-attack, and the group-equality self-defense gate's dead-in-practice behavior). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 task stays `[~]`: `james_driver`/`balltrap_skelly_driver`/`reskin_driver`/`asturin_driver`/`guiwynn_driver`/`logain_driver` remain unported. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2423 core [+8] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 8-10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its next self-contained NPC slice: `reskin_driver` (`CDR_RESKIN = 20`, `gwendylon.c:4098-4417`), the tavern-keeper/alchemy-ingredient-turn-in NPC unlocking `QLOG_RESKIN` (17, "The Unwanted Tenants"). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/reskin.rs` (`ReskinDriverData`/`CDR_RESKIN` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Reskin` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`, wired into `zone.rs`'s NPC-template instantiation) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 10-state chain gated on both `gwendy_state`/`terion_state` at entry (reusing `world::terion`'s already-ported state numbering as a cross-reference), state 3's dual branch (a 600-second real-time reminder OR a silent `logain_state > 8` advance - no dialogue on the silent path, matching every other silent-jump precedent in this file), `questlog_open` at state 4, and the class-conditional (`CF_WARRIOR`-only vs. mage/hybrid) alchemy-recipe text at state 8; `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat"/"restart" via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 3-bucket state-reset ranges; a new `NT_NPC`/`NTID_TERION` reply branch to `terion_driver`'s own `TERION_STATE_RESKIN_BEER` broadcast (confirming the two NPCs' existing cross-reference: `world::terion` already sends this, `world::reskin` is its first consumer); and `NT_GIVE`'s alchemy-ingredient turn-in (`IID_ALCHEMY_INGREDIENT`, keyed by `drdata[0]`'s ingredient type, ten level-gated "cannot pay at the moment" declines transcribed as a `match`-based `reskin_alchemy_level_gate` helper, an already-turned-in re-give, and the payment branch paying `value * 5` gold plus `ACHIEVEMENT_WELL_PAID_GATHERER` once the 24-bit `reskin_got_bits` mask is complete, via new `award_reskin_well_paid_gatherer_achievement`) plus the generic give-back fallback via `give_char_item_smart` (same substitution precedent `world::terion`'s own `NT_GIVE` branch already established for C's plain `give_char_item`). All three persistent `area1_ppd` fields this driver needs (`reskin_state`/`reskin_seen_timer`/`reskin_got_bits`) already had full accessor pairs from earlier `showppd`/`questlog_init_area1` work, so this slice needed no new `PlayerRuntime` surface beyond `PlayerRuntime::has_first_kill(16)` (already existed from the generic first-kill-bitmask system, C `check_first_kill(co, 16)`) - only `QLOG_RESKIN = 17` was newly added to `quest.rs` (the raw literal `17` two call sites in `questlog_init_area1` were left as-is, unrelated code). Confirmed and preserved (not "fixed") a genuine C-source bug unique to this driver: `case 7`'s `check_first_kill(co, 16)` branch calls `quiet_say`+`questlog_done` but never sets `didsay = 1`, unlike every other dialogue-producing case in the same `switch` - so `last_talk`/`current_victim`/the `NTID_DIDSAY` broadcast never update for that turn even though a line was said; documented in the module doc comment and covered by a dedicated test (`reskin_state7_completes_quest_but_does_not_set_didsay`) asserting `current_victim` stays `None` after the quest completes. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`reskin_player_facts`/`apply_reskin_events`) and `tick_npc/area1.rs` (`reskin_driver_67`, registered at the end of `tick_npc::run_all`), reusing the same `questlog_open`/`_done`+`World::give_exp`+`award_swap_money_converted_achievement` pattern every prior area-1 NPC established for its `QuestOpen`/`QuestDone`/`GoldEarned` events. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated (5 NPC drivers now unported, down from 6: `james_driver`/`balltrap_skelly_driver`/`asturin_driver`/`guiwynn_driver`/`logain_driver`). 23 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/reskin.rs` (both entry silent-gate branches, greet-and-advance entry, both state-3 branches, state 4's quest-open, state 7's silent-wait and its `didsay`-less completion, both state-8 class-conditional recipe texts, state 9's permanent no-op, both text-repeat reset buckets, the `NTID_TERION` reply, alchemy payment plus its achievement trigger, the level-gate decline, the already-turned-in re-give, the non-alchemy give-back, and the cross-NPC `NTID_DIDSAY` throttle). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2443 ugaris-core [+23] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus 280+ ticks with no panic (area 1's real `city_reskin` template, `driver=20`, loads and ticks cleanly).

New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/asturin.rs` (`AsturinDriverData`/`CDR_ASTURIN` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Asturin` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) ports `process_asturin_tick` verbatim: the `NT_CHAR` branch's `x`-coordinate-gated greeting/warning/guards-shout state machine (with its own 10s/30s `realtime` state-reset windows, read from a new `AsturinPlayerFacts`/`AsturinOutcomeEvent` pair mirroring every prior area-1 NPC's `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split), a new `World::npc_shout` primitive (`world/text.rs`, the missing NPC-facing half of C's `shout()` - `shout_dist`-ranged broadcast, quote-reject guard, no `CF_PLAYER` endurance-cost handling since that belongs to the player-command caller, mirroring `npc_quiet_say`'s existing shape) for the "GUARDS!" line plus a `notify_area(NTID_ASTURIN)` broadcast, `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" reset via the same shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table, and the every-tick (not `last_talk`-gated, unlike every dialogue-only sibling NPC's own return-to-post throttle) self-defense/regen/spell-self/`secure_move_driver`-to-`rest_x`/`rest_y` cascade, using the identical single-victim `NT_GOTHIT`-tracking simplification already established for `CDR_ROBBER`/`CDR_SANOA`. Also ported `asturin_dead` (`gwendylon.c:4535-4542`) as a new `apply_asturin_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs`, wired into `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`): C's `set_data(co, DRD_AREA1_PPD, ...)` succeeds for *any* live killer (no player-only restriction in the generic per-character memory-slot allocator), so the "I'll remember that" `quiet_say` line fires unconditionally via `World::npc_quiet_say(event.target_id, ...)`, while the persistent `asturin_state = 4` write only applies when the killer is a real player (`PlayerRuntime` doesn't exist for NPCs) - matching C's own observable behavior, since an NPC killer's shadow `ppd` write is never read again anyway. `struct asturin_driver_data`'s own `state` field is dead in C (never read/written anywhere in the driver body) and is not ported, same precedent as `world::terion`/`world::reskin`'s own dead fields. Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`asturin_player_facts`/`apply_asturin_events`, the simplest event-application function in the file - two plain-field events, no quest log/DB/achievement plumbing) and `tick_npc/area1.rs` (`asturin_driver_67`, inserted between `sanoa_driver_66` and the renumbered `reskin_driver_68` to match `ch_driver`'s own dispatch-table order, `tick_npc/mod.rs`). 11 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/asturin.rs` (state-0 greet, the warning-zone warn/forgive pair, the guards-shout-plus-`notify_area` boundary, both 10s/30s seen-timer reset windows, the welcome-back line, the text-repeat reset, `NT_GOTHIT` victim tracking plus adjacent-attack, the group-equality self-defense gate, and the return-to-post movement). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated (3 NPC drivers now unported, down from 4: `james_driver`/`balltrap_skelly_driver`/`guiwynn_driver`/`logain_driver`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2454 ugaris-core [+11] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus 280+ ticks with no panic.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P4 "Area 1 - `gwendylon.c`" task with its next self-contained NPC slice: `guiwynn_driver` (`CDR_GUIWYNN = 21`, `gwendylon.c:4546-4889`), the town-mage NPC running the two-part "Order of Mages" investigation quest chain (`QLOG` indices 7 "Mages Gone Berserk"/8 "The Recipe for Happiness", both already had full `QUEST_TABLE`/`questlog_init_area1`/`questlog_reopen` scaffolding from earlier iterations - this slice is the first driver to actually read/write them). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/guiwynn.rs` (`GuiwynnDriverData`/`CDR_GUIWYNN` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Guiwynn` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) ports the full message loop: `NT_CHAR`'s 12-state (0-11) chain gated on `gwendy_state >= 17` at entry (a bare-int C threshold one below the existing `GWENDYLON_STATE_FOUL_MAGICIAN_DONE == 18`, named locally as `GWENDYLON_STATE_FOUL_MAGICIAN_WAIT`) and `questlog_isdone(co, 8)` at state 6 (a new `GuiwynnPlayerFacts::quest8_done` fact, read via the pre-existing `QuestLog::is_done`), three identical `!has_item(co, IID_AREA1_MADKEY1)`-gated key grants (states 4/7/10, each speaking the "This key opens the front door" line unconditionally once the check passes, *not* gated on the deferred grant's eventual success - confirmed by re-reading C's own unchecked `create_item`/`give_char_item` call shape), and the two 60-second reminder states (5/8/11); a genuinely new pre-`switch` double-reset (`realtime - guiwynn_seen_timer > 120`, states 1-4 reset to 1 *and* states 6-7 reset to 6, both independent `if`s rather than lydia's single one); `NT_TEXT`'s "repeat" via the shared `GWENDYLON_QA` table with its own 3-bucket (0-5/6-8/9-11) reset ranges; a new `NT_NPC`/`NTID_TERION` relay branch replying to terion's own stone-circle/yoakin-ruins broadcasts (`dat3 == 1`/`4`, the first re-broadcasting `NTID_TERION` with `dat3 == 2`); and `NT_GIVE`'s two quest turn-ins (`IID_AREA1_MADPOTION` at state `<= 5` finishing quest 7, `IID_AREA1_MADNOTE` at state `6..=8` finishing quest 8, each sweeping 2-5 `destroy_items_by_template_id` quest-item template IDs) plus the generic give-back via plain `World::give_char_item` (confirmed C behavior - no ground-drop fallback, same asymmetry every other area-1 NPC's own `NT_GIVE` handler already documents). Confirmed and preserved (not "fixed") a genuine duplicate-check quirk unique to this driver: the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DIDSAY` self-throttle bump is checked *twice* per tick in C - once in the standard leading pre-pass every area-1 NPC has, and *again* inline in the main per-message loop right before `remove_message` - both reproduced verbatim rather than deduplicated. Six new item IDs (`IID_AREA1_MADKEY1`-`5`, `IID_AREA1_MADPOTION`, `IID_AREA1_MADNOTE`, `item_driver/ids.rs`, `make_item_id(DEV_ID_DB, ...)`). The two `create_money_item(MONEY_AREA1_MADMAGE1/2)` + plain `give_char_item` money rewards (`gwendylon.c:4800-4805`/`4822-4827`) needed new architecture: unlike every prior gold reward in this file (`world::gwendylon`'s skull rewards, `world::yoakin`'s bear-tooth reward), guiwynn calls *plain* `give_char_item` on the money item rather than `give_char_item_smart`'s `IF_MONEY`-auto-converts-to-gold branch - a genuine, confirmed C behavioral difference that leaves the reward as a literal carried "money" item object, not `ch[cn].gold` - so `World::give_char_item` was widened from `pub(crate)` to `pub` (matching its `give_char_item_smart` sibling) for `ugaris-server`'s `apply_guiwynn_events` to call directly, and a new local `create_money_item_sprite` helper (full ladder including the C-exact `amount == 1`/`2` special sprites) plus manual `value`/`sprite`/`description` overrides on the `ZoneLoader`-instantiated "money" template item replicate `create_money_item` exactly; both rewards are gated on `QuestCompletion::times_done == 1` (C's `if (tmp == 1)`), resolved entirely inside `apply_guiwynn_events`'s `QuestDone` handler (no separate outcome event needed, unlike `world::lydia`'s two-step `QuestDone`+`GrantPotion` split, since guiwynn's reward doesn't depend on any `World`-only fact `ugaris-server` couldn't otherwise see). Server-side wiring extends `crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs` (`guiwynn_player_facts`/`apply_guiwynn_events`, the second `apply_*_events` in this file - after `apply_lydia_events` - to take a `&mut ZoneLoader` parameter) and `tick_npc/area1.rs` (`guiwynn_driver_69`, registered at the end of `tick_npc::run_all`). 18 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/guiwynn.rs` (entry silent-gate/greet-and-advance, key-grant-when-missing/skip-when-carried, both reminder-state gate/silence pairs, the quest8-already-done silent skip-to-11, quest8-not-done quest-open, the terminal reminder-only state, both quest turn-ins including item-sweep/state-advance, the out-of-range give-back fallback, and all three text-repeat reset buckets). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated (`james_driver`/`balltrap_skelly_driver`/`logain_driver` now the only named still-unported drivers, plus the rest through `ch_driver`'s dispatch table). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2470 ugaris-core [+16] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with no panic.

New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/balltrap.rs` (`BalltrapDriverData`/`CDR_BALLTRAP` added to `character_driver.rs` plus the new `CharacterDriverState::Balltrap` variant and its three exhaustive-match update sites in `character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`). `ch_died_driver`'s `CDR_BALLTRAP` case dispatches to the empty no-op `balltrap_skelly_dead`, so no death hook was needed. Server-side `balltrap_driver_71` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area1.rs`, registered in `tick_npc::run_all`) is the simplest area-1 registration yet: a single `world.process_balltrap_actions(config.area_id)` call, no `zone_loader`/DB/achievement plumbing. Confirmed against live zone data (`above1.map`) that only 1 of this NPC's 3 map placements actually has an adjacent `balltrap` item to fire (the other 2 simply no-op `do_use` every 3 seconds, matching C's own `if (do_use(...)) return;` fallthrough - not a bug in either the C source or this port). 9 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/balltrap.rs` (return-to-post walk, fire-after-3-seconds with `last_fire` update, no-fire-before-3-seconds, unconditional `last_fire` bump even when no item is present to fire at, `NT_GOTHIT` victim tracking plus adjacent-attack, and the group-equality self-defense gate). `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated: only `logain_driver` (`CDR_LOGAIN`, a large dialogue quest chain) remains unported in this file's `ch_driver` dispatch table. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2494 ugaris-core [+9] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with no panic.

`james_raisehint`'s full weighted-priority computation (offense/defense/immunity/flash/fire/pulse/warcry/warrior-skill blocks plus the misc-value loop, ~40 repeated `raise[v] += 1.0/(raise_cost(...)*k)` lines factored through two small `james_add_raise`/`james_add_base_raise` helpers to cut the transcription-error surface) and its four advice-tier messages plus balance-summary line are ported as `James_raisehint_advice`, reusing newly-`pub(crate)`-visibility-bumped `character_values::skill_base_attributes`/`values::skill_display_name` (previously private helpers already correct from earlier `update_char`/`/values` work) instead of duplicating either table. **Deliberately not ported** (documented gap, not silently dropped - see the module's own doc comment): the `CF_GOD`-only "raise me" debug command (`answer_code` 10) and its `james_create_eq`/`james_create_armor_piece`/`james_create_weapon`/`james_create_equipment` equipment-grant tail, plus `james_raisehint`'s own `doraise == 1` tail (the threshold loop's `raise_value` branch and the "Set professions" auto-learn block, which would need a whole new `prof[].base`/`.step`/`free_prof_points` subsystem nothing else in this codebase needs yet) - this command is not reachable by any real player and is orthogonal to the reachable-by-everyone "buy advice" path, which is fully ported. `has_empty_inventory`/`take_money` are small private per-NPC copies matching every sibling NPC's own established convention (`world::gatekeeper::gate_take_money`'s precedent) rather than new shared primitives. Unlike Lydia/Guiwynn, James needs no `ZoneLoader` or achievement wiring at all (no equipment grant, no quest completion - James only *opens* `QLOG_LYDIA` at state 0, mirroring Lydia's own state-0 greeting, never completes it), making `apply_james_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area1.rs`) the simplest `apply_*_events` function in the file (three plain events: `UpdateState`/`SetStorageHint`/`QuestOpen`, no `world`/`ZoneLoader`/DB parameter at all). Server-side wiring: `james_player_facts`/`apply_james_events` in `area1.rs`, `james_driver_70` in `tick_npc/area1.rs` (registered at the end of `tick_npc::run_all`, following the established "port order, not C dispatch order" numbering convention this file already uses). 21 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/james.rs` (state-0 greet/quest-open, the lydia-state-6 skip-ahead's no-`didsay` silence, the hardcore-invite's unconditional co-occurrence with the greeting, state 2's silent-yet-advancing quirk, both state-3 branches, the storage-hint fire-once/already-set pair, both text-repeat in/out-of-range branches, the advice-fee quote for a low-level player vs. the high-level decline, buy-advice's insufficient-funds decline, a full buy-advice-charges-money-and-produces-a-raise-hint round trip using a single-nonzero-value character to make the weighted computation's dominant entry deterministic, the nothing-raisable "stays completely silent, not even the closing line" `mr == 0` early-return, the hardcore-rules text, the accept-rules paid/unpaid and successful-transformation branches, the raiseme command's documented no-op, and the generic give-back). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2488 core [+18 net, 21 new minus test-count overlap from the shared greeter cross-reference tests already counting `CDR_JAMES` reads] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1091 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 REMAINING note updated: `james_driver` is now ported; `balltrap_skelly_driver`/`logain_driver`/the rest of `ch_driver`'s dispatch table remain the only named still-unported area-1 drivers.

I'M SUPPOSED TO BE IMMORTAL!" line already ported for `CDR_GATE_WELCOME`/`CDR_DUNGEONMASTER` - added as one generic `apply_area1_quest_giver_death_from_hurt_event` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs`, wired into `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events`), same immortal-so-unreachable-in-practice caveat (every one of these ten NPC templates carries `CF_IMMORTAL` in the live zone data, confirmed by grep); `balltrap_skelly_dead` needed no port (empty C no-op, already noted). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tutorial.rs` (`World::process_tutorial_hints`/`process_tutorial_hint`/`tutorial_torch_hint`) ports all 17 throttled hint branches: the login-window welcome, the Lydia/thief quest-direction hints, the three-way dark-tile torch sub-system (light/equip/create-and-grant, reusing `world::npc::area1::robber`'s `instantiate_item_template`-then-insert pattern), the area-1-only battle-prep pair (warrior Warcry vs. mage Bless/Shield plus a one-time-only Lightning-Flash hint), shop/chest/cursor-item-too-long/skill-raise-available, and the "todays hint" generic tail (potion F-key scan, shift/ctrl/left-click/chat/channel-chat/raise-strategy). New `World::queue_player_special`/`drain_pending_player_specials` (`world/text.rs`, a `WorldPlayerSpecial` single-character `SV_SPECIAL` send - the missing sibling of the existing area-broadcast `WorldSoundSpecial`) backs the two torch hints' UI-highlight packets, wired through a new `send_pending_world_player_specials` drain in `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/mod.rs`/`tick_sync.rs`. Since `World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime` (the same split as `world::gatekeeper`/`world::lydia`), a new `PlayerRuntime::tutorial: TutorialPpd` field (17 `_cnt`/`_last` counter pairs plus `timer`/`citem_start`, plain `#[serde(default)]`, no migration) is snapshotted/applied by new `crates/ugaris-server/src/tutorial.rs::tutorial_player_facts`/`apply_tutorial_outcomes`, wired into `main.rs`'s tick loop right after the pre-existing autobless/autopulse block. Two C-oracle quirks preserved rather than "fixed" (see `world::tutorial`'s module doc comment for the full reasoning): the mage Lightning-Flash hint's `ticker`-vs-wall-clock-epoch unit-mismatch bug (fires at most once ever per character - reproduced via `battle2_cnt == 0` rather than an epoch this codebase's tick-relative clock has no equivalent of), and the "create a torch" sub-branches' unconditional `torch_last`/`timer` reset but conditional `torch_cnt` bump (re-derived by the applier from the pre-update stamp). 19 tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/tutorial.rs`, 4 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/tutorial.rs`. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 1 task is now `[x]` - every `ch_driver` NPC, every death hook, and the tutorial system are all ported. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace` (2531 ugaris-core [+19] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1098 server [+4], all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild), `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- 2026-07-07: started the P0.5 "Area-text color markers" task. Root cause: C's `say`/`quiet_say`/etc. dialogue embeds a raw `COLOR_MARKER` byte (`\xb0`) around keywords (e.g. `COL_LIGHT_BLUE "repeat" COL_RESET`), which is not a valid standalone UTF-8 byte and so cannot round-trip through the `String`-typed `WorldAreaText`/`log_text.rs` message pipeline (`say_message`/`quiet_say_message`/etc. take `&str`); every prior NPC module that needed a colored keyword documented the drop as a deviation instead of fixing it. Initially attempted changing `WorldAreaText.message` from `String` to `Vec<u8>` directly (matching a naive read of the task), but that broke 300+ existing test call sites across ~15 test files that assert `text.message.contains("...")`/`== "..."` against a `String` - reverted that in favor of the codebase's own established low-blast-radius precedent: `WorldSystemText`/`WorldSystemTextBytes` are already byte-native *siblings* of a String-typed struct, not a replacement, and `system_text`/`system_text_bytes` already both exist in `ugaris-protocol`. Applied the same sibling pattern here: (1) `crates/ugaris-core/src/text.rs` gained `COL_STR_RESET`/`COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE`/etc. - `&str` sentinel constants using Unicode Private-Use-Area codepoints `U+E0C0..=U+E0D2` (never legal game text) mapped 1:1 to the existing byte-native `COL_*` color codes c0-c18, plus `expand_color_sentinels(&str) -> Vec<u8>` which walks the string's `char`s and expands sentinels back to the raw `\xb0c<N>` byte sequence (everything else re-encoded as normal UTF-8). This lets a plain `&str`/`format!()` dialogue literal carry color "through" the type system without ever containing an actual invalid byte. (2) `crates/ugaris-core/src/log_text.rs`'s six message builders (`say_message`/`shout_message`/`holler_message`/`emote_message`/`whisper_message`/`quiet_say_message`) now expand sentinels via a new private `sanitize_formatted` helper instead of a bare `.as_bytes()`, so any caller that embeds a `COL_STR_*` sentinel in its format string gets real marker bytes in the returned `Vec<u8>` - `sanitize_log_bytes` was already confirmed (no change needed) to preserve `LEGACY_COLOR_MARKER = 0xb0` unmangled while treating the following ASCII digit/letter bytes as ordinary graphic characters. (3) `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/text.rs` gained `WorldAreaTextBytes` (byte-native sibling of `WorldAreaText`, same 4 fields with `message: Vec<u8>`) plus `World::npc_quiet_say_bytes` (byte-native sibling of `npc_quiet_say`, pushes to a new `pending_area_text_bytes` queue instead of lossily `String::from_utf8_lossy`-converting) and `World::drain_pending_area_text_bytes`; the existing `npc_say`/`npc_quiet_say`/`npc_shout`/`npc_emote`/`npc_murmur`/`npc_whisper` and `WorldAreaText`/`pending_area_texts` are completely unchanged, so none of the ~300 existing test assertions needed touching. Only `npc_quiet_say_bytes` was added (not all six `_bytes` siblings) since it is the only one any currently-documented deviation site actually needs; add more siblings on demand as later slices need `npc_say_bytes`/etc. (4) `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/mod.rs` gained `send_pending_world_area_text_bytes` (byte-native sibling of `send_pending_world_area_texts`, using the pre-existing `ugaris_protocol::packet::system_text_bytes`), wired into `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_sync.rs`'s sync phase right after the existing area-text drain. Restored the actual color as the worked example: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area1/camhermit.rs`'s `CAMHERMIT_STATE_QUEST1DO`/`QUEST2DO` reminder line (`gwendylon.c:806-808,895-897`, `"...to " COL_LIGHT_BLUE "repeat" COL_RESET " mine words?"`) now builds its `reminder` string with `{COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE}repeat{COL_STR_RESET}` sentinels and calls `npc_quiet_say_bytes` instead of `npc_quiet_say`; module doc comment updated to point at the mechanism instead of documenting a drop. Updated `camhermit_quest1do_reminds_after_sixty_seconds_without_enough_kills` (the only existing test asserting on this specific line) to drain `pending_area_text_bytes` instead of `pending_area_texts` and assert both the plain text and the literal `\xb0c4repeat`/`\xb0c0 ` marker byte windows are present. 2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/text.rs` (sentinel-expansion round-trip, plain-text passthrough) and 1 new test in `crates/ugaris-core/src/log_text.rs` (`quiet_say_message` end-to-end sentinel expansion). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the mechanism is built and proven end-to-end on one NPC; ~12+ other documented/undocumented deviation sites (`gwendylon.rs`/`greeter.rs`/`yoakin.rs`/`jessica.rs`/`reskin.rs`/`lydia.rs`/`james.rs`/`guiwynn.rs`/`logain.rs`/`brithildie.rs`/`npc/trader.rs`/`npc/bank.rs`'s `BANK_QA`/`area32/military.rs`) still need their `npc_say`/`npc_quiet_say` call sites switched to the byte-native siblings with sentinels, same pattern as this slice - each is independently self-contained and can be done one at a time in future iterations. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 ugaris-core [+3] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1098 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with no panic.
- 2026-07-07: continued the P0.5 "Area-text color markers" task: restored the C `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_LIGHT_RED`/`COL_LIGHT_GREEN`/`COL_RESET` keyword markers at the remaining 12 documented/undocumented deviation sites via the `COL_STR_*` sentinel + `_bytes` sibling mechanism iteration 219 built. Area-1 NPCs (`gwendylon.rs`'s four `_WAIT` skull reminders; `greeter.rs`'s "learn"/two "repeat" lines; `yoakin.rs`'s state-4 reminder; `jessica.rs`'s quest1/quest2 reminders; `reskin.rs`'s state-3 reminder; `lydia.rs`'s state-4 reminder; `james.rs`'s hardcore-invite `COL_LIGHT_RED`+`COL_LIGHT_BLUE` nested line plus "advice"/"buy advice"; `guiwynn.rs`'s two undocumented state-5/state-8 reminders; `logain.rs`'s undocumented state-5 reminder; `brithildie.rs`'s undocumented "Repeat all" reminder) all follow the exact `camhermit.rs` precedent: `npc_quiet_say` -> `npc_quiet_say_bytes` at the call site, `{COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE}word{COL_STR_RESET}` sentinels in the `format!` literal, module doc comment updated from "dropped" to "restored", and every affected test's `drain_pending_area_texts()` -> `drain_pending_area_text_bytes()` plus a `text.message.windows(N).any(|w| w == b"\xb0cK...")` byte-marker assertion (contiguous-substring assertions spanning a marker, e.g. "Hardcore character?"/"account...Bank?", were split into two `&&`-joined `contains` checks since `String::from_utf8_lossy`'s replacement-char handling breaks a literal span crossing the raw marker bytes). Two sites needed more than a call-site swap: `npc/trader.rs`'s "help"/"accept trade"/"stop trade"/"show trade" NPC-speech lines used the same mechanical swap, but its separate `NT_GIVE` "gave me:" cross-notify (a *private system-text* line, not NPC area speech, rendered in `ugaris-server`'s `world_events/npc_events.rs::apply_trader_events`) needed the already-existing-but-unused `World::queue_system_text_bytes`/`expand_color_sentinels` pair instead, called directly since that call site has no `World` method wrapper to route through. `npc/bank.rs`'s `BANK_QA` table answer for `"account"` is a `'static str` table literal (not a runtime `format!`), so its three embedded keywords use raw `\u{E0C4}`/`\u{E0C0}` escapes (the same codepoints `COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_STR_RESET` expand to - `concat!` can't reference a `const &str`) instead of the sentinel constant names; since `BANK_QA`'s shared reply-rendering loop (`process_bank_actions`) pushes *every* QA answer through one `Vec<String>` regardless of whether it carries color, a new `crate::text::has_color_sentinels(&str) -> bool` helper lets that one loop pick `npc_quiet_say_bytes` vs. plain `npc_quiet_say` per-reply without duplicating it or forcing every other (uncolored) `BANK_QA` answer onto the bytes path. `bank.rs`'s own greeting line ("Would you like to open an account...") got the same direct sentinel-in-`format!` treatment as every other NPC's greeting. REMAINING: only `area32/military.rs` - a much larger slice with 20+ `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` call sites scattered across `process_military_master_actions`/`_messages`, `process_military_advisor_actions`, mission-text rendering (`describe_mission_text`/`offer_missions_text`), and `offer_favor`/`adv_favor_desc`/`adv_introduction_text` in a file already at 3,598 lines - left for a dedicated future slice per its own checkbox note. `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1098 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild (no new test functions added, only new assertions extending existing ones). `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics.
- 2026-07-08: closed the P0.5 "Area-text color markers" task with its last remaining site, `src/module/military.c`'s Military Master/Advisor NPCs (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/military.rs` + `crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`). Read the full C file (2881 lines) and cross-referenced every `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_MAUVE`/`COL_RESET` occurrence (27 call sites total, confirmed by full-file grep) against its Rust port: the `COL_MAUVE` "Grats:" rank-promotion broadcast (`complete_mission`, C line 1427) was already byte-native from an earlier iteration - no change needed. Restored all 26 remaining `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` sites via the established `COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_STR_RESET` sentinel + `_bytes` sibling mechanism, split across two files: **core** (`world/npc/area32/military.rs`) - the pure text-building functions that return `String`/enum-outcome fields consumed by the server crate: `describe_mission_text` (wraps the difficulty word, C `get_colored_difficulty_name`/`describe_mission`, also flows through `offer_missions_text`'s per-slot list and the Advisor's specific-mission preview - one fix, three call sites covered), `adv_introduction_text` (wraps "favor" in all 4 rotating greetings, using raw `\u{E0C4}`/`\u{E0C0}` escapes since the match arms return `&'static str` literals, matching `bank.rs`'s established literal-table convention), `adv_favor_desc_lines` (wraps all 5 favor-size words plus the two "easy demon"/"insane mining" example phrases), `handle_mission_request`'s advisor-recommendation "saying X" prompt and the reroll-footer line, and `process_advisor_recommendation`'s "Say X to accept this mission" followup. **Server** (`crates/ugaris-server/src/military.rs`) - every `format!`+`npc_quiet_say` call site building or forwarding one of the above, or embedding its own literal keyword directly (not routed through a core pure-text helper): both `GreetPlayerOutcome::HasActiveMission`/`HasRank` branches ("hear"/"failed", "mission"/"reroll"), the `master_state == 1` rank-follow-up ("mission"), `MissionRequestOutcome::AlreadyHasMission` ("hear"), `AcceptMissionOutcome::AlreadyHasMission` ("hear"), both `apply_military_master_failed` branches ("mission", only the second "another mission" occurrence - the first "did not take any" branch's own "mission" is also colored per C but the two branches use different surrounding text), `apply_military_master_hear`'s no-mission branch ("mission"), `MissionRerollOutcome::ConfirmationRequested` ("reroll"), `adv_introduction_text`/`adv_favor_desc_lines`/`describe_mission_text`-forwarding call sites switched from `npc_quiet_say` to `npc_quiet_say_bytes`, `OfferFavorOutcome::Offered` and `SpecificMissionRequestOutcome::Offered` (both "Say pay if you want it." lines, C `military.c:556`/`:2375`). Confirmed via direct C source reads that several superficially-similar lines are genuinely *not* colored and left untouched: `display_mission_text` (C `display_mission`, the accept/hear-time "Your mission is to..." description - no `COL_` at all, unlike its offer-time sibling `describe_mission`), the admin-only `/info` quest-statistics dump (C `military.c:2062`/`:2737` read `diff_name[n]` directly, not `get_colored_difficulty_name`), `process_favor_payment`'s two success lines ("Excellent! I've arranged..."/"Alright, I'll mention..."), and every plain "already completed today"/"not enrolled"/gate-rejection line. 3 tests updated `assert_eq!`/`.contains(...)` calls to expect the sentinel-wrapped text (`describe_mission_text_renders_each_mission_type`'s 3 exact-match assertions, `handle_mission_request_generates_and_offers_missions_for_enrolled_player`'s reroll-footer contains-check, `handle_mission_request_advisor_recommendation_short_circuits`/`process_advisor_recommendation_specific_mission_regenerates_and_prompts_accept`/`_difficulty_text_falls_through_to_insane`'s "saying X"/"Say X to accept" prompts, `adv_introduction_text_rotates_by_storage_id_modulo_four`'s two "favor" contains-checks, `adv_favor_desc_lines_matches_c_text`'s exact-match line 0) - no new test functions needed since every colored branch already had test coverage from the mission-system porting iterations; only the string literals being asserted against changed. This closes the "Area-text color markers" P0.5 task entirely - all 13 originally-listed deviation sites (iteration 219's `camhermit.rs` worked example, the 12-site iteration, and this final `military.rs` slice) are now restored end-to-end. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1098 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with no panic.
- 2026-07-08: worked the P0.5 "Retire legacy blob writes" task's write-path slice (backfill migration deferred - see `PORTING_TODO.md` REMAINING note; `sqlx::migrate!` only runs plain SQL, and decoding `ppd_blob`/`subscriber_blob` needs full `PlayerRuntime`/`AccountAchievements`/`AccountDepotState` Rust logic across dozens of typed block layouts, so it needs a one-off Rust startup routine, not a `.sql` file - not started this iteration). Stopped writing the legacy columns entirely (froze them, rather than overwriting with empty bytes, to avoid destroying pre-0020 read-fallback data on the rare `player_state_json` serialize-failure edge case): `crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs` - `ppd_blob = $32, subscriber_blob = $33` dropped from `SAVE_CHARACTER_BACKUP_SQL`/`SAVE_CHARACTER_LOGOUT_SQL`'s `SET` list (placeholders renumbered, e.g. `player_state_json` now `$32` not `$34`), `CharacterSaveRequest` struct lost its `ppd_blob`/`subscriber_blob` fields (the load-side `CharacterSnapshot` struct is unchanged - `LOAD_CHARACTER_SNAPSHOT_SQL` still reads both columns as the pre-0020 fallback), `bind_character_snapshot` no longer binds them; the two `save_queries_keep_legacy_area_guard_shape` SQL-shape assertions updated to the new placeholder numbers and to assert the columns are *absent*. `crates/ugaris-server/src/snapshots.rs` - the three save builders (`character_save_request`/`character_area_transfer_save_request`/`character_backup_save_request`) no longer call `encode_legacy_ppd_blob`/`encode_legacy_*_subscriber_blob`; `apply_character_snapshot`'s pre-0020 read-fallback branch is unchanged (still decodes `ppd_blob`/`subscriber_blob` from the loaded snapshot), now under one `#[allow(deprecated)]`. `world_events/{admin_events,npc_events}.rs` - 7 offline-mutation `CharacterSaveRequest` literals (clan rank set/fire, rename, etc.) dropped their now-nonexistent `ppd_blob: snapshot.ppd_blob`/`subscriber_blob: snapshot.subscriber_blob` echo-back fields (`player_state_json: None` + SQL `coalesce` already fully preserves state on these offline saves; the removed blob echo-back was a no-op even before this change once 0020 rolled out, since the columns weren't being read anymore for those rows). `crates/ugaris-db/tests/postgres_roundtrip.rs` updated to match. Marked `#[deprecated(note = "read-fallback for pre-migration-0020 rows only")]`: `PlayerRuntime::decode_legacy_ppd_blob` (`ugaris-core/src/player/mod.rs`), `decode_legacy_account_depot_subscriber_blob` (`ugaris-server/src/depot.rs`), `decode_legacy_achievement_data_subscriber_blob`/`decode_legacy_achievement_stats_subscriber_blob` (`ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) - all four remain fully functional, called only from `apply_character_snapshot`'s fallback branch now. The four now-write-path-unreferenced `encode_legacy_*` functions (`encode_legacy_account_depot_subscriber_blob`, `encode_legacy_achievement_data_subscriber_blob`, `encode_legacy_achievement_stats_subscriber_blob`, plus their two shared helpers `write_legacy_subscriber_block`/`account_depot_has_items` and `encode_legacy_achievement_data`/`encode_legacy_achievement_stats`) got `#[allow(dead_code)]` with a doc comment - they're reachable only from tests now (round-trip coverage of the still-live decoders' byte layout), not from any production save path; `PlayerRuntime::encode_legacy_ppd_blob` needed no such attribute since it's a `pub fn` (public API, not subject to the `dead_code` lint). Added `#![allow(deprecated)]` to `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/tests/mod.rs` and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/mod.rs` (both test-module roots) rather than sprinkling `#[allow(deprecated)]` across ~50 individual test call sites that deliberately still exercise the deprecated decoders directly to lock down the pre-0020 byte layout. Rewrote `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/login.rs`'s `character_save_request_encodes_runtime_ppd_and_carried_items` (renamed `..._encodes_runtime_state_and_carried_items`) to assert against the deserialized `PersistedPlayerState`/`player_state_json` instead of the now-removed `request.ppd_blob`/`request.subscriber_blob` fields; incidentally fixed a pre-existing wrong-slot assertion in the process (`decoded_depot.slots[0]` -> `slots[4]`, matching the actual `depot.slots[4] = Some(stored)` the test sets up - the old legacy-blob round trip happened to still pass with the wrong index, the direct JSON assertion does not). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1098 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings on a clean rebuild (`cargo build --workspace --tests` also zero warnings, including the non-test `cargo build -p ugaris-server` path where the new `#[allow(dead_code)]` markers matter). `cargo test -p ugaris-db -- --ignored` (live testcontainers Postgres) green. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` left `[~]` with a REMAINING note describing the backfill-migration follow-up.
- 2026-07-08: closed the P0.5 "Retire legacy blob writes" task with its final piece, the backfill startup routine. `crates/ugaris-db/src/character.rs` - added two `CharacterRepository` trait methods: `find_legacy_blob_only_characters` (new `LegacyBlobRow { character_id, ppd_blob, subscriber_blob }` struct, `select id, ppd_blob, subscriber_blob from characters where player_state_json is null and (ppd_blob <> ''::bytea or subscriber_blob <> ''::bytea)`) and `backfill_player_state_json(character_id, value)` (`update characters set player_state_json = $1 where id = $2 and player_state_json is null` - the `is null` guard is defense in depth since this only ever runs once at startup before any session exists). `LegacyBlobRow` re-exported from `ugaris-db`'s `lib.rs`. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/legacy_backfill.rs` (`mod legacy_backfill;` in `main.rs`, no `pub(crate) use` re-export needed - called via one explicit `legacy_backfill::backfill_legacy_player_state(&characters)` path, same convention as `tick_client_actions`/`tick_item_use_*`): fetches every stale row, replays `snapshots.rs::apply_character_snapshot`'s exact pre-0020 fallback decode sequence (`decode_legacy_account_depot_subscriber_blob`/`decode_legacy_achievement_data_subscriber_blob`/`decode_legacy_achievement_stats_subscriber_blob`/`PlayerRuntime::decode_legacy_ppd_blob`, all under one `#[allow(deprecated)]`) against a throwaway `PlayerRuntime::connected(0, 0)`, wraps the result through the existing `persisted_player_state_json` into a `PersistedPlayerState` document, and writes it back; a row that fails to decode (corrupt/short PPD block) or serialize is logged and left with `player_state_json` still `null` so the next startup retries it automatically, rather than aborting the whole scan. `main.rs` calls it once, right after `db.run_migrations().await?`, reusing the same `characters` repository handle for both the backfill and the rest of startup (no second `db.characters()` call). Added `[dev-dependencies] async-trait.workspace = true` to `ugaris-server/Cargo.toml` for a test-only `CharacterRepository` stub. 3 new unit tests in `legacy_backfill.rs` (no stale rows is a no-op, all-empty blobs still backfill a minimal document, a corrupt PPD blob is skipped and left for retry) plus 1 new live-DB test in `crates/ugaris-db/tests/postgres_roundtrip.rs` (`legacy_blob_only_rows_are_found_and_backfilled_exactly_once`, seeding a stale row, an already-migrated row, and an empty row, confirming the predicate finds only the stale one and that backfilling it removes it from future scans). Beyond the automated suite, manually verified against a real `docker compose up -d` Postgres: inserted a character with a genuinely corrupt 2-byte `ppd_blob` (confirmed skipped-and-retried across two consecutive server restarts, `player_state_json` still null both times) and one with only a garbage `subscriber_blob` (confirmed backfilled - `count=1` logged, account-depot/achievement decode failures there are silently `None`-tolerant per the existing C-oracle-matching decoder contract, only the PPD decoder's `bool` failure blocks a row). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server (3 new), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo test -p ugaris-db -- --ignored` (live testcontainers Postgres) green, 2 passed. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean. This closes the "Retire legacy blob writes" task entirely - `PORTING_TODO.md` now `[x]`.
- 2026-07-08: closed the P0.5 "`military.rs` (3.2K) split" task: mechanically split the 3,615-line `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/military.rs` (which had grown two full NPC drivers plus their shared mission-generation math into one file) into `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/military/{mod,missions,military_master,military_advisor}.rs` using `tools/rust_split/splitter.py`. Read the whole file first to categorize every top-level item: `missions.rs` (773 lines) got the rank system (`army_rank_for_points`/`ARMY_RANK_NAMES`/`give_military_pts*`) and every pure per-type mission generator/scaling helper shared by both NPCs; `military_master.rs` (1,300 lines) got `CDR_MILITARY_MASTER`'s own greet/offer/accept/complete/reroll flow, its `MilitaryMasterStorage(Registry)` quest counters, and `process_clan_recommendation`/`update_clan_points`; `military_advisor.rs` (1,006 lines) got `CDR_MILITARY_ADVISOR`'s paid-favor/specific-mission-recommendation sales economy and its own `MilitaryAdvisorStorage(Registry)`; `mod.rs` (554 lines) kept the shared `use` imports, the 44-row `MILITARY_QA` table (genuinely shared by both drivers' `analyse_text_qa` dispatch), and the full historical module doc comment (moved verbatim, not trimmed - it documents the whole module, not one driver). Wrote a name-keyed `ASSIGN` spec exploding the file's several distinctly-positioned-but-identically-named `impl World { ... }` blocks via the splitter's `EXPLODE_IMPLS = ["World"]` (qualifying each method as `World::method_name`, no collisions since every method name across all `impl World` blocks in this file is unique) plus whole-block assignment for every other `impl Foo`/struct/enum/fn/const by exact name (no `prefix`/`contains`/`re` rules needed - every one of the 105 top-level items got its own explicit exact-name entry, `DEFAULT_FILE` set to an obviously-wrong sentinel to catch any accidental omission via the tool's own unmatched-item report, which came back empty). One manual post-process step required: the tool always emits `mod x; ... pub use x::*;` *before* the collected `//!` module-doc/`use` items in `mod.rs`, which is a hard `E0753` compile error (inner doc comments must precede all other items in a module) - fixed with a small Python reordering pass moving the doc block above the `mod`/`pub use` lines once; not needed for the 3 leaf files (each got a short new `//!` header written by hand, `use super::*;` only follows it, no reordering needed there). `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area32/mod.rs`'s existing `pub mod military; pub use military::*;` needed no change (a directory `military/mod.rs` resolves identically to the old flat `military.rs`), and `character_driver.rs`'s `crate::world::npc::area32::military::{...}` import likewise needed no change. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2534 core + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all unchanged (pure code motion), zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings. 10s boot-smoke confirmed the tick loop running with NPC-driver logging and no panics.
- 2026-07-08: closed the P4 "Area 2" task: ported all four remaining character drivers from `src/area/2/area2.c` (`CDR_SUPERIOR`/`CDR_MOONIE`/`CDR_VAMPIRE`/`CDR_VAMPIRE2`, one file each under `world/npc/area2/`) plus their two death hooks (`vampire_dead_driver`/`vampire2_dead_driver`, completing quests 18/19 gated on `area3_crypt_state`). See the dedicated table row above for the full breakdown; highlights: `CDR_SUPERIOR`'s true-name stun mechanic and a documented `fight_driver_flee` simplification (flee-from-single-victim instead of C's full 9-direction light-weighted algorithm), `CDR_MOONIE` reusing the existing `simple_baddy_can_bless_friend`/`setup_bless_spell` primitives, `CDR_VAMPIRE`'s sun-amulet vulnerability window/fake-death mist escape, and `CDR_VAMPIRE2`'s `kill_char` substitution via `apply_legacy_hurt` with `armor_percent=0` and near-max damage (reaching the same `kill_character_followup` bookkeeping as a real C `kill_char` through the normal death path). `destroy_items_by_template_id` and the six `IID_AREA2_SUN*` constants were widened from `pub(crate)` to `pub` for the death hooks' first cross-crate (`ugaris-server`) callers. 19 new focused tests (`world/tests/superior.rs`/`moonie.rs`/`vampire.rs`/`vampire2.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2553 core [+19] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` now `[x]` for Area 2.
- 2026-07-08: started the P4 "Area 3" task: researched and documented the full 9-remaining-driver breakdown of `src/area/3/area3.c` (2,995 lines - `analyse_text_driver`'s `qa[]` table, `kassim_driver` engraving service, `seymour_driver`/`kelly_driver`/`astro2_driver`/`thomas_driver`/`sir_jones_driver`/`carlos_driver`/`supermax_driver` quest chains, `lampghost_driver` lamp-hunting AI) in `PORTING_TODO.md`'s task note, then ported the simplest driver, `astro1_driver` (ambient 15-state moon-telescope monologue, zero dialogue/quest/item interaction), establishing the `world/npc/area3/` NPC-file/`tick_npc/area3.rs`/immortal-death-hook scaffolding the remaining 9 drivers will reuse. See the dedicated table row above for the full breakdown. 5 new focused tests (`world/tests/astro1.rs`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2558 core [+5] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 3 left `[~]` with the full per-driver remaining-work breakdown.
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `thomas_driver` (`area3.c:1682-1823`, the crypt entrance guard - a single `crypt_state` `0`->`1` greeting gated on `ch[co].level > 18`) and `sir_jones_driver` (`:1830-2065`, the sixteen-state (`0`-`15`) crypt quest chain itself: greeting -> "Aye"/"Nay" choice, optionally sweetened to a 25-gold offer -> "go slay the toughest creature" -> external `CDR_VAMPIRE` death (already-ported `apply_vampire_death_from_hurt_event`) completes quest 18 and sets `crypt_state=10` -> "well done" + optional carried-money-item reward -> "an even tougher creature" -> external `CDR_VAMPIRE2` death completes quest 19 and sets `crypt_state=15`), one file each under `world/npc/area3/thomas.rs`/`sir_jones.rs` (`CDR_THOMAS`=35/`CDR_SIRJONES`=36, both new `CharacterDriverState` variants, both new `zone.rs` spawn-time driver-state initializers). Added `area3_ppd.crypt_bonus` (the one field between `crypt_state` and `clara_state` that had no accessor yet - `AREA3_PPD_CRYPT_BONUS_OFFSET = 8*4`, `area3_crypt_bonus`/`set_area3_crypt_bonus` in `player/area3.rs`). Built a new shared `AREA3_QA` table (`world/npc/area3/mod.rs`, C `area3.c:106-204`'s file-local `qa[]`) with only the 13 entries these two drivers actually interpret (the 7 canned greetings plus `repeat`/`restart`/`please repeat`/`please restart`(code 2)/`aye`(3)/`nay`(4)) - deliberately deferred the `list`(5)/`money`(6)/`shortcut to caligar`(7)/`explain`(9)/`engrave: `(8) entries and the ~86-entry `raise`/`lower` skill block (`V_WIS`..`V_RAGE` offsets) to whenever `kassim_driver`/`supermax_driver` are ported, rather than guessing the `V_*` skill-id mapping ahead of time; documented this scope boundary directly in the table's own doc comment. Two genuine C quirks reproduced verbatim in `sir_jones_driver`, both documented in the module doc comment: (1) `case 10`'s missing `break` falls through into `case 11`'s bare `ppd->crypt_state++`, so a single `NT_CHAR` visit at `crypt_state==10` double-increments straight to `12`, speaking only the `case 10` line; (2) `case 12`/`case 13` both call `say()` but never set C's `didsay = 1` (unlike every other talking case in this driver), so those two lines are spoken without refreshing `last_talk`/`current_victim`/`talkdir` - tested explicitly (`sir_jones_state12_jumps_silently_to_14_when_quest19_already_done`/`_opens_quest19_without_updating_last_talk_when_not_done` assert `last_talk` stays at its pre-call value). The crypt reward (`create_money_item(MONEY_AREA3_VAMPIRE1)` + plain `give_char_item`, not `give_char_item_smart`'s auto-gold-convert `IF_MONEY` branch) needed `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template`, which `World` doesn't have access to - new `SirJonesOutcomeEvent::GoldEarned` applied server-side in `apply_sir_jones_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area3.rs`, new file, mirroring `area1.rs`'s `apply_logain_events`'s identical mad-knight-quest-reward precedent exactly); `area1.rs::create_money_item_sprite` was widened from module-private to `pub(crate)` for this second caller rather than duplicating the C `create_money_item` sprite ladder. Extended `apply_area3_immortal_death_from_hurt_event`'s `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` array to include `CDR_THOMAS`/`CDR_SIRJONES` (both dispatch to the same `immortal_dead` no-op in C's `ch_died_driver`). New `tick_npc::area3::thomas_driver_78`/`sir_jones_driver_79` registered in `run_all` right after `astro1_driver_77`, matching C's `ch_driver` dispatch order (`astro2_driver` between them is still unported, so they sit adjacent). 17 new focused tests (`world/tests/thomas.rs` x5, `world/tests/sir_jones.rs` x12) covering both the happy-path state transitions and both documented C quirks. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2575 core [+17] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed both area 1 (unaffected) and area 3 (`placed_characters=156`, includes the new Thomas/Sir Jones NPCs) boot with "entering Rust game loop" and no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 3 left `[~]`; REMAINING note updated to describe the still-unported 7 drivers plus the deferred `AREA3_QA` entries.
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `astro2_driver` (`area3.c:1493-1675`, the lost-astronomer's-notes quest giver, `QLOG` 16). `CDR_ASTRO2 = 33` gets a new `CharacterDriverState::Astro2` variant plus `zone.rs` spawn-time init, mirroring `thomas.rs`'s `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split exactly: a four-state `0`-`3` `NT_CHAR` greeting chain opens `QLOG` 16 at state 0 and advances to state 4 ("idle, waiting for the notes"), `NT_TEXT` reuses the shared `AREA3_QA` table (`repeat`/`restart` reset the state to 0, same as `thomas`/`sir_jones`), and the `NT_GIVE` handler checks the new `IID_AREA2_ASTRONOTE` item id (`item_driver/ids.rs`, `0x000038`): handing it over while `astro2_state <= 4` sets state 5, destroys the NPC's held note plus any copies still on the player, and completes the quest via `Astro2OutcomeEvent::QuestDone` (first-completion-only `MONEY_AREA3_MOONIES` = 2500 reward as a literal carried money item, matching C's plain `give_char_item`, not the auto-gold-converting `give_char_item_smart`); handing over anything else (or the note after state 5) just gives it back with the stock "better use for this than I do" line. `Astro2OutcomeEvent::QuestDone`'s reward needs `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template`, so `apply_astro2_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area3.rs`) takes `&mut ZoneLoader`, same precedent as `apply_sir_jones_events`/`apply_logain_events`. Extended `apply_area3_immortal_death_from_hurt_event`'s `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` array to include `CDR_ASTRO2` (dispatches to the same `immortal_dead` no-op in C's `ch_died_driver`). New `tick_npc::area3::astro2_driver_80` registered in `run_all` between `astro1_driver_77` and `thomas_driver_78`, matching C's `ch_driver` dispatch order (`astro2_driver` sits between `astro1_driver`/`thomas_driver` in `area3.c`'s own `switch`). 6 new focused tests (`world/tests/astro2.rs`): greeting/quest-open, silent no-op at state 4, text-repeat reset, astronote hand-off completing the quest, non-astronote items handed back, and astronote handed back once already done (`astro2_state == 5`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2581 core [+6] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 3 left `[~]`; REMAINING note updated to describe the still-unported 6 drivers.
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `seymour_driver` (`area3.c:660-958`, the Seyan'Du Staff Sergeant greeting/army-enrollment quest chain, `QLOG` 10-12). `CDR_SEYMOUR = 24` (note: unlike every other area-3 driver id ported so far, this is C's actual fixed legacy numeric id from `drvlib.h`, not a sequential Rust-assigned one - `CDR_SEYMOUR`'s C value happens to be lower than `CDR_ASTRO1`'s 32) gets a new `CharacterDriverState::Seymour` variant plus `zone.rs` spawn-time init, mirroring `sir_jones.rs`'s `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split exactly: an eighteen-state (`0`-`17`) `NT_CHAR` chain (greeting opens quest 10 at state 0, seven lore lines, state 10 opens quest 11 with a rank-string-templated line unless quest 11 is already done (jumps straight to 12), states 12/13/14 collapse into one match arm reproducing C's `case 12`/`case 13` "`ppd->seymour_state = 14; // fall through intended`" double-fallthrough into `case 14`'s body verbatim, state 14 opens quest 12 unless already done (jumps to 16), state 16 hands off to Kelly), `NT_TEXT` reuses the shared `AREA3_QA` table with a from-scratch 5-bucket repeat/restart reset (C's `seymour_state` ladder covers the *entire* `0..=17` range exhaustively across 5 `if` blocks, unlike `sir_jones`'s/`thomas`'s partial-coverage `Option<i32>` pattern, so this one is a plain exhaustive `match`), and a 3-branch `NT_GIVE` handler keyed on item template id + exact `seymour_state`: `IID_AREA2_LOISANNOTE` (new item id, `item_driver/ids.rs`, `MAKE_ITEMID(DEV_ID_DB, 0x28)`) at state 15 completes quest 12 and awards 2 military points + 1 exp on first completion; `IID_AREA2_ZOMBIESKULL1` at state 9 completes quest 10 with no conditional points reward, but also runs C's `if (!get_army_rank_int(co)) { set_army_rank(co, 1); say("Welcome to the Imperial Army...") }` enrollment gate; `IID_AREA2_ZOMBIESKULL2` at state 11 completes quest 11 and awards 1 military point + 1 exp on first completion; anything else gets the stock "better use for this than I do" give-back. The enrollment gate is this driver's one genuine architectural gap: C's `set_army_rank` writes a separate `DRD_RANK_PPD` field directly, but Rust derives rank from `Character.military_points` via `army_rank_for_points` (cube-root formula, see that function's own doc comment, which already claimed - now inaccurately - that `give_military_pts_core`'s two call sites were the *only* `set_army_rank` call sites in the C tree; `seymour_driver` is a third, undocumented one, plus 6 more across `military.c`/`fdemon.c`/`command.c` not yet audited). Reproduced as a direct `character.military_points = 1` write (any value in `1..=7` derives to rank 1 under the cbrt formula, matching what `set_army_rank(co, 1)` would read back as) gated on `army_rank_for_points(military_points) == 0`, with no exp/hardcore-bonus/promotion-broadcast side effects - matching C's raw field write exactly, deliberately not routed through `give_military_pts_from_npc` (which the two *conditional-on-quest-completion* rewards above correctly do use, since C's own calls there are the real `give_military_pts(cn, co, pts, exps)` function, not a raw `set_army_rank`). `SeymourOutcomeEvent::LoisanNoteQuestDone`/`ZombieSkull2QuestDone` both carry `seymour_id` (the NPC's own character id) so `apply_seymour_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area3.rs`) can pass it as `give_military_pts_from_npc`'s `master_id` for the promotion-announcement text - same precedent as `military.rs`'s `master_id`-carrying `MilitaryMasterEvent`/`MilitaryAdvisorEvent` variants. Unlike Sir Jones's/Astro2's item rewards, Seymour's rewards are military points, not a carried item, so `apply_seymour_events` needs only `world`/`runtime`, no `ZoneLoader`. Extended `apply_area3_immortal_death_from_hurt_event`'s `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` array to include `CDR_SEYMOUR`. New `tick_npc::area3::seymour_driver_81` registered last in `run_all` (next sequential ordinal after `sir_jones_driver_79`/`astro2_driver_80`, no C dispatch-order constraint since C's own `switch` lists `seymour_driver` first among area 3's character drivers - the Rust ordinal numbering is a porting-order artifact, not a C fidelity requirement, same as every other `tick_npc` pass). 11 new focused tests (`world/tests/seymour.rs`): greeting/quest10-open, silent no-op at state 9, state-10 quest11-open vs. already-done skip-ahead, the 12/13-fallthrough-to-14 collapse (parametrized over both start states), the 5-bucket repeat/restart reset (parametrized over all 5 buckets), all three `NT_GIVE` item branches (Loisan note completion, zombie skull 1 completion + enrollment for both an unranked and an already-ranked player, zombie skull 2 completion), and the generic give-back fallback. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2592 core [+11] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 3 left `[~]`; REMAINING note updated to describe the still-unported 5 drivers (`kelly_driver`/`carlos_driver`/`kassim_driver`/`supermax_driver`/`lampghost_driver`).
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `kelly_driver` (`area3.c:984-1379`, the Seyan'Du Sergeant running area 3's biggest quest chain: park shrines/`QLOG` 13-14, reaching Clara/`QLOG` 15, swamp-beast-head bounties, and the Caligar-plaque hunt/`QLOG` 54/60). `CDR_KELLY = 30` gets a new `CharacterDriverState::Kelly` variant plus `zone.rs` spawn-time init, mirroring `seymour.rs`'s `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split exactly across a twenty-seven-state (`0`-`26`) `NT_CHAR` chain with four conditional single-case fallthroughs (`1`->`2` on `seymour_state>=16`, `13`->`14` on `level>=22`, `15`->`16` on `clara_state>=5`, `19`->`20` on `level>=56`); three of the four fallthrough targets (`2`/`16`/`20`) are never independently persisted (confirmed against every `kelly_state` write site including the `NT_TEXT` reset buckets) so are inlined directly into their source case, but `14` *is* independently reachable via the reset bucket `14..=15 -> 14`, needing a shared `kelly_case14_body` helper called from both the `13` (gated) and `14` (direct) match arms - a new precedent beyond `sir_jones.rs`'s simpler unconditional `10`->`11` single-level fallthrough. `case 9`'s per-shrine progress reads three new `PlayerRuntime` accessors (`area3_kelly_found1`/`_found2`/`_found3`/`area3_kelly_found_cnt`/`set_area3_kelly_found_cnt`, offsets already reserved by the existing `memorize_park_shrine` writer from the Area 2 port) and calls `World::give_military_pts_from_npc` directly (no outcome event needed - it only touches `Character` fields), while the `kelly_found_cnt` write itself needs `KellyOutcomeEvent::UpdateFoundCnt` since that's `PlayerRuntime`. `collect_heads` (`area3.c:965-982`, C's own local helper, not part of the `qa[]`-driven dialogue) sells every carried `IID_AREA15_HEAD` (new item id) for `125 + drdata[0]*75` silver each; both this and the plaque `NT_GIVE` reward use the `give_money_message`/inline-gold-add `World` precedent (`world::gwendylon` et al.) instead of a `ZoneLoader`-backed item reward, with a new `KellyOutcomeEvent::GoldEarned` applied server-side via `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` for the wealth-ladder half only (matching every other `GoldEarned` event in this codebase per `area1.rs`'s module doc comment). `case 24`'s conditional `IID_CALIGARLETTER` grant reuses a new `character_has_item_template` helper (C `has_item`'s full-inventory-plus-cursor scan) to decide whether to push `KellyOutcomeEvent::GrantCaligarLetter` (applied server-side via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template("caligar_letter", ...)`, same precedent as `apply_sir_jones_events`/`apply_astro2_events`). Added a `shortcut to caligar`(7) entry to the shared `AREA3_QA` table for `kelly_driver`'s own god-only `case 7` fast-forward to state 19 - the first entry added to that table since it was created, deliberately still deferring `list`/`money`/`explain`/`engrave: `/the skill-raise block. New `tick_npc::area3::kelly_driver_82` registered last in `run_all`. 24 new focused tests (`world/tests/kelly.rs`): greeting, both fallthrough-blocked/fallthrough-taken cases for all four conditional fallthroughs, state-14's direct-reachability regardless of level, shrine partial-progress point award without completion, all-three-shrines completion, already-at-three idempotency, head-selling gold award, quest-54/60 skip-ahead branches, letter-grant gate (present/absent), the 6-bucket `NT_TEXT` reset (including the 3 excluded transient states left untouched), the god-only shortcut (granted/ignored), and all three `NT_GIVE` branches (creeper head, plaque, generic give-back). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2616 core [+24] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 3 left `[~]`; REMAINING note updated to describe the still-unported 4 drivers (`carlos_driver`/`kassim_driver`/`supermax_driver`/`lampghost_driver`).
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `kassim_driver` (`area3.c:327-656`, Aston's jewelry engraver), closing the last quest-free NPC gap. New `CharacterDriverState::Engrave` variant (a player-side transient-state precedent, same shape as `ClanFoundData`) carries the pending inscription text; 2 new `PlayerRuntime` timer accessors, 1 new `AREA3_QA` entry (`explain`). Preserved a real C oddity verbatim: `NT_GIVE`'s `didsay` is only truthy for 3 of 6 sub-branches. 11 new tests; 2639 core + 1101 server tests pass, clean build/boot-smoke. Only `supermax_driver`/`lampghost_driver` remain for Area 3.
- 2026-07-08: Area 3: ported `supermax_driver` (`area3.c:2432-2594`, the past-maxes raiser), including its three helpers `supermax_list`/`supermax_raise`/`supermax_lower` (`area3.c:2328-2430`) and the shared C helpers `skillmax`/`supermax_canraise`/`supermax_cost` (`src/system/skill.c:103-172`, the latter two newly ported to `item_driver/scrolls.rs` as crate-visible siblings of the pre-existing `skillmax`/`raise_cost`, avoiding the divergent `legacy_supermax_canraise` already in `ugaris-server/src/commands_admin/skills.rs`). `CDR_SUPERMAX = 98` gets a new `CharacterDriverState::Supermax` variant plus `zone.rs` spawn-time init. Added 82 new `AREA3_QA` entries (`list`(5)/`money`(6) plus the 80-row `raise <skill>`/`lower <skill>` block, `answer_code` computed as `CharacterValue as i32 + 100/200` since `CharacterValue`'s numeric values already equal every C `V_*` index one for one - no separate lookup table needed). `ppd->supermax_state`/`supermax_gold` live on C's *global* `misc_ppd` struct (not the area-3-specific `area3_ppd`), so the new `PlayerRuntime` accessors (`supermax_state`/`set_supermax_state`/`supermax_gold`/`add_supermax_gold`, `MISC_PPD_SUPERMAX_STATE_OFFSET`/`MISC_PPD_SUPERMAX_GOLD_OFFSET`) sit in `player/settings.rs` alongside `swapped_at`/`complaint_date`, not `player/area3.rs`. Two real C oddities preserved verbatim: `supermax_raise` calls `update_char`/sets `CF_ITEMS` after bumping the skill, `supermax_lower` does neither; every driver-initiated line uses the *loud* `say()`, not `quiet_say` like most other area-3 drivers (only `supermax_list`'s `log_char` lines and the `CF_NOEXP` guard messages are private, via `World::queue_system_text`). Extended `apply_area3_immortal_death_from_hurt_event`'s `AREA3_IMMORTAL_DRIVERS` array to include `CDR_SUPERMAX`. New `tick_npc::area3::supermax_driver_85` registered last in `run_all`. 14 new focused tests (`world/tests/supermax.rs`): greeting-sequence 0->4 plateau, min-talk-window suppression, text `repeat` reset,
- 2026-07-08: closed the P4 "Area 3" task with its last and most architecturally distinct driver, `lampghost_driver`/`_respawn`/`_dead` (`area3.c:2631-2752`, the palace-light puzzle janitor). Ported the self-defense/aggressive-sighting cascade (same `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 1, 0)` shape as `CDR_SUPERIOR`) plus the nearest-lit-lamp claim/walk/extinguish job loop. C's `lamp[MAXLAMP]` registry's `cn`/`cost` claim fields become a new `World::area3_lamp_claims: HashMap<ItemId, (CharacterId, i32)>` (registration membership was already `Item::driver_data[6]`); cost ties broken by lowest `ItemId`, same precedent as `world::janitor`. `lampghost_respawn`'s `map[m].light > 4` gate lives in `ugaris-server`'s `spawns::respawn_npc_character`; `lampghost_dead`'s claim release is its own dedicated `apply_lampghost_death_from_hurt_event` hook (every sibling area-3 driver shares the `immortal_dead` no-op instead). See the dedicated table row above for the full breakdown. 10 new core tests (`world/tests/lampghost.rs`) + 3 server tests (2 respawn-gate, 1 death-hook claim-release). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2663 core [+10] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1104 server [+3], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` now `[x]` for Area 3. `list` with/without maxed skills (private system-text output), `money` gated on gold>0, successful raise (gold+exp charge, value bump, `CF_ITEMS`), raise rejected below skillmax/insufficient gold/insufficient exp/beyond-250 ceiling, successful lower (exp refund, no gold/`CF_ITEMS` touch), lower rejected at-or-below skillmax, and `/noexp` blocking both raise and lower with private messages. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2653 core [+14] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1101 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. Only `lampghost_driver` remains for Area 3.
- 2026-07-08: Area 4 (`src/area/4/pents.c`) STARTED: ported the full pentagram solve/reward pipeline, the biggest self-contained slice of this file. `World::pentagram_quest: PentagramQuestState` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/pents.rs`) now owns every file-static C global (`solve_serial`/`total_pentagrams`/`active_pentagrams`/`required_activations`/`power_levels[MAXLEVEL]`/`area_pentagram_counts`/`area_activated_counts`/`area_serials`/`training_power`/`last_solve_tick`/`pentagram_record*`) and the system-wide half of the pipeline: `activate_pentagram`/`deactivate_pentagram` (item mutation: `drdata[1]`/`sprite`/light/`mod_value[0]`/sound), `check_for_quest_completion`/`complete_pentagram_quest`'s `update_power_levels` half, and `calculate_required_pentagrams` (including its `areaID == 25`/`34` player-counting special cases and the `areaID == 21` required-activations-fixed-at-12 override). `initialize_pentagram_system`'s one-time level/color/count bootstrap runs lazily the first time a pentagram is used (`init_done`-equivalent `initialized` flag), scanning every live `IDR_PENT` item on `World`.
