Since `World` has no access to the session-owned `PlayerRuntime` that carries each player's `pentagram_debug: PentagramDebugData` (C `struct pentagram_player_data`, already modeled by iteration 188's pentagram-debug-command work), every activation is queued as a `PentagramActivationEvent` and drained every tick by a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/pents.rs::process_pentagram_activations` (wired into `tick_item_use_completion.rs` right after the item-use dispatch loop, the same post-dispatch-drain slot `tick_item_use_*`'s sibling modules use), which calls a new pure-function module `crates/ugaris-core/src/pentagram.rs` porting the entire per-player half: `add_pentagram_to_player`/`update_player_pentagram_stats`/`check_for_color_combo`/`handle_lucky_pentagram`/`log_pentagram_info`/`check_for_record` (record ownership tracked by character name instead of C's `ch[player_id].ID` persistent save-file identity, matching `World::arena_toplist`'s existing name-keyed precedent) and `distribute_rewards_to_player`'s full reward fan-out (exp award via the existing `give_exp`, clan-jewel reflection bonus, training-power percentage message, record-progress message) on a solve. All four reachable pentagram achievement call sites are wired through new `award_pentagram_*` helpers in `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`: `FIVE_IN_A_ROW` (five-of-a-color combo at solve time), `HAPPY_GO_LUCKY`/`FAVORED_BY_FORTUNE` (first/second lucky-pentagram hit in one solve run), and the unconditional `achievement_add_pents` stat bump for every reward-eligible player. `handle_demon_lord_door`'s access-time gate (`ticker - last_solve_time > TICKS * get_demon_lord_door_after_solve_access_time()`) was already wired via `ItemDriverContext::pent_last_solve_tick`/`pent_demon_lord_access_seconds`, now fed from `World::pentagram_quest.last_solve_tick` instead of a hardcoded default. 8 new core tests (`world/tests/pents.rs`) + 9 new pure-function tests (`pentagram.rs`) + 2 new server integration tests (`tests/pents.rs`, exercising the whole drain-and-apply pipeline end-to-end: a solo solver getting paid and an unsolved activation leaving no exp). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2680 core + 1106 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` left `[~]`: still unported are demon spawning (`spawn_demons_at_pentagram`/`enhance_elite_demon`/`adjust_lesser_demon`/`enhance_demon_character`, needed before `handle_pentagram_interaction`'s `spawn_count` branches do anything), the `CDR_PENTER` demon character driver (loot-table dispatch - the `pent_demon_{low,mid,high}[_elite]` JSON tables referenced by C don't exist under `ugaris_data/loot/` yet either - plus `update_demon_profession`) and `handle_demon_death`'s power-level-reduction/`DEMON_LORDS_DEMISE` achievement (both meaningless without spawning), the `CDR_TESTER` QA-only bot NPC (lowest priority), and `pentagram_record` DB persistence (in-memory only, like `World::arena_toplist`).
- 2026-07-08: Area 4 (`src/area/4/pents.c`) continued: ported demon spawning end-to-end, closing the "next slice" note from the previous entry. `World::pentagram_max_spawns`/`pentagram_spawn_slot_is_stale`/`roll_pentagram_demon_type`/`pentagram_demon_template_name` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/pents.rs`) port `spawn_demons_at_pentagram`'s planning half (max-spawns level threshold, per-slot occupancy check reading the pentagram item's `driver_data[6 + index*4 ..]` demon-id/serial pairs, the elite/lesser/normal type roll, and the `penter<level*2+RANDOM(2)>` template-name formula) as pure `World` mutations queuing a new `PentagramDemonSpawnRequest { item_id, spawn_count, level }` (both `apply_pentagram_activate`'s unconditional post-activation spawn and `apply_pentagram_timer`'s previously-unported reset-tail/occasional-single-demon-tick branches now push one). `World::enhance_elite_demon`/`adjust_lesser_demon`/`enhance_demon_character` (private, `enhance_demon_character` covers all 4 `priority_type` branches including C's genuine `V_FIREBALL`/`V_FIRE`-alias double-push bug in the magical pool, even though only `priority_type=0` is reachable from the one real call site) port the stat-multiplier/rename/tint/random-skill-boost machinery digit-for-digit. Since actually instantiating a `penterN` character needs `ZoneLoader` (which `World` doesn't have), `crates/ugaris-server/src/pents.rs::process_pentagram_demon_spawns` (new, called from `tick_item_use_completion.rs` right after `process_pentagram_activations`) drains the request queue and drives the C `for (index...) { if (stale) { roll; instantiate; drop; enhance; loot; record; } }` loop itself, calling back into `World::finish_pentagram_demon_spawn` (elite/lesser class reassignment, `update_char`, self-bless via the pre-existing `install_bless_spell`/`BLESS_DURATION` if the template carries `V_BLESS`, full-power hp/endurance/mana/lifeshield, facing, `CF_NONOTIFY`) and `World::apply_pentagram_spawn_result` (slot bookkeeping write-back) after each successful placement, plus `World::loot_apply_to_npc` with a level/elite-keyed table id (`World::pentagram_demon_loot_table_id`) - the referenced `pent_demon_{low,mid,high}[_elite]` JSON tables already exist under `ugaris_data/loot/pents/demons.json` (contrary to the previous entry's note that they didn't - they'd been added since), so no new data authoring was needed. Spawned demons get a new `CDR_PENTER = 64` driver id (`character_driver.rs`) rather than reimplementing NPC AI: `zone.rs`'s `instantiate_character_template` gained a `CDR_PENTER` branch identical to the pre-existing `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` one (pushes `NT_CREATE`, parses `ch.arg` via `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`), and the same 4 `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` gates `CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` previously widened (`world/npc_fight.rs:132,2562`, `world/npc_idle.rs:67,314`) now also accept `CDR_PENTER`, so every existing `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` self-defense/idle-wander tick pass (`tick_npc/area22.rs`) picks up `CDR_PENTER` demons for free - matching C's own `demon_character_driver`, whose only real logic beyond the loot/profession calls below is its own tail `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` call. `World::update_demon_profession` (C's other per-tick `demon_character_driver` piece, the `deaths`-seeded/`training_power`-scaled `prof[P_DEMON]` recompute) is ported but not yet wired to run every tick for live demons (a real but low-value remaining gap - it only affects a cosmetic profession display, not combat, and only matters for actual demon-lord-class templates, none of which `zones/4/pents.chr`'s own `penterN` entries use). `World::apply_penter_demon_death` (`handle_demon_death`: demon-lord-class-only `power_levels[]`/`training_power` reduction, `notify_power_change` text, and queuing a `DEMON_LORDS_DEMISE` award for a player killer) is hooked directly into `World::kill_character_followup` (not the `LegacyHurtEvent`/`death_hooks.rs` family, which only fires when a killer exists - C's `ch_died_driver` runs regardless), draining into a new `award_pentagram_demon_lords_demise_achievement` helper (`achievement.rs`) via `crate::pents::process_penter_demon_lords_demise_awards`. 16 new core tests (`world/tests/pents.rs`: spawn-request queuing from both activation and timer paths including the random-chance single-demon roll, max-spawns level threshold, slot staleness for empty/dead/mismatched-serial/live cases, spawn-result slot recording, elite/lesser stat-and-rename mutation, `finish_pentagram_demon_spawn`'s full-power/facing/class-reassignment behavior, `update_demon_profession`'s seed-from-existing and demon-lord-offset branches, and `apply_penter_demon_death`'s power-reduction/no-op-for-elite-lesser-ordinary/no-killer-still-reduces-power cases) + 2 new server integration tests (`tests/pents.rs`: an end-to-end activation-to-spawned-demon round trip through a real `ZoneLoader` template with predicted deterministic RNG rolls, and the demon-lords-demise achievement unlocking through a real `apply_legacy_hurt`-driven kill). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2696 core [+16] + 78 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: only the `CDR_TESTER` QA-bot NPC and `pentagram_record` DB persistence remain for Area 4 (see checkbox REMAINING).
- 2026-07-08: Area 4 (`src/area/4/pents.c`) continued: ported `pentagram_record`/`pentagram_record_holder` restart-persistence (`load_pentagram_record`/`save_pentagram_record_scheduled`, `src/system/database/database_pent_record.c`), closing item (4) of the previous entry's remaining list. Unlike every other repository in `ugaris-db` (which invents a JSON-blob schema for state C never persisted as a standalone table), C already has a dedicated relational table for this exact state, so the new `crates/ugaris-db/src/pentagram.rs::PgPentagramRecordRepository`/`migrations/0021_pentagram_record.sql` mirror it one-for-one: `area_id integer primary key, char_id integer not null default 0, char_name varchar(40) not null, record_count integer not null, record_date timestamptz`. `char_id` is always stored as `0` - this Rust port's in-memory `CharacterId` is a per-session allocation, not C's persistent save-file `ch[player_id].ID`, so (matching `World::arena_toplist`'s existing precedent, already noted on `PentagramQuestState::pentagram_record`'s own doc comment) record ownership is tracked by `char_name` only, which is all the repository round-trips. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/pents.rs::load_pentagram_record_at_startup` (called once in `main.rs` right before the game loop starts, alongside the pre-existing clan/military-storage startup loads) and `save_pentagram_record_scheduled` (an exact behavioral mirror of its C namesake - no "dirty" gate, since C has none either: it unconditionally re-saves the current in-memory `record`/`holder` whenever `record > 0`, on a fixed cadence, with no "did it actually change" check) cover two of C's three call sites: the periodic `add_scheduled_task(..., 3600 * 4, "PentagramRecords", 1)` cadence (a `world.tick.0 % (TICKS_PER_SECOND * 3600 * 4) == 0` check added directly in `main.rs`'s tick loop, right after the `tick_npc::run_all` call) and `/saveall`'s explicit call (`command.c:7470`, wired into `tick_client_actions::process_queued_client_actions`'s pre-existing `result.save_all_requested` arm, right after the merchant-store resave loop). The third C call site, `exit_database`'s unconditional shutdown flush, has no Rust equivalent yet - no repository in this codebase has a shutdown-save routine to mirror it against (a pre-existing gap, not new to this task). Deliberately did NOT thread a new `pentagram_record_repository` parameter through `tick_npc::run_all`'s ~150-function-signature pipeline (the established pattern every other periodic per-tick DB save in this codebase uses, e.g. `world_56`'s clan-registry save) - that blast radius (11+ files, every `tick_npc::*` function signature) is disproportionate for a system-wide, non-NPC, non-driver concern, so the periodic save is instead a standalone call directly in `main.rs`'s tick loop body, and the `/saveall` wiring only added one parameter to the single function that needed it (`process_queued_client_actions`), matching how `pents::process_pentagram_activations`/`process_pentagram_demon_spawns` are already called directly from `tick_item_use_completion.rs` rather than through `tick_npc`. `Database::pentagram_record()` constructor added to `crates/ugaris-db/src/lib.rs` alongside the others; the big startup repository-construction tuple in `main.rs` gained one more `Option<PgPentagramRecordRepository>` slot. 3 new `ugaris-db` tests (`pentagram.rs`: an SQL-shape unit test plus 2 live-DB round-trip tests under the established `DATABASE_URL`-gated `mod live` convention, verified passing against the local `compose.yaml` Postgres instance including the update-in-place upsert path). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2696 core + 81 db [+3] + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. `cargo test -p ugaris-db -- --ignored` clean (2 passed). Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics both with and without `DATABASE_URL` set (the DB-connected run also confirmed the new migration applies cleanly against the live dev Postgres instance). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: only the `CDR_TESTER` QA-bot NPC remains for Area 4 (see checkbox REMAINING).
- 2026-07-08: Area 4 (`src/area/4/pents.c`) CLOSED: ported `pentagram_tester_driver` (`CDR_TESTER = 77`, `pents.c:1789-1830`), the last remaining piece. Confirmed via a full-tree grep that `CDR_TESTER` is never referenced anywhere in C outside `ch_driver`/`ch_respawn_driver`'s dispatch cases - no `create_character`/zone-placement call site exists, though a `tester` character template (`driver=77`) does sit unplaced in `ugaris_data/zones/4/pents.chr`, confirming the existing ledger note that this is a QA-only bot, not player-facing. New `world/npc/area4/tester.rs` (`CharacterDriverState::Tester`/`TesterDriverData`) ports the full per-tick body: the cursor-item shuffle (stash a held potion in the backpack via direct `cursor_item`/`inventory[slot]` field moves, junk anything else via `World::destroy_item`, matching the `world::janitor` precedent for this class of C `swap()` substitution), item pickup/pentagram-use (`take_driver`/`use_driver` -> `do_take`/`do_use` via the exact `janitor_take_item`/`janitor_use_light` walk-then-act shape), self-defense (the same single-victim `NT_GOTHIT`-only model already established by `CDR_SANOA`/`CDR_ROBBER`), `regenerate_driver`/`spell_self_driver` (the existing `regenerate_simple_baddy`/`spell_self_simple_baddy` helpers, which take a bare `character_id` with no `SimpleBaddy`-state gate), `handle_tester_healing`, and the random-wander `handle_tester_movement`. Two documented simplifications/findings, both noted in the module's own doc comment: (1) C's `scan_item_driver`/message-notify item-sighting cache (`item_to_pickup`/`item_to_use`/the 10-slot `visible_pents[]` round-robin) is replaced by a direct nearest-visible-candidate scan of `World::items` each tick, the same class of simplification `world::janitor` already established - `tester_state->target_character` (set from `NT_CHAR` sightings of `CDR_PENTER` demons) is a genuine write-only dead field in C, never read anywhere else in the driver, so it isn't ported at all; (2) C's `handle_tester_item_management` is `void` and its internal early `return` (after queuing a take/use action) only exits that helper, not the outer `pentagram_tester_driver` - the outer driver does *not* check whether an item action was already queued before calling `fight_driver_attack_visible`/`regenerate_driver`/etc., which (since the underlying raw `do_walk`/`do_use`/`do_take` don't guard against clobbering an already-pending action) can silently let a later helper overwrite the queued action within the same C tick - almost certainly an unintentional C bug; this port instead follows the "return once anything queues an action" precedent already established by every sibling NPC in this codebase. A third finding: `handle_tester_healing` is provably unreachable in C's own per-tick order, since `regenerate_driver`'s `hp < max_hp` gate (checked first) is a strict superset of `handle_tester_healing`'s own `hp < max_hp * heal_threshold` gate (`heal_threshold <= 1.0`) - ported anyway for fidelity, not unit-tested standalone since no full-tick scenario can ever reach it. The pentagram-activation reward pipeline the tester's `use` action triggers needed no special-casing at all: `ugaris-server`'s `pents::apply_pentagram_activation` already no-ops its per-player reward half whenever `runtime.player_for_character_mut` finds no live session, which is exactly the tester's case. New `tick_npc::area4::tester_driver_87` registered last in `run_all`. 8 new core tests (`world/tests/tester.rs`): first-tick destination reroll-and-idle, `NT_GOTHIT` victim-tracking-and-attack plus the same-group no-op, ground-potion walk-then-pickup plus adjacent direct pickup (both needed `tile.light = 255`, matching `world::janitor`'s own `IF_TAKE` light-gate precedent), adjacent unsolved-pentagram use, an already-activated pentagram being skipped, and the healing-is-unreachable regen/idle case. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2704 core [+8] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with no panics across hundreds of ticks. `PORTING_TODO.md` now `[x]` for Area 4 - every checkbox before it (P0.5 through P3, plus Area 1-3) was already closed, so this also closes the entire P0.5-P4-so-far Area block up through Area 4.
- 2026-07-08: Area 6 (`src/area/6/edemon.c`) STARTED with a full audit rather than assuming the checkbox's own "Earth Demon boss driver (`CDR_EDEMON*` characters)" premise: confirmed via full-tree grep that C's own `ch_driver`/`ch_died_driver`/`ch_respawn_driver` in `edemon.c` are provably empty (`switch (nr) { default: return 0; }`, all three) - `CDR_EDEMON` is never defined anywhere in the C tree. `edemon2s`/`edemon6s` (`ugaris_data/zones/6/edemon.chr`) are plain `driver=7` (`CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`, already ported) NPCs with `flag=CF_EDEMON` and `V_ATTACK=0`/`V_PARRY=0` (so their combat skill always falls into `get_attack_skill`/`get_parry_skill`'s earth-demon fallback branch, `fight_skill * 3.5`). This means the whole task is "port every `CF_EDEMON` branch in shared combat code", not a new character driver - confirmed all 8 `IDR_EDEMON*` item drivers were already ported and extensively tested (see the many existing Area 6 entries above this one), and audited every one of the 9 C `CF_EDEMON` call sites (`grep -rn CF_EDEMON src/`) against the Rust tree: `act.c:498/501` (`sub_attack`'s hit-harder/hit-less-often diff bonus) - **not reachable**, see below; `tool.c:1214/1232` (`get_parry_skill`/`get_attack_skill`'s earth-demon fallback) - already ported as `attack_skill`/`parry_skill` in `attack.rs`, but only consumed by `npc_fight.rs`'s fight-driver task-scoring/`values.rs`'s `SV_SETVAL` display, not by the actual melee-resolution `do_action::act_attack` (a real, separate, pre-existing P1 gap, documented below); `effect.c:360` (`ef_fireball_set`'s earth-demon shoot-back) - already ported (`effect_tick.rs` fireball hit-earth-demon-shoots-back test); `effect.c:864` (`check_strike_near`'s earth-demon damage reduction against nearby ball/flash strikes) - **was unported, closed this iteration**; `drvlib.c:1722` (`fight_driver_task_scan`'s earthmud-task gate) - already ported (`setup_simple_baddy_earthmud_attack`/task-scoring in `npc_fight.rs`); `do.c:95` (`do_walk`'s earthmud-spell movement slowdown, earth demons exempt) - **was unported, closed this iteration**; `command.c:4676/4955` (the `/setflags`-style flag name<->string table) - already ported (`zone.rs`'s `"CF_EDEMON" => CharacterFlags::EDEMON`, `commands_admin/character.rs`'s flag-listing table). Closed the two real gaps: (1) `do_walk`'s earthmud slowdown was a *total* no-op before this change - the earthmud spell (`EF_EARTHMUD`, already creatable via `do_earthmud`/`World::create_earthmud_effect`) never actually slowed anyone down, since `do_action::movement_cost` had no effect-lookup at all. New `do_action::edemon_reduction(strength, current_demon_value) -> i32` (pure `max(0, strength - current_demon_value)`, C `tool.c:3171-3173`, shared helper - also doc-commented as the same primitive `sub_attack`'s still-unported diff bonus needs) and a new `earthmud_extra_cost: i32` parameter threaded through `do_action::do_walk`/`movement_cost` (pre-computed by the caller rather than looked up inside `do_action.rs`, since only `World` has `self.effects` - mirrors the existing `weather_movement_percent` precedent exactly), added *before* the swamp-sprite/underwater branches so a muddy swamp tile still silently discards the mud bonus for players, an authentic C quirk (C's swamp-sprite `if`s are unconditional `cost = N` assignments, not `+=`) preserved digit-for-digit. New `World::earthmud_extra_movement_cost(character_id)` (`world/actions.rs`) sums `edemon_reduction(effect.strength, current_demon) * 2` over every active `EF_EARTHMUD` effect on the walker's own tile (`0` immediately for `CF_EDEMON` walkers, matching C's gate around the whole scan), called at all 6 `do_walk` call sites (`apply_player_action_setup`'s `WalkDir` arm plus its two diagonal-slide alternates, `walk_or_use_driver`, `walk_swap_or_use_driver`, `setup_walk_direction`). (2) `check_strike_near`'s earth-demon damage reduction is now applied inside `World::apply_ball_strikes` (`effect_tick.rs`, the Rust home of C's `check_strike_near`'s ball-effect call site - `check_strike_near`'s other caller, `ef_flash`/`EF_FLASH`, remains entirely unported, a separate pre-existing gap noted but not touched): `if target.flags.contains(CharacterFlags::EDEMON) { damage -= min(damage/4, damage*max(0, target_demon_present - caster_demon_present)/10) }`, using `character_value_present` (`value[1]`, trained/max) for both sides exactly like C (note this is *not* `edemon_reduction`, which C itself defines using `value[0]` - an authentic C inconsistency between the two call sites, preserved rather than "fixed"). Documented but explicitly NOT fixed: `sub_attack`'s earth-demon hit-harder/hit-less-often diff bonus (`act.c:495-501`) is unreachable because `do_action::act_attack` resolves melee to-hit from raw `character_value(_, Attack/Parry)` (`value[0]`, the trained skill points) instead of calling `get_attack_skill`/`get_parry_skill` at all - confirmed via `create.c`'s own `update_char` that `V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY` are never overwritten with the skill-formula result, so this is a genuine, pre-existing gap in melee to-hit math for *every* character in the game (not edemon-specific), not something to retrofit inside one area's task: it would need `get_fight_skill`'s right-hand-item-type lookup threaded into `act_attack`'s signature (which currently has no `items: &HashMap<ItemId,Item>` parameter), touches every melee call site (`world/combat.rs`), and would change to-hit outcomes for every existing combat test in the tree. Flagged in `PORTING_TODO.md`'s REMAINING note for a future dedicated P1 slice. 3 new core tests for `do_walk`'s earthmud cost (`do_action.rs`: `edemon_reduction_matches_c_max_of_zero_and_strength_minus_demon`, the additive-before-terrain-override case, the swamp-sprite-discards-the-bonus quirk case) + 3 new `World`-level tests (`world/tests/actions.rs`: `earthmud_extra_movement_cost` sums only the walker's own tile and exempts `CF_EDEMON`, plus a `setup_walk_direction` integration test) + 1 new `world/tests/effect_tick.rs` test (`ball_effect_earth_demon_target_takes_reduced_strike_damage`, cross-checked against the file's existing non-edemon baseline test's exact damage number to confirm the 25%-reduction-cap math). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2711 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean with zero warnings, 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with hundreds of ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` left `[~]`: only the `act_attack`/`get_attack_skill` gap remains, and it's explicitly out-of-scope for this area (see REMAINING note).
- 2026-07-08: Area 6 (`src/area/6/edemon.c`) CLOSED: fixed the `act_attack`/`get_attack_skill` gap flagged by the previous entry as "explicitly out-of-scope for this area" - re-examined and decided it *was* in scope, since it's the direct cause of the earth-demon `CF_EDEMON` fallback branch (`fight_skill * 3.5`) being unreachable dead code, and the fix turned out to be a small, well-contained change since both halves it needed (`attack::attack_skill`/`parry_skill` and `world::npc_fight::simple_baddy_fight_skill`, C's `get_attack_skill`/`get_parry_skill`/`get_fight_skill`) were already ported and tested, just never wired into the one melee-resolution call site that matters. C `act_attack` (`act.c:747-748`) always resolves to-hit via `vcn = get_attack_skill(cn); vco = get_parry_skill(co);` - never the raw `V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY` stat. `do_action::act_attack` previously read `character_value(attacker, CharacterValue::Attack)`/`character_value(defender, CharacterValue::Parry)` directly (both functions are literally identical - `character_value_base`/`character_value` are the same `values[0]` lookup under two names), meaning every character in the game (not just earth demons) fought with a to-hit formula that ignored weapon skill (`get_fight_skill`'s right-hand-item-type lookup), Tactics, and the three fallback branches (earth-demon `fight_skill*3.5`, magic-shield-caster `fight_skill + V_MAGICSHIELD*2`, and pure-spellcaster `fight_skill + spell_average*2 [- level for attack]`) whenever `V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY`'s C `value[1]` "present" flag was unset. Fixed by giving `act_attack` a new `items: &HashMap<ItemId, Item>` parameter (threaded from its one production call site, `World::complete_attack_with_rolls_and_clash_roll` in `world/combat.rs`, via `&self.items`) and replacing the raw-stat reads with `attack_skill(...)`/`parry_skill(...)` calls built from `simple_baddy_fight_skill(character, items)` (fight skill) plus a freshly-computed `spell_average(...)` per side - both already-existing, already-tested `pub(crate)` functions reused verbatim, no formula logic duplicated. `rage` stays hardcoded to `0` for both sides, the same documented pre-existing gap every other `attack_skill`/`parry_skill` caller in the tree already has (`Character` has no `rage` field yet - see `values.rs`'s `show_values_lines` doc comment) - not something this slice introduces or need fix. Added a new local `character_value_present` helper in `do_action.rs` (mirroring `world::character_values`' identical one, needed for the `has_attack_base`/`has_parry_base`/`has_magic_shield_base` gates) since `do_action.rs` is outside the `world` module and only had the `character_value`/`character_value_base`-equivalent already. Updated the two existing `act_attack` tests to set the C "present" flag (`values[1][V_ATTACK]`/`values[1][V_PARRY]`) so they exercise the raised-stat branch instead of silently falling through to the fallback formula (which previously produced the same numeric output only by coincidence, since `character_value`==`character_value_base` and no weapon/tactics/rage were involved) - `act_attack_uses_strict_hit_roll_and_applies_damage`'s expected `attack_skill`/`parry_skill` moved from the old raw-stat `10`/`10` to the correct fight-skill-composited `20`/`20` (`fight_skill=0` since no weapon is worn in the test + `V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY`=`10`*2), and `world/tests/hurt.rs`'s `completed_attack_queues_legacy_showattack_pre_hurt_line` (the `/showattack` debug-line test) similarly moved from `(10 10)` to `(20 20)` in its expected message. Added a new `act_attack_uses_get_attack_skill_get_parry_skill_not_raw_stat` regression test proving the fix: a pure spellcaster with no `V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY` base now gets a nonzero `attack_skill` (`5`, from the `fight_skill + spell_average*2 - level` fallback) that the old "just read the raw stat" code could never have produced (it would always have been `0` for an untrained attacker). This closes every one of the 9 `CF_EDEMON` call sites audited in the previous entry, and closes Area 6 for real - the `edemon.c` file itself needed no new character driver (confirmed empty in C), only these shared-combat-code fixes. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2712 core [+1] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build --workspace`/`-p ugaris-server` clean with zero warnings, boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with hundreds of ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` now `[x]` for Area 6.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) STARTED: ported `CDR_FDEMON_DEMON`, the roaming Fire Demon/Fire Golem hunt AI - the only one of the file's three character drivers (`CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`/`CDR_FDEMON_BOSS`/`CDR_FDEMON_DEMON = 44/45/46`) not entangled with the soldier-recruitment `farmy_data`/`farmy_ppd.soldier[]` system. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/fdemon.rs` ports C's file-static waypoint graph (`struct waypoint wp[MAXWAY]`/`maxway`, `find_waypoints`/`dist_to_waypoint`/`find_closest_waypoint`/`add_enemy_to_waypoint`/`may_hunt_there`/`hunt_driver`) as a new `World::fdemon_waypoints: Vec<FdemonWaypoint>` (1-based indexing, index 0 an unused sentinel matching C's `wp[]`/`maxway`), built lazily from every placed `IDR_FDEMONWAYPOINT` item the first time any Fire Demon ticks; `find_way_to_waypoint`'s C array-based-queue-plus-full-resort-by-cost search degenerates to plain BFS since every edge has uniform weight 1 (documented in the module doc comment, including the resulting - already-unspecified-in-C - tie-break nondeterminism). C's `fdemon_demon`'s very first check, `if (ch[cn].sprite==190) { char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...); return; }`, makes the "Fire Golem" boss variant (`fdemon_big1`) 100% observably indistinguishable from a plain `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` character every tick, so `zone.rs`'s new `CDR_FDEMON_DEMON` spawn branch assigns it `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` directly instead (with its real zone-file `arg=`, parsed exactly like C's own tail-call would); the other ten non-190-sprite "Fire Demon" trash-mob templates keep `CDR_FDEMON_DEMON` and reuse `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy` wholesale for combat/message dispatch (same `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` gate-widening precedent as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER`, just for the message pass, which already dispatches on `driver_state` not `driver` id - only `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow` needed a visibility bump to `pub(crate)` so the new `world::npc::area8::fdemon_demon` driver could call it directly in C's exact per-tick order (gohome-check before attack, matching `fdemon_demon`'s own sequence - deliberately *not* achieved by widening the generic batch-attack-pass gate, which runs earlier in `tick_npc::run_all` and would have let attack preempt a pending gohome walk). `add_enemy_to_waypoint`'s C `NT_CHAR`-message trigger is replaced by a direct per-tick radius scan (`SIGHTING_SCAN_RADIUS = 32`, matching `notify_area`'s own broadcast radius) for visible `PLAYER`/`PLAYERLIKE` characters, since the same messages are already unconditionally drained by the generic `SimpleBaddy` message pass earlier in `run_all` - same "replace message-driven sighting with a direct scan" precedent as `world::npc::area4::tester`/`world::npc::janitor`. The `fdemon_demon_dead` death hook (`farmy_ppd.boss_stage` 16/17 -> 18 + "Well done. Now go back to the Commander.") is matched by `world.area_id == 8 && target.sprite == 190` rather than driver id, since the Fire Golem no longer carries `CDR_FDEMON_DEMON`; new `PlayerRuntime::advance_farmy_golem_kill_stage` accessor (`player/areas_misc.rs`, same raw-blob-offset pattern as the pre-existing `advance_farmy_blood_stage`/`advance_farmy_lava_stage`) with a documented gap (the C branch crediting a recruited soldier's platoon leader isn't reachable yet, since `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` soldiers don't exist). New `tick_npc::area8::fdemon_demon_driver_88`. 12 new focused tests (`world/tests/fdemon.rs`: waypoint-graph build/connect/idempotent/unreachable, `find_way_to_waypoint` first-hop, `may_hunt_there` asymmetric bounds, `add_enemy_to_waypoint`+`hunt_driver`, and 4 full-driver-action tests for gohome/hunt/wander/sighting-scan) plus 2 `zone.rs` spawn tests (sprite-190 -> plain SimpleBaddy, trash-mob -> fixed 0/30/0 distances ignoring zone-file args). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2724 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke verified live against real `zones/8/fire.map` data with `--area-id 8` (confirmed real `fdemon_demon_acted` counts every tick, no panics). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 left `[~]`: `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` (soldier recruitment/formation/emotes) and `CDR_FDEMON_BOSS` (33-stage mission-giver dialogue) remain, both built around the same `farmy_data`/`farmy_ppd.soldier[]` structs - do them together next.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`): ported `CDR_FDEMON_BOSS` (the underground army Commander's 33-stage mission-giver dialogue chain) plus the matching `IDR_FDEMONLOADER` defense-station bookkeeping, closing the "boss dialogue chain is meaningless without recruitable soldiers" gap the previous iteration flagged - the mission chain (activate stations 1-6, slay a Fire Golem, discover golem blood/lava uses, then open-ended station scouting) is fully playable solo, without any `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` soldiers, since `platoon_exp`'s player-reward half never actually needed them. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area8/fdemon_boss.rs`: `World::fdemon_boss_character_ids`/`fdemon_boss_sighted_players` (a direct radius-scan replacement for C's `NT_CHAR` sighting loop, same "replace message-driven sighting with a direct scan" precedent as `fdemon_demon`/`area4::tester`/`janitor` - `char_dist < 16`, visible, `CF_PLAYER`, driver != `CDR_LOSTCON`), `World::fdemon_boss_greet_player` (the full `switch (ppd->boss_stage)` transcription, all 33 stages: the four "story beat" runs 0-27 with their exact message text including case 4/10/16/22's `get_army_rank_string` vs `ch[co].name` distinction, case 1's `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET`-wrapped "take"/"drop" line via a new `World::npc_say_bytes` - the `say`-family sibling `npc_quiet_say_bytes` already had, filling a gap its own doc comment had pre-announced - the `28`-falls-through-`29` double-transition, and case `30`'s open-ended scouting-phase bit-counting/promotion-to-31 logic), `World::fdemon_platoon_exp` (the always-live player-exp/rank-promotion half of `platoon_exp` - custom `(amount+1999)/2000`-unit exp-cap formula + raw `military_points` add reusing the existing `army_rank_for_points`/`army_rank_name` derived-rank machinery, capped at rank 24 not the shared `MAX_ARMY_RANK=40`; the soldier-exp loop is a documented gap, same class as `advance_farmy_golem_kill_stage`'s), `World::fdemon_boss_process_text_messages` (the `NT_TEXT` half, wired through the *real* `Character::driver_messages` queue rather than a scan substitute, since `ugaris-server`'s `commands_chat.rs::push_driver_text_message` already reliably fans player speech out to every nearby NPC - `analyse_text_qa` against a new 15-row `FDEMON_QA` table (`world/npc/area8/mod.rs`, only the C `needs_name: 0` rows; the ~35 `needs_name: 1` rows are recruited-soldier-only small talk deferred to `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`, along with the `needs_name` concept itself - not yet supported by `character_driver::TextQaEntry`), replying to greetings directly and special-casing `answer_code == 1` ("what's your name") to reproduce `analyse_text_driver`'s own built-in "I'm %s." fallback), and the free function `fdemon_boss_repeat_reset` (pure `boss_stage` ladder math for the "repeat" `NT_TEXT` branch). New `PlayerRuntime` accessors (`player/misc.rs`/`areas_misc.rs`): `FARMY_PPD_BOSS_TIMER/COUNTER/REPORTED_OFFSET` (`4`/`332`/`336`, derived from the C `struct farmy_ppd` byte layout - `boss_stage`+`boss_timer` (2 ints) + `soldier[3]` (`27 ints` each: 7 scalar fields + an embedded 20-int `emote`) + `boss_counter`+`boss_reported`, confirming the existing `LEGACY_FARMY_PPD_SIZE = 85*4 = 340` end-to-end) plus `farmy_boss_timer`/`set_farmy_boss_stage`/`set_farmy_boss_timer`/`farmy_boss_counter`/`set_farmy_boss_counter`/`farmy_boss_reported`/`set_farmy_boss_reported`. The loader-side half lives in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/fdemon.rs`'s new `fdemon_loader_station_report` (pure function over `station_id`/`boss_stage`/`boss_counter`/`boss_reported`/`character_level`, transcribing `fdemon_loader`'s five `it[in].drdata[6]`-gated `if` blocks - stations `1`/`3`/`2`/`6` solve solo missions, `4`+`5` are a "find both twins" pair needing 2 bits set, `7..=35` is the open-ended scouting counter with its exp-cap discovery-pacing warning) plus a new `station_id: u8` field on `ItemDriverOutcome::FdemonLoaderChanged` (populated from `item.driver_data[6]` in `item_driver/area8_fdemon.rs`'s already-ported `fdemon_loader_driver`) dispatched in `tick_item_use_edemon_fdemon.rs` (moved out of `tick_item_use_completion.rs`'s no-op catch-all into the existing Edemon/Fdemon family's or-pattern) using the same `runtime.player_for_character_mut` pattern `FdemonBloodFilled`/`FdemonLavaActivated` already established. Server-side glue lives in a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area8.rs` (`apply_fdemon_boss_tick`, threading `PlayerRuntime` into both the text-message and sighting-scan halves - ticks substitute for C's wall-clock `realtime` in the `boss_timer` 5-second throttle, same substitution precedent as `world::fdemon`'s `last_enemy_tick`) and a new `tick_npc::area8::fdemon_boss_driver_89`. 29 new focused tests (17 in `world/tests/fdemon_boss.rs`: sighted-player filtering, every "story beat" stage's message/rank-string-vs-name choice, the color-marked stage-1 line via `drain_pending_area_text_bytes`, all eight "waiting" stages' exact no-op, reward-stage exp/points/promotion-announcement, the 28-into-29 fallthrough vs. plain 29, all three stage-30 branches (silent no-op / new-stations-found / cnt2>=26 advance), the dead-end stage 33, the full repeat-reset ladder, and both `NT_TEXT` paths (repeat detection honoring the 12-tile talk range, ordinary small-talk replies); 11 in `world/tests/fdemon.rs`/`item_driver/tests/area8_fdemon.rs` for `fdemon_loader_station_report`'s five station-id branches plus the new `station_id` outcome field; 1 in `player/tests/areas_misc.rs` for the four new byte-offset accessors' round-trip). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2753 core [+29] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke verified live against real `zones/8/fire.map` data (`army_boss:` template, `driver=45`, is placed on the map) with `--area-id 8`: 280+ ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 left `[~]`: only `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` (soldier recruitment/formation/emotes, plus the boss's own "take"/"drop" `NT_TEXT` tail and `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp loop that depend on it) remains.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: first slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` (the recruitable-soldier system, the last unported piece of this file). Ported only the pure, spawning-independent half of `take_soldiers`/`assign_profile` (`fdemon.c:301-514`): a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area8/fdemon_army.rs` carries C's `struct profile profile[]` table (14 named recruits with gender/cuddly/angst/bore/bigmouth/sprite, digit-for-digit) and `plan_soldier_recruitment` - a pure function reproducing `take_soldiers`'s three-slot type/rank-gate/profile-uniqueness eligibility logic (rank>0/`>4`/`>6` thresholds, gendered profile-range rolls, the `do {...} while` unique-profile-vs-already-assigned-slots loops) via an injected `random_below` closure, matching the existing `legacy_random_below_from_seed` calling convention. One subtlety caught by porting the C literally instead of by pattern-matching against slot 1/2's rolls: slot 0's profile roll is `RANDOM(ARRAYSIZE(profile)) / 2 [+ ARRAYSIZE(profile)/2]` (a full `RANDOM(14)` divided by 2), not `RANDOM(ARRAYSIZE(profile)/2)` (a direct `RANDOM(7)`) like slots 1/2 - same distribution range, different PRNG-stream consumption, documented inline and covered by a dedicated test asserting `below == 14` for slot 0 vs `below == 7`/`below == 14` for slots 1/2. New `PlayerRuntime::farmy_soldier_type`/`_rank`/`_base`/`_profile`/`_exp`/`_cn`/`_serial` accessors (`player/areas_misc.rs`) expose C's `struct soldier` (`type`/`rank`/`base`/`profile`/`exp`/`cn`/`serial`, 7 ints per slot) within the existing `farmy_ppd` legacy blob at `FARMY_SOLDIER_ARRAY_OFFSET=8` + `slot * FARMY_SOLDIER_STRIDE(108)` + field offset (`player/misc.rs`), confirming the blob's `LEGACY_FARMY_PPD_SIZE=340` byte layout end-to-end for the first time (the embedded 20-int `emote` sub-struct per slot, offsets `+28..+108`, stays unexposed pending the emote engine - only its size matters so `boss_counter`/`boss_reported` keep their already-ported offsets). 15 new focused tests (8 in `fdemon_army.rs`: profile table shape, `assign_profile`'s tendency-field carry, `soldier_base_strength`'s `43 + rank*4` formula, and 5 `plan_soldier_recruitment` cases covering rank-0/rank-1-gendered/rank-5-collision-reroll/rank-7-collision-reroll/already-occupied-idempotency; 1 in `player/tests/areas_misc.rs` for the 7 soldier accessors' round-trip, neighbor-slot isolation, out-of-range no-op, and outer-blob round-trip). Still needed for `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`: the actual `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers` character spawning (`ZoneLoader::instantiate_character_template("army1s"/"army2s", ..)`, `update_soldier`'s skill-tiered equipment/stat gearing, `drop_char` placement, `farmy_data` follow-state init), the driver id + `CharacterDriverState` variant + `army_follow_driver`/`army_front_driver`/`army_back_driver`/`army_behind_driver` formation AI (needs a waypoint-free `pathfinder`, unlike `fdemon_demon`'s waypoint-graph hunt), `find_platoon`/`platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp half, the `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality-driven emote engine, and the `it_driver` item triggers (`it[in].drdata[6]` values 1-6) that call `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2762 core [+9, one is the accessor test] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of ticks and no panic (this slice is unreachable dead code from any dispatch path, so behavior is unchanged by construction). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: second pure slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`, porting `update_soldier`'s stat-scaling/equipment-selection half (`fdemon.c:394-441`), still deliberately independent of any `World`/`ZoneLoader` spawning wiring. `fdemon_army.rs` gains `scale_soldier_skill`/`scale_soldier_values` (the three-branch `value[1][m] == 1/2/3 -> base/2 / base-5 / base` per-skill scaling loop, marker `0` leaves the target value untouched matching C's no-`else` fallthrough) and `soldier_equipment_items` (the `sprintf("sleeves%dq1"/"armor%dq1"/"helmet%dq1"/"leggings%dq1"/"sword%dq1", skill/10+1)` warrior five-piece kit vs. the mage's single `dagger%dq1`, returning `(WN_slot, template_key)` pairs against new `WN_HEAD`/`WN_ARMS`/`WN_BODY`/`WN_RHAND`/`WN_LEGS` constants matching C's `server.h` `WN_*` indices `1`/`3`/`4`/`6`/`7`). Confirmed the `army1s`/`army2s` templates' literal `V_*=n` fields (`ugaris_data/zones/8/fire.chr:921-1032`) are themselves the `1`/`2`/`3` tier markers `update_soldier`'s throwaway `cc` instance reads, and that `ugaris_data/zones/generic/armor.itm`'s `sleeves`/`armor`/`helmet`/`leggings`/`sword`/`dagger` templates go up to tier 10, matching the `.../10+1` formula's range. Confirmed `calc_exp`/`exp2level`-based exp/level assignment and the actual character/item spawning remain unported (`exp2level` exists in `world/exp.rs` but `calc_exp`, C's skill-sum exp formula, does not yet exist anywhere in the Rust tree) - left for a future slice alongside the real spawning integration. 4 new focused tests in `fdemon_army.rs`: the three-branch scaling formula, a multi-marker `scale_soldier_values` pass using a real slice of the `army1s` template's markers, and both equipment branches (warrior's five-piece kit, mage's single dagger). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2766 core [+4] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean (no runtime/protocol/login/map-sync code touched, so no boot-smoke required). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`, REMAINING note trimmed to reflect the smaller remaining surface.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: third pure slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`, porting `calc_exp(cn)` (`src/system/skill.c:174-196`), the general skill-sum exp formula C's `create_char` and `update_soldier` both call to keep `exp`/`exp_used` consistent with a character's bare `value[1]` skill array. Added as a new free function `calc_exp` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/exp.rs` (alongside the already-ported `exp2level`/`level2exp`/`level_value`), reusing the existing `crate::item_driver::{raise_cost, skill_start, skill_raise_cost_factor, skillmax, supermax_cost}` helpers (`item_driver/scrolls.rs`, already ported for `supermax_driver`/stat scrolls/`CL_RAISE`) rather than duplicating their formulas - for each raisable skill with a nonzero bare value, sums `raise_cost`/`supermax_cost` (the latter only for `CharacterFlags::PLAYER` characters past `skillmax`) across every step from `skill_start(v)+1` to the current value, matching C's `for (n = skill[v].start+1; n <= ch[cn].value[1][v]; n++)` loop exactly. `fdemon_army.rs` gains `finalize_soldier_exp_and_level`, a thin wrapper composing `calc_exp`+`exp2level` matching `update_soldier`'s `ch[co].exp = ch[co].exp_used = calc_exp(co); ch[co].level = exp2level(ch[co].exp);` tail exactly - the last piece of `update_soldier`'s pure math, everything else in that function now needs only the real spawning/item-creation wiring. 4 new focused tests in `world/tests/exp.rs` (single-skill sum, non-raisable-skill skip, player-vs-non-player `supermax_cost` branch crossing `skillmax`, seyan `4/30` vs. non-seyan `/10` cost multiplier - all cross-checked against a standalone Python transcription of the C formula) plus 1 in `fdemon_army.rs` (`finalize_soldier_exp_and_level` end-to-end against a real `Character` fixture with `scale_soldier_values`-scaled skill values). Still needed for `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`: the actual `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers` character spawning (`ZoneLoader::instantiate_character_template("army1s"/"army2s", ..)`, `drop_char` placement, `destroy_items`+equipment re-creation via `soldier_equipment_items`, `farmy_data` follow-state init), the driver id + `CharacterDriverState` variant + `army_follow_driver`/`army_front_driver`/`army_back_driver`/`army_behind_driver` formation AI, `find_platoon`/`platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp half, the `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality-driven emote engine, and the `it_driver` item triggers (`it[in].drdata[6]` values 1-6) that call `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers`. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2771 core [+5] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1108 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean (no runtime/protocol/login/map-sync code touched, so no boot-smoke required). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated to drop `calc_exp` from the missing-pieces list.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: fourth slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` closes the spawning gap - a recruited soldier now actually exists as a real `World` character and follows its leader around. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area8_army.rs` ports `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers` (`fdemon.c:451-625`) end to end: `drop_soldiers` uses the already-persisted `PlayerRuntime::farmy_soldier_cn`/`_serial` (a direct index instead of C's O(all characters) group scan - behaviorally identical for at most `MAXSOLDIER` soldiers) to destroy the live character and fold `exp - exp_used` back into `farmy_soldier_exp`, resetting only `serial` (matching C - `type`/`rank`/`profile` survive a drop so a later `take` rebuilds the same slot without re-rolling); `take_soldiers` calls the already-ported `plan_soldier_recruitment` for newly-eligible slots, then unconditionally (re)spawns every slot with a nonzero `type` via a new `spawn_army_soldier` helper (`ZoneLoader::instantiate_character_template("army1s"/"army2s", ..)`, reusing the already-ported `scale_soldier_values`/`soldier_equipment_items`/`finalize_soldier_exp_and_level` - the just-instantiated template's own `value[1]` doubles as C's marker array, so no second throwaway `cc` instance is needed - name/gender/sprite from `SOLDIER_PROFILES`, `World::spawn_character` placement, `World::update_character` + `hp`/`endurance`/`mana` from `value[0]`, same tail pattern as `dungeon.rs`'s `finish_dungeon_guard_spawn`), then rebuilds every spawned soldier's `platoon[]` array. `set_army_rank(co, rank)` on the soldier has no separate persisted-rank field in this port (same "derive from `military_points`" precedent as `area3::seymour`'s `set_army_rank(co, 1)` deviation) - `military_points = rank.pow(3)` since `army_rank_for_points` is an exact cube root. New `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY = 44` driver id + `CharacterDriverState::FdemonArmy(FarmyData)` variant (`character_driver.rs`, widened the 3 exhaustive `CharacterDriverState` matches in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`) plus `FarmyData` (C `struct farmy_data` minus the `emote` field, which stays unmodeled - deferred to the emote-engine task) and two new `World` methods in `fdemon_army.rs`: `army_follow_driver` (C `fdemon.c:633-655` digit-for-digit, `min_dist=2` when the leader is visible via `char_see_char`/`setup_walk_toward` vs. `min_dist=0` toward the last-known `lx`/`ly` when not) and `fdemon_army_tick` (the `MIS_FOLLOW`/leader-lost-disintegration slice of `fdemon_army`'s per-tick dispatch, `fdemon.c:1327-1532` - `army_follow_driver(10)` then a fallback `army_follow_driver(3)`, `remove_character` when the leader is gone or group-mismatched). Wired into `tick_npc::area8::fdemon_boss_driver_89` (renamed from boss-only, now also calls the new `area8_army::apply_fdemon_army_tick` every tick) and `area8.rs`'s boss `NT_TEXT` handling, extended with a new `FdemonBossTextOutcome` struct (replacing the old bare `Vec<CharacterId>` return) carrying `take_requests`/`drop_requests` alongside the existing `repeat_requests` - C's raw `strcasestr(dat2, "take"/"drop")` scan with its own independent `char_dist < 16` gate (no `char_see_char`/12-tile small-talk-range gate, unlike "repeat") is ported as a separate check inside the same message-drain loop, then `apply_fdemon_boss_tick` (now taking `&mut ZoneLoader` too) applies the `boss_stage 1..=30` gate (`drop_soldiers`+`take_soldiers` in range, else the "You cannot take soldiers at this time" line) exactly matching `fdemon.c:1871-1881`. Explicitly deferred, documented gaps (not silent): `army_back_driver`/`army_front_driver`/`army_behind_driver` (the other 4 missions - unreachable in practice since nothing sets `FarmyData::mission` away from `MIS_FOLLOW` yet, as the "follow"/"back"/"retreat"/"front"/"behind" `NT_TEXT` command reception in `fdemon_army`'s own message loop isn't ported), all combat/self-defense (`fight_driver_update`/`do_heal`/`do_bless`/`fight_driver_attack_visible` - a soldier follows but never fights back), the entire `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality/chat system, and `platoon_exp`'s now-reachable-but-still-unported soldier-exp/promotion loop (its doc comment is updated to say so, not fixed). 10 new focused tests: 6 in `world/tests/fdemon_army.rs` (`army_follow_driver` visible-walks/already-close/not-visible-walks-to-last-known/no-driver-state, `fdemon_army_tick` leader-lost/group-mismatch-disintegrate/follows-same-group-leader) plus 4 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area8_army.rs` (`take_soldiers` recruits+spawns+equips slot 0 end-to-end against inline `army1s`/`army2s`/equipment `ZoneLoader` templates mirroring the real `fire.chr` data, `drop_soldiers` destroys the character and preserves `type` for a later rebuild, rank-0 recruits nobody) plus updated the 3 pre-existing `fdemon_boss_process_text_messages` tests for the new `FdemonBossTextOutcome` return type. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2778 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1111 server [+3], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of ticks with no panic (verified both against `--area-id 1`'s default data and separately with the `army1s`/`army2s`/`fdemon_boss` real `zones/8/fire.*` data). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated to reflect the smaller remaining surface (4 missions, combat, emotes, soldier-exp, command reception, item triggers).
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: fifth slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` ports the mission-command reception and three of the four remaining formation missions. New `World::fdemon_army_process_text_messages` (`fdemon_army.rs`, C `fdemon_army`'s `NT_TEXT` branch, `fdemon.c:1370-1423`) drains each soldier's real `driver_messages` queue (same "use the real message queue, not a scan substitute" precedent as `fdemon_boss_process_text_messages`), gates on C's `find_platoon` (only a platoon member - leader or fellow soldier, `FarmyData::platoon` - may address the soldier at all) and then on `speaker_id == dat.leader_cn` (only the leader may issue a command), and matches spoken text against the already-existing `FDEMON_QA` table's `follow`/`back`/`retreat`/`front`/`behind` entries (`answer_code` 2-6, ported in an earlier slice alongside `fdemon_boss`'s QA sharing but never wired to anything until now) via the shared `analyse_text_qa`. Each match reproduces C's exact reply text (`"Sir! Yes, Sir, {rank}, Sir!"`/`"Will do, {rank}."`/`"Aye Aye {rank}, Sir."`/`"So be it, {rank}."`/`"I'll go rub his back, {rank}."`, `{rank}` from the already-ported `army_rank_name(army_rank_for_points(...))`, C's `get_army_rank_string`) and sets `FarmyData::mission` accordingly; the "back" branch also records `dat.opt1`/`dat.opt2` = the soldier's own current position and `dat.timer` = the current tick (C `ticker`), matching `fdemon.c:1394-1400`. The `res >= 20` emote-reaction dispatch (`got_emote`) and case `7`'s emote-stats debug dump are both skipped (meaningless without the still-unported emote system) - documented in the module doc comment, not silently dropped. Two new `World` movement primitives complete the picture: `army_back_driver` (C `fdemon.c:675-686` - if still standing at the recorded guard post, step exactly once in the opposite of the current facing direction via a new free-function `opposite_direction` transcribing C's `(dir+3)%8+1`, using the same raw-`do_walk`-call convention `fdemon_demon.rs`'s random-wander fallback established rather than the door-bump-opening `move_driver`/`walk_or_use_driver`; otherwise a 5-second/`TICKS*5`-tick timeout either idles for half a second or reverts the mission to `MIS_FOLLOW`) and `army_front_driver` (C `fdemon.c:657-673` - walks toward a point 4 tiles ahead of the leader in its current facing direction, computed via `Direction::delta()` as C's `dx2offset` equivalent, reusing `setup_walk_toward` like `army_follow_driver` already does). `fdemon_army_tick` is restructured from its single `if dat.mission == MIS_FOLLOW` special case into C's actual two-`switch(dat->mission)`-block shape (`fdemon.c:1447-1481` then, after the still-skipped combat fallback, `fdemon.c:1500-1514`): `MIS_FOLLOW`/`MIS_FRONT` get both dispatch attempts (dist 10 then dist 3, matching C's "try to keep distance, then try to close up" two-pass shape), `MIS_BACK`/`MIS_RETREAT` get only the first block's single attempt (no second-block case for them in C either), and `MIS_BEHIND` is an explicit no-op arm (the command is accepted and the mission is set, matching C's reply text, but `army_behind_driver` itself - needing a live map-tile lookup at "whatever the leader is facing" plus `do_attack` combat, unlike the other three missions' pure movement - is not ported, so a soldier told to go "behind" simply stands still until commanded elsewhere). Wired into `ugaris-server`'s `area8_army.rs::apply_fdemon_army_tick`, which now calls `fdemon_army_process_text_messages` before `fdemon_army_tick` per soldier per tick, matching C's own per-character message-loop-then-dispatch order. 16 new focused tests in `world/tests/fdemon_army.rs`: `army_back_driver` stepping back from a held post / idling within the timeout / reverting to `MIS_FOLLOW` after the timeout; `army_front_driver` walking toward the front point / stopping when already close / returning false when the leader isn't visible; `fdemon_army_tick` dispatching `MIS_BACK`/`MIS_RETREAT`/`MIS_FRONT` through the new drivers and confirming `MIS_BEHIND` is a genuine no-op (position and `action` both unchanged); and the text-command reception's platoon/leader/talk-range gates (non-platoon speaker ignored, non-leader platoon member ignored, out-of-range leader ignored) plus all five successful commands (the "front" reply text, the "back" command's `opt1`/`opt2`/`timer` recording, and a parametrized "retreat"/"behind" mission-set check). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2794 core [+16] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1111 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" both against `--area-id 1`'s default data and live against real `zones/8/fire.*` data (`--area-id 8`, 280+ ticks, `fdemon_demon_acted` logging every tick), no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated: only `army_behind_driver`'s combat, self-defense (`fight_driver_update`/`do_heal`/`do_bless`/`fight_driver_attack_visible`), the emote engine, `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp loop, and the `it_driver` item triggers remain for this file.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: sixth slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` ports `army_behind_driver` (`fdemon.c:688-705`), the last of the five formation missions. New `World::army_behind_driver` (`fdemon_army.rs`) resolves whatever character the leader is currently facing via a direct map-tile lookup (C's `dx2offset(ch[cc].dir,...)` then `map[...].ch`), computes the tile one step behind that character in *its own* facing direction (reusing the `opposite_direction` helper `army_back_driver` already established for the same `(dir+3)%8+1` formula), walks there via `World::setup_walk_toward` if not already positioned (confirmed this is an exact `move_driver(cn,tx,ty,0)` equivalent - `move_driver`'s own C definition is literally `pathfinder` + `walk_or_use_driver`, and `setup_walk_toward` already composes those same two ported primitives - unlike `army_follow_driver`/`army_front_driver`/`army_back_driver`, which call raw `do_walk`, this is the first `fdemon_army.rs` mission driver to actually need the door-bump-opening `move_driver` semantics), and on success returns immediately without attacking (matching C's early `return 1`); on a failed move it says "cannot go there" and reverts the mission to `MIS_FOLLOW` but - matching C's lack of an early return there - still falls through to attack. The final attack reuses `do_action::do_attack` directly (`target_dir` as the attack direction, matching C's `do_attack(cn, ch[co].dir, co)` - the soldier and target are lined up back-to-back so "attack in the target's own facing direction" lands exactly on it), with the same pre-existing `action::ATTACK1`-only simplification (no `RANDOM(3)` variant pick) every other `do_attack` caller in this codebase already uses (`setup_simple_baddy_attack_driver`/`attack_driver_direct` in `world/npc_fight.rs`). `fdemon_army_tick`'s `MIS_BEHIND` arm now dispatches through it instead of being a documented no-op. 5 new/updated focused tests in `world/tests/fdemon_army.rs`: `army_behind_driver` attacking when already positioned behind the target (asserting the queued attack's `act1`/`dir` fields), walking toward the behind-tile when not yet there (and confirming no attack is queued that tick), returning `false` when the leader faces an empty tile, `fdemon_army_tick` dispatching `MIS_BEHIND` through the new driver end-to-end, plus the old "documented no-op" test renamed/kept covering the truly-unreachable "leader has no facing direction" case (C's `dx2offset` failing on `dir=0`). This closes every formation mission in `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY`; only self-defense combat (`fight_driver_update`/`do_heal`/`do_bless`/`fight_driver_attack_visible`), the `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality/chat engine, `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp/promotion loop, and the `it_driver` item triggers (`it[in].drdata[6]` 1-6) remain for this file. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2798 core [+4] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1111 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated to drop `army_behind_driver` from the missing-pieces list.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: seventh slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` ports the self-defense/support-caster combat fallback that sits between `fdemon_army`'s two mission-dispatch `switch` blocks (`fdemon.c:1484-1497`: `fight_driver_update` + heal-a-hurt-friend + bless-a-lower-ranked-friend + attack-a-visible-enemy) plus the two shared-machinery message cases (`standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 1, 1)`'s `NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SEEHIT` cases, `drvlib.c:2478-2538`, hardcoded `agressive=1, helper=1` at `fdemon_army`'s own call site) that the previous `fdemon_army_process_text_messages` silently discarded (it drained the *entire* message queue via `mem::take` but only ever handled `NT_TEXT`, dropping every `NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SEEHIT` combat event on the floor). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area8/fdemon_army_combat.rs` (380 lines, split out of `fdemon_army.rs` - now 939, still over the ~800 NPC-file guideline pre-existing from before this slice, not fully resolved this iteration) holds: `World::fdemon_army_scan_sightings` - a direct per-tick radius scan replacing `fdemon_army`'s own `NT_CHAR` handling (`fdemon.c:1349-1368`: `leader_cn` tracking + bless/heal target selection) *and* `standard_message_driver`'s `NT_CHAR` aggro-add case, same "replace message-driven sighting with a direct scan" precedent already established twice in this same area by `fdemon_demon.rs`/`fdemon_boss.rs` (their own module doc comments) - since the `NT_CHAR` messages this substitutes for are themselves only ever generated by a `notify_area` broadcast within the same fixed radius the scan reproduces; returns `(bless_target, heal_target)`, the last qualifying same-group candidate found (C's per-message overwrite, `ch[cn].value[1][V_BLESS] > ch[co].value[1][V_BLESS]`/`ch[co].hp < ch[co].value[1][V_HP]*POWERSCALE/3` formulas ported digit-for-digit using the codebase's existing `character_value`/`character_value_present` split for C's `value[0]`/`value[1]` distinction), and adds every visible non-group `is_valid_enemy` target to the driver-independent `Character::fight_driver` slot via the already-ported `simple_baddy_can_add_standard_enemy`/`add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked` pair (same machinery every other `aggressive=1` `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` caller already goes through, `world::npc_messages`) - confirming that machinery is genuinely driver-state-agnostic (keyed on `Character::fight_driver`, not `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy`) by reusing it unchanged for a `FdemonArmy` character for the first time. `World::fdemon_army_try_heal`/`fdemon_army_try_bless` call the already-ported `do_heal`/`do_bless` directly (`None` direction, matching `spell_self_simple_baddy`'s own self-cast convention, just against a different target). `World::fdemon_army_process_messages` (renamed from `fdemon_army_process_text_messages`, `area8_army.rs`'s one call site updated) keeps the existing `NT_TEXT` mission-command handling (moved verbatim into a new private `fdemon_army_process_text_message` helper) and adds `NT_GOTHIT` (note-hit skipped - see below - `simple_baddy_can_add_standard_enemy(.., false, true)` + `add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked(.., priority: 1, ..)`, matching C's `fight_driver_add_enemy(cn,co,1,1)`) and `NT_SEEHIT` (the already-ported `simple_baddy_seen_hit_enemy` helper, same driver-agnostic pattern) in the same single drain pass, since a second `mem::take` would find the queue already emptied by the first. `fdemon_army_tick` (`fdemon_army.rs`) now calls the scan first (matching C's message-loop-before-leader-lost-check order) and wires the fallback into the gap between the two mission switches, threading `self.legacy_random_seed` through a scoped closure for `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow`'s `random` parameter (same `process_simple_baddy_attack_action`-style get/use/restore idiom, since `fdemon_army_tick`'s own public signature stays unchanged). Uncovered and fixed a real, independent gap while writing tests: `area8_army.rs`'s `spawn_army_soldier` never seeded the driver-independent `DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` slot C's own `fdemon_army` `NT_CREATE` handler sets (`fight_driver_set_dist(cn, 0, 20, 0)`, `fdemon.c:1346`) - without it, `simple_baddy_can_add_standard_enemy`'s `simple_baddy_enemy_within_start_limits` sub-check (which requires `Character::fight_driver` to already be `Some`) silently rejected every potential enemy, so a freshly recruited soldier could never register anyone as an enemy at all; added the same three-field `FightDriverData { start_dist: 0, char_dist: 20, stop_dist: 0, .. }` seed at spawn time (test helper `soldier_npc` updated to match). Also confirmed and corrected a stale claim in the previous iterations' REMAINING notes: the "`it_driver` item triggers (`it[in].drdata[6]` 1-6) that call `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers`" do not exist anywhere in `fdemon.c` - grepped every `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers` call site and found only `fdemon_boss`'s `NT_TEXT`-based "take"/"drop" commands (already ported in an earlier slice); the `drdata[6]` 1-35 range only ever gates `fdemon_loader`'s (already-ported) defense-station mission report. Deliberately left unported (documented, not silent): `fight_driver_note_hit`'s `lasthit` write on `NT_GOTHIT` for a `FdemonArmy` character (the shared `NoteHit` outcome handler is `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy`-gated; inconsequential since a soldier never calls `regenerate_simple_baddy`, the only reader). 22 new focused tests in `world/tests/fdemon_army.rs`: `fdemon_army_scan_sightings` (leader-tracking, bless-target selection + its higher-or-equal-level rejection, heal-target selection + its not-hurt-enough rejection, non-group enemy recording, no-driver-state early-out), `fdemon_army_try_heal`/`fdemon_army_try_bless` end-to-end mana-spend checks, `fdemon_army_process_messages`'s `NT_GOTHIT` (adds attacker, ignores a same-group attacker) and `NT_SEEHIT` (helps a platoon-mate being attacked) cases, and two `fdemon_army_tick` integration tests (attacks a visible enemy once close enough to the leader to skip movement, heals a hurt groupmate before ever reaching the attack branch) - the latter two needed `world.map.tile_mut(x,y).character = id` (matching `army_behind_driver`'s own test precedent) since `do_attack` requires real map-tile occupancy that a bare `world.characters.insert` bypasses, a real pitfall this iteration's debugging uncovered and is now documented in the test file. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2812 core [+22, some tests consolidated one assertion into existing ones] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1111 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed both `--area-id 1`'s default data ("entering Rust game loop", no panic) and live `--area-id 8` real `zones/8/fire.*` data (280+ ticks, `fdemon_demon_acted` logging every tick, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`: only the `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality/chat engine and `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp/promotion loop remain - the entire remaining surface for this file.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: eighth slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` ports the `do_emote`/`got_emote` personality/chat engine (`fdemon.c:323-1325`). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area8/fdemon_army_emote.rs`: `SoldierEmote` (C `struct emote`) as a new `FarmyData::emote` field; `World::fdemon_army_do_emote` (C `do_emote`) reproduces a real C quirk digit-for-digit - `bestscore`/`bestco`/`bestn` are declared once and never reset across the four lonely/boredom/fear/praise `if` blocks, so a later block silently reuses an earlier block's leftover target if its own scan doesn't beat the leftover score, and if the very first *triggered* block's scan finds zero candidates the whole function returns immediately, skipping every later block even if its own threshold was also crossed - implemented via a single `best`/`best_score` pair shared across all four blocks plus a `break 'emote` exactly where C's `if (!bestco) return;` sits; `World::fdemon_army_got_emote` (C `got_emote`) is a 30-arm match over every `QA_YES`..`QA_COWARD` code, most checking `answer_type`/`answer_cn`/`answer_timer` against whatever `do_emote` last asked (`ticker - answer_timer > TICKS*30` window) with a generic "didn't ask that" fallback on mismatch (including a verbatim-kept C duplicate-text bug: `QA_YOUAFRAID` and `QA_NONEED` share the exact same fallback line, "Afraid? Me? That's silly, %s."); `World::fdemon_army_emote_stats_line` reproduces case `7`'s debug dump text exactly. A new needs-name-gated small-talk table (`FDEMON_ARMY_EMOTE_QA`, `world/npc/area8/mod.rs`, 40 rows, C's own `"and you small like a ratling"` typo kept verbatim) reproduces C's `struct qa::needs_name` field without adding it to `TextQaEntry` (and therefore its ~300 other call sites across every other `analyse_text_driver` table in the codebase) - `character_driver.rs` gained a sibling `tokenize_text_words_with_name_flag`/`analyse_text_qa_needs_name` pair instead, since C's shared `qa[]` array happens to be the only table anywhere in the C tree that ever sets `needs_name: 1`. `fdemon_army_combat.rs`'s `fdemon_army_process_text_message` now tries the small-talk table first, then (only on `NoMatch`) the needs-name table, matching C's single-array declaration order; the `res >= 20` `got_emote` dispatch runs for *any* platoon member (not just the leader) before the existing leader-only command gate, matching C's call-site order exactly. `NT_DEAD`-triggered praise (`dat->emote.praise += dat->emote.bigmouth` when we're the killer, `msg->dat2 == cn`) is wired into `fdemon_army_process_messages`; `fdemon_army_tick` (`fdemon_army.rs`) gained the HP<50%-triggered `fear += angst; boredom -= 500` branch (before the leader-lost check, matching C's placement) and the tail's `lonely += cuddly; boredom += bore; if (ticker - last_emote > TICKS*10) do_emote(...)` accumulation. `area8_army.rs`'s `spawn_army_soldier` now seeds a freshly recruited soldier's four base tendencies from `assign_profile(profile_index)` (already-ported, previously unused by any caller); cross-recruit-cycle persistence of the "current"/relationship fields (C's `ppd->soldier[n].emote`, copied to/from `dat->emote` across `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers`) remains a documented gap - the exact byte range was already reserved in `player/misc.rs` by an earlier slice, so this is a follow-up wiring task, not a missing byte-layout fact. `fdemon_army.rs`'s four `army_*_driver` movement functions (`army_follow_driver`/`army_back_driver`/`army_front_driver`/`army_behind_driver`) were split into a new `fdemon_army_movement.rs` to bring the file back under the ~800-line guideline after this addition (`fdemon_army.rs` 980 -> 704, new file 290 lines). 21 new focused tests: 18 in `world/tests/fdemon_army.rs` (`do_emote`'s lonely-block message + gender filter, the no-candidate-early-return path, the stale-leftover-target-carries-into-a-later-block bug reproduced end to end with 2 distinct `say()`s, `got_emote`'s `QA_YES` success/mismatch-fallback paths, `QA_NICEDAY`'s always-replies-no-answer-state-check path, the leader-vs-any-platoon-member dispatch distinction via `fdemon_army_process_text_message`, the emote-stats debug line's exact format, `NT_DEAD` praise-on-kill vs. praise-ignored-for-a-kill-by-someone-else, the HP<50% fear/boredom tick branch, the lonely/boredom accumulation + 10-second `do_emote` trigger, and a 40-row/answer-code-range sanity check on the new QA table) plus 3 in `character_driver.rs` (`analyse_text_qa_needs_name` requires-the-name / matches-once-addressed / case-insensitive-name). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2828 core [+21, some assertions folded into existing tests during the movement-file split] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1111 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`: only `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp/promotion loop and the emote cross-recruit-cycle persistence gap above remain - the smallest remaining surface yet for this file.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) continued: ninth and final slice of `CDR_FDEMON_ARMY` ports `platoon_exp`'s soldier-exp/promotion loop (`fdemon.c:729-751`), the last unported piece of this file besides the documented emote cross-recruit-cycle persistence gap. `World::fdemon_platoon_exp` (`fdemon_boss.rs`) now takes a `soldiers: [SoldierPlatoonFacts; MAXSOLDIER]` parameter (new struct carrying `cn`/`serial`/PPD `stored_exp`/`soldier_type`, read by the caller from `PlayerRuntime::farmy_soldier_cn`/`_serial`/`_exp`/`_type` - same "caller supplies `PlayerRuntime`-resident facts" split `FdemonBossPlayerFacts` itself already established) and returns `Vec<SoldierPlatoonExpUpdate>` (`slot`/`new_stored_exp`/`promoted_rank: Option<i32>`): for each live, serial-matched soldier it folds the soldier's accumulated-but-unspent `exp - exp_used` into the PPD-tracked exp (matching C's `ch[co].exp = ch[co].exp_used;` reset), computes `rank = cbrt((stored_exp+amount+unspent)/1000)` (integer division first, exactly like C's `int/int`) capped one rank below the player's own current rank (`plr_rank`, read once before the loop and before the player's own `pts` grant, matching C's `plr_rank = get_army_rank_int(cm)` read order), and - on promotion - writes `military_points = rank.max(1).pow(3)` directly onto the soldier `Character` (same "`set_army_rank` via `military_points`" convention `area8_army::spawn_army_soldier` already established) plus the promotion-announcement `say()`. `FdemonBossPlayerFacts` gained the new `soldiers` field and `FdemonBossStageUpdate` gained a `soldier_updates: Vec<SoldierPlatoonExpUpdate>` field (dropped its `Copy` derive - `Vec` isn't `Copy` - no caller relied on it); all 9 `fdemon_platoon_exp` call sites inside `fdemon_boss_greet_player`'s `match boss_stage` now thread `soldiers` in and `extend` `update.soldier_updates` with the result. C's `update_soldier(co, n, ppd)` re-equip call on promotion (rescaling `value[1]` at the new rank's `base`, swapping equipped items for the new tier) needs a throwaway `army1s`/`army2s` character-template instantiation for fresh markers (the live soldier's own `value[1]` was already overwritten by the previous scaling pass, so unlike `spawn_army_soldier`'s initial equip it can't reuse the live character's own values) plus real `create_item` calls - both need `ZoneLoader`, which `World` doesn't have - so that half is a new `area8_army::reequip_soldier_for_promotion` (destroys every carried item via the existing `World::destroy_item`, matching C's `destroy_items(cn)` scope of "every inventory slot + cursor"), applied by `area8.rs` for every `Some(promoted_rank)` update alongside writing the PPD exp/rank deltas back to `PlayerRuntime`. `reset_name`'s purely-cosmetic "resend colored name to nearby clients" cache-bust is not ported, matching the same documented gap already noted on `world/exp.rs`'s own level-up path. This closes Area 8's entire soldier-exp/promotion surface (the emote cross-recruit-cycle persistence gap remains, tracked separately). 4 new focused tests: 3 in `world/tests/fdemon_boss.rs` (a fresh recruit promoted on its first exp grant with the exact PPD delta/military_points/promotion text asserted, promotion capped one rank below the player's own, and a serial-mismatch/empty-slot slot producing no update at all) plus 1 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area8_army.rs` (`reequip_soldier_for_promotion` end to end against a real spawned soldier: `value[1]` rescale, `exp`/`exp_used` resync, and every old equipped item destroyed and replaced by 5 fresh ones at the new tier). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2831 core [+3] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1112 server [+1], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 stays `[~]`: only the emote-state cross-recruit persistence gap remains, the smallest possible remaining surface for this file.
- 2026-07-08: Area 8 (`src/area/8/fdemon.c`) FINISHED: closed the last documented gap, `SoldierEmote` cross-recruit-cycle persistence (C `take_soldiers`/`drop_soldiers` copy `dat->emote` to/from `ppd->soldier[n].emote`, `fdemon.c:559-563,608-612`). New `PlayerRuntime::farmy_soldier_emote`/`set_farmy_soldier_emote` (`player/areas_misc.rs`) expose the full 20-int `struct emote` sub-layout within each soldier slot (offsets `+28..+108`, already reserved by an earlier slice - new `FARMY_SOLDIER_EMOTE_*_FIELD` constants in `player/misc.rs`), returning/accepting `world::npc::area8::fdemon_army_emote::SoldierEmote` directly. Wired into `crates/ugaris-server/src/area8_army.rs`: `take_soldiers`'s newly-recruited-slot loop now calls `assign_profile` and persists the resulting base-tendency `SoldierEmote` into the PPD immediately (matching C's `assign_profile(n, pro, ppd)` call inside the recruitment eligibility loop, not just at spawn time); the per-slot respawn loop reads the persisted `SoldierEmote` back, resets `boredom`/`fear`/`praise` to `0` (both on the value passed to `spawn_army_soldier` and written back to the PPD, matching C's `ppd->soldier[n].emote.boredom = 0; ...; dat->emote = ppd->soldier[n].emote;`, which runs for *every* occupied slot on *every* `take_soldiers` call, not just first recruits), and threads the result into `spawn_army_soldier` as a new `emote: SoldierEmote` parameter (replacing its previous always-fresh-from-`assign_profile` construction); `drop_soldiers` now reads the live soldier's `FarmyData::emote` before destroying the character and saves it back via `set_farmy_soldier_emote`, matching C's `ppd->soldier[n].emote = dat->emote;`.
