Updated `fdemon_army_emote.rs`/`fdemon_army.rs`/`area8_army.rs` module doc comments to drop the now-closed gap. 2 new focused tests: `player/tests/areas_misc.rs::farmy_soldier_emote_round_trips_and_does_not_clobber_neighbors_or_prefix` (byte-layout round-trip, neighbor-slot/prefix-field isolation, out-of-range no-op, outer-blob round-trip) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area8_army.rs::drop_and_retake_carries_emote_state_except_current_needs` (end-to-end: recruit, mutate a live soldier's `likes`/`talked`/`answer_*`/`last_emote`/`boredom`/`fear`/`praise`, drop, assert the PPD carries every field including the "current needs", re-recruit, assert tendencies/relationship/pending-answer state survive while `boredom`/`fear`/`praise` reset to `0` both on the live respawned soldier and in the PPD). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2832 core [+1] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1113 server [+1], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean (no runtime/protocol/login/map-sync code touched, so no boot-smoke required, though one was still run to confirm "entering Rust game loop" with no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 8 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 10 (`src/area/10/ice.c`) CLOSED after a full audit: confirmed C's own `ch_driver`/`ch_died_driver`/`ch_respawn_driver` in `ice.c` are all provably empty (`switch (nr) { default: return 0; }`) - no `CDR_ICE*`/`CDR_IDEMON*` character driver exists in C. `ugaris_data/zones/10/ice.chr` confirms: all 21 `idemon1`-`idemon20`+`idemon4_key` "Ice Demon" NPCs use plain `driver=7` (`CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`, already ported) with `CF_IDEMON`. `ice.c`'s `it_driver` (`IDR_ITEMSPAWN`/`IDR_MELTINGKEY`/`IDR_WARMFIRE`/`IDR_BACKTOFIRE`/`IDR_FREAKDOOR`, all delegating to `src/common/ice_shared.c`) and the freeze-cast `CF_IDEMON`-gated curse-install (`act.c:1524-1527`, `install_curse_spell`) were already fully ported per earlier ledger rows (`item_driver/ice.rs`, `tick_item_use_ice.rs`, `world/spells.rs`). This left "port every `CF_IDEMON` branch in shared combat code" (same precedent as Area 6/`CF_EDEMON`) as the real task: audited all 5 non-header `CF_IDEMON` call sites (`grep -rn CF_IDEMON src/`) - `act.c:1524` (freeze-cast curse install) already ported; `command.c:4682/4961` (the `/setflags`-style flag name<->string table) already ported (`zone.rs`'s `"CF_IDEMON" => CharacterFlags::IDEMON`, `commands_admin/character.rs`'s flag-listing table); `tool.c:2187-2188` (`freeze_value`'s ice-demon-vs-target-V_COLD bonus term) was ported as `freeze_speed_modifier` (`spell.rs:328-345`) and correctly wired at its one live cast-completion call site (`world/spells.rs:667-669`, `character_value_present` for the caster's `V_DEMON`) - but found and fixed a real bug at `freeze_speed_modifier`'s 3 *other* call sites, all in `npc_fight.rs`'s combat-AI decision logic (`simple_baddy_freeze_modifier`, `setup_simple_baddy_freeze_attack`'s spacing gate, `setup_simple_baddy_spell_attack`'s cast gate): each passed `character_value(attacker, Demon)` (C `value[0]`, the current/reducible value) instead of `character_value_present(attacker, Demon)` (C `value[1]`, the base/present value `freeze_value` itself always reads at `tool.c:2188`) for the ice demon's own `V_DEMON` stat - `update_char`'s `V_DEMON` special-case (`character_values.rs:468-470`, `value[0] = min(value[0], value[1])`) means these two can genuinely diverge whenever a demon's current value has been reduced, so the bug was a real, reachable freeze-speed-modifier miscalculation (the AI would systematically compute a *stronger* IDEMON freeze bonus than C for any ice demon whose current V_DEMON had dropped below its base, since `value[0] <= value[1]` always after that cap - `40 - 0 = 40` vs. the correct `40 - 30 = 10`, per the test below). Fixed all 3 call sites to use `character_value_present`, matching the already-correct `spells.rs` call site. New regression test `world::tests::npc_fight::simple_baddy_freeze_modifier_uses_ice_demons_base_not_current_value_like_c` (direct call to `simple_baddy_freeze_modifier` with `value[0][Demon]=0`/`value[1][Demon]=30` and target `value[0][Cold]=40`, asserting the modifier's `+100` bonus term uses `30` not `0`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2833 core [+1] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1113 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke (`--bind-addr 127.0.0.1:5556`, default area-1 data) confirmed "entering Rust game loop" with hundreds of ticks and no panic (change touches shared combat AI, not login/protocol, but the smoke was run anyway since it's in the tick loop). Noted but explicitly out of scope (not IDEMON-specific, a separate shared-tick mechanic - `act.c:1965-1981` `demon_protect_check`, a player-only "sunlight destroys demonic protection" `V_DEMON` zeroing check unrelated to any of the three demon-flag families): left unflagged as a future gap since it isn't part of this checkbox's file and touches no ice-specific code. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 10 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 11 (`src/area/11/palace.c`) STARTED: ported `palace_islena`/`CDR_PALACEISLENA` (`palace.c:578-767`), Islena's boss dialogue/aggro/death behavior - the item-driver half (bomb/cap/door/gate/islena-door) was already ported (see the earlier area-11 rows above). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area11/islena.rs`: `World::process_islena_actions`/`process_islena_tick` port the per-tick body digit-for-digit - the four-line greeting dialogue (`islena_state` 0..=3, gated by a 5-tick talk-cooldown, each PlayerRuntime-side state write returned as an `IslenaOutcomeEvent::UpdateState` following the `AsturinPlayerFacts`/`AsturinOutcomeEvent` split precedent, since `World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime`), the `islena_state >= 10` permanent-hostility short-circuit (any sighting once a player has ever hurt her immediately re-adds her as an enemy, no more dialogue), and all three "Power of X" full-heal self-defense triggers (`palace.c:668-680`'s in-loop "Power of Two" when a second, different attacker hits within 30 ticks of the last recorded hit; `:694-707`'s post-attack "Power of none" when an attack action left her still idle; `:712-721`'s unconditional post-tick "Power of None" fallback whenever she was hit at all that tick and neither of the first two paths already re-healed her) - each is textually distinct (verbatim "Two"/"none"/"None" capitalization kept) but shares one `islena_power_heal` helper for the actual `hp`/`mana`/`endurance`/`lifeshield = value[0][...] * POWERSCALE` reset plus its independent 15-tick announcement throttle. `NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SPELL(V_FREEZE)` both pin `islena_state` to `10` and feed the single-victim self-defense tracker (same simplification as every other ported NPC - see the module's own doc comment), matching C's `((msg->type == NT_GOTHIT) || (msg->type == NT_SPELL && msg->dat2 == V_FREEZE)) && (co = msg->dat1)` guard exactly (a `NT_SPELL` message with any other `dat2` is ignored). `apply_tabunga_text_notification` (already generic) covers the `NT_TEXT` branch's GM-only stat dump. `World::apply_islena_death` (C `islena_dead`, `:741-767`) is hooked directly into `World::kill_character_followup` (same "no `PlayerRuntime` needed, hook straight into the death-followup path" precedent as `CDR_PENTER`'s `apply_penter_demon_death` - the only per-player state here, `CharacterFlags::WON`, lives on the killer `Character` itself): first kill sets `CF_WON`, says "So it must end? Why me?", queues the "thou shalt be known as {Sir/Lady/Neuter} {name}" system text plus the "Grats: {name} is a {title} now!" channel-6 broadcast (new `islena_sirname` mirrors C's `Sirname` exactly), and queues the killer's id into a new `pending_islena_ladykiller_awards: Vec<CharacterId>` `World` field (same architectural split as `pending_penter_demon_lords_demise_awards` - the actual `ACHIEVEMENT_LADYKILLER` award needs the async DB-backed achievement repository `World` doesn't have); a repeat kill (`CF_WON` already set) instead says "Why again? Why? Thou shalt die with me!" and calls `apply_legacy_hurt(killer, 1500*POWERSCALE, ...)` as revenge damage, matching C's own re-triggering-the-death-cascade behavior. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area11.rs` (`islena_player_facts`/`apply_islena_events`, mirroring `area3.rs`'s established shape, plus `process_islena_ladykiller_awards` draining the pending-awards queue once per tick via a new `award_islena_ladykiller_achievement` wrapper in `achievement.rs`, called alongside the existing pentagram award drain in `tick_item_use_completion.rs`) plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area11.rs`'s `islena_driver_90`, registered in `tick_npc::run_all` as the new last entry. New `PlayerRuntime::islena_state: i32` (`player/mod.rs`, a plain `#[serde(default)]` field per `AGENTS.md`'s persistence rule - `DRD_ISLENA_PPD` has never had a Rust-side blob codec, so no legacy fallback is needed). Not ported this iteration: `palace_guard`/`CDR_PALACEGUARD` (the patrol/alert/scream sentries plus the moving `ice_eye` "line" NPC) - left `[~]`, see `PORTING_TODO.md`'s REMAINING note. 12 new focused tests in `world/tests/islena.rs`: first-sighting dialogue advance, talk-cooldown throttling, `islena_state >= 10` instant-victim sighting, `NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SPELL(V_FREEZE)` both pinning state to 10 and tracking the attacker (`V_FREEZE`-only guard confirmed against a non-freeze spell type), the "Power of Two" full heal firing for a genuinely different second attacker within the window vs. not firing (checked via the absence of that specific message text, since C's own unconditional post-tick fallback still heals her regardless - see the test's own comment) for a repeat attacker, the GM-only `tabunga` stat dump, and both `apply_islena_death` branches (first kill: `CF_WON` set + award queued + "Grats" broadcast text; repeat kill: no re-award + the killer actually takes damage). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2844 core [+12, some pre-existing test-count drift from unrelated ordering] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1113 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed both `--area-id 1`'s default data and live `--area-id 11` real `zones/11/palace.*` data (139 placed characters, "entering Rust game loop", 12+ seconds of ticks, no panic).
- 2026-07-08: Area 11 (`src/area/11/palace.c`) CLOSED: ported `palace_guard`/`CDR_PALACEGUARD` (`palace.c:84-353`), the palace's patrol/reserve-ambush demon sentries - the last remaining area 11 gap. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area11/palace_guard.rs` (`PalaceGuardDriverData` mirroring C's `struct palace_guard_data` field-for-field, including `unsigned char patrolx[20]/patroly[20]` as `[u8; 20]` and the `unsigned char dox/doy/alertx/alerty` truncating-cast-from-map-coordinate quirk preserved verbatim): `World::process_palace_guard_actions`/`process_palace_guard_tick` port the per-tick body in C's exact branch order - the message loop (palace-cap-immunity `NT_CHAR` short-circuit that clears any tracked victim and skips further processing for that message; the unused-in-current-data `alertx`/`dofreeze` freeze-chokepoint escalation, `do_freeze` success aborting the whole tick early exactly like C's own `return;`; the `scream` distance/cooldown area-alert shout; the reserve-only `NT_NPC`/`NTID_PALACE_ALERT` scout-alert reception), the `doalert`-walk block, a `fight_driver_update`-equivalent visibility refresh, the attack-visible/follow-invisible cascade, the `doscout` give-up-timeout/arrived/still-walking three-way branch (including C's own `do_idle(TICKS/2); return;`-without-checking-the-result quirk and the timeout branch's fall-through-without-returning), `regenerate_driver`/`spell_self_driver`, the unused-in-current-data `line`-mode "Ice Eye" sprite-guided walk (`51050`/`51051`/`51052` ground-overlay tiles, including C's own switch-fallthrough where a blocked `do_walk` on a line tile falls through to the same `move_driver` default case a non-line tile takes), and the `patrol`/`docheck` waypoint-loop-vs-walk-home `else` branch. Unlike `robber`/`islena` (whose `standard_message_driver` calls always use `helper=0`, collapsing to `NT_GOTHIT`-only self-defense), most live `palace_guard` templates in `zones/11/palace.chr` run with `scream` unset (`aggressive=1, helper=1`), so `World::palace_guard_standard_message` additionally ports the `NT_CHAR` aggressive-sighting-adds-enemy and `NT_SEEHIT` group-ally-helps branches on top of the usual single-victim-model `NT_GOTHIT` self-defense (confirmed via `is_valid_enemy`'s exact five-condition port, `palace_guard_is_valid_enemy`). New `World::notify_area_shout` (`world/text.rs`, C `notify.c:170-195`) ports the scream/scout alert broadcast primitive - the same `notify_area` bounding-box scan additionally gated per-recipient by `SoundSectors::sector_hear_shout` (already-ported, previously unused). New `CDR_PALACEGUARD = 47` constant (confirmed against `src/system/drvlib.h` and the live `driver=47` zone data), a new `PalaceGuard` `CharacterDriverState` variant (added to the three exhaustive matches in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`), and `zone.rs`'s new `CDR_PALACEGUARD` spawn-time branch (`apply_palace_guard_create_message`, parsing `ch[cn].arg`'s repeated `patrolx=N;patroly=N;` pairs via a hand-written index-tracking loop - no existing parser in this codebase handled a repeated-name-pair-into-array shape before this - plus the `item[30]`/`CF_NOBODY` -> `CF_ITEMDEATH` transform, same shape as `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`'s own copy of that transform). Registered as `tick_npc::area11::palace_guard_driver_91` (no `PlayerRuntime`/`ugaris-server::area11.rs` involvement needed, unlike `islena_driver_90` - this driver touches nothing outside `World`). Confirmed against the live `ugaris_data/zones/11/palace.chr`/`palace.map`: no guard instance sets `alertx`/`alerty` and no map tile carries the `51050-52` overlay sprites, so both the freeze-chokepoint and `line`-mode branches are dead in current data but still ported digit-for-digit as reachable C behavior (documented in the module's own doc comment) - the live templates instead exercise two real behaviors: `palace_guard1`/`2`'s 10/8-waypoint patrol-and-scream loop, and 13 stationary `reserve=1` demons (11 `palace_demon*` + `palace_guard3`/`4`) that snap awake on a scream broadcast. `ch_died_driver`/`ch_respawn_driver`'s `CDR_PALACEGUARD` cases are both confirmed-empty no-ops in C - no death/respawn hook needed. 12 new focused tests in `world/tests/palace_guard.rs`: `parse_palace_guard_driver_args` pair-parsing (patrolx/patroly index tracking, reserve), aggressive-vs-screaming sighting behavior (with the screaming case also asserting the scream branch itself still fired via `lastfight`), `NT_GOTHIT` self-defense firing even while screaming, palace-cap immunity clearing an existing tracked victim (plus the inactive-cap negative case), reserve-guard scout-alert reception and walk, non-reserve guards ignoring the same alert, patrol waypoint walking and index advancement on arrival, and the stationary-guard-walks-home-when-displaced `else` branch. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2856 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1113 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. Boot-smoke (`--area-id 11`, live `zones/11/palace.*` data, 139 placed characters) confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus 350 ticks over 15 seconds with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 11 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 12 (`src/area/12/mine.c`) STARTED: ported `handle_mining_result`'s weighted-event roll and four of its six branches (silver find, gold find, golem spawn, cave-in), closing the previously-fully-unported reward cascade that sits behind the already-ported `minewall`/`process_wall_digging` mechanic. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/mining.rs` (pure `impl World` methods, following the `world/loot.rs` "RNG lives in `impl World` via `self.legacy_random_seed`" precedent): `roll_mining_event`/`classify_mining_roll` (the `RANDOM(100000)` cumulative-band walk, split into a pure classifier so it's testable with explicit roll values instead of pre-computed LCG seeds), `roll_mining_silver_amount`/`roll_mining_gold_amount` (`handle_silver_find`/`handle_gold_find`'s `RANDOM(base*2+1)+base` plus miner-profession scaling - silver has no `amount > 0` guard in C, unlike gold, preserved as a real quirk), `roll_mining_golem_rare` (`handle_golem_spawn`'s `RANDOM(get_rare_golem_chance())==0`), `calculate_golem_drop_amount` (`calculate_drop_amount`), and `apply_mining_cave_in` (`handle_cave_in`'s full miner-avoid-chance/wall-tier-scaled-loss/athlete-reduction mechanic, including the C quirk where the athlete "instead of" message value is reverse-derived from the *already* `min(_, endurance)`-clamped loss, not the pre-clamp value). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/mine.rs` holds everything needing `ZoneLoader`/`PlayerRuntime`/achievement-repository access: `apply_mine_wall_reward` (the C `handle_mining_result` dispatch tail, wired from `tick_item_use_minewall::dispatch_minewall_outcome` when a `MineWallDig` outcome carries `opened: true`), `give_mine_item` (C `give_mine_item`'s create-then-merge-or-cursor logic, reusing the existing `stacks.rs` `StackKind`/`stack_template`/`stack_count`/`set_stack_count` primitives already established for silver/gold `IDR_ENHANCE` items - the 2% add-to-cursor roll and the inventory-pile search are both new), `apply_military_mission_silver_check` (C `check_military_silver`, new `PlayerRuntime::check_military_silver`/`MilitaryMissionSilverProgress` in `player/military.rs`/`world/npc/area32/military/missions.rs` sharing the existing `military_mission`/`set_military_mission_opt1`/`set_military_solved_mission` accessors `check_military_solve` already established - note C calls `sendquestlog` for *any* active unsolved mission, not just silver-type ones, unlike `check_military_solve`'s match-only-fires-on-match gate), and `spawn_normal_golem`/`spawn_rare_golem` (C `spawn_normal_golem`/`spawn_rare_golem`, following the `spawn_warp_trial_fighter`/`spawn_chestspawn_character` template-instantiate-then-place pattern; confirmed and preserved a real C quirk - `spawn_rare_golem` explicitly re-sets only `hp` (scaled by both `POWERSCALE` and `get_rare_golem_hp_multiplier()`, computed from the template's *pre-level-boost* `value[0][V_HP]` since `update_char` is never re-run after the level bump) while `endurance`/`mana` are left at whatever `instantiate_character_template` already initialized them to, which is already the full computed value - so the omission is a real but harmless no-op in practice). Two new `award_silver_mined_achievement`/`award_gold_mined_achievement` helpers (`crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs`) follow the existing `award_potion_brewed_achievement` no-op-without-`PlayerRuntime`/DB-first-unlock-announce shape, closing the specific achievement-wiring half of this checkbox the Achievements task (iteration 84) had flagged as blocked on this system. 9 new core tests (`world/tests/mining.rs`: cumulative-band classification incl. multiplier zeroing, silver/gold amount rolls incl. the zero-gold-base guard, golem-rare-chance sweep, drop-amount scaling, cave-in avoid/collapse/athlete-reduction) and 10 new server tests (`tests/mine.rs`: `give_mine_item` merge-vs-cursor placement, `check_military_silver` progress/solved/non-silver/no-mission, cave-in feedback, normal/rare golem stat-setting, and two `apply_mine_wall_reward` end-to-end branches using a seed-search helper to force a specific weighted-event outcome deterministically). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2865 core [+9] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1123 server [+10], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` with hundreds of ticks and no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: `handle_orb_find`/`handle_artifact_find` (both rare branches) and `CDR_GOLEMKEYHOLDER` (`keyhold_fight_driver`, the locked-treasure-room boss fight NPC spawned by the already-ported `keyholder_door`/`MineKeyDoor` mechanic) remain.
- 2026-07-08: Area 12 (`src/area/12/mine.c`) continued: ported the two remaining rare `handle_mining_result` branches. `handle_orb_find` (`mine.c:328-360`, the 5-in-100,000 orb-of-skill reward) is `mine.rs::apply_mine_orb_find`: rolls one of five skills (`V_IMMUNITY`/`V_ATTACK`/`V_PARRY`/`V_FLASH`/`V_MAGICSHIELD`, confirmed every one of C's local display names is identical to the shared `CHARACTER_VALUE_NAMES` table, so no separate name table was needed), instantiates a `+5` orb via the same `"empty_orb"` template/`drdata` layout `commands_admin/grants.rs::grant_created_orb` already established for `/create_orb`, and places it with the plain (non-"smart") `World::give_char_item` - matching C's `give_char_item(cn, in2)` exactly, including the real quirk that the success/failure message branches purely on whether item *creation* succeeded, not on `give_char_item`'s own placement return value. `handle_artifact_find` (`mine.c:405-504`, the 200-in-100,000 twelve-flavor relic table) is `mine.rs::apply_mine_artifact_find`: the full `ye_olde_artifacts[]` flavor-text table (transcribed digit-for-digit including every embedded `COL_*` marker, via the existing `COL_STR_*` sentinel/`expand_color_sentinels` convention `world_events/npc_events.rs` established) plus all four rarity-tier reward branches (50% a pittance of exp via `World::give_exp`, 30% 50-4950 silver via the already-ported `achievement::give_money`, 15% up to 10 military points via `World::give_military_pts`, 5% both a larger exp grant and 150-9950 silver) and the independent 5% trailing punchline - reusing a small local `mine_sirname` (C `Sirname`, same no-shared-helper-yet precedent as `world/npc/area11/islena.rs::islena_sirname`) for the two messages that address the player by title rather than name. One raw non-ASCII byte in C's source (`Cristal\xEDm`, mid-word, not valid standalone UTF-8) is reproduced as the Unicode codepoint U+00ED ('i' with acute) with a comment explaining the substitution, since the literal byte can't appear in a Rust `&str`. `dispatch_minewall_outcome`/`apply_mine_wall_reward` both gained a new `area_id: u16` parameter (threaded from `tick_item_use_completion.rs`'s `config.area_id`) since `give_exp`/`give_military_pts` need it for the `CF_NOEXP`/arena-zone and rank-promotion-broadcast logic. 5 new focused tests in `tests/mine.rs` (orb: cursor placement + both message lines' exact text; artifact: one test per rarity tier using a new `find_seed_for_artifact_rarity` seed-search helper - common exp/uncommon silver/rare military-points/very-rare exp-and-silver - each asserting the exact numeric grant against a level-30 test character plus a characteristic message substring), plus 3 existing `apply_mine_wall_reward` end-to-end tests updated for the new parameter. This closes every `handle_mining_result` branch; only `CDR_GOLEMKEYHOLDER` (`keyhold_fight_driver`) remains for Area 12. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2865 core (unchanged) + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1128 server [+5], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 12s boot-smoke confirmed hundreds of ticks with no panic (default area-1 data - this change touches the tick-loop item-use-completion dispatch, not login/protocol directly, but was smoked anyway since it changed a function signature threaded through `main.rs`'s async tick loop). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated to drop `handle_orb_find`/`handle_artifact_find`.
- 2026-07-08: Area 12 (`src/area/12/mine.c`) CLOSED: ported `CDR_GOLEMKEYHOLDER`/`keyhold_fight_driver` (`mine.c:1211-1270,1305-1313,1338-1345`), the locked-treasure-room boss golem `keyholder_door`/`IDR_MINEKEYDOOR` (already ported) spawns once a player pays 2000 gold. Confirmed `keyhold_fight_driver` is byte-for-byte identical to the already-ported `src/system/gatekeeper.c::gate_fight_driver` (`world::npc::gate_fight`) except for one constant (`TICKS*60*5` self-destruct vs. `TICKS*60*10`) and the absence of the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_GATEKEEPER` victim-assignment message; both drivers' `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 1, 0)` call means the true C mechanism is "attack whichever valid enemy is first seen", which in a private single-opponent room the summoning player. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area12/golemkeyholder.rs` (`GolemKeyholdDriverData` - same field shape as `GateFightDriverData`, kept as its own type per the one-file-per-NPC convention - plus `World::process_golemkeyhold_actions`/`process_golemkeyhold_tick`, a line-for-line adaptation of `gate_fight`'s tick body with the 5-minute timeout and no message-based victim assignment, since the spawn function already knows the summoning player's id directly), registered as `tick_npc::area12::golemkeyhold_driver_92`. New `CDR_GOLEMKEYHOLDER = 107` constant + `CharacterDriverState::GolemKeyhold` variant (added to the three big "every other driver state" exhaustive-match catch-alls in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, same as every prior new variant); `zone.rs`'s `create_character_with_id` gained a `CDR_GOLEMKEYHOLDER` branch mirroring `CDR_GATE_FIGHT`'s (zero-initialized state + `NT_CREATE` push) - this NPC is never statically zone-placed, only created at runtime. C's own `ch_died_driver` case for this driver is a no-op `return 1;` (no reward logic at all, unlike `gate_fight_dead`), so no death hook was needed. The spawn side (C `keyholder_door`'s tail, `mine.c:1196-1208`) required a small `ItemDriverOutcome` reshape: `MineKeyDoor` (the pure `mine_key_door_driver`'s output) is now consumed entirely inside `World::apply_item_driver_outcome`, which synthesizes a new `MineKeyDoorOpened { golem_nr, room_x, room_y, .. }` carrying the already-computed free-room coordinates (`first_free_mine_keyholder_room`) back out to `ugaris-server`, since the pure item driver has no map access to pick the room itself - same "outcome enrichment after teleport" shape `MineKeyDoorBusy` already established for the failure path. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/mine.rs::spawn_keyholder_golem` (`ZoneLoader`-needing golem instantiation, following `spawn_normal_golem`/`gate_enter_test_spawn_room`'s established shape: `format!("keyholder_golem{golem_nr}")` template lookup, explicit `hp`/`endurance`/`mana` = `value[0][...] * POWERSCALE`, `dir = DX_LEFTUP`, `rest_x`/`rest_y` standing in for C's `tmpx`/`tmpy` "return to post" coordinates - the same substitution `gate_enter_test_spawn_room` made - set to the golem's own spawn tile 4 east of the player's teleport target, and `driver_state.victim` set directly to the summoning player's id), wired from `tick_item_use_keyassembly::dispatch_keyassembly_outcome`'s new `MineKeyDoorOpened` arm. 7 new core tests (`world/tests/golemkeyholder.rs`, mirroring `gate_fight.rs`'s test shape: NT_CREATE creation-time seeding, 5-minute self-destruct timing both sides, adjacent-attack, walk-toward-distant-visible-victim, return-to-post-when-no-victim, give-up-chasing-invisible-victim) and 2 new server tests (`tests/mine.rs`: template-driven stat/dir/rest-position/victim assignment, silent no-op for an unknown `golem_nr` template) plus 1 existing core test (`world_mine_key_door_consumes_gold_and_teleports_to_first_free_room`) updated for the `MineKeyDoorOpened` rename. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2872 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1130 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 12s boot-smoke with `--area-id 12` confirmed "loaded area zone map" (119 character templates, incl. the 9 `keyholder_golemN` templates) and "entering Rust game loop" with no panic. This closes Area 12 in full.
- 2026-07-08: Area 14 (`src/area/14/random.c`) STARTED: ported `shrine_indecisiveness`/`shrine_bribes` (`random.c:1780-1843`) plus the `sendquestlog(cn, ch[cn].player)` resend every successful `shrine_*` function in this file calls as its last line - previously missing for all 8 already-ported random-shrine kinds too, not just these two new ones. `apply_random_shrine_indecisiveness` (`area_apply.rs`) loops all `CHARACTER_VALUE_NAMES.len()` values, skipping any already raised past `skillmax` (C: `if (ch[cn].value[1][n] > skillmax(cn)) continue;`), then calls the existing `lower_value` 3x per remaining value - required bumping `lower_value`/`skillmax`/`bare_value` from `pub(crate) fn` (item-driver-crate-internal) to `pub fn` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/scrolls.rs` (with an explicit `pub use scrolls::{bare_value, lower_value, skillmax};` alongside the module's existing `pub(crate) use scrolls::*;` in `item_driver/mod.rs`, keeping every other `scrolls` helper crate-internal) since `ugaris-server::area_apply` is a different crate and `lower_value` itself already no-ops correctly at the `skill_start` floor / non-raisable values, matching C's own internal `lower_value` guards exactly. `apply_random_shrine_bribes` ports the `level = min(level+5, shrine_level)` abuse-avoidance clamp (same pattern as edge/braveness/continuity), the `want`/`need` `level_value` thresholds, the gold-pickpocket-vs-`NotEnoughGold` gate, and an `almost_empty` result field driving C's `want < val ? "almost " : ""` feedback-text selector (the two-message split - "You feel a hand..." then "Shocked, you reach for your purse and find it %sempty." - is reproduced as two separate `feedback.push` calls in `tick_item_use_shrines.rs`, matching C's two separate `log_char` calls). The questlog resend is a new private `resend_random_shrine_questlog(runtime, character_id)` helper in `tick_item_use_shrines.rs` (same re-borrow-after-first-match pattern as `mine.rs::apply_military_mission_silver_check`'s pre-existing `legacy_questlog_payload`/`sessions_for_character`/`send_to_session` idiom), called from every "Used"/success arm of the `RandomShrineUse` `kind` match (Security/Jobless/Edge/Kindness/Death/Vitality/Braveness/Continuity/Indecisiveness/Bribes) - never on a blocked/early-return arm, matching C's placement of `sendquestlog` as the unconditional last line of each function's success path only. Only `shrine_welding` (`random.c:1845-2026`, plus its `can_receive_mod`/`can_give_mod` helpers) remains unported in this file; per the research done this iteration it needs a new `World`-level `recompute_item_requirements` wrapper around the existing-but-private `set_item_requirements` (`world/special_item.rs`) before it can be written, since welding needs to mutate two different equipped items' modifiers simultaneously plus recompute both items' level/skill requirements - left as a clearly-scoped follow-up slice rather than rushed this iteration. 8 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area_apply.rs`: indecisiveness (3x-lowers-raisable-values-only-Speed-untouched, `NOEXP` block) and bribes (partial-pickpocket-with-`almost_empty`-true, full-pickpocket-at-exactly-`need`-gold, below-`need`-blocks, `NOEXP`-blocks). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2872 core (unchanged) + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1136 server [+8], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 10s boot-smoke confirmed hundreds of ticks with no panic (default area-1 data; this change touches the tick-loop item-use-completion dispatch). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`, REMAINING note updated to only list `shrine_welding`.
- 2026-07-08: Area 14 (`src/area/14/random.c`) CLOSED: ported `shrine_welding` (`random.c:1929-2013`) plus its `can_receive_mod`/`can_give_mod` helpers (`:1845-1928`), the last remaining random-shrine effect. New `World::apply_random_shrine_welding` plus a public `World::recompute_item_requirements` wrapper around the previously-private `set_item_requirements` (both `world/special_item.rs`, per the prior iteration's REMAINING note) reproduce C's two-pass weighted-pick exactly: count every worn item's donatable modifier slots (`shrine_can_give_mod`, excluding weapon/armor/speed/demon/light mods), `RANDOM(cnt)`-pick one, then count every *other* worn item that can receive that modifier (`shrine_can_receive_mod` - a real C quirk kept verbatim: `V_SPEED` is *not* excluded from its "at most 2 other generic mods" cap, unlike `can_give_mod`'s own exclusion list), `RANDOM(cnt)`-pick a receiver, swap the modifier over, zero it on the donor, and rewrite both items' descriptions (unless "Christmas") via `recompute_item_requirements`. `RandomShrineWeldingResult` (`NotPowerfulEnough`/`NotPaying`/`Contempt`/`Regret`/`Bug`/`Used{item1_name,item2_name}`) is re-exported from `world::special_item` and dispatched in `tick_item_use_shrines.rs`'s new `Welding` arm (the file's `_` catch-all for `RandomShrineKind` is now dead code and removed, since every variant has an explicit arm), calling `player.mark_random_shrine_used`/`resend_random_shrine_questlog` only on `Used`, matching every other `RandomShrineUse` kind's success-only-`shrine_set` precedent. 7 new focused tests in `world/tests/special_item.rs` (underpowered/non-paying gates, contempt with no worn items, regret with a single donor and no receiver, a full weld end-to-end asserting both items' modifiers/descriptions/`CF_ITEMS`, the weapon/armor/speed/demon/light donation exclusion, and the `NOENHANCE`/driver-item exclusion). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2879 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1136 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean. 12s boot-smoke confirmed hundreds of ticks with no panic (default area-1 data; this change touches the tick-loop item-use-completion dispatch). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 14 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 16 (`src/area/16/forest.c`) CLOSED: ported the file's three character drivers (`imp_driver`/`william_driver`/`hermit_driver`, `CDR_FORESTIMP`/`CDR_FORESTWILLIAM`/`CDR_FORESTHERMIT`) plus `CDR_FORESTMONSTER`'s `monster_dead` death hook - `IDR_FORESTCHEST` (the two-key money chest, gated on `PlayerRuntime::area3_ppd.imp_flags`) was already fully ported by an earlier iteration, confirmed via the pre-existing `item_driver/area16_forest.rs`/`area_apply.rs::apply_forest_chest`. New one-file-per-NPC modules under `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area16/`: `hermit.rs` (the simplest - a self-contained 8-state greeting chain unlocking `QLOG` 24 "The Spider Queen", only `hermit_state`); `william.rs` (`QLOG` 22 "Impish Bear Hunt" + `QLOG` 23 "Praying Mantis Stew" - reproduces a real, deliberately-preserved C quirk where `case 0`'s greeting fires *before* its own `questlog_isdone(22)` early-out (so it always speaks, even on a repeat visit after the whole chain is done) while `case 3`'s otherwise-identical early-out speaks *nothing* (the `questlog_isdone(23)` check comes first there) - neither early-out sets C's `didsay`, so neither stamps `last_talk`/turns to face the player, documented and tested both ways; the mantis turn-in's `give_money(co, 2000, ...)` direct-gold reward is deferred entirely to `ugaris-server`'s `apply_forest_william_events`, the only one of the three area-16 appliers that needs to be `async` since it calls `achievement::give_money` - the literal C-function-name port, not the differently-scoped `award_swap_money_converted_achievement` some other files' carried-item rewards use); `imp.rs` (the most complex - an ambient-invisible NPC that only reveals itself for ~8 seconds after speaking or ~3 seconds after a 1-in-4-chance heal-reflex on a low-HP `NT_SEEHIT` sighting; ports C's `case 7`/`case 8` and `case 9`/`case 10` switch-fallthroughs as literal nested `if`s executing both cases' bodies in one message pass rather than deferring to the next tick, and a new `imp_hardkill_weapon_facts` helper - deliberately *not* reusing `world::npc::area3::clara::clara_hardkill_weapon_facts`, since C's own `has_item(co, IID_HARDKILL)` scans every inventory/cursor slot, not just the worn right hand `clara`'s equivalent check does). All three share a new file-local `FOREST_QA` 9-row small-talk table (`world/npc/area16/mod.rs`, `william`/`hermit` only - `imp` has no `NT_TEXT` handling in C at all), distinct from `world::npc::area3::AREA3_QA`/`world::npc::area15`'s `CLARA_QA` (every `area/*.c` file keeps its own file-local copy in C). `CDR_FORESTMONSTER` (wolves/bears/skeletons, confirmed via `zones/16/forest.chr`) reuses the SimpleBaddy AI end-to-end exactly like `CDR_SWAMPMONSTER`/`CDR_PENTER` (widened the same 4 `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` gates in `npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, added the matching `zone.rs` spawn branch) - its `monster_dead` death hook (`forest.c:817-853`) splits the same way `apply_swamp_monster_death_from_hurt_event`/`apply_area1_monster_death_from_hurt_event` already do: `World::apply_forest_monster_death_driver` (`world/hurt.rs`, field-for-field identical to the swamp version but with area 16's own `x:182..=192,y:185..=192` bounding box and `+= 6` charge amount) for the item weapon-glow half, `ugaris-server::apply_forest_monster_death_from_hurt_event` for the `imp_kills`(sprite `306` "Bear" + `imp_state==2`)/`hermit_state`(`CF_HARDKILL` flag + `hermit_state==4`) counter halves that need `PlayerRuntime`. New `IID_AREA16_MANTIS` item-id constant (`item_driver/ids.rs`) - the only genuinely-missing constant found (`CDR_FOREST*`/`DRD_*DRIVER` were all new-driver registrations, expected); every `area3_ppd.imp_state`/`imp_kills`/`imp_flags`/`william_state`/`hermit_state` `PlayerRuntime` accessor this file needed already existed from earlier area-3 iterations (Clara/Kelly's shared-PPD precedent), confirmed via research before writing any code, so no new persistence surface was needed. `himname` (C `tool.c:1528-1535`, "him"/"her"/"it" pronoun helper) got its first Rust port, local to `imp.rs`. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area16.rs` (`forest_imp_player_facts`/`forest_william_player_facts`/`forest_hermit_player_facts` + their matching `apply_*_events`, mirroring `area3.rs`'s established `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split shape) and `tick_npc/area16.rs` (`forest_imp_driver_94`/`forest_william_driver_95`/`forest_hermit_driver_96`, registered as the new last three entries in `tick_npc::run_all`). 28 new focused tests: 25 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/{forest_hermit,forest_imp,forest_william}.rs` (greeting/state-advance chains, both William early-out quirks, both imp switch-fallthrough directions plus their non-fallthrough negatives, the imp SEEHIT heal reflex both hitting and missing its 1-in-4 roll via seed-search, and every NT_GIVE/repeat-reset path) plus 2 in `world/tests/hurt.rs` (`forest_monster_death_driver_upgrades_midnight_stone_circle_weapon`/`_rejects_repeated_or_non_midnight_circles`, mirroring the pre-existing swamp-monster pair) and 2 new `ServerRuntime`-level tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` (`lethal_forest_monster_hurt_counts_imp_kills_and_advances_state_past_twenty`, `lethal_forest_monster_hurt_with_hardkill_flag_advances_hermit_state` - the latter needed a real `IID_HARDKILL`-carrying weapon on the killer to actually land lethal damage, since `CF_HARDKILL` on the target zeroes incoming damage from any other weapon, `hurt.rs:119-125`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2918 core [+27] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both default area-1 data and live `--area-id 16` real `zones/16/forest.*` data (116 placed characters, "entering Rust game loop", 15+ seconds of ticks, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 16 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 15 (`src/area/15/swamp.c`) CLOSED: ported `clara_driver`'s runtime NPC message-loop integration (`swamp.c:510-764`), the last remaining gap after earlier iterations had already ported the three `IDR_SWAMP*` item drivers, the pure `clara_dialogue_step` state machine, and `monster_dead`'s Clara-facing death hook. `World::process_clara_actions`/`process_clara_messages` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area3/clara.rs`, extended rather than moved - Clara stays under `area3/` since her state shares `area3_ppd` with `world::kelly`, per the file's own updated module doc comment) drive the existing pure `clara_dialogue_step` from a real `NT_CHAR` sighting handler (talk-cooldown/current-victim/distance/visibility guards copied digit-for-digit from `swamp.c:530-562`, then the pure state machine's `military_points`/`military_exp` reward applied directly via `World::give_military_pts_from_npc`, same precedent as `world::kelly`'s per-shrine reward), a new `NT_TEXT` handler wired through a **new, distinct** `CLARA_QA` table (swamp.c has its own file-local 8-row `qa[]`/`analyse_text_driver` copy - *not* the same array as `world::npc::area3::AREA3_QA`, which belongs to the separate `area3.c` translation unit; only the `area3_ppd` struct layout is actually shared per Clara's own C comment - `CLARA_QA`'s doc comment documents this distinction explicitly, including that `swamp.c`'s own `analyse_text_driver` checks `CF_PLAYER | CF_PLAYERLIKE` and has its own distance check commented out, unlike `area3.c`'s active `>12` check `world::kelly` inherits), and a trivial `NT_GIVE` give-everything-back handler (Clara has no turn-in item, unlike `kelly`/`seymour`). New `ClaraPlayerFacts`/`ClaraOutcomeEvent` types follow the established `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split (`clara_state`/`kelly_state`/hardkill-weapon-carry facts snapshotted by the caller; `UpdateClaraState`/`QuestOpen`/`QuestDone` events applied by `ugaris-server`); a new `pub fn clara_hardkill_weapon_facts` helper (also in `clara.rs`) encapsulates the `WN_RHAND`-item/`IID_HARDKILL`/`drdata[37]` lookup so `ugaris-server`'s `area3.rs::clara_player_facts`/`apply_clara_events` (registered as `tick_npc::area3::clara_driver_93`, the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`) doesn't need direct access to the crate-internal `IID_HARDKILL` constant. Two real, independent bugs were also fixed as part of this slice, both required for correctness: (1) a duplicate `EXP_AREA15_HARDKILL` constant in `character_driver.rs` was `5_000` instead of C's actual `#define EXP_AREA15_HARDKILL 7500` (`quest_exp.h:43`) - the already-correct `crate::quest::quest_exp::EXP_AREA15_HARDKILL: i64 = 7500` existed unused in a different module; `character_driver.rs`'s copy is now a `pub const ... = crate::quest::quest_exp::EXP_AREA15_HARDKILL as i32;` re-export instead of an independent (and wrong) literal; (2) `CDR_SWAMPMONSTER` (the `swamp25n`/`27n`/`29n`/`31n` "Swamp Beast" spawns, confirmed via `zones/15/swamp.chr`'s real `driver=56` + SimpleBaddy-style `arg=` entries) was missing from the `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY | CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER | CDR_PENTER` AI-gate widening in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` (4 call sites) and from `zone.rs`'s spawn-time `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` dispatch - C's own `ch_driver`'s `CDR_SWAMPMONSTER` case is an unconditional one-line tail call to `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` (`swamp.c:807-809`), the exact same shape as the already-ported `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_DUNGEONFIGHTER` precedent, so every live swamp-beast character was silently never getting SimpleBaddy AI wired up at all before this fix. Also added `zone.rs`'s missing `CDR_SWAMPCLARA` spawn-time `ClaraDriverData::default()` initialization (C never parses zone-file args for this driver, same as every other zero-arg NPC). Also renamed `ClaraDriverData.last_talk_tick: i32` to `last_talk: u64` to match every sibling area-3 driver's field name/type convention (`ThomasDriverData`/`SeymourDriverData`/`SirJonesDriverData`/`KellyDriverData` all use `last_talk: u64`) - this field had never been wired to a real tick comparison before this iteration, so the rename carried no behavioral risk. 17 new focused tests in `world/tests/clara.rs` (greeting/state-advance, the two kelly-gated fallthrough cases both directions, the hardkill-quest military-point reward both granted and skipped-on-repeat, quest-21 completion with its first-time bonus, the 5-bucket `repeat` state-reset sweep, a canned `hello` QA reply, and give-back), plus 2 new `zone.rs` tests (`swampmonster_template_installs_simple_baddy_state_from_arg`, `clara_template_installs_default_clara_driver_state`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2891 core [+12, net of some pre-existing test-count churn from the `mod.rs` reordering] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1136 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both default area-1 data and live `--area-id 15` real `zones/15/swamp.*` data (11 placed characters, "entering Rust game loop", hundreds of ticks, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 15 marked `[x]` - fully ported, no remaining gaps.
- 2026-07-08: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) STARTED: ported `CDR_TWOSKELLY`/`skelly`+`skelly_dead` (`two.c:2761-2943`), "Scarcewind" the murdered governor's raised-ghost quest giver (quest 30, "The Old Governor's Cross") - the first Two-City *character* driver ported (the file's item drivers - `pick_door`/`pick_chest`/`burndown`/`bookcase`/`colortile`/`skelraise` - were already fully ported by earlier iterations). Completed the existing `CDR_TWOSKELLY` registry-stub scaffolding (const/`CharacterDriverState`/data struct already present, zero logic) into a real driver: `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/two_skelly.rs`'s `World::process_two_skelly_actions`/`process_two_skelly_tick` port the per-tick body digit-for-digit - the `NT_CREATE` alive-tick stamp, the `NT_CHAR` 4-state greeting ladder (0: greeting + `questlog_open(30)`; 1: emote + riddle; 2/3: silent) gated by the same talk-cooldown/current-victim/distance/visibility guards `world::npc::area16::william` established (only `CF_PLAYER`, not `CF_PLAYERLIKE`, matching C's own narrower check here), the `NT_TEXT` handler wired through a new file-local `TWOCITY_QA` table (`world/npc/area17/mod.rs`, only the 8 entries this NPC needs - `guest`/`citizen`/`honor`/`enemy`/`chat`/`bribe`/`threaten`/`pay bribe`/`pay`/`buy pass`/`status`/`pay a fee`/`i am done` belong to the still-unported guard/barkeeper/servant/thiefguard/thiefmaster/sanwyn drivers and are deliberately not pre-added), the `NT_GIVE` cross-turn-in (quest completion + `destroy_items_by_template_id` cleanup of the green/red puzzle keys, matching C's belt-and-suspenders triple `destroy_item_byID` call) vs. give-back-anything-else branch, and the 30-second self-destruct (`World::remove_character`, following the `world::npc::area12::golemkeyholder` precedent) independent of whether it ever talked to anyone. A real C quirk reproduced exactly: `analyse_text_driver`'s canned QA replies use `say()` here (not `quiet_say()` like `william_driver`'s own reply path) - the existing `analyse_text_qa` helper is dispatch-agnostic (just returns the interpolated text), so this port calls `World::npc_say` at the call site instead of `npc_quiet_say`, a real per-file difference now documented in the module's own doc comment rather than silently copying william's choice. Found and fixed a real, adjacent gap this NPC's own move-back-to-post/self-destruct logic exposed: `ugaris-server::area_apply::raise_skeleton_from_template` (the function every raised-skeleton template, including `quest_skeleton`, spawns through) never set the newly-raised character's `rest_x`/`rest_y` (this codebase's `tmpx`/`tmpy` substitution) to its actual spawn tile, unlike C's own `create_drop_char` (`tool.c:3319-3321`, `ch[cn].tmpx = ch[cn].x` right after placement) - without this fix every raised skeleton would treat `(0, 0)` as its home tile and never settle down long enough to reach its self-destruct check. `TwoSkelly` was also removed from the generic `CharacterDriverKind`/`from_legacy_id`/`legacy_id` stub-dispatch surface in `character_driver.rs` (plus its now-obsolete stub-behavior test blocks) - it runs entirely through its own `tick_npc::area17::two_skelly_driver_97` pass now, same as every other fully-ported NPC; the driver constant/`CharacterDriverState` variant/data struct/its own dedicated data-shape test all stay (mirroring `CDR_FORESTWILLIAM`'s precedent, confirmed absent from `CharacterDriverKind` for comparison). New `IID_AREA17_GREENKEY`/`IID_AREA17_REDKEY`/`IID_AREA17_CROSS` item-id constants (`item_driver/ids.rs`, hex values confirmed against `src/common/item_id.h:172-175`). New `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`CDR_TWOSKELLY` -> `TwoSkellyDriverData::default()`) applies to both the (never-used-in-practice) static zone-placement path and the real runtime-raise path, since both go through the same `instantiate_character_template`. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area17.rs` (`two_skelly_player_facts`/`apply_two_skelly_events`, mirroring `area16.rs`'s established `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split shape) and `tick_npc/area17.rs` (`two_skelly_driver_97`, registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`). 12 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/two_skelly.rs`: `NT_CREATE` alive-tick stamping, the state-0/1 greeting/riddle advance (with quest-open assertion), both silent states, the QA `repeat` reset (both the `<=2`-resets and the `>2`-ignored branches), a canned `hello` QA reply's speaker-name substitution, the cross turn-in (quest-done event + green/red key cleanup + skeleton's own cursor item destroyed) vs. an unrelated item's give-back, and both self-destruct timing boundaries (needed an explicit `rest_x = x`/`rest_y = y` in the test setup itself, reproducing the `raise_skeleton_from_template` fix above, since the `ugaris-core` test harness calls `World::spawn_character` directly and doesn't go through the `ugaris-server`-side glue). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2929 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 marked `[~]`: `CDR_TWOGUARD`/`CDR_TWOBARKEEPER`/`CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`CDR_TWOSANWYN`/`CDR_TWOALCHEMIST` (plus `CDR_TWOROBBER`'s trivial `robber_dead` death hook) remain - suggested next-slice order (smallest/least-new-persistence-surface first): `CDR_TWOALCHEMIST` (1 new item id) -> `CDR_TWOSANWYN` (3 new item ids + military-points integration, `seymour.rs` precedent) -> `CDR_TWOBARKEEPER` (first driver needing new `legal_status`/`citizen_status`/`barkeeper_state` PPD accessors) -> `CDR_TWOGUARD` (needs the shared `call_guard`/`illegal_place` infra plus most remaining PPD fields) -> `CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD` -> `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` (the biggest quest-chain, best done last).
- 2026-07-08: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOALCHEMIST`/`alchemist`+its no-op `ch_died_driver`/`ch_respawn_driver` (`two.c:2950-3144`), "Cervik" the spider-poison quest giver (quest 31, "Spider Poison") - the smallest remaining Two-City NPC per the prior iteration's suggested slice order. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/alchemist.rs` (`World::process_two_alchemist_actions`/`process_two_alchemist_tick`) ports the per-tick body digit-for-digit: the `NT_CHAR` 6-state greeting ladder (0: greeting + `questlog_open(31)`; 1: favor request; 2: dig-site hint; 3: red-spider hint; 4/5: silent) gated by the same talk-cooldown/current-victim/distance/visibility guards `two_skelly.rs` established, the `NT_TEXT` handler reusing the existing file-local `TWOCITY_QA` table (only its first 8 rows are needed, same as `two_skelly`), and the `NT_GIVE` spider-poison turn-in vs. give-back-anything-else branch. A real C quirk reproduced exactly and documented in the module's own doc comment: the "repeat" QA response can only reset `alchemist_state` back to `0` while it is still `<= 4` - once a turn-in sets it to `5`, nothing in this file ever resets it, so in practice this repeatable-flagged quest only completes once per character; ported as-is, not "fixed". Because the potion reward (`combo_potion3` if `ch[co].level < 30` else `security_potion`, gated on the quest completion count being the 1st/3rd/7th/10th time - `tmp == 1 || 3 || 7 || 10`) depends on `PlayerRuntime::quest_log`'s completion count (only known after `QuestLog::complete_legacy` runs) and on `ZoneLoader` item instantiation, both outside `World`'s reach, the full completion/reward/`say` sequence is deferred to a new `TwoAlchemistOutcomeEvent::QuestDone{player_id, alchemist_id}` event finished entirely by `ugaris-server::area17.rs`'s `apply_two_alchemist_events` (which now also takes `&mut ZoneLoader`) - unlike `two_skelly`'s quest 30, which has no reward branching and could finish its `say`s inside `World` itself. New `CDR_TWOALCHEMIST = 71` driver constant, `CharacterDriverState::TwoAlchemist` variant (added to the three exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, always right after `ForestHermit(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`cervik` template, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:3847-3907`), and `IID_AREA17_POISON` item-id constant (`item_driver/ids.rs`, hex value confirmed against `src/common/item_id.h:176`) - `twocity_alchemist_state`/`set_twocity_alchemist_state` `PlayerRuntime` accessors and quest 31's `QUEST_TABLE`/`init_twocity_quests` metadata already existed from earlier iterations, confirmed via research before writing any code. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_alchemist_driver_98` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`) and the matching `two_alchemist_player_facts`/`apply_two_alchemist_events` glue in `area17.rs`. 12 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/alchemist.rs` (greeting/state-advance through state 1, both silent states, the QA `repeat` reset both directions - `<=4`-resets and `==5`-ignored, a canned `hello` QA reply's speaker-name substitution, the poison turn-in at-or-below-state-4 asserting both the cursor item and a matching inventory item are destroyed with no `say` fired yet, poison-while-already-turned-in and an unrelated item both handed back), plus 1 new `character_driver.rs` data-shape test. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2939 core [+12+1, note some pre-existing per-run count variance] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed hundreds of ticks with no panic (default area-1 data). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOGUARD`/`CDR_TWOBARKEEPER`/`CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`'s death hook/`CDR_TWOSANWYN` remain - see the prior iteration's suggested next-slice order (now starting at `CDR_TWOSANWYN`).
- 2026-07-08: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOSANWYN`/`sanwyn` (`two.c:2249-2484`), "Sanwyn" the military quest giver, quest 29 ("Dirty Hands") - per the prior iteration's suggested next-slice order. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/sanwyn.rs` (`World::process_two_sanwyn_actions`/`process_two_sanwyn_tick`) ports the per-tick body digit-for-digit: the `NT_CHAR` 8-state greeting ladder (0: greeting + `questlog_open(29)`; 1/2/3/4/5: lore lines, states 1/5 interpolating the player's *own* army-rank string via `army_rank_for_points(player.military_points)`/`army_rank_name`, matching C's `get_army_rank_string(co)`; 6: silent, waiting for documents; 7: "Dirty hands indeed!" + `questlog_done(29)`; 8: done) gated by the same talk-cooldown/current-victim/distance/visibility guards `two_skelly.rs`/`alchemist.rs` established, the `NT_TEXT` handler reusing the existing file-local `TWOCITY_QA` table (first 8 rows, same as its siblings), and a `NT_GIVE` branch that is a genuine structural departure from `two_skelly`/`alchemist`'s single-item turn-in: three distinct palace notes (`IID_AREA17_PALACENOTE1/2/3`, new item-id constants confirmed against `item_id.h:161-163`), each gated on its own bit (`1`/`2`/`4`) in a new `sanwyn_bits` PPD field (`PlayerRuntime::twocity_sanwyn_bits`/`set_twocity_sanwyn_bits`, `TWOCITY_PPD_SANWYN_BITS_OFFSET = 17*4` - confirmed against the C `struct twocity_ppd` field order in `common/two_ppd.h`, immediately after the pre-existing `sanwyn_state` at index 16), promoting `sanwyn_state` to `7` only once all three bits are set (`sanwyn_bits == 7`), and awarding `15` military points (exp `min(level_value(level)/5, 15000)`) via `World::give_military_pts_from_npc` on *every* accepted note (up to 3 times per character, matching `questlog.c`'s own "exp awarded in driver, 45000 total" comment on quest 29's zero `exp` table field). Because this reward only needs `Character::level` (already visible to `World`), it is applied directly inside `World` rather than deferred to a server-side event like `alchemist`'s potion reward - `TwoSanwynOutcomeEvent` only carries `UpdateSanwynState`/`UpdateSanwynBits`/`QuestOpen`/`QuestDone`, all plain PPD/quest-log bookkeeping finished by `ugaris-server::area17.rs`'s new `apply_two_sanwyn_events`. New `CDR_TWOSANWYN = 69` driver constant, `CharacterDriverState::TwoSanwyn` variant (added to the three exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, right after `ForestHermit(_)`, before `TwoAlchemist(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`sanwyn` template, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:2704-2765`, `driver=69`, no `arg=` line). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_sanwyn_driver_99` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`) and the matching `two_sanwyn_player_facts`/`apply_two_sanwyn_events` glue in `area17.rs`. 12 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/sanwyn.rs` (greeting/quest-open, the army-rank-string state-1 line, silent waiting state 6, quest completion at state 7, silent done state 8, both `repeat` directions - `<=6` resets to `0`, `==8` resets to `7` not `0` - a canned `hello` QA reply, first-note-turn-in awarding nonzero military points, third-note completing the set and promoting state to 7, an already-turned-in note and an unrelated item both handed back, and a note received after quest completion also handed back), plus 1 new `character_driver.rs` data-shape test. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2953 core [+16, incl. the quest/PPD-accessor tests that already existed from earlier scaffolding] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=1` plus hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOGUARD`/`CDR_TWOBARKEEPER`/`CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`'s death hook remain - suggested next-slice order per the prior iteration's ledger entry, now starting at `CDR_TWOBARKEEPER` (first driver needing new `legal_status`/`citizen_status`/`barkeeper_state` PPD accessors).
- 2026-07-08: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOBARKEEPER`/`barkeeper` (`two.c:770-958`), the Two Towns tavern barkeeper/guest-pass broker into Exkordon - per the prior iteration's suggested next-slice order, and the first Two-City driver needing the shared `legal_status`/`legal_fine`/`citizen_status` `twocity_ppd` fields (also used by the still-unported `guard_driver`). New shared constants in `world/npc/area17/mod.rs`: `LS_CLEAN`/`LS_FINE`/`LS_DEAD` (`legal_status`) and `CS_ENEMY`/`CS_GUEST`/`CS_CITIZEN`/`CS_HONOR` (`citizen_status`), matching C's `#define`s at `two.c:271-278` verbatim - these are deliberately placed in the shared `mod.rs` (not `barkeeper.rs`) since `guard_driver` will need them too. New `twocity_ppd` field offsets in `player/misc.rs` (`TWOCITY_PPD_LEGAL_STATUS_OFFSET`/`_LEGAL_FINE_OFFSET`/`_CITIZEN_STATUS_OFFSET`/`_BARKEEPER_STATE_OFFSET`/`_BARKEEPER_LAST_OFFSET`, all computed from C's `struct twocity_ppd` field order in `common/two_ppd.h`: `legal_status`=0, `legal_fine`=1, `citizen_status`=2, `barkeeper_state`=6, `barkeeper_last`=26) plus matching `PlayerRuntime` accessor pairs in `player/twocity.rs` (`twocity_legal_status`/`twocity_legal_fine`/`twocity_citizen_status`/`twocity_barkeeper_state`/`twocity_barkeeper_last`, all following the existing `twocity_sanwyn_state`-style read/write pattern). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/barkeeper.rs` (`World::process_two_barkeeper_actions`/`process_two_barkeeper_tick`) ports the per-tick body digit-for-digit: the `NT_CHAR` 3-state ladder (0: greeting, barkeeper_state->1; 1: if `citizen_status < CS_GUEST || legal_status == LS_DEAD`, offer a guest pass with a `legal_status`-conditional price line - `LS_FINE` shows the accumulated fine plus a running total, `LS_DEAD` asks a flat 2500G "forgiveness" price, otherwise a flat 150G - and stamps `barkeeper_last`/advances to state 2, *silently doing nothing* (no `didsay`, no state advance) if the player already has a pass; state 2 is dead code in C itself - `ppd->barkeeper_state = 2` reassigns the field to the value it already holds - ported as a bare `_ => {}` with the C oddity documented rather than reproduced literally), the `NT_TEXT` handler extending the existing file-local `TWOCITY_QA` table with a new `buy pass`/answer_code-13 row (the only remaining row this iteration ported; `guest`/`citizen`/`honor`/etc. still belong to `guard_driver`) - case 2 ("repeat") resets state to 0, case 13 ("buy pass") computes the exact same three-tier cost (`15000+legal_fine`/`250000`/`15000` raw gold units) as the display text and calls a private `take_money`-equivalent (`two_barkeeper_take_money`, same precedent as `world::gatekeeper::gate_take_money`/`world::npc::area1::james::james_take_money`) directly against `Character::gold` (which `World` can see, unlike the `citizen_status`/`legal_status`/`legal_fine` writeback, deferred via `TwoBarkeeperOutcomeEvent::BuyPass`) - and the `NT_GIVE` handler, a genuine structural departure from every sibling Two-City NPC: C unconditionally destroys *any* item handed over (`two.c:931-938`) with no give-back fallback at all, ported as `two_barkeeper_handle_give_message` with no facts/events parameters. The "buy pass" hint text uses `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` markers around the literal keyword in C (`two.c:845,851,854`) - restored via the project's established `COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_STR_RESET` sentinels + `World::npc_say_bytes` (not the plain `npc_say`/`String`-based `pending_area_texts` queue), same precedent as `world::npc::area1::james`'s `buy advice` hint. New `CDR_TWOBARKEEPER = 63` driver constant (confirmed against `drvlib.h:110`, notably *lower* than `CDR_TWOSANWYN`/`CDR_TWOSKELLY`/`CDR_TWOALCHEMIST`'s already-ported 69-71, since C's own numeric ids don't follow file-declaration order), `CharacterDriverState::TwoBarkeeper` variant (added to the three exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, right after `TwoAlchemist(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`barkeeper` template, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:1756-1816`, `driver=63`, no `arg=` line). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_barkeeper_driver_100` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`, using `current_unix_time()` for C's `realtime`, same idiom as `kassim_driver_84`/`supermax_driver_85`) and the matching `two_barkeeper_player_facts`/`apply_two_barkeeper_events` glue in `area17.rs` (no quest of its own, so every event is a plain `twocity_ppd` writeback - no `ZoneLoader`/`QuestLog` dependency, unlike `alchemist`). 13 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/barkeeper.rs`: greeting/state-advance, all three state-1 price-line variants (clean/fine-with-total/dead, using `drain_pending_area_text_bytes` + `String::from_utf8_lossy` to check the `npc_say_bytes` payload, same pattern as `world::tests::james`), the already-a-guest silent no-op (asserting *no* `current_victim` stamp, since C's `didsay` never fires), the state-2 dead-code no-op, the `repeat` reset, a canned `hello` QA reply, both `buy pass` outcomes (enough gold - asserting the exact `Character::gold` deduction - and not enough gold - asserting `gold` is unchanged and no `BuyPass` event fires), the already-a-citizen decline, and the unconditional-destroy `NT_GIVE` behavior, plus 1 new `character_driver.rs` data-shape test. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2966 core [+13] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data (345 placed characters), 10+ seconds of ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOGUARD`/`CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`'s death hook remain - suggested next-slice order per the prior iteration's ledger entry, now starting at `CDR_TWOGUARD` (needs the shared `call_guard`/`illegal_place` infra plus most remaining PPD fields, and can now reuse this iteration's `LS_*`/`CS_*` constants and `legal_status`/`legal_fine`/`citizen_status` accessors).
- 2026-07-08: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOGUARD`/`guard_driver` (`two.c:325-742`) plus its `guard_dead` death hook (`two.c:744-769`) - the biggest single driver in this file and the last one needing wholly new shared infra. New `World::two_city_call_guard` (`world/npc/area17/mod.rs`, shared with the still-unported `servant_driver`, which also calls `call_guard`) ports `call_guard(cn, co)` (`two.c:219-237`): scans every live character for the nearest higher-level same-`group` character and pushes it an `NT_NPC`/`NTID_TWOCITY` alert, preserving a real C quirk digit-for-digit - the alert's packed `dat3` coordinate mixes the *caller's* `x` with the *target's* `y` (`ch[cn].x + ch[co].y * MAXMAP`), not a matched `(x, y)` pair from either character. New `illegal_place(x, y)`/`PLACES` (also `mod.rs`, a direct port of C's `places[]` bounding-box table, checked in declaration order) plus 5 new `TWOCITY_QA` rows (`pay`/`guest`/`citizen`/`honor`/`enemy`, answer codes 3/5/6/12) round out the shared table this driver needs beyond `barkeeper`'s `buy pass`. New `twocity_ppd` fields (`player/misc.rs` offsets + `player/twocity.rs` accessors, confirmed against `common/two_ppd.h`'s real field order): `current_guard`(3)/`current_guard_time`(4)/`last_attack`(5)/`guard_intro`(7). Split across two files to stay under the ~800-line NPC guideline (same precedent as `world::npc::area8::fdemon_army_movement`'s split from `fdemon_army.rs`): `guard.rs` (678 lines - module doc, the day/night torch on/off sensor reproducing C's real `check_dlight`/`check_light` two-signal logic digit-for-digit via new local helpers since the existing `see::check_light` combines both signals differently for vision purposes, the tick dispatch loop, the victim-timeout/fight-cascade/movement tail, `guard_parse`'s patrol-waypoint parsing, and the driver-data struct) and `guard_messages.rs` (648 lines - the six `NT_CHAR`/`NT_TEXT`/`NT_GOTHIT`/`NT_SEEHIT`/`NT_NPC` message handlers as a second `impl World` block). The single-victim self-defense simplification needed no extra collapsing here, unlike most ported NPCs: C's own `dat->current_victim` field already tracks exactly one player (for the leave/fine warning ladder), and every C call site that sets it also sets `dat->last_x`/`last_y`/`last_co` together, so this port's `TwoGuardDriverData::victim`/`victim_last_x`/`victim_last_y` directly represents all four C fields at once. `NT_CHAR` ports the full illegal-territory state machine: `LS_DEAD` immediate-attack, `place > citizen_status` territory-violation leave-warning ladder (state 0 warns + turns to face via `offset2dx`/`turn`, state 1 repeats every 10s with a 30s/60s-by-`CS_GUEST` escalate timeout, state 2 attacks), `LS_CLEAN`'s one-time `guard_intro` guest-pass-etiquette speech, and `LS_FINE`'s parallel fine ladder (15s repeat, 60s escalate). `NT_TEXT`'s "pay" command (`two_guard_handle_pay`) ports both the direct-`Character::gold` and bank-account-fallback branches - the bank fallback is a genuine deviation from `barkeeper`'s "buy pass" (which has no C-side bank fallback to begin with, not a missed precedent) and needed a new `bank_gold: i32` `TwoGuardPlayerFacts` field plus a `TwoGuardOutcomeEvent::bank_gold_deduction: Option<i32>` side channel (`PlayerRuntime::bank_gold` lives outside `World`, same as every other bank-touching driver). Given the driver's seven independently-mutable `twocity_ppd` fields (vs. `TwoSanwynOutcomeEvent`'s two), `TwoGuardOutcomeEvent` is one struct carrying a full new snapshot rather than one enum variant per field, applied by a single `apply_two_guard_events` loop in `ugaris-server::area17.rs` - a deliberate departure from the per-field-variant precedent, documented in the type's own doc comment. `NT_GOTHIT` ports both the low-HP `call_guard` alert (30s cooldown) and the attacking-a-guard fine (20G, `CS_GUEST` downgrades to `CS_ENEMY` instead), plus `standard_message_driver`'s `NT_GOTHIT` case (the *only* one of its three cases this driver's `(agressive=0, helper=0)` call actually reaches, since `NT_CHAR`/`NT_SEEHIT` both no-op under those flags - confirmed by reading `drvlib.c:2466-2540` fully) folded into the same handler, gated by `nofight_timer`. `NT_SEEHIT` ports the protect-an-ally fine (75G). `NT_NPC` ports `NTID_TWOCITY`'s called-to-help destination (`dat->tx`/`ty`, driving the movement tail's `swap_move_driver` chase) and `NTID_TWOCITY_PICK`'s lockpicking fine (30G) - including a real quirk preserved as-is: the `CS_GUEST` branch never sets `ppd->last_attack` (`two.c:646-649`), unlike every other fine site in this file. Two real bugs were found and fixed while writing tests against this driver: (1) the torch-creation branch had accidentally copied `world::robber`'s own `self.update_character(guard_id)` call after assigning a fresh torch to `WN_LHAND` - C's `guard_driver` (unlike `robber_driver`) never calls `update_char` there, and doing so was silently recomputing (and discarding) every manually-set `Character::values`/`hp` field via `recompute_character_values`, a real behavior difference between the two files' otherwise near-identical torch snippets, now documented in `guard.rs`; (2) confirmed (not a bug, but worth noting) that `player_driver_stop` - C's "pay" success branch cancels the payer's queued movement - has no Rust equivalent anywhere in the tree yet, a pre-existing cross-cutting gap, not guard-specific, left as a documented deviation. 30 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/guard.rs`: torch on/off in darkness vs. bright daylight, the illegal-territory leave-warning trigger, `LS_DEAD` immediate attack, the guest-pass intro speech, the fine-ladder state-0 warning, both "pay" success paths (direct gold and bank fallback) plus the both-insufficient failure, `NT_GOTHIT`'s guard-attack fine and low-HP call-guard alert (needing a second `process_two_guard_actions` call since `HashMap` iteration order between the two guards in one tick is unspecified), `NT_SEEHIT`'s ally-protection fine, `NTID_TWOCITY_PICK`'s lockpicking fine, a god-only citizen-status command and its non-god rejection, the `repeat` QA reset, `parse_two_guard_driver_args`'s patrol-pair parsing, and `two_city_call_guard`'s packed-coordinate quirk. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2984 core [+18] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data (345 placed characters, including live `CDR_TWOGUARD` patrol/stationary instances with real `patx=`/`paty=` waypoint args), 10+ seconds of ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`'s death hook remain - suggested next order: `CDR_TWOROBBER`'s `robber_dead` (trivial, likely a one-line stub per its own C comment) first, then `CDR_TWOSERVANT` (reuses `call_guard`, small state machine per its own `two.c` code around line 960-1055), then `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`, then `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` last (the biggest remaining quest chain).
- 2026-07-09: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOROBBER` (the Exkordon forest-camp robbers, `robber1`-`robber4`/`robber_guard`/`robber_baron` templates, `zones/17/two.chr`) plus its `robber_dead` death hook (`two.c:2211-2247`) - per the prior iteration's suggested next-slice order, and simpler than initially described ("trivial... likely a one-line stub" turned out to mean "reuses SimpleBaddy AI plus a small but non-trivial death hook", not "no-op"). C's own `ch_driver`'s `CDR_TWOROBBER` dispatch (`two.c:3163-3165`) is an unconditional tail call to `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)`, the same "reuse the SimpleBaddy AI end-to-end" shape as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_SWAMPMONSTER`/`CDR_FORESTMONSTER` before it: new `CDR_TWOROBBER: u16 = 68` driver constant (`character_driver.rs`, confirmed against `drvlib.h:116`, comment "robber (simple baddy with special death)"), widened the `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` gate (now also accepting `CDR_TWOROBBER`) at all 4 existing call sites in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` (the single-character and aggregate-iteration attack/noncombat dispatchers), and a new `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`push_driver_message(NT_CREATE, ...)` + `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`, mirroring `CDR_FORESTMONSTER`'s branch exactly) since the `robberN`/`robber_guard`/`robber_baron` templates carry the same `arg="aggressive=1;helper=0;scavenger=...;"` shape SimpleBaddy's own `NT_CREATE` handler parses. `robber_dead(cn, co)` is genuinely non-trivial (unlike `barkeeper`'s/`sanwyn`'s no-op `ch_died_driver` entries): it only bumps a `thief_killed[N]` bounty-tracking counter (read by the still-unported `thiefmaster_driver`'s scoreboard, `two.c:1856-1857`/`1931`) while the killer's own `thief_state` sits in the narrow `6..=9` active-bounty-hunt window, mapping the dead robber's `level` (35/39/43/47/51/55) to an index 0-5 via a `switch` whose `default:` arm is a log-only `elog(...)` (not ported, same precedent as every other bare `elog` call in this file, e.g. `guard.rs:618`). Ported as `ugaris-server::world_events::death_hooks::apply_two_robber_death_from_hurt_event` (needs `PlayerRuntime`, so lives in `ugaris-server` not `World`, same shape as `apply_two_guard_death_from_hurt_event`/`apply_asturin_death_from_hurt_event`), wired into `world_events::mod.rs`'s `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` fan-out right after the guard hook. New `PlayerRuntime::set_twocity_thief_killed(index, value)` setter (`player/twocity.rs`) fills the write-side gap next to the pre-existing read-only `twocity_thief_killed(index)` getter and `mark_twocity_burndown_kill`'s own direct-index-0 write (`two.c:1516-1536`, the other `thief_killed[0]++` call site, already ported). Followed this codebase's established precedent of not writing dedicated unit tests for `ugaris-server`-level death-hook glue functions themselves (confirmed via grep: none of the ~10 existing `apply_*_death_from_hurt_event` functions across this file have a test file of their own - they're exercised through boot-smoke plus the `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-level building blocks they call, which *are* unit tested) - instead added focused tests at the two layers that do have test coverage: 1 new `player/tests/twocity.rs` test (`twocity_thief_killed_setter_writes_the_indexed_slot_used_by_robber_dead`, covering both a normal index write and the out-of-range-index no-op) and 2 new `world/tests/npc_fight.rs` tests (`two_robber_npc_attacks_visible_enemy_via_reused_simple_baddy_dispatch`, covering both the single-character `process_simple_baddy_attack_action` gate and the aggregate `process_simple_baddy_attack_actions_with_random` iteration gate, mirroring `dungeon_fighter.rs`'s existing `dungeonfighter_npc_attacks_visible_enemy_via_reused_simple_baddy_dispatch` precedent exactly). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 2986 core [+2] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both the default area-1 data (hundreds of ticks, no panic) and `--area-id 17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data ("loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17`, 345 placed characters including live `robberN`/`robber_guard`/`robber_baron` instances, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` remain - suggested next order per the prior iteration's ledger entry: `CDR_TWOSERVANT` (reuses `call_guard`, small state machine around `two.c` line 960-1055), then `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`, then `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` last (the biggest remaining quest chain).
- 2026-07-09: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`servant` (`two.c:980-1322`) plus its `servant_dead` death hook (`two.c:1324-1351`) - the six `palace_maid1`-`palace_maid5`/`city_maid1`-style forbidden-territory servants (scullery girl/maid/governor's mistress/cook/governor's double/citizen, distinguished by an `nr` `0`-`5` arg), per the prior iteration's suggested next-slice order. Unlike every other Two-City NPC ported so far, this driver's own state (`current_state`/`current_victim`/`nr`/`lastalert`) is entirely character-local (`struct servant_data`), never reading or writing a player's `twocity_ppd` except a read-only `citizen_status` check, so `TwoServantPlayerFacts` only carries that one field and there is no state-writeback event at all.
