New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/servant.rs` (`World::process_two_servant_actions`/`process_two_servant_tick`) ports the per-tick body digit-for-digit, including two real, deliberately-reproduced C quirks documented in the module's own doc comment: (1) the `NT_CHAR` handler runs *two* `switch (dat->current_state) { case 0: ...; dat->current_state++; case 1: break; }` blocks back to back (`two.c:1048-1065` then `:1069-1080`, the second gated behind an `illegal_place` check) - since the first switch already increments `current_state` from `0` to `1` before the second reads it, the second switch's own "My greetings... how may I serve you?" branch is unreachable on every encounter, confirmed by a dedicated regression test (`servant_greets_new_player_with_not_supposed_to_be_here`, asserting exactly one text is queued); (2) `illegal_place(ch[cn].x, ch[co].x)` (`two.c:1067`) passes the player's `x` twice (mislabeled as the player's `y`) instead of the real `(x, y)` pair - also preserved as-is. `nr` is parsed from the zone file's `arg="nr=N;"` at spawn time (`zone.rs`, following `guard_driver`'s established precedent of moving C's own `NT_CREATE`-time parse to spawn time - new `parse_two_servant_driver_args`). The `NT_TEXT` handler wires `chat`/`bribe`/`threaten`/`pay bribe` (answer_codes 8/9/10/11, 4 new `TWOCITY_QA` rows) each switching on `nr` (0/1/2/3/5, with `nr == 4` "governor's double" replying to none of them - `didsay` stays truthy per C's own `switch ((didsay = analyse_text_driver(...)))` semantics even when the inner `nr`-switch matches nothing, reproduced via `TextAnalysisOutcome::Matched(8..=11)`'s `_ => {}` inner arms still falling through to `didsay = true`). `threaten`'s `nr == 2` (mistress) branch and `pay bribe`'s `nr == 1`/`nr == 2` branches reward a secret-passage key (`palace_key1`/`palace_key2`) that only `ZoneLoader` can instantiate - deferred via 2 new `TwoServantOutcomeEvent::GivePalaceKey1`/`GivePalaceKey2` variants, applied by new `ugaris-server::area17.rs::apply_two_servant_events` (`zone_loader.instantiate_item_template("palace_key1"/"palace_key2", ...)` + `give_char_item`, same precedent as `alchemist`'s potion reward); the gold-taking (`take_money`, new private `two_servant_take_money`, `2000`/`5000`-gold tiers) and all dialogue happen directly inside `World`. `pay bribe`'s `nr == 0` branch uses `Sirname(co)` ("Sir"/"Lady"/"Neuter", new private `servant_sirname` helper mirroring `world::npc::area11::islena`'s own copy) instead of the player's real name - a genuine C quirk, tested. New `IID_AREA17_PALACEKEY1`/`IID_AREA17_PALACEKEY2` item-id constants (`item_driver/ids.rs`, hex values confirmed against `item_id.h:158-159`; not referenced by id anywhere else in this file, matching C's own `has_item(IID_AREA17_PALACEKEY3)` gate belonging to the still-unported thief-guild chain, not these two). `servant_dead` (new `ugaris-server::world_events::death_hooks::apply_two_servant_death_from_hurt_event`, needs `PlayerRuntime`) only applies the harsh `citizen_status = CS_ENEMY`/`legal_status = LS_DEAD` punishment when `nr == 4`; every other servant just says "Arrgh! GUARDS!"; both branches always call `call_guard` afterward - required promoting `World::two_city_call_guard` from `pub(crate)` to `pub` (documented at the call site) since this is the first cross-crate `ugaris-server` call site for it. New `CDR_TWOSERVANT = 65` driver constant (confirmed against `drvlib.h:113`), `CharacterDriverState::TwoServant` variant (added to the three exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, right after `TwoBarkeeper(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (all 6 `palace_maid*`/`city_maid1` templates, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:637-981`+, `driver=65`, each carrying its own `arg="nr=N;"`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_servant_driver_102` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`) and the matching `two_servant_player_facts`/`apply_two_servant_events` glue in `area17.rs`. 18 new focused tests: 16 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/servant.rs` (the double-switch dead-second-branch quirk, `nr == 4`'s guard-call greeting, the `repeat`/canned-`hello`-QA-reply pair, `chat`'s `nr == 0` reply and `nr == 4`'s silent-but-still-`didsay` case, `bribe`'s `nr == 2` male-vs-female branch, `threaten`'s `nr == 2` male-calls-guard-vs-female-gets-key branch, `pay bribe`'s `nr == 0` success/failure pair including the `Sirname` substitution and `nr == 1` success/failure pair including the `GivePalaceKey2` event, the `NT_GOTHIT` alert-cooldown pair, and the unconditional-destroy `NT_GIVE` behavior) plus 2 in `character_driver.rs` (data-shape test, `parse_two_servant_driver_args` arg-parsing test). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3004 core [+18] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data (345 placed characters), 10+ seconds of ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` remain - suggested next order per the prior iteration's ledger entry: `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD` next, then `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` last (the biggest remaining quest chain).
- 2026-07-09: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) progress: ported `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD`/`thiefguard` (`two.c:1541-1727`), the Exkordon thieves-guild sewer entrance guard - per the prior iteration's suggested next order. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/thiefguard.rs` (`World::process_two_thiefguard_actions`/`process_two_thiefguard_tick`) ports the per-tick body digit-for-digit: the `NT_CHAR` handler's unconditional (independent of the talk-cooldown/current-victim gates below it) `fight_driver_add_enemy(cn, co, 1, 1)` call whenever a player with `thief_state < 3` is spotted at `y < 27` (inside the sewers, not just the entrance) - reproduced via the pre-existing driver-independent `Character::fight_driver` slot (`add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`, same machinery `world::npc::area8::fdemon_army_combat` already established as driver-state-agnostic) - followed by the same talk-cooldown/current-victim/distance/visibility guard cascade every other Two-City NPC uses, then a 5-state (`0`/`1`/`2` waiting-for-fee/`3`/`4` done) plus `50`/`51` (post-thiefmaster-kill recognition, dead code until the still-unported `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`'s `thiefmaster_dead` ever sets `thief_state = 50`) greeting ladder; the `NT_TEXT` handler reuses `TWOCITY_QA` plus two new rows this iteration added (`status`/answer_code 14 - confirmed genuinely dead code in C itself, no driver's `switch (didsay)` has a `case 14` arm at all, kept for table parity; `pay a fee`/answer_code 15, thiefguard's own `take_money(co, 10000)` guild-membership fee, `case 2`'s "repeat" only resetting while `thief_state <= 2` with no higher-state branch, matching C's simpler ladder here vs. `sanwyn`'s dual-branch); the `NT_GIVE` handler destroys any item unconditionally (same precedent as `barkeeper`'s). A real, cross-cutting gap this NPC's own tail exposed and fixed: C's `fight_driver_attack_visible(cn, 0)` here (`two.c:1721`) has no accompanying `fight_driver_follow_invisible` call at all - unlike `simple_baddy_driver`/`lostcon_driver`/`guard_driver`, which all call both - so the existing shared `World::fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow` (previously always calling `fight_driver_follow_invisible`'s Rust equivalent whenever `!suppressions.nomove`, with no way to opt out independently of movement-suppression) grew a new `may_follow_invisible: bool` parameter (`world/npc_fight.rs`) so a caller can request "full movement for the attack task itself, but never chase an invisible enemy" - the exact C semantics `suppressions.nomove` alone cannot express (it also suppresses in-attack movement). Auditing the other three call sites while adding the parameter found a second, pre-existing instance of the same bug: `world::npc::area8::fdemon_army`'s own self-defense fallback (`fdemon.c:1494`, `!dat->closeup && fight_driver_attack_visible(cn, 0)`) also has no `fight_driver_follow_invisible` call in C, but the Rust port had been calling the combined function with implicit follow-enabled since it was first wired (iteration 84's fdemon_army combat slice) - now fixed to pass `may_follow_invisible: false`, closing a real behavior gap unrelated to this checkbox but found via the same investigation; `fdemon_demon`'s hunt-tick call site (`fdemon.c:2812-2816`, which *does* call both) and the two original NPC-side/lostcon-side callers (`process_simple_baddy_attack_action_with_random`/`process_lostcon_attack_action_with_random`, which C's `simple_baddy_driver`/`lostcon_driver` both call-both for) all pass `true`, preserving their existing behavior unchanged. New `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD = 66` driver constant (confirmed against `drvlib.h:114`), `CharacterDriverState::TwoThiefGuard` variant (added to the four exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`(x2)/`npc_idle.rs`, right after `TwoServant(_)`/`TwoGuard(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`thief_guard` template, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:2240-2306`, `driver=66`, no `arg=` line). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_thiefguard_driver_103` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`) and the matching `two_thiefguard_player_facts`/`apply_two_thiefguard_events` glue in `area17.rs` (plain `twocity_ppd.thief_state` writeback, same shape as `apply_two_skelly_events`, no `ZoneLoader`/`QuestLog` dependency - `thief_state`'s existing `twocity_thief_state`/`set_twocity_thief_state` accessors and `TWOCITY_PPD_THIEF_STATE_OFFSET` already existed from earlier scaffolding, confirmed via research before writing code). 18 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/thiefguard.rs`: the full 0/1/2/3/4/50/51 greeting-ladder progression (silent states asserted empty-events-and-no-text), the enemy-add firing (with the guard on talk-cooldown, proving it's independent of the dialogue gates) vs. not firing outside the `y < 27` sewer boundary vs. not firing once `thief_state >= 3`, both `repeat` branches (`<= 2` resets, `> 2` is inert-but-`didsay`-true, asserted via `current_victim` still being stamped), all three `pay a fee` outcomes (success with exact gold deduction, insufficient gold, wrong state replying "Hu?"), a canned `hello` QA reply, the unconditional-destroy `NT_GIVE`, and a dedicated `may_follow_invisible: false` regression test (a lone invisible enemy leaves the NPC at its spawn tile with `action == IDLE`, unlike the pre-existing `lostcon_attack_action_follows_an_invisible_enemy_toward_its_last_position` test's opposite assertion for a caller that *does* pass `true`) - plus 1 new `character_driver.rs` data-shape test and a `CDR_TWOTHIEFGUARD == 66` id assertion. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3023 core [+19, incl. 18 new thiefguard tests + 1 data-shape test] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged, confirming the `fdemon_army`/`fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow` fix didn't regress any existing test), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings.   Boot-smoke confirmed both the default area-1 data (600+ log lines, no panic) and `--area-id 17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data ("loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17`, 345 placed characters, 235+ ticks, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 stays `[~]`: only `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER` remains - the biggest remaining Two-City quest chain (the guild master, `thief_state` 4-21 progression through several sub-quests, `thiefmaster_dead`'s `thief_state = 50` writeback this iteration's dead-code `50`/`51` branches depend on).
- 2026-07-09: Area 17 (`src/area/17/two.c`) CLOSED: ported `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER`/`thiefmaster` (`two.c:1746-2188`) - "Guild Master", the biggest remaining Two-City quest chain, and the last unported driver in this file. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area17/thiefmaster.rs` (`World::process_two_thiefmaster_actions`/`process_two_thiefmaster_tick`) ports the full 18-state (`4` through `21`) "Earning the Lockpick" chain digit-for-digit: four sequential quests (25 "Earning the Lockpick", 26 "Extortion", 27 "Price Fix Exposed", 28 "The Golden Lockpick"), each gated on `has_item` checks (`lockpick`/`sewer_key1`/`sewer_key2`/`palace_key3`) that let a returning player silently skip ahead to wherever their inventory says they already are, and symmetrically fall back (with a scolding line) to an earlier state if a prerequisite item was lost - reusing the existing `World::character_has_item_template` helper (`world::npc::area3::kelly`) for every one of these checks. Quest 25/26 complete via `NT_CHAR` states `10`/`14` whose reward tier depends on `twocity_ppd.thief_killed[6]` (fed by the already-ported `PlayerRuntime::mark_twocity_burndown_kill`/`ugaris-server::world_events::death_hooks::apply_two_robber_death_from_hurt_event`); quest 25 additionally needs a new `NT_TEXT` "i am done" row (`TWOCITY_QA` answer_code `16`, the last row this shared table was still missing per the prior iteration's own doc comment) to transition `thief_state` `9` (waiting) -> `10` (ready to grade). Because quests 25/26 have nominal `exp: 0` in `QUEST_TABLE` ("exp awarded in driver" per `questlog.c`'s own comment) and the manual reward math (`tmp = val; val = questlog_scale(questlog_count(co, N), val); give_exp(co, min(level_value(level)/5, val))`) only needs `Character::level` plus the prior completion count, the exp grant itself is computed and applied directly inside `World` (new `World::grant_thiefmaster_reward`, reusing `crate::quest::scale_exp` + `level_value`) rather than deferred like `alchemist`'s reward - the prior completion count is supplied as two new `TwoThiefMasterPlayerFacts::quest25_count`/`quest26_count` fields (`PlayerRuntime::quest_log.count(25/26)`, read *before* the completion increment, matching C's own `questlog_count` call order relative to `questlog_done`). Only the resulting `PlayerRuntime`/`ZoneLoader`-only bookkeeping (six/one `thief_killed` resets, `thief_bits |= 1/2`, `quest_log.mark_done`+`sendquestlog`, and the `lockpick`/`sewer_key1` reward item) is deferred via new `TwoThiefMasterOutcomeEvent::Quest25Reward`/`Quest26Reward` events. Quests 27/28 complete via `NT_GIVE` (a merchant's note/a golden lockpick) and use real `QUEST_TABLE` exp values (`15000` each), so they follow `alchemist`'s/`two_skelly`'s precedent instead: `World` says the reward line directly (`NT_GIVE` already knows the giver's name) and defers only `quest_log.complete_legacy`/the `sewer_key2`/`palace_key3` item to new `Quest27Done`/`Quest28Done` events. Every `questlog_open`/`questlog_close` call along the chain (four distinct quest numbers scattered across states `5`/`11`/`15`/`18`) is ported through a pair of new generic `TwoThiefMasterOutcomeEvent::QuestOpen`/`QuestClose { player_id, quest: u8 }` events instead of one dedicated variant per quest number, a new precedent versus every prior Two-City driver's per-quest-number variants (documented in the events' own doc comments) since C calls both with a plain numeric argument at each of the four sites. Unlike `thiefguard`, this driver has no explicit `NT_GOTHIT` handler of its own in C - its per-tick tail's `standard_message_driver(cn, msg, 0, 0)` fallback call is the *only* source of hostility (`agressive=0`/`helper=0` no-ops `NT_CHAR`/`NT_SEEHIT`, confirmed via `drvlib.c:2466-2540`), so its `NT_GOTHIT` case (`fight_driver_note_hit` plus a group/`can_attack`-gated `fight_driver_add_enemy`) is ported directly in this file rather than reused from a shared helper (none exists yet for a non-`CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` `driver_state`) - new `World::two_thiefmaster_handle_gothit_message`, writing the driver-independent `Character::fight_driver.last_hit`/`enemies` slot via the pre-existing `add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`. Also like `thiefguard`, the per-tick tail calls `fight_driver_attack_visible(cn, 0)` but never `fight_driver_follow_invisible`, so `World::fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow`'s `may_follow_invisible: false` parameter (added by the prior iteration) is reused here too. New `twocity_ppd` field offsets/accessors for the two previously-unexposed reserved fields this driver needed: `TWOCITY_PPD_THIEF_LAST_SEEN_OFFSET`(9)/`TWOCITY_PPD_THIEF_BITS_OFFSET`(18) in `player/misc.rs` (confirmed against `common/two_ppd.h`'s real field order) plus `twocity_thief_last_seen`/`set_twocity_thief_last_seen`/`twocity_thief_bits`/`set_twocity_thief_bits` in `player/twocity.rs` (`thief_bits` is write-only in C itself beyond JSON debug serialization - no other C code reads it back - ported for `player_state_json` fidelity, not because anything consumes it). New `IID_AREA17_PALACEKEY3`/`IID_AREA17_MERCHANTNOTE1`/`IID_AREA17_SEWERKEY1`/`IID_AREA17_SEWERKEY2`/`IID_AREA17_GOLDENLOCKPICK` item-id constants (`item_driver/ids.rs`, hex values confirmed against `item_id.h:160,164,167-169`). New `CDR_TWOTHIEFMASTER = 67` driver constant (confirmed against `drvlib.h:115`, "two cities: thieves guild master"), `CharacterDriverState::TwoThiefMaster` variant (added to the three exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`, right after `TwoThiefGuard(_)`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branch (`thiefmaster` template, confirmed via `zones/17/two.chr:2311-2380`, `driver=67`, no `arg=` line). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area17.rs::two_thiefmaster_driver_104` (registered as the new last entry in `tick_npc::run_all`, using `current_unix_time()` for C's `realtime`, same idiom as `barkeeper_driver_100`/`guard_driver_101`) and the matching `two_thiefmaster_player_facts`/`apply_two_thiefmaster_events` glue in `area17.rs` (the biggest `apply_*` function in this file, handling all 8 event variants: 2 plain PPD writebacks, 2 generic quest open/close, 2 manual-exp-already-applied reward tails with a fresh reward item, and 2 real-`complete_legacy`-exp quest completions with their own reward item). One real bug was found and fixed while writing tests: state `18`'s silent skip-ahead check had been copy-pasted as `has_item(IID_AREA17_GOLDENLOCKPICK)` instead of C's actual `has_item(IID_AREA17_PALACEKEY3)` (`two.c:2009`) - caught by a dedicated regression test exercising the real item, not the golden lockpick (which state `18` never checks at all; only state `20` does, plus `NT_GIVE`'s quest-28 completion). 26 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/thiefmaster.rs`: the `thief_state < 4` unconditional bump-to-4, the state-4 greeting, both state-5 branches (silent skip-to-11 with lockpick vs. the robber-mission offer plus its 4 quest-open/close events), the state-9 wait-nag cooldown boundary (needed `drain_pending_area_text_bytes` since this line uses `npc_say_bytes` for its embedded `COL_STR_LIGHT_BLUE` "i am done" hint, unlike most of this driver's plain-`npc_say` lines), state 10's both branches (zero-kills scolding-and-reset vs. a scored reward with an exact `Character::exp` assertion, plus the robber-baron-always-hails-the-slayer tier), state 11's three branches (no-lockpick reset, silent skip-to-15, and the burndown-mission offer), the four silent waiting states (`13`/`17`/`19`/`21`) in one parametrized test, state 14's burndown reward (both dialogue lines plus the exact exp grant), state 18's now-correctly-tested `palace_key3` silent skip, state 20's full-completion finish, both `repeat`-range resets and the `i am done` QA row (both the state-9-success and other-state-"Hu?" branches), a canned `hello` QA reply, both `NT_GIVE` quest completions (27/28, each asserting the state advance + completion event + reward text + cursor-item cleanup), the unconditional-destroy `NT_GIVE` fallback, both `NT_GOTHIT` branches (different-group attacker added as an enemy with `last_hit` stamped vs. same-group ally never added), and the `may_follow_invisible: false` regression (mirroring `thiefguard`'s own test), plus 1 new `character_driver.rs` data-shape test. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3049 core [+26] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both the default area-1 data and `--area-id 17` against real `zones/17/two.map` data ("loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=17`, 345 placed characters including the live `thiefmaster` "Guild Master" instance, 10+ seconds of ticks, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 17 marked `[x]` - every Two-City driver in `two.c` is now ported.
- 2026-07-09: Area 18 (`src/area/18/bones.c`) CLOSED: ported `bonebridge_driver`'s last remaining gap, the "bones in inventory" partial-add/remove-from-a-carried-bridge path (`bones.c:236-270`). Five new `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`BoneBridgeAddBone`/`BoneBridgeFinished`/`BoneBridgeWrongCursorItem`/`BoneBridgeRemoveBone`/`BoneBridgeNotEnoughBones`) resolve the carried-bridge branch (`drdata[0] != 0 && drdata[1] == 0`) exactly like C: cursor holds another `IID_AREA18_BONE` item -> increments the bridge's own count (capped at 5, C's exact "The bridge is finished." text past that) and decrements/destroys the cursor stack (new `World::add_bone_to_bridge`, `world/area_mech.rs`, sprite `13030 + drdata[0]` matching C); cursor holds something else -> C's literal "Hu?"; empty cursor with `>= 2` bones -> pulls one back out (`World::remove_bone_from_bridge`), with the new "bone" item landing on the cursor via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template("bone", ...)` + `World::give_char_item` in `tick_item_use_completion.rs` (same empty-cursor-first precedent as the existing `BoneHolderRemoveRune` wiring, since the driver already verified the cursor was empty - matches C's direct `ch[cn].citem = in2` assignment); empty cursor with `< 2` bones -> the same literal "Hu?" (C reuses this exact log line 3 times across the function; the third site, the ground-placement branch's own cursor-mismatch check, was already a documented pre-existing `Noop` gap, not touched by this slice). 5 new focused tests: 2 in `item_driver/tests/area18_bones.rs` (pure-driver add/finish/wrong-cursor and remove/not-enough branches) + 1 in `world/tests/area_mech.rs` (`World`-level add-then-remove round trip verifying sprite/`drdata` math and cursor-item cleanup on cursor-bone exhaustion). This closes every gap `bones.c` had left; Area 18 is fully ported. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3064 core [+3] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed `--area-id 18` against real `zones/18/bones.map` data ("loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=18`, 567 placed characters), 250+ ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 18 marked `[x]`.
- 2026-07-09: Area 19 (`src/area/19/nomad.c`) CLOSED: ported both character drivers this file defines - `CDR_NOMAD` (`nomad`, `:927-1155`, all 6 personas: Kalanur the Vana Kiru tribe recruiter/quest 32, Irakar the dice seller, the `Llakal Sla` game host, the two Kir monastery monks - Sarkilar's-fate quest 33 and the life-teacher quest 34 - and the golden-statue seller, differentiated only by their own `arg="nr=N;diceskill=N;minbet=N;maxbet=N;maxloss=N;"` zone-file line) and `CDR_MADHERMIT` (`madhermit_driver`, `:1177-1239`, a stationary self-defense NPC with one quirk: it picks a fight with anyone seen `USE`ing an `IDR_FLOWER` item nearby, `shout`ing a warning first) - plus every helper function both drivers call: `analyse_text_driver`'s nomad-specific copy (`:113-211`, a `char_dist > 11` gate, one tile more permissive than the codebase's usual `> 10`), `nomad_1`..`nomad_6`'s greeting ladders (`:302-516`), `nomad_1_repeat`..`nomad_6_repeat`'s resets (`:518-600`), `nomad_2_text`/`nomad_6_text`'s dice/statue purchases (`:602-679`), `nomad_1_give`/`nomad_4_give`/`nomad_5_give`'s quest-item turn-ins (`:681-789`), and the full `Llakal Sla` dice-betting minigame `nomad_bet`/`nomad_roll` (`:791-925`, reusing the already-ported `legacy_lucky_die_from_rolls`). Split across six files to stay under the ~800-line NPC-file guideline (same precedent as `world::npc::area17::guard`/`guard_messages`): `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area19/{mod,nomad,nomad_dialogue,nomad_text,nomad_give,nomad_bet,madhermit}.rs` - `mod.rs` carries the shared `TM_TRIBE1/2/3` constants and the 14-row `NOMAD_QA` table; `nomad.rs` the driver-data struct/spawn-time arg parsing/`NT_CHAR` dispatch/shared state-update helpers; `nomad_dialogue.rs` the six greeting-ladder functions themselves; `nomad_text.rs` the `NT_TEXT` qa-table match, `repeat`, dice/statue purchases, and the raw `strstr(msg->dat2, "bet ")` command trigger (independent of the qa-table match, per C); `nomad_give.rs` the `NT_GIVE` quest-item turn-ins; `nomad_bet.rs` the betting minigame plus `count_salt`/`remove_salt`/`set_salt_sprite`/`configure_fresh_salt_item` (the salt-currency helpers reading/writing a carried "salt" stack's `*(unsigned int *)(it[in].drdata)` amount, ported from scratch - no prior equivalent existed) and the `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DICE` receiver. A real, previously-existing gap was found and fixed while wiring the betting minigame: `IDR_NOMADDICE`'s server-side completion (`ugaris-server::tick_item_use_completion.rs`, ported in an earlier iteration per the ledger) computed the player's own dice roll and sent them a chat message, but never called C's `notify_area(ch[cn].x, ch[cn].y, NT_NPC, NTID_DICE, cn, d1+d2+d3)` to actually deliver the roll to whichever nomad NPC is mid-game with them - so the "USE the dice" half of a real game could never complete. Fixed by adding the `world.notify_area(...)` call at that exact spot; `NTID_DICE`'s constant already existed (unused) from the same earlier iteration. `World::process_nomad_actions`/`process_madhermit_actions` follow the established `PlayerRuntime`-snapshot/outcome-event split every other quest-giver NPC in this codebase uses (`NomadPlayerFacts`/`NomadOutcomeEvent`); several outcomes (salt/dice/statue item creation) additionally need `ZoneLoader`, which `World` cannot see either, so those are finished by `ugaris-server::area19.rs`'s `apply_nomad_events` (mirroring `area17.rs`'s `apply_two_servant_events`/`apply_two_alchemist_events` precedent for `ZoneLoader`-needing rewards). New `player/areas_misc.rs` accessors for the 5 previously-unexposed `nomad_ppd` fields this task needed (`open_roll1/2/3`/`open_bet`/`tribe_member` - `nomad_state`/`nomad_win` already existed from the questlog-init port) plus their byte offsets in `player/misc.rs` (confirmed against `src/common/nomad_ppd.h`'s real field order: `nomad_state[10]`, `nomad_win[10]`, `open_roll1/2/3`, `open_bet`, `tribe_member` = 25 `int`s total, matching the pre-existing `LEGACY_NOMAD_PPD_SIZE`). New `IID_AREA19_WOLFSSKIN`/`_SALT`/`_WOLFSSKIN2`/`_KIR`/`_KIRLETTER` item-id constants in `item_driver/ids.rs` (core-crate-visible; the first three already existed as `ugaris-server`-only duplicates in `constants.rs` for `stacks.rs`'s unrelated stack-splitting use - both copies now coexist, matching this codebase's existing cross-crate constant-duplication precedent rather than refactoring unrelated code). New `CDR_NOMAD = 73`/`CDR_MADHERMIT = 76` driver constants (confirmed against `drvlib.h:121,124`), `CharacterDriverState::Nomad`/`Madhermit` variants (added to the four exhaustive non-SimpleBaddy fallback match lists in `character_driver.rs`/`npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`), `zone.rs` spawn-time branches (`CDR_NOMAD` parses `template.args` via `parse_nomad_driver_args`, same "parse at spawn instead of `NT_CREATE`" precedent as `CDR_TWOSERVANT`/`CDR_TWOGUARD`; `CDR_MADHERMIT` seeds `Character::fight_driver` directly with C's fixed `fight_driver_set_dist(cn, 30, 0, 60)` values, same precedent as `ugaris-server::area8_army::spawn_army_soldier`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area19.rs::nomad_driver_105`/`madhermit_driver_106` (registered as the new last two entries in `tick_npc::run_all`) and the matching `nomad_player_facts`/`apply_nomad_events` glue in the new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area19.rs`. `MadhermitDriverData` is a zero-field marker type (C's own driver has no zone-file-parsed data at all - only the driver-independent `DRD_FIGHTDRIVER` slot) - same "no real data" precedent as `TraderDriverData` before its own first field was added. 30 new focused tests: 28 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/nomad.rs` (arg parsing; the `nomad_1` greeting/quest-open and its silent state-8; `nomad_2`'s non-tribe-member refusal; the `repeat`/`hello` qa-table integration; `nomad_1_give`'s exact-100-salt membership grant, insufficient-salt rejection, and both wolf-skin exchange rates; `nomad_4_give`'s letter turn-in; `nomad_5_give`'s first-statue completion, lost-exp-restoration branch (with the exact `diff/2` non-hardcore math), and the no-lost-exp rejection; `count_salt`/`remove_salt`'s stack-summing/partial-shrink math; `nomad_bet`'s too-cheap/stranger-refused/accepted paths (all driven through the real `"bet "` `NT_TEXT` trigger, not called directly, since the betting methods stay `pub(super)`); `nomad_roll`'s win/loss outcomes via a real `NT_NPC`/`NTID_DICE` message, including a regression test proving it only reacts to the current opponent) and 2 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/madhermit.rs` (the flower-theft aggro/shout, and a negative test confirming a non-flower item use is ignored). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3088 core [+30] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1138 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both the default area-1 data and `--area-id 19` against real `zones/19/nomad.map` data ("loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" `area_id=19`, 178 placed characters including live `nomad1`-`nomad3`/`nomad6`/`monk1`/`monk2`/`madhermit` instances), 280+ ticks over 12 seconds, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 19 marked `[x]` - every driver in `nomad.c` is now ported.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) STARTED: ported `lqnpc`'s per-tick dialogue/movement driver (`lq.c:2744-2898`) and `lqnpc_died`'s death hook (`:2929-2958`), extending the earlier "spawn/raise/equipment ported" scaffolding rather than duplicating it. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area20/lqnpc.rs`: `LqNpcDriverData` (C `struct lq_npc_data`, minus the admin-only `thrallname` field - the `#nspawn`-as-thrall path is only reachable through the unported admin command table) plus `World::process_lqnpc_actions`/`process_lqnpc_tick` port the message loop (`NT_CHAR` greeting via the established `mem_check_driver`/`mem_add_driver` slot-7 precedent, plus `standard_message_driver`'s aggressive-mode self-defense when `dat->mode == 'a'`; `NT_GOTHIT` hurt-mark queuing plus the same self-defense case; `NT_TEXT` substring-matched trigger/reply dialogue, the "followme"/"stopfollow" `CF_LQMASTER`-gated admin-mirroring commands, and `tabunga` via the existing `apply_tabunga_text_notification`; `NT_GIVE` quest-item turn-in) and the movement/fight/regen tail (`follow`-target walk via `setup_walk_toward`, `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow`, `secure_move_driver` back to `rest_x`/`rest_y` as C's `tmpx`/`tmpy`, `regenerate_simple_baddy`/`spell_self_simple_baddy` - same precedent as `world::npc::area19::madhermit`). A new `LqItemSpec` (C `struct lq_item`) plus `make_lq_item_template_id`/`Item::template_id` model C's `it[in].ID` (`MAKE_ITEMID(DEV_ID_LQ, keyID)`) generically - the existing `write_lq_door_key_id` door-specific `drdata[1..5]` hijack is a *different*, C-verified mechanism (`update_lqdoor`'s own `*(unsigned int*)(it[in].drdata+1)` write) and was left untouched; `ugaris-server::area20::create_lq_item` (C `create_lq_item`) is the only place that ever instantiates one of these items, reused by both `spawn_lq_npc_character`'s `carry_item` (now also seeds `character.gold`/`sprite`/`driver_state`/`fight_driver` - the `fight_driver_set_dist(cn, 30, 0, 60)` `NT_CREATE` seed, `..lq.c:2761`) and the new `LqNpcOutcomeEvent::GiveRewardItem` (turn-in reward, applied by `area20::apply_lqnpc_events`, drained from `tick_npc::area20::lqnpc_driver_107`). `LqNpcState`/`LqNpcSpawnRequest` widened with every dialogue/reward field C's `spawn_npc` copies into `DRD_LQ_NPC_DATA` (`sprite`/`greeting`/`trigger[5]`/`reply[5]`/`want_key_id`/`reward_item`/`reward_mark_id`/`kill_mark_id`/`hurt_mark_id`/`carry_item`/`carry_gold`) with matching truncation guards in `configure_lq_npc`. New typed `PlayerRuntime::lq_marks: [bool; MAXLQMARK]` (C `struct lq_plr_data::mark[]`, `DRD_LQ_PLR_DATA`) with `lq_mark`/`set_lq_mark` accessors (`crates/ugaris-core/src/player/lq.rs`) - a genuinely new persistent field per `AGENTS.md`'s post-migration-0020 rule, not a legacy blob (no pre-existing C byte layout to preserve since this struct was never PPD-persisted the way `misc_ppd`/`twocity_ppd` are - it's a `set_data` generic slot like `DRD_LQ_NPC_DATA`). `apply_lqnpc_death_from_hurt_event` (`world_events/death_hooks.rs`) reproduces C's exact identity-matched respawn scheduling (`lq_npc[dat->n].cn == cn && .cserial == ch[cn].serial` before clearing/rescheduling, guarding against a since-reused slot) plus the killer's kill/hurt mark writes. Deliberately NOT ported (unreachable dead code without it, documented in `lqnpc.rs`'s own module doc comment): the `usurp` god/LQMaster-possession `domirror` movement-mirroring branch - `dat->usurp` can only ever be set by the not-yet-ported `#usurp` admin command, so C's own branch would be equally dead on a stock server with no admin issuing it; the `follow` half of the same mechanic (driven entirely by any `CF_LQMASTER` player's own "followme"/"stopfollow" speech, no admin command needed) *is* fully ported. 9 new focused tests: 7 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lqnpc.rs` (greeting-once via mem-slot, `NT_GOTHIT` hurt-mark-plus-self-defense and its same-group/out-of-range-mark negative, trigger/reply plus followme/stopfollow state transitions, follow-target walk initiation, and both `NT_GIVE` turn-in branches) and 2 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs` (`lethal_lqnpc_hurt_schedules_respawn_and_marks_a_player_killer` end-to-end through the real `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` pipeline, and the non-lethal/wrong-driver negative). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3095 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1140 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed both `--area-id 20` against real `zones/20/lq.*` data ("loaded area zone map", 1 placed character, "entering Rust game loop", no panic) and default `--area-id 1` (hundreds of ticks, no panic). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: the ~45-subcommand `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (NPC template CRUD, quest lifecycle, door lock/unlock, `#usurp`/`#follow` possession), `questsave`/`questload` file I/O, and `#xinfo` all remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the first slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`, `:2505-2742`) - the 18-subcommand NPC-template CRUD family (`#npc`/`#npcname`/`#npcgold`/`#npcsprite`/`#npcpos`/`#npcdescription`/`#npcgreeting`/`#npcreply`/`#npclist`/`#npcdelete`/`#npcwantitem`/`#npcitem`/`#npcshow`/`#npckillmark`/`#npchurtmark`/`#npcrewarditem`/`#npcmodlevel`/`#npcrespawn`). Confirmed via the C oracle (`system/command.c:5855-5859`) that `CDR_LQPARSER` is dispatched purely by an `areaID == 20 || areaID == 35` gate in the universal `command()` function, independent of any spawned character/`CharacterDriverKind` - never assigned to `character.driver` anywhere - so the whole slice is a new `World::apply_lq_admin_command(character_id, area_id, command) -> bool` entry point (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq_admin.rs`, new module), wired into `tick_client_actions.rs` right after `clan_command::apply_clan_command` (same "if let Some/if-recognized" chain position C's own `special_driver` check occupies relative to normal command dispatch). Every field these 18 commands touch already lived on the pre-existing `LqNpcState`/`world::lq` scaffolding (built in an earlier iteration in anticipation of exactly this), so the entire slice needed zero new `World` state and zero `ZoneLoader`/`PlayerRuntime` access - genuinely pure `World` logic, unlike almost every other NPC/driver port in this codebase. New pieces: a small `cmdcmp`-port (`cmd_word_matches`, prefix-with-minlen, first internal Rust port of this idiom even though the "lower.len()>=minlen && full.starts_with(&lower)" *pattern* already existed ad hoc at several call sites like `clan_command.rs`); an `ArgReader` tokenizer porting C's `get_str`/`get_int`/`get_chr`/`check_anything` (`lq.c:238-317`) space/quote-aware argument parsing; a `legacy_atoi` port; the shared `resolve_lq_npc_slots` nick-or-slot-ID target-resolution idiom (with the `"all"` keyword for `#npcmodlevel`/`#npcrespawn`) that all 18 commands reuse instead of re-deriving; and `queue_lq_error`, a `COL_LIGHT_RED`-prefixed byte-payload wrapper around the pre-existing `World::queue_system_text_bytes` (same "`\xb0` isn't valid UTF-8" precedent as `give_money_message`) for every `log_sys(cn, COL_LIGHT_RED ...)` call site, vs. plain `World::queue_system_text` for uncolored ones - all feedback flows through these two pre-existing pending-queue drains, flushed by `tick_sync::sync_phase` later the same tick, so no new plumbing was needed in `ugaris-server` beyond the one dispatch call. Two C existence checks (`lookup_char`/`lookup_item` on `"lq_"+basename`, used by `#npc` and `#npcitem`/`#npcrewarditem`'s shared `get_lq_item`) are permanently deferred rather than ported - `World` has no character/item-template registry (only `ugaris-server`'s `ZoneLoader` does) - documented in the module doc comment as matching the existing `spawns.rs::spawn_lq_npc_character` precedent (a bad basename silently no-ops at the still-unported `#nspawn`/scheduled-respawn step instead of erroring immediately at creation time). Several verbatim-preserved C oddities: `cmd_npcsprite`'s error messages literally reuse `cmd_npcgold`'s usage string/wording (a copy-paste bug, `lq.c:602,609`); `cmd_npcrewarditem`'s success message says "Set item of %d NPCs", identical to `cmd_npcitem`'s, not "Set reward item..." (`lq.c:1200,1237`); `cmd_npcreply`'s bounds-check message has a literal typo, "Nr %d it out of bounds." (`lq.c:1012`). `#npcshow`'s hurt/killmark exp-preview line reads C's `lq_data.reward[]`/`reward_desc[]`, which is not a simplification but an accurate reflection of a table with no ported writer yet (`struct lq_data`/`#questreward` are a future quest-lifecycle slice) - genuinely always "0 exp"/empty description until that slice lands. 29 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_admin.rs`: area/prefix/permission gating (area 20 and its area-35 mirror, no `#`/`/` prefix falls through to speech, `CF_GOD`-absent and `CF_LQMASTER`-alone cases), `cmdcmp` minlen/ambiguous-prefix-resolves-to-first-match-in-C-order (`"npcre"` -> `#npcreply`, not `#npcrewarditem`/`#npcrespawn`), `#npc` creation/position-collision/missing-args, per-field setters plus their not-found paths, `#npcgold`'s 2000-cap rejection, `#npcsprite`'s Islena-sprite block, `#npcreply`'s out-of-bounds typo message, `#npcmodlevel`'s both clamp-and-warn branches plus `all`, `#npcrespawn`'s `all` keyword, `#npcpos`'s default-to-caller-position/out-of-bounds/ambiguous-nick/position-conflict branches, `#npcdelete`'s live-character teardown, `#npclist`'s nick filter plus summary line, `#npcshow`'s populated-fields-only output, `#npcitem`/`#npcrewarditem`'s parsed-spec storage (plus the copy-paste success-message assertion), and mark setters. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3124 core [+29] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1140 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of ticks, no panic (this slice needed no area-20-specific zone data to exercise, being pure command dispatch). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: `#thrall`/`#killthrall` (need `DRD_LQ_NPC_DATA.thrallname`), `#usurp`/`#follow`/`#stop`/`#exit` (need a new `PlayerRuntime.usurp` field), live-instance control (`#doorlist`/`#doorlock`/`#nspawn`/`#nremove`/`#nsay`/`#nimmortal`/`#nemote`/`#nattack`/`#wimp`), and the whole quest-lifecycle family (`#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questend`/`#questload`/`#questshow`/`#questreward`/`#questlevel`/`#questreset`/`#questentrance`/`#queststart`, `#xinfo`, plus novel file I/O for save/load) all remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the second slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - `#doorlist`/`#doorlock` (`lq.c:2443-2503`), operating on the pre-existing `World::lq_doors`/`LqDoorState` scaffolding (`world::lq`'s `discover_lq_doors_once`, ported for the `LqTicker` periodic scan). Both new `lq_admin_cmd_doorlist`/`lq_admin_cmd_doorlock` handlers (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq_admin.rs`) call `discover_lq_doors_once` themselves first, matching C's own lazy `lq_ticker`-driven `init_done` populate-on-first-use behavior in case no ticker tick has run yet. `#doorlist` is a verbatim `nick`-then-`x,y`-then-`Key` listing (item position read live from `self.items`, since a door's map position doesn't move) and is the rare `cmd_*` handler in this table that never validates its `ptr` argument in C (no "Trailing garbage" check) - kept exactly, with a dedicated regression test. `#doorlock` reuses the existing `write_lq_door_key_id` (already ported alongside `discover_lq_doors_once`, C's `update_lqdoor`) for the actual `drdata[1..5]` byte write, and the same numeric-slot-or-nick target resolution idiom as the NPC-CRUD slice's `resolve_lq_npc_slots`, inlined here since door nick matching has no `"all"` keyword and no `nick[1]` second slot to also check. 8 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_admin.rs`: `#doorlist`'s discover-on-first-use plus its exact listing format, `#doorlist`'s trailing-garbage-is-ignored quirk, `#doorlock` by nick and by numeric slot (with an exact byte-layout assertion on `MAKE_ITEMID(DEV_ID_LQ, keyID)`'s little-endian encoding), door-not-found, missing-args usage messages, and trailing-garbage rejection. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3131 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1140 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of ticks, no panic (pure command dispatch, no area-20-specific zone data needed). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: `#thrall`/`#killthrall`, `#usurp`/`#follow`/`#stop`/`#exit`, `#nspawn`/`#nremove`/`#nsay`/`#nimmortal`/`#nemote`/`#nattack`/`#wimp`, and the whole quest-lifecycle family all remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the third slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - the live-instance-control family: `#nremove`/`#nsay`/`#nimmortal`/`#nemote`/`#nattack` (pure `World` logic, wired straight into `apply_lq_admin_command`'s dispatch table like every prior slice) and `#nspawn` (the one command in this family whose underlying C `spawn_npc` needs a fresh character - `create_char`/`drop_char` - which only `ugaris-server`'s `ZoneLoader`/`ServerRuntime` can provide). New `World::get_lq_char` (C `get_lq_char`, `lq.c:1935-1946`) is the shared "live-and-serial-matches" lookup all five of these commands need (unlike the NPC-CRUD slice, which only ever touches template state). `#nremove` (C `cmd_nremove`, `:1898-1932`) reuses `resolve_lq_npc_slots(nick, allow_all=true)` plus a widened `lq_admin_remove_npc_instance` (previously a `#npcdelete`-only void helper) that now returns C `remove_npc`'s real `bool` return value (`true` if a live instance was destroyed, or if there was no live instance but a scheduled respawn was pending and got cancelled) and resets the template's `character_id`/`character_serial` to `None`/`0` on removal (C's `lq_npc[n].cn = lq_npc[n].cserial = 0`) - a real gap fix, since without the reset a stale `character_id` could coincidentally collide with a later-reused ID. `#nsay`/`#nemote` (`:1953-1988`/`:2045-2087`) reuse the pre-existing `World::npc_say`/`npc_emote` and replicate C's own "`cnt > 10`: cancel" guard (only reachable via the nick/`all`-scan branch in C, though applying it uniformly is harmless since the numeric-ID branch can only ever match one slot). `#nimmortal` (`:1996-2043`) toggles `CharacterFlags::IMMORTAL | CharacterFlags::NOATTACK`. `#nattack` (`:2088-2137`) looks up the target player by exact case-insensitive name among every loaded character (`getfirst_char`/`getnext_char`, no `CF_PLAYER` filter, same idiom as `world::admin_flag::find_loaded_character_by_name`) then queues it via the existing `character_driver::add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`, reproducing C's `hurtme` distinction exactly: `0` for the single-numeric-ID branch, `1` for the nick/`all`-scan branch (`fight_driver_add_enemy`'s full `start_dist`/`char_dist`/neutral-zone gating is not modeled, same pre-existing simplification the helper's own doc comment already documents). `#nspawn` (`:1863-1896` plus `spawn_npc`'s own `:1734-1741` already-there/still-respawning guard) is split across a new `LqNspawnDispatch` enum + `World::try_dispatch_lq_nspawn`/`report_lq_nspawn_result` pair: the pure half (area/permission gate, arg parsing, per-slot eligibility filtering) stays in `lq_admin.rs` and returns `Vec<LqNpcSpawnRequest>` candidates; `tick_client_actions.rs` calls it *before* `apply_lq_admin_command` (since `apply_lq_admin_command`'s own table never recognizes "nspawn"), attempts each candidate via the pre-existing `spawns::spawn_lq_npc_character` (the same instantiation path `#npcrespawn`'s scheduled respawns already use - no new spawn logic needed), and reports the real count back through `report_lq_nspawn_result`. A new `build_lq_npc_spawn_request` free function in `world/lq.rs` factors the `LqNpcState -> LqNpcSpawnRequest` field-copy out of `queue_due_lq_npc_respawns` so both the scheduled-respawn path and `#nspawn` share it instead of duplicating the 20-field struct literal. 26 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_admin.rs`: `#nremove`'s despawn-but-keep-template, `all` keyword, and not-found paths; `#nsay`/`#nemote`'s live-text delivery (asserting the exact `WorldAreaText` wire format) and not-spawned/missing-args paths; `#nimmortal`'s on/off flag toggling and missing-args; `#nattack`'s numeric-ID-vs-nick `hurtme` distinction (asserting `SimpleBaddyEnemy::priority`) and player-not-found; `#nspawn`'s not-matched (wrong area/wrong word), rejected (missing args), a real request for an unspawned template plus `report_lq_nspawn_result`'s two message shapes, and both eligibility-skip cases (already-spawned live instance, respawn cooldown still active including the exact `>=` boundary tick). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3150 core [+26] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1140 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of ticks, no panic (pure command dispatch plus the pre-existing `spawn_lq_npc_character` path, no new area-20-specific zone data needed). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: `#thrall`/`#killthrall`, `#usurp`/`#follow`/`#stop`/`#exit`/`#wimp`, and the whole quest-lifecycle family all remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the fourth slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - `#thrall`/`#killthrall` (`lq.c:427-503`), `spawn_npc`'s on-the-fly template-detached `isthrall` spawn/despawn pair. `LqNpcDriverData` (`world/npc/area20/lqnpc.rs`) gained the previously-deferred `thrallname: String` field (C `dat->thrallname`); `LqNpcSpawnRequest` (`world/lq.rs`) gained `is_thrall`/`thrall_name`, with `build_lq_npc_spawn_request` defaulting both to the non-thrall values so the pre-existing `#nspawn`/scheduled-respawn callers are unaffected. `spawn_lq_npc_character` (`ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs`) now branches on `is_thrall`: `slot` stays `0` and `greeting`/`trigger`/`reply` stay empty (C's `if (!isthrall) { ... }` guard, `lq.c:1819-1825`), `thrallname` is copied, and `World::apply_lq_npc_spawn_result` (the `lq_npc[n].cn`/`cserial` slot bookkeeping) is skipped entirely - matching C's `if (!isthrall) { lq_npc[n].cn = cn; ... dat->n = n; } else { dat->n = 0; strcpy(dat->thrallname, ...); }`. `#thrall` itself is a new `World::try_dispatch_lq_thrall`/`LqThrallDispatch` pair (same `#nspawn`-precedent split: pure `World` arg-parsing/single-slot-resolution/position-rolling half here, actual `ZoneLoader` instantiation in `ugaris-server`, dispatched ahead of `apply_lq_admin_command` in `tick_client_actions.rs`, chained after the existing `try_dispatch_lq_nspawn` check) - notably it resolves to the *first* matching template slot only (C's `for` loop `break`s at the first nick match, unlike `#nspawn`'s `resolve_lq_npc_slots(.., allow_all: true)` which collects every match plus `"all"`), rolls one independent `RANDOM(4)`-based drop position per spawned thrall via the pre-existing `legacy_random_below_from_seed(&mut self.legacy_random_seed, ..)`/new `clamp_world_coordinate` helper (C `ch[cn].tmpx = tx + 2 - RANDOM(4)`), and - matching C's `cmd_thrall`, which never inspects `spawn_npc`'s return value - the caller in `tick_client_actions.rs` doesn't report a spawn count back to the player either. The C `MAXCHARS - used_chars < 150` capacity guard is not ported, matching the established "`World`'s character store is an unbounded `HashMap`, not a fixed-capacity array" precedent (`commands_admin/character.rs`'s `/memstats` doc comment). `#killthrall` is pure `World::lq_admin_cmd_killthrall` (needs no `ZoneLoader`): unlike every other command in this table, it can't use `resolve_lq_npc_slots`/`world::lq_npcs` at all (a thrall has no template row) - it scans every live `CDR_LQNPC` character directly and matches `driver_state`'s `thrallname` case-insensitively, `remove_character`-ing (C `remove_destroy_char`) each match. Two verbatim-preserved C message-formatting oddities: `"Sorry, maximum number of NPCs you can spawn in one call is 20."` and `"Template not found"` are both plain `log_sys` calls with no `COL_LIGHT_RED` prefix (unlike almost every other error in this table), and `"Template not found"` has no trailing period. 18 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_admin.rs` (area/word/permission not-matched, missing-nick/count, trailing-garbage, over-20-count, template-not-found, one-request-per-count with `is_thrall`/`thrall_name`/position-window assertions, omitted-thrallname-defaults-to-empty, zero-count-yields-no-requests, `#killthrall`'s missing-name/case-insensitive-multi-match/zero-match/ignores-non-thrall-characters) plus 1 in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/spawns.rs` (`spawn_lq_npc_character` with `is_thrall: true` end-to-end: `slot == 0`, `greeting == ""`, `thrallname` set, template's own `lq_npcs` slot bookkeeping untouched). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3164 core [+14] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1141 server [+1], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of ticks, no panic. `lq_admin.rs` is now 1,925 lines (under the 2,000 hard cap, but the next slice should double-check headroom before adding more). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: `#usurp`/`#follow`/`#stop`/`#exit`/`#wimp` (need a new `PlayerRuntime.usurp` field), and the whole quest-lifecycle family (`#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questend`/`#questload`/`#questshow`/`#questreward`/`#questlevel`/`#questreset`/`#questentrance`/`#queststart`, `#xinfo`) remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the sixth slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - the non-file-I/O half of the quest-lifecycle family: `#questlevel`/`#questreward`/`#questshow`/`#questentrance`/`#queststart`/`#questreset` (`lq.c:2278-2441`). New `LqData` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq.rs`, C `struct lq_data` - `min_level`/`max_level`/`reward[MAXLQMARK]`/`reward_desc[MAXLQMARK]`/`open`/`entrance_x`/`entrance_y`) plus a new `World::lq_data` field (`World` derives `Default`, so `LqData` just needed its own `#[derive(Default)]`). `#questlevel`/`#questreward`/`#questshow`/`#questentrance`/`#queststart` are straightforward field getters/setters with C's exact bounds checks and message text (including the `COL_LIGHT_RED`-vs-plain-`log_sys` distinction verified per message: `#questlevel`'s bounds errors are colored, `#queststart`'s "not ready yet" replies are not). `#npcshow`'s hurt/killmark exp-preview line (`lq_admin_show_npc`) now reads the real `lq_data.reward[]`/`reward_desc[]` table instead of the previously-documented always-empty placeholder, now that there's a writer. `#questreset` (`lq.c:2278-2321`) is the heaviest of the six: it scans every live character (removing `CDR_LQNPC` instances via the existing `remove_character`, matching the `remove_destroy_char` precedent already established by `#thrall`/`#nremove`; teleporting every `CF_PLAYER` character - including the issuing admin themselves if they happen to be a player standing in the reset area, matching C's unconditional scan - through the same 7-position fallback chain via the existing `teleport_char_driver`) then scans every on-map item (`IF_TAKE` destroyed via the existing `destroy_item`; `IF_PLAYERBODY` relocated through the same 7-position fallback chain via a new `World::lq_reset_drop_body_item` - unlike the pre-existing `drop_body_item` used by fresh `die_char` bodies that were never registered on the map, this item is already placed, so its old tile registration and light must be explicitly cleared first via `map.remove_item_map`/`remove_item_light` before the plain (non-extended) `map.drop_item` retry chain, matching C's own `remove_item_map(n); drop_item(n, x, y)` pairing exactly, including the "landless but not destroyed" outcome if every fallback spot is blocked) before clearing every quest registry (`lq_npcs`/`lq_npc_respawns`/`lq_doors`+`lq_doors_initialized`/`lq_data`, all reset to empty/default). 12 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_admin.rs`: `#questlevel`'s missing-args/trailing-garbage/all three bounds-check branches (with a `LqData::default()` round-trip assertion confirming no partial writes on rejection) plus its success path; `#questreward`'s out-of-bounds `nr`/`amount` branches and its success path verified end-to-end through `#questshow`'s exact three-line output; `#questentrance` reading the caller's live position; `#queststart`'s three-stage gate (no levels -> no entrance -> opens); `#questreset`'s NPC removal plus player teleport, full registry-reset assertion, and `IF_TAKE`-destroyed/`IF_PLAYERBODY`-relocated item outcomes. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3196 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1141 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area data, hundreds of ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: `#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questload` (need file I/O, a genuinely new pattern for this codebase) and `#questend`/`#xinfo` (need to read/mutate every *online* player's `PlayerRuntime::lq_marks`, which `World` has no access to - needs a queued-dispatch pattern analogous to `#nspawn`/`#thrall`/`#usurp` but iterating all sessions instead of one target) remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the fifth slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - `#usurp`/`#follow`/`#stop`/`#exit`/`#wimp` (`lq.c:2139-2334`), the possessed-NPC "me"/"emote" relay sub-command, the possessed-NPC plain-speech relay, and the per-tick `domirror` movement-mirroring branch it all drives (`lqnpc.c:2855-2868`). `lq_admin.rs` was already at 1,925 lines (near the 2,000 hard cap), so this slice lives in a brand new sibling module, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq_usurp.rs`, reusing `lq_admin.rs`'s `ArgReader`/`cmd_word_matches`/`queue_lq_error` (widened from private to `pub(super)`) rather than duplicating them. The C `pdat->usurp` field (`struct lq_plr_data`, `DRD_LQ_PLR_DATA`, no `PERSISTENT_PLAYER_DATA` bit) is modeled as a new `Character::lq_usurp: Option<CharacterId>` field - not `PlayerRuntime`, since `World`'s own command dispatch needs to read it synchronously to decide whether to relay speech, and `World` has no access to `PlayerRuntime` (same "independently-set-data" precedent as the pre-existing `merchant`/`dungeonfighter`/`fight_driver` fields, not the `DRD_PENT_NPPD`-precedent `PlayerRuntime` route the module's earlier draft note assumed). `LqNpcDriverData` gained `usurp: Option<CharacterId>`/`udx`/`udy: i32` (C `dat->usurp`/`udx`/`udy`). `cmd_usurp`/`cmd_follow`/`cmd_stop`'s C sector-quantized nearest-name search (`getfirst_char_sector`, 8-tile strides over a `[cn.x-12,cn.x+12]x[cn.y-12,cn.y+12]` box) is ported as a direct linear scan with the same 12-tile Chebyshev box and `char_dist`-nearest tiebreak - a documented simplification of a pure spatial-indexing optimization, not a behavior difference (see the module's own doc comment). `cmd_wimp`'s free-tile search turned out to need no new helper at all - the already-ported `teleport_char_driver` (used by `bones.rs`/`jail.rs`) is exactly C's own `teleport_char_driver`, so the module's earlier "needs a `teleport_char_driver`-equivalent search" note was simply wrong; only the `ppd->last_lq_death = realtime` write needed new plumbing (a `World::pending_lq_wimps: Vec<CharacterId>` queue, drained by `tick_client_actions.rs` right after the queued-action loop into a new `PlayerRuntime::set_last_lq_death`/`last_lq_death` pair at `misc_ppd` offset 8). The whole `apply_lq_usurp_command` entry point is dispatched from `tick_client_actions.rs` immediately after alias expansion - unlike every prior `CDR_LQPARSER` slice (which stayed at the table's existing, much later dispatch point since none of their verbs collide with anything earlier in the chain), this one *must* run first to match C's real priority (`special_driver` runs before `command()`'s entire switch, `system/command.c:5855-5859`): only from that position can the possessed-NPC plain-speech relay actually intercept ordinary `say` text before `apply_local_speech_command` claims it (that handler treats *any* unprefixed text as `LocalSpeechKind::Say` unconditionally) and can `#wimp` in area 20/35 pre-empt the pre-existing generic `apply_wimp_command` stub (which always shows "You're not in the live quest area..." regardless of actual area - now correctly unreachable for area 20/35, since this dispatch always claims `#wimp` first there). The `c9`/`mirror` relay sub-command (`lq.c:2710-2723`) is deliberately NOT ported - it needs `src/system/chat/chat.c`'s `server_chat`, `AGENTS.md`'s permanently-deferred cross-server chat transport. The `domirror` port in `lqnpc.rs`'s `process_lqnpc_tick` follows C's exact gate structure: the mirroring move/turn runs first (mutual-pairing-checked via a new shared `World::lq_usurp_possessor_of`, reused by both the tick and the relay dispatch's own `lq_active_usurp_target`), then `follow`/`fight_driver_follow_invisible`/`secure_move_driver` are all gated on `!domirror` (only `fight_driver_attack_visible`/`regenerate_driver`/`spell_self_driver` still run unconditionally, matching C's own `if (!domirror && ...)` placement) - `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow`'s existing `may_follow_invisible` bool parameter (previously hardcoded `true`) now carries `!domirror` directly. C's own `ch[cn].tmpx = ch[cn].x; ch[cn].tmpy = ch[cn].y;` tail (only reached when the mirroring move itself didn't already early-`return`) is dropped, matching the exact same simplification the pre-existing `follow` branch already made before this slice (neither branch's write is reachable through the always-`return`s-on-success move path, and the residual "does an idle NPC's rest position drift while remote-controlled" edge case was already an accepted gap). 20 new focused tests: 17 in a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_usurp.rs` (area/permission gating for both `#usurp` and the ungated `#wimp`, nearest-match-wins plus out-of-range-is-no-candidate for `#usurp`, `#follow`/`#stop`'s every-match-plus-count-message semantics, `#exit`'s always-shows-"Done." even with no active usurp, re-usurping replaces the old target with only one "Done." message, the plain-speech relay firing only with mutual pairing, and the `me`/`emote` relay including its verbatim-preserved doubled-space quirk) and 3 new `domirror` tests appended to `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lqnpc.rs` (mirrors possessor movement, turns to match facing when already aligned, and does *not* mirror without mutual pairing). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3184 core [+20] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1141 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, ~39 ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: only the whole quest-lifecycle family (`#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questend`/`#questload`/`#questshow`/`#questreward`/`#questlevel`/`#questreset`/`#questentrance`/`#queststart`, `#xinfo` - needs a new `lq_data` `World` field C never got ported here) plus its file I/O remain.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) progress: ported the seventh slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table (`special_driver`) - `#questend`/`#xinfo` (`lq.c:1314-1345`, `2638-2642`, `2695-2701`), the last two subcommands needing anything beyond pure `World` state. `lq_admin.rs` was already at 2,243 lines (over the 2,000 hard cap), so this slice lives in a brand new sibling module, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq_quest_admin.rs`, reusing `lq_admin.rs`'s `ArgReader`/`cmd_word_matches` (same split precedent as `lq_usurp.rs`). Both commands need `PlayerRuntime::lq_marks`, which only `ugaris-server`'s `ServerRuntime::players` has - same `#nspawn`/`#thrall`-precedent split: `World::lq_admin_wants_questend`/`lq_admin_wants_xinfo` are pure gates (area/prefix/`CF_GOD`|`CF_LQMASTER` match, matching the shared `if (ch[cn].flags & (CF_GOD|CF_LQMASTER))` block both commands sit inside in C) that never fail argument validation (C's `cmd_questend`/inline `xinfo` block never call `get_str`/`check_anything` on `ptr` at all, so there's no `Rejected` case to model, unlike `LqNspawnDispatch`/`LqThrallDispatch`); `World::apply_lq_questend_reward(target_id, sum)`/`report_lq_xinfo(character_id, marks)` are the pure-`World` reward-math/formatting halves, given data the caller already extracted from `PlayerRuntime`. New `tick_client_actions.rs::dispatch_lq_questend_or_xinfo` (checked inside the existing `LqThrallDispatch::NotMatched` arm, right before the `apply_lq_admin_command` fallback - order among distinct command words doesn't matter since none collide) does the actual `runtime.players.values_mut()` iteration for `#questend` (summing+clearing each `CF_PLAYER` character's `lq_marks` against `world.lq_data.reward[]`, matching C's `pdat->mark[n] = 0` inside the same summing loop, then calling `apply_lq_questend_reward` per player and tallying the "Rewarded N players." count) and the single `runtime.player_for_character` read for `#xinfo`. `apply_lq_questend_reward`'s exp formula is copied digit-for-digit from `cmd_questend` (`val = level_value(level) / (level/10+1); val = val/100.0*min(100,sum)`, `f64` intermediate matching C's mixed int/float math, `give_exp(target_id, val, world.area_id)` matching C's global `areaID` - this Rust server is already single-area-per-process, so `world.area_id` is the exact equivalent) including the "`if (sum)` gate on the *original* sum, not the post-clamp/computed `val`" quirk (a `sum == 1` grant that rounds to `0` exp still counts as "rewarded" and still sends the feedback line). 14 new focused tests in a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_quest_admin.rs`: both gates' area/permission/prefix/word-match cases (including `#questend` not falsely matching `#questentrance`/`#questlevel`, and `#xinfo`'s 2-char minlen), `apply_lq_questend_reward`'s zero-sum no-op, level-1 exp-grant-plus-feedback-text assertion, sum-clamped-at-100 (`sum=400` grants identically to `sum=100`), and the "sum=1 still rewards" quirk; `report_lq_xinfo`'s in-order multi-mark listing, no-marks-set no-op, and mark-index-0-ignored. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3210 core [+14] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1141 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 stays `[~]`: only `#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questload` (need file I/O, a genuinely new pattern for this codebase) remain - the last gap in this task.
- 2026-07-09: Area 20 (`src/area/20/lq.c`) CLOSED: ported the eighth and final slice of the `CDR_LQPARSER` admin command table - `#questsave`/`#questdelete`/`#questload` (`cmd_questsave`/`cmd_questdel`/`cmd_questload`, `lq.c:1346-1502`, dispatched at `:2631-2645`), the genuinely-new-file-I/O gap every prior slice deferred. `lq_admin.rs` was already at 2,243 lines (over the 2,000 hard cap), so this lives in another brand new sibling module, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/lq_quest_file.rs` (same split precedent as `lq_usurp.rs`/`lq_quest_admin.rs`, reusing `lq_admin.rs`'s `ArgReader`/`cmd_word_matches`, plus `queue_lq_error` widened from `pub(super)` to `pub` so `ugaris-server` can queue the exact same `COL_LIGHT_RED`-prefixed error text). `LqNpcState`/`LqDoorState`/`LqData` all gained `serde::Serialize`/`Deserialize` (all their leaf types - `CharacterId`/`ItemId`, `LqItemSpec` - already had it); a new `LqQuestSnapshot { data, npcs, doors }` + on-disk envelope `LqQuestFile { password, snapshot }` are this port's JSON replacement for C's raw `write(handle, &lq_data, ...); write(handle, lq_npc, ...); write(handle, lq_door, ...)` byte dump (`lq.c:1388-1391`) - no cross-version/cross-process binary-compat requirement to preserve for a save format this port owns end to end, unlike the legacy client wire protocol. Same `ZoneLoader`-needing split precedent as `LqNspawnDispatch`/`LqThrallDispatch`, except here the external resource is a filesystem path, not a fresh character: `World::try_dispatch_lq_quest_file` is the pure half (area/permission gate; `get_str` name+optional-password parsing; trailing-garbage rejection; the per-character `isalpha` name-validation loop, `lq.c:1364-1369` etc., with the exact per-C-function usage string - note `cmd_questdel`'s own `usage` local literally says `/questdel`, not `/questdelete`, even though the dispatch keyword is `questdelete`, preserved verbatim) returning an `LqQuestFileDispatch::{NotMatched,Rejected,Save,Delete,Load}` with the already-validated `name`/`password`; `World::lq_quest_snapshot`/`apply_lq_quest_snapshot` are the pure payload build/apply halves. `apply_lq_quest_snapshot` reproduces a real C quirk: `cmd_questload`'s `init_done = 0` (`lq.c:1498`) defers the door-`keyID` restore to the *next* `lq_ticker` rescan tick, because C's `lq_door[]` "identity" is purely a live-map scan-order index (`m`, incremented per `IDR_DOOR` item with `drdata[10]` set, in map-item-index order), not a stable stored key - the just-loaded `lq_door[]` array's `keyID` values just sit unused until the next scan re-derives the same slot indices and calls `update_lqdoor(m)`, pushing whatever `keyID` is already sitting in that slot onto the live door item. This port performs the equivalent rescan (a fresh `lq_doors_initialized = false` + `discover_lq_doors_once()`) synchronously instead of waiting a tick - there is no reason to delay a GM command's own visible effect, and the end state (each rescanned slot's `key_id` restored by slot index, written onto the live item via the pre-existing `write_lq_door_key_id`) is identical either way. `ugaris-server`'s new `area20::handle_lq_quest_file_dispatch` (parameterized over a `quest_dir: &Path`, `LQ_QUEST_DIR = "quest"` at the real `tick_client_actions.rs` call site, a scratch temp dir in tests) does the actual `read_to_string`/`create_dir_all`+`write`/`remove_file` against `<quest_dir>/<name>.qst`, with a shared `lq_quest_password_matches` helper reproducing C's exact gate (`if (xpassword[0] && strcmp(xpassword, password))` - an empty stored password never blocks; a corrupt/unparsable existing file is treated as "no stored password" for the overwrite-mismatch check specifically, a reasonable equivalent to C's raw-40-byte-header read since every file this port ever writes is its own valid JSON). Dispatched from `tick_client_actions.rs` in the same `LqThrallDispatch::NotMatched` arm as `dispatch_lq_questend_or_xinfo`, checked first (order among these three commands vs. `#questend`/`#xinfo` doesn't matter, no keyword collision). 16 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/lq_quest_file.rs` (gate/word/permission matching for all three subcommands including the `questdel`-vs-`questdelete` usage-string quirk, missing-name/illegal-character/trailing-garbage rejection, default-empty-password, slash-prefix acceptance, snapshot round-trip through actual JSON serialization, and the door-rescan-restores-by-slot-with-a-second-untouched-door assertion including an exact `MAKE_ITEMID` little-endian byte check) plus 7 new `#[cfg(test)]` tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/area20.rs` (save-then-load round-trip against a real scratch directory, file-not-found, save/delete/load password-mismatch rejection for all three commands, delete-then-delete-again reports not-found, and a corrupt on-disk file reporting the C "different version" message on load). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3226 core [+16] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1148 server [+7], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, 280+ ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 20 is now `[x]` - the last subcommand in the `CDR_LQPARSER` table, and this file's own last gap.
