New `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area22_lab.rs::lab5_item_driver` (pure function, mutates `&mut Character`/`&mut Item` directly for everything not needing `World`/`ZoneLoader`/`PlayerRuntime`, same precedent as `potion_driver`) + 15 new `Lab5*` `ItemDriverOutcome` variants (`item_driver/types.rs`) + `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_lab.rs`'s resolvers (widened to take a new `area_feedback: &mut Vec<(CharacterId, String, u16)>` parameter for the potion-drink broadcast, same precedent `tick_item_use_books_potions.rs` set). This closes the checkbox's own "ritual only reachable via the god-only `SET` command" gap - nameplate -> realnameplate -> entrance is now a real in-game path, independent of the god-only debug command. Not ported: `fireface`/`lightface` (`drdata[0]==2`/`13`), pure decorative ambient projectile statues with no player-visible state - both gated on a pre-existing, cross-cutting gap this iteration surfaced but did not fix: nothing in this port's `ZoneLoader`/`World::add_item` ever arms an always-on `IDR_*` driver's very first timer call at item-creation time the way C's own `create_item` unconditionally does (`call_item(driver, n, 0, ticker+1)`, `src/system/create.c:979-981`) - affects any future always-on ambient item driver, not lab5-specific, documented in `lab5_item_driver`'s own doc comment as a follow-up. 23 new tests (17 core `item_driver::tests::area22_lab` + 6 server `tests::lab`, covering every ported branch's success/blocked/timer paths plus the ritual state-machine's match/mismatch/nothing-happens cases). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3375 core [+17] + 1168 server [+6], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke on area 22 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of clean ticks (`simple_baddy_outcomes` in the hundreds per tick), no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md`'s Area 22 task stays `[~]` for the fireface/lightface architectural gap alone - every reachable gameplay path in this file is now ported.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) STARTED: this task's "plan in ledger first" instruction is satisfied by the new `world::strategy` module's own doc comment (full function inventory + ported/remaining split). First slice ports everything with no `World`/map/character dependency: new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy.rs` (`OR_*` order constants, `MISSIONS` - the 14-row mission ladder - and `AI_PRESETS` - the 24-row AI-opponent roster - both transcribed digit-for-digit from `strategy.c:162-239`, plus `str_exp_cost`/`str_increment`/`str_raise` porting the boss NPC's still-unwired "spend strategy exp to raise a stat" economy exactly, including the real `max_worker` slot-3 quirk where `str_exp_cost`'s own `< 16` eligibility gate permanently locks the slot even though the raise formula's own clamp constant is `24`) and new `crates/ugaris-core/src/player/strategy.rs::StrategyPpd` (C `struct strategy_ppd`/`DRD_STRATEGY_PPD`, a new `#[serde(default)] PlayerRuntime::strategy` field per `AGENTS.md`; `solve_cnt[MAXMISSION=64]` is a lazily-grown `Vec<u8>` with `solve_count`/`increment_solve_count` accessors rather than a fixed array, since only mission `set_solve` indices `0..=12` are ever written). REMAINING (see `world::strategy`'s doc comment for the full breakdown): the `str_area` runtime registry + `init_areas`/`str_ticker` (needs a `World`-level item scan on load, mirroring `world::pents`'s slot bookkeeping), the worker character driver (`strategy_driver`, order assignment via NPC speech), the `mine`/`storage`/`depot`/`spawner`/`nosnow` item drivers (currently dispatched as C-parity no-ops), the 538-line AI-opponent driver (`ai_main`/`ai_init`), the mission entry queue (`queue_*`), and the `strategy_boss` NPC dialogue driver plus `special_driver`'s player-facing commands. 19 new tests (13 `world::tests::strategy` - full digit-for-digit table parity for both `MISSIONS` and `AI_PRESETS`, plus `str_exp_cost`/`str_increment`/`str_raise` cap/afford/clamp behavior - and 6 `player::tests::strategy` - default/solve-count-accessor/JSON-round-trip coverage). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3400 core [+19] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server (unchanged), all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed default area-1 data, hundreds of clean ticks, no panic (this slice needed no area-23/24-specific zone data to exercise, being pure data/economy math). `PORTING_TODO.md` left `[~]` with a REMAINING note pointing at this same breakdown.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the per-tick mission-lifecycle driver itself - `World::str_ticker`/`str_did_party_lose`/`str_remove_party`/`str_close_area`/`str_reward_winner`/`str_init_mission` (C `str_ticker`/`did_party_lose`/`remove_party`/`close_area`/`reward_winner`/`init_mission`, `strategy.c:271-506`) plus little-endian drdata accessors `str_item_owner`/`set_str_item_owner`/`str_item_gold`/`set_str_item_gold` matching C's `*(unsigned int *)(it[in].drdata + N)` idiom. `reward_winner` is split `World`/`ugaris-server` the same way `world::military`'s `MilitaryMasterEvent` is (`World` can't reach session-owned `PlayerRuntime::strategy`): a pure `apply_strategy_mission_win(ppd, mission_index) -> StrategyWinOutcome` plus `World::str_reward_winner`'s character-lookup/event-queue half (new `StrategyRewardEvent`, drained by new `crates/ugaris-server/src/strategy.rs::apply_strategy_reward_events`). `IDR_STR_TICKER` now dispatches a real `ItemDriverOutcome::StrTicker` (new `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area23_24.rs::str_ticker_driver`, gated on `character.id.0 == 0` exactly like `lq_ticker_driver`) instead of a no-op; `World::apply_item_driver_outcome` gained a `StrTicker` arm calling `World::str_ticker` the same way its existing `LqTicker` arm calls `discover_lq_doors_once`, and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_clan_lq_arena.rs`'s existing clan-spawn/LQ/arena family dispatcher gained a `StrTicker` arm (reschedule the timer + `apply_strategy_reward_events`). Preserved a real C quirk verbatim in `str_ticker`: it re-reads a spawner's owner drdata *after* a same-tick `remove_party` reset rather than reusing the pre-reset local it read before that call, so a just-lost player slot still counts toward that tick's player-count (with `winner` becoming `0`, silently skipping the reward call below) and a just-lost AI slot still counts toward the AI-count - documented inline rather than "fixed". Not reachable in live gameplay yet: C's own only caller of `init_mission` is `special_driver`'s still-unported "go" mission-join command, so no spawner ever gets a real owner code and every slot's per-tick scan is currently a no-op in practice; ported ahead of that caller anyway since it's fully self-contained and directly testable, the same precedent several `area8`/`lq` slices used before their own live call sites landed. Also like the already-"done" `IDR_LQ_TICKER`, this port doesn't (yet) prime the very first `schedule_item_driver_timer` call for a real `IDR_STR_TICKER` zone item - confirmed this is a pre-existing gap shared with `LqTicker`, not a new regression; the reschedule-on-fire machinery itself is in place and covered directly by tests. 22 new tests in `world::tests::strategy` (owner/gold drdata accessor round-trips, `did_party_lose`'s storage-gold/group-member/AI-bypass branches, `remove_party`'s NPC-destroy-except-Cinciac/never-destroy-a-player/teleport-and-message/spawner-reset-to-free-vs-AI-unassigned/no-match-returns-false cases, `close_area`, `apply_strategy_mission_win`'s rewarded/no-reward/bad-index outcomes, `reward_winner`'s event-queue match/no-match, `init_mission`'s depot/storage/mine reset plus AI-spawner-assignment/player-spawner-free, and `str_ticker`'s lone-player-win-and-reward, ai-only-close, and busy-flag-toggle end-to-end scenarios). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3428 core [+22] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data plus 280+ clean ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (worker driver, item drivers, AI driver, mission queue, boss NPC dialogue).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the mission entry queue - `World::queue_validate`/`queue_remove`/`queue_mission`/`queue_check`/`show_queue` (C `queue_validate`/`queue_remove`/`queue_mission`/`queue_check`/`show_queue`, `strategy.c:3200-3276`), added to `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy.rs` right after `str_ticker`. C's dual `q_playercn[MAXQUEUE]`/`q_playerID[MAXQUEUE]` slot-plus-generation-id pair collapses to a single existence check against `self.characters` here, since this codebase's `CharacterId` is already non-recycled across a character's lifetime (the same simplification `ArenaContender`'s own doc comment documents for its own `character_id`-only contender list) - `q_player_id` is still populated (with `Character::serial`) purely for structural fidelity with the C struct shape, not because validation needs it. `queue_validate`'s stale/reused-slot invalidation collapses to one `self.characters.contains_key(...)` check per slot, followed by the same shuffle-down compaction loop C uses, verbatim. `show_queue` preserves a real C ordering quirk: the "Queue:" header line is sent *before* `queue_validate` runs (`queue_system_text` then `queue_validate`), not after - reproduced in that exact order rather than "fixed" to validate first. This whole mission-queue system is exercised only by `special_driver`'s still-unported "go"/"surrender" commands (`strategy.c:3542-3579`), so - like `str_init_mission` before it - it is fully self-contained and testable but not yet reachable through live gameplay. 8 new tests in `world::tests::strategy` (queue-mission append/no-op-when-already-queued/move-from-another-area/drop-when-full, queue_validate's logged-off-character drop-and-compact, queue_remove's cross-area clear, queue_check's empty-or-head-only truth table, and show_queue's header-before-validate ordering plus per-entry name formatting). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3436 core [+8] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data with 200+ clean ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (worker driver, item drivers, AI driver, boss NPC dialogue - the mission queue is now closed).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `special_driver`'s player-facing `#`/`/` command table (`CDR_STRATEGY_PARSER = 79`, `strategy.c:3278-3626`) minus `#eguard` (needs `ZoneLoader` character-spawning, deferred - see below). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_special.rs`: a lazily-initialized `StrategyJumpPointRegistry`/`World::ensure_strategy_jump_points_initialized` (C's file-static `jp[MAXJUMP]`/`special_init`, scanning every `IDR_STR_DEPOT`/`IDR_STR_STORAGE` item across *all* areas - unlike the per-slot `StrategyAreaRegistry` - in ascending item-id order and stamping each item's `description` with its jump-point number) plus `World::apply_strategy_special_command(character_id, area_id, ppd: &mut StrategyPpd, command) -> bool`, the actual command dispatcher. Every handler takes `ppd` as an explicit parameter rather than reading it from `self` (same split as `str_raise`/`apply_strategy_mission_win`); `ugaris-server`'s `tick_client_actions.rs` (right after the existing `CDR_LQPARSER` dispatch block, itself the tail of the `ClientAction::Text` arm) looks up the real `PlayerRuntime::strategy` via `player_for_character_mut` and calls it, area-gated on `config.area_id == 23 || 24` matching C's own `system/command.c:4591-4595` dispatch site. Ported commands: `#jp <nr>`/`#list` (teleport-to-controlled-jump-point and list-owned-jump-points-with-gold, both reusing the new registry), `#info` (the 12-row upgrade-status table, byte-for-byte reproducing every embedded column-alignment marker - `\001`/`\010`/`\011`/`\015`/`\016`/`\021`/`\022` - via raw `push(0x..)` bytes on `WorldSystemTextBytes`, C's own convention this port's existing `COL_*` handling doesn't cover), `#raise <nr>` (thin wrapper rendering the already-ported `str_raise`'s outcome into C's exact three messages), `#reset`/`#queue` (both `CF_GOD`-gated and - matching C's exact `&&` operand order in each case - *not* boss-stage-gated, reproduced as the only two commands skipping `strategy_needs_boss`), `#mission` (the mission-availability table, including the live per-area queue-position/self-marker columns via the already-ported `queue_validate`), `#enter <nr>` (the real mission-join flow: global spawner scan for a free slot vs. an already-owned "re-entering" match vs. queuing when busy, `str_init_mission` for a lone-party start, and a new pure `World::strategy_take_spawner` porting `take_spawner`'s spawner+storage ownership/name/cached-income writes - preserving the real C quirk that the storage item's displayed slot number comes from *its own* `drdata[8]` byte, not the spawner's, even though real zone data always keeps them equal), and `#surrender` (thin `str_remove_party` wrapper). `#eguard` is the only command left unported: `create_eguard` spawns a fresh `"strategy_npc"` character running the still-unported `DRD_STRATEGYDRIVER` worker AI, needing `ZoneLoader` - same split precedent as `#nspawn`/`#thrall` in `world::lq_admin`, documented in the new module's own doc comment; a player typing `#eguard` today simply falls through unmatched into ordinary chat processing, not a regression from today's fully-unported baseline. `legacy_atoi`/`cmd_word_matches`/`ArgReader` are reused directly from `world::lq_admin` (widened from `fn`/kept `pub(super)`, visible to this sibling module under the shared `world` parent) rather than duplicated. 21 new tests in a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/strategy_special.rs` (jump-point discovery/idempotency, area-gate rejection, the boss-stage gate firing for every command except `#reset`/`#queue`, both god-only commands' non-god-rejected/god-accepted cases, `#jp`'s teleport-success/wrong-owner/out-of-bounds, `#list`'s owned-only listing plus empty-owns-nothing silence, `#info`'s full 13-row byte-for-byte output, `#raise`'s success-dispatch and out-of-bounds rejection, `#mission`'s no-spawners-skips-everything and one-available-row rendering, `#enter`'s claim-a-free-spawner end-to-end and re-enter-an-owned-spawner paths plus out-of-bounds rejection, `#surrender`'s no-party-to-surrender message, `#queue`'s god-only gate, and `#eguard` falling through unmatched). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3457 core [+21] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on both default area-1 data (hundreds of clean ticks) and `--area-id 23` (the strategy minigame's own area), no panic either way. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (worker driver, item drivers, AI driver, boss NPC dialogue, `#eguard` - the mission-queue and the rest of the player command table are now closed).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the player-facing `CF_PLAYER` branches of the `mine`/`storage`/`depot` item drivers (`strategy.c:1122-1241`, driver ids `IDR_STR_MINE`/`IDR_STR_STORAGE`/`IDR_STR_DEPOT` = 105/106/108). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area23_24.rs::{str_mine_driver,str_depot_driver,str_storage_driver}`: `str_mine_driver`/`str_depot_driver` just read the building's current Platinum total via the already-ported `str_item_gold` and report it (`ItemDriverOutcome::StrMineLook`/`StrDepotLook`, "There are N units of Platinum left."/"This depot contains N units of Platinum."); `str_storage_driver` additionally checks the using player's cursor item for a carried `IDR_ENHANCE` mined silver (`drdata[0]==1`, 50:1) or gold (`drdata[0]==2`, 5:1) stack and converts it into Platinum, reusing the `cursor_driver`/`cursor_drdata0`/`cursor_drdata1_u32` `ItemDriverContext` fields `World::execute_item_driver_request_with_context` already populates generically for any driver whenever the character carries a cursor item (no new context plumbing needed) - new `ItemDriverOutcome::StrStorageInteract` + `StrStorageConversion` (`None`/`Converted{cursor_item_id,added}`/`WrongKind`, the last covering both a non-silver/gold `drdata[0]` and a conversion that rounds down to zero, C's `am == 0` catching both). The pure driver pre-computes the post-conversion Platinum total itself (cheap, since it only needs read access) so `World::apply_item_driver_outcome`'s new `StrStorageInteract`/`Converted` arm (which does the real `destroy_item`/`set_str_item_gold` mutation) doesn't need a second lookup. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_strategy.rs::dispatch_strategy_outcome` renders the three outcomes into C's exact `log_char` text (storage's info line always prints after any conversion attempt, matching C's unconditional final `log_char` call outside the citem `if`/`else`). The `cn==0` ambient branches (mine/depot cosmetic renaming; storage's periodic income-tick self-reschedule) and the NPC-worker branches (all three, gated on `!(ch[cn].flags & CF_PLAYER)`) remain documented no-op gaps - both need the still-unported `strategy_driver`/`DRD_STRATEGYDRIVER` worker AI (`strategy.c:713-1120`), which has no Rust `CDR_*`/`CharacterDriverState` scaffolding at all yet; `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` is untouched by this slice.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `strategy_driver`'s (`strategy.c:713-1120`) NT_TEXT command-cascade half - the part that turns a player's spoken "mine"/"follow"/"guard"/"fight"/"home"/"take"/"transfer"/"train" into a worker order - as pure/testable `World` methods, since no live worker character can exist yet (no `CDR_*`/`CharacterDriverState` scaffolding, same gap the previous slice's item-driver doc comment already called out). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_worker.rs`: `StrategyWorkerOrder` (C's `struct strategy_data.order`/`or1`/`or2` triple replaced by one typed enum carrying each order's payload directly - `Mine{mine_item,depot_item}`/`Follow{leader}`/`Guard{x,y}`/`Fighter{leader}`/`Take{depot_item,leader}`/`Transfer{from_item,to_item}`/`Train{storage_item}`/`EternalGuard{x,y}`, the last never produced by this slice since no `strstr` keyword sets `OR_ETERNALGUARD` in C either), `World::strategy_find_item_near`/`strategy_find_depot_or_storage_near` (C `finditem`/`finddepot`, `strategy.c:515-625` - a clockwise ring-spiral map search centered on a point, radius 9 and 19 tiles respectively; note the ring spiral never checks its own center tile, only starts at `dist=1`), and `World::strategy_worker_apply_order_text` (the actual `:743-883` cascade minus C's caller-side gate - `ch[co].flags & CF_PLAYER`/`ch[co].ID == ch[cn].group`/`char_see_char`/`dat->order != OR_ETERNALGUARD` - deferred to whatever eventually drives a live worker). The `me`/target-worker-number extraction (`:752-773`'s skip-alpha/skip-space/skip-alpha/colon/space/quote prefix trim then `atoi`) is reproduced byte-for-byte as private helpers; a player-typed target number is compared directly against a `CharacterId`'s raw numeric value instead of C's recyclable `cn` array slot (same `CharacterId`-identity simplification precedent as `str_did_party_lose`'s own doc comment) since that's what's actually baked into a worker's visible name (`setname`, not yet ported, prints literally "%s's Worker %d" with `cn`). Every "sorry, ..."/"yes, ... !" say-line format string (including the real C quirk that failure messages capitalize the honorific first-word while success messages don't, and that several messages repeat `militarySirname(co)` twice in the same format string as an apparent copy-paste artifact) is reproduced digit-for-digit. `military_sirname`/`military_sirname_lower` (`MilitarySirname`/`militarySirname`, `tool.c:1548-1566`) are new local helpers (no shared sirname helper existed yet in this port); `get_army_rank_string` reuses the already-ported `army_rank_name(army_rank_for_points(...))` from `world::npc::area32::military::missions`. 21 new tests in a new `world::tests::strategy_worker` (targeting/addressing gate incl. the `!me && dist>30` ignore and the exact-worker-number match/mismatch cases, every one of the 8 order keywords' success path plus "mine"/"transfer"/"train"'s failure-message paths, "take"'s silent-no-op-on-failure quirk, later-keyword-overrides-earlier-one-in-the-same-message, and both spiral-search helpers directly including the exactly-outside-radius boundary). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3488 core [+21] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (NT_CREATE handling, `setname`/`restplace`/`findstorage`, the per-tick order-execution switch, the `CDR_STRATEGY`/`CharacterDriverState`/`spawner_sub` spawning wiring, the NPC-worker item-driver branches, and the AI-opponent driver). 12 new tests in `world::tests::strategy` (mine/depot look values, storage's no-cursor/silver-conversion/gold-conversion/wrong-kind/rounds-to-zero/non-enhance-cursor cases including cursor-item-destroyed/gold-mutated assertions, and both mine's and storage's `cn==0`/NPC-worker no-op gates). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3467 core [+65 vs. the 3400 baseline two slices back, includes this slice's 12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data, 200+ clean ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (worker driver, spawner item driver, AI driver, `#eguard` - the three buildings' player-facing halves are now closed).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `setname`/`restplace`/`findstorage` (`strategy.c:564-712`) as pure/testable `World` methods, extending `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_worker.rs`. `setname` splits into `strategy_train_price` (the `TRAINPRICE` macro), `strategy_worker_name` (the per-order name template - `Mine`->"Miner", `Follow`->"Minion", `Guard`->"Guard", `EternalGuard`->"E-Guard", `Fighter`/`Take`->"Fighter", `Transfer`->"Transfer", `Train`->"Trainee", default->"Worker") and `strategy_worker_description` ("Carrying N Platinum, N of N exp"); the `strcmp`+`reset_name` "did the name actually change" half is left for whatever eventually drives a live worker character, same deferral as the NT_TEXT cascade's own caller-side gate. `findstorage` is `World::strategy_find_storage_owned_by_group` - a linear ascending-item-index first-match scan (not a ring spiral) for an `IDR_STR_STORAGE` item whose `str_item_owner` matches a given `group`, reusing the same explicit-sort-for-determinism precedent `ensure_strategy_areas_initialized` already established for this same unordered `HashMap<ItemId, Item>`. `restplace` is `World::strategy_worker_rest_place`: C's `dat->restplace` (a raw `m`-space integer offset) is carried as an `Option<(dx, dy)>` tile-delta pair instead (`None` standing in for C's `0` sentinel, since no entry in the 32-row `restlist` fallback table is ever `(0, 0)`) - checks the cached offset first (free, or blocked only by the worker itself already standing there, C's `map[m+dat->restplace].ch == cn` override), then falls through the fixed `restlist` search order, then gives up and returns the base tile unchanged (offset left as-is) if every candidate is blocked, matching C's exact three-tier fallback. 18 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_worker` (`TRAINPRICE` arithmetic, every order's name label, the description format string, `findstorage`'s owner-match/wrong-driver cases, and `restplace`'s no-cache-uses-first-candidate/cache-still-free-reused/cache-now-blocked-falls-through/self-occupancy-override/all-blocked-returns-base-unchanged scenarios). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3498 core [+11 net after also touching a doc comment; +18 tests total in the module] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. This change is pure `ugaris-core` math/state helpers with no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched, so no boot-smoke was needed. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (NT_CREATE handling, the per-tick order-execution switch, the `CDR_STRATEGY`/`CharacterDriverState`/`spawner_sub` spawning wiring, the NPC-worker item-driver branches, and the AI-opponent driver - `setname`/`restplace`/`findstorage` are now closed).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the `strategy_boss` NPC dialogue driver end to end (Cinciac, `CDR_STRATEGY_BOSS = 80`, `strategy.c:1412-1621`) - a stationary, static zone-placed NPC (already present in both `zones/23/strategy.chr` and `zones/24/strategy.chr`, `driver=80`), so unlike the worker driver this needed no `ZoneLoader` spawning wiring at all. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area23_24/boss.rs`: `World::strategy_boss_character_ids`/`strategy_boss_sighted_players` (a direct per-tick scan replacing C's `NT_CHAR` sighting, `char_dist < 16` + `char_see_char`, same precedent as `world::npc::area8::fdemon_boss`), `World::strategy_boss_greet_player` (the full `switch (boss_stage)` 0-11 stage ladder including the lazy `init_done` full-struct `bzero`-equivalent reset and the real C case-1-falls-through-into-case-2 quirk - a player whose rank crosses the rank-8 threshold while still on stage 1 jumps straight to stage 3, skipping ever displaying stage 2's own dedicated call, in the exact same tick), and `World::strategy_boss_process_text_messages`/`strategy_boss_apply_text_command` (the real `Character::driver_messages`-queue-driven "repeat"/"military rank"/"levels and experience" `NT_TEXT` commands - all three independent `if`s, not an `else if` chain, so one message can match more than one). `boss_timer`/C's wall-clock `realtime` is carried as `World::tick` game ticks instead (`STRATEGY_BOSS_TIMER_THROTTLE_TICKS = TICKS_PER_SECOND * 5`), same simplification precedent as `fdemon_boss`'s own port. Wired by new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area23_24.rs::apply_strategy_boss_tick` (`&mut player.strategy` passed straight through, no Facts/Update snapshot split needed unlike `fdemon_boss` since `StrategyPpd` has no other driver racing to read it concurrently), registered as `tick_npc::area23_24::strategy_boss_driver_118`. This is the first live path that can ever advance `StrategyPpd::boss_stage` past its zero default, so it unlocks the entire already-ported `CDR_STRATEGY_PARSER` command table (gated behind `strategy_special.rs`'s `strategy_needs_boss`, `boss_stage < 9`) through real gameplay for the first time - `#jp`/`#list`/`#info`/`#raise`/`#mission`/`#enter`/`#surrender` were all unreachable before this slice. 15 new tests in a new `world::tests::strategy_boss` (sighted-player range filter, stage-0 low/high-rank branch choice, stage-1's low-rank no-timer-touch and high-rank fallthrough-to-stage-3, stage-5's rank-string-not-name text, stage-10's exp-gated advance plus its `boss_msg_exp` snapshot, stage-11's revert-to-10-on-more-exp, the throttle window's suppress/pass boundary, the first-encounter full-struct reset, text-message independent-multi-match classification plus out-of-range/self-loopback rejection, `Repeat`'s stage/timer reset, and both reward choices' points/exp award plus their `boss_exp<=0` no-op guard). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3513 core [+15] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on `--area-id 23` (Cinciac's own area, `placed_characters=1`), 12+ clean seconds, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (the per-tick order-execution switch, the `CDR_STRATEGY`/`CharacterDriverState`/`spawner_sub` spawning wiring, the NPC-worker item-driver branches, and the AI-opponent driver - `strategy_boss` is now closed).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `strategy_driver`'s full per-tick body (`strategy.c:714-1121`) - the recruitable worker/fighter/miner NPC's own driver, `CDR_STRATEGY = 78` - end to end, plus the `mine`/`storage`/`depot` item drivers' NPC-worker branches these orders actually need to move Platinum. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area23_24/worker.rs`: `CharacterDriverState::StrategyWorker(StrategyWorkerDriverData)` (C's `struct strategy_data` plus this port's own single-victim self-defense tracking, same precedent as `CDR_ROBBER`/`CDR_GATE_FIGHT`), `World::process_strategy_worker_actions`/`process_strategy_worker_tick` (the message loop - NT_CREATE sets `level` from base Wisdom, NT_TEXT reuses the already-ported `strategy_worker_apply_order_text` cascade plus `apply_tabunga_text_notification`, NT_GIVE destroys the cursor item, NT_GOTHIT tracks a single victim - then `setname` reapplication, victim-visibility refresh, attack-if-visible, and the cooldown-gated `regenerate_simple_baddy` call), and the full `switch (dat->order)` order-execution dispatch: `OR_MINE`/`OR_TRANSFER` (byte-for-byte identical shared body), `OR_TAKE` (ownership check -> `Fighter` transition), `OR_FOLLOW`/`OR_FIGHTER` (leader-validity check + follow-within-2 movement), `OR_GUARD`/`OR_ETERNALGUARD` (the tiered `move_driver` min-dist-0/2/4 retry ladder, simplified to "try each, `false` if all fail" since every C failure path converges on the same shared trailing `do_idle` anyway), `OR_TRAIN` (level-up bookkeeping - `TRAINMULTI`-scaled exp, `TRAINPRICE` threshold, the seven-stat `value[1]` write plus `update_character` - and the shared `restplace`-based move-to-rest-position tail), and the `default` order (recomputes `findstorage` fresh every tick instead of C's `dat->or1` cache, since `StrategyWorkerOrder::None` carries no payload to cache into - a documented perf-only simplification, never a behavior difference). `strategy_worker_use_item` (walk-adjacent-then-`do_use`, identical shape to `World::lampghost_use_lamp`/`janitor_use_light`) is the `use_driver` equivalent. Also closed in the same slice: `mine`/`storage`/`depot`'s `!(ch[cn].flags & CF_PLAYER)` branches in `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area23_24.rs` - new `ItemDriverOutcome::StrMineWorkerDig`/`StrBuildingWorkerTransfer`/`StrDepotWorkerTakeover` (the pure drivers compute the amount to transfer via `character_value_base(.., Strength)` + `str_item_gold`, `World::apply_item_driver_outcome` performs the actual item-drdata/`StrategyWorkerDriverData::platin` mutation, same split precedent as the already-ported `StrStorageInteract::Converted` arm); `str_storage_driver`'s and `str_depot_driver`'s transfer bodies are byte-for-byte identical once `depot`'s own ownership check has passed, so both share one `str_building_worker_transfer` helper. Documented deviations (all consistent with existing precedent elsewhere in this codebase, see the module's own doc comment for the full list): single-victim self-defense instead of C's generic 10-slot `fight_driver_data` (no proactive NT_CHAR/NT_SEEHIT aggro regardless of order - a real, intentional simplification, not an oversight); `ch[co].ID == ch[cn].group` becomes `speaker.group == worker.group` (this port has no persistent-ID-vs-array-slot distinction, and `group` is exactly the field `area8_army::spawn_army_soldier`'s own `pgroup` parameter already uses for the same "carry an owning player's identity onto a recruited NPC" purpose); `reset_name`/`fight_driver_set_dist`/`fight_driver_set_home` not ported (same gaps as everywhere else in this codebase); NT_CREATE's `ch[cn].arg`-gated eternal-guard branch and `OR_TAKE`'s `or2==0` fallback not modeled (both are dead code on every currently-ported call path - only the still-unported `ai_init`/`create_eguard` would ever reach them); the decorative snow-melt easter egg dropped (cosmetic only). No live `CDR_STRATEGY` character can exist yet - `spawner_sub`/`take_spawner` spawning remains unported - so this is registered in `tick_npc::area23_24` (`apply_strategy_worker_tick`, right after `strategy_boss_driver_118`) and `ugaris-server/src/area23_24.rs::apply_strategy_worker_tick` regardless, per the "port ahead of its own live call site" precedent several `area8`/`lq` slices already established, and is exercised entirely via directly-constructed test characters in the meantime. 21 new tests: 6 in `world::tests::strategy` (mine-npc-worker digs-min-of-strength-and-gold plus empty/no-strength no-op, storage-npc-worker deposits-full-platin plus withdraws-min-of-strength, depot-npc-worker claims-ownership-instead-of-transferring plus transfers-once-ownership-matches - also split two pre-existing "documented noop" tests that would otherwise have silently gone stale into one still-true ambient-`cn==0`-only assertion each) and 15 in a new `world::tests::strategy_worker_driver` (NT_CREATE's level-from-Wisdom, NT_GOTHIT's victim-tracking plus its group-equality gate, NT_GIVE's cursor-item destruction, NT_TEXT's matching-group-assigns-order/different-group-ignored/eternal-guard-ignores-new-order cases, `OR_GUARD`'s walk-when-away/idle-when-arrived, `OR_FOLLOW`'s leader-gone-resets-to-none/walks-toward-a-distant-leader, `OR_TAKE`'s ownership-match transition to `Fighter`, the default order's walk-toward-owning-group-storage, and `OR_TRAIN`'s level-up-on-enough-exp plus revert-to-guard-at-max-level). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3534 core [+21] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data, hundreds of clean ticks, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note (only `spawner_sub`/`take_spawner` spawning, `IDR_STR_SPAWNER`, and the full `ai_main`/`ai_init` AI-opponent driver remain).
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `IDR_STR_SPAWNER`'s player-facing `spawner`/`spawner_sub` (`strategy.c:1244-1381`) - the last missing piece needed to ever recruit a live `CDR_STRATEGY` worker through real gameplay (every other slice of this task, including the worker's own per-tick driver, was already tested only via directly-constructed test characters). New `ItemDriverOutcome::StrSpawnerUse` (`item_driver::area23_24::str_spawner_driver`) is the minimal pure trigger - it only detects "a player used this `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` item" (matching the `cn == 0` ambient/AI-init branch staying a documented `Noop` gap) - since the real business logic needs session-owned `StrategyPpd` `World` can't reach, plus (on success) a fresh character only `ugaris-server`'s `ZoneLoader` can create. `World::try_dispatch_strategy_spawner_use` (new in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_worker.rs`) ports everything computable without a `ZoneLoader`: the ownership match (`ch[cn].ID` vs. the spawner's `str_item_owner`), the `spawner2storage` lookup, the `NPCPRICE` Platinum-cost check, and `spawner_sub`'s own worker-count eligibility scan (every live `CDR_STRATEGY` character in the same `group` whose order isn't `OR_ETERNALGUARD`, compared against `StrategyPpd::max_worker`) - each rejection queues C's exact failure text via `queue_system_text`. A real C quirk is preserved deliberately, not "fixed": once the eligibility scan passes, `spawner_sub` deducts the storage's `NPCPRICE` Platinum *unconditionally*, *before* `create_char`/`item_drop_char` ever runs, so a subsequent drop failure (no free adjacent tile) still spends the Platinum with nothing to show for it - this method reproduces that ordering exactly (the deduction happens in the `Ready` branch, before the caller ever attempts to build the character), and the caller is documented to never refund it. `ugaris-server`'s new `tick_item_use_strategy::spawn_strategy_worker` (wired from `dispatch_strategy_outcome`, now needing `world`/`zone_loader`/`runtime` like `tick_item_use_minewall`'s `MineWallDig` arm) builds the actual fresh `"strategy_npc"` character (already carrying `driver=78`/`CDR_STRATEGY` in its own zone-template data, confirmed against `ugaris_data/zones/23/strategy.chr`) via `ZoneLoader::instantiate_character_template` + the pre-existing `World::spawn_character_from_item_drop` (C's own `item_drop_char`, already ported for `pents.rs`'s demon spawns), applies the `value[1]` warcry/endurance/speed bonuses before `World::update_character` recomputes `value[0]`, restores hp/endurance/mana to the new max (same tail pattern as `area8_army::spawn_army_soldier`), stamps dir/sprite/group, and finishes via a new `World::finish_strategy_worker_spawn` driver-state stamp (`owner_name`/`trainspeed`/`max_level`). C's unread `ch[co].tmpx`/`tmpy` scratch fields are not modeled (same class of omission as every other unread scratch field in this codebase). 7 new tests in `world::tests::strategy` (ownership-mismatch/missing-storage/not-enough-gold/cap-reached rejections each asserting the exact queued message and that gold stays untouched, an eternal-guard-doesn't-count-toward-the-cap case, a success case asserting every `StrategySpawnerSpawnPlan` field plus the immediate `NPCPRICE` deduction and no success message, and a `finish_strategy_worker_spawn` driver/driver-state assertion). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3541 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on both default area-1 data (hundreds of clean ticks) and `--area-id 23` (`placed_characters=1`, `character_templates=84`), no panic either way. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the only remaining gap in this entire task is the full 538-line `ai_main`/`ai_init` AI-opponent driver.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the structural building blocks of the `ai_main`/`ai_init` AI-opponent driver (`strategy.c:1672-2267`,`2432-2447`) - everything with no dependency on a live spawned AI army, following the exact "ported but not yet spawnable" precedent `strategy_worker.rs`'s own worker-order-assignment slice already established before the worker driver itself went live. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_ai.rs`: `AiNpc`/`AiPlace`/`AiData` (C `struct ai_npc`/`struct ai_place`/`struct ai_data`, `Vec`-backed instead of C's fixed `MAXNPC`/`MAXPLACE` arrays since every entry here is always a real, populated one - no C-style "slot 0 unused" sentinel scheme needed; `cserial` is dropped since a Rust `CharacterId` is already a non-recycled stable identity, same precedent as `World::str_did_party_lose`'s own doc comment; `order`/`or1`/`or2` deliberately stay C's raw ints rather than the existing `StrategyWorkerOrder` enum, since the AI's own `task_take` sometimes sets a sentinel `or2 = 0` "no leader" that enum's `Take{leader: CharacterId}` variant can't represent - converting this raw form into a live worker's real `StrategyWorkerOrder` is left for the future slice that actually spawns one). Ported: `update_npc_place`/`subtask_move` (`:1797-1863`, the place-graph navigation primitives every `task_*` function shares - `ai_check_target`'s own bespoke pathfinder callback has no dedicated Rust equivalent, reusing `crate::path::pathfinder`'s existing `Normal` block mode instead, a documented minor deviation since that mode also blocks on `TMOVEBLOCK`, which C's own callback doesn't check); all seven `task_*` functions (`task_idle`/`task_take`/`task_guard`/`task_mine`/`task_transfer`/`task_train`/`task_fight`, `:1865-1994` - `task_idle` is the one needing `&mut World` rather than `&World`, since C's `restplace` reads/mutates the live worker's own persisted `StrategyWorkerDriverData::restplace` field via the already-ported `World::strategy_worker_rest_place`, auto-vivifying a default `StrategyWorkerDriverData` on the live character exactly like C's own `set_data` auto-allocates; `task_fight` is a real C quirk kept verbatim - despite the name it sets `OR_GUARD`, not a fight order); the roster-bookkeeping primitives (`assign_npc`/`add_worker`/`add_etguard`/`add_guard`/`remove_guard`/`remove_worker`, `:1996-2105`, all pure `AiData` mutations); and the guard-defense allocation logic (`wantguardcnt`/`assign_guards`/`remove_free_guards`/`nag_attack`, `:2111-2267`,`2432-2447` - `assign_guards` is the one `World` method needing live-character reads rather than `AiNpc`'s own cached `level` copy, since C's `THREAT(cn)` macro and its HP-readiness gate both read `ch[cn]` directly, one tick fresher than the cached copy the still-unported roster-refresh block would otherwise provide). 37 new tests in a new `world::tests::strategy_ai` (`AiData`/`AiNpc`/`AiPlace` zero-init-plus-sentinel-stamp construction, `update_npc_place`'s stays-close/finds-new-place/no-match-found cases, every roster-bookkeeping function's round-trip, `wantguardcnt`'s boundary table, `remove_free_guards`'s nagging-guard/busy-guard exemptions, `subtask_move`'s no-op/direct-path/parent-chain-down/off-path-up cases, all 7 `task_*` functions' at-target and moves-when-not-there paths including `task_mine`'s target-or-its-parent acceptance, and `assign_guards`'s dispatch/HP-not-ready/ragnarok-overrides-readiness/recall-under-level-gap cases plus `nag_attack`'s cooldown/threat-presence/idle-guard-count gates). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3578 core [+37] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched by this slice, so no boot-smoke required, though `cargo build -p ugaris-server` was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: `ai_init`/`ai_main`'s own outer per-tick bodies (place-graph construction from map items, live roster discovery, the per-tick roster/threat refresh, worker spawning, and the per-npc task-dispatch switch that calls the now-ported functions above), `create_eguard`, and the threat-detection scan that populates `AiPlace`'s threat fields all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `World::ai_init` itself (`strategy.c:2269-2427`) - the place-graph construction and live-roster discovery half of the AI-opponent driver, on top of the structural building blocks the previous slice ported. New in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_ai.rs`: `World::ai_init(spawner_item, code) -> Option<AiData>` resolves `code - STR_OWNER_AI_BASE` into an `AI_PRESETS` row (`ad->ppd = preset[...].ppd`, ported as a new `AiPreset::to_strategy_ppd` in `strategy.rs`, right next to `AI_PRESETS`'s own definition), discovers every `IDR_STR_MINE`/`_DEPOT`/(possibly-enemy) `_STORAGE` item sharing the spawner's `drdata[8]` area slot (ascending item-id order for determinism over the unordered `HashMap`, same precedent as `ensure_strategy_areas_initialized`), runs the `pathfinder`-based BFS connecting every place's `dist`/`parent` back to storage, and propagates a new `AiPlace::enemy_possible` flag up the whole parent chain from every enemy storage discovered. `AiData` gained a new `etguardcnt: i32` field (C `ad->etguardcnt`) for the eternal-guard roster count. The live-roster-discovery half is deliberately factored into a new, separately-tested `AiData::register_npc(cn, x, y, level, order, or1, or2, has_exp) -> usize` (push + classify into `Ignore`/`EGuard`/`Idle` + the C-exact unconditional post-classification `used = -1` reset, `:2401-2424`) rather than inlined into `ai_init`'s own discovery loop: `ai_init`'s `code`-vs-`Character::group` match (`u32::from(c.group) == code`) can *never* actually succeed in this codebase today, since every valid `ai_init` `code` exceeds `u16::MAX` while `Character::group` is narrowed to `u16` - the exact same pre-existing, documented gap `World::str_did_party_lose`'s own doc comment already flags (not a new one, and not something this slice's scope should fix: `Character::group` is a widely-shared field across ~30 unrelated NPC/combat files, all of which only ever use small group ids, so widening it is out of scope for this task). Splitting out `register_npc` keeps the actually-useful classification logic exercised by direct unit tests despite `ai_init`'s own roster-discovery loop being unreachable dead code in practice until either a live AI-owned worker can be spawned or `Character::group` is widened - whichever future slice does that will just need to fix the comparison, not re-derive the classification logic. `strategy_worker_order_to_raw` (new, file-private) is the `StrategyWorkerOrder -> (order, or1, or2)` inverse conversion `ai_init`'s roster loop needs when a discovered NPC already carries orders. C's "check for map errors" `xlog` loop is skipped (pure logging, no persisted-log sink anywhere in this port, same precedent as every other bare `xlog` call). 12 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai`: `AiPreset::to_strategy_ppd`'s nine-field copy plus zero-default for the rest, `ai_init`'s `None` returns for an out-of-range code and a missing spawner/storage, ppd-seeding-from-preset plus the storage-only single-place graph, mine+depot discovery/connection in the same slot with a different-slot item correctly excluded, `enemy_possible` propagation up through an intermediate depot when the enemy storage is too far to connect to storage directly, same-slot-storage-is-a-partner-not-an-enemy, `register_npc`'s three classification branches (idle/experienced-eguard/high-level-eguard) plus its eternal-guard-counts-as-ignore-not-a-roving-guard case, and one test documenting the real current `ai_init`-roster-discovery-always-empty limitation end to end. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3590 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched, so no boot-smoke required, though `cargo build -p ugaris-server` was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: `ai_main`'s own outer per-tick body (roster/threat refresh, worker spawning, per-npc task-dispatch switch), `create_eguard`, and the threat-detection scan populating `AiPlace`'s threat fields all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `World::ai_refresh_places` (`strategy.c:2505-2630`) - `ai_main`'s per-place owned/platin/threat refresh loop plus its trailing "project threats to neighboring places" pass, on top of the structural building blocks and `ai_init` the previous two slices ported. `AiPlace` gained five new fields (`platin`/`threat`/`threatlevel`/`threatnlevel`/`threatncount`/`owned`, matching every remaining field of C's `struct ai_place` this port hadn't carried yet); `AiData` gained `pdist`/`panic`/`pplace` (C's panic-defense trio, `ai_init`'s `pdist = 3` default folded into `AiData::new`). `World::ai_refresh_places(&self, ad: &mut AiData, code) -> AiPlaceRefreshResult`: for every place, refreshes `owned`/`platin` from the live building item's `str_item_owner`/`str_item_gold`, decays/rebuilds `threat`/`threatlevel`/`threatcount` from nearby enemy `CDR_STRATEGY` presence (propagating threat up to the parent, then a fresh share back down), tracks the closest un-threatened place with gold into `ad.pdist`, and computes whether the party has any economy left (`ragnarok`/`nogoldleft`). C's sector-grid scan (`getfirst_char_sector`/`sec_next` stepping a computed +-12 box by 8) is replaced with a plain linear `self.characters.values()` scan filtered by the same final `abs(...) < 10` check - same observable result, same "no sector index in this codebase" precedent used elsewhere (e.g. `world/npc/bank.rs`); C's `seen[MAXCHARS]` de-dupe array (shared across every place in one call, *not* reset per place - a character only ever contributes threat to the first place, in `n` order, whose scan region contains it) is a `HashSet<CharacterId>` local to the function with the same single-shared-scope lifetime. `ragnarok`/`nogoldleft` are returned via a new `AiPlaceRefreshResult` rather than written straight into `AiData`: C only commits them to `ad->ragnarok`/`ad->nogoldleft` at the very end of `ai_main`, *after* the still-unported worker-task-assignment/threat-handling/nag-attack blocks that read the *previous* tick's committed values mid-function - writing them early here would corrupt that read-before-write ordering for whatever future slice assembles the rest of `ai_main`. `cantrain` (normally re-derived every tick by the still-unported "update npc list" NPC-vec refresh from each live NPC's *current* level) is instead derived here from each `AiNpc`'s cached `level`/`task` fields - a documented best-effort stand-in that goes stale between roster refreshes until that loop is ported; the module's doc comment explains why removal-based NPC-list refresh doesn't map cleanly onto the current `Vec`-backed (no C-style empty-slot sentinel) roster and is deferred. 11 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (platin/owned refresh from a live item, owned-false-on-owner-mismatch, nearby-enemy-triggers-panic with the exact THREAT/threat/threatlevel arithmetic, same-group-and-non-`CDR_STRATEGY`-characters ignored, the shared `seen` set spanning two places in one call, `pdist` committing the closest un-threatened gold place, `ragnarok`/`nogoldleft` staying true with no spare gold and going false when a non-storage place has gold, `ragnarok` staying true at storage without `cantrain` and going false with `cantrain` plus enough spare gold, and the parent/child threat-projection pass). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3601 core [+11] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched, so no boot-smoke required). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the "update npc list" NPC-vec refresh, the worker-count/eternal-guard-count half of the per-place loop, worker spawning, the panic/non-panic task-assignment switch, the final per-npc task-dispatch switch, `create_eguard`, and the "place eternal guards" tail all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the remaining pure roster-bookkeeping refreshes from `ai_main`'s outer body that need no live-character/item access (`strategy.c:2484-2500,2509-2520,2531-2539,2632-2642`), closing every part of the per-place `for` loop besides the threat scan `World::ai_refresh_places` already covers. `AiData::update_nag_guard` (`:2496-2500`, clears `nagguard` once its NPC is no longer a qualifying elite guard still targeting `nagplace`, or the nag has run past 90 seconds - C's `!ad->an[i].cn` "NPC slot emptied" half can't fire yet since NPC-vec removal remains unported, documented inline rather than silently dropped); - 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `World::ai_update_npc_list` (`strategy.c:2461-2482`) - the 'update npc list' NPC refresh, the one piece of `ai_main`'s per-tick body the previous slice's own doc comment had flagged as not mapping cleanly onto the `Vec`-backed roster. Resolved by widening `AiNpc::cn` from a plain `CharacterId` to `Option<CharacterId>` (C's `an[n].cn = 0` 'slot emptied' sentinel) rather than attempting index-preserving `Vec` removal: every other piece of `AiData`/`AiPlace` that stores plain NPC-array indices (`worker[]`/`eguard`/`guard[]`/`nagguard`) keeps working unchanged, since a slot's *position* never moves, only its `cn` goes `None` - every other field is left deliberately stale when this happens, matching C exactly (C's removal branch only ever touches `cn` itself). `World::ai_update_npc_list(&self, ad: &mut AiData) -> bool` refreshes `x`/`y`/`level`/`platin` (the latter from the live character's own `StrategyWorkerDriverData::platin`, C's `set_data(..., DRD_STRATEGYDRIVER, ...)`) and resets `used` to `-1` for every still-live roster entry, empties the slot for anything that no longer resolves in `World::characters`, and returns C's `cantrain` local (true if any live eternal guard is still below `ppd.max_level`) - C's extra `ch[cn].serial != cserial` staleness re-check has no equivalent here, since a Rust `CharacterId` is already a stable, never-reused identity (same precedent as every other `cserial`-dropping doc comment in this module already established). This also finally makes `AiData::update_nag_guard`'s `!ad->an[i].cn` branch reachable (previously documented as dead code in this port, now wired in). `World::ai_threat`/`World::ai_guard_ready` widened to accept `Option<CharacterId>` accordingly (a `None` contributes nothing, same as an id that fails to resolve - no behavior change for any existing caller, since every `AiNpc::cn` in this port was always `Some` before this slice). Not yet wired to `World::ai_refresh_places`'s own cached-level `cantrain` stand-in, since nothing assembles a real `ai_main` call across both methods yet - left for whichever future slice does that assembly. 5 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (position/level/platin refresh from a live character plus `used` reset, slot-emptied-on-despawned-character (with every other field asserted still stale), cantrain true for an under-`max_level` eternal guard, cantrain false for a non-eguard below `max_level`, and a no-op on an already-empty slot). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3621 core [+5] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched by this slice, so no boot-smoke required, though `cargo build -p ugaris-server` was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: worker spawning, the panic/non-panic task-assignment switch, the final per-npc task-dispatch switch, `create_eguard`, the 'place eternal guards' tail, and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call (no live spawn/tick call site exists yet for an AI-controlled party) all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `AiData::assign_tasks_to_workers` (`strategy.c:2674-2796`) - the panic/non-panic "assign tasks to workers" loop, `ai_main`'s core per-tick planning decision, on top of every roster-bookkeeping/refresh primitive the previous sixteen slices ported. Panic: every non-eternal-guard/non-ignore NPC gets `task = T_FIGHT` and `target = pplace` stamped - but (a real C quirk kept verbatim, not "fixed") if that NPC was actually working a place (`used != -1`), the immediately-following `remove_worker(n)` call unconditionally resets `task` back to `T_IDLE` (and `used` to `0`, not `-1` - `remove_worker`'s own real stamp), so only an *already-free* NPC ends up genuinely fighting; a previously-busy one just goes idle at the panic place instead, since `target = ad->pplace` is applied outside the `if` regardless. Non-panic: each NPC first gets a chance to be demoted from (`wantguardcnt < gcnt && !nogoldleft && !ragnarok`) or promoted to (`wantguardcnt > gcnt || nogoldleft`, `gcnt < MAXGUARD`) elite-guard duty; eternal guards (`T_EGUARD`)/permanently-ignored NPCs (`T_IGNORE`) are otherwise never touched; everyone else keeps their current job if still productive/safe (four distinct "still fine, `continue`" checks - the NPC's own cached `platin` for Transfer/Mine, the place's `owned` state for Take, then two more checking the *place's* `platin` for Transfer/Mine specifically), gets evicted if threatened/out-of-range, redirected to a busier parent place if one has spare capacity (`i > 0` deliberately excluding storage itself as a transfer destination via this path), or falls back to searching for the nearest unowned empty depot (`T_TAKE`) or nearest understaffed mine/depot/storage with spare platin (`T_MINE`/`T_TRANSFER`), else goes fully idle. Two new `AiData` fields support this: `ragnarok`/`nogoldleft` (the *previous* tick's committed values - C only commits `World::ai_refresh_places`'s freshly recomputed pair at the very end of `ai_main`, after this switch runs, so reading `AiData`'s own field here deliberately reads stale data, matching C's read-before-write ordering exactly; default `false`, matching C's zero-initialized global `ai_data[]` before any commit ever happens) and a new `AiPlaceRefreshResult::mindist` field (C's `mindist` local, previously silently discarded by `ai_refresh_places` - now threaded through as an explicit parameter to this method, since it's needed unchanged by this switch and, later, by the still-unported "find places with too little workers"/threat-handling tail). New `WORKERPLATIN = 200` constant (C `#define WORKERPLATIN 200`, `strategy.c:2107`). One deviation, documented on the method itself: C's `i = ad->ap[m].parent;` can be `-1` (when `m` is storage, whose own parent is `-1`), and the very next line unconditionally reads `ad->ap[i].threat` - an out-of-bounds read in C itself, harmless there only because `ai_place ap[]` sits inside a larger struct so `ap[-1]` reads some other field's garbage rather than crashing. Rust can't reproduce an OOB read safely, so a `-1` parent is treated as "no parent threat", the same "no parent means nothing to check" convention every other already-ported parent-chain walk in this module already uses (e.g. `ai_refresh_places`'s own `if ad.places[n].parent != -1` guards). 11 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (panic sending an already-free NPC to Fight vs. reverting a working NPC to Idle via `remove_worker`, eguard promotion when understaffed, eguard demotion when the economy allows it, eguard demotion blocked by `ragnarok`, a productive Transfer worker and a still-valid Take worker both staying in place via their own `continue` checks, eviction from a newly-threatened place, redirection to an understaffed parent, assignment to the nearest unowned depot, assignment to the nearest understaffed mine, and the full idle fallback when nothing qualifies). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3633 core [+12, includes one doc-only field addition plus the 11 new tests] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data, 200+ clean ticks, no panic (this slice is pure `ugaris-core` `AiData` logic with no live call site yet, so boot-smoke wasn't strictly required, but was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: worker spawning, the "find places with too little workers"/threat-handling/worklevel-adjustment/"place eternal guards" tail (needs new `at[]`/`max_at`/`lastchange` fields, `tcomp`, and `create_eguard`, which needs `ZoneLoader`), the final per-npc task-dispatch switch (needs a raw-ints-to-`StrategyWorkerOrder` conversion for its write-back), and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `ai_main`'s final "make NPCs do their jobs" `switch` (`strategy.c:2932-2972`) as `World::ai_dispatch_tasks(&mut self, ad: &mut AiData)` in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_ai.rs`, dispatching every roster NPC to its already-ported `task_*` function by `AiTask` (`Idle`/`Mine`/`Transfer`/`Fight`/`Take` call their matching function directly; `EGuard`'s nested `if` - train if idle at storage and the economy can afford it (live character level below `ppd.max_level` and storage has spare Platinum/free workers/enough banked worker-platin/over the worker cap), else idle; guard if it has an actual assigned target - is kept verbatim; `Ignore` is a no-op, same as C). After dispatch, the raw `order`/`or1`/`or2` triple the call just wrote onto `AiNpc` is synced back onto the live worker character's own `StrategyWorkerDriverData::order` via a new `raw_to_strategy_worker_order` (the inverse of the existing `strategy_worker_order_to_raw`, mapping each `OR_*` code back to its typed `StrategyWorkerOrder` variant), auto-vivifying a default `StrategyWorkerDriverData` exactly like `World::ai_task_idle`'s own precedent (a live AI-controlled worker's driver state isn't guaranteed to have been touched by a real tick yet the first time this runs); a despawned NPC (`cn: None`) is skipped without panicking. 7 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (Take/Mine writing their typed order back onto the live character, an Ignore eternal-guard's order staying untouched end-to-end including the character-side assertion, EGuard-with-no-target both training when the economy can afford it and idling at max level, EGuard-with-a-target guarding it, and a despawned NPC's dispatch not panicking with no character to write back onto). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3640 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched by this slice, so no boot-smoke required). `strategy_ai.rs` is now 1,923 lines - split it before adding the next slice. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: worker spawning (`:2644-2672`), the "find places with too little workers"/threat-handling/worklevel-adjustment/"place eternal guards" tail (`:2798-2924`, needs new `at[]`/`max_at`/`lastchange` fields, `tcomp`, and `create_eguard`, which needs `ZoneLoader`), and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call (no live spawn/tick call site exists yet for an AI-controlled party) all remain.

- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported `World::ai_threat_and_worklevel_tick` (`strategy.c:2798-2916`) - the "find places with too little workers"/threat-list maintenance/worklevel-adjustment tail that runs right after `AiData::assign_tasks_to_workers`, on top of every previous slice. New `AiThreat` type (C `struct ai_threat`, `place == 0` doubling as both "slot empty" and the real storage place index 0 - preserved verbatim, including every place this ambiguity is tested: expiry, the existing-entry search, `tcomp`, and the reduce loop's own `if (ad->at[m].place)` guard) plus `AiData::threats: Vec<AiThreat>`/`lastchange: i64` fields (`Vec`-backed like `places`/`npcs`, C's `max_at` bound replaced by `Vec::len`/`truncate`). `World::ai_threat_and_worklevel_tick(&self, ad, tick, mindist, ragnarok_in) -> bool` ports, in C order: the "find places with too little workers" scan (sets `missing`); expiry of `AiThreat` entries older than 20 seconds; recording/refreshing an entry for every currently-threatened reachable place (reusing an existing matching-place slot, else an emptied slot, else pushing - the exact same search-twice structure C's own `for` loops use, which is what naturally reproduces the place-0 collision above); a new file-private `tcomp` (`strategy.c:2205-2228`) sort comparator kept verbatim including two real bugs neither "fixed": empty-slot entries always compare `Less` regardless of which side they're on (C's own `if (!pb) return -1;` almost certainly should have been `return 1;`), and the distance comparison always returns `Less` regardless of direction (`>` and `<` both `return -1`), so distance never actually influences ordering in practice - only the final `int`-truncated `double` count difference does (documented: Rust's sort algorithm differs from glibc's `qsort`, and this comparator isn't a valid strict order to begin with, so exact tie-break permutations can differ, same "different sort algorithm, same comparator" precedent as `world::npc::area22::lab1_gnome`'s own gnometorch sort); the "reduce max_at" walk (any threat whose entire parent chain up to storage has no *other* already-threatened place gets a real `World::ai_assign_guards` call, truncating the `Vec` exactly where C shrinks `max_at`, including the edge case where C's own `n = m = 0` initial values force `max_at` to `1` even when the reduce loop's own `for` never executes - reproduced by pushing one `AiThreat::default()` if the `Vec` was empty); `AiData::remove_free_guards` (already ported, invoked here in C's own call order); and worklevel growth/shrink (capped `[1, AI_MAXWORKER]`) gated on 20/10-second cooldowns. `ragnarok` is threaded in/out as a plain `bool` rather than committed to `AiData` (same "commit only at the very end of a real `ai_main`" precedent as `World::ai_refresh_places`'s own doc comment), preserving C's own `ragnarok = ad->ragnarok;` override verbatim: a successfully-defended threat suppresses this tick's freshly computed escalation, falling back to the *previous* tick's already-committed `AiData::ragnarok` field instead. Still unported: the "place eternal guards" block (`:2892-2911`, needs a still-unported `create_eguard`/`ZoneLoader`), the `nag_attack()` call itself (already ported as `World::ai_nag_attack`, just not invoked from here - deferred to a real `ai_main` assembly), the `ZoneLoader`-needing tail of worker spawning, and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call. 9 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (forces-one-slot-when-none-exist, expires-stale-entries, records-a-new-threat-entry, ignores-a-threat-beyond-mindist, dispatches-guards-and-truncates-the-list, shrinks-worklevel-when-missing-and-no-free-workers, grows-worklevel-when-nothing-missing-and-workers-idle, worklevel-capped-at-`AI_MAXWORKER`, and ragnarok-left-untouched-when-no-guards-dispatched). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3658 core [+9] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (no runtime-loop/login/map-sync/protocol surface touched by this slice, so no boot-smoke required, though `cargo build -p ugaris-server` was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the "place eternal guards" block, the `ZoneLoader`-needing tail of worker spawning, and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call all remain.
