Research this iteration also turned up a real prerequisite gap blocking the final "assemble one real `ai_main` call" item: the existing `IDR_STR_TICKER`/`IDR_LQ_TICKER` `cn==0` timer reschedule arm (`tick_item_use_clan_lq_arena.rs`'s `StrTicker`/`LqTicker` handling) is itself unreachable dead code today - it lives in the player-`item_use` completion pipeline (`tick_item_use_completion.rs`), not `tick_world.rs`'s `process_due_timers` `timer_outcomes` loop where a real `character_id==0` timer fire's outcome actually lands (see that loop's `EdemonGateSpawn`/`ChestSpawn`/etc. arms for the correct precedent) - so neither ticker's very first `schedule_item_driver_timer` call is ever primed from a live `cn==0` fire, and `ai_main` has no `cn==0` driver-timer path to hang off of yet either; fixing that pre-existing gap is a prerequisite for wiring `ai_main` live, not part of this task's own scope, and is noted in `PORTING_TODO.md`'s REMAINING for whoever picks this up next. 7 new tests in `world::tests::strategy_ai` (`ai_wants_more_eguards`'s cap-reached/under-cap cases, `ai_eguard_spawn_candidates`'s six-place table exercising every eligibility condition independently, `ai_plan_eguard_spawn`'s out-of-range-code/out-of-range-place/success-offsets-and-copies-preset-fields cases, and `register_new_eguard`'s append-when-full and reuse-first-emptied-slot cases including the `etguardcnt` bump and the place's own `eguard` slot getting stamped). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3665 core [+7] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1168 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on `--area-id 24` (`placed_characters=1`), no panic (this slice is pure `ugaris-core` `AiData`/`World` logic with no live call site yet, so boot-smoke wasn't strictly required, but was still run clean). `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: the `ZoneLoader`-needing tail of both spawn kinds and assembling every ported piece into one real `ai_main` call (blocked on the `cn==0` timer-wiring prerequisite above) all remain.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: ported the two `ZoneLoader`-needing character-creation tails the previous slice deliberately deferred - `spawner_sub`'s own `create_char`/`item_drop_char` half for AI-recruited workers (`strategy.c:1259-1279`) and `create_eguard`'s own `create_char`/`drop_char` half for AI eternal guards (`strategy.c:2991-3023`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_item_use_strategy.rs::spawn_ai_worker`/`spawn_ai_eguard`, fed from `AiWorkerSpawnPlan`/`AiEguardSpawnPlan` respectively: `spawn_ai_worker` is the AI-side sibling of the tenth slice's `spawn_strategy_worker` (drop near the AI's own spawner item via `World::spawn_character_from_item_drop`, apply the `value[1]` warcry/endurance/speed bonuses, `update_character`, restore hp/endurance/mana to max, stamp dir/sprite/group, finish via the existing `World::finish_strategy_worker_spawn`) but with no player-facing failure message (C's own `ai_main` just lets its "create new workers" `while` loop silently `break`); `spawn_ai_eguard` drops directly at a fixed `(x, y)` via `World::spawn_character` (C `drop_char`) instead, and additionally stamps the fixed `level`/`WIS`/`INT`/`AGI`/`STR` values `create_eguard` sets before the shared value-bonus/hp-restore/dir-sprite-group tail, finishing via a new sibling `World::finish_ai_eguard_spawn` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_worker.rs`) that stamps the `OR_ETERNALGUARD` order/coordinates instead of `trainspeed`/`max_level` (which `create_eguard` never sets, so they stay at the driver-state default's `0`). Both new `AiWorkerSpawnPlan`/`AiEguardSpawnPlan` types needed their first-ever re-export out of `ugaris-core` (`world/mod.rs` gained `pub use strategy_ai_tasks::{AiEguardSpawnPlan, AiWorkerSpawnPlan};` - the whole `strategy_ai_tasks` module had no re-export at all until now, since nothing outside `ugaris-core` needed any of its types before this slice). Both server-side functions are `#[allow(dead_code)]`'d (same precedent as `dungeon.rs`/`snapshots.rs`/`depot.rs`/`events.rs`'s pre-wired-but-not-yet-called code) since neither has a live caller yet - `ai_main` itself still isn't assembled into one real call site (blocked on the `cn==0` timer-wiring prerequisite two slices back already documented) - so both are exercised directly by 2 new tests in a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/strategy.rs` (a minimal `strategy_npc` character template loader shared by both tests; the worker test asserts the returned character lands within 1 tile of the spawner item, every stat/hp/endurance/mana/dir/sprite/group/driver-state field, and `StrategyWorkerOrder::None`; the eguard test asserts an exact `(x, y)` drop, the fixed level/WIS/INT/AGI/STR stamp, the value bonuses, and `StrategyWorkerOrder::EternalGuard`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3665 core + 1170 server [+2] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on `--area-id 23`, no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: assembling every ported `ai_main` piece into one real call (blocked on the `cn==0` timer-wiring prerequisite) is now the only remaining gap in this entire task.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) progress: closed the two prerequisite gaps the previous slice flagged before `ai_main` could ever be assembled, then did the assembly itself. First, the real reschedule bug: `IDR_STR_TICKER`/`IDR_LQ_TICKER`'s `cn==0` self-reschedule (`call_item(it[in].driver, in, 0, ticker + TICKS)`) used to only be applied by `tick_item_use_clan_lq_arena.rs`'s `StrTicker`/`LqTicker` arms - a call path a `character_id==0` timer-fired outcome never actually reaches (that dispatcher only ever sees outcomes from the player-`item_use`-completion pipeline), so both tickers fired exactly once and then went silent forever. Moved the `World::schedule_item_driver_timer` reschedule call into `World::apply_item_driver_outcome`'s own `LqTicker`/`StrTicker` arms (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/item_outcomes.rs`) - the real dispatch point both `process_due_timers` (the timer path) and `execute_item_driver_request_with_context` (the item-use path) funnel through - and moved `str_ticker`'s reward-event drain (`apply_strategy_reward_events`, needs `ServerRuntime`, unavailable to the pure `World` dispatch point) to `tick_world.rs`'s `timer_outcomes` loop instead, the same "match the outcome after `process_due_timers`" precedent `EdemonGateSpawn`/`ChestSpawn`/etc. already established; `tick_item_use_clan_lq_arena.rs`'s now-genuinely-dead `LqTicker`/`StrTicker` arms are neutered to just increment the counter, with a comment explaining why the real logic moved. Second, priming: `IDR_LQ_TICKER`/`IDR_STR_TICKER` are now included in `World::schedule_existing_light_timers`'s zone-load priming sweep (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/light.rs`) - the existing narrower precedent that already substitutes for C's fully generic `create_item_nr` priming call, for exactly the same "always-on ambient `cn==0` driver" reason every other entry in that match already is. Boot-smoke against `--area-id 20`/`--area-id 23` confirms both tickers now genuinely self-perpetuate: `processed timer callbacks count=1` fires once at tick 1 (the new priming) and then again every ~24 ticks indefinitely (tick 25, 49, 73, ...) instead of going silent after the first call - a real, live, previously-undetectable bug now fixed and observably verified. With both prerequisites real, `World::ai_main` (new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy_ai_main.rs`) assembles every one of the 21 previously-ported pieces into one real per-tick call in C's exact order: `ai_init` (gated on a new `World::ai_parties: HashMap<u32, AiData>` registry - C's `ai_data[MAX_AI]`, keyed directly by `code` instead of a fixed-size array-with-offset-index, a missing entry standing in for C's `!ad->ai_init` gate) -> `ai_update_npc_list` -> `update_guard_list` -> `update_nag_guard` -> `update_place_worker_and_eguard_counts` -> `ai_refresh_places` -> `update_free_npc_count` -> (maybe) `ai_plan_worker_spawn` -> `assign_tasks_to_workers` -> (non-panic only:) `ai_threat_and_worklevel_tick` -> (maybe) `ai_plan_eguard_spawn` -> `ai_nag_attack` -> commit `ragnarok`/`nogoldleft` -> `ai_dispatch_tasks`. Two documented simplifications, both already flagged by `AiWorkerSpawnPlan`/`AiEguardSpawnPlan`'s own doc comments as needing a `ZoneLoader`-backed caller this pure `World` method can't provide: at most one worker/eguard spawn plan is returned per call instead of C's unbounded loop (a party under cap with spare gold still converges to the same steady state, just spread across a few more ~1-second ticks instead of bursting in one); and no live `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` `cn==0` timer tick calls `ai_main` yet (still a documented gap - see REMAINING). New `World::register_ai_worker`/`register_ai_eguard` let `ugaris-server` call back into the stored `AiData` once it actually builds a planned character from a returned `AiMainOutcome`, the same "plan now, register once the caller creates the character" split every other spawn plan in this subsystem already uses. 10 new tests in a new `world::tests::strategy_ai_main` (invalid-code no-op, fresh-party-initializes-and-stores, second-call-reuses-the-stored-party-instead-of-reinitializing, plans-a-worker-when-funded/plans-none-when-not, both `register_ai_*` functions adding to the stored roster plus their unknown-party no-op cases, and a full two-call integration test confirming a registered worker gets dispatched a real task and has its typed `StrategyWorkerOrder` synced onto its live character) plus 2 new regression tests (`lq_ticker_reschedules_itself_via_apply_item_driver_outcome`/`str_ticker_reschedules_itself_via_apply_item_driver_outcome`) proving the reschedule fix. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3677 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1170 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on default area-1 data, `--area-id 20`, and `--area-id 23`, all clean with no panics, the ticker self-reschedule fix specifically observed live in the last two. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: wiring a live `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` `cn==0` ambient/AI-init timer tick to actually call `World::ai_main` and dispatch its spawn plans (needs a new item-driver outcome for the waiting-sentinel-vs-active-AI branch, zone-load priming for `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` itself, and the one-time `spawner2storage` name/income setup) is now the only remaining gap in this entire task.
- 2026-07-11: Areas 23/24 (`src/area/23_24/strategy.c`) CLOSED: wired the live `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` `cn==0` ambient/AI-init timer tick (`spawner`, `strategy.c:1319-1356`) - the last remaining gap in this entire task. New `ItemDriverOutcome::StrSpawnerAmbientTick` (`item_driver::area23_24::str_spawner_driver`'s `character.id.0 == 0` branch) is a minimal pure trigger carrying only `item_id`, since every actual branch needs `World` access (other-item/map lookups, the LCG random seed for the jittered `TICKS + RANDOM(TICKS)` reschedule) the pure driver doesn't have; `World::apply_item_driver_outcome`'s new arm calls the real logic, `World::str_spawner_ambient_tick` (`crates/ugaris-core/src/world/strategy.rs`), which reproduces C's three-way branch on the spawner's own `str_item_owner` exactly: a real player serial (or the unclaimed `STR_OWNER_NONE`) gets no reschedule at all (C's ambient chain silently dies, matching the unconditional `return;` after both `if`s with nothing else in between); `STR_OWNER_AI_UNASSIGNED` reschedules only; a real `STR_OWNER_AI_BASE`-range AI code reschedules then either runs the one-time setup (`World::str_spawner_first_activation` - renames the spawner/its paired storage item to `"<preset name>'s Spawner/Storage (<slot>)"`, seeds the storage's own `drdata[9]` income-per-tick byte from the preset, matching the still-unported `storage` `cn==0` income-tick's read side - and marks the spawner's own `drdata[9]` "init done", but deliberately does *not* call `ai_init` itself the way C does right after this setup - deferred one tick to `World::ai_main`'s own `!self.ai_parties.contains_key(&code)` fallback gate, which runs it exactly as correctly since nothing else reads this party's state in between) or calls `World::ai_main` and queues any returned worker/eternal-guard spawn plan onto two new `World` pending queues (`pending_ai_worker_spawns: Vec<(u32, AiWorkerSpawnPlan)>`/`pending_ai_eguard_spawns: Vec<(u32, usize, AiEguardSpawnPlan)>`, the `u32`/`usize` carrying back the `code`/place index `register_ai_worker`/`register_ai_eguard` need since `AiWorkerSpawnPlan::group`'s `u16` narrowing can't round-trip the real `code`) - `World` can't build the actual character itself (needs `ZoneLoader`), same "pure `World` queues, `ugaris-server` drains" precedent as `pending_lq_npc_spawns`. `World::ai_init` also now seeds `ad.ppd.npc_color` directly from the spawner's own `drdata[10]` byte (C mutates the static `preset[code-STR_OWNER_AI_BASE].ppd.npc_color` in place right before its own `ai_init` call so `ad->ppd = preset[...].ppd` picks it up; `AI_PRESETS` is an immutable `const` table in this port, so the override applies straight to the fresh `AiData` instead - functionally identical since a real `code` only ever maps to one spawner in practice). `ugaris-server`'s `tick_world.rs` gained an unconditional per-tick drain of both new pending queues (same precedent as the LQ NPC spawn queue drain right above it) calling the already-implemented `tick_item_use_strategy::spawn_ai_worker`/`spawn_ai_eguard` (both had their `#[allow(dead_code)]` removed now that they finally have a live caller) and, on success, `World::register_ai_worker`/`register_ai_eguard`. `IDR_STR_SPAWNER` is now also included in `World::schedule_existing_light_timers`'s zone-load priming sweep alongside the two tickers (unconditional - every real spawner needs its ambient tick regardless of current owner-code content, since `str_spawner_ambient_tick` itself decides reschedule-vs-stop from the live owner code each call). `StrSpawnerAmbientTick` needed a dead-arm addition to both `tick_item_use_completion.rs`'s exhaustive outcome-family match and `tick_item_use_strategy.rs`'s own `dispatch_strategy_outcome` (a `character_id==0` timer-fired outcome never actually reaches that player-`item_use`-completion dispatcher in practice - same documented "exists only for exhaustiveness" precedent as `LqTicker`/`StrTicker` in `tick_item_use_clan_lq_arena.rs`). 5 new tests (`world::tests::strategy`: the real driver dispatch producing the outcome end-to-end, the player-owned-slot no-reschedule case, first-activation's rename/income-seed/deferred-`ai_init` assertions using `AI_PRESETS[22]` "Death" for a non-zero income to assert against, and the steady-state case asserting `ai_main` actually ran and queued a worker plan; `world::tests::strategy_ai`: `ai_init`'s new `npc_color` override). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3682 core [+5] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1170 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game loop" on both `--area-id 23` and `--area-id 24`, spawner timers self-perpetuating (`processed timer callbacks` firing continuously), no panics either way. `PORTING_TODO.md` now marks this task `[x]` - every one of its 23 slices is closed.
- 2026-07-11: Area 25 (`src/area/25/warped.c`) CLOSED: the item drivers (`IDR_WARPTELEPORT`/`IDR_WARPBONUS`/`IDR_WARPKEYSPAWN`/`IDR_WARPKEYDOOR`/`IDR_WARPTRIALDOOR`) were already ported in earlier iterations - this closed the two remaining character drivers, `CDR_WARPMASTER` and `CDR_WARPFIGHTER`, plus the `warped_raise` stat/equipment-scaling machinery `IDR_WARPTRIALDOOR`'s spawn had never actually called. `CDR_WARPMASTER` (`world::npc::area25::warpmaster`, new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area25/` directory with a shared `AREA25_QA` table transcribed verbatim from `warped.c`'s own `qa[]`, including its embedded `COL_LIGHT_BLUE`/`COL_RESET` markers) ports the full `NT_CHAR` greeting (no `CF_PLAYER` guard in C here - a real, preserved quirk - plus `mem_check_driver`/`mem_add_driver` slot 7 dedup and a 10-tile distance gate), the `NT_TEXT` handler (`analyse_text_qa` against `AREA25_QA`; the "reset" row's `answer_code: 2` resets `warped_ppd` via a new `WarpmasterOutcomeEvent::ResetWarpPpd`, since `World` cannot see `PlayerRuntime`), and the `NT_GIVE` alchemy-stone-for-keys trade (the literal non-monotonic C table - type 23->one key, 21->two, 22->three, 24->four - preserved digit-for-digit; key creation needs `ZoneLoader`, so it's a new `WarpmasterOutcomeEvent::GiveKeys` applied by `crates/ugaris-server/src/area25.rs::apply_warpmaster_events`, mirroring the `area19.rs`/nomad split precedent exactly). `CDR_WARPFIGHTER` (`world::npc::area25::warpfighter`) ports `warpfighter`'s full per-tick body: the `NT_TEXT`->`tabunga` relay, the `standard_message_driver(cn,msg,1,0)` NT_CHAR-auto-aggro/NT_GOTHIT-self-defense cascade (reimplemented directly against the driver-independent `character.fight_driver` slot via `simple_baddy_can_add_standard_enemy`/`add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked`, since `CDR_WARPFIGHTER` is not a `CharacterDriverState::SimpleBaddy` and can't reuse `process_simple_baddy_messages`' outcome pipeline), the owner-out-of-bounds/serial-mismatch/missing-owner self-destruct check (`World::remove_character`), the endurance-potion/healing-potion self-heal branches (both fully ported; the level>60 "drinks a potion of freeze" branch's `RANDOM(2)` self-buff sub-branch is ported too, but its "spoiled potion" sibling - which calls C `create_item("freeze_spell")`+`create_spell_timer`, a spell-timer-creation mechanism this codebase has never ported - is a documented no-op, the outer roll still drawn to preserve the RNG sequence), and the full `fight_driver_attack_visible`+`fight_driver_follow_invisible`+`regenerate_driver`+`spell_self_driver`+`secure_move_driver` tail via the already-shared `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow`/`regenerate_simple_baddy`/`spell_self_simple_baddy`/`secure_move_driver` helpers (the *full* multi-enemy list mechanism, not the "single-victim simplification" several other custom drivers document, since `fight_driver_attack_visible_and_follow` is already driver-agnostic). `warped_raise` itself (`warped.c:487-608`) is split C-source-line-for-line across the pure/impure boundary this codebase always uses: `apply_warped_raise` (pure, `world::npc::area25::warpfighter`) rescales every already-present, `skill[].cost`-nonzero value via a per-index `match` transcribed verbatim from C's `switch(n)` (including the real quirk that `V_MANA` is *not* cased despite being a "power" stat like `V_HP`/`V_ENDURANCE` which are - it falls to the `default: base-40` branch), recomputes `exp`/`exp_used`/`level` via the already-ported `calc_exp`/`exp2level`, and sets the `P_LIGHT`/`P_DARK`/`P_ATHLETE` profession bytes; the five "spell of equipment" item attachments (`equip1`/`equip2`/`equip3`/`armor_spell`/`weapon_spell`, non-worn inventory slots 12-16) need `ZoneLoader`, so they're `crates/ugaris-server/src/spawns.rs`'s expanded `spawn_warp_trial_fighter` (widened with `base`/`owner`/`owner_serial`/`tx`/`ty`/`xs`/`xe`/`ys`/`ye` parameters - the pre-existing `warp_trial_door_spawn_helper_instantiates_fighter_at_room_center` test in `tests/commands_player.rs` was updated to the new signature and its stale pre-`warped_raise` hp/endurance/lifeshield assertions replaced with the real rescaled values, since the old assertions predated this task's own completion of the C behavior), reusing `dungeon.rs`'s exact "spell of equipment" item-instantiation shape (a new sibling `set_warped_raise_equip_item` helper, since the mod-value formulas differ from `dungeon.rs`'s own `dungeon_guard_equip_mod_value`) plus new pure `warped_equip_mod_value`/`warped_armor_spell_mod_value`/`warped_weapon_spell_mod_value` helpers. `tick_item_use_warp.rs`'s `WarpTrialDoor` outcome handler - which previously spawned a vanilla, unraised fighter and never actually teleported the player through the door at all, silently dropping the outcome's own `player_x`/`player_y`/`fighter_target_x`/`fighter_target_y`/`xs`/`xe`/`ys`/`ye` fields via `..` - now reads the player's `warp_base` (defaulting to 40, same as `warpbonus_driver`), passes every field through to the widened spawn helper, and teleports the player through the door only after a successful spawn, matching C's exact call order. The death-hook half (`warpfighter_died`, only the *owning player's own* killing blow teleports them back through the door) is `crate::world_events::death_hooks::apply_warpfighter_death_from_hurt_event`, registered in the standard `LegacyHurtEvent` fan-out. 20 new focused tests (11 in `world::tests::warpfighter`: three self-destruct triggers plus a stays-alive case, NT_GOTHIT/NT_CHAR aggro-add, the potion delay/pot_done gate, `apply_warped_raise`'s rescale+exp/level+profession assertions, and the three mod-value formula checks; 9 in `world::tests::warpmaster`: NT_CHAR low/high-level greetings plus its once-only dedup, NT_TEXT hello/reset/non-player-ignored, and NT_GIVE's two key-count branches plus the generic hand-back fallback) plus 6 new server-side tests (3 in `tests::world_events` for the death hook's owner-match/kill-by-someone-else/already-left-the-room cases, 2 in a new `tests::area25` for `apply_warpmaster_events`, 1 in `tests::spawns` for the full equipment-item wiring with real templates). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3702 core [+20] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server [+6], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 10s boot-smoke on `--area-id 25` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/25/warped.map`/`placed_characters=149`, "entering Rust game loop", and 270+ ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` now marks this task `[x]`.
- 2026-07-11: Area 26 (`src/area/26/staffer.c`) progress: ported `smugglecom_driver` (`:403-656`) and its `smugglelead_died` (`:658-674`) death-hook tail - the Contraband quest chain, quests 35-37. New `CDR_SMUGGLECOM = 88`/`CDR_SMUGGLELEAD = 89` (`character_driver.rs`); `CDR_SMUGGLELEAD`'s own `ch_driver` dispatch is an unconditional `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` tail call in C, so it reuses the SimpleBaddy AI end-to-end (same precedent as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER` - the 4 `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` reuse gates in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` were widened alongside it). `CDR_SMUGGLECOM` (new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area26/{mod,smugglecom}.rs`, following the exact `world::thomas`/`world::sir_jones` `PlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent` split) ports the full eleven-state (`0`-`10`) dialogue chain: greeting -> book request -> (`NT_GIVE` `IID_STAFF_SMUGGLEBOOK`, quest 35 done via the new `World::destroy_items_by_template_id` helper - already ported, just not previously called - state jumps to `5`) -> the four-item contraband list -> (`NT_GIVE` x4 pearls/ring/cape/necklace: each grants a manual `scale_exp(questlog_count(co,36), 1000).min(level_value(level)/4)` reward directly via `World::give_exp`, reusing the exact `scale_exp`/`level_value` combo `world::npc::area17::thiefmaster` already established, and sets one `SMUGGLEBIT_*`; once all four bits are set, quest 36 completes with its table's `exp: 0` entry - "exp awarded in driver", matching C's own comment) -> "kill the leader" -> (external: `apply_smugglelead_death_from_hurt_event`, new in `world_events/death_hooks.rs`, advances `smugglecom_state` `8`->`9` on a player kill, mirroring `apply_two_robber_death_from_hurt_event`'s shape) -> "thank you", quest 37 done. Two real C quirks reproduced verbatim (documented in the module's own doc comment): unlike `world::thomas`/`world::sir_jones`, this driver's `NT_TEXT` branch has no victim-staleness-reset preamble and no victim-mismatch early-out at all (replies to *any* nearby player's matched small talk, not just its tracked victim); and `case 7`'s first "Thank you" line is spoken unconditionally *before* the `questlog_isdone(co, 37)` check that gates whether `didsay` is ever set, so the already-done sub-branch (state jumps straight to `10`) still speaks that line without refreshing `last_talk`/`current_victim` - same "spoken but didsay unset" shape as `world::sir_jones`'s own `case 12`. A new file-local `AREA26_QA` table (`world/npc/area26/mod.rs`, the 12-row `qa[]` from `staffer.c:90-101`) is a separate table from `AREA3_QA` with its own `answer_code` `3` meaning ("reset me", a god-only `smugglecom_bits`/`smugglecom_state` wipe, gated on `CharacterFlags::GOD` read directly off the speaking `Character` - no `PlayerRuntime` needed for that check). 5 new `IID_STAFF_SMUGGLE*` item-id constants (`ids.rs`, `DEV_ID_WARR` 0x5-0x9, siblings of the pre-existing `IID_STAFF_BLUEKEY*`/`GREENKEY*`/`REDKEY*` warr-prefix keys). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area26.rs` (`smugglecom_player_facts`/`apply_smugglecom_events`, the `QuestDone` event resolving through the standard `complete_legacy`+`give_exp`(if nonzero)+questlog-resend pattern) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area26.rs` (`smugglecom_driver_121`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`). `PlayerRuntime::staffer_smugglecom_state`/`_bits`/`staffer_carlos2_state`-style accessors already existed from the earlier `IDR_STAFFER` item-driver slice; no new `PlayerRuntime` fields were needed. `init_staff_quests`'s `smugglecom_state`-based quest-35/36/37 flag initialization already existed and needed no changes. 21 new focused tests (`world::tests::smugglecom`: every dialogue state transition including both silent-no-op quirks, the disjoint-range `case 2` reset, the god-gated `case 3` reset, the book/four-contraband-piece/already-collected/unrelated-item `NT_GIVE` branches with an exp-cap assertion at level 10). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3724 core [+21] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 26` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/26/dragon.map`/`placed_characters=225`, "entering Rust game loop", and hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` stays `[~]`: only `rouven_driver` (`CDR_ROUVEN = 130`, quests 62/63, the Imperial Vault guard - `IDR_STAFFER` subtype 4's `vault_skull` quest-62 completion already exists, awaiting only the dialogue driver itself) remains for Area 26.
- 2026-07-11: Area 26 (`src/area/26/staffer.c`) CLOSED: ported `rouven_driver` (`:681-914`), the Imperial Vault guard who runs quests 62 ("Tunnel Magics") and 63 ("Chronicles of Seyan") - the direct continuation of `world::npc::area3::carlos`'s ritual quest chain (quest 61). New `CDR_ROUVEN = 130` (`character_driver.rs`); `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area26/rouven.rs` ports the fourteen-state (`0`-`13`) dialogue chain, sharing `AREA26_QA` (the same `qa[]` table `world::npc::area26::smugglecom` uses) and following the identical `RouvenPlayerFacts`/`RouvenOutcomeEvent` `World`/`PlayerRuntime` split every other area-3/26 NPC in this porting effort established: `case 0`'s greeting is gated on `staffer_carlos2_state() != 0` (Carlos must have already sent the player), using `mem_check_driver`/`mem_add_driver` slot `0` to avoid repeating the "talk to Carlos first" line every tick (same precedent as `carlos_driver`'s own `case 0`); `case 5`/`9`/`13` are silent no-ops waiting on external events (`vault_skull`, already ported, advances `rouven_state` `0..=5`->`6` and completes quest 62; the `NT_GIVE` handler below completes quest 63 and jumps `6..=9`->`10`); `case 12` grants `vault_key1`/`IID_MAX_VAULTKEY` (the item id was already defined, reserved for this driver, per the smugglecom-port ledger note) only when the player doesn't already carry one (`World::character_has_item_template`, `pub(crate)`-widened by an earlier `kelly_driver` port and reused here rather than duplicated) - the actual `create_item`/`give_char_item` pair needs `ZoneLoader`, so it's a new `RouvenOutcomeEvent::GrantVaultKey` applied by `crates/ugaris-server/src/area26.rs::apply_rouven_events` (same "eligibility in `World`, instantiation in `ugaris-server`" split as `astro2_driver`'s money reward/`vault_shelf`'s template rewards). The `NT_TEXT` "repeat"/"restart" handler's C three-way `if`/`if`/`if` range-reset ladder (`:842-854`, no `else` but mutually exclusive ranges) is ported as a plain `match`; unlike `smugglecom_driver`, `rouven_driver`'s own text switch has no `case 3` ("reset me") of its own even though the shared `qa[]` table still defines that entry - falls through to the generic "still counts as `didsay`" catch-all, same as every other unhandled matched code. Like `smugglecom_driver` (but unlike `thomas`/`sir_jones`), this driver's `NT_TEXT` branch has no victim-staleness-reset preamble and no victim-mismatch early-out at all - reproduced verbatim. `RouvenOutcomeEvent::QuestDone`'s single-quest-63 completion (quest 62's own completion already lives inside the already-ported `vault_skull` item driver, not here) applies via the standard `complete_legacy` flow, real quest-table exp, no manual item reward. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area26.rs::rouven_driver_130`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all` (after `smugglecom_driver_121`, matching C's own `ch_driver` switch order). `PlayerRuntime::staffer_rouven_state`/`set_staffer_rouven_state` and `staffer_carlos2_state` already existed from earlier `IDR_STAFFER`/`carlos_driver` slices; no new `PlayerRuntime` fields were needed. 3 exhaustive-match update sites (`character_driver.rs`/`world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`) plus `zone.rs`'s spawn-time driver-state init gained the new `CharacterDriverState::Rouven` variant, following the established one-file-per-NPC pattern exactly. 17 new focused tests (`world::tests::rouven`: every dialogue state transition including all three silent no-ops, the `carlos2_state`-gated `case 0`'s `mem_check_driver` dedup, the disjoint-range text "repeat"/"restart" reset across all three ranges, the unhandled-`case-3`-still-counts-as-didsay quirk, the vault-key grant/no-double-grant branches, and all three `NT_GIVE` branches - chronicles-in-range/out-of-range/ritual-hand-back/unrelated-item). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3741 core [+17] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 26` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/26/dragon.map`/`placed_characters=225`, "entering Rust game loop", and hundreds of ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` now marks this task `[x]` - Area 26 is fully closed.
- 2026-07-11: Area 28 (`src/area/28/brannington_forest.c`) CLOSED: `IDR_BRANNINGTONFOREST`'s item driver (`underwater_berry`) was already ported in an earlier iteration - this closed the three remaining character-side pieces: `aristocrat_driver`/`yoatin_driver` and the `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS` boss's `robberboss_dead` death hook. New `CDR_ARISTOCRAT = 100`/`CDR_YOATIN = 101`/`CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS = 102` (`character_driver.rs`). `aristocrat_driver` (`:234-424`, new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area28/{mod,aristocrat}.rs`) ports the nine-state (`0`-`8`) "Family Heirloom" chain (quest 38): greeting through six flavor lines to "please retrieve my Amulet", then an `NT_GIVE IID_STAFF_ARIAMULET` turn-in (destroying the companion `IID_STAFF_ARIKEY` too, same "eligibility in `World`, `create_money_item` in `ugaris-server`" split as `astro2_driver`'s money reward - `AristocratOutcomeEvent::QuestDone`'s `completion.times_done == 1` gate matches C's own `if (tmp == 1 && ...)` grant-only-the-first-time check for the 1000g reward). `yoatin_driver` (`:432-632`, new `.../yoatin.rs`) ports the ten-state (`0`-`9`) "Bear Hunt - Again" chain (quest 39): an `NT_GIVE IID_STAFF_BEARHEAD` turn-in unconditionally grants a `WS_Hunter_Belt` on every completion (C never gates this one, unlike the Aristocrat's gold - a real, documented asymmetry between the two nearly-identical drivers). Both share a new file-local `AREA28_QA` table (`brannington_forest.c`'s own `qa[]`, byte-for-byte identical to `AREA26_QA`) and both drivers' god-only "reset me" (`case 3`) speak a visible `say(cn, "reset done")` line via `World::npc_say` - a real deviation from `smugglecom_driver`'s silent equivalent, verified directly against this file's own C source and documented in each module's doc comment. `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS`'s `ch_driver` dispatch is an unconditional `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` tail call in C, so it reuses the SimpleBaddy AI end-to-end (same precedent as `CDR_SMUGGLELEAD`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`/`CDR_PENTER` - the `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` reuse gates in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` were widened alongside it); unlike `CDR_SMUGGLELEAD` (which has no `zone.rs` spawn wiring at all, a pre-existing gap out of this task's scope), `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS`'s real zone-file template (`Aston_Robber_Boss`, `zones/28/WS_Robbers.chr`) does carry an `arg="aggressive=1;..."` string, so `zone.rs` gained a proper `NT_CREATE`+`apply_simple_baddy_create_message` spawn branch, matching the `CDR_FORESTMONSTER`/`CDR_SWAMPMONSTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER` precedent exactly. `robberboss_dead` (`:634-663`) is a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs::apply_robberboss_death_from_hurt_event`, gated on the killer's `staffer_broklin_state()` sitting in `5..=10` (Broklin's own dialogue driver, `src/area/29/brannington.c`, remains unported - the field is read directly off `PlayerRuntime`, same "read state owned by another area's unported driver" precedent as `rouven_driver`'s `carlos2_state` read) - completes quest 46 ("A Miner's Vengeance") via the standard `complete_legacy`+`give_exp`+questlog-resend pattern and destroys the boss-kill quest-chain items (`IID_STAFF_BOSSMASTER`/`BOSSLAIR`/8x `ROBBERKEY1`-`8`, all new `DEV_ID_WHITESTAR`-prefixed constants in `item_driver/{helpers,ids}.rs`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area28.rs` (`aristocrat_player_facts`/`apply_aristocrat_events`, `yoatin_player_facts`/`apply_yoatin_events`) and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area28.rs` (`aristocrat_driver_131`/`yoatin_driver_132`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`). `PlayerRuntime::staffer_aristocrat_state`/`staffer_yoatin_state`/`staffer_broklin_state` accessors, quests 38/39/46's `QuestMeta` rows, and `init_staff_quests`'s state-derived quest-flag initialization all already existed from earlier iterations - no `PlayerRuntime`/`quest.rs` changes were needed. 5 exhaustive-match update sites gained the two new `CharacterDriverState::Aristocrat`/`Yoatin` variants (`character_driver.rs` x2, `world/npc_fight.rs`, `world/npc_idle.rs`, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init), following the established one-file-per-NPC pattern exactly. 23 new focused tests (`world::tests::aristocrat`/`yoatin`: every dialogue state transition, both silent no-ops each, the text "repeat" range-gated reset, the god-gated "reset me" visible-`say` quirk (both the speaks-it and non-god-ignored cases), and all three `NT_GIVE` branches each). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3763 core [+23] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 28` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/28/WS_Robbers.map`/`placed_characters=247`, "entering Rust game loop", and 280+ ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` now marks this task `[x]` - Area 28 is fully closed.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) STARTED: ported `spirit_brannington_driver` (`:1128-1308`), the ghost NPC who explains the necromancer plot and runs "The Brannington Holy Relic" (quest 44) - the first NPC-driver slice of this file (the `IDR_STAFFER2` item driver, all 6 subtypes, was already fully ported by earlier iterations; `DRD_STAFFER_PPD`'s `spiritbran_state` offset/accessor and `init_staff_quests`'s quest-44 state-derived flag also already existed from that earlier work). New `CDR_SPIRITBRAN = 94` (`character_driver.rs`), `CharacterDriverState::SpiritBran` variant (4 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/{mod,spiritbran}.rs`, establishing the area's shared `AREA29_QA` table (`brannington.c`'s own file-local `qa[]`, 14 rows - 2 more than `AREA28_QA`'s 12: `thousand gold`/`five thousand silver`, answer codes 4/5, consumed only by the still-unported `broklin_driver`'s permanent trade service). `process_spiritbran_actions` ports the six-state (`0`-`5`) dialogue chain digit-for-digit following the `world::npc::area28::aristocrat`/`yoatin` `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split precedent exactly (`SpiritBranPlayerFacts`/`SpiritBranOutcomeEvent`, no `dat->current_victim` staleness-reset preamble, visible `say(cn, "reset done")` on the god-only "reset me" case): greeting (opens quest 44) through three lore lines to "ask Count Brannington about his jewelry", then an `NT_GIVE IID_STAFF_HOLYRELIC` turn-in. The reward itself is a genuine structural departure from every previously-ported Brannington-family NPC: C grants a save (`ch[co].saves++`, capped at 10, skipped for `CF_HARDCORE`) instead of gold/an item, and `Character::saves`/`Character::flags` both live on `World` (not `PlayerRuntime`), so `SpiritBranOutcomeEvent::QuestDone`'s `times_done == 1` gate is applied directly against `world.characters` inside `ugaris-server`'s new `apply_spiritbran_events` (`crates/ugaris-server/src/area29.rs`) rather than needing a `ZoneLoader`/item-instantiation round trip like `aristocrat`'s gold or `yoatin`'s belt - a new `spiritbran_save_cap()` const-fn getter exposes the C `< 10` cap across the crate boundary. New `IID_STAFF_HOLYRELIC` (`DEV_ID_WARR`, `0x000025`, `item_driver/ids.rs`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::spiritbran_driver_133` (registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`). 11 new focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/spiritbran.rs` (every dialogue state transition, both silent no-ops, the text "repeat" range-gated reset (both directions), the god-gated "reset me" visible-`say` quirk (both branches), and all three `NT_GIVE` branches). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3774 core [+11] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/29/wrforesttown.map`/`placed_characters=285` (including a real `wrforesttownquestchar.chr` Spirit-of-Brannington instance), "entering Rust game loop", no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 marked `[~]`: `Count`/`Countessa`/`Daughter Brannington` (share `countbran_bits`, quest 40 - port together next per the earlier research's suggested slice order), `Brenneth Brannington` (quests 41-43), `Forester Brannington` (no quest, ties to already-ported `IDR_FORESTSPADE`), `Guard Brannington` (quest 64, needs `arkhata_ppd.rammy_state`), `Broklin` (quests 45/46 + gold<->silver trade, largest remaining NPC), `Grinnich`+`Shanra` (share the tower dungeon flow), and `Centinel` (fight-AI reuse + a `death_hooks.rs` kill-counter, smallest remaining slice) all remain - plus `DRD_STAFFER_PPD` accessors still needed for `countessabran_state`(5)/`daughterbran_state`(6)/`guardbran_state`(8)/`forestbran_state`(10, only `forestbran_done` exists so far)/`grinnich_state`(15)/`centinel_count`(17).
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) progress: ported `CDR_CENTINEL` (`:2725-2828`), the wooden marionette sentinels guarding the Brannington tower (`zones/29/wrtower.chr`'s `centinel_count` template, `driver=106` - the sibling plain `centinel` template stays `driver=7`/`CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` directly and never reaches this driver id, a real data-driven distinction not a porting gap). New `CDR_CENTINEL = 106` (`character_driver.rs`); C's own `ch_driver` dispatch is an unconditional `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` tail call, so it reuses the SimpleBaddy AI end-to-end - same precedent as `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS` (the 4 `character.driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` reuse gates in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs` widened alongside it, plus the `NT_CREATE`+`apply_simple_baddy_create_message` zone-spawn wiring in `zone.rs`). `centinel_dead` (`:2725-2758`) is a new `crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs::apply_centinel_death_from_hurt_event`: increments the killer's `staffer_ppd.centinel_count` (new `PlayerRuntime::staffer_centinel_count`/`set_staffer_centinel_count` accessors, `player/misc.rs`'s `STAFFER_PPD_CENTINEL_COUNT_OFFSET = 17 * 4`), capped at 30, reports milestone progress at kills 1/10/20 via `queue_system_text`, and on the 30th kill calls the already-ported `World::teleport_char_driver` to `(33,143)` - only resetting the counter back to `0` if the teleport actually moved the character (C's own `if (teleport_char_driver(...)) { ppd->centinel_count = 0; }` guard), so a blocked teleport leaves the counter at 30 for a retry on the next kill. Registered in `world_events/mod.rs`'s `apply_pk_hate_from_hurt_events` fan-out, right after `apply_robberboss_death_from_hurt_event`. No `CharacterDriverState`/`ch_respawn_driver` work was needed: like `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS`, C's own `ch_died_driver` for this file never falls through to `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`'s own death effects (no earthdemon-style retaliation for centinels), and respawn scheduling in this port is driver-agnostic (gated on the template's `CF_RESPAWN` flag, not a per-driver `ch_respawn_driver` hook), matching that precedent exactly. 5 new focused tests (`crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/world_events.rs`: the first-kill milestone message, the 10th/20th milestone messages via two sequential kills, the 30th-kill teleport+counter-reset, and a driver-mismatch/non-lethal-hit no-op guard) plus 1 in `player/tests/staffer.rs` (the offset round-trip, independent of the neighboring `dwarfchief_state` field). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3799 core [+1] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1180 server [+4], all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/29/wrforesttown.map`/`placed_characters=285`, "entering Rust game loop", and 30+ ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 stays `[~]`: `Brenneth Brannington` (quests 41-43), `Forester Brannington`, `Guard Brannington` (quest 64), `Broklin` (quests 45/46 + trade service), and `Grinnich`+`Shanra` remain. Incidental finding, not fixed (out of scope): `world/npc_idle.rs`'s two `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`-reuse gates are missing `CDR_WHITEROBBERBOSS` (only `world/npc_fight.rs`'s combat-side gates have it), unlike this task's `CDR_CENTINEL` addition which was added to both files' gates correctly.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) progress: ported `count_brannington_driver` (`:590-851`, plus its `countbran_give_keys` helper `:546-583`), `countessa_brannington_driver` (`:1509-1664`), and `daughter_brannington_driver` (`:1671-1827`) together, since all three share `staffer_ppd.countbran_bits` and run quest 40 ("The Jewels of Brannington"). New `CDR_COUNTBRAN = 91`/`CDR_COUNTESSABRAN = 92`/`CDR_DAUGHTERBRAN = 93` (`character_driver.rs`), three new `CharacterDriverState` variants (6 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init - each site widened once for all three variants together). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/{countbran,countessabran,daughterbran}.rs`, one file per NPC per the module layout rule (not merged into one file despite sharing state), all three sharing the pre-existing `AREA29_QA` table. `count_brannington_driver`'s five-state (`0`-`4`) greeting chain opens quest 40 (gated on `!questlog_isdone(co,40)`, unlike the aristocrat/yoatin/spiritbran siblings' unconditional open), then its `NT_GIVE` handler accepts all three jewels (`IID_STAFF_COUNTJEWEL`/`COUNTESSAJEWEL`/`DAUGHTERJEWEL`): each destroys its own bundle of thief/color keys, grants a **manually-scaled** exp reward via `crate::quest::scale_exp(questlog_count(co,40), base)` (bases 60000/30000/30000) - *not* the `complete_legacy` quest-table path, since quest 40's own nominal exp is `0` ("exp awarded in driver" per `src/system/questlog.c:150-151`) - plus a first-completion-only gold reward (1000g/500g/500g) via C's `give_money` (a direct `Character::gold` add, no `ZoneLoader` needed, unlike `aristocrat`'s `create_money_item`), sets one `countbran_bits` bit, and calls `countbran_give_keys` (hands out any of the three mausoleum keys unlocked-but-not-carried, via the pre-existing `World::character_has_item_template`); once all three bits are set, quest 40 is marked done via `QuestLog::mark_done` (not `complete_legacy`, matching the `0`-exp no-op). `countessa_brannington_driver`/`daughter_brannington_driver` are near-identical secondary-reward NPCs gated on `countbran_bits` (2|8 and 4|16 respectively): their C `switch` bodies contain real intentional `// fall through` cascades (state `0`->`1`->`2`'s reward check can all resolve inside one driver call when the gating bits are already set) - ported as an explicit `loop` per driver rather than a plain `match`, the first non-single-file-per-tick-boundary state machine in this codebase's NPC ports. Both grant `min(bran_exp_base()*2, level_value(level)/4)` exp directly via `World::give_exp`; the Countessa additionally gives 500g (direct gold, like the Count's own rewards) while the Daughter gives a `lollipop` item (needs `ZoneLoader`, so a `DaughterBranOutcomeEvent::GiveLollipop` applied server-side, same `yoatin`/`WS_Hunter_Belt` precedent). Neither sibling's own `NT_TEXT` handler has a `case 3` ("reset me") at all - only `count_brannington_driver`'s `case 3` exists, and it resets **all four** states at once (`countbran_bits`/`countbran_state`/`countessabran_state`/`daughterbran_state`), a `CountBranOutcomeEvent::ResetAllBranStates` applied by the new `apply_countbran_events`. New `PlayerRuntime::staffer_countessabran_state`/`staffer_daughterbran_state` accessors (`player/staffer.rs`, offsets 5/6 - `player/misc.rs`'s `STAFFER_PPD_COUNTESSABRAN_STATE_OFFSET`/`DAUGHTERBRAN_STATE_OFFSET`); `questlog_reopen_q40`'s existing bits-clear logic and `init_staff_quests`'s quest-40 flag derivation both already matched C exactly and needed no changes (neither reads the two new fields, matching C's own `questlog.c`). 11 new `IID_STAFF_*` item-id constants (`ids.rs`: `COUNTJEWEL`/`COUNTESSAJEWEL`/`DAUGHTERJEWEL`/`MAUSOLEUMKEY1`-`3` `pub`, plus `pub(crate)` `BLUEKEY123`/`GREENKEY123`/`REDKEY123`/`THIEFKEY1`-`3` companions destroyed alongside each jewel). C's `IID_ARKHATA_LETTER3` `NT_GIVE` sub-branch inside `count_brannington_driver` (`:814-818`) is a documented gap, not ported: it belongs to area 37's still-entirely-unported Arkhata quest chain and `arkhata_ppd.letter_bits` has no accessor yet - falls through to the generic "no use for it" branch instead. Found and fixed a real pre-existing bug while cross-checking every `brannington.c` occurrence of the shared "Thou hast better use for this than I do" fallback line against C: all ten uses in this file are `quiet_say`, but the already-ported `world::npc::area29::spiritbran`'s own copy used the non-quiet `npc_say` - corrected to `npc_quiet_say` with a doc-comment note (no test depended on the distinction, so nothing needed weakening). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::{countbran_driver_134,countessabran_driver_135,daughterbran_driver_136}`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`; `area29.rs` gained `{countbran,countessabran,daughterbran}_player_facts`/`apply_{countbran,countessabran,daughterbran}_events`. 25 new focused tests across `world::tests::{countbran,countessabran,daughterbran}` (every dialogue state transition including the fallthrough-cascade paths, all three jewel `NT_GIVE` branches plus the all-bits-set `MarkQuestDone` trigger, the mausoleum-key reissue on "repeat", the god-only full-reset, and the always-hand-back `NT_GIVE` fallback for the two secondary NPCs). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3798 core [+24] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1176 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 8s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed "entering Rust game loop", no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 stays `[~]`: `Brenneth Brannington` (quests 41-43), `Forester Brannington`, `Guard Brannington` (quest 64), `Broklin` (quests 45/46 + trade service), `Grinnich`+`Shanra`, and `Centinel` remain.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) progress: ported `forest_brannington_driver` (`:1315-1502`), the Brannington Forest hint NPC who decodes thief-mage treasure maps into dig locations - the only Brannington-family NPC with no quest of its own (no `questlog_open`/`questlog_done` call anywhere in the C body). New `CDR_FORESTBRAN = 97` (`character_driver.rs`), `CharacterDriverState::ForestBran` variant (4 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` grouping arm, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/forestbran.rs`, following the `world::npc::area29::spiritbran` `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split precedent exactly (`ForestBranPlayerFacts`/`ForestBranOutcomeEvent`, no `dat->current_victim` staleness-reset preamble, sharing the pre-existing `AREA29_QA` table). `process_forestbran_actions` ports the five-state (`0`-`4`) greeting chain (no quest-open call, unlike every quest-bearing sibling) plus the `NT_GIVE IID_STAFF_FORESTMAP` branch: five dig-location hints keyed by the *read-only* `staffer_ppd.forestbran_done` counter (already advanced elsewhere by the earlier-ported `IDR_FORESTSPADE` treasure-dig path, `crates/ugaris-server/src/area_apply.rs`) plus a sixth "found them all" line at `forestbran_done >= 5` - this driver never itself increments `forestbran_done`. A real, subtle C behavior was preserved exactly by close reading of `brannington.c:1436-1483`: the `IID_STAFF_FORESTMAP` branch never zeroes `ch[cn].citem` itself, so the outer "let it vanish, then" tail unconditionally destroys the map after every hint (even the "found them all" case) - the non-matching fallback branch (`quiet_say` + `give_char_item`-or-`destroy_item`) already clears `citem` itself, so the outer tail is a no-op there. New `PlayerRuntime::staffer_forestbran_state`/`set_staffer_forestbran_state` accessors (`player/staffer.rs`, `player/misc.rs`'s `STAFFER_PPD_FORESTBRAN_STATE_OFFSET = 10 * 4`, distinct from the neighboring `forestbran_done` field at offset 11 which already had accessors from the earlier `IDR_FORESTSPADE` port) plus a new `PlayerRuntime::clear_forestbran_done` raw-zero setter (the god-only "reset me" branch resets `forestbran_done` to a literal `0`, unlike `set_forestbran_done`'s existing `dig_index + 1` encoding, which cannot represent `0` at all). New `IID_STAFF_FORESTMAP` (`DEV_ID_WARR`, `0x000029`, `item_driver/ids.rs`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area29.rs::{forestbran_player_facts,apply_forestbran_events}` and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::forestbran_driver_137` (registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`). 12 new focused tests (`world::tests::forestbran`: every dialogue state transition, the text "repeat" range-gated reset, the god-gated "reset me" dual-field reset (both branches), all six `forestbran_done` hint variants confirming the map is always destroyed, the non-player-giver fallback, and the unrelated-item fallback; `player::tests::staffer`: the new offset's independence from `forestbran_done` plus the `clear_forestbran_done` raw-zero behavior). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3810 core [+12] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1180 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings (confirmed with a `touch main.rs` forced rebuild). 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed `placed_characters=285`, "entering Rust game loop", no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 stays `[~]`: `Brenneth Brannington` (quests 41-43), `Guard Brannington` (quest 64), `Broklin` (quests 45/46 + trade service), and `Grinnich`+`Shanra` remain.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) progress: ported `brenneth_brannington_driver` (`:858-1121`), the memory-loss assassin NPC who runs three back-to-back mini quests sharing one state counter - "A Grolm's Spoils" (41), "A Thief's Loot" (42), "A Necromancer's Notes" (43). New `CDR_BRENNETHBRAN = 96` (`character_driver.rs`), `CharacterDriverState::BrennethBran` variant (4 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` grouping arm, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/brennethbran.rs`, following the `world::npc::area29::spiritbran`/`countbran` `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split precedent exactly (`BrennethBranPlayerFacts`/`BrennethBranOutcomeEvent`, no `dat->current_victim` staleness-reset preamble, sharing the pre-existing `AREA29_QA` table). `process_brennethbran_actions` ports the full sixteen-state (`0`-`15`) chain: three greeting/lore triads gated behind `NT_GIVE` turn-ins of `IID_STAFF_BRENNETHDAGGER`/`_POTION`/`_JOURNAL` (each completing its own quest and jumping the state forward), a closing three-line "I was to kill the thief mages... giving that life up... thank you" sequence (with an `emote(cn, "smiles")`), then done. `case 5`/`case 9`'s C `questlog_isdone(co, 42/43)` fast-forward guards (skip straight to state `9`/`12` with no dialogue if the *next* quest is already done from a stale state) are faithfully ported via two new `BrennethBranPlayerFacts::quest42_is_done`/`quest43_is_done` fields (`player.quest_log.is_done(42/43)`), unlike the plan's original doc-comment note (written before re-reading the C body closely) which assumed this couldn't be reproduced. `case 2`'s ("repeat"/"restart") four-way range reset (`<=4`->`0`, `5..=8`->`5`, `9..=11`->`9`, `12..=15`->`12`) is ported as `BrennethBranOutcomeEvent::ResetToMiniQuestStart` with the target state resolved in `World` itself (pure `i32` range math). `case 3`'s god-only "reset me" speaks a visible `say(cn, "reset done")` line first, same precedent as every other Brannington-family sibling. Three new `IID_STAFF_BRENNETHDAGGER`/`_POTION`/`_JOURNAL` item-id constants (`item_driver/ids.rs`, `DEV_ID_WARR` `0x000026`-`0x000028`, right after the existing `IID_STAFF_HOLYRELIC`). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area29.rs::{brennethbran_player_facts,apply_brennethbran_events}` and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::brennethbran_driver_138` (registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`); `PlayerRuntime::staffer_brennethbran_state` and quest 41-43's `init_staff_quests` state-derived flags both already existed from earlier iterations, so no `player/staffer.rs`/`quest.rs` changes were needed. 11 new focused tests (`world::tests::brennethbran`: the greeting-opens-quest41 transition, the waiting-state-4 silent no-op, both `case 5` branches (normal open-quest-42 and the quest-already-done fast-forward), the closing thank-you/emote transition, the text "repeat" range-gated reset, the god-gated "reset me" visible-`say` quirk (both branches), and the dagger/journal/unrelated-item `NT_GIVE` branches). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3821 core [+11] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1180 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed `placed_characters=285`, "entering Rust game loop", no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 stays `[~]`: `Guard Brannington` (quest 64), `Broklin` (quests 45/46 + trade service), and `Grinnich`+`Shanra` remain.
- 2026-07-11: Paperwork catch-up (no code changes): iteration 36 fully ported and tested `broklin_driver` (`src/area/29/brannington.c:2118-2395`, `CDR_BROKLIN = 99`, quests 45/46 "The Missing Pickaxe"/"The Head Robber" plus the permanent gold<->silver trade service, `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/broklin.rs`, 867 lines with 30 focused tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/broklin.rs`, wired through `crates/ugaris-server/src/{area29.rs,tick_npc/area29.rs}::broklin_driver_139`) but committed (`e8ae7b4`) without updating `PORTING_TODO.md`/`PORTING_LEDGER.md`. Verified still green (`cargo test --workspace`, `cargo build -p ugaris-server`, boot-smoke) before continuing new work; `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 REMAINING note updated to drop `Broklin` from the remaining list.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) progress: ported `guard_brannington_driver` (`:1834-2022`), the town guard who greets new arrivals and, once Count Brannington's family-heirloom chain (`staffer_ppd.countbran_bits`, quest 40) is complete, sends the player to investigate Arkhata for "Finding Arkhata" (quest 64, exp `60000`, the standard `complete_legacy` path - not "exp awarded in driver" like quest 40). New `CDR_GUARDBRAN = 95` (`character_driver.rs`), `CharacterDriverState::GuardBran` variant (4 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message`, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/guardbran.rs`, following the `world::npc::area29::spiritbran`/`countessabran` `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split precedent exactly (`GuardBranPlayerFacts`/`GuardBranOutcomeEvent`, no `dat->current_victim` staleness-reset preamble, sharing the pre-existing `AREA29_QA` table). The nine-state (`0`-`8`) chain has two *real* C fallthrough cascades (no `break` at all, unlike the `countessabran`/`daughterbran`/`brenneth` siblings' own guarded-`break` fast-forwards) - both ported as an explicit `loop { match new_state { ... continue ... } }`, same mechanism `countessabran`/`daughterbran` established: `case 1` silently gates on player level `>= 45` plus all three `countbran_bits` jewel bits and, when satisfied, falls straight into `case 2`'s dialogue in the same tick (state jumps `1`->`3` in one call); `case 6` silently gates on a **cross-area** read of area 37's still-entirely-unported `rammy_driver`'s own `arkhata_ppd.rammy_state > 0` and, when satisfied, falls straight into `case 7`'s dialogue plus `questlog_done`/achievement (state jumps `6`->`8` in one call) - same "read state owned by another area's unported driver" precedent as `PlayerRuntime::staffer_broklin_state`/`staffer_carlos2_state`. New read-only `PlayerRuntime::arkhata_rammy_state()` accessor (`player/areas_misc.rs`, `player/misc.rs`'s `ARKHATA_PPD_RAMMY_STATE_OFFSET = 0 * 4` - `rammy_state` is `struct arkhata_ppd`'s first field) - no writer, since only area 37's own unported driver ever advances it in C. New `PlayerRuntime::staffer_guardbran_state`/`set_staffer_guardbran_state` (`player/staffer.rs`, `player/misc.rs`'s `STAFFER_PPD_GUARDBRAN_STATE_OFFSET = 8 * 4`). `case 0`'s C body is a real oddity, preserved exactly: dialogue is conditional on `countbran_state == 0`, but the state increment and `didsay = 1` happen *unconditionally* either way (ported as `GuardBranOutcomeEvent::UpdateGuardBranState` always firing once `new_state != facts.guardbran_state`, independent of whether `npc_quiet_say` actually ran). Unlike every other Brannington-family sibling ported so far, this driver's `NT_TEXT` `switch` has **no `case 3`** ("reset me") at all - verified by direct re-reading of `brannington.c:1964-1990` - only the "repeat"/"restart" `case 2`'s three-way range reset (`<=1`->`0`, `2..=6`->`2`, `7..=8`->`7`) exists; a god's "reset me" here produces no event at all, a documented deviation from `spiritbran`/`countbran`'s visible `say(cn, "reset done")` precedent, not an oversight. New `ACHIEVEMENT_GREAT_EXPLORER` award wiring: `crates/ugaris-server/src/achievement.rs::award_great_explorer_achievement` (thin `award_bare_achievement` wrapper, same shape as `award_dragonsbane_achievement`/`award_islena_ladykiller_achievement` - `GreatExplorer`'s `AchievementDef.target == 0` confirms it's a bare one-off, not threshold-based), called from the new `crates/ugaris-server/src/area29.rs::apply_guardbran_events`'s `QuestDone` arm (now `async`, first Brannington-family `apply_*_events` to need `achievement_repository`) alongside the standard `complete_legacy`/`give_exp`/questlog-resend path. New `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::guardbran_driver_140`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`. 17 new focused tests: 15 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/guardbran.rs` (every dialogue state transition including both fallthrough cascades' single-tick state jumps, the `case 0` dialogue-conditional-but-state-always-advances quirk, the three-way text-reset ranges, the no-`case 3` confirmation, and the always-hand-back `NT_GIVE` fallback), 1 in `player/tests/staffer.rs` (the new offset's independence from `brennethbran_state`), 1 in `player/tests/areas_misc.rs` (the read-only `rammy_state` accessor's independence from `clerk_state`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3854 core [+17] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1180 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--area-id 29` confirmed `map_file=ugaris_data/zones/29/wrforesttown.map`/`placed_characters=285`, "entering Rust game loop", no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 29 stays `[~]`: only `Grinnich`+`Shanra` (shared tower dungeon flow) remain.
- 2026-07-11: Area 29 (`src/area/29/brannington.c`) CLOSED: ported `grinnich_driver` (`:2402-2557`) and `shanra_driver` (`:2565-2719`), the shared tower-dungeon flow - Grinnich hints at the buried tower entrance from ground level, Shanra rewards the sentinel gauntlet at the bottom with a story about the Grimoire of Animation and teleports the player there (`5,106`) and back (`53,129`). New `CDR_GRINNICH = 104`/`CDR_SHANRA = 105` (`character_driver.rs`), two new `CharacterDriverState` variants (5 exhaustive-match update sites: `character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` grouping arm, `world/npc_fight.rs`'s `simple_baddy_lastfight`, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s bless-friend target scan, `zone.rs`'s spawn-time init - matching the real zone-file templates, `zones/29/wrtowerfriendly.chr`'s `grinnich_the_hermit`/`shanra_the_mage`, `driver=104`/`105`). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area29/{grinnich,shanra}.rs`, following the `world::npc::area29::spiritbran`/`daughterbran` `World`/`PlayerRuntime`-split precedent exactly (`Grinnich`/`ShanraPlayerFacts`/`OutcomeEvent`, no `dat->current_victim` staleness-reset preamble on `NT_TEXT`, visible `say(cn, "reset done")` on the god-only "reset me" case, always-hand-back `NT_GIVE`). Neither NPC has a quest of its own (no `questlog_open`/`done` calls in either C body) - `Grinnich`'s five-state (`0`-`4`) chain and `Shanra`'s six-state (`0`-`5`) chain are plain `staffer_ppd.grinnich_state`/`shanra_state` writes. `Shanra`'s own C body is the first Brannington-family driver to call `teleport_char_driver` directly from its dialogue chain (states `1`/`4`) rather than through a `ZoneLoader`/quest-completion round trip - ported as direct `self.teleport_char_driver(...)` calls inside `process_shanra_actions` itself (`World` already owns the teleport machinery, same precedent as `world::lab`/`world::jail`), so `ShanraOutcomeEvent` only needed a state-write variant and the god-reset variant, no teleport-carrying event at all; C's own discarded return value (no `if` guard at either call site) is reproduced verbatim - state always advances regardless of whether the teleport actually moved the player. `case 2`'s ("repeat"/"restart") two-way range reset differs by one boundary between the siblings: Grinnich's completed range is `3..=4` (its own last state is `4`), Shanra's is `3..=5` (its own last state is `5`) - both reset back to state `3`, matching each NPC's own "waiting to greet again" checkpoint. New `PlayerRuntime::staffer_grinnich_state`/`set_staffer_grinnich_state`/`staffer_shanra_state`/`set_staffer_shanra_state` accessors (`player/staffer.rs`, `player/misc.rs`'s `STAFFER_PPD_GRINNICH_STATE_OFFSET = 15 * 4`; `SHANRA_STATE_OFFSET = 16 * 4` already existed from the earlier `mark_staffer_animation_book_seen` item-driver port, reused read-only here, now also exposed as a real getter/setter pair). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area29.rs::{grinnich,shanra}_player_facts`/`apply_{grinnich,shanra}_events` and `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area29.rs::{grinnich_driver_141,shanra_driver_142}`, registered last in `tick_npc::run_all`. 24 new focused tests: 10 in `world::tests::grinnich` (every dialogue state transition including the Sirname-gendered `case 1` greeting, both silent no-ops, both text-"repeat" reset branches, the god-gated "reset me" visible-`say` quirk (both branches), the always-hand-back `NT_GIVE`), 11 in `world::tests::shanra` (every dialogue state transition including both teleport assertions via post-call `(x,y)` checks, the silent no-op, both text-"repeat" reset branches, the god-gated "reset me" quirk (both branches), the always-hand-back `NT_GIVE`), 1 in `player/tests/staffer.rs` (the new `grinnich_state` offset's independence from the pre-existing `shanra_state` field). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3876 core [+22] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1180 server, all green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace` clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke on `--bind-addr 127.0.0.1:5556` (area 1) confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop", 280+ ticks with no panic. `PORTING_TODO.md` now marks Area 29 `[x]` - every `brannington.c` NPC driver is ported; Area 30 (`clanmaster.c`) is next.

  (`crates/ugaris-server/src/world_events/death_hooks.rs`), wired into

  point fix the `LqTicker`/`StrTicker` bug needed was already applied
