- 2026-07-11: Area 32 progress: ported `start_mission`/`build_fighter`
  (`missions.c:678-1130`, the 41x41-tile instance-dungeon spawn "accept
  job Alpha/Beta/Gamma" triggers). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/
  area32/mission_start.rs` (`World::plan_start_mission` - busy-slice
  search over the 6x6 area/n grid using only plain `World` map/item
  mutation, existing-occupant/junk-item cleanup, `IID_MISSIONFIGHTER`/
  `_CHEST`/`_DOOR1`/`_DOOR2`/`_ENTRY` map scan and key wiring,
  `build_fighter_stat_values`/`special_item_tier_for_level` pure math,
  `mission_status_lines` HUD text) plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/
  area32.rs::spawn_mission_fighter` (the `ZoneLoader`/`ServerRuntime::
  allocate_character_id`-needing half: template instantiation, stat
  rescale, `mis_key`/big-boss-special-item/`armor_spell`/`weapon_spell`
  equip, exp/level finalize, map placement) - wired through a new
  `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::SpawnMissionFighters` event from
  `governor.rs`'s case 7-9 text handler, which also now calls
  `teleport_char_driver` directly (already a plain `World` method, no
  event needed). Spawned fighters get `driver = CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` directly
  rather than introducing an unused `CDR_MISSIONFIGHT` id, since nothing
  in this port yet needs to distinguish a mission fighter from any other
  SimpleBaddy by driver id (documented deviation, revisit alongside
  `mission_fighter_dead`). New `IID_MISSIONFIGHTER`/`IID_MISSIONCHEST`/
  `IID_MISSIONDOOR1`/`IID_MISSIONDOOR2`/`IID_MISSIONENTRY` constants
  (`item_driver/ids.rs`) and `DEV_ID_MISSION` (`item_driver/helpers.rs`).
  Confirmed against the real `ugaris_data/zones/32/{monsters,chests,
  dungeon_doors,mission}.itm`/`newdungeon.map` data that every marker
  item and fighter template this code depends on already exists and
  loads correctly. Still not ported: `missionchest_driver`
  (`IDR_MISSIONCHEST`)/`mission_done`/`mission_fighter_dead`'s
  kill-counter hook - a mission can now be started and its dungeon
  populated, but nothing yet closes the loop back to `ppd->kill_*`/
  `find_item` or lets the reward chest open. 8 new tests in
  `world/tests/mission_start.rs`, 2 new in `world/tests/governor.rs`, 2
  new in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs`. `cargo fmt
  --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3927 core [+10] + 81 db + 3 net + 45
  protocol + 1184 server [+2], all green, zero failures, zero warnings.
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean. Boot-smoke confirmed both area 1
  (unaffected) and a dedicated area-32 run ("loaded area zone map"
  `placed_items=2217 placed_characters=4`, "entering Rust game loop", no
  panics). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 32 stays `[~]`; REMAINING note updated.
- 2026-07-12: Area 32 progress: ported `mission_fighter_dead`'s kill-
  counter hook (`missions.c:1852-1881`). Spawned fighters now get a new
  `driver = CDR_MISSIONFIGHT = 112` (`character_driver.rs`) instead of
  plain `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`, widening every `driver == CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`
  SimpleBaddy-AI-reuse gate in `world/npc_fight.rs`/`world/npc_idle.rs`
  to also accept it (same shape as the existing `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_
  CENTINEL`/etc entries on those lists). New pure `record_mission_
  fighter_kill`/`try_solve_mission` (`world/npc/area32/mission_start.rs`,
  the `nr = ch[cn].deaths` kill-counter switch and `mission_done`'s
  five-objective completion check) plus `crates/ugaris-server/src/
  world_events/death_hooks.rs::apply_mission_fighter_death_from_hurt_
  event` (needs `PlayerRuntime::governor`, registered in `world_events::
  mod.rs`'s hurt-event fan-out): a mission-fighter kill now bumps the
  matching counter, re-prints `mission_status`'s HUD lines to the killer,
  and on completing every objective promotes `active` to `solved` and
  announces "You've finished the job...". Closes the loop for the
  kill-type objectives; `find_item` (chest loot) still can never
  complete since `missionchest_driver` remains unported, so no real
  mission can fully auto-solve yet unless it rolls zero chest items. 4
  new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs`
  (partial kill re-prints status without solving, the completing kill
  solves and announces, non-player-killer no-op, non-mission-fighter-
  driver no-op); updated the existing `spawn_mission_fighter` test's
  driver-id assertion. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3927
  core + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1188 server [+4], all green, zero
  failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 10s
  boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener ready"/"loaded area zone
  map"/"entering Rust game loop", no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 32
  stays `[~]`; REMAINING note updated.
- 2026-07-12: Area 32 progress: ported `missionchest_driver`/`mission_done`
  (`missions.c:1790-1847`, `IDR_MISSIONCHEST`). New pure gate
  `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/area32_missions.rs::missionchest_
  driver` (only checks `character.id != 0`, mirroring the `chest_driver`/
  `ChestTreasure` precedent of deferring everything else) returns a new
  `ItemDriverOutcome::MissionChestOpen`, dispatched in `dispatch.rs` and
  routed through the chest family in `tick_item_use_chests.rs`/
  `tick_item_use_completion.rs`. All the real logic - `mdtab[ppd->
  md_idx]->itemtemp == NULL` empty-chest check, the `drdata[1..5]` key
  search (reusing `chest_required_key_id`/`exact_carried_door_key_access`,
  the same inventory-30../cursor-only search `IDR_EDEMONDOOR` already
  established, no skeleton-key/keyring fallback per C), cursor-occupied
  check, `create_item`+name/desc stamp, `find_item[0]` bookkeeping,
  `mission_status` HUD re-print, and `mission_done`'s auto-solve - lives
  in `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::apply_mission_chest_open`
  (needs `PlayerRuntime::governor` and a `ZoneLoader`, neither reachable
  from pure `World`/item-driver code). `MissionFighterData` (`mission_
  start.rs`) gained `itemtemp`/`itemdesc` fields (previously only
  `itemname` existed) populated digit-for-digit from C's `mdtab[]`
  literal table (thief/spy/graverobber/hideseek carry a find-item
  objective; beast/ruffian/vampire do not). One real C quirk is preserved
  deliberately: the key-search/unlock message runs *before* the cursor-
  occupied check (`missions.c:1811-1831`), so if the only carried copy of
  the required key sits on the cursor itself, C still reports "You use...
  to unlock the chest." even though that same non-empty cursor then
  blocks the reward item - `MissionChestApplyResult::CursorOccupied`
  carries an optional `key_name` for exactly this case. This closes the
  loop for the `find_item` objective: a real mission can now fully
  auto-solve end-to-end (kill objectives plus chest loot) through live
  gameplay. 2 new tests in `crates/ugaris-core/src/item_driver/tests/
  area32_missions.rs` (pure-gate character-zero/real-player dispatch) and
  9 new tests in `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs`
  (empty template, grant without solving, grant that solves and
  announces, key required, key unlocks, the cursor-blocked-by-its-own-key
  quirk, and plain cursor-occupied with no key). `cargo fmt --all`,
  `cargo test --workspace`: 3929 core [+2] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol +
  1195 server [+7], all green, zero failures, zero warnings.
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke on area 32 confirmed
  "entering Rust game loop", no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 32 stays
  `[~]`; REMAINING note updated (only `CTPOT`'s stat-potion flow and the
  rotating "special offer" purchase remain).
- 2026-07-12: Area 32 progress: ported the rotating "special offer" gear
  purchase (`missions.c:1300-1325`/`1588-1589`/`1627-1648`, qa codes
  18/19). New `crates/ugaris-server/src/area32.rs::
  regenerate_mission_giver_special_offers` runs once per driver call,
  *before* `World::process_mission_giver_actions` - matching C's own
  single-function ordering (the regen check sits at the top of
  `mission_giver_driver`, ahead of the message loop) - since only
  `ugaris-server` can reach `ZoneLoader::instantiate_item_template`.
  Rerolls a fresh `World::create_special_item` into the governor's own
  new `SPECIAL_OFFER_SLOT = 30` inventory slot (`world/npc/area32/
  governor.rs`, mirroring C's `ch[cn].item[30]`) whenever the 12-hour
  `next_spec` timer elapses or the slot is empty; `World::destroy_item`
  already null-clears every inventory slot pointing at the destroyed old
  item, so no manual slot-clear was needed there. New pure
  `special_offer_strength_base`/`special_offer_cost` (`governor.rs`) port
  the two strength/base and price ladders digit-for-digit.
  `MissionGiverDriverData` gained `next_spec`/`spec_cost` fields
  (`#[serde(default)]`, mirroring C's `struct mission_giver_data`).
  "special offer" (qa code 18) extends the 5-minute visibility window
  directly in `World` (owns `data` already) and queues a new
  `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::ShowSpecialOffer` for the `ugaris-server`-only
  `legacy_item_look_text` preview plus price/prompt lines, in the same
  order C prints them (preview, then price, then prompt - all inside one
  event handler so ordering can't be split by the World-vs-event text
  queue boundary). "buy the special offer" (qa code 19) is fully handled
  in `World` with no event at all: giving an item that already exists
  needs no `ZoneLoader`, just `World::give_char_item` + point deduction +
  clearing the slot + resetting `next_spec` to `0` to force an immediate
  reroll. "offer" (qa code 11)'s previously-dropped trailing "I also have
  a special offer..." teaser line is restored too. One documented,
  self-healing timing gap: since the regen pre-pass and message
  processing are two separate calls (C has them as one), a freshly
  spawned governor's very first tick can see an empty slot if a player
  asks before any tick elapses - heals itself the very next tick.
  `dlog`'s audit-log line on a successful buy has no reproduction, same
  as every other `dlog` call in this file. 8 new tests in `crates/
  ugaris-core/src/world/tests/governor.rs` (teaser line, visibility
  extension + event queuing, successful buy incl. item transfer/point
  deduction/slot clear/`next_spec` reset, insufficient-points refusal,
  no-inventory-space refusal) and 4 new in `crates/ugaris-server/src/
  tests/area32_missions.rs` (fresh-governor seed, skip-when-not-due,
  reroll-and-destroy-old-item-after-12h, and the `ShowSpecialOffer` event
  handler's preview/price/prompt text) - the latter's `special_item_
  loader` helper duplicates `ugaris-core`'s own private `create_special_
  item`-family-template-registration test helper since it isn't exported
  across the crate boundary. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`:
  3933 core [+8] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1199 server [+4], all
  green, zero failures, zero warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`
  clean, zero warnings. 10s boot-smoke confirmed "legacy TCP listener
  ready"/"loaded area zone map"/"entering Rust game loop", no panics.
  `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 32 stays `[~]`; REMAINING note updated (only
  `CTPOT`'s multi-turn stat-potion flow remains, a deeper cross-cutting
  gap needing `Item::mod_index`/`mod_value` fields that don't exist
  anywhere in this port yet).
- 2026-07-12: Area 32 progress: ported `CTPOT`'s multi-turn custom-stat-
  potion skill-naming flow (`missions.c:1202-1211,1652-1739`, `find_
  skill_text`). Turns out the "deeper cross-cutting gap" the previous
  REMAINING note described was already closed by unrelated earlier work:
  `Item::modifier_index`/`modifier_value` (C `mod_index`/`mod_value`)
  already existed on `crates/ugaris-core/src/entity.rs`'s `Item` struct
  and were already exercised by `world::special_item`/`world::alchemy`/
  several area-procedural-item systems - no `Item`/protocol/wire changes
  were needed at all, just wiring three already-scaffolded pieces
  together: `mission_giver_give_reward`'s `"CTPOT"` arm now sets
  `ppd.statowed`/resets `statcnt`/`stat` and prompts for a skill count
  (previously fell through to the generic "out of stock" branch); qa
  codes `20`/`21`/`22` now set `statcnt` and print the per-count prompt
  text (previously only checked the `statowed < 1` guard and did
  nothing else); a new `parse_stat_potion_skill_text` (`governor.rs`)
  recognizes free-text skill names in the `NT_TEXT` `NoMatch` arm once
  nothing else matched, preserving two real C quirks digit-for-digit
  (the `"lightning"` prefix check only compares 8 of its 9 characters,
  `strncasecmp(text, "lightning", 8)`; `"speed"` maps to `V_SPEED`/
  `CharacterValue::Speed`, not `V_SPEEDSKILL`) plus the "only `stat[0]`/
  `stat[1]` are ever checked for an empty slot, so a third skill always
  lands in `stat[2]` by elimination" quirk. The finalize step
  (`create_item("mis_potionbase")` + `mod_index`/`mod_value` stamping +
  `give_char_item`, success resets `statowed` to `0` leaving `stat[]`
  as-is, failure resets `stat[]` to `[0,0,0]` leaving `statowed`
  untouched so the player can retry) is a new `MissionGiveOutcomeEvent::
  GiveCustomStatPotion` applied server-side in `crates/ugaris-server/src/
  area32.rs::apply_mission_giver_events`, since it needs `ZoneLoader` -
  same split precedent as the pre-existing `GiveItemReward`. Once drunk,
  `mis_potionbase`'s own `driver=32`/`IDR_FLASK` template (its `arg=`
  bytes pre-set `shaken=1`, confirmed against the real
  `ugaris_data/zones/generic/mission_bonus.itm` data) needed zero new
  code: the already-ported generic `flask_driver`/`install_beyond_
  potion_spell`/`recompute_character_values` pipeline reads any item's
  `modifier_index`/`modifier_value` fields directly, and boot-smoke on
  area 32 confirms it loads (`item_templates=1562`). This raises the
  reward-shop functional count to 23/24 - only `RNORB` ("Random Orb")
  remains unported, needing a `create_orb()`-equivalent (`tool.c:
  3679-3760`, a distinct general 32-skill-table orb roll, not the
  narrower 5-skill `handle_orb_find` already ported for Area 12 mining)
  that doesn't exist anywhere in this port yet. 20 new focused tests
  (11 in `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/tests/governor.rs`: buy/skill-
  count-select/single-and-two-and-three-skill-naming/unrecognized-text/
  no-"skill"-substring/"speed"-quirk/"lightning"-quirk cases; 4 in
  `crates/ugaris-server/src/tests/area32_missions.rs`: the
  `GiveCustomStatPotion` event's success/per-`statcnt`-modifier-value/
  no-room/missing-template cases). One real test-authoring gotcha worth
  recording: `find_skill_text`'s leading skip chain expects the actual
  upstream NT_TEXT payload shape (`"<Name> says: <text>"`, same
  precedent already established by `world::strategy_worker_trim_
  command_prefix`'s own tests) - plain unprefixed text like `"attack
  skill"` gets both words eaten by the skip itself and never reaches the
  prefix table, so the new skill-naming tests use that prefixed shape
  while the pre-existing qa-table-driven tests (buy/skill-count-select)
  correctly keep using plain text, since `analyse_text_qa`'s tokenizer
  was never ported with this skip in the first place. `cargo fmt --all`,
  `cargo test --workspace`: 3944 core [+11] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol
  + 1203 server [+21], all green, zero failures, zero warnings.
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke on area 32 confirmed
  "entering Rust game loop", no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 32 stays
  `[~]`; REMAINING note updated (only `RNORB` remains).
- Area 33 started: ported `gorwin_driver`/`CDR_TUNNELER_GORWIN`
  (`world/npc/area33/gorwin.rs`) - the Long Tunnels entrance-lobby NPC's
  full greeting/QA/`change_tunnel_level` state machine, reusing the
  already-ported `tunnel_used`/`tunnel_clevel`/`gorwin_tunnel_level`
  `PlayerRuntime` accessors. `IDR_TUNNELDOOR`/`IDR_TUNNELDOOR2` item
  drivers and the procedural creeper-dungeon generator (`build_fighter`
  et al.) remain - see the `src/area/33/tunnel.c` row above.
  `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3954 core [+12] + 81 db +
  3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green. `cargo build -p
  ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke on areas 1 and 33 confirmed "entering
  Rust game loop", no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 33 marked `[~]`.
- Area 33 progress: ported `IDR_TUNNELDOOR`'s `DOOR_EXIT_EXP`/
  `DOOR_EXIT_MILITARY` exit-pillar branches and `give_reward` in full
  (`item_driver::area33_tunnel::tunneldoor_driver` +
  `world::tunnel::apply_tunnel_reward`, dispatched via a new
  `tick_item_use_tunnel.rs`), including the auto-promote-on-mastery ladder
  and the `achievement_add_tunnel_level` wiring the P4 task's own checkbox
  text called out (`award_tunnel_level_achievement`, same shape as
  `award_silver_mined_achievement`). Factored the shared
  `find_next_available_level` search out of `gorwin.rs`'s own private copy
  into `player::find_next_available_tunnel_level`, reused by both. C's
  `COL_HEADING`/`COL_YELLOW` color markers around the "Tunnel Mastery!"/
  promoted-level text are dropped (documented deviation - this item-use
  dispatcher's `feedback: Vec<(CharacterId, String)>` has no raw-byte
  counterpart, matching `dispatch_minewall_outcome`/`dispatch_lab_outcome`'s
  existing plain-text precedent). `DOOR_ENTRY`/`DOOR_CONTINUE` (the
  procedural creeper-dungeon generator) and `IDR_TUNNELDOOR2`
  (`mean_door`) remain unported, so a player currently has no live-
  gameplay way to actually reach an exit pillar - this slice ports the
  reward math ahead of that wiring, tested directly (13 new tests: 6 in
  `world/tests/tunnel.rs` covering every `give_reward` branch with exact
  reward-value arithmetic, 4 in `item_driver/tests/area33_tunnel.rs`
  covering the driver's automatic-call/exit-pillar/unsupported/area-gate
  paths, 3 pre-existing `player::tests::tunnel` unaffected). `cargo fmt
  --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3964 core [+17] + 81 db + 3 net + 45
  protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures. `cargo build -p
  ugaris-server` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust
  game loop" with no panics (touches the completed-action item-use
  pipeline). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 33 stays `[~]`; REMAINING note
  updated.
- Area 33 progress: a previous iteration ported `IDR_TUNNELDOOR2`
  (`mean_door`, `tunnel.c:736-772`) - `item_driver::area33_tunnel::
  mean_door_driver`, `World::tunnel_mean_door_area_clear` (C's
  `check_area_clear`), and `apply_item_driver_outcome`'s
  `TunnelDoorAreaCheck`/`TunnelDoorFlavor` arms (door-open map mutation +
  unconditional `DOOR_CHECK_INTERVAL` self-reschedule, and the "mind of
  its own" player-interaction flavor line respectively) - but left
  `tick_item_use_completion.rs`'s outer `match outcome { ... }` without
  arms for those two new variants, breaking `cargo build -p ugaris-server`
  (non-exhaustive match) with zero test coverage added. Fixed this
  iteration: added the missing match arms (both variants are pure
  `executed += 1` no-ops there - the actual world mutation already
  happened inside `apply_item_driver_outcome`, same shape as the
  pre-existing `PalaceDoorTick`/`PalaceDoorKeyRequired` arms), and added
  the tests that should have shipped with the original port: 9 new tests
  (4 in `item_driver/tests/area33_tunnel.rs` covering the automatic-call
  open/stay-closed/missing-context-defaults-closed paths, the player-
  interaction flavor line, and the area-33 gate; 4 in
  `world/tests/tunnel.rs` covering `tunnel_mean_door_area_clear`'s empty/
  non-player-blocks/player-ignored/out-of-rectangle cases). `DOOR_ENTRY`/
  `DOOR_CONTINUE` (the procedural creeper-dungeon generator) remain the
  only unported piece of this task - until that lands there is still no
  live-gameplay path to either an exit pillar or a mean door. `cargo fmt
  --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3973 core [+9] + 81 db + 3 net + 45
  protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures. `cargo build -p
  ugaris-server` clean, zero warnings. Boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust
   game loop" with no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 33 stays `[~]`;
   REMAINING note updated to reflect `IDR_TUNNELDOOR2` now being ported.
- Area 33 closed: ported `tunneldoor`'s `DOOR_ENTRY`/`DOOR_CONTINUE`
  branches (`World::plan_tunnel_entry` - `find_unused_sector`, seeded
  block/creeper marker resolution, `update_exit_door`, HP/teleport;
  `tick_item_use_tunnel.rs`'s `spawn_tunnel_creeper` for the actual
  `ZoneLoader` character/item instantiation) - see the `src/area/33/
  tunnel.c` row above for the full breakdown. This was the last unported
  piece; every C function in `tunnel.c` now has a Rust counterpart.
  `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3985 core [+31] + 81 db +
  3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures/warnings.
  `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, boot-smoke on areas 1 and 33
  confirmed "entering Rust game loop", no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area
  33 marked `[x]`.
- Area 34 (`src/area/34/teufel.c`) STARTED: ported `CDR_TEUFELQUEST`, the
  rat-hunt-reward NPC's full per-tick body (`teufelquest_driver`,
  `teufel.c:1476-1608`) plus `special_rat_reward` (`:1442-1474`) - the
  first character driver in this file to get a live tick pass (the item
  drivers `IDR_TEUFELDOOR`/`IDR_TEUFELARENA`/`IDR_TEUFELARENAEXIT`/
  `IDR_TEUFELRATNEST` and the `CDR_TEUFELRAT` death-scoring hook were
  already ported in earlier iterations). New `crates/ugaris-core/src/
  world/npc/area34/{mod.rs,teufelquest.rs}`: `mod.rs` carries the shared
  `is_demon` sprite helper, the full 34-row `struct qa qa[]` table
  (`teufel.c:138-239`, transcribed verbatim as `TEUFEL_QA` even though
  the "play"/"bet"/"prizes" rows only matter once a future
  `teufelgambler_driver` port exists, since C shares one `qa[]`/
  `analyse_text_driver` across both NPCs), and `teufel_analyse_text` - a
  bespoke wrapper (not the plain `analyse_text_qa` call every other NPC
  file uses) documenting a real, verified-from-source C quirk this file's
  own `analyse_text_driver` has that no other ported NPC's copy does: it
  *always* returns a truthy "recognized" value once its guard clauses
  pass (own-name match, a real code-2..8 match, or no match at all - even
  an empty word list - all fall through to the same trailing
  `return 1;`), so the caller's `talkdir`-facing side effect fires
  regardless of whether anything was actually said. `teufelquest.rs`
  ports the `NT_CHAR` greeting (is_demon-gated pitch text, 16-tile
  `char_dist`, `mem_check_driver`/`mem_add_driver` slot-7 greet-once,
  12-hour `memcleartimer` erase cadence - the shared `struct gamble_data`
  narrowed to just the one field this driver reads/writes, since `nr` is
  gambler-only), the `NT_TEXT` "give experience"/"give
  military"/"give money"/"give godly" reward dispatch (reusing the
  already-ported `World::give_exp`/`give_military_pts`/
  `PlayerRuntime::teufel_rat_kills`/`teufel_rat_score` from the
  `teufelrat_dead` death-scoring slice, plus a new file-local
  `teufelquest_give_money` mirroring `countbran_give_money`'s no-
  achievement-ladder shape), and `special_rat_reward`'s 7-tier reward
  ladder (`create_special_item`/`healing_potion{1,2,3}`, the latter via
  `loader.instantiate_item_template`). Unlike most NPC ports,
  `World::process_teufelquest_actions` takes `&mut ZoneLoader` directly
  (not a deferred outcome event) since the top 4 reward tiers need
  `create_special_item`'s template access - same precedent as
  `world::npc::area1::robber`. New `CharacterDriverState::TeufelQuest`
  variant (`character_driver.rs`, plus the usual exhaustive-match
  additions in `npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`) and `zone.rs` spawn wiring;
  server-side `crates/ugaris-server/src/{area34.rs,tick_npc/area34.rs}`
  mirror the `area33`/Gorwin precedent exactly (a `TeufelQuestPlayerFacts`
  snapshot + `TeufelQuestOutcomeEvent::SetRatKillsScore` apply function,
  no `ZoneLoader`-dependent server-side glue needed beyond passing it
  through). 13 new focused tests (`world/tests/teufelquest.rs`) covering
  both greeting branches, greet-once memory, the 12/16-tile distance
  gates (NT_CHAR's own 16-tile gate vs `analyse_text_driver`'s internal
  12-tile gate), all three cash-out rewards, the god-only "give godly"
  debug set, the "who are you" name-question reply, and the below-1000
  no-reward-item floor. Still unported in this file: `teufeldemon_driver`
  (a custom `is_demon`-filtered `NT_CHAR` self-defense hook feeding an
  otherwise-unconditional `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` tail call - this NPC's own
  zone template is `aggressive=0`, so the generic SimpleBaddy sighting
  path won't add enemies on its own; needs a bespoke sighting hook plus
  either widening the `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` fight/idle gates or a direct
  `fdemon_demon`-style dispatch), `teufelgambler_driver` (the 3 chip-tier
  dice-betting NPC, sharing `TEUFEL_QA`'s "play"/"bet"/"prizes" rows and
  needing new `IID_BRONZECHIP`/`IID_SILVERCHIP`/`IID_GOLDCHIP` item-id
  constants plus a `chip_stack`-consumption helper distinct from the
  already-ported `nomad_stack_driver` splitting logic), and
  `teufelrat_driver`'s own live tick body (the death-scoring hook exists,
  but nothing yet widens `CDR_TEUFELRAT` into the `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY`
  fight/idle gates the way `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_FORESTMONSTER` were, so
  spawned rat-nest rats don't actually wander/fight yet). `cargo fmt
  --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 3998 core [+13] + 81 db + 3 net + 45
  protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero failures/warnings. `cargo build
  -p ugaris-server` clean, 8s boot-smoke on area 34 confirmed "entering
  Rust game loop" `area_id=34`, no panics. `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 34
  marked `[~]` with the above REMAINING note.
- Area 34 `CDR_TEUFELDEMON` (`teufel.c::teufeldemon_driver`, `:373-394`)
  is now ported. New `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area34/
  teufeldemon.rs`: `World::apply_teufeldemon_sighting_messages` ports the
  driver's own `NT_CHAR` self-defense hook (`(ch[co].flags & CF_PLAYER)
  && !is_demon(co)` then `is_valid_enemy(cn, co, -1)` +
  `fight_driver_add_enemy(cn, co, 0, 1)`), reusing the already-ported
  `World::simple_baddy_can_add_standard_enemy(.., require_visible: true,
  hurtme: false)` + `add_simple_baddy_enemy_unchecked(.., priority: 0,
  ..)` pair instead of reimplementing `is_valid_enemy`'s group/
  `can_attack`/`char_see_char` checks plus `fight_driver_add_enemy`'s own
  `start_dist`/`char_dist`/neutral-zone gating. Unlike the *pure*
  tail-call NPCs (`CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_FORESTMONSTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`, just a
  gate widening with no extra logic), this driver needed a real
  architectural call: C's `char_driver` never calls `remove_message`
  between `teufeldemon_driver`'s own `NT_CHAR` loop and its
  `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` tail call, so both handlers observe the *same*
  still-queued messages in the same tick - reproduced by calling the new
  hook from `world::npc_messages::process_simple_baddy_message_actions_
  with_random` immediately *before* that function's own generic message
  drain (which does consume/clear `driver_messages`), rather than as a
  separate `tick_npc` pass (any pass registered at its own position in
  `tick_npc::run_all`, which is what every other NPC file does, would
  observe an already-drained queue, since the generic SimpleBaddy message
  pass runs very early - `area22::pass_0` - relative to where an
  area34-numbered pass would land). The real zone data confirms why the
  hook matters: `zones/34/teufel.chr`'s `teufer1`-`teufer3` templates
  spawn with `aggressive=0`, so the generic SimpleBaddy `NT_CHAR` aggro
  branch (gated on that flag) never fires on its own - this driver's own
  override is the *entire* source of Teufelheim demons attacking
  disguise-less players. `zone.rs` spawn wiring seeds `NT_CREATE` +
  `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` from the template's own
  `aggressive=0;helper=0;scavenger=0;startdist=20;chardist=0;stopdist=40;`
  arg string, same precedent as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`. Also widened
  the `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` fight/idle gates (`world/npc_fight.rs`'s two
  lists, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s two lists) to include `CDR_TEUFELDEMON`,
  since without that the enemies this hook records would never actually
  get attacked (`process_simple_baddy_attack_action`'s driver gate would
  reject them). 4 new focused tests (`world/tests/teufeldemon.rs`):
  disguise-less-player attacked, demon-suit-disguised player ignored,
  non-player sighting ignored, and an end-to-end message-then-attack
  check proving the gate widening actually lets a recorded enemy be
  attacked. `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`: 4002 core [+4] +
  81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green, zero
  failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server` clean, 8s boot-smoke
  on area 34 confirmed "entering Rust game loop" `area_id=34`, no panics.
  Still unported in this file: `teufelgambler_driver` (the 3 chip-tier
  dice-betting NPC) and `teufelrat_driver`'s own live tick body (death
  scoring exists; nothing yet widens `CDR_TEUFELRAT` into the
  `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` fight/idle gates the way `CDR_TEUFELDEMON` now is, so
  spawned rat-nest rats still don't wander/fight). `PORTING_TODO.md` Area
  34 left at `[~]` with an updated REMAINING note.
- Area 34 (`src/area/34/teufel.c`) CLOSED. Ported the last two remaining
  drivers: `CDR_TEUFELGAMBLER` (`teufelgambler_driver`, `:1233-1435`,
  plus its three `give_rewardN` tables `:635-1204` and `set_chip_data`,
  `:1211-1231`) as a new `crates/ugaris-core/src/world/npc/area34/
  teufelgambler.rs` - the three-tier ("bronze"/"silver"/"gold" chip)
  dice-betting NPC, spawn-time `nr` parsed from `arg="1"/"2"/"3"` in
  `zone.rs` (same `CDR_LOSTDWARF` precedent as the sibling
  `teufelquest.rs`), reusing the shared `TEUFEL_QA` table's previously-
  unwired `2`/`3`/`4` ("bet one/two/five") answer codes. All 3 reward
  tables (60 total roll-outcome cases across the three tiers, symmetric
  low/high dice-sum ranges 3-20/43-60) transcribed as literal `match`
  tables rather than a shared formula, since the three tiers genuinely
  diverge in *kind* (not just multiplier) for several roll values (e.g.
  roll 7/56 is a goldchip item in tables 1-2 but a money grant in table
  3) - verified case-by-case against the C source rather than assumed
  symmetric. Chip-stack consumption (`it[in].drdata+0` count, `set_chip_
  data`'s sprite/description update) reimplements the same byte layout
  `ugaris-server::stacks::stack_count`/`set_stack_count` already use for
  `StackKind::BronzeChip`/`SilverChip`/`GoldChip`, independently (crate
  boundary - `ugaris-core` cannot see `ugaris-server`'s `stacks.rs`). The
  `(CF_GOD) && strstr(msg->dat2, "reward: ")` debug/cheat branch is
  ported verbatim, including firing independently of whether the QA table
  matched anything that tick (a sibling `if`, not `else if`, in C) and
  including the `Bug #1778`/`Bug #1779` fallback text for a cheat roll
  that doesn't land on any table case (reusing `crate::world::npc::
  area30::clanclerk::parse_int_atoi` for the `atoi` call instead of a new
  copy). `CDR_TEUFELRAT` (`teufelrat_driver`, `:1610-1626`) turned out to
  need no new NPC file at all: its own `NT_CHAR` case body is empty
  (commented out in C - `// co = msg->dat1;`), making it a *pure*
  unconditional tail call to `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)` - the
  exact same shape as `CDR_PENTER`/`CDR_FORESTMONSTER`/`CDR_TWOROBBER`
  (no dedicated `world/npc/*.rs` file either), unlike its sibling
  `CDR_TEUFELDEMON` (which needed one for its own extra `NT_CHAR` hook).
  Closed by widening the same 4 `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` fight/idle gate sites
  `CDR_TEUFELDEMON` already widened (`world/npc_fight.rs`'s two lists,
  `world/npc_idle.rs`'s two lists) to also include `CDR_TEUFELRAT`, plus
  a new `zone.rs` spawn-time block seeding `NT_CREATE` + `apply_simple_
  baddy_create_message` from the real `rat70`-`rat94b` templates' own
  `arg="aggressive=1;helper=1;scavenger=10;startdist=15;chardist=0;
  stopdist=40;"` string (death-scoring was already ported in an earlier
  iteration - `PlayerRuntime::add_teufel_rat_kill`/`world_events::
  death_hooks::apply_teufel_rat_death_from_hurt_event` - this closes the
  other half, the live wander/attack AI). New `CharacterDriverState::
  TeufelGambler(TeufelGambleDriverData)` variant added 3 exhaustive-match
  arms the compiler forced (`character_driver.rs`'s `apply_simple_baddy_
  create_message` fallback, `npc_fight.rs`/`npc_idle.rs`'s "no driver-
  specific enemy tracking" fallbacks) - `CDR_TEUFELRAT` needed none of
  these since it has no dedicated `CharacterDriverState` variant at all.
  New tick pass `crates/ugaris-server/src/tick_npc/area34.rs::
  teufelgambler_driver_160` (registered in `tick_npc::run_all` right
  after the existing `teufelquest_driver_159`) calls a pure `World::
  process_teufelgambler_actions(&mut ZoneLoader)` with no `PlayerRuntime`
  facts/events plumbing at all (unlike `teufelquest`'s `PlayerFacts`/
  `OutcomeEvent` split) - gold/inventory are both directly mutable
  through `World` alone. 17 new focused tests: 12 in a new `world/tests/
  teufelgambler.rs` (tier-specific greeting dialogue, "no chips, no game"
  when the stack is missing/wrong-color, partial vs. full chip-stack
  consumption on a bet regardless of the RNG-dependent win/lose roll, the
  god-only "reward: N" cheat granting money and its `Bug #1778` fallback
  for an off-table roll, non-god cheat rejection, greet-once memory,
  "who are you" self-reply, and the 3 `IID_*CHIP` constants against the C
  header), 1 in `world/tests/npc_fight.rs` (the `CDR_TEUFELRAT` gate
  widening, mirroring the existing `CDR_TWOROBBER` test), and 2 in
  `zone.rs`'s own test module (`arg="2"` -> `TeufelGambleDriverData.nr`
  spawn parsing; the `rat80` template's SimpleBaddy-state seeding from
  its zone-file `arg=`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`:
  4017 core [+15] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green,
  zero failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace`
  clean, zero warnings. 12s boot-smoke confirmed "entering Rust game
  loop" plus hundreds of clean ticks with NPC-driver logging, no panic.
  `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 34 now `[x]` - every driver in `teufel.c` is
  ported.
- 2026-07-12: started the P4 "Area 36 - `caligar.c`" task (item drivers
  were already fully ported; the character drivers were not). Ported the
  three non-quest-chain drivers: `CDR_CALIGARGUARD` (`guard_driver`,
  `:234-393` - Eulc/Margana, the "backwards is the key to entry" riddle
  duo, `world/npc/area36/caligar_guard.rs`, new shared `AREA36_QA` table
  in `world/npc/area36/mod.rs`), `CDR_CALIGARGUARD2` (`guard2_driver`,
  `:395-442` - a combat-capable sentry that taunts before falling through
  to plain `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` AI, `world/npc/area36/caligar_guard2.rs`),
  and `CDR_CALIGARSKELLY` (`skelly_driver`, `:444-446` - a pure
  unconditional tail call to `char_driver(CDR_SIMPLEBADDY, ...)`, same
  shape as `CDR_TEUFELRAT`/`CDR_TWOROBBER` - no dedicated `world/npc/*.rs`
  file needed; its own `skelly_dead_driver` door-unlock reward was
  already ported in an earlier iteration). Unlike every other dialogue
  driver in this codebase, `guard_driver` keeps *no* NPC-local state at
  all - C's `struct caligar_ppd` fields it touches (`guard_state`/
  `guard_last_talk`) are keyed entirely by the *player* being spoken to,
  shared between both guard NPCs, so which guard's turn it is falls out
  automatically from state parity plus a live `!strcmp(name, "Eulc")`
  check - no `CharacterDriverState` variant exists for either
  `CDR_CALIGARGUARD` or `CDR_CALIGARGUARD2`. `CDR_CALIGARGUARD2`/
  `CDR_CALIGARSKELLY`'s `CDR_SIMPLEBADDY` AI reuse follows the exact
  `CDR_TEUFELRAT` precedent: widened the same 4 fight/idle gate sites
  (`world/npc_fight.rs`'s two lists, `world/npc_idle.rs`'s two lists,
  re-exported through `world/mod.rs`) plus two new `zone.rs` `NT_CREATE`+
  `apply_simple_baddy_create_message` seed blocks (both templates carry
  no `arg=` line, same "nothing to parse" precedent as `CDR_FORESTIMP`).
  New `struct caligar_ppd` field offsets/accessors added to `player/
  misc.rs`+`areas_misc.rs` for all 15 non-dead fields at once (matching
  the struct's real declaration order end to end, confirmed byte-for-
  byte against `caligar.c:216-232`): `guard_state`/`guard_last_talk`/
  `guard2_last_talk` are consumed by this iteration's two drivers;
  `glori_state`/`arquin_state`/`smith_state`/`homden_state` (+ their
  `_last_talk` pairs, plus their C `analyse_text_driver`-code-2 mini-
  block-reset helpers) are staged ahead of time for the four still-
  unported quest NPCs since the whole struct's layout had to be worked
  out anyway. `obelisk_flag`/`amazon_flag` are declared in C but
  confirmed dead (never read or written by any driver in `caligar.c`) -
  no accessor exists for them, their bytes still round-trip verbatim via
  the raw blob. Also added a read-only `arkhata_monk_state` accessor
  (`ARKHATA_PPD_MONK_STATE_OFFSET = 4*4`, `struct arkhata_ppd::
  monk_state`) for `smith_driver`'s own "did you talk to the wise monk
  yet" gate on the still-entirely-unported Area 37 - same "read state
  owned by another area's unported driver" precedent as the pre-existing
  `arkhata_rammy_state`. 19 new focused tests: 7 in a new `world/tests/
  caligar_guard.rs` (state-0 turn-taking between the two guards, the 3
  second talk cooldown, the 600 second state-5 timeout reset, the
  `y > 106` fence check, the `NT_TEXT` "repeat" state-3 reset, and a
  canned-reply "hello" line), 3 in a new `world/tests/caligar_guard2.rs`
  (taunt + cooldown update, the 15 second cooldown gate, the distance-10
  cutoff), and 9 in `player/tests/areas_misc.rs` (round-trips and mini-
  block resets for all four staged quest-NPC state pairs plus guard/
  guard2, the shared `watch_flag` offset, and the read-only
  `arkhata_monk_state`). `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test --workspace`:
  4036 core [+19] + 81 db + 3 net + 45 protocol + 1204 server, all green,
  zero failures/warnings. `cargo build -p ugaris-server`/`--workspace`
  clean. 12s boot-smoke against `--area-id 36` confirmed "loaded area
  zone map" (`character_templates=132`, `placed_characters=263`) and
  "entering Rust game loop" plus hundreds of clean ticks, no panic.
  `PORTING_TODO.md` Area 36 left `[~]`: `glori_driver`/`arquin_driver`/
  `smith_driver`/`homden_driver` (the full quest-54-59 chain) remain
  unported for a future iteration.
